BARKAT ALI, KHAN BAHADUR, late Indian Rly. Accts. Service (b. 2nd Feb., 1883).—Retd., Feb., 1938.*

BARKAT ALI, MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late India Posts and Tels. (b. Aug., 1885).—Retd., Aug., 1940.†

BARKER, ARTHUR NEAL, B.Sc., A.I.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 15th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 31st Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930; civ. aff. serv., Sept., 1945.

BARKER, ALFRED EDWARD, Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 18th Feb., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Dec., 1940.

BARKER, BASIL HYDE, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 24th Oct., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 23rd Nov., 1928; exec. engr., Oct., 1937; mil. duty Feb., 1941 to Jan., 1946; exec. engr. again, Dec., 1945.

BARKER, FREDERICK ALLAN, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., M.D., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S., Jail Dept., Punjab.—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

BARKER, F., Ind. State Ryis. (b. 13th Sept., 1896).—Apptd. 16th Feb., 1923; dep. ch. accts. officer, B. and A. Rly., Jan., 1943.

BARKER, HENRY THOMAS, late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 7th March, 1885).—Retd. March, 1940.†

BARKER, PERCY EDGAR, O.B.E., late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 11th Apl., 1884).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

BARKER, ROBERT, Indian Police (b. 2nd Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 24th Nov., 1921; dist., supt., June, 1926; l.p.r. from Feb., 1946.

BARKER, RONALD WILLIAM, O.B.E., B.A., Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Burma Commn. (b. 20th Sept., 1883).—Retd., from civil employ, Jan., 1939.*

BARKER, S.R. WILBERFORCE ROSS, K.C.I.E., C.B.—Chairman, public services commn., May, 1926, to Nov., 1932; K.C.I.E., June, 1930.

BARLEE, SIR KENNETH WILLIAM, B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S., Bombay (b. 29th Nov., 1877).—Retd., Nov., 1937.*

BARLOW, HAROLD EVERARD, late Indian C.S., (United Provs.) (b. 12th May, 1902).—Educ. at Haileybury and Institut Minerva, Zurich; joined the service, 26th August, 1920; arrived, 7th Dec., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. commr.; jt. mag., July, 1922; city mag., Nov., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1925; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1926; confd., March, 1932; ret'd., Jun., 1946.

BARLOW, HENRY ARTHUR NORTHEY, O.B.E., Indian C.S., ret'd. late Indian Political Service (b. 11th Aug., 1905).—Educ. at Marlborough and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived, 18th Nov., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. coll. and mag.; asst. commr., March, 1931; jt. mag., March, 1931; under secty. to A.G.G. in States of W.I., Dec., 1933; vice-consul, Kashgar, Oct., 1934; and again, Aug., 1936; city mag., Peshawar, Dec., 1936; secty. to Resident for Eastern States, Nov., 1937; under secty., home dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1940; H.M.'s vice-consul, Zahidan, Oct., 1941; ditto, Meshed, Feb., 1942; ditto, Kerman, Sept., 1942; sec. to resdt., Cent. India, Sept., 1944; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta-Pishin, May, 1946; O.B.E., June, 1946; ret'd., June, 1946.

BARLOW, JOHN CUTHBERT, Burma Police (Cl. 1). (b. 7th Apl., 1921).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

BARNARD, ALBERT ALFRED, O.B.E., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs., (addl. ch. engr., Assam) (b. 5th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1914; asst. engr., Nov., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1919; exec. engr. Oct., 1923; addl. ch. engr., July, 1944.

BARNARD, ALBERT WILLIAM STANDFIELD, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 1st Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. suptd., 5th Oct., 1920; offg. supt., March, 1923;

confd., Apl., 1929; exec. offer., regional transp. authy., March, 1940; dep. insp. genl., W.I. and Gujarat states and police advr. to resdt., Rajkot, Sept., 1944; Ind. Pol. Medal, 1946.

BARNE, REV. GEORGE DUNSFORD, M.A., C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 6th May, 1879).—Joined the service, 11th May, 1908; princpl. and chaplain, The Lawrence Royal Military Sch., Sanawar, Oct., 1912; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1923; Bishop of Lahore, Apl., 1932; vice-chancellor of Punjab univ., Jan., 1936.

BARNES, SIR GEORGE STAPYLTON, K.C.B., K.C.S.I. (late member, Viceroy's Exec. Council).—Retd., Apl., 1921.*

BARNES, REV. JOHN, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 7th Jan., 1898).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, Bombay 25th Feb., 1928; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1930.

BARNES, PAUL, Indian Police (b. 5th Jan., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 26th Nov., 1936; confd., Jan., 1939.

BARNS, C. J., O.B.E., All India Radio (News Editor, Central News Orgn.) (b. Oct., 1902).—Joined the service 9th Sept., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

BARR, DAVID COURTNEY, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 30th July, 1914).—Educ. at Kingswood Sch., Bath, and Imperial Coll. of Science and Technology, London; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; cane inspr., Dec., 1940; jt. mag. and dep. collr., served under central govt., 1942, to March, 1944; dist. mag. and collr. July, 1944.

BARRACLOUGH, NORMAN, O.B.E., B.Sc., Dept. of Mines. (b. 10th June, 1896).—Joined the service 31st Dec., 1923; inspr., Dec., 1926; dep. ch. inspr., April, 1940; on depn. June, 1944.

BARRETT, HAMILTON BERESFORD, late Indian Forest Service (b. 4th June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 23rd Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1924; retd., Sept., 1943.

BARRETT, PAULIN FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (b. 19th July, 1913).—Educ. at Ald. Newton's Boys' Sch., Leicester, Univ. Coll., Leicester, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Jan., 1937; arrived in India, March, 1937, and served in U.P. as asst. magte., and collr.; on depn. with the Rampur Darbar, Oct., 1944.

BARRETT, LEONARD MILLER FRANK, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Madras) (b. 2nd March, 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; on depn. to Orissa, Apl. to Oct., 1936; and cent. govt., Jul., 1941 to Jul., 1943; suptg. engr., (confd.) Apl., 1944.

BARRINGTON, JAMES, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 15th Aug., 1911).—Educ. at Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. settlement officer, Oct., 1939; dep. sec., fin. dept., 1944; civ. aff. serv., Nov., 1944; dep. commr., Oct., 1945; dep. sec. home and judcl. dept. Feb., 1946.

BARRON, DOUGLAS SHIELD, Indian C.S., C.I.E. (addl. secty. (genl.), United Provs.) (b. 18th March, 1904).—Educ. at Archbishop Holgate's Sch., Barnsley, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, 20th Dec., 1926, and served in

the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933; addl. dep. secty. (genl.), United Provs., Aug., 1939; mag. and collr. (confd.) Jan., 1940; addl. secty., ditto, March, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

BARRON, HARRIE, B.A., B.A.I., late Indian State Ryis. (b. 5th Feb., 1890).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

BARSON, ALFRED GEOFFREY, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag.) (b. 24th Aug., 1916).—Educ. at Dulwich and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939 confd., 23rd Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940; collr. and jt. mag. 1942; jt. sec. cent. road traffic bd., Madras, Jan., 1945.

BARTELS, REV. REGINALD CAIN, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 18th Nov., 1883).—Apptd. temporary chaplain, 6th June, 1921; junior chaplain, Apl., 1924; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1934.

BARTER, ELDER GAUL, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 9th Jan., 1892).—Educ. at Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, 6th Dec., 1916, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1928; confd., Sept., 1930; retd., 4th Aug., 1943.

BARTLEY, SIR CHARLES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th Dec. 1882).—Educ. at St. Columba's Coll., Londonderry, Methodist Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived 10th Dec., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., and dep. collr., Sept., 1914; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1921; transfed. to Assam as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1930; supt. and remembr. of legal affairs and secty. to govt., legal dept., Bengal, Nov., 1931; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Apl., 1932; Apl. and Nov., 1933; confd., March, 1934; kt., June, 1942; retd., Dec., 1942.

BARTLEY, HERBERT GWYTHER, Indian Police (dep. insp. genl., Assam) (b. 18th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 6th Dec., 1912; supt., Dec., 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Assam, Jan., 1936; conf., Dec., 1937; Ind. police medal, 1944.

BARTLEY, SIR JOHN, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., LL.B., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Mar., 1886).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; pol. agen., Hill Tippera (temp.), July, 1915; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1916; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1924; offg. supt. and rembr. of legal affairs, Nov., 1923; offg. sec. govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept., Apl., 1924; confd. and sec., legisl. council, Aug., 1925; offg. jt. sec. and draftsman to Govt. of India, legisl. dept., Apl., 1932; addl. ditto, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, legisl. dept., Apl., 1933; secty., council of state, Apl. 1933; jt. secty. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Oct., 1933; addl. secty., ditto, Jan., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; kt., June, 1945; retd., 1946.

BARTON, JAMES CALVERT, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 6th Apl., 1881).—Retd., March, 1938.*

BARTON, JOHN EVERARD, Indian State Ry. (dist. contr. of stores) (b. 30th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1921; resdt. engr., Feb., 1922; dist. contr. of stores, G.I.P. Ry., Dec., 1932.

BARTON, LESLIE ERIC., C.I.E., Lieut.-Col.

ret'd., late I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 3rd. Feb. 1889).—Ret'd., Aug., 1944.†

BARTON, PHILIP TREHERNE, Burma Frontier Service (b. 15th Nov., 1917).—Apptd. 7th Jan., 1940.

BARTON, SIR WILLIAM PELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 29th May, 1871).—Ret'd., Apl., 1930.*

BARTY, DERMOT CAMPBELL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 27th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Fettes and St. Andrew's Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Sind as asst. collr.

BARUA, GURU PRASAD, B.L., late Indian Police, Assam, (b. March, 1888).—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1914; dep. supt., Oct., 1921; supt., Sept., 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1943.

BARUA, HARI PRASAD, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Assam) (b. 12th Apl., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1916; asst. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Sept., 1928; asst. ch. engr. and under secty. to Govt., P.W.D., Assam, March, 1934, to Nov., 1934; suptg. engr., July, 1944.

BARVE, SADASHIV GOVIND, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., Bombay) (b. 27th Apl., 1914).—Educ. at New English Sch., Budhwar Peth, Poona, Bombay Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Sind as asst. collr.; under secty., home dept., Bombay, Feb., 1940; dep. sec. ditto, Apl., 1940.

BASAK, KANTI CHANDRA, B.Sc. (Cal.), B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (magte. and collr., Bengal) (b. 17th Sept., 1901).—Educ. at Univ. of Calcutta and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-secty. to govt. fin., etc., depts., Bengal, Nov., 1930; confd., Feb., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1932, and again Feb., 1935; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1933; offg. asst. commr. of income tax, Oct., 1935; addtl. dist. magte., Dec., 1938; magte. and collr., confd., Dec., 1940; sec. educ., etc., dept., March, 1944; sec. edcn. co-opern. credit and reliefs dept., Oct., 1945.

BASHEER, AHMAD MUHAMMAD (see Ahmad M. B.).

BASHI RAM, SAHGAL, M.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 10th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; under-secty. to govt., P.W.D., Punjab, March, 1931; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1935; suptg. engr. (roads), subst., Jan., 1938; ret'd., 10th July, 1944.

BASHIR-UD-DIN, SAIVID, late Judicial dept., Bihar (b. 11th Dec., 1885).—Ret'd., Apl., 1941.

BASKIN, REV. JOHN EVETT D'ACRE, Indian Eccles. Estab. (junior chaplain) (b. 18th Dec., 1908).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 25th July, 1944.

BASSETT, THOMAS RALPH, late Indian Police (b. 25th July, 1894).—Joined the service 31st May, 1920; asst. supt., Apl., 1923; King's police medal, Jan., 1924; supt., June, 1925; asst. insp.-genl., rly. police, Oct., 1936; ret'd.; re-empld. from Sept., 1939; dep. dir. intel. bureau, govt. of India, July, 1944.

BASTIN, REGINALD WALTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd April, 1910).—Educ. at Trowbridge High Sch.,

University Coll. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1934; settlement offr., Nov., 1935; sec., bd. of rev., Bengal, Nov., 1942; addl. dist. mag., July, 1943; mag. and collr., July, 1944.

BASU, ADYA NATH, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 3rd Oct., 1886).—Joined the service, 11th Jan., 1911; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1912; exec. engr., Jan., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1930; confd., Nov., 1934.

BASU, A.T., (b. 1st May, 1897).—Joined the service, 25th Oct., 1921; held non-gaz. posts to Sept., 1943; supt., commn., dept. govt. of India, Sept., 1943; offg. asst. sec., ditto, June 1945 to Aug., 1945; asst. sec. ditto, June, 1946.

BASU, BIMALA PRASANA, B.Sc., B.A., Indian Forest Service (asst. consvr., Orissa) (b. 1st July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bihar and Orissa, on 9th Nov., 1923; div! for offr., Sasanda divn., Apl., 1944.

BASU, BIRENDRA KUMAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 8th Aug., 1888).—Educ. at schools in India, Calcutta univ., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived 20th Oct., 1913, and served as asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1919; confd., June, 1925; commr. for workmen's compensation, Bengal, Aug., 1945.

BASU, MRIGANKA MAULI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st March, 1912).—Educ. at the Scottish Church Collegiate Sch., Calcutta, Univ. of Calcutta, the Sch. of Economics, London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1936; addl. dist. mag., Apl., 1941; mag. and collr., July, 1943; commr., commel. taxes, Bengal, Apl., 1944.

BASU, NABA KUMAR, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (transpn. (power) supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 12th Feb., 1918, as asst. dist. loco. supt.; asst. supt. (junr.), E.I. rly, Oct., 1926; transpn. (power) supt., E.I. rly., May, 1934.

BASU, PRABODH CHANDRA, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th Jan., 1883).—Apptd. exec. engr., Bengal, 1st Jan., 1931.

BASU, SARADINDU, M.Sc., Meteorological Dept., Govt. of India, (b. 23rd Oct., 1903).—Apptd. Meteorologist, 31st Oct., 1927; confd. Oct., 1929.

BASU, SUBODH KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 7th April, 1900).—Joined the service on 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

BASU, SUKUMAR, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sec. agric. dept.) (b. 30th October, 1900).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Jesus Coll. Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1925; arrived 21st Dec., 1925, and served in the Central Province as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1933; offg. dep. secty. to govt., Bengal, fin., comm. and marine dept., Oct., 1934; ditto, pol. and appt. depts., March, 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; dep. secty., Home dept., Apl., 1937; secty., imp. cl. of agric. research, July, 1938; mag. and collr. (confd.), Feb., 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; C.I.E.,

June, 1944; sec., agric. dept., Jan., 1944; sec. dept. agric. for. and fisheries, Oct., 1945.

BATALIA RAM, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 20th Nov., 1887).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

BATES, HARRY STUART, B.A., Indian Civil Service (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 16th March, 1893).—Educ. at St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; arrived 11th May, 1920, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., April, 1921, to Nov., 1923; city magistrate, July, 1925; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1926; confd., April, 1932; dept. sec. to govt., U.P. rev. dept. (temp.), Oct., 1933; spec. manager, Court of Wards, Balrampur estate, Jan., 1935.

BATHEJA, HASSANAND RADHAKRISHNA, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (princpl., Patna coll.) (b. 11th July, 1891).—Joined the service, 6th Nov., 1918; prof. of economics, Ravenshaw coll., Cattuck, Jan., 1925; ditto, Patna coll., Oct., 1927; member tariff board, Jan., 1933; offg. dep. dir. of public instn., Bihar and Orissa, May, 1933; member, tariff board, Oct., 1934; offg. princpl., Ravenshaw coll., Cuttack, Sept., 1935; offg. dep. dir. of public instn., Bihar, March, 1936; princpl., G.B.B. Coll., Muzaffarpur, Dec., 1936; again, Jan., 1939; princpl. Patna Coll., July, 1942.

BATHGATE, KEITH, Indian State Ryls. (transpn. traffic commcl. supt.) (b. 1st March, 1900).—Joined the service, Jan., 1921; asst. supt. jur. scale, May, 1923; senr. scale (provl.), March, 1938; confd., Jan., 1939.

BATRA, BODH RAJ, India Posts and Telegs. (div. engr.) (b. 10th May, 1902).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1925; div. engr., July, 1933; confd., July, 1945.

BATRA, HARGOBIND LAL, M.C., Lieut.-Col., ret'd., late I.M.S. (Assam) (b. 14th June, 1885).—Retd., June, 1940.

BATRA, RAM KANWAR, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 8th Aug., 1898).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1922; apptd. to Punjab Engrg. Service, 14th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Central Provs., June, 1924; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1929; and again, Jan., 1933; confd., June, 1933; suptg. engr., March, 1943.

BATTEN, KENNETH CHARLES, Office of High Commr. for India (hr. exec. officer, genl. dept.) (b. 5th Aug., 1890).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 9th March, 1914; on mil. service from Jan., 1917, to Jan., 1919; transfld. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer (temp.), May, 1926; hr. exec. offr., Oct., 1938.

BATTERSBY, ERIC WORSLEY, Indian Police (b. 18th Apl., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., 6th Dec., 1935; a.d.c. to govr. of Burma, May, 1940; on depn. with Br. Army, Jan., 1944.

BATTYE, RICHMOND KEITH MOLESWORTH, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Aug. 1905).—First commn., 3rd Sept., 1925; in military employ until 28th Jan., 1931; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, June, 1932; under sect. to A.G.G., Balu, Feb., 1933; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, May, 1933; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, May, 1934; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse, June, 1935; offg. pol. officer in Sikkim in addn., Nov., 1935; under secty. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Aug., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1937; pol. agent, Gilgit (in addition), June, 1939, to Oct., 1939; asst. comdt., Malwa Bhil corps and ex-officio asst. to pol. agent in Malwa, March, 1945; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr. in Quetta-

Pishin, May, 1941; pol. agt., E. Kathiawar agency, Jan., 1943; on for. serv., Bastar State, Sept., 1943; pol. agt., E. Rajputana States, Jul., 1945; ditto Jaipur (addl. charge) Jul., to Dec., 1945; sec. to resident for Rajputana, July, 1946.

BATUK SINGH, M.B.E., Mil. Accts. Dept. of Govt. of India, (b. 21st Sept., 1903).—Joined the service, Apl., 1929; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., May, 1944.

BAUM, ERNEST FREDERICK, B.A., late Indian C.S., Burma (b. 4th Oct., 1881).—Retd., 1940.†

BAVDEKAR, RAJARAM SHRIPAD, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (addl. judge, high ct. Bombay) (b. 16th Sept., 1898).—Educ. at Bombay and Cambridge (Sidney Sussex); joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1922; arrived, 9th Feb., 1923; and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1931; and again, Sept., 1932; confd., Oct. 1933; regr., high ct., Bombay, appellate side, June, 1934, to June, 1937; dist. and sess. judge from March, 1938; addl. judge, high ct., Bombay, Mar., 1945.

BAVIN, LANCELOT NEVILLE, late Indian Police (b. 12th May, 1892).—Retd., Apl., 1937.*

BAW, MAUNG SHWE, M.A., Indian C.S. (excise commr., Burma) (b. 28th Apl., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Rangoon and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Apl., 1939; secty., health and p.w., May, 1940; civ. aff. serv., May, 1945; excise commr., Nov., 1945.

BAVINGTON, REV. REGINALD, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 29th April, 1902).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 1st Feb., 1942.

BAWA KARTAR SINGH, M.A., D.Sc., F.I.C., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 17th Apl., 1886).—Retd. 16th Apl. 1940.†

BAXTER, DHUNJIBHOY CURSETJEE, B.Sc. Eng., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.E. (b. 31st Jan., 1901).—Apptd. project offr. (civil) central tech. power bd. Sept., 1945.

BAXTER, GEORGE HERBERT, C.I.E., M.A., India Office (actg. asst. under sec. of state) (b. 30th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; war service: Roy. Fus., Sept., 1914, to Jan., 1915; R.G.A., Jan., 1915, to Armistice; apptd. jun. clerk, mil. dept., July, 1920; transfld. to fin. dept., Jan., 1921; asst. principal, Apl., 1921; deputed on special mission to Baghdad, Oct. to Dec., 1921; actg. pte. sec. to permt. under sec. of state, Jan., 1924; appt. as pte. sec. confd., Apl., 1924; principal, Nov., 1924; sec., Royal Commn. on Indian Currency and Finance, Aug., 1925, to July, 1926; adviser to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; actg. asst. secty., finl. dept., Jan.-Apl., 1932; and again, Dec., 1933; secty., cte. on Indian Reserve Bank legislation, July-Aug., 1933; secty. to Lord Privy Seal's confce. on air raid shelters (U.K.), Feb. to Apl., 1939; C.I.E., 1st Jan., 1942; actg. asst. under sec. of state, July, 1943; commr. income tax, Dec., 1945.

BAXTER, GERALD DORRETT, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 16th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 7th April, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1927; under sec. to govt., Aug., 1941; supt. of works, March, 1943.

BAXTER, JAMES, C.M.G., M.A., Fin. Advr. to the Govt. of Burma; apptd., Apl., 1937; C.M.G., 1st Jan., 1942.

BAXTER, NORMAN BERNARD, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 12th June, 1883).—Retd., June, 1938.*

BAYLEY, VERNON THOMAS, O.B.E., Indian Police (supl., Punjab).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 20th Nov., 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; asst. dir., intell. bureau, Home Dept., March, 1937; liaison offr., ditto, March, 1942; M.B.E., June, 1941; supt., C.I.D., Punjab, Sept., 1942; supt. (confd.), Feb., 1945; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

BAYLIS, ROBERT FRANCIS SARJEANT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 11th June, 1903).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., July, 1929; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Oct., 1932; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1934; confd., Jan., 1940.

BAYNES, FREDERIC WILLIAM WILBERFORCE, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. United Provs.) (b. 4th Aug., 1889).—Educ. at Harrow and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after the exam. of 1912; arrd., 22nd Nov., 1913, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1917; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1920; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1923; confd., Nov., 1929; dep. commr., March, 1930; commr. (confd.) Feb., 1945; C.I.E.; Jan., 1946.

BAZALGETTE, JOHN EVELYN ARNOLD, Maj. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 24th Jan., 1907).—First commn., 30th Aug., 1926; in military employ prior to 11th Oct., 1932; under training in Punjab, Oct., 1932; P.A. to Resident at Hyderabad, Jan., 1933; under secty. to the A.G.G., Punjab States, Aug., 1934; under secty. to the Pol. Resdt., Persian Gulf, June, 1935; H.M.'s Consul, Kerman, Nov., 1935; vice-consul, Khorramshahr, Apl., 1936; confd. in pol. dept., Nov., 1936; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Dec., 1939; sec. in cmd. and adjt., Kolhapur infantry and ex-officio under secty. to resdt. for Kolhapur and Deccan States, Apl., 1945; secty. to the resident for Kolhapur and the Deccan States, Sept., 1941; asst. comdt., C.R.'s police, March, 1943; under sec. to resdt., Baroda and Gujwar States, Nov., 1943; asst. pol. agt., Punjab hill States, May, 1944; pol. agt. (in addn.), Sept., 1944; pol. agt., Punjab Hill States, April, 1946.

BEADON, CORNWALLIS WILLIAM ROGER, Indian Police (b. 11th Oct., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 14th Nov., 1922; offg. dist. supt., March, 1932.

BEADON, ERIC HAMMET FAIRFAX, Indian Police (b. 19th March, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 10th Dec., 1926; confd. dist. supt., June, 1940; dist. supt., Oct., 1945.

BEADON, VERNON, Lieut.-Col., M.C., I.A., late Burma commn. (b. 14th Apl., 1884).—Retd., May, 1939.*

BEALE, REGINALD ARTHUR, Burma Agric. Serv. (b. 28th Mar., 1893).—Apptd. asst. botanist, Jan., 1921; asst. dir. agric. Apl., 1927; dep. director July, 1933; again Oct., 1945.

BEAMAN, GERALD WILLIAM DEVENISH, late Indian State Rlys., Burma (b. 18th Nov., 1887).—Retd. Apl., 1940.†

BEAMAN, VALENTINE ARDEN HULME,

M.B.E., late Burma Excise Service (b. 14th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Feb., 1940.†

BEAMISH, R. P., Lieut.-Col. (b. 13th May, 1895).—Asst. dir., intell., govt. of India, Oct., 1941; dep. dir. ditto, Dec., 1944.

BEAN, BASIL CLARENCE LAHEY, O.B.E., B.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 4th Sept., 1886).—Retd., Sept., 1941.†

BEASLEY, SIR HORACE OWEN COMPTON, Kt., B.A. (Cantab.), Barr., late Madras Judicial Dept. (b. 2nd July, 1877).—Retd., July, 1937.*

BEAUMONT, HERBERT CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 2nd June, 1912).—Educ. at Uppingham and Worcester Coll., Oxford; appointed after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Dec., 1936, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. liaison offr., Agartala, June, 1944; under sec. to resdt. for Rajputana, Jan., 1945; and again, Aug., 1945; under-sec., ext. affairs dept., June, 1946.

BEAUMONT, RT. HON. SIR JOHN WILLIAM FISHER, P.C. (b. 4th Sept., 1877).—Chief justice, high ct., Bombay, June, 1930, to Oct., 1944; kt., Jan., 1931; apptd. member of judcl. cte. of Privy Council, Oct., 1944.

BECKETT, JAMES, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Madras) (b. 25th March, 1891).—Joined the service, 16th Dec., 1911; asst. supt., July, 1913; offg. supt., July, 1919; confd., Sept., 1926; asst. inspr.-genl., March, 1927; dep. commr., crime bch., Oct., 1932; awarded Indian Police medal, Jan., 1933; dep. inspr. genl. (confd.), Oct., 1939; C.I.E.; Jan., 1944.

BECKETT, RONALD BRYMER, B.A., late Indian C.S., (b. 17th Jan., 1891).—Educ. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 30th Nov., 1914, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; guardian to H.H. Nawab of Mamdot, Oct., 1915, to Jan., 1917; sec., municipal ctee., Lahore, March, 1923; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1926; registrar, high ct., Lahore, and addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1927; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, March, 1932; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, May, 1934; legal remembrancer and secty., legis. dept., Feb., 1937; again, Oct., 1939; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1938; spec. enq. offr., July, 1940; addtl. judge, high ct., Lahore, Sept., 1940; judge, ditto, July, 1941; retd., Oct., 1946.

BEDDEKAR, GOPAL VAMAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 10th Oct., 1908).—Educ. at the High Sch., Miraj, Bombay Univ., Battersea Polytechnic, London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

BEDFORD, JAMES DOUGLAS HARDY, C.S.I., late Indian Service of Engrs., Punjab (b. 1st June, 1884).—Retd., June, 1939.*

BEDI, BABA DAYA SINGH, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Jan., 1899).—First commn., 14th July, 1921; in milty. employ to Sept., 1928; asst. pol. agent in southern states and Central India, Sept., 1931; under secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in the states of Western India, March, 1932; under-secy. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Oct., 1933; asst. commr., Thal, Sept., 1935; ditto, Kohat, Apl., 1937; under secty. to A.G.G., Resident and Chief Commr., Baluchistan, Dec., 1937; secty., ditto, June, 1938; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1938; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, March,

1939; pol. agent, Loralai, Aprl., 1939; dep. commr., Hazara, Jan., 1942; pol. agt., Kurram, Sept., 1944; dep. commr. Hazara, Aprl., 1944; pol. agt., Kurram, Sept., 1944; ditto, Orissa states, Aprl., 1946.

BEDI, SHEO SINGH (see Sheo Singh Bedi).

BEDI, TARLOCHAN DAS, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 25th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1936; ditto, sub perm't., Nov., 1939.

BEESON, CYRIL FREDERICK CHANINGTON, C.I.E., M.A., D.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 10th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1911; asst. consvr., Nov., 1912; dep. cons. Nov., 1916; forest zoologist, July, 1920; forest entomologist, Dec., 1926; offg. consvr., variously from March, 1931; confd., Dec., 1936; offg. inspr.-genl. of forests and presd't., forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Aug., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1941; ret'd., Dec., 1943.

BEEVOR, RALPH BRANTHWAYT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 29th Aprl., 1895).—Educ. at Felsted and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1919; arrived, 1st Dec., 1919, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1927; confd., Nov., 1932; confd. in sel. grade, May, 1943; acting judge, high ct., Patna, Nov., 1943; offg. addtl. ditto, March, 1944.

BEG, SULTAN AHMAD, M.A., LL.B., (mag. and collr.) (United Provs.) (b. 16th March, 1893).—Joined the U.P. civil (exec.) service, 1st Oct., 1917; mag. and collr. (confd.) March, 1944.

BEHARI LAL, UPPAL, LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 19th Aprl., 1888).—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1910; asst. engr., Sept., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Aprl., 1933; ret'd., 31st May, 1943.

BELCHER, RONALD HARRY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 5th Jan., 1916).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital, Jesus Coll., Cambridge and Brasenose Coll., Oxford, appointed on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; a.r.p. offr., Sept., 1941; sub. divl. offr.

BELHAVEN and STENTON, LORD, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.).—Ret'd., March, 1923.*

BELL, CYRIL FRANCIS, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service, Central Provs. (b. 23rd Feb., 1883).—Ret'd., Feb., 1938.*

BELL, SIR ERNEST ALBERT SEYMOUR, Kt., C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., F.C.H., late Railway Dept. —Ret'd., Oct., 1926.*

BELL, FRANK OWEN, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep., collr. Bengal) (b. 2nd June, 1907).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1931; settlem't. offr., from Oct., 1936; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

BELL, JAMES ALAN, M.C., Madras Judl. Dept.—Appt'd. judge, high ct., Madras, Jan., 1942.†

BELL, JOHN ALTON, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 27th July, 1887).—Ret'd. Aug., 1941.

BELL, JOHN MICHAEL GEOFFREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b.

17th Aprl., 1913).—Educ. at Winchester, Marlborough Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Jan., 1938; arrived in India, Feb., 1938; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1940; mag. and collr., Aug., 1944; dep. sec. civ. supplies, Dec., 1944.

BELL, SIR ROBERT DUNCAN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S., Bombay (b. 18th May, 1878).—Ret'd., Dec., 1937.*

BELL, ROGER CECIL SUTHERLAND, Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 4th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Fettes, Worcester Coll., Oxford and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., March, 1931; offg. under secty. to govt., Madras, public dept., June, 1934; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from Aprl., 1936; collr. and dist. mag., Madras.

BEMROSE, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 21st June, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 23rd Nov., 1925; offg. addl. supt., Aprl., 1932; confd., supt., Aprl., 1938; asst. inspr. genl., March, 1941, to Jan., 1942.

BENCE-JONES, PHILIP REGINALD, M.A., M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I.M.E. (b. 12th Jan., 1897).—Appt'd. princpl., Maclagan engrg. coll., 29th Mar., 1934; on milty. serv., March, 1941, to Oct., 1944.

BENEGAL, SANJIVA RAO (see Sanjiva Rao, B.).

BENJAMIN, HERBERT DANIEL, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 4th March, 1899).—Educ. at Monmouth Gr. Sch., and Oxford (Jesus); joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; and sub. divl. officer, June, 1924; under-secty. to govt. of Bengal, finl., etc., dept., May, 1927; offg. dep. secty. to ditto, March, 1929; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1930; confd. March, 1935; offg. secty. to govt., Bengal, legisl. dept., March, 1935; attd. legisl. dept., govt. of India, May, 1936; special offr., fin. dept., govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1936; secty., legis. dept., govt. of Bengal, May, 1937; addl. secty. and draftsman, govt. of India, legis. dept., Dec., 1938; jt. secty. and addl. draftsman, ditto, Sept., 1939; secty. legis. dept., govt. of Bengal, Sept., 1940; addl. dep. sec., govt. of India legis. dept., Jan., 1941; addl. sec. and draftsman, ditto, Mar., 1945.

BENN, RT. HON. W. WEDGWOOD, D.S.O., D.F.C. (see Stansgate, Viscount).

BENNET, SIR EDWARD, B.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (ret'd.) (b. 26th Oct., 1880).—Ret'd. Oct., 1940.†

BENNETT, MAJOR CHRISTOPHER MACAULAY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr. Bihar) (b. 15th Feb., 1903).—Joined the service as exec. engr., Madras, 1st Oct., 1926; offg. exec. engr. March, 1933; confd. Oct. 1935; ex. engr., P.W.D., Bihar.

BENNET, ERIC, M.A., Indian C.S. (member board of revenue, Madras) (b. 20th Nov., 1922).—Educ. at Berkhamstead and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 7th March 1921; arrived 29th April 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1923; spl. asst. settlt. offr. Oct., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1928; ditto (subs.), Sept., 1935; member board of revenue, Madras, 1946.

BENNETT, REV. FRANK, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estab. (United Provs.) (chaplain on probn.) (b. 6th Nov., 1901).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Feb., 1939.

BENNETT, HERBERT JOHN, I.S.O., Office of High Commr. for India (princpl. exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 30th June 1881).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd cl., India Store Depot, 6th Apl., 1901; trsfld. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; supervisor, India Store Dept., Apl., 1921; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1922; princpl., ditto, May, 1938; I.S.O., June, 1946.

BENNETT, HUGH KENNETH, M.B.E. (b. 13th Apl., 1888).—Joined Mint Service as asst. engr., 1st March, 1910; head engr., 6th Dec., 1933; dy. mint master (offg. temp.), Jan., 1941; M.B.E., 12th June, 1941.

BENNETT, JOHN REGINALD WILLIAM, B.A., (Oxon), Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Allahabad) (b. 18th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at King's Sch., Warwick, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., July, 1917; under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1919, to March, 1921; offg. dep. sec. to govt., judl. dept., Oct., 1923; sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1927; and again, Nov., 1933; judl. secty. to govt., U.P., July, 1931; addtl. judge, ch. ct., Oudh, July, 1940; judge, ditto, Oct., 1940; judge, high ct., Allahabad, Oct. 1944.

BENNETT, SIR JOHN THORNE MASEY, C.I.E., C.B.E., M.C., Ind. Police (inspr.-genl., Punjab) (b. 7th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1914; asst. supt., Jan., 1919; supt., July, 1924; C.B.E., June, 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl. variously from Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1939; inspr.-genl. and jt. sec. to govt., Punjab, Jan., 1940; dep. inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Feb., 1940; again Jan., 1943; awarded Indian Police Medal, Sept., 1940; inspr.-genl. and jt. sec. to govt., Oct. 1945; kt., Jan., 1946.

BENNISON, JOHN JENNINGS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th June, 1892).—Educ. at Middlebrough High Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; appid. after exam. of 1915; arrived in India, Dec., 1916 and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from July, 1918, to July, 1919; offg. dep. commr., and jt. regr., co-op. societies, Nov., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1925; confd., June, 1930; supt., census operations, Burma, Apl., 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1944.

BENSON, WILFRED JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 27th July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935; under secty., p.w.d. (irrgn.), Apl., 1938; on mil. serv. from Oct., 1941.

BENTHALL, SIR EDWARD CHARLES, K.C.S.I. (b. 26th Nov., 1893).—Govr. Imp. Bank of India, 1928-30; delegate, round table conf., London, 1931-32; served in Ind. Army retrenchment cttee., 1931; member, Council of State, 1932-33; member, Bengal legis. assembly, 1934-35; ditto, Council, 1937-38; dir., reserve bank of India, 1935-36; under home govt., 1940-42; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. cl. (war transp. and rlys), July, 1942 to July, 1946; K.C.S.I., June, 1945.

BENTON, GORDON WILLIAM, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Central Provs.) (b. 25th Mar., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Nov., 1912; dist. supt., Sept., 1924; dep. dir. genl., police, Hyderabad State, Feb., 1929;

and again Nov., 1932; offr. on spl. duty under for. and pol. dept., Aug., 1936; attd. for service with dir., intel. bureau, Oct., 1936; ditto, with rly. bd., Oct., 1937; on special duty, Kolhapur Residency, Apl. to June, 1939; confd. dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1944; C.I.E., June, 1946.

BENTON, WILLIAM STEPHEN, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 21st Jan., 1893).—Asst. engr., 27th Dec., 1919; exec. engr. (prov.), Nov., 1935; confd., March, 1938; dir., civ. engrg., rly. bd., Apl., 1944.

BERG, CLEMENT LEONARD, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 1st Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Sept., 1927; confd., Oct., 1930; deputed to mil. engrg. serv., Karachi, Oct., 1937, to Apl., 1938; served under cent. govt., July, 1941 to June, 1943; on depn., ex.-India.

BERRINGTON, ARTHUR DAVIES, Lieut.-Commdr., R.N.R. (ret'd.) (Dep. Port Consvr., Madras) (b. 26th Nov., 1895).—Apptd. harbour-master, 2nd Aug., 1921; dep. port consvr., Sept., 1930; on mil. duty, Aug., 1943 to Feb., 1946.

BERRINGTON, PETER DAVIES, Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 21st Nov., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

BERRY, WILLIAM, M.B.E., late Public Works Dept., Punjab (b. 30th Mar., 1889).—Appt. prfr. Maclagan Engrg. Coll., Lahore, 10th Oct., 1924; ret'd. and re-empld., 20th March, 1944 to Oct., 1946; M.B.E., June, 1945.

BERTRAM, THOMAS, O.B.E., I.S.O. (b. 18th Sept., 1884).—Supt., viceregal estates, Feb., 1921; ret'd., Sept., 1939.

BERY, HARBANS LAL, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 9th Dec., 1912).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 30th Jan., 1936; confd., 1938.

BEST, KENNETH HENRY, Indian Police (Sind) (b. 20th March, 1918).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1937; confd. as asst. supt., Jan., 1940 offg. var. from May, 1942; asst. commdt., Sind Police Rifles, Dec., 1945.

BESTALL, FREDERICK GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 22nd July, 1910).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 3rd Dec., 1929; asst. commr. of police, Rangoon, Feb., 1940; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945.

BETHAM, SIR GEOFFREY LAWRENCE, K.B.E., C.I.E., M.C., Lieut-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) late Indian Political Service (b. 8th Apl., 1889).—First commn., dated 20th Jan., 1909; asst. commr., May, 1919; asst. pol. agent, Mekran, March, 1924; pol. agent, Chagai, Jan., 1925; ditto, Sibi, July, 1927; ditto, Zhob, March, 1929; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Apl., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; rescdt., Mewar, March, 1935; H.B.M.'s minister, Nepal, Apl., 1938; ret'd., April, 1944; K.B.E., 1944.

BETT, JOHN RAYMOND, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 12th Nov., 1901).—Educ. at Wintringham Secondary Sch., Grimsby, Univ. Coll., London, and St. Edmunds Hall, Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., variously from Apl., 1936; sec. home dept., Apl., 1944.

BETTERTON, FREDERICK ARCHIBALD, late Indian Service of Engrs., Bihar (b. 29th Dec., 1882).—Ret'd., Jan., 1938.*

BEVAN, GILBERT JOHN BECKFORD, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent, Burma Rlys.) (b. 19th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as asst.

engr. (temp.), 6th Jan., 1910; ditto (perm.), July, 1911; on milty. duty from 27th Sept., 1916, to 19th Sept., 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; dist. engr., Feb., 1921; offg. dep. agent, Apl., 1933; confd., March, 1934.

BEVAN, WILLIAM JOHN, Indian State Ry's., Coal Dept. (elec. and mech. engr.) (b. 28th Aug., 1893).—Joined service, Apl., 1928; mech. engr., Apl., 1929; elec. and mech. engr., Jan., 1937.

BEVERIDGE, ERNEST JOHN, Indian Police (b. 14th Nov., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 26th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; asst. supt., Deoli deten. jail, Jan., 1935.

BEWERS, DUDLEY FRANCIS, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, education dept.) (b. 28th Sept., 1893).—Apptd. clerk to local adviser to Indian Students, London, 25th Aug., 1919; transfed. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; clerical officer, Apl., 1922; higher clerical officer, Apl., 1925.

BEWOOR, SIR GURUNATH VENKATESH, K.C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 20th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at schools in India, Bombay Univ., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived 27th Nov., 1912, served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. under sec. to ch. commr., Mar., 1919; offg. postmr. gen., Bihar and Orissa, July, 1922; confd. as postmr.-gen., July, 1924; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1924; confd., June, 1925; dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., Nov., 1927; delegate to internal Univ. Postal Congress, London, March, 1929; P.M.G., Bombay, Oct., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1932; dir.-genl., posts and tels., March, 1934; member, Council of State, March, 1936; kt., June, 1939; addl. sec., govt. of India (def.), Aug., 1941; sec. ditto (posts and air) July, 1942; del. commonwealth commns. council, Australia, Oct., 1942; commonwealth civ. aviation conf., Montreal, Oct., 1944; intl. civ. aviation conf., Chicago, Nov., 1944; comm'th air transpt. council, London, Jul., 1945; telecomms. conf. Bermuda, Nov., 1945; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1946; member govr.-genl.'s council, July to Sept., 1946.

BEYNON, ALBERT CYRIL, M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S., Burma (b. 28th Aug., 1896).—Retd., March, 1938,* and re-empld., serving as dep. sec. govt., of India (war), Nov., 1944; jt. sec. to govt. of India, war dept., Jan. to Mar., 1946.

BHABHA, COOVERJI HORMUSJI, (b. 1911). Works Mines and Power, member of Governor-General's Council, Sept., 1946.

BHADKAMKAR, BALAKRISHNA VASUDEO, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 28th Aug., 1893).—Educ. at Bombay and University Coll., London; arrived, 23rd March, 1919, and posted to Burma; transferred to United Provs as asst. mag. and collr., July, 1920; jt. mag., July, 1920; offg. mag. and collr. March, 1926; confd., May, 1932; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1932; excise commr., United Provs., May, 1938.

BHADRAN, CHAKAPANY AIYENGAR RAMA, M.B.E., M.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 24th Sept., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 15th Nov., 1932; dep. consvr., Nov. 1941; M.B.E., June, 1943; dir. Indian Forest Coll., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1945.

BHAGAT SINGH, M.A., late India Posts and Teles. (b. 30th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

BHAGAT, P. G., M.B.E., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., (b. 19th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as inspr. Indian stores dept., 27th Dec., 1927; asst. contr.

of purchase ditto, Oct., 1936; asst. dir., ditto, July, 1939; dep. contr. of purchase, supply dept., Feb., 1942; contr. ditto, April, 1943; offr. on spl. duty (contracts) June to Sept., 1945; again contr., Oct., 1945; offr. on spl. duty, lab. dept., Feb., 1946; contr. of printing and stationery, April, 1946.

BHAGWATI, NATWARLAL HARILAL, Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 7th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. addl. judge, high ct., Bombay, Aug., 1944; puisne judge high ct., Feb., 1946.

BHAGWATI PRASAD, M.Sc., (b. 31st July, 1894).—Joined U.P. civil service, 8th April, 1918; mag. and collr. (confd.), Jan., 1946.

BHAGWATI PRASAD SAKSENA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 17th July, 1899).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1932; apprentice engr., Sept., 1924; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1925; offg. exec. engr., March, 1929; confd., Oct., 1934.

BHALJA, GOVARDHAN SHANKERLAL, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (addl. sec., govt. of India, war) (b. 8th Aug., 1895).—Educ. at Bombay Univ., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1920; arrived 8th Jan., 1921, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925, and Nov., 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; dir. of industries and regr. of co-op. societies, Aug., 1933; sec. to local self govt., etc., depts., Apl., 1937; finl. sec. to govt., and sec., p.w. dept., Nov., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1943; addl. sec., govt. of India (war), Nov., 1944.

BHALLA, T. P. (see Tejasvi Prasad Bhalla). BHANDARKAR, KASHINATH YESHWANT (asst. solr.) (b. Oct. 1902).—2nd cl. sub-judge, variously from Nov. 1927, to Oct. 1936; P.A. to rembr. legal affairs, Bombay, March, 1933; asst. sec. Bombay Govt. legal dept., Oct. 1933; asst. solr. Govt. of India, legis. dept. Apl., 1938.

BHANDARKAR, VAMAN PRABHAKAR, O.B.E., Indian State Ry's. (genl. mgr., B and A rly.) (b. 18th April, 1892).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1914; dist. ditto, Dec., 1936; cent. publicity offr., E.I.R., May, 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1946; genl. mgr. B. and A. rly., April., 1946.

BHANDARI, AMAR NATH, B.A., Indian C.S. dist. and sess. judge Punjab (b. 21st Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Oxford Univ. (non-collegiate); joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 9th Dec., 1924, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1929; and again, Oct., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1933; confd., July, 1934; attd. govt. of India, legisl. dept., Nov., 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Delhi, Sept., 1936; dep. legal remembrancer and dep.-sec., legis. dept., Punjab, June, 1939; addtl. judge, high ct., Lahore and again Sept., 1946.

BHANDARI, JAIGOPAL, C.I.E., M.A., late India Finance Dept. (b. 14th Dec., 1882).—Retd., March, 1938,* and re-empld. on contract for 5 yrs. from Nov., 1939.

BHANDARI, MADAN GOPAL, C.I.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bombay (b. 3rd Jan., 1892).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ to 21st Oct., 1924; addl. supt., Oct., 1924; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Sept., 1927; supt., Borstal Institute, Lahore, Apl., 1928, to Dec., 1928; ditto, Central prison, Bombay, Apl., 1933; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Bombay, Feb., 1936; confd., July, 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

BHANDARI, PRAKASH NATH, (under sec. commerce dept.).—Joined the service 24th Apl.,

1940; asst. acct.-genl. (Cl. II) I.A. and A.S. (confd.) Apl., 1941.

BHANDARI, RAM NATH, M.D., Ch.B., Major, I.M.S., Jail Dept., United Provs. (b. 15th Aug., 1892).—Apppt. supt., central prison, Bareilly, 5th Jan., 1929; ditto, Allahabad (Naini), March, 1937.

BHANOT, HARNAM DAS, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. Punjab) (b. 6th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 9th Jan., 1922, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; dep. commr. (prov.), May, 1928; ditto (subs. perm.), Dec., 1931; senr. secty. to finl. commrs., and dep. secty. to govt., Punjab, dev. dept., July, 1934; secty., ditto, fin. dept., Sept., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; commr., March, 1942; ch. sec. to govt. May, 1945; C.S.I., Jan., 1946.

BHANSALI, MANSEN DAMODAR, M.A., LL.B., Barr., Indian C.S. (sec., govt. of Bombay, gen. and education dept.) (b. 7th Nov., 1898).—Educ. at Bombay and Dulwich Coll., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. in 1923; arrived in India and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte., Dec., 1923; offg. dep. sec. to govt., finance dept., Jan., 1931; confd., Nov., 1931; member, legisl. council, Bombay, 1932; offg. regr. of co-op. societies, Bombay, July, 1936; regr. ditto, Dec., 1938; apptd. ex-officio jt. secty. to Govt. of Bombay, revenue dept., and regr., co-ops. and dir. of rural development, Sept., 1939; dep. sec., govt. of India, communications dept., Oct., 1941; sec. govt. of Bombay, gen. and educ. dept., Apl., 1945.

BHAN SINGH, CHANDRA, M.B., B.S., F.R.C.S., P.M.S., prof. of anatomy, medl. coll., Agra (U.P.) (b. 11th Jan., 1900).—Appptd. 6th Mar., 1942.

BHARGAV, LACHHMAN PRASAD (see Lachhman, P.B.).

BHARGAVA, KALKAD PRASAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 15th July, 1908).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Orai, Allahabad Univ., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1937.

BHARGAVA, MAHESH PRASAD (b. 19th Dec., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. research offr. Forest Research Inst., Dehra Dun, 1st Aug., 1923; offcr., in charge paper pulp section, in addn., for various periods, 1925-27; on depn., Nov., 1927; o.i.c. paper pulp section, Mar., 1929; paper prodn. commr., comm., ind., and civ. supplies dept., in addn., Nov., 1942 to Mar., 1944.

BHARGAVA, MURARI LAL, B.Sc., LL.B., A.S.A., Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 1st Aug., 1895).—Appptd., Sept., 1926.

BHARGAVA, RAMESHWAR PRASAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 27th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London. Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1934.

BHARGAVA, VASHISTHA, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 5th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.

and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1934; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1940.

BHARUCHA, JAMASJI CURSETJI, Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (b. 9th July, 1887).—Retd.†

BHARUCHA, JEHANGIR SORAB, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 13th Oct., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 6th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1932; confd., Nov., 1937; sec. and exec. offr., R.T.A., var. from Jan., 1942.

BHARUCHA, KAIKHUSRU BYRAMJI, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bihar (supt.) (b. 19th July, 1889).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ up to 15th March, 1924; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., Jan., 1928.

BHARUCHA, PHIROZSHAH BYRAMJI, D.S.O., O.B.E., M.D. (Lond.), F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (medl. supt., Mayo hosp., Lahore) (b. 21st Feb., 1886).—First commn., 30th July, 1910; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; on mil. duty till Apl., 1921, when he was apptd. prof. of anatomy, King Edward Medical Coll., Lahore; offg. prof. of operative surgery, ditto, Oct., 1921; and again, March, 1926; medl. supt., Mayo hosp., Lahore, Nov., 1935; offg. princpl., King Edward Medl. Coll., Feb., 1936.

BHASKARA, RAO NAYUDU TAMARAPAKAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 10th May, 1896).—Educ. at Presidency Coll., Madras; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub.-coll. and jt. mag., Feb., 1925; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Feb., 1930; confd., May, 1942; retd., Feb., 1946.

BHASKARAIYA, CHATTA, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 27th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 6th March, 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Bombay, July, 1921; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Calcutta, Jan., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., central revs., Calcutta, Feb., 1933; ditto, Bengal, Apl., 1936; examiner, outside audit dept., Bombay, Aug., 1938; ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Delhi, Sept., 1938; financial adviser to the ch. commr., Delhi, Apl., 1939; chief auditor, E.B. Rly., July, 1941; ditto, N.W. Rly., Aug., 1943; ditto, G.I.P. rly., May, 1945; ditto, E.I. and B.N. rly., Aug., 1945; acct. gen. Cl. II. (prov.), Nov., 1945; acct. gen., C.P. and Bewar, July, 1946.

BHASKARAN, PUDIYA VITTIL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Orissa) (b. 18th Nov., 1908).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Madras, 23rd Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1935.

BHAT, MADHUSUDAN DAMODAR, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (municipal commr. for city of Bombay) (b. 4th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Elphinstone Coll., Bombay, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Nov., 1921; arrived, 27th Jan., 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., offg. collr., dist. magte. and pol. agent, May, 1925, and again Aug., 1929; temp. under secty. to govt., fin. dept., Apl., 1929; offg. collr. and pol. agent, Jewhar, offg. collr. and pol. agent, Jath, Feb., 1933; collr., March, 1936; secty. to govt., Sind, home, etc., depts.; member, Sind. rev. trib., June, 1938; municipal commr. for the city of Bombay, Dec., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1945; commr. excise and commr. sales tax (in addn.) May, 1946.

BHATIA, KISHINCHAND MULCHAND, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Dec., 1907).—Appptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 12th Aug., 1931; confd. as exec. engr., Sind, Aug., 1940.

BHATIA, KRISHNA BEHARI, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 15th July, 1907).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad, Royal

Coll. of Science, London, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 7th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. coll. and mag., jt. mag., Apl., 1933; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1936; sec., tariff bd., Apl., 1937, to Apl., 1938; sec. to govt., United Prov. (agric.) Sept., 1942; mag. and collr. (confd.) Oct., 1944; O.B.E., June, 1946.

BHATIA, SOHAN LAL, C.I.E., M.A., M.D., B.Ch. (Cantab.), L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S., F.C.P.S. (Bomb.), M.C., Col., I.M.S. (dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S.) (b. 5th Aug., 1891).—First commn. dated 15th May, 1920; served in milty. dept. prior to 22nd Aug., 1920; offg. profr. of physiology, etc., G.M. Coll., Sept., 1920; confd., Sept., 1923; princpl., G.M. Coll., and supt., J.J. group hosp., Oct., 1937; addl. dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Aug., 1941; dep. ditto, 4th Oct., 1943; member, academic coun. of Univ. of Delhi, Feb., 1944; inspr.-genl., civil hosp. and prisons, June, 1945; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

BHATNAGAR, SIR SHANTI SWARUP, O.B.E., D.Sc., F.R.S., F.R.I.C., F. Inst. P., F.S.C.I. (Hon.) F.N.I., (dir. scien. and indus. research, govt. of India) (b. 21st Feb., 1895).—Prof. of chem. at Benares Hindu Univ., 1921; prof. of phys. chem. and dir. univ. chem. labs., Punjab Univ., Lahore, 1924; dir. sci. and indus. research, govt. of India, 1940.

BHATTACHARJI, DAKSHINA RANJAN, M.Sc., Ph.D., D.Sc., late U.P. Edn. Serv., Cl. I (b. 18th Jan., 1888).—Retd., Aug., 1940.†

BHATTACHARJI, DHARMADAS, B.A., Indian Police (supi., Bengal) (b. 1st March, 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 12th June, 1927; confd., June, 1929; offg. addl. supt., Apl., 1934; offg. supt., Jan., 1936; confd., Aug., 1940; Indian police medal, 1941.

BHATTACHARJI, DURGAGATI, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 1st July, 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 1st Feb., 1936; confd., 1938.

BHATTACHARJI, MAHENDRA KUMAR, B.Sc. B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. Apl., 1889).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1919; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1927; confd., Jan., 1928.

BHATTACHARJI, TARAKESWAR, Raj Bahadur, B.A., Excise and Salt dept., Bengal (dep. commr.) (b. 25th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 10th Apl., 1906; sub-dep. collr., Dec., 1907; supt. of excise and salt, Apl., 1915; pers. asst. to commr. of excise, June, 1928; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1928; confd., March, 1930.

BHATTACHARYA, AMBARNATH (see Ambarnath Bhattacharya).

BHAUMIK, AMULYAR KUMAR, B.Sc. Eng., A.M.I.E.E. (b. 2nd Feb., 1907).—Apptd. Project offr. (elec.) central tech. power bd., June, 1945.

BHAVE, DIVAN BAHADUR MAHADEV ANANT, B.A., LL.M., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 14th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 4th Apl., 1914; asst. judge, Feb., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge variously from Apl., 1934; ret'd., June, 1939; apptd. member, public service commission, Bombay-Sind, June, 1939; Dwan Bahadur, June, 1939.

BHAWANISHANKAR RAO, KHAMBADKONE, O.B.E., B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 5th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as offer., cl. II, May, 1924; junr. dep. fin. advr. mil., fin., Apl., 1935; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1935; revtd. to mil. accts. dep., March, 1941; contr. field. dy. accts., Apl., 1941; O.B.E., June, 1941;

jt. finl. advr., mil. fin., June, 1943 to Oct., 1943; promoted Cl. I, May, 1945.

BHIDE, MAHADEVA VISHNU, B.A., late Indian C.S., Punjab (b. 13th Feb., 1883).—Retd. 5th Feb., 1943.†

BHIDE, MANOHAR RAMCHANDRA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 5th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Nagpur Univ., University Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; regr. co-op societies, June, 1938; under sec., Govt. of India, dept. of supply, Nov., 1941; dep. sec., branch secretariat, New Delhi, Dept. of Supply, Nov., 1942; regr., co-op. societies, and dir., rural reconstr., March, 1944; regr., co-op. societies, March, 1946.

BHIDE, VITHAL SHIVARAM, C.I.E., B.A., LL.B., Barr.-at-Law, Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 23rd Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Miraj High Sch., Bombay Univ., and Cambridge (non-collegiate); apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915 and served in Burma as asst. commr.; transf. to Bombay as asst. collr., Apl., 1919; asst. collr. and personal asst. to collr., Feb., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Apl., 1926; confd., Apl., 1935; offg. regr. co-op. societies, Oct., 1927; secty. to genl. and educational depts., Bombay, Nov., 1938, to May, 1939; secty. rev. dept., Bombay, May, 1939, to Oct., 1941; offg. (1st grade) commr., June, 1942; C.I.E., June, 1946.

BHIM SEN, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Punjab) (b. 26th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1918; asst. engr., Sept., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1927; confd., Apl., 1929; on depn. to Orissa, Apl., 1936; supig. engr. (perm.), Sept., 1943.

BHIMA RAO, D., M.A., M.B.E., Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 15th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as proby. supt. July, 1919; confd., June, 1921; dep. P.M.G., July, 1944; M.B.E. Jan., 1946.

BHOOTHALINGAM, SUBRAHMANYA, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 22nd Feb., 1909).—Educ. at Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931; sub-collr. and jt. mag., May, 1933; spec. asst. settlement offr., Nov., 1934, and Nov., 1935; offr. in charge, agric. audit dept., Reserve Bank of India, Dec., 1938; under sec. govt. of India, dept. of supply, Jan., 1940; dep. ditto, July, 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1945; spl. duty supply dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1945; collr. and dist. mag., May, 1946.

BHORE, DAVID GORDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. sec. govt. of India, war dept.) (b. 28th Oct., 1915).—Educ. at Bromsgrove and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 12th Oct., 1937; confd., 15th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; served in I.A.F.V.R., Nov., 1940 to Dec., 1944; attached govt. of India (war) dept., Dec., 1944; under sec. ditto, Jan., 1945; dep. sec., ditto, Apl., 1946.

BHORE, SIR JOSEPH WILLIAM, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S., Madras (b. 6th Apl., 1879).—Retd., Apl., 1936.*

BHOTE, J.D., K.B. Ind. State Ryls. (b. 21st Dec., 1893).—Joined 18th Feb., 1934; dep. ch. accts. offr., M.S.M. Ry., March, 1945.

BHUTTO, SIR SHAH NAWAL KHAN, G.M.K.,

C.I.E., O.B.E., Member, Bombay-Sind public sev. commn., from Apl., 1937.

BICKERS, MAURICE HENRY TOWNSHEND, Lieut-Col., O.B.E., India Posts and Tele. (Post-master-General) (b. 9th June, 1891).—Joined the service as proby. supt., June, 1913; confd. June, 1915; Expedy. Force, Mesopotamia, Dec. 1917; depn. to Iraq Administration, March, 1921, to June, 1930; depy. Postmr.-General, Sept., 1937; deputation to field svce., Sept., 1941; dir., army postal services, Oct., 1943; O.B.E., June, 1946.

BICKFORD, WILLIAM REGINALD, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 14th Sept., 1911).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under sec., home dept., Apl., 1939; instructor in A.R.P., May, 1941; M.B.E., Jan., 1943; civ. aff. serv., Oct., 1944; sec. def. and ext. affairs dept., 1945-46.

BIGGIE, HENRY JAMES LESLIE, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 12th March, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., on 20th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1927; confd., Nov., 1936; comdt. and dist. supt., Port Blair, Apl., 1944.

BIGGIE, LAWRENCE PATRICK, Indian Police (b. 24th Apl., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Nov., 1936; confd. Apl., 1938.

BIGGS-DAVISON, JOHN ALEC, Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 7th June, 1918).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn. after seln. in June, 1941, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; on depn. to Bengal, Aug., 1943; again asst. commr., Dec., 1944; under settlement training, May, 1945.

BIGSBY, SYDNEY HERBERT, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs., Punjab (b. 26th July, 1885).—Retd., July, 1940.†

BIJAWAT, MUL CHAND (see Mul Chand Bijawat).

BILARAM, RUPCHAND (see Rupchand, B.).

BILL, CHARLES ESTWICK SYDNEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 15th March, 1914).—Educ. at Haileybury and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

BILL, VEN. SYDNEY ALFRED, M.A., Eccles. Dept., United Provs. (Bishop of Lucknow) (b. 21st Feb., 1884).—Apptd. a chaplain on probn., 15th Dec., 1911; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1914; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1924; archdeacon of Lucknow, Nov., 1925, to May, 1937; retd., Aug., 1938; Bishop of Lucknow, Feb., 1939.

BILLCLIFFE, LESLIE, M.B.E., India Office (asst. to advr. on publicity) (b. 25th Feb., 1893).—On mil. service, Aug., 1914 to May, 1919; apptd. clerical offr., June, 1922; resdt. clerk, Oct., 1937, to Feb., 1938; asst. to advr. on publicity, Feb., 1938; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

BILLIMORIA, KAIKHOSRU DHANJISHAH, B.Sc. (b. 21st Sept., 1902).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1924, as dep. supt. of police, Bombay; prom. to Indian Police, as supt., 1st Apl., 1937; supt. police, B.B. and C.I. rly., Feb., 1945.

BILMORIA, RUSTORNE Sorabji, M.A., L.L.B., Barr., Bombay Judl. Dept. (offl. assignee).—Apptd. offl. assignee, high ct., Bombay, 1st Dec., 1935.

BINDRA BAN, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 7th July, 1892).—Joined the

service, 1st May, 1913; asst. auditor genl., Jan., 1933; dept. acct.-genl., posts and tels., May, 1936; asst. auditor genl., Nov., 1936; ditto, personnel, Apl., 1938; Rai Bahadur, Jan., 1939; dep. acct. genl., P. & T., Delhi, June, 1940; D.A.G., United Provs., Apr., 1941.

BINGHAM, HON. HUGH TERENCE DE BURGH, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 31st Dec., 1885).—Retd., Dec., 1940.†

BINNEY, ANTHONY LOCKHART, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (b. 18th July, 1890).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1914, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; on mil. service from March, 1915, to Sept., 1919; dep. commr. (prov'l.) August, 1925; confd., May, 1926; with resident, Hyderabad, from 3rd Sept., 1928; dir.-general and sec., rev. dept., Nizam's govt., Aug., 1931; offg. commr., June, 1934; finl. secty. to govt., C.P., Oct., 1935; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Central Provs., Feb., 1936; commr. (prov'l.), Nov., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; commr. confd., Feb., 1939; fin. commr., Jan., 1941; adviser to the govr., Jan., 1944; C.S.I., Jun., 1945; on l.p.r. from Apl., 1946.

BINNS, BERNARD OTTWELL, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. of settlements, etc., Burma) (b. 16th July, 1898).—Educ. at Grammar Sch., Bury, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1927; offg. dep. commr., June, 1930; dep. secty., fin. dept., Dec., 1935; commr. of settlements and record, Burma, Dec., 1936; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; jt. sec., reconstrn. dept., 1944; fin. commr., lands and rural devel., Dec., 1945.

BION, ARNOLD EUSTACE, C.I.E., late Indian Police, Bihar and Orissa (b. 2nd Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 1st Dec., 1910; supt., June, 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1934; and again, Apl., 1936; confd., Dec., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1942; inspr.-gen., Bihar, July, 1940; retd., 23rd Oct., 1944.

BIRD, RICHARD ERNEST ROWLAND, Capt., I.A. (b. 5th March, 1912), apptd. to I.P.S. from mil. employ on probn., Feb., 1938; confd. in I.P.S., May, 1944; pol. offr., Trucial Coast, Oct., 1944; asst. pol. agt., Bahrein, Apl., 1945; pol. agt., Kuwait, Mar., 1946.

BIRDWOOD, BARON (SIR WILLIAM RIDDELL BIRDWOOD), G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.V.O., C.I.E., D.S.O., L.L.D., Field Marshal, I.A. (b. 13th Sept., 1865).—C.-in-C. in India and member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Aug., 1925, to Nov., 1930.*

BISCOE, DUDLEY HUGH, Maj., M.B.E., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 12th July, 1909).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1929; pers. asst. to A. to G.G., Gujarat States and redt. at Baroda, Dec., 1934; ditto, Cent. India, March, 1935; under trng. in U.P., June and Oct., 1935; asst. claims commr., Quetta, July, 1935; under secty. to A.G.G. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, June, 1937; confd., Feb., 1938; under-secty. and pers. asst. to A.G.G., resdt. and ch. commr., in Baluchistan, Apl., 1938; special duty in Mysore, Oct., 1939; asst. pol. agent, Nov., 1940, to Apl., 1943; M.B.E., June, 1943; pol. agt., Chhattisgath States, Feb., 1944; asst. pol. agt., Punjab Hill States, Nov., 1945; pol. agt. ditto, March, 1946; sec. to resident, ditto, April, 1946.

BISCOE, WALTER TREWEEK, C.I.E., Indian

State Rlys., (ch. optg. supt.) (b. 8th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 11th July, 1910; on milty. duty from 6th Oct., 1914, to 28th Oct., 1918; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; dist. traffic supt., Dec., 1925; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., June, 1928; resd. mtanager, Indian rly. publicity bureau, New York, Dec., 1930; divl. comml. manager, N.W. Rly., Feb., 1936; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager, May, 1936; divl. supdt. (junior), July, 1938; ditto, senr., Oct., 1939; dep. chief comml. manager, provl., Apl., 1940; permanent Sept., 1941; ch. optg. supt., (confd.) Apl., 1945; C.I.E., June, 1946.

BISHUMBHAR DAYAL, M.A., Indian Police (b. 29th July, 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 10th Jan., 1930; confd., Jan., 1932.

BISHEN CHAND, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1st Oct., 1884).—Retd. Oct., 1939.*

BISHOP, JOHN EADIE, Indian Police (b. 15th Dec., 1917).—Asst. supt., Bihar, apptd. on probn., Dec., 1937; confd., Apl., 1939.

BISHOP, LAURENCE ARTHUR, O.B.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 3rd Feb., 1895).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1914; asst. supt., Jan., 1920; on milty. duty from 4th Aug., 1916, to 2nd Jan., 1919; offg. supt., Nov., 1924; confd., Feb., 1930; on foreign service as commr. of police, Travancore, Aug., 1928; reverted to Brit. service, Nov., 1932; confd. as D.I.G., July, 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; confd. as inspr.-genl., Aug., 1944.

BISHOP, THEODORE BENDYSH WATSON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 13th Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Rugby, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; pte. sec. to the lieut.-govr., United Provs., May to July, 1916; jt. mag., Dec., 1916; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1924; mag. and collr. (prov. sub.), Jan., 1927; confd., July, 1927; dep. commr., Nov., 1927; magte. and collr. again, Nov., 1932; commr., Aug., 1939; confd., Nov., 1942; advr. to the govr., Dec., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1946; retd., Nov. 1946.

BISHWA NATH LAHIRI, O.B.E., M.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 13th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs. 9th Aug., 1922; offg. supt., Dec., 1925; confd., Dec., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1946.

BISSETT, ALLAN, India Audit Office (asst. to auditor) (b. 4th March, 1895).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 5th Aug., 1914; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1926; suptg. exec. officer, Oct., 1934; asst. to auditor, Oct., 1943.

BISWAS, CHARU CHANDRA, C.I.E., Judl. Dept., Bengal, offg. judge, Calcutta high ct., from March, 1937, judge, Feb., 1940.

BISWAS, SARAT CHANDRA, Indian State Rlys. (axec. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 1st June, 1897).—Joined service Jan., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1924; exec. engr. (provl.), Nov., 1938.

BISWAS, SATISH CHANDRA, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Indian State Rlys. (dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 12th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as dist. surg., 13th Apl., 1918; dist. medl. officer, Apl., 1929.

BISWAS, SATISH CHANDRA, Rai Bahadur, M.B.E., (b. 23rd Oct., 1884).—Retd., Mar. 1940.†

BIVAR, HUGH GODFREY STUART, Indian C.S.,

A.I.R.O., (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 28th Nov., 1897).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford (Classical Exhibitioner); joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-divl. officer, Aug., 1923; addl. presdy. mag., Apl., 1926; addl. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1928; offg. ditto, Nov., 1931; confd., Feb., 1934; on war service, May, 1940.

BLACK, D. G. LYALL, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 4th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 9th July, 1920; offg. supt., Apl., 1923; confd., Aug., 1927.

BLACKALLER, REV. DAVID WILLIAM, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 31st July, 1913).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 27th Oct., 1942.

BLACKBURN, CAPT. C. W.—Joined the service as spec. duty offr., home dept., 9th Mar., 1946.

BLACKBURN, JOHN INGAMELLS, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 24th Nov., 1910).—Educ. at County High Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr.; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1943.

BLAGDEN, JOHN BASIL (b. 8th Oct., 1901).—Judge, high ct., Rangoon, Feb., 1940; ditto, Bombay, July, 1944.

BLAIR, FRANCIS RITCHIE, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service, Bihar (b. 10th Apl., 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1938.*

BLAIR, JAMES RICHARD, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 20th August, 1890).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's College, Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service from March, 1915, to Feb., 1919; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Feb., 1920; confd., March, 1921; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1922; mag. and collr., Nov., 1926; dep. secty., pol. and appt. dept., Apl., 1927; dep. secty. to govt. of India, army dept., June, 1930; offg. addl. secty., pol. dept., Bengal, Feb., 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; secy., communications and works dept., Bengal, Oct., 1938; ch. sec. to Govt., (confd.), Apl., 1942; retd., Jan., 1946.

BLAKE, EDGAR VALENTINE MYLES, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Dec., 1903).—Retd., Feb., 1940.

BLAKE, REGINALD GILBERT, India Office (executive officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 20th Feb., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 3rd Dec., 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct., 1915; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; acting hr. exec. offr., July, 1939.

BLANFORD, HARRY RICHARD, O.B.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 15th June, 1884).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

BLANK, ABRAHAM LEWIS, M.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 19th May, 1891).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914, arrived in India, Nov., 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Nov., 1919; jt. mag. and dy. collr., Oct., 1920; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1923; affg. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Nov., 1925; confd., Nov., 1926; commr. Assam, Nov., 1925; confd., Nov., 1926; commr. for workmen's compn., March, 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Assam, legisl. dept., March,

1935; confd., Nov., 1935; secty., judicial dept. and legal remembrancer. Bengal, May, 1939; judge, Calcutta high ct., Feb., 1942.

BLAZEY, N. C., Burma Engrg. Serv. (b. 10th June, 1897).—Apptd. asst. elec. and mech. engr. June, 1923; civ. affairs service, June, 1945.

BLAQUIERE, NORMAN ARTHUR, Burma Frontier Service, (b. 18th Apl., 1895).—Apptd. 22nd Feb., 1927.

BLEASE, REV. RUPERT GORDON, B.Sc., Indian Eccles. Estab. (junr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 12th Oct., 1896).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 11th Dec., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1929.

BLENCH, THOMAS, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Nov., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 15th Oct., 1927; exec., engr., Oct., 1936; on mil. duty to Dec., 1944; under sec. to govt., p.w.d. (irrgn.) Jan., 1945; on depn. to U.S.A. from July, 1945.

BLEWITT, ALBERT ERNEST, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 2nd Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 15th June, 1920; offg. supt., June, 1924; and again, Feb., 1930; confd., Aug., 1932.

BLOMFIELD, DOUGLAS JOHN, C.I.E., A.C.G.I., late Indian Service of Engrs., Bengal (b. 20th Dec., 1885).—Retd., Dec., 1940.†

BLUMENTHAL, SIMON, late India Audit Office (b. 2nd Dec., 1881).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India Audit Office, 25th Apl., 1901; minor staff clerk, Oct., 1919; staff clerk, Feb., 1922; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1922; suptg. ditto, Aug., 1933; retd., Jan., 1944.

BLUNDEN, ALFRED CHARLES, Indian Police (cent. intell. offr., United Provs.) (b. 18th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Dec., 1891; supt., Aug., 1924; addl. asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Jan., 1931; ditto, spec. bch., Apl., 1936; cent. intell. offr., U.P., Nov., 1937; confd. as dep. inspr. genl., March, 1942.

BLUNT, ALFRED DICKINSON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Nov., 1883).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

BO, U, Indian Police (dist. supt. Burma) (b. 13th Aug., 1911).—Apptd. asst. supt., on probn., 21st Jan., 1936; civ. aff. serv. Oct., 1945; dist. supt., 1946.

BOAG, SIR GEORGE TOWNSEND, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 12th Nov., 1884).—Retd., Nov., 1943.†

BOKHARI, A. S., C.I.E., M.A. (Cantab.), All-India Radio (contr. of broadcasting) (b. 1st Oct., 1898).—Apptd. station dir., All-India Radio, March, 1936; dep. contr. of broadcasting June, 1936; contr. of broadcasting, Apl., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1944.

BOLAM, DOUGLAS HAIG, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 10th Oct., 1916).—Educ. at Whitgift Sch., Croydon, and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 7th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; pers. asst. to commr., July, 1940; on famine duty, May, 1941; sub. divl. offr., Nov., 1941; pol. asst. and comdt., Border mil. police, May, 1942; asst. dir., food supplies, 1944.

BOLAM, JOHN WALLACE, Indian Police (b. 30th May, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 17th Nov., 1926; asst. to A.G.G. in C., India, in criminal bch., March, 1933; supt. of police, Benares State, Feb., 1934; supt., provl. sub., March, 1940; confd., June, 1940.

BOLDEY, TIGRANE DAVID, P.W. Dept., Burma (div. elec. engr.) (b. 16th June, 1896).—Joined the service as temp. engr., March, 1927; div. elec. engr. Apl., 1937; on depn. in U.K., Sept., 1944 to March, 1946.

BOLTON, SIR HORATIO NORMAN, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1875).—Retd., Oct., 1931.*

BOLTON, REGINALD RAINSBURY, M.C., B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Nov., 1896).—(exec. engr., Burma).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1927; confd., Oct., 1929; admin. offr., P.W.D., Simla, Oct., 1942.

BOMFORD, GUY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 28th June, 1899).—First commn. dated 28th Feb., 1917; in milty. employ to 24th Aug., 1921; proby. asst. supt., Aug., 1921; confd., Oct., 1925; supt., Aug., 1929; on depn. for demarcation of the Perso-Baluch Frontier, Feb. to May, 1932; in mil. employ, Aug., 1941.

BONAR, ROBERT, A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. mech. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 10th Apl., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. works manager, 1st Apl., 1927; production engr., Sept., 1928; works manager, Oct., 1930; offg. divl. supt., mechl., E.I. Rly., Sept., 1935; dep. chief mechl. engr.; confd., Sept., 1937; chief ditto, provl., Nov., 1940.

BONARJEE, DENYS IVAN, (b. 2nd May, 1910).—Apptd. to office of high commr. for India, Feb., 1936; genl. asst. to. H.C., May, 1946.

BONARJEE, NEIL BRUNIAT, Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., United Provs.) (b. 10th March, 1901).—Educ. at Dulwich and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1925; arrived, 9th Dec., 1925; and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr.; jr. magte., March, 1927; offg. dep. commr., March, 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1934; offer. on spec. duty Imp. Council Agric. Research, Nov., 1936; ditto, dept. of e.h. and l., Nov., 1937; collr. and magte., Jan., 1939; sec. to govt., U.P., local self govt., etc., March, 1944.

BOON, GEORGE ALAN JAMES, Indian Police (b. 3rd Oct., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, United Provs., Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

BOOTH, JOHN REGINALD TREVOR, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 14th Aug., 1883).—Retd., 25th Nov., 1942.†

BOOTH, JOSEPH, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Sind) (b. 14th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood Coll., Co. Kildare, Balliol Coll., Oxford, University Coll., London, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. minister, Khairpur state, May, 1935; collr. and dist. magte. (confd.), Jan., 1940; jt. sec. to govt., Bombay (fin.) and dir. of civ. supplies, July, 1943; O.B.E., June, 1945.
