BOOTH-GRAVELY, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd May, 1882).—Retd., June, 1940.†

BOR, NORMAN LOFTUS, C.I.E., B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (forest botanist) (b. 4th May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 20th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Aug., 1924; pol. offer., Balipura frontier tract, Dec., 1931; bot. for. offer. and silvicult., Assam, Nov., 1936; for. botanist, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1937; admstr. (south), Assam refugee orgn., Oct., 1942; ch.

refugee admstr., Burma refugee orgn., Assam, Dec., 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

BOSE (see also Basu).

BOSE, A. C., Indian Audit and Accts. service.—Apptd. under sec., govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1944; asst. fin. advr. (commerce) Jan., 1946.

BOSE, CONSTANCE HOPE, Office of High Commr. for India (educal. officer, exec. grade, educn. dept.).—Apptd. educal. officer, 14th Apl., 1930.

BOSE, FANINDRA NATH, B.Sc., M.R.S.I., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E.E., Indian State Ryis. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 10th May, 1887).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1914, as junr. engr.; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; asst. exec. engr., E.I. Rly., Nov., 1927; exec. engr. (junr.), March, 1928; ditto (senr.), Oct., 1933.

BOSE, HEMENDRA KUMAR, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 10th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service as probnr., 11th Feb., 1916; asst. acct.-genl., Feb., 1918; dep. acct.-genl., Burma, May, 1929; ditto, Bengal, Sept., 1929; examr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Aug., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., June, 1932; offg. dep. ch. auditor, Burma ryis., Feb., 1933; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Apl., 1934; dep. acct.-genl., United Provs., Jan., 1935; offg. dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Jan., 1935; dep. ch. accounts offr., E.B. Rly., Jan., 1937; ditto, E.I. Rly., Jan., 1939; finl. advr. and ch. accts. offcr., E.B. Rly., June, 1940; acct.-genl., Bengal, Nov., 1940; confd. acct.-genl. (cl. I), Sept., 1944; acct.-genl., posts and tel., May, 1945; on depn. to govt. of Bengal, Apl., 1946.

BOSE, KUMUDINI KANTA, B.S. (Univ. of Illinois), Assoc. A.S.M.E. (contr. of purchase) (b. 1st March, 1887).—Joined the service, 12th July, 1924, as asst. contr. of purchase, Indian Stores Dept.; contr. of purchase, Calcutta Circle, 9th July, 1937; deputy contrl. of purchase (supply), dept. of supply.

BOSE, NABA KUMAR, Diwan Bahadur, B.Sc. Cal. (Glas.), A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Ryis. (asst. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1892).—Asst. dist. loco. supt., Feb., 1918; asst. supt. junior scale, Oct., 1926; asst. supt. senior scale, May, 1934; dep. ch. mech. engr. provl., Oct., 1938; confd., Aug., 1939; ch. mech. engr. (confd.) 1945.

BOSE, SUDHANSU KUMAR, B.Sc., A.R.S.M., Indian Sch. of Mines (b. 7th Oct., 1900).—Apptd. prof. of mining and surveying, 7th Dec., 1927; offg. vice-princl., Indian sch. of mines, May, 1935.

BOSE, SIR SUDHANSU MOHAN, M.A., LL.B. (Cantab.), Barr.-at-Law, apptd. Member, Public Service Commission, Bengal, 13th Apl., 1937; Kt., June, 1946.

BOSE, SHRI SARAT CHANDRA, (b. 1889).—Works, Mines and Power, member of Governor-General's Council Sept., to Oct., 1946.

BOSE, VIVIAN, B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.), Barr. Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (judge, high ct., Nagpur).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936.

BOTTOMLEY, JOHN EDWARD, Indian State Ryis. (G.I.P. rly.) (b. 13th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 13th January, 1920; asst. works manager, G.I.P. Rly., Jan., 1925; works manager, Oct., 1932; dep. ch. mech. engineer, provl., Feb., 1941; on i.p.r. from June, 1946.

BOTTOMLEY, JOHN MELLOR, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Educ. Service (Bengal) (b. 21st March, 1888).—Retd., March, 1943; re-empld., March, 1943.†

BOTTOMS, ALVAN DOUGLAS, India Audit

Office (higher executive officer) (b. 22nd Sept., 1899).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 8th Aug., 1919, after previous service from 25th Apl., 1917; second class clerk, 29th Apl., 1920; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; transf. to India Audit Office, March, 1922; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; higher exec. officer, Oct., 1934; lent to Min. of Wks., Oct., 1941 to Sept., 1946; actg. supt. exec. offr., Jan., 1944 (in absentia).

BOUCHIER, EDWARD WALLACE, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sub collr., Madras) (b. 1st Jan., 1906).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar Sch. and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Sept., 1932; under secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, June, 1934; ditto, home dept., Apl., 1936; ditto, public dept., Sept., 1937, to July, 1938; secty., Central Road Traffic Board, Madras, Nov., 1938; O.B.E., June, 1944; sub. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1945.

BOUCHIER, OWEN PLATT, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 2nd March, 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 14th Apl., 1920; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1925; actg. higher exec. offr., July, 1940.

BOUGHTON, ERIC JAMES, O.B.E., temp. Brig., I.A.—Apptd. offr. on sp. duty, food dept., June, 1946; ch. shipping offr. Aug., 1946.

BOULD, REGINALD PERRY, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 6th Jan., 1884).—Retd., Apl., 1940.†

BOULTON, REV. WALTER, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 11th July, 1901).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Bengal, Jan., 1927; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1939.

BOURDILLON, SIR BERNARD HENRY, K.C.M.G., K.B.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1883).—Transferred to Colonial Service as Colonial sec., Ceylon, 27th Nov., 1929; K.B.E., June, 1931; govr. of Uganda, Nov., 1932; K.C.M.G., Jan., 1934; ret. from Colonial Service, Dec., 1943.†

BOURNE, SIR FREDERICK CHALMERS, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., (Oxon) Indian C.S. (govr. of Central Provs. and Berar) (b. 12th Aug., 1891).—Educ. at Rugby and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Aug., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1926; ditto (perm.), Oct., 1929; senr. secty. to finl. commrs. and dy. secty. to govt., Punjab, development dept., Feb., 1932; secty. to govt., Punjab, elec. and industries dept., July, 1934; to March, 1937; dep. commr., March, 1937; secty. to govt., Punjab, Home Dept., Aug., 1940; ch. sec. to govt., Oct., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; C.S.I., Jan., 1944; actg. govr. of Cent. Provs. and Berar, May, 1945 to Oct., 1945; K.C.S.I., Aug., 1946; govr. of Cent. Provs. and Berar, Sept., 1946.

BOURNE, GERALD COOKE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 23rd Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Gresham's Sch., Holt, Norfolk, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. registrar, high ct., Rangoon, Aug., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1934; regr., high ct., Rangoon, March, 1936; confd. dist. and sessions judge, March, 1938; consular liaison offr. in Persia to Apl., 1944, on spl. duty, govt. of Burma, civ. supplies dept., Dec., 1944; commr., ditto, Nov., 1945.

BOWDEN, JAMES WILLIAM, M.B., B.Ch. (Dub.), D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), Capt., I.M.S. (civil surgeon) (b. 16th Nov., 1902).—Date of first commn., 20th Oct., 1932, granted an antedate of one year; date of rank, 1st May, 1934; civil employ in Orissa, 15th Oct., 1938.

BOWEN, JOHN CHARLES EDWARDS, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 8th Oct., 1909).—First commn., 28th Aug., 1930; pers. asst. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Nov., 1934; under trng. in U.P., June, 1935; again, Oct., 1935; asst. to claims commr., Quetta, July, 1935; under secty. to resdt., Punjab States, Sept., 1937; confd., Sept., 1938; vice-consul, Zohid, Apr., 1939; under sec. to resident for Rajputana and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1941; on deputation, 1941; asst. commr., Nov., 1943; dep. commr. Mardan, Feb., 1944; asst. commr., Mansehra, Mar., 1944; dep. commr. Hazara (addl. charge) Mar., 1944; consul, Bushire, May, 1945.

BOWEN, ROWLAND, Indian Police (b. 27th Feb., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1936; confd. Apr., 1938, on l.p.r.

BOWER, GEORGE NOEL, B.A., late Imperial Customs Service (b. 25th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

BOWER, REV. JAMES ARNOLD HAROLD, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 19th Dec., 1897).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 21st Dec., 1944.

BOWLER, VERNON ROBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 8th Aug., 1889).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 23rd Jan., 1911, and attd. to record and registry dept.; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to March, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apr., 1922; higher clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1926; higher exec. offr., March, 1939; actg. prin. exec. offr., July, 1941.

BOWMAN, ARCHIBALD IAN, M.A., Indian C.S., U.P. (b. 11th May, 1915).—Educ. at Glasgow High Sch. and Glasgow Univ.; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.; on l.p.r. from Jan., 1947.

BOWMAN, JOHN BLAKE, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 26th May, 1917).—Educ. at George Watson's Boys' Coll., Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

BOWN, ARTHUR MERVYN, B.A., M.C., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Aug., 1894).—Retd. 30th Apr., 1940.†

BOWSTEAD, JOHN, C.I.E., M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (chief sec. to govt., Orissa) (b. 16th Feb., 1897).—Educ. at King's Sch., Worcester, and Corpus Christi, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Nov., 1921; arrived, 13th Jan., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-sec., pol. and appt. dept., Apr., 1925; confd., July, 1926; offg. asst. pol. agent, Orissa Feudatory States, Apr., 1928; confd., Oct., 1931; offg. agent, ditto, Feb., 1932; offg. secty. to A.G.G., Eastern States, and pol. agent, Sambalpur, Apr., 1933; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1935; confd., March, 1936; secty. to govt., Orissa, March, 1938; addtl. pol. agent, Orissa States, July, 1939; sec. to govt., Feb., 1940; addtl. sec., govt. of Orissa (home and fin.), July, 1940; offg. ch. sec. to govt. of Orissa, Aug., 1940; again, June, 1942; again, Feb., 1943; also dir.

of civil def., and provl. press advr.; C.I.E., June, 1942; commr., Jan., 1945; ch. sec. pol. and appt. dept., Bihar, Mar., 1945.

BOYCE, PETER MCCONNELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 13th Aug., 1918).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., Wimbledon, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. after seln. in May, 1940, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; spec. offr., commn. dept., June, 1944; regl. grain offer., Oct., 1944; under sec. pol. and appt. dept., Apr., 1945.

BOYD, SIR DONALD JAMES, K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S., Punjab (b. 23rd Dec., 1877).—Retd., Apr., 1937.*

BOYD, THOMAS CRAWFORD, C.I.E., Col., late I.M.S. (b. 26th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1908; in mil. employ up to 7th Aug., 1921; chemical examr. and prof. of chemistry, Aug., 1921; prin. medl. coll., Calcutta, Apr., 1932; offg. surg.-genl., Bengal, Dec., 1934; and again, Oct., 1936; inspr. gen., civil hospitals, United Provs., June, 1939; retd., Aug., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

BOZMAN, GEOFFREY STEPHEN, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (sec. govt. of India, infm. and arts) (b. 26th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Whitgift Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922; spl. asst. settlt. offr., Feb., 1925; under secty. to govt., law dept., Madras, May, 1927; offg. secty., ditto, May, 1928; secty., tariff bd., June, 1930; secty. to agent of govt. of India in South Africa, Oct., 1932; commr. of Coorg, March, 1935; addl. dep. secty. to the Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, March, 1936; dep. secty., educn., health and lands dept., March, 1939; jt. secty., ditto, Apr., 1940; secty., dept. of Indians Overseas, govt. of India, Oct., 1941; secty., infm. and broadcasting, Dec., 1943; on depn. to U.K. and U.S.A., June-Oct., 1944; sec. to Govt. of India, infm. and broadcasting (now arts), Oct., 1944; on depn. to U.K., Dec., 1945 to Feb., 1946; C.S.I., Jan., 1946; sec., dept. of infm. and arts, Feb., 1946.

BRACKEN, SIR GEOFFREY THOMAS HIRST, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S., Madras (b. 11th Apr., 1879).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

BRACKENBURY, SIR CECIL FABIAN, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th March, 1881).—Retd., Sept., 1940.†

BRADBURY, REV. JOHN HENRY, A.K.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Punjab) (b. 8th Oct., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 23rd Oct., 1925; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; senr. ditto, Apr., 1937; retd., Aug., 1946.

BRADFIELD, SIR ERNEST WILLIAM CHARLES, K.C.I.E., O.B.E., Lieut.-Gen., I.M.S., retd. (presdt., medical bd., India Office) (b. 28th May, 1880).—Joined the service, 31st Aug., 1903; prof. of surg., medl. coll., Jan., 1924; offg. supt., gen. hosp., Madras, May, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1928; reverted to milty. employ, Apr., 1931; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Bombay, June, 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; hon. surg. to the King, Dec., 1935; presdt. medl. council of India, Feb., 1937; dir.-genl., I.M.S., March, 1937; member Council of State, Feb., 1937; presdt., medical bd., India Office, Nov., 1939; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

BRADFORD, WALTER GLYNNE, late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 15th Oct., 1890).—Retd., 14th July, 1939.*

BRADLEY, CHARLES ALFRED KEMBLE, late Indian State Railways (E.B. Rly.) (b. 19th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst.

loco. supt., 2nd Nov., 1913; dist. loco. supt. Nov., 1922; offg. dep. loco. supt., March 1930; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. rly., May, 1934; confd., Nov., 1936; offg. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1934; and again, Aprl. 1937; ret'd., June, 1944.

BRADLEY, JOHN BERTRAM GRANVILLE, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (commissioner, Burma) (b. 12th March, 1899).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; sub-divl. officer, June, 1923; offg. under-sec., to govt., fin. and rev. dept., June, 1926; under-secy. to govt., home and pol. dept., Aprl., 1927; dep. registrar, high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1930; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1932; confd., Nov., 1933; registrar, high ct., March, 1939; civ. aff. serv., 1944; commr., Oct., 1945; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1945; under def. and ext. dept., July, 1946; chairman, def. serv. (terms of serv.) ctee., July, 1946.

BRADLEY, ROBERT JOSEPH, Burma Police (Cl. I). (b. 7th Sept., 1920).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug. 1946.

BRADSHAW, ERIC JEAN, B.A., B.A.I., M.Sc., Govt. Survey of India (b. 30th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service 8th Jan., 1923; served in Burma, April, 1937 to May, 1942; supt. (geologist) (confd.) Feb., 1943; transferred to Burma, April, 1946.

BRADSHAW, JOHN RICHARD LONDON, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 8th Nov., 1896).—First commn. dated 16th Dec., 1914; joined the pol. dept., 23rd Oct., 1922; asst. commr., Bannu, March, 1925; asst. pol. agent Chitral, May, 1926; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1928; asst. commr., Charsadda, Jan., 1929; 3rd asst. resdt., Aden, Feb., 1930; secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Dec., 1930; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Jan., 1933; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transf. depts., Jan., 1936; ditto, devel. depts., Aprl., 1937; secty. to resdt. for Rajputana, Aprl., 1938; resdt., Western Rajputana States, Aprl., 1939; civil a.r.p. offr., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1940; dep. commr., Mardan, Apr., 1941; resdt., Waziristan, Oct., 1943; pol. agt., Malwa, Sept., 1944; pol. agt. in Bhopal (addl. charge) May, 1945.

BRADSHAW-SMITH, ROBERT ALFRED, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 5th May, 1891).—Retd. Aprl., 1940.

BRAGANZA, LEOPOLD SCHOLASTICUS, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 10th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 19th Aprl., 1913; temp. engr., G.I.P. Rly., Dec., 1921; resdt. engr., Aprl., 1924; exec. engr., May, 1935; ret'd., Feb., 1943.

BRAHMAWAR, RAJ NARAYAN (see Raj Narayan, B.).

BRAILSFORD, REV. CYRIL, A.K.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1887).—Retd. Feb., 1942.†

BRAMWELL, ERNEST CHARLES, Office of the High Commr. for India (asst. engr., mechanical transport) (b. 16th Jan., 1889).—Apptd. asst. inspr. of stores, March, 1926; asst. engr., mechanical transport, Aug., 1926.

BRANDER, GEORGE MACONACHIE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Edin. Academy and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; colonization offr.,

Dec., 1936; dep. commr., subst. provl., May, 1941; divl. sec. to fin. commrs. and dep. sec. to govt., Oct., 1942; sec. to govr., March, 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

BRANDON, HAROLD FRANK, Office of High Commissioner for India (hr. exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 28th July, 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., India Office, 13th Oct., 1919; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Aprl., 1922; actg. hr. exec. offcr. variously from Nov., 1936; confd., Feb., 1939.

BRANDON, EDWIN HAROLD, M.B.E., late Central P.W.D. (b. 6th Sept., 1885).—Retd., Sept., 1940.†

BRAUND, SIR HENRY BENEDICT LINTHWAITE, Barr.-at-Law, Judl. Dept., India (judge, high ct., Allahabad).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, June, 1934; ditto, Allahabad, Sept., 1939; Kt., June, 1945.

BRAY, SIR DENYS DE SAUMAREZ, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Nov., 1875).—Retd. from I.C.S., May, 1930; adviser to sec. of state for India, Aprl. to July, 1937.*

BRAYDEN, DESMOND ANTHONY, O.B.E., Indian Police (cent. intell. offcr., Bengal and Assam) (b. 28th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 29th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., Aprl., 1926; confd., March, 1932; on depn. under G. of I. Home Dept. Intelligence Bureau, as central intelligence officer, Bengal and Assam, March, 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

BRAYE, PHILLIP GEORGE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 1st Dec., 1894).—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 13th Sept., 1923; arrived, 13th Nov., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1926; confd., Nov., 1932; excise and opium commr. in C.I., Nov., 1934; to Nov., 1936; civil recruiting and liaison offr., C.P., May, 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; offg. fin. commr., Dec., 1945.

BRAYNE, FRANK LUGARD, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.C., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 6th Jan., 1882).—Retd., Nov., 1941.†

BRAYSHAY, SIR MAURICE WILLIAM, Kt., M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Railways (b. 7th March, 1883); ret'd., Oct., 1932; and re-empld.*

BREAKE, ARTHUR WILLIAM HUMPHREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (addl. dep. commr. Burma) (b. 23rd March, 1909).—Educ. at the High Sch., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; appointed after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Dec., 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. supt., Shan States, Dec., 1938; on mil. service, Nov., 1940 to Aug., 1942; civ. aff. serv., 1942; dep. commr. (addl.), Oct., 1945.

BREBNER, SIR ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th Aug., 1883).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

BREE, PHILIP GEORGE, name changed to BRAYE (q.v.).

BREMNER, CLAUDE EDWARD URQUHART, M.C., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Aug., 1891).—First commn. dated 4th March, 1911; in milty. employ. to July, 1919; asst. pol. agent, July, 1919; asst. commr., Dec., 1924; on foreign service in Baroda State from 17th May, 1927; pol. agent, South Waziristan, Aprl., 1928; vice-consul, Ahwaz, May, 1932; pol. agent and consul, Muscat, Nov., 1932; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Aprl., 1936; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Feb., 1937;

offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, June, 1937; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, March, 1938; rev. and iudl. commr., Baluchistan, June, 1938; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, March, 1939; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, July, 1939; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin Oct., 1939; H.B.M. consul in Portuguese possessions in India, Nova Goa, Nov., 1940.

BRENDON, PATRICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr. Punjab) (b. 17th March, 1913).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. col. offr., Nov., 1940; dep. commr., Feb., 1942; offr. on spl. duty, civil supply dept., Oct., 1943; again commr., Dec., 1943.

BRERETON, CHARLES CECIL TRELAWNY, M.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Dec., 1889).—Retd., Aug., 1939.*

BRETT, CYRIL TEMPLETON, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 23rd July, 1885).—Retd., 23rd July, 1940.†

BRETT, WILLIAM BAILIE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Nov., 1889).—Retd., July, 1940.†

BREWITT, CHARLES PATRICK, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 17th March, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 2nd Jan., 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rly., March, 1931; offg. dep. agent, Burma rlys., Apl., 1935.

BRIDGE, MAURICE FREDERICK, M.B.E., B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 14th March, 1893).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

BRIDGNELL, E., Impl. Sect. Service (b. 19th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service 18th July, 1916; asst. commr., cent. excises, Mar., 1938; asst. finl. advr., mil. fin., Aug., 1939; under sec., mil. fin. dept., Sept., 1945.

BRIDGMAN, FRANCIS HARRY GEORGE, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 14th Nov., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; offg. supt., June, 1935; a.d.c. to the Viceroy, Dec., 1938 to Jan., 1941.

BRIDLE, HENRY LEWIS, India Office (actg. hr. exec. officer acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 1st May, 1892).—Apptd. clerical offr., 21st Oct., 1921; higher clerical officer, Apl., 1926; lent to min. of aircraft prodn. June, 1940; actg. hr. exec. offr., April, 1942 (in absentia); rejoined Aug., 1945.

BRIGGS, HERBERT SAMUEL, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 22nd Dec., 1881).—Retd., Jan., 1942.†

BRIGGS, NORMAN, C.I.E., Col., I.M.S., Jail dept., Sind (b. 18th Nov., 1891).—First commn. dated 8th Aug., 1917; in milty. employ to 24th March, 1925; supt., borstal institution, Lahore, Apl., 1925; offg. dep. insp.-genl. of prisons, Punjab, March, 1932; dir. of health and prisons, Sind, Apl., 1936; inspr. genl., civil hospitals, United Provs., Aug., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1946.

BRIJ BHUSAN SARAN JETLY, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 10th July, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 21st July, 1924; offg. supt., Nov., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

BRIJ BHUSHAN SARAN VARMA, B.A., LL.B., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 10th June, 1908).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 25th Jan., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

BRIJ BHUSNAN SINGH, M.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 21st Aug., 1904).—Joined the service 10th Oct., 1929;

arrived, Dec., 3rd 1929 and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr. Dec., 1934; sec. bd. of rev., Nov., 1939; sec. govt. U.P. local self-govt. and public health depts., Feb., 1943.

BRIJ MOHAN LAL, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (supt. engr., Punjab) (b. 13th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1922; offg. exec. engr., May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1931; suptg. engr. (subst.), July, 1944.

BRIJ NARAYAN, B.A., Rai Bahadur, Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., Orissa) (b. 10th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1925; confd., Oct., 1926; under secty. to govt., P.W.D. (b. and r.), Nov., 1933; chief engr., P.W.D. (confd.), Mar., 1940, Orissa.

BRIJ NARAYAN, O.B.E., M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 18th Aug., 1895).—Held non-gaz. appts., May, 1918, to Apl., 1923; apptd. offcr., cl. II, Apl., 1923; addl. dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1939; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Nov., 1941, to Nov., 1944; O.B.E., June, 1943; promoted Cl. I, Aug., 1944.

BRISTOW, SIR CHARLES HOLDITCH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commissioner Bombay) (b. 28th Dec., 1887).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1911, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., and supt., land records, etc.; on mil. duty, May, 1915, to Apl., 1919; actg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agt., May, 1920; settlement officer, Dec., 1923; dep. sec. to govt., genl., edl. and marine depts. (temp.), Nov., 1925; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, Dec., 1926, and again, Apl., 1928; on special duty, Deccan canals, June, 1927; collr., Sept., 1929; offg. secty. to govt., Bombay, home and eccl. depts., Jan., 1935; secty. to govr. of Bombay, Apl., 1935, to Aug., 1938; C.I.E., May, 1937; commr. (confd.), Dec., 1937; advr. to govr. of Bombay, July, 1940; kt., June, 1944; actg. govr. of Bombay, Aug. to Sept., 1945; advr. to govr. of Bombay, Sept., 1945 to Jan., 1946.

BROACH, FRANK ERNEST, Indian Police (b. 2nd Feb., 1919) (asst. supt., Bihar).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd., Apl., 1942, a.d.c. to govr., Nov., 1942.

BROADBENT, ROBERT EVELYN COOPER B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Dec., 1909).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Simla, March, 1938, and dep. commr. in addn.

BROADFOOT, ROBERT CAMPBELL, N.D.A., late Indian Agric. Service (Madras) (b. 5th Feb., 1887).—Retd., Feb., 1942.†

BROADRIBB, PETER, India Office (actg. hr. exec. offr., mil. dept.) (b. 15th Oct., 1891).—apptd. cler. offr., 24th Feb., 1922; actg. hr. cler. offr. June, 1940; actg. hr. exec. offr. Mar., 1946.

BROADWAY, SIR ALAN BRICE, Kt., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Punjab (b. 2nd Nov., 1873).—Retd., Oct., 1933.*

BROCK, HENRY NORMAN, Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 17th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th March, 1914; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; offg. divl. ditto, variously from March, 1927; divl. supt., confd., Jan., 1938.

BROCK, LESLIE EVELYN, late Indian State

Rlys. (b. 13th Jan., 1885).—Retd., Jan., 1940.†
BRODIE, NEAL, M.Sc., F.C.S., A.I.C. (b. 11th July, 1893).—Retd., Feb., 1942.

BROKENSHAW, ARTHUR, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 8th Nov., 1885).—Retd., June, 1941.†

BROMAGE, JOHN ALDHELM RAIKES, C.I.E., Public Works Dept., Punjab (exec. sany. engr.) (b. 7th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., 25th Dec., 1920; exec. sany. engr., June, 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1933; suptg. engr., health (temp.), Aug., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

BROMLEY, RALPH EDWARD, late Indian Police (b. 12th Jan., 1893).—Retd., June, 1939.*

BROMMAGE, JOSEPH CHARLES, O.B.E., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 4th Feb., 1897).—Joined the service as offr., cl. II, March, 1923; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., May, 1933, to Feb., 1937; on depn. to Burma, Apl., 1937, to March, 1940; revtd. to mil. accts. dept., March, 1940; dep. fin. advr., sy. fin., Apl., 1941; jt. fin. advr., sy. fin., Nov., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; cl. I (prov. subs.), Jan., 1942; on depn. to mil. fin. dept., Feb., 1944; addtl. fin. advr., March to Sept., 1944; on depn. to mil. fin. dept., Mar., 1945; addl. fin. advr. mil. fin., Apl., 1945; comd., contr. Mar., 1945; addl. fin. advr., mil. fin., Apl., 1945.

BROOKE, REV. JOHN, Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 26th June, 1903; apptd. junr. chaplain on probn., 19th Jan., 1939).

BROOKES, ALBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service, Burma (b. 22nd Aug., 1885).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

BROOKS, JOHN BIRTWHISTLE TYRILL, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Forest Service, Bombay (b. 13th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1912, as asst. consvr.; on milty. duty from 8th Nov., 1917, to 24th Nov., 1918; dep. consvr., Dec., 1917; offg. consvr., Feb., 1934; and again, Nov., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; ch. consvr., June, 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1945; retd., Dec., 1945.

BROOME, WILLIAM, Indian C.S., (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 18th Mar., 1910).—Educ. at Latymer Upper Sch., Hammersmith, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1936; regr. high ct., Allahabad, Dec., 1943; dist. and sess. judge (confd.), Sept., 1945.

BROOMFIELD, SIR ROBERT STONEHOUSE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 1st Dec., 1882).—Retd., Dec., 1942.†

BROWN, ALAN SUNLEY, Office of the High Commr. for India (lexec. officer, store dept.) (b. 15th Feb., 1893).—Apptd. to store dept., Dec., 1921; clerical officer, Feb., 1924; exec. officer, Jan., 1926.

BROWN, ARTHUR FREDERICK ROBERT, M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 9th March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 18th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Aug., 1925; on mil. duty with Burma Army, Oct., 1942 to July, 1944, and Sept., 1944 to Mar., 1945; civ. aff. serv., Nov., to Dec., 1945.

BROWN, CAMPBELL OSBORNE, Indian State Rlys. (works mang., E.I. Rly.) (b. 11th Feb., 1895).—Production engr., Jan., 1928; works manager, senr. scale, Jan., 1937.

BROWN, DENYS THOMPSON, Major (b. 13th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service as agric. engr., Impl. Agric. Research Inst., 31st Oct., 1945.

BROWN, GILBERT ALEXANDER MURRAY, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief

engr., N.W.F. Prov.) (b. 24th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service 1st Oct., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., May, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; on spl. duty in Waziristan survey circle, May, 1929; chief. engr. and secty. to govt., N.W.F. Prov., Oct., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1942.

BROWN, JAMES BIRCH, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. sec. to govt., Madras) (b. 18th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 25th Nov., 1913; on mil. duty, Jan., 1918, to March, 1919; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1918; temp. under-sec. to govt., June, 1919; offg. sec., bd. of revenue (separate rev.), July, 1922; dep. sec. to govt., finance dept., August, 1923; offg. sec. to ditto, Apl., 1925; dep. sec. to ditto, June, 1925; offg. collr. of salt rev., March, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. collr. and dist. mag., June, 1932; confd., July, 1933; offg. commr. of lab., Madras, May, 1934, to Aug., 1934; member, bd. of rev., June, 1941; chief sec. govt. of Madras, June, 1944; C.S.I., Jan., 1943; sec. to govt. of Madras, Apl., 1946.

BROWN, OSCAR HENRY, O.B.E., B.A., Barr.-at-Law, Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 4th July, 1896).—Presdy. mag., Bombay, Apl., 1935, confd. as chief presdy. mag., May, 1941.

BROWN, RICHARD HAROLD, India Office (higher clerical officer, milty. dept.) (b. 27th Apl., 1882).—Appointed clerical officer, 10th March, 1922; higher clerical officer, Sept., 1932; actg. hr. exec. offr., Nov., 1941.

BROWN, ROBERT SIDNEY, O.B.E., India Office (actg. asst. sec. fin. dept.) (b. 10th Feb., 1889).—Apptd. second-class clerk, India Office, 1st March, 1911, and attd. to the registry and record dept.; transfed. to mil. dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to Jan., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; asst. principal, Apl., 1921; tempy. reading clerk to council, June, 1926, to Aug., 1927; jt. editor, India Office List, Aug., 1927, to Oct., 1932; actg. princpl., Oct., 1932; confd., Dec., 1934; O.B.E., Jan., 1945; actg. asst. sec., Apl., 1946.

BROWN, SIR STUART KELSON, K.C.I.E., C.B., C.V.O., late India Office (b. 24th Dec., 1885).—Retd., 6th Jan., 1942.†

BROWN, WILLIAM SCOTT, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., Madras) (b. 30th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and University, Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 12th Dec., 1914; under-sec. to govt., home dept., April, 1920; offg. sec., board of rev., Madras, March, 1924; offg. collr. of Madras, etc., Aug., 1927; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Nov., 1928; collr. and magte. (prov. subs.), Sept., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; secty. to govt., p.w. and lab. depts., Madras, Jan., 1935, to Oct., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1936; sec. to govt., finance dept., Madras, March, 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1942; member, bd. of rev., Nov., 1942; ch. sec. govt. of Madras, Apl., to Aug., 1945, member bd. of rev., Sept., 1945.

BROWNE, CECIL-ARTHUR, M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., cent. P.W.D.) (b. 17th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 1st Nov., 1927; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1934; confd., Oct., 1936; relief engr., and supply offr., Nov., 1936; M.B.E., Jan., 1941; on depn. to cent. P.W.D., July, 1942; exec. engr., July, 1942; suptg. engr., Apl., 1943.

BROWNE, ERNEST GARDINER, late Oplum. Dept., United Provs. (b. 8th May, 1884).—

Joined the service as proby. asst. opium agent, 23rd April, 1906; confd., Oct., 1907; dist. opium officer, Apl., 1925; opium agent and manager, opium factory, Ghazipur, Jan., 1938; ret'd., 8th June, 1940; re-empld. in A.R.P. Dept., U.P., July, 1940.

BROWNE, ROBERT STEEL, O.B.E., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 31st July, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926; pers. asst. to chief consvr., June, 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; on mil. duty, Jan., 1940.

BROWNE, WALTER FITZGERALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 3rd March, 1897).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1921; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1921; asst. mag. and collr., United Provs., 3rd Dec., 1921; asst. commr. from Oct., 1923, to May, 1924; jt. mag., Nov., 1927; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1930; offg. magte. and collr., Feb., 1935; confd., Apl., 1935.

BROWNLOW, ANDREW L'ESTRANGE, late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 19th Feb., 1921; dist. supt., Jan., 1924; ret'd., Oct., 1946.

BROWNSDON, THOMAS EDWARD, Capt., I.A., Indian Political service (b. 26th June, 1910); first commn., 30th Jan., 1932; pers. asst. to res. at Hyderabad, Jan., 1936; under trng. in U.P., Sept., 1936; under sec. to A.G.G., res. and C.C. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1938; confd. in Indian Political Service, June, 1939; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, July, 1939; asst. pol. agent in Baluchistan, under orders of pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Oct., 1939; 2nd asst. pol. agent, Mekran, Dec., 1939; attaché, British Legation, Kabul, Dec., 1940; asst. commr., Kohat, 1941; under sec., ext. affairs dept., Sept., 1944, and again Oct., 1945; ditto, pol. dept., Apl., 1946.

BRUCE, HAROLD EASTON, M.C., Indian Police (centr. intell. offcr., Bihar and Orissa) (b. 18th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 20th March, 1921; offg. supt., Oct., 1923; confd., March, 1931; supt., police training coll., Feb., 1931; cent. intell. offcr., Bihar and Orissa, March, 1938.

BRUCE-JOY, ARTHUR WILLIAM, late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 23rd March, 1891).—Joined the service as dist. loco. supt., 22 May, 1920; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; supt., May, 1926; offg. divl. ditto, variously from March, 1927; confd., March, 1939; ret'd., Mar., 1946.

BRUFORD, STANLEY JOHN, M.I.C.S., M.(Am.)I.E.E., A.M.I.M.E., F.R.G.S., F.R.S.A., (b. 24th Nov., 1889).—Ch. engr., Nepal govt., 1921 to 1926; exec. engr. etc., Punjab P.W.D. (hydro-elec.), 1927 to 1935; res. engr. Travancore govt. 1938 to 1945; project offr. (civil), central tech. power bd. (temp.) Jan., 1946.

BRUNYATE, SIR JAMES BENNETT, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (ret'd.)—Retd. from Indian C.S., May, 1917; member of the Council of India, from May, 1917, to Sept., 1924.*

BRYAN, DENZIL ARNOLD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 15th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at the Lawrence Coll., Ghora Galli, Univ. of the Punjab, and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. offr., Apl., 1939; addtl. dep. commr., March, 1939; regr., high ct., Apl., 1939; sec. to Premier, May, 1944.

BRYANT, STANLEY CLARE, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 13th March, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 11th May, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Apl., 1931.

BRYANT, WALTER TELHAM, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 12th February, 1906).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Apl., 1931; pte. secty. to govr., Madras, June, 1938; O.B.E., June, 1943; collr. and dist. mag., Feb., 1946.

BRYSON, CHRISTOPHER LENWOOD, B.A., Indian, C.S. (district mag. Orissa) (b. 9th Nov., 1908).—Educ. at Dulwich College, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1935; under-secty., def. co-ord. dept., govt. of India, March, 1940; ditto, home dept., May, 1940; ditto, civil def., Oct., 1941 to 1943; dist. mag. Orissa, July, 1945; addl. agent to prov. govt. and agency sess. judge, also settlement. offr.

BU, SIR MYA, BARR., Judl. Dept., Burma (ch. justice, high ct., Rangoon) (b. 10th March, 1885).—Apptd. asst. gov. advocate, March, 1924; addl. judge, high ct., June, 1926; judge, ditte, Dec., 1926; ch. justice, Nov., 1935; kt., Feb., 1937.

BUCH, NILKANTHRAI MOHANLAL, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 9th July, 1908).—Educ. at Sch. of Economics and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1935; chairman, Lahore impr. trust, Nov., 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; dir. of civil supplies and addtl. dep. sec. to govt. (home), Apl., 1943.

BUCHANAN, REV. GEORGE, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 11th July, 1906).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Church of Scotland, July, 1934; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1936.

BUCKLAND, SIR PHILIP LINDSAY, Kt., Barr. (b. 12th Sept., 1874).—Retd., Sept., 1934.*

BUCKLEY, HUGH CLIVE, C.S.I., M.D., F.R.C.S.E., Maj.-Gen., late I.M.S. (b. 31st Oct., 1880).—Retd., Dec., 1940; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; re-empld., as princpl., medl. coll., Agra, Aug., 1941.†

BUCKNALI, MARK, B.A., India Office (b. 8th July, 1917).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. principal, 2nd Oct., 1939; on milty. service from May, 1940.

BUCKNEY, FRANK, B.A., late Imperial Customs Service (b. 1st Jan., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

BUDD, BERNARD WILFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 18th Dec., 1912).—Educ. at West Leeds High Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bombay as asst. commr.; served in Sind from Apl., 1941; offg. var. from May, 1944; dep. sec., govt. of Sind, Apl., 1946.

BUDH SINGH, SARDAR, Indian Police (b. 20th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 27th Jan., 1929; confd., Jan., 1931.

BULKLEY, ALFRED FRANCIS, late Indian Police, Madras (b. 9th March, 1888).—Retd., Aug., 1941.†

BULL, HERBERT GEORGE, O.B.E., late India Office (b. 13th Feb., 1882).—Educ. at Owen's Gr. Sch., Islington; served as sec. div. clerk, War Office, from April, 1900; apptd. 2nd class clerk, acct.-gen'l's dept., 17th April, 1902; trsfed. to registry and record dept., July, 1906; transfed. to mil. dept., January, 1917; minor staff clerk, April, 1918; staff clerk (temp.) Oct., 1919; higher exec. offr., April, 1922; actg. supt. exec. offr., March, 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; jt. editor, India Office List, May to Nov., 1934; actg. ch. exec. offr., June, 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; ret'd., Jan., 1946.

BUMSTEAD, W. R., Indian State Ry's. (dist. signal engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 28th Aug., 1893).—Joined service on M. & S.M. Rly., Jan., 1925; services transferred to N.W. Rly. as asst. signal engr., Apl., 1929; dist. signal engr., Nov., 1938.

BUNN, ARTHUR GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. mag. and collr., Orissa) (b. 29th Jan., 1911).—Educ. at Rutlish Sch., Merton, and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; dist. mag. and collr., Orissa, May, 1945.

BUNYAD HUSSAIN KHAN BAHADUR SAYAD, M.B.E., B.A., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 2nd Feb., 1886).—Joined the serv., 13th May, 1912; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1913; dep. commr. confd., July, 1934; M.B.E., June, 1946.

BURBIDGE, HERBERT FREDERICK GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 25th July, 1904).—Joined the service, Dec., 1923; and deputed to police training coll., Sardah, Bengal; proby. asst. supt., Oct., 1925; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Dec., 1932; confd., Apl., 1935; asst. to the inspectr.-gen'l. of police, 1938; supt., Cachar, May, 1940.

BURDON, SIR ERNEST, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Jan., 1881).—Ret'd., Aug., 1940.

BURDON, THOMAS WILKINSON, B.Sc., Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 4th Mar., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Dec., 1940; confd. Mar., 1943.

BURFORD, WALTER BECKWITH, late Indian State Ry's. (b. 8th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 29th December, 1911; resdnt. engr. G.P.I. Rly., April, 1920; offg. supt. of statistics, July, 1929; exec., engr. May, 1932; offg. dep. ch. engr. bridges, G.I.P. Rly., April, 1935; dep. gen'l. mgr., Feb., 1941 to 1946; ret'd., Jan., 1946.

BURGESS, FREDERICK GEORGE, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 1st Oct., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928; civ. aff., serv., Dec., 1943 to Dec., 1945.

BURGESE, GEOFFREY, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sec. to govr., Central Provs.) (b. 29th Apl., 1906).—Educ. at Hanley High Sch., Stoke-on-Trent, and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, 12th Nov., 1928, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. sub-judge, Dec., 1931; regr., judl. commr's ct., Apl., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; confd. as sub-judge, Jan., 1935; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; sec. to govr., Jan., 1942; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

BURKE, EDMUND PLUNKETT, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th June, 1885).—ret'd., July, 1938; chairman, Assam publ. serv. commn., March, 1940.*

BURKE, GERALD TYLER, C.I.E., M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (member, medl. bd.,

India Office) (b. 31st May, 1882).—First commn. dated 31st July, 1909; in mil. employ to 15th Feb., 1924; profir. King George's medl. coll., Lucknow, Feb., 1924. civil surg., Nov., 1925; employed under Lucknow Univ., Apl., 1929; secty., India Medl. Council, May, 1935; ret'd., May, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; member, medl. board, India Office, May, 1940.

BURKE, SIR RICHARD JOHN CHARLES, Kt., Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd. (b. 5th May, 1878).—Ret'd., Sept., 1933.*

BURKE, SAMUEL MARTIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 3rd July, 1906).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrvd in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1936; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, July, 1941; presdnt. election petitions tribunal, May, 1946.

BURMAN, HAROLD PERCY, Indian State Ry's. (dist. elec. engr., E.B. rly.) (b. 25th June, 1896).—Joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1923; offg. dist. elec. engr., E.B. rly., Jan., 1929; confd., March, 1933.

BURMAN, RAM PRASAD (see Ram Prasad B.).
BURNS, ERNEST ALBERT, Burma Fr. S. (b. 21st Nov., 1916).—Apptd. on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

BURN, REV. JOHN HUMPHREY, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 6th May, 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 29th Sept., 1924; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1926; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1933.

BURN, SIR RICHARD, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.).—Ret'd., Jan., 1927.*

BURN, SIR SIDNEY, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 19th June, 1881).—Ret'd. June, 1942; chairman, Madras pub. serv. commn., July, 1946.

BURNETT, ROBERT RICHARDSON, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Oct., 1897).—First commn. dated 3rd July, 1915; in milty. employ prior to 18th March, 1922; adjutant, Zhob levy. Corps, March, 1922; probnr. in pol. dept., Feb., 1925; asst. pol. agent, Mekran, May, 1926; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1927; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Sept., 1929; pol. agent, Zhob, Oct., 1929; secty. to agent to G.G. and C.C., Baluchistan, Oct., 1930; O.B.E., June, 1931; offg. dep. secty., for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1932; and again, May, 1933; offg. under secty., ditto, Feb., 1933; principl. officer and adviser, Tonk state, Apl., 1934; pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Oct., 1936; pol. agent, Chhattisgarh States, Oct., 1939; addl. dy. secy., pol. dept., Sept., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1944; dep. secy. (pol.), Feb., 1943; jt. secy. ext. affairs dept., Apl., 1944; sec., ditto, Sept., 1944; jt. sec. ditto, Jan., 1946; offg. resdnt. Cl. I and resdnt. for Rajputana, Apl., 1946.

BURNEY, A. A.—Apptd. dep. sec., govt. of India, fin. dept., April, 1946.

BURNS, HENRY GEORGE, M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 29th Apl., 1903).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 6th Nov., 1924; asst. exec. engr., 5th Sept., 1926; offg. exec. engr., March, 1931; confd., Oct., 1934; p.a. to ch. engr., March, 1938; M.B.E., June, 1943.

BURNS, WILLIAM, C.I.E., D.Sc., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 6th July, 1884).—Apptd. economic botanist, Poona, 11th Oct., 1908; deputed to mil. dept., Nov., 1917; principl. Agric. Coll., Poona, May, 1922; jt. dir. of

agric. (temp.), Apl., 1926; offg. dir. of agric., Oct., 1932; confd., July, 1933; offg. agric. expert, Aug., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; retd., July, 1939; agricl. commr. (on contract), July, 1939.

BURRELL, OSCAR LAWRENCE, Indian Police (district supt., Madras) (b. 13th March, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 14th Nov., 1927; confd., Nov., 1929; dist. supt. (confd.), July, 1941; asst. insp.-genl., Mar. 1945.

BURRELL, ROY HERBERT ADAMS, Indian Police (b. 14th June, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 16th Nov., 1925; offg. dist. supt., March, 1928; supt. of police, jt. police force, Eastern States, July, 1940; confd., as dep. supt., Nov., 1941.

BURROWS, LIONEL BURTON, C.B.E., B.A., late Bengal Civil Service (b. 12th March, 1883).—Retd., March, 1938.*

BURTON, EDWARD STANLEY VINER, B.Sc., (Edin.), Indian Forest Service (b. 25th Nov., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1930; served with defence dept., air branch, Jan., 1942.

BURTON, FRANK, India Office (hr. exec. officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 4th Aug., 1889).—Apptd. second divn. clerk, Board of Trade, Sept., 1908; transf. to Board of Educn., Sept., 1909, and to Census Office, Apl., 1910; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 8th May, 1913, and attd. to registry and record dept.; temporarily transf. to mil. dept., Sept., 1914; higher clerical officer, July, 1922; exec. officer, Sept., 1932; actg. hr. exec. officer, Apl., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937; lent to min. of supply, June, 1940 to June 1945.

BURTON, SIR GEOFFREY POWNALL, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (retd.), Central Provs. (b. 19th Nov., 1884).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1909, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., Sept., 1913; spl. duty in sectt., Nov., 1916, to Jan., 1918; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1920; confd. as dep. commr., Feb., 1921; excise commr., March, 1924; rev. secty. to govt., C.P., Jan., 1929; offg. commr., Feb., 1932, and again, March, 1933; offg. commr. of settlements and dir., land records, etc., Sept., 1932; commr., May, 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; member, govr.'s exec. council, Central Provs., Apl., 1936; fin. commr., March, 1937; K.C.I.E., May, 1937; advr. to govr., Nov., 1939; and fin. commr. in addn.; retd., 22nd Nov., 1944; re-empld. as advr. and retd. Dec., 1945; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1946.

BURTON, LIONEL HAND, late Indian Police (b. 19th Aug., 1883).—Retd., March, 1937.*

BURTT, THEODORE, Indian Service of Engrs. (supfg. engr., Punjab) (b. 2nd July, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. on 1st Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Apl., 1927; under-secty. (estab.), Punjab, March, 1932; offr. on spec. duty, Punjab irrgn. sectt., Apl., 1941; suptg. engr. (perm.), Oct., 1943; offr. on spl. duty (Sthn. Administration), Jan. to March, 1945.

BURY, REV. PHINEAS, Indian Eccles. Establ. (chaplain on probation, Cent. Provs.) (b. 8th Oct., 1902).—Apptd., 23rd Sept., 1939.

BUSH, SYDNEY POYNTZ, A.R.I.B.A. (b. 8th Sept., 1889).—Apptd. consulting architect to govt. of Burma, 27th Apl., 1925.

BUSHBY, WALTER EDWIN, C.I.E., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engineers. (b. 28th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service,

1st October, 1913; asst. engr., November 1913; in mil. employ from 31st July, 1916 to 21st January, 1920; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr. Oct., 1922; suptg. engr., Oct., 1940; chairman Karachi Port Trust, Apl., 1942 to July, 1946; retd., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

BUSK, MICHAEL EDWARD, Burma Police (Cl. I), (asst. supt.) (b. 25th Feb., 1920).—Educ. Uppingham Sch.; apptd. on probn. 19th Oct., 1939; civ. aff. serv. to Dec., 1945.

BUSS, JOHN ARTHUR GREGORY, Capt., I.A. (b. 10th Oct., 1912).—Apptd. on probn., to I.P.S. from mil. employ Nov., 1938; under-sec., res. Punjab States and asst. pol. agt. for Loharu and Dujana, March, 1944; under sec. to resdt., Punjab States, Sept., 1945; confd. in I.P.S., Feb., 1946.

BUSSELL, EDWARD IRVINE, C.B.E., Air Commdr., R.A.F. (Retd.) Civil Aviation Directorate. (b. 23rd July, 1896).—Apptd. dir. of training and licensing, Feb., 1946; C.B.E., Jan., 1946.

BUTCHER, WILLIAM HENRY, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 15th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as temp. asst. engr., Burma rlys., 26th Jan., 1921; asst. engr., Jan., 1926; exec. engr., Burma rlys., March, 1935.

BUTLER, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.B.E., Office of High Commr., for India (b. 3rd Nov., 1876).—Apptd. to office of High Commr., 18th Oct., 1920; temp. admin. offr., Dec., 1922; high. exec. offr., Oct., 1929; retd., Oct., 1942, and re-employed as dep. trade commr., London; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

BUTLER, SIR G. S., C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon), F.I.C., late Indian Ordnance Department.—Apptd. chemist, cordite factory, Aruvankadu, 2nd Sept., 1910; manager and chemist, ditto, Dec., 1921; offg. supt., ditto, May, 1928; confd., Feb., 1930; C.I.E., Jan. 1939; dep. dir.-genl. munitions production depart. of Supply, Aug., 1940; kt., Jan., 1943; retd. May, 1946.

BUTLER, HAROLD EDWIN, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 1st April, 1893).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1913, as asst. supt.; on milty. duty from Oct., 1917, to May, 1919; supt. of police, Sept., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; O.B.E., May, 1937; supt. (sel. grade), confd., Feb., 1942; addtl. dep. commr. (temp.), Feb., 1942; commr. of police (confd.), Nov., 1942; C.I.E., June, 1944; on I.P.R.

BUTLER, JAMES MICHAEL BEECROFT, Indian Police (b. 9th Jan., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Nov., 1938, confd. May, 1940.

BUTLER, SIR MONTAGU SHERARD DAWES, K.C.S.I., C.B., C.I.E., C.V.O., C.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th May, 1873).—Gouv. of Central Provs., Jan., 1925, to Nov., 1929; and again, March, 1930, to Sept., 1933.*

BUTLER, RICHARD AUSTEN, M.P. (b. 9th Dec., 1902).—Member, Indian Franchise ctee., Jan. to June, 1932; parly. under-sec. of state for India, Sept., 1932, to May, 1937; parly. under-secty. of state for Burma, Apl.-May, 1937; delegate to Indian round-table conf., London, Nov.-Dec., 1932.

BUTLER, SAW, MAJOR, M.C., Burma Frontier Service (b. 13th May, 1920).—Apptd., 13th Feb., 1946.

BUTT, MOHAMMAD YAKUB, B.A., (b. 10th, Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 1935; supt., March, 1942; Hajj. offr. govt. of India, Aug.,

1943 to May, 1944; asst. sec. govt. of India, commonwealth relations dept., Aug., 1946.

BUTTER, JOHN HENRY, M.B.E., Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Punjab) (b. 20th Apr., 1916).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr. under govt. of India from Apr., 1942; M.B.E., June, 1946.

BYERS, JOSEPH AUSTEN, M.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 1st Oct., 1895).—Educ. at King's Sch., Chester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived 12th December, 1921; sub. collr. and mag., Jan., 1924; addl. sess. judge, June, 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; confd., June, 1934; served temp. with govt. of Orissa, from Dec., 1936; addl. judge, high ct., Madras, Jan., 1942; puisne judge, June, 1942.

BYRON, FRANCIS HARVEY, late Salt Rev. Dept., Burma (b. 21st Sept., 1884).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

BYU, U TUN, Barr., Burma Judl. Dept. (govt. advocate) (b. 22nd Aug., 1895).—Apptd. offg. asst. govt. advocate, 1st March, 1924; confd., Dec., 1926; offg. govt. advocate, Burma, May, 1936; confd. May, 1937; and again, 1946; actg. advocate-genl., June, 1946.

CADELL, SIR PATRICK ROBERT, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Retd., Dec., 1926*; kt., Jan., 1935.

CADMAN, SAMUEL PARKES HUBERT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bangal) (b. 15th Nov., 1906).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

CAFFIN, ALBERT EDWARD, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. commr. Bombay) (b. 16th June, 1902).—Joined the serv. as asst. supt., Bombay, on 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Mar., 1927; and again, Jul., 1933; confd., Sept., 1933; asst. to inspr.-genl. police, C.I.D. intell., Oct., 1939, to 1944; Indian police medal, 1941; dep. commr., C.I.D., Oct., 1944; dep. commr. Bombay, Apr., 1945; O.B.E., June, 1946.

CALDECOTT, CHARLES D'ARCY, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (Central Provs.) (b. 18th Oct., 1893).—Retd., June, 1940.†

CALDER, CHARLES CUMMING, B.Sc., F.L.S. (b. 3rd Dec., 1884).—Supt., Royal Botanic Garden, Calcutta, and cinchona cultivation in Bengal, May, 1925; retd., Dec., 1939.*

CALDER, WILLIAM BUCHANAN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr.) (b. 18th Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1927; offg. exec. engr., Apr., 1931; confd., Oct., 1936; dep. sec., govt. Sind., Apr., 1941; addtl. ditto, Jan. to March, 1942; suptg. engr., Oct., 1945.

CALLOW, FRANK ALBERT, India Office (actg. hr. exec. offr. acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 3rd May, 1900).—Apptd. cler. offr., Apr., 1920; actg. exec. offr., May, 1939; clerk to the advisers, July, 1938 to June, 1941; actg. hr. exec. offr., May, 1944.

CALVERT, WILLIAM HANDLEY, late Indian Police (Assam) (b. 27th Jan., 1896).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

CAMBRIDGE, SYDNEY JOHN PELHAM, O.B.E., V.D., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E., Indian State Rlys. (genl. mgr. B.N. rly.) (b. 11th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service, 22nd Apr., 1912; asst. engr., Apr., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Apr., 1928; exec. engr., July, 1938; divl. supt., Dec., 1943; O.B.E., June, 1944; genl. mgr., B.N. rly., Jan., 1946.

CAME, WILLIAM GERALD, C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bihar) (b.

8th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; suptg. engr. (temp.), March, 1934; confd., under-secty. to govt., P.W.D., Nov., 1923; Apr., 1936; dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1938; tfd. to Orissa, July, 1940; ch. eng., and sec., govt. P.W.D., Nov., 1942; C.I.E., June, 1944; Retd., July, 1945, and re-employed from Aug., 1945.

CAMERON, DUNCAN CHARLES THOMAS, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 6th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. dist. supt., March, 1929, and again, June, 1933; confd., Aug., 1935; on depn. as cent. intell. offr. under govt. of India, Oct., 1944; offg. d.i.g. police in addn.

CAMERON, GILBERT STUART, Dept. of Mines in India (inspr.) (b. 22nd Apr., 1891).—Joined the service as junr. inspr., 25th Apr., 1921; offg. inspr., June, 1934; confd., Sept., 1936.

CAMERON KER, EDWARD, late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 31st March, 1888).—Retd., Nov., 1940.

CAMPBELL, ALEXANDER, M.C., M.A., Indian Educational Service (Burma) (b. 29th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as professor of English on 24th March, 1915; on mil. serv. from 31st August, 1916, to 14th February, 1919; registrar, Rangoon Univ., Dec., 1921; inspr. of schools, Burma, June, 1933; offg. dir. of public instn., Aug., 1933; and again, June, 1936; asst. dir. of public instn., Feb., 1935; dir. of public instn., Apr., 1939; joined army under civ. aff. serv., Feb., 1944; on depn. to U.K., Sept., 1944, to Oct., 1945; dir. of public instn., Apr., 1939; l.p.r. from Dec., 1945.

CAMPBELL, SIR ARCHIBALD YOUNG GRIFFS, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 18th May, 1872).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

CAMPBELL, DOUGLAS COLIN, C.I.E., late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 28th May, 1891).—Joined the service as deputy ch. accts. offr., Burma Rlys., 20th March, 1932, on abolition of appt. as dir. of comml. audit; confd. as chief accts. offcr., July, 1933; dir., rly., clearing accts. office, October, 1934; chief accts. offcr., N.W. Rly., Oct., 1937; controller of rly. accts., Apr., 1939, to Mar., 1946; C.I.E., June, 1946.

CAMPBELL, JAMES DONALD, D.S.O., Col., R.E., late Survey of India (b. 3rd May, 1884).—Retd., May, 1939.*

CAMPBELL, SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., O.B.E., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Provs.)—Retd., Oct., 1922.*

CAMPBELL, JOHN HERBERT, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 23rd May, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 20th Oct., 1930; offg. exec. engr., May, 1936; confd., Oct., 1939; serv. under govt. of India, second engr. offr., civ. aviation directe., July, 1940; under sec., P.W.D., Bihar, Apr., 1945.

CAMPBELL, KENNETH RICHARD, Burma Frontier Service, (b. 23rd Nov., 1923).—Apptd. on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

CAMPBELL, SIR WALTER FENDALL, K.C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th May, 1894).—First commn., dated 14th Jan., 1914; joined the pol. dept., 21st Jan., 1921; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, June, 1921; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, May, 1926; asst. commr., Hangu, Nov., 1926; pol. agent, Kurram, Nov., 1927; dep. commr., D.I.

Khan, Oct., 1928; ditto, Kohat, Oct., 1928. asst. commr., Thal, Nov., 1928; dep. ditto; Bannu, Nov., 1929; secty. to A.G.G., in the states of W.I., Oct., 1933; prime minister, Alwar, March, 1935, to Sept., 1936; pol. agent, Bundelkhan, Oct., 1937; resdt. in Waziristan, Feb., 1939; advr. to govr., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; confd. as resdt., C.I., and C.C. of Panth-Piploda, May, 1942; K.C.I.E., June, 1946.

CANDY, RONALD HERBERT, C.I.E., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 13th June, 1886).—First commn. dated 27th Jan., 1912; offg. civil surg., Apl., 1923; confd., Aug., 1927; offg. surg.-genl. with the Govt. of Bombay, July, 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; C.M.O. and civ. surg., Delhi, March, 1938.

CANTLIE, SIR KEITH, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 6th Feb., 1886).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll. (Aberdeen), Aberdeen Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. commr., June, 1911; ditto, Assam, Apl., 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), May, 1915, to Apl., 1919; presdt., Manipur State Darbar, Sept., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1923; confd., May, 1927; offg. commr., March, 1937, to Feb., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1939; member, rev. trib., Jan., 1942; kt., June, 1944; chairman, Assam pub. serv. comm., Dec., 1945; ret'd., Dec., 1945.

CAPOOR, SHYAM BEHARI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 15th Jan., 1907).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addtl. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1938; addtl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1938; offg. ditto, March, 1939; confd. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1942; addtl. dep. sec. govt. of India, legisl., dept., Aug., 1945; again dist. and sess. judge, March, 1946.

CARBERRY, MURLOUGH, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., D.S.O., M.C., late Indian Agricultural Service (b. 24th July, 1890).—Joined the service as agricultural chemist on 1st Dec., 1920; offg. asst. dir. of agric., Bengal June, 1933; and again, Sept., 1933; confd., Oct., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; ret'd., Aug., 1945.

CARDEW, JOHN HORACE, late Indian State Ryis. (Burma Ryis.) (b. 31st July, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 3rd July, 1913; on milty. duty from 13th Dec., 1914, to 8th May, 1920; offg. variously as dist. loco. supt. from July, 1920; offg. dep. loco. supt., Feb., 1930; confd., May, 1931; ret'd., 5th May, 1944.

CAREY-EVANS, SIR THOMAS JOHN, Kt., M.C., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 26th June, 1884).—Ret'd., July, 1926.†

CARGILL, IAN PETER MACGILLIVRAY, M.B.E., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 29th Sept., 1915).—Educ. at Malvern and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 9th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; served in Sind from Oct., 1939; dep. admtr., martial law, June, 1942; asst. rev. offr., Oct., 1943; under sec., fin. dept., Apl., 1944; M.B.E., Jan., 1944.

CARLESS, HENRY ALFRED, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 26th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt.

on 20th Nov., 1914; offg. supt., Feb., 1921; confd., Jan., 1926; princpl., police trng. school, Moradabad, Sept., 1930, to July, 1931; confd. in sel. grade, Oct., 1940; police advr. to resdt., Rajputana.

CARLESTON, HADDEN HAMILTON, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 25th July, 1904).—Educ. at St. Olaves's Gr. Sch., and Cambridge (Trinity Hall); joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1929; collr. and dist. mag., May, 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

CARMAN, FRANK, M.B.E., Indian Police (b. 15th May, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 26th Nov., 1936; confd., Jan., 1939; M.B.E., June, 1944.

CARNAGHAN, DOUGLAS WILLIAM BERESFORD, Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 21st Dec., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, Nov., 1932; confd., Apl., 1935; exec. offr., regional transp. authy., March, 1940, to Jan., 1942; supt. (confd.) Dec., 1943; transferred to Sind, Dec., 1945.

CAROE, SIR OLAF KIRKPATRICK, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S., Ind. Political Service, (govr. North West Front Prov.) (b. 15th Nov., 1892).—Educated at Winchester and Magdalen College, Oxford; joined the service, 31st Aug., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; city mag., Peshawar, Nov., 1923; dep. commr., Peshawar, Apl., 1926; asst. dep. commr., Mardan, Oct., 1926; dep. commr., Hazara, Aug., 1927; ditto, Kohat, Nov., 1928; jt. dep. commr., Peshawar, Apl., 1930; dep. commr., ditto, July, 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1933; dep. secty. to govt. of India, pol. dept., May, 1934; offg. resdt., Persian Gulf, Aug., 1937; offg. A.G.G., Resident and C.C. in Baluchistan, Nov., 1937; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, March, 1938; temp. resdt. in Waziristan, July, 1938; rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, March, 1939; secty., external affairs dept., Govt. of India, July, 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1944; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1945; govr. N.W.F.P., Mar., 1946.

CARPENTER, JOHN HOWARD, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 23rd Nov., 1886).—Ret'd., Oct., 1939.*

CARR, SIR WILLIAM, Kt., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 9th Feb., 1872).—Ret'd., Feb., 1932.*

CARREY, JOHN, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., B.Sc., Maj., I.M.S., Medl. Dept., Central Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 23rd July, 1894).—First commn., 20th Jan., 1923; offg. civil surgeon, Central Provs., March, 1932; confd., March, 1934.

CARRIER, PHILIP LESLIE CARO, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Ryis. (C.M.O., Burma Ryis.) (b. 14th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as dist. medl. officer, Burma Ryis., 14th Jan., 1927; offg. C.M.O., ditto, Nov., 1933; and again, March, 1936; confd., May, 1936.

CARRITT, MICHAEL JOHN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 3rd Jan., 1906).—Ret'd., Feb., 1939.*

CARROLL, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 21st July, 1890).—Ret'd., March, 1940.†

CARROTT, WILL JUDSON, Burma Civil Service (Cl. I) (dep. commr.) (b. 18th Feb., 1916).—Educ. at Govt. English High Sch., Maymyo and Rangoon Univ.; apptd. on probn., 30th Sept., 1938; on mil. serv., Nov., 1940 to 1945;

on duty in def. dept., Oct., 1942; civ. aff. serv., 1943; dep. commr., Dec., 1945.

CARSE, ROBERT, Indian Police (suppt., Assam) (b. 4th Nov., 1902).—Joined the service, Nov., 1923, and deputed to police training coll. Sardah, Bengal; proby. asst. supt., Oct., 1925; confd., May, 1926; offg. supt., Feb., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935; pol. offcr., Balipara fron. tract, July, 1938 to Nov., 1938; supt., Sibsagar, March, 1939; supt. river police, Sept., 1941; supt. rly. police, Nov., 1942; supt. Sildrar, July, 1945.

CARSON, SIR CHARLES FRED, M.C., Col., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 10th Feb., 1886).—Retd., Feb., 1941.†

CARTER, ALFRED COLDRICK, Indian Police (commr. Burma) (b. 6th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 28th Oct., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1931; asst. commr., Nov., 1933; confd. as dist. supt., Nov., 1937; inspr.-genl. and police advr., East. States, Feb., 1941; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; dist. supt., Nov., 1945; commr., Dec., 1945.

CARTER, ARTHUR CHARLES, O.B.E., Indian Police (suppt., Bihar) (b. 27th May, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 24th March, 1922; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Feb., 1933; O.B.E., June, 1939.

CARTER, HORACE LEONARD, M.I.R.S.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. signal engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 17th Dec., 1898).—Asst. signal engr., 15th Dec., 1922; dist. ditto, 5th Feb., 1935.

CARTER, MALCOLM OGILVY, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd July, 1898).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 15th Dec., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1923; offg. mag. and collr., variously from Feb., 1929; offg. secty., bd. of rev., Bengal, Sept., 1934; mag. and collr., Apl., 1936; secty. land rev. commission, Bengal, Nov., 1938, to Sept., 1939; dir. of land record and surveys, Bengal, Dec., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; addl. sec. to govr., July, 1940; sec., ditto, Aug., 1940; confd. mag. and collr., Sept. 1942; civ. representative of govt. of Bengal with eastern army, Sept., 1942, to Nov., 1943.

CARTER, REGINALD ROBERT LANGHAM, Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 16th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Bradfield and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to gov., home and pol. dept., March, 1930; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1932; confd., May, 1937; secty. to finl. commr., Jan., 1940; petrol rationing offr. and addl. dep. sec. govt. of India, War transport dept., Oct., 1942; on spl. duty, ch. sec.'s office, govt. of Burma, Nov., 1944; civ. aff. serv., 1944; commr., March, 1946.

CARTER, THOMAS, O.B.E., Dept. of Printing and Stationery (manager G. of I. Press) (b. 22nd Nov., 1891).—Joined the service, 27th March, 1920; asst. manager, G. of I. press, Feb., 1927; offg. manager, ditto, March, 1927; confd., Apl., 1931; M.B.E., June, 1934; on sp. duty, Nov., 1943; 1st grade manager, Mar., 1945; O.B.E., June, 1946.

CARTWRIGHT, ALBERT, Indian Police (b. 27th Dec., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 21st Nov., 1931; confd., Apl., 1934; sec., provl. transpt., authy., Sept., 1943; addtl. dep. supt., March, 1944.

CARVEY, EUSTACE MALTRAVERS, Indian

State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly.) (b. 22nd July, 1893).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1915; asst. contr. of stores, Aug., 1919; offg. dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., variously from Feb., 1921; confd., Dec., 1932.

CASE, ROBERT CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. mech. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 19th Apl., 1893).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1919, as asst. loco. supt.; offg. dist. ditto, E.B. rly., Dec., 1925; offg. dep. dir., rly. bd., Nov., 1927; dep. ch. mechl. engr., G.I.P. rly., Nov., 1931; offg. dir., rly. bd., Sept., 1936; dep. ch. contr., standardisation (mechl.), Oct., 1936; asst. dep. director-genl., munitions prod., dept. of supply, July, 1940; ch. mechl. engr., G.I.P. rly., Aug., 1940.

CASEMENT, EDGAR REGINALD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Oct., 1886).—Retd., Oct., 1941.†

CASEY, RICHARD GARDINER, P.C., D.S.O., M.C. govr. of Bengal.—Jan., 1944, to Feb., 1946.

CASSELLS, SIR ROBERT ARCHIBALD, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., D.S.O., p.s.c., General Indian Army (b. 15th March, 1876).—C.-in-C., India, and member Govr.-Genl.'s exec. council, 29th Nov., 1935, to Dec., 1940; G.C.B., 1933; G.C.S.I., July, 1940.*

CASTENS, HERBERT ERNEST, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 10th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, on 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930; on mil. service, Aug., 1942 to Dec., 1945.

CATER, SIR ALEXANDER NORMAN LEY, K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. and Indian Political Service (b. 15th June, 1880).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

CAULTON, MICHAEL JOHN STEWART, Burma Frontier Service (b. 20th Dec., 1920).—Apptd. on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

CAVE, LESLIE STORRER, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd March, 1894).—Retd., May, 1940.

CHADWICK, SIR DAVID THOMAS, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 7th Apl., 1876).—Retd., March, 1929.*

CHADWICK, FREDERICK JOHN, Indian Police (suppt., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1894).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1921; asst. supt., Sept., 1925; offg. dist. supt., July, 1932; confd., July, 1933.

CHAGLA, MOHOMEDALI CURRIM B.A., Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 30th Sept., 1900).—Addl. judge (temp.), high ct., Bombay, Aug., 1941, to Aug., 1942; judge, ditto, Aug., 1942.

CHAIN, U. BA MAUNG, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 24th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 1st Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1936; civ. aff. serv., Oct., 1945 to Jan., 1946.

CHAINANI, HASHMATRAI KHUBCHAND, Indian C.S. (b. 29th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay; and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. coll. and mag.; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, Aug., 1933; offg. remembr. of legal affairs, June, 1935; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1935; offg. sec., legisl. council, Bombay, March, 1936 to 1938; commr. for workmen's compensation (in addn.), Apl., 1940; dep. sec., legal dept., and sec., legisl. cl., var. from Apl., 1938, to Jan., 1942; addtl. jt. sec. (home), June, 1942; served under central govt. from June, 1942, as dept. sec., home dept.; service with govt. of Bombay, Jan., 1945; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1945.

CHAKKO, K. C., B.A., B.Sc., late Ind. Educl.

Service (b. 24th August, 1892).—Apptd. prof. of civil engrg., coll. of engrg., Madras, 18th Nov., 1921; princpl., coll. of engrg. Guindy, May, 1936; princpl. govt. coll., Kumbakonam, Mar., 1942; retd., Jan., 1946.

CHAKRAVARTHI, SUBRAHMANYAM, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. Madras) (b. 30th Aug. 1910).—Educ. at the Hindu High Sch., Triplacane, University of Madras, The Sch. of Economics, London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935; sub. collr., Dec., 1937; under sec., devel. dept., June, 1940; ditto, govt. of India (war transport), Sept., 1942; dep. sec., ditto, Oct., 1945; M.B.E., June, 1945.

CHAKRAVARTI, A., M.A., late Indian Educational Service, Madras (b. 1st July, 1883).—retd., July, 1938.*

CHAKRAVARTI, PHANI BHUSAN, O.B.E., Rai Bahadur late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 16th June, 1884).—Joined the serv., 1st Mar., 1906; asst. auditor genl., Dec., 1934; O.B.E., May, 1937; offer on spec. duty in the fin. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1937; retd., July, 1943.

CHAKRAVARTI, SURENDRA NATH, O.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., United Provs.) (b. 10th June, 1892).—Joined the service, 20th Sept., 1915; asst. engr., Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; municipal engr., Delhi, Dec., 1933, to Apl., 1939; M.B.E., Feb., 1937; confd. as suptg. engr., July, 1942; O.B.E., June, 1946.

CHAKRAVARTY, ASHWINI KUMAR, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1890).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1914; pers. asst. to contr. of currency, Dec., 1925; supt., Govt. of India fin. dept., Dec., 1928; asst. acct. genl., Bengal, Oct., 1936; dep. ditto, Oct., 1939; L.A.D., Bengal, Sept., 1940.

CHAKRAVARTY, BIRENDRA NARAYAN, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr. Bengal) (b. 20th Dec., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Calcutta, University Coll., London, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served as asst. collr. and mag., Bengal; jt. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1930; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1936; jt. sec. fin. dept., Apl., 1944; offg. sec., ditto, Nov., 1944; addl. sec. ch. minister's dept., Oct., 1945; offg. dir. of orgn. method and accom. Mar., 1946; O.B.E., June, 1945.

CHAKRAVARTY, NARENDRA NARAIN, Rai Bahadur, M.A., late Income Tax Dept., Bengal (b. Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1912; sub. dep. collr., Dec., 1913; inc. tax officer, Aug., 1923; asst. commr., July, 1929; retd., 16th Aug., 1943.

CHALAM, BALAJIPAT SESHAN, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 7th Nov., 1886).—Retd., Nov., 1941.†

CHAMBERS, DAVID GOSTLING, B.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt. Sind) (b. 19th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; under-sec. to govt. of India, def. dept., Apl., 1941; served in Bombay and Sind as collr. and dist. mag. var., March, 1942; sec. to govt. of Sind, agric. and indus. dept., April, 1946.

CHAMBERS, John, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Bengal) (b. 17th March, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1919;

exec. engr., Apl., 1926; O.B.E., June, 1935; suptg. engr., Oct., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

CHAMBERS, LESLIE RAPER, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 30th Apl., 1910).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; asst. finl. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1940, to Dec., 1942.

CHAMBERS, STANLEY PAUL, C.I.E., Bd. of Inland Revenue, on loan as income-tax adviser to the Central Bd. of Revenue, from Dec., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

CHAMIER, Miss ETHEL, O.B.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 19th Jan., 1885).—Retd., Jan., 1940.

CHAMPA LAL, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 1st Jan., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; offg. exec. engr., March, 1928; confd., Oct., 1932.

CHAMPHEKAR, MAHADEO LAXMAN, M.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Sind) (b. 6th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 7th Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., June, 1925, March, 1926, March, 1927, and Nov., 1927; confd., Oct., 1931; dep. secty, govt. of Sind, p.w.d., May, 1938; suptg. engr. (provl. permt.), Dec., 1943; confd. Dec., 1944.

CHAMPION, FREDERICK WALTER, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., United Provs.) (b. 24th Aug., 1893).—Joined the service, as asst. consvr., on 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927; consvr. (confd.), Aug. 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

CHAMPION, HARRY GEORGE, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 17th Aug., 1891).—Retd., 20th July, 1942; C.I.E. Jan., 1941.†

CHAMPION, REGINALD STUART, O.B.E. (b. 21st March, 1895).—On milty. service, 1914-1917. Served in Occupied Enemy Territories Administration; appointed Deputy Governor, Grade III, Hebron, Palestine, July, 1920; transferred to Beersheba, Apl., 1924; title changed to administrative officer, Jan., 1925; transferred to Jerusalem Division as asst. dist. commissioner, July, 1927; seconded for service as protectorate secretary, Aden, Oct., 1928; first asst. and protectorate secretary (now designated political sec.), Aden, March, 1932; offg. ch. commr., Aden, Apl., 1933; O.B.E., Jan., 1934.

CHANTOON, JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 4th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Nov., 1931.

CHANCE, PERCIVAL VINCENT, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Central Provs.) (b. 12th June, 1888).—Retd., June, 1943.†

CHANCE, WILLIAM HENRY, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 28th Feb., 1888).—Joined the service, 15th Feb., 1912; senr. asst. auditor, Nov., 1926; dep. ch. accts. officer, Nov., 1930; dep. agent, Burma rly, March, 1931; ch. accts. officer, March, 1934; retd., March, 1944.

CHAND, AJIT (see Ajit Chand).

CHAND, BISHEN (see Bishen Chand).

CHAND, AMIR (see Amir Chand).

CHAND, KARM (see Karm Chand).

CHAND, KHUB, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 16th Dec., 1911).—Educ. at Government High Schs., Delhi and Simla, Univ. of Delhi, and Ariel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 4th Sept., 1935; arrived in India,

Oct., 1935, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1937; under secty., govt. of India, def. dept., Apl., 1940, to Apl., 1941.

CHAND, KRISHAN, LL.M., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 30th Nov., 1917).—Educ. at Cantonment High Sch., Lahore, King's Coll., London, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd., on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 23rd Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

CHAND MAL, B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 7th Apl., 1911).—Retd., 5th Sept., 1942.†

CHAND, MUNISHWAR (see Munishwar Chand).

CHAND, PREM (see Prem Chand).

CHAND, SUMER (see Sumer Chand).

CHAND, TEK (see Tek Chand).

CHANDA, APURVA KUMAR, Indian Educl. Service (princip., presidency coll.) (b. 12th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1920; vice-prin., Dacca Coll., June, 1922; prof., presby. coll., Calcutta, Oct., 1926; offg. prin., Krishnagore Coll., March, 1930; confd., June, 1930; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, May, 1934; confd., Oct., 1934; princip., David Hare trng. coll., Nov., 1936; ditto, presby. coll., Apl., 1943.

CHANDA, ASOKA KUMAR, O.B.E., B.Sc., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 2nd Feb., 1902).—Joined the serv., 3rd Mar., 1926; dept. acct.-genl., p. and t., Nagpur, June, 1936; examr., local fund accts. with Govt. of Madras, Nov., 1936; examr., outside audit, Bengal, Feb., 1940; O.B.E., June, 1945.

CHANDAVARKAR, PROBHAKAR NARAYAN, C.I.E., B.A., LL.B., late Impl. Customs Service (b. 20th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as prob., 20th Jan., 1914; asst. collr., July, 1915; offg. collr., Sept., 1933; confd., Jan., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1942; ret., Aug., 1944.

CHANDIRAMANI, SANTDAS BULCHAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 1st Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, King's Coll., London; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927; and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1930; temp. sess. and sub-judge, Oct., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; confd., Jan., 1940; judl. sec. and legal rememb. to govt., Apl., 1945.

CHANDRA, CHAUDHURI NANAK, C.I.E., M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (commr., Punjab) (b. 19th Oct., 1896).—Educ. at Govt.
