High Sch., Mooltan, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Nov., 1921; arrived, 9th March, 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Punjab as asst. commr., Apl., 1923; dep. commr. (prov.), Oct., 1929; confd., Jan., 1934; junr. secty. to finl. commrs., Dec., 1931; dep. secty. to govt., rev. dept., Feb., 1934, to Oct., 1935; addtl. ditto, Nov., 1938; sec. to govt., fin. dept., Jan., 1942; C.I.E., June, 1945; commr., Nov., 1945.

CHANDRA, GANESH CHANDRA, A.I.I.A., Archaeological Survey Dept. (b. 1st Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 29th Apl., 1920; supt., Archl. Survey, Nov., 1927; supt., Southern Circle, July, 1937.

CHANDRA HARISH (see Harish Chandra).

CHANDRA KISHOR, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 9th Nov., 1886).—Retd., 11th Nov., 1941.

CHANDRA, RAM (see Ram Chandra).

CHANDRA, ROMESH (see Romesh Chandra).
CHANDRA, SATISH, B.Sc., M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 16th Oct., 1916).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ.; apptd. on probn., after exam. of 1940, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

CHANDRASEKHARA AYYAR, NAGAPUDI, Rao Bahadur, Judl. Dept., Madras (addl. judge, Madras high ct.) (b. 25th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as offg. city civil judge, Madras, 18th July, 1927; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; addtl. judge, Madras high ct. Jul., 1941.

CHANDUIAL, DONALD, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 28th June, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 29th Apl., 1927; confd., Apl., 1929; asst. I.G. rly. police, Apl., 1937; supt., spec. bch., C.I.D., Apl., 1939; confd. as supt., March, 1942; Indian police medal, Jan., 1943.

CHANDWANI, PESUMAL BHAGWANDAS, M.A., late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.), (b. 1st Feb., 1888).—Retd., Oct., 1945.†

CHANING PEARCE, WILLIAM ALAN, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 1st Nov., 1931; confd., Apl., 1934; King's Police Medal, Dec., 1946.

CHAPMAN, EDWARD FREDERIC GILBERT, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Aug., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., July, 1930.

CHAPMAN, HAROLD VICTOR, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 15th April, 1900).—Joined the service 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; ret., Oct., 1946.

CHAPMAN, HENRY, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 19th Oct., 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1940.

CHAPMAN, R. C. G., O.B.E., Maj. R. E., (deputy master, Security Printing) (b. 18th May, 1899).—Under mil. dept. up to 31st Aug., 1934; deputy master, Security Printing, Sept., 1934; O.B.E., June, 1942.

CHARAGH, MAHBUB ILAHI (see Mahbub Ilaahi Charagh).

CHARAN, ATMA, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, 7th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1934; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1936; under secty., govt. of India, ind. and lab. dept., Nov., 1936; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1938; confd., Jan., 1940.

CHARI, PERUNGAVUR VARADA, M.B.E., A.C.G.I., M.I.E.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., A.A.I.E.E. (b. 11th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. elec. engr. Madras, 1st Dec., 1918; elec. inspr., Feb., 1939; ch. elec. inspr. to govt. Oct., 1939; ret., Dec., 1942.

CHARI, RAJAGOPALA THIRUVENKATA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to rep. of govt. of India in Ceylon) (b. 30th Nov., 1913).—Educ. at Madras Univ.; joined the service, 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938; and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., 1939-43; agent of the govt. of India in Ceylon, Nov., 1943; sec. to the rep. of ditto, Sept., 1944.

CHARLTON, JAMES, M.Sc., F.I.C., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 11th June, 1894).—Joined the service as agricl. chemist, agricl. coll., Mandalay, 26th Jan., 1921; princip., ditto, in addn., Dec., 1922; offg. dir. of agricl., Burma,

Sept., 1935; confd., March, 1936; ret'd., 10th June, 1943.

CHARSLEY, CLAUDE ROBERT, O.B.E., late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 11th Feb., 1894).—Joined the service, 4th Nov., 1921, as asst. supt. on probn.; confd. July, 1924; offg. dist., supt., Oct., 1928; confd., March, 1932; awarded King's Police medal, Jan., 1934; Indian ditto, July, 1940; confd. as dep. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1945; ret'd., 1946.

CHATARJI, ABANIBHUSHAN, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 13th Jan., 1907).—Educ. at Patna Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934; addl. dist. mag., May, 1939; provl. organiser, nat. war front., Apl., 1942; M.B.E., 1944; mag. and collr. May, 1944.

CHATARJI, ANIL CHANDRA, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (dir. of pub. health, Bengal) (b. 21st Dec., 1891).—Date of commn., 17th Apl., 1919; in mil. employ up to 18th May, 1934; asst. dir. of public health, Delhi Prov., to Apl., 1936; dir., ditto, Bengal, June, 1936.

CHATARJI, GANESH CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (profr., govt. coll., Lahore) (b. 4th March, 1894).—Joined the service as profr., C.T. coll., Lahore, on 1st Dec., 1919; profr., govt. coll., Lahore, Sept., 1921; princpl., cent. trng. coll., July, 1939.

CHATARJI, SUKUMAR, M.B.E., M.A., Bengal C.S. (inspr.-genl. of regn.) (b. 16th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1908; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1911; inspr.-genl. of regn., Bengal, May, 1936.

CHATARJI, SURRENDRA NATH, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 31st Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 29th June, 1925; offg. supt., March, 1932; confd., Nov., 1937; King's police medal, 1937.

CHATARJI, SURESH CHANDRA, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 5th June, 1892).—Joined the service, 24th Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1917; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1925; confd., Jan., 1928; suptg. engr., March, 1940.

CHATTARJI, RANGA LAL, M.A., B.L., Bihar C.S. (asst. and sess. judge) (b. 9th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, 9th May, 1911; sub-judge, Aug., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1934; confd., Oct., 1934.

CHATTERJEE, AKHIL CHANDAR, O.B.E., B.A., Indian State Ryls. (divl. transportation officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1895).—Joined service, Feb., 1917; asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1919; junior scale, Aug., 1920; divl. transportation (traffic-commercial) officer, senior scale, Dec., 1937; O.B.E., June, 1939.

CHATTERJEE, SIR ATUL CHANDRA, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Nov., 1874); ret'd. Jan., 1925*; high commr. for India, 5th Jan., 1925, to 30th June, 1931; K.C.S.I., July, 1930; member of council of India, July, 1931, to July, 1936; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1933; advr. to sec. of state for India, Feb., 1942.

CHATTERJEE, BASANTA KUMAR, M.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 14th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service, 11th June, 1913; asst. acct.-genl., Bengal, May, 1914; ditto, Punjab, Jan., 1918; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, May, 1923; ditto, Calcutta, Jan., 1924; ditto, Punjab, March, 1925; ditto, Calcutta, Apl., 1926; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Jan., 1927; offg. dep. auditor-genl., June, 1930; dep. ch. auditor, E.I.

ry., Feb., 1932; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, March, 1933; comptr., Assam., Nov., 1933; and again, Sept., 1935; offg. acct.-genl., Burma, March, 1935; ditto, Central Provs., March, 1936; acctnt.-genl. (confd.), July, 1939; ch. auditor, E.I. rly. and govt. exr. accts., B.N. rly., Jan., 1940.

CHATTERJEE, RISWESWAR, Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 15th Aug., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., on probn., Feb., 1940.

CHATTERJEE, HRISHI KESH, Dept. of Mines (inspector) (b. 12th June, 1893).—Joined the service as junr. inspr. on probn., 29th Nov., 1925; confd., Aug., 1930. inspr. (confd.) July, 1944.

CHATTERJEE, NRITYA GOPAL, Dept. of Mines (inspr. of mines) (b. 15th Jan., 1896).—Apptd. junior inspr. of mines on probn., 14th July, 1923; confd., July, 1925; inspr. of mines, Apl., 1938.

CHATTERJEE, SANTOSH KUMAR, B.A. (Cal. and Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 12th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Calcutta, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1929; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1935; dir. in directorate of food, Jan., 1945; addl. sec. civil supply dept., Nov., 1945; dir.-genl. of food, Mar., 1946.

CHATTERJEE, SISIR CHANDRA, M.D., M.R.C.S., D.P.H., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 4th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as dist. surg., G.I.P. Rly., 28th March, 1918; offg. dep. C.M.O., May, 1929; ditto, G.I.P. Rly., May, 1931; offg. C.M.O., E.B. Rly., Apl., 1932; ditto, N.W. Rly., March, 1933; ditto, G.I.P. Rly., Sept., 1933; C.M.O., E.B. Rly., Nov., 1934; ditto, N.W. Rly., Feb., 1940; ret'd., Dec., 1943.

CHATTERJEE, SUBODH CHANDRA (judge, high ct., Patna).—Apptd., Sept., 1939.

CHATTERJEE, SURENDRA NATH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. Nov., 1888).—Joined the service, 10th March, 1910; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Nagpur, Sept., 1930; asst. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Nov., 1931; dep. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Dec., 1932; ditto, Bengal, Jan., 1934; exr., local audit dept., Bengal, Dec., 1935; dep. acct. genl., posts and tels., Calcutta, Oct., 1939.

CHATTERJI (see entries under Chatarji).

CHATTERTON, SIR ALFRED, C.I.E., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., late Indian Educ. Service (Madras).—Ret'd., Aug., 1918.*

CHATURVEDI, MANOHAR DAS (see Manohar, D. C.).

CHAUDHARI, KALYAN CHANDRA, M.A. (Cal.), Indian Railway Accts. Service (dep. ch. accts. officer) (b. 20th Jul., 1898).—Joined the service, Jan., 1929; accts. officer, Jan., 1930; dep. ch. accts. offr., G.I.P. rly.

CHAUDHARY, KEWAL SINGH, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1915).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Jaranwala, Lyall Pur., Punjab Univ. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 8th Sept., 1939; arrived India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

CHAUDHRI AKBAR HUSAIN (see Akbar Husain, Chaudhri).

CHAUDHURI, CHANDRA MOHAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bihar) (b. 26th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr.,

Bihar and Orissa, on 19th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., direction divn., May, 1944.

CHAUDHURI, HARI SADHAN GHOSH (see Ghosh Chaudhuri, H. S.).

CHAUDHURI, KUMUD RANJAN, M.B.E., M.A., Indian Police (supt. Assam) (b. 2nd Oct., 1901).—Joined the service, 11th May, 1925; in training to Apl., 1927; asst. supt., May, 1927; offg. supt., March, 1932, and again, Jan., 1933; confd., 1937; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

CHAUDHURI, KSHITISH CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1st Feb., 1901).—Joined the service, 14th March, 1925; dep. acct.-genl., B. and O., Apl., 1935; dep. compr., Assam, Nov., 1936; exmr., local fund accts., Central Provs., Dec., 1940.

CHAUDHURI, MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late Assam C.S. (b. 10th Nov., 1886).—Retd., Nov., 1941.†

CHAUDHURI, PRABHAT CHANDRA, O.B.E., Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir. of sericulture, Bengal) (b. 9th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service, 10th Oct., 1913; dep. dir. of sericulture, Oct., 1922; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

CHAUDHURI, PRAPHULLA CHANDRA, M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 14th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1916; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, Apl., 1918; ditto, Bengal, Feb., 1924; asst. auditor, E.B. rly., Dec., 1925; ditto, N.W. rly., June, 1927; dep. auditor (constn. bch.), N.W. rly., July, 1927; audit and accts. officer, Bombay dev. scheme, Apl., 1929; accts. officer, G.I.P. rly., March, 1930; ditto, E.B. rly., Apl., 1931; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts. office, Apl., 1933; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, E.B. rly., Jan., 1935; dep. ch. accts. offcr., E.I. rly., May, 1936; comptr., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1937; ch. auditor, N.W. rly., Oct., 1939; acct. genl., Bihar, May, 1940; ch. accts. offcr., E.I. rly., Nov., 1941; confd. as acct.-genl. (cl. I), Aug., 1944.

CHAUDHURI, PRASANTA CHANDRA, C.I.E., M.A. (Punjab), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 21st Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab, Sch. of Oriental Studies, London, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1928; arrived, 9th Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1936; dep. contr. of broadcasting, govt. of India, Aug., 1940; addtl. dep. ch. contr., imports, central govt., June, 1942; dep. ditto, Jan., 1943; jt. ch. contr., Dec., 1944; spec. offr., office high commr., for India, May, 1945; C.I.E., June, 1945.

CHAUDHURI, SIKUMAR, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 2nd Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 14th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

CHAUDHURI, SURATH NATH (see Surath Nath, C.).

CHAUDHURI, TAJAL ISLAM MUHAMMAD NURANNABI (see Tajal I. M. N. Chaudhuri).

CHAUDHURI, TAPENDRA KUMAR GHOSH (see Ghosh Chaudhuri, T. K.).

CHAUNCY, FREDERICK CHARLES LESLIE, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Dec., 1904).—First commn., 27th Aug., 1924; in milty. employ until 12th Feb., 1930; under sec. to A.G.G. in C.I., Feb., 1931; under sec. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf; vice-consul, Mohammedah, Nov., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Zohb, May, 1937; sec. to resdt. for Baroda and the Gujarat States, Apl., 1939; ditto for Central India, Oct., 1940; asst. com-

mandant, Malwa Bhil Corps and ex-officio asst. to pol. agent in Malwa, May, 1941; sec. to the resdt., Central India, July, 1941; H.B.M.'s consul, Khorramshahr, March, 1942; sec. to resdt., Punjab States, June, 1943; pol. agt., Chhattisgarh States, Nov., 1945.

CHAVE, ELMER HARGREAVES, C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engineers (Madras) (b. 10th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 14th Apl., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; ch. engr., Nov., 1940; served under cent. govt. for duty on Indus commn., Sept., 1941, to July, 1942; C.I.E., June, 1944; ret'd., 1946.

CHAVES, HENRY JOSEPH, late Indian Posts and Telegraphs (b. 8th Oct., 1883).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

CHEEMA, SARDAR GANDASING (see Gandasing, C. S.).

CHEESMAN, GEORGE MAURICE, M.B.E., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 16th May, 1876).—Retd., May, 1941.

CHEIN, U. AUNG, B.A., Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt. police, Burma, 21st Apl., 1928; confd., Apl., 1930; offg. dist. supt., Aug., 1934; confd., ditto, June, 1941; in enemy hands 1942-5; resumed duty under govt. of Burma, Nov., 1945; dist. supt., 1945.

CHEVENIX-TRENCH, CHARLES POCKLINGTON, M.C., Capt., I.A.—Under sec. to resdt. for Central India, May, 1946.

CHENEVIX-TRENCH, SIR RICHARD HENRY Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), (b. 4th Apl., 1876).—Retd., Apl., 1931.*

CHENGAPPA, KETOLI, C.I.E., DIWAN BAHADUR, B.A., (ch. commr. of Coorg) (b. 3rd March, 1878).—Joined the service, May, 1899; asst. commr. Coorg, 1916; Rao Sahib, 1919; dist. mag., Coorg, 1921; Rao Bahadur, 1924; sec. Coorg Legis. Council, 1924; offg. commr., Coorg, 1929; and 1934; Diwan Bahadur, 1934; ret'd., May, 1935; ch. commr., Coorg, Apl., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1946.

CHENGAPPA, P. M., RAO SAHIB, (Elec. depl., Madras) (b. 24th June, 1900).—Joined as asst. engr., May, 1929; exec. engr., Nov. 1937; suptg. engr., Apl., 1946.

CHETPAT, PATTABHERAMA RAMASWAMI AYYAR (see Ramaswami Ayyar, Sir C. P.).

CHETTLE, GEORGE, O.B.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl. Burma) (b. 18th June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., on 19th Feb., 1921; offg. dist. supt., June, 1926; confd., July, 1927; awarded King's police medal, June, 1932; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; civ. aff. serv. to Dec., 1945; inspr.-genl., Dec., 1945.

CHETTUR, KRISHNA KRISHNA, M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 10th March, 1901).—Joined the service, 5th March, 1925; currency officer, Rangoon, Oct., 1930; ditto, Calcutta, Apl., 1933; under-secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1937; 1st secty. to cent. bd. rev., and ex-officio dep. secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1940; dep. sec. ditto, commerce dept., Aug., 1944; on deptn. to China, Feb. to Apl., 1946; jt. sec., govt. of India, commerce dept., Mar., 1946.

CHETTUR MADHAVAN NAYAR (see Nayar, C. Madhavan).

CHETTUR, SANKARA KRISHNA, Indian C.S. (rep. of govt. of India in Malaya) (b. 20th May, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct. 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, sub.-collr., 1929 to 1942; collr. 1942 to 1945; agt. to govt.

of India in Malaya, Sept., 1945; rep. of ditto, and liaison offr. with S.E.A.C., Nov., 1945.

CHETWODE, Baron (Sir Philip WALHOUSE) G.C.B., O.M., G.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., D.S.O., D.C.L., Field-Marshal (b. 21st Sept., 1869).—C.-in-C. in India and member govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Nov., 1930, to Nov., 1935.*

CHEYNE, GEORGE COLLIE, M.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Public Works Dept., Burma (b. 1st. Jan. 1887).—Retd., Feb., 1943.†

CHIDELL, JOHN WALLACE PHILIP, M.A., India Office (principl., public and jud. dept.) (b. 26th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. princpl., 21st Dec., 1933; resdt. clk., Oct., 1935; pte. accty. to permnt. under-secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1937; acting princpl., Oct., 1938; confd., Feb., 1939; lent to min. of food, June, 1940; ditto, min. of home security, July, 1941; sec. to Allied Contr. Commn., Feb., 1945.

CHINMULGUND, PANDURANG JAIRO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 20th Oct., 1913).—Educ. at New English Sch., Poona, Bombay Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 12th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; regr. co-operative societies, Bombay, Mar., 1946.

CHIRRAVOORIBALKRISHNA, RAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 15th July, 1914).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ.; apptd. on probn., 2nd Oct., 1937; confd., 29th Oct., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., infm., and hony. secty., United Provs. soldiers' bd., Dec., 1939.

CHISHOLM, DONALD MALCOLM FRANCIS, O.B.E., V.D. late Indian State Rlys. (B.E. Rly.) (b. 23rd Oct., 1894).—Joined the service 17th Sept., 1914; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., March, 1930; dep. ch. comml. manager (provl.) April, 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1945; ret'd., July, 1945.

CHIT, U BA, B.A., B.L., late Burma Judl. Service (b. 23rd Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1912; extra asst. commr., Jan., 1924; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1935 to Oct., 1940; ret'd., Aug., 1945.

CHITHAM, SIR CHARLES CARTER, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1886).—Retd., Sept., 1941.†

CHITHAM, JOHN ROWLAND, Indian Police (b. 21st Nov., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 24th Nov., 1935; confd., 1938.

CHO, U, K.S.M. B.A., Burma Educl. Serv. (b. 25th May, 1896).—Joined the service 10th Jan., 1923; apptd. to Burma Educl. Serv., (Cl. I) 24th July, 1930; addl. asst. dir. public instrn., Dec., 1940; joined civ. aff. serv., Sept., 1945; asst. dir. public instrn., 16th Oct., 1945.

CHOPRA, AMAR NATH, O.B.E., M.B., B.S., D.T.M., D.P.H., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (addl. asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S.) (b. 18th May, 1899).—First commn. dated 2nd March, 1930; in mil. employ to 7th Oct., 1935; P.A. to surg. genl. with govt., Bombay, 8th Oct., 1935; asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S., 1st Apl., 1938; offcr. i.c. medl. store dept., Lahore cantt., Apl., 1939; att'd. C.P. jail dept., July, 1939; supt., central jail, Jubbulpore, Sept., 1939; addl. asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S., March, 1940; O.B.E., June, 1945.

CHOPRA, AMAR AATH, B.A., A.M.I.E., Indian Service of Engineers (suptg. engr.) (b. 15th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 4th Oct., 1915; asst. engr., Oct., 1916; on mil.

duty with Indian D.F., 1st Aug. to 18th Oct., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. ditto, confd., Oct., 1939; on depn. to cent. P.W.D., govt. of India, Sept., 1942; suptg. engr. cent. P.W.D., July, 1943.

CHOPRA, B. N., Indian State Rlys. (b. 7th Sept., 1895).—Dep. ch. engr., B and A. rly., Nov., 1945.

CHOPRA, INDER SEN, O.B.E., Capt., I.A. (b. 7th Sept., 1909).—Apptd. on probn., to I.P.S. from mil. employ, Jan., 1937; apptd. asst. pol. agt., Loralai Feb., 1937; dir., food supplies and dep. sec. revenue for Baluchistan, March, 1943; sec. to A.G.G. in Baluchistan, May, 1946; O.B.E., June, 1946.

CHOPRA, PRATAP CHANDRA, B.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 24th Feb., 1895).—Retd. May, 1940.

CHOPRA, SIR RAM NATH, C.I.E., M.B., Bt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 17th Aug., 1882).—Retd., Aug., 1939*; kt., Jan., 1941.

CHOWDHARY, GOPI NATH SINGH, M.B., B.Sc., F.R.C.S., (Edin.), United Provs. Medl. Service (b. 15th Dec., 1891).—Apptd., 1st Aug., 1919; civil surgeon (confd.) 21st July, 1939.

CHOWDHURY, KHEM CHANDRA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 25th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at D.A.V. High Sch., Lahore, Punjab Univ., and London Sch. of Economics; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CHOWDHURY, DR. K. A., M.B.E., (b. Feb., 1902).—Joined the service as offcr. wood technologist, Forest Research Inst., Dehra Dun, 1st June, 1927; in training in U.S.A., from Aug., 1927; wood technologist (temp.) Oct., 1929, ditto, (confd.) March, 1934; M.B.E., 1945.

CHRISTIE, ANTHONY HERBERT, Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (b. 7th July, 1921).—Educ. at Wrekin Coll., Salop; apptd., on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

CHRISTIE, WALTER HENRY JOHN, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dep. sec. govt. of India (food)) (b. 17th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., offg. under secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and appts. depts., Aug., 1931; confd., Feb., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1936; under secty., govt. of India, dept. of communications, Dec., 1937; dep. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Sept., 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; dep. secty., govt. of India (food), Feb., 1943; jt. sec., ditto, Nov., 1945; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

CHRISTIE, WILLIAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.C., Indian C.S. (ch. commr., Delhi) (b. 29th Feb., 1896).—Educ. at St. Andrew's Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1920; arrived, 29th Dec., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., May, 1924; asst. commr., March, 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1926; offg. dep. commr., Delhi, Apl. to Nov., 1928; secty., bd. of rev., United Provs., Nov., 1929; offg. addl. dep. secty., govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Oct., 1932; dep. secty. to the govt. of India, finance dept., Apl., 1933; mag. and collr. (prov. subs.), Apl., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; offg. commr., N.I. salt rev., Apl., 1936, to Oct., 1937; finance secty., United Provs., May, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; confd. as ch. secty., govt. United Provs., July, 1944; C.S.I., Jan., 1945; ch. commr., Delhi, Sept., 1945.

CHRISTOPHERS, SIR SAMUEL RICHARD Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., Bt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 27th Nov., 1873).—Retd., Nov., 1930.*

CHUDASAMA, MANSINHJI MERUJI, LL.B., Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 25th Apl., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 24th Dec., 1929; confd., Apl., 1931; confd. as supt., Sind, Dec., 1940; served under cent. govt. from Nov., 1943; sec. and exec. offr., R.T.A., N.D., Nasik, Dec., 1945; dist. supt., March, 1946; sec. to exec. offr., R.T.A., Ahmedabad, April, 1946.

CHUNDER (see Chandra).

CHUNDER, KAMAL CHUNDER, B.A. (Calc.), B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Assam) (b. 4th Jan., 1895).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1919; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1919, and posted to Bengal; as asst. mag. and collr., services placed temp. under govt. of Assam, Dec., 1924; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Dec. 1924; offg. ditto, Bengal, Apl., 1927; confd., July, 1931.

CHUNI LAL, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., LL.B., Punjab C.S., Judicial Bch. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 22nd June, 1883).—Joined the service as proby. extra. asst. commr., 24th Jan., 1906; confd., Sept., 1910; sub-judge, Oct., 1912; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1921; confd., Oct., 1932; commr. for workmen's compensation (confd.), Nov., 1941.

CHUNI LAL, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, late Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 8th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th May, 1909; dep. supt., Dec., 1913; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Apl., 1933; ret'd., 7th Apl., 1943.

CHUNI LAL, HANDA, RAI BAHADUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr. Punjab) (b. 2nd Feb., 1907).—Joined the service as app. engr., 21st Jan., 1929; proby. asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1930; confd., Jan., 1933; under-secty. (constrn.) and dir. cent. designs, March, 1944; Rai Bahadur, Jan., 1945; on depn. to U.S.A., Jan., to Oct., 1945; again dir., cent. designs, Oct., 1945.

CHURCHER, JOHN EDWARD GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 29th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa. Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 18th Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Feb., 1933; confd. Aug., 1938; awarded King's police medal, June, 1946.

CHURCHILL, EDWARD ARCHIBALD HICKLING, O.B.E., B.Sc., late Indian Agricultural Service (b. 15th December, 1889).—Apptd. asst. direc. of agric., 31st Jan., 1922; dep. dir. of agric., January, 1930; offg. princpl., agric. coll., Nagpur, Nov., 1935; confd., Sept., 1938; O.B.E., June, 1945; on I.p.r. from May, 1946.

CINNA SAMUDRAM SESHAGIRI RAO HANUMANTHA RAO, RAO SAHIB, B.A. (inspr. of mun. cls., etc.) (b. 2nd May, 1884).—Apptd. dep. collr., 13th Aug., 1920; asst. secty., bd. of rev., Nov. to Dec., 1932; dep. inspr. of mun. councils and local bds., Sept., 1937, to March, 1938; inspr. ditto, Apl., 1938.

CLAGUE, SIR JOHN, C.M.G., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (adviser to sec. of state for Burma) (b. 29th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and as settl. offr.; offg. dep. dir., land records, Nov., 1916; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; on mil. duty, May, 1918, to Feb., 1919; dir. of agric., Burma, Nov., 1920; dep. commr.,

July, 1921; addl. sec. to govt., F.R. Dept. (in addn.), Nov., 1923; confd., July, 1924; offg. ch. secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., Apl., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; offg. commr., May, 1931; confd., Aug., 1933; ret'd., March, 1937; adviser to sec. of state for Burma, Apl., 1937; to March, 1942; C.M.G., June, 1938; kt., June, 1938.

CLARE, REV. HEBER, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 16th Feb., 1933.

CLARENCE SMITH, JOHN ASHTON, Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 8th March, 1917).—Educ. at Rugby and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 9th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

CLARK, ALLAN McDONALD, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 16th Oct., 1895).—Educ. at Keith Gr. Sch. and Aberdeen, and Oxford (Non-Coll.) Universities; apptd. after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the prov. of Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and sub-divl. officer, Nov., 1923; addl. mag. and collr., March, 1925; on spl. duty, rev. dept., Nov., 1925, to March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1927; confd., Nov., 1932; dep. sec., pol. and apptt. depts., Bengal, March, 1932; on mil. duty to 1946; ret'd. March, 1946.

CLARK, ARTHUR STIRLING, M.A., Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.) (b. 3rd Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh, and at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. junior clerk, store dept., 19th Feb., 1920; war service—European War, Oct., 1914, to Apl., 1919; transf'd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; services lent to Colonial Office, Apl. to Dec., 1921; principal exec. officer, Apl., 1922; contr. (temp.), Apl., 1927; actg. dep. dir. inspection, Jan., 1940.

CLARK, FRANK KYNOC, M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th March, 1885).—Retd., March, 1940.

CLARK, GEORGE EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 6th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Sandown (I. of W.) Secondary Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; addtl. under-secty., def. dept., July, 1940; under sec. ditto, March, 1941; dep. sec., fin. and rev. dept., April, 1946.

CLARK, PHILIP ROY, Burma Police (Cl. I.) (b. 21st Mar., 1921).—Apptd. on probn., after seln., Aug., 1946.

CLARK, REGINALD (judge, high ct., Madras).—Apptd., July, 1944.

CLARK, THOMAS JOHN, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 26th Feb., 1895).—Retd., June, 1938.*

CLARK, THOMAS VINSON, Indian Police (b. 6th May, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, Nov., 1932; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; on spl. duty, inspr.-gen'l's office, 1946.

CLARK, SIR WILLIAM HENRY, G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I.—Member of council of govt.-gen'l. of India, commerce and industry dept., Nov., 1910, to Apl., 1916.*

CLARK, WILLIAM MORRISON, B.Sc., M.B.E., Indian Agricl. Service (b. 23rd May, 1895).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, on 3rd Apl., 1923; M.B.E., June, 1918; services transferred to Govt. of Bengal from Feb., 1938.

CLARKE, REV. ARTHUR, Indian Eccles. Establishment (junr. chaplain, United Provs.) (b. 13th March, 1901).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 9th Jan., 1937; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1939.

CLARKE, E. W., late Income Tax Dept., (b. 12th June, 1888).—Apptd. to income tax dept., central board of revenue, 7th March, 1919; asst. commr., Western range, Madras, March, 1924; offg. commr. variously from Dec., 1928; confd., March, 1937; ret'd., May, 1943.

CLARKE, SIR GEOFFREY ROTHER, Kt., C.S.I., O.B.E., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Provs.).—Ret'd., Jan., 1926.*

CLARKE, MATTHEW JOHN, Maj., C.I.E., late I.A. (b. 19th July, 1895).—First commn. dated 1st December, 1914; asst. commr., July, 1921; sec. to finl. commr., 11th Nov., 1926; offg. dep. commr. May, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1932; chairman board of trustees for development of City of Rangoon, Aug., 1934; dep. commr., Nov., 1934; offg. commr., Jan., 1941; commr., Feb., 1941; regr. Indian mil. hosp., Shillong, 1945.

CLARKE, VEN. MAURICE, M.A., late, Indian Ecclesl. Estabt. (b. 21st Jan., 1893).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, Madras, 15th Oct., 1927; junior chaplain, Oct., 1929; senr. ditto, Oct., 1939; canon, 1939; archdeacon of Madras, 1940; ret'd., Aug., 1946.

CLARKE, MAURICE NOBLE, Barr., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1891).—Ret'd., Sept., 1942.†

CLARKE, SIR REGINALD, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Police Service (Bengal).—Ret'd., June, 1925.*

CLARK, THOMAS HERBERT, M.B.E., Burma Engrg. Serv., (b. 9th Feb., 1903).—Joined the service, 22nd Feb., 1929; confd. in Burma engrg. serv., June, 1932; exec., engr. Apl., 1937.

CLARKSON, WILLIAM M.B.E., India Office (hr. clerical officer, econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 10th May, 1899).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1920, after previous service from Apl., 1917; hr. clerical officer, Sept., 1936; M.B.E., June, 1942; actg. staff offcr., July, 1942.

CLAUSON, MILES JOHN, B.A., India Office (actg. asst. secty., pol. (states) dept.) (b. 29th March, 1902).—Educ. at Eton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; secty.'s office, G.P.O., Oct., 1925 to Dec., 1926; asst. princpl., India Office, Jan., 1927; resdt. clerk, 1st Oct., 1927; asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state, Dec., 1928; actg. princpl., Jan., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; pte. secty. to secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1937, to Nov., 1941; princpl. pol. dept., Nov., 1941; loaned to Treasury, Nov., 1943, to Nov., 1944; actg. asst. sec., pol. (states) dept., Dec., 1944.

CLAY, SIR JOSEPH MILES, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 6th Sept., 1881).—Ret'd., Aug., 1937; adviser to sec. of state for India, Aug., 1937 to July, 1942.†

CLAYDON, REV. EVAN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 15th May, 1898) (junr. chaplain, Punjab).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 27th March, 1928; junr. chaplain, March, 1929.

CLAYTON, SIR HUGH BYARD, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Dec., 1877).—Ret'd., Dec., 1936.†

CLAYTON, RICHARD HENRY MICHAEL, B.A., I.C.S. (asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 11th Aug., 1907).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., Christ Church, Oxford, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after the exam. of

1931; arrived in India, Jan., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Apl., 1939; on depn., govt. of India, def. dept., Sept., 1939.

CLAYTON, ROBERT VICKERS, M.B., B.S., D.P.H., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 18th Dec., 1887).—Ret'd. Oct., 1942.

CLEAR, THOMAS, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 17th June, 1886).—Ret'd., Nov., 1939.*

CLEARY, DENIS MACKROW, M.A., India Office (b. 20th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at St. Ignatius Coll., Stamford Hill, and St. Olave's and St. Saviour's Gr. Sch., St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. princpl., 19th Oct., 1931; resdt. clerk, Sept., 1934; pte. secty. to permit under-secty. of state, Aug., 1936, to Oct., 1937; actg. princpl., Oct., 1937; confd., Jan., 1939; lent to min. of huome security, June, 1940 to Sept., 1944; tfd. on loan to Control Off., Germany and Austria, Oct., 1946.

CLEARY, MICHAEL FRANCIS, Indian Police (dep. dir., intell. bureau) (b. 4th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., on 4th Dec., 1914; confd., Nov., 1916; addl. supt., June, 1922; pers. assistant to dir. intell. bureau, govt. of India, Nov., 1923; supt., Dec., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; cent. intell. offcr., Bengal and Assam, Nov., 1935; on depn. to Imp. Defence Coll., Jan., 1938; dep. dir., intel. bureau, March 1939 to Jan., 1941; asst. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1942, to Jan., 1943.

CLEE, CHARLES BEAUPRE BELL, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (secty., rev. dept., Govt. of Sind) (b. 5th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Fitzwilliam Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1919; arrived, 28th Nov., 1919, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; actg. dir. of information, March, 1924; sec., tariff bd., 1st June, 1925; offg. dep. secty. to govt., finance dept., Bombay, Apl., 1928; confd., Dec., 1928; offg. secty. to govt. of Bombay, home and eccl. depts., May, 1932; member, legisl. council, Oct., 1932; offg. secty. to govt. of Bombay, fin. dept., June, 1933, again Jan., 1936; collr. and mag., June, 1935; secty., fin. dept., Sind, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938; ch. sec. to govt., Sind, June, 1938 to Jan., 1939; offg. rev. commr. and sec. to govt. (rev.), Jan., 1943; C.S.I., June, 1946.

CLEOBURY, F. H., Burma Office.—Apptd. princpl., 3rd June, 1946.

CLERICI, MARIO CLAUDE, Indian Police (b. 8th Nov., 1914).—Asst. supt., Bihar, apptd. on probn., 11th Nov., 1935; confd. Sept., 1937; asst. to d.i.g., April, 1945.

CLIFF, ARNOLD PEARSE, B.A., Indian Agric. Service (b. 3rd Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 5th Oct., 1920; secty., Indian Central Jute Ctee., Dec., 1936; addtl. dir. of agric., July, 1944.

CLIFFORD, REGINALD CHARLES, D.S.O., M.C., F.R.C.S.E., M.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., Medical Dept., United Provs. (b. 17th Sept., 1884); ret'd. Aug., 1939.*

CLIFFORD, WILLIAM WALTER, late Indian Police, Bombay (b. 26th Sept., 1887).—Ret'd., March, 1941.

CLIFT, GEORGE, B.Sc., Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 9th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 19th Aug., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1933; confd. Oct., 1938; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; again dist. supt., 1946.

CLINTON-THOMAS, ROBERT ANTONY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 5th Apl.,

1913).—Educ. at Haileybury and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; attaché, ext. affairs dept., Sept., 1941; vice-consul, Kerman, Dec., 1941; under sec. to resdt., E. States, Feb., 1944; I.P.S. (confd.) Mar., 1945; asst. pol. agent, Chhattisgarh States, July, 1945.

CLIVE, JOHN M'CINTOCK, Lieut.-Col. M. C., Mil. accts. dept. (b. 20th Oct., 1891).—First commn., Apr., 1915; joined service as an offcr., cl. II, Aug., 1930; ret'd., 1946.

CLOSE, ERNEST REGINALD WARD, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 4th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1921; asst. supt., July, 1923; offg. dist. supt., May, 1928; confd., July, 1930.

CLOUGH, ARTHUR ROBERT, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.), (b. 28th Oct., 1891).—Ret'd., 4th Apr., 1941.†

CLOW, SIR ANDREW GOURLAY, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., F.S.S., Indian C.S. (actg. govr. of Bombay) (b. 29th Apr., 1890).—Educ. at Merchiston Castle Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd., after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1914, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. record offr. and asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1917; dep. commr. (prov.), and settlt. offr., Sept., 1918, to June, 1919; sec. to Disorders Enquiry Ctee., Feb., 1920; controller, labour bureau, May, 1920; under-sec. to Govt. of India, dept. of industries, Apr., 1923; dep. sec., ditto, Aug., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; sec. ditto (temp.), Apr., 1928; adviser and substitute delegate, internl. labour conference, Geneva, May-June, 1929; member of R.C. on labour in India, July, 1929; on spl. duty in dept. of indus. and labour, Oct., 1931; jt. secty., ditto, Nov., 1931; offg. secty., ditto, June, 1935; and again, Jan., 1936; C.S.I., June, 1935; confd. as secty. to govt. of India, indus. and lab. dept., July, 1936; member, council of state, Feb., 1936; secty. dept. of lab., Nov., 1937; temp. member, govr.-genl's. exec. council, May-Sept., 1938; confd. Apr., 1939; Kt., June, 1939; pres. war transport bd., July, 1940; K.C.S.I., June, 1941; govr. of Assam, May, 1942; actg. govr. of Bombay, Aug., 1946.

CLUTTERBUCK, JACK ERNEST, Lt.-Col. R.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. engr. G.I.P. rly.) (b. 8th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1918; asst. exec. engr. Khyber Rly. Survey and Construction, Aug., 1919; asst. exec. engr. E.I. Rly., Apr., 1921; provl. perm. exec. engr., G.I.P. Rly., May, 1939; on mil. duty, Dec., 1939; confd. as exec. engr., March, 1940; dep. ch. engr., Dec., 1943; ch. engr., May, 1946.

CLUTTERBUCK, SIR PETER HENRY, Kt., C.I.E., C.B.E., F.Z.S., F.R.G.S., V.D., late Indian Forest Service.—Ret'd., Oct., 1926.*

CLYDE, DAVID, C.I.E., M.B., D.P.H., Col. I.M.S. (b. 30th March, 1894).—First commn., dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ to 12th Feb., 1924; asst. dir. of public health, Feb., 1924; civil surg., Nov., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; inspr. gen. civil hospitals, May, 1944.

COATES, CHARLES LESLIE, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (under-sec. govt. of India supply dept.) (b. 1st Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. regr. co-op. societies, March-May, 1937; under-sec., govt. of India, communications dept., Feb.,

1940; dep. sec. ditto, war transport., July, 1942; dep. sec. ditto, supply, June, 1944; O.B.E., June, 1946.

COATES, SIR ERIC THOMAS, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (b. 1st Oct., 1897).—Educated at Heath Grammar Sch., Halifax and Queen's College Oxford; joined the service 28th Oct., 1921; arrived 3rd Dec., 1921, and posted to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and sub. divl. officer, April, 1923; on spl. duty in finl., commerce and marine depts., Nov., 1925, to March, 1926; offg. under-sec. in ditto, March, 1926; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Nov., 1928; ditto, central revenues, Apr., 1929; offg. acct.-genl., Bombay, July, 1930; accts. offr., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, Nov., 1931; offg. dep. contr. of currency, Calcutta, Feb., 1932; offg. acct.-gen., Central Provs., Oct., 1933; ditto, Bombay, Apr., 1934; ditto, posts and tels., May, 1934; on spl. duty, fin. dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1935; jt. sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, Apr., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1938; fin. adviser, mily. fin., Apr., 1939; acct.-genl. (confd.), cl. II., Aug., 1940; acct.-genl., cl. I., (prov.), Feb., 1941; C.S.I., June, 1941; kt., Jan., 1945; sec. co-ordination ctee. of council, Oct., 1945; sec. executive council, Jan., 1946; member exec. council, govr.-genl. July to Sept., 1946.

COATES, WILLIAM JOSEPH, B.A., late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 21st Aug., 1885).—Ret'd., 21st Aug., 1941.†

COBB, EVELYN HEY, O.B.E., Maj., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 15th Sept., 1899).—First commn., dated 15th Apr., 1919; in milty. employ prior to 15th June, 1921; pers. asst. to ch. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1924; asst. collr., Agra, Dec., 1925; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Nov., 1927; supy. asst. commr., Peshawar, June, 1929; city mag., ditto, May, 1930; asst. commr., Charsadda, Dec., 1931; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1935; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, March, 1937; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1939; first asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Sept., 1945; homé sec. to govt. N.W.F.P., July, 1946.

COBURN, MARMADUKE ROBERT, C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., India Posts and Teles. (b. 9th March, 1885).—Joined the service as milty. acctn., 11th March, 1910; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; dep. finl. adviser, May, 1920; offg. finl. adviser, July, 1925; offg. dir. of establishment, Nov., 1916; finl. advr., April, 1928; reverted to milty. accts, dept., April, 1933; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1935; mil. acct. genl., April, 1937; C.I.E., June, 1939; addl. fin. adviser mil. fin., Oct., 1939, and again Sept., 1944; on depn. to U.K., Apr., 1944; C.S.I., June, 1945.

COCHRANE, HON. SIR ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS, G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., D.S.O., Commander, R.N., ret'd. (b. 8th Jan., 1885).—European War, 1914-1918 (desp. thrice, D.S.O. and bar); M.P. for East Fife, 1924-1929; M.P., Dumfries and Galloway, 1932-1936; K.C.S.I., March, 1936; govr. of Burma, May, 1936 to May, 1941; G.C.M.G., Apr., 1937.

COCHRANE, GEORGE ALFRED DAVIES, O.B.E., B.Sc., A.M.I. Struct. E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 10th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Central Provs., 26th Sept., 1924; exec. engr. and under-secty. to govt. p.w.d., Sept., 1935; suptg. engr., Apr.-June, 1941; supt. of works, May, 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; dep. consultg. engr., govt. of India, war transp. dept., March, 1946.

COCK, CHARLES MATTHEW, late Indian

State Railways, (b. 27th January, 1895).—Apptd. traction engr., distribn., G.I.P. Rly., 21st Oct., 1929; traction supt., Apl., 1941; ret'd., Jan., 1945.

COCKBURN, ARTHUR BALCOMBE, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 23rd Aug., 1885).—Ret'd., July, 1940.†

COCKBURN, GEORGE WILLIAM MACLOY, M.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I.) (dep. commr.) b. 27th May, 1913).—Educ. at Fettes and St. Andrew's Univ.; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.; asst. supt., Shan States, May, 1940; on mil. service, Nov., 1940 to Mar., 1946; dep. commr., 1945.

COCKS, EDWARD JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 31st July, 1916).—Educ. at Leys School, Cambridge, and Christ's College, Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

COEN, TERENCE BERNARD CREAHE, C.I.E., Indian C.S., Indian Political Serv., (b. 31st Mar., 1903).—Educ. at Uppingham and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-secty., local self-govt. dept., Dec., 1932; reg. high ct., Lahore, Nov., 1933; under-secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Oct., 1935; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar Agency, March, 1936; secty. to the Resident at Hyderabad, Oct., 1937; confd. in the pol. service, Feb., 1938; secty. to resident for Rajputana and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Apr., 1941; on deputation, 1941; pol. agent Gujarat, 5th Nov., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

COFFEY, THOMAS MALO, C.I.E., Indian For. Service (dep. consr. Bengal) (b. 4th May 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1922; C.I.E., June, 1946.

COGHILL, DUNCAN JOSEPH KNIGHT, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 25th Apr., 1903).—Educ. at Cheltenham and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr.; jt. mag., June, 1929; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Nov., 1930; asst. pol. agent, Zhob, Nov., 1932; offg. under-secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., March, 1934; offg. dep. secty., ditto, May, 1934 to Apl., 1935; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1935; confd. as mag. and collr., Nov., 1941.

COGHLAN, JOHN ALOYSIUS, B.A., M.C., late Indian Police Service (Bombay) (b. 16th Jan., 1886).—Ret'd., Dec., 1940.

COHEN, WILLIAM CHARLES HENRY, late India Office (higher clerical officer, acctt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 28th Oct., 1896).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital; apptd. second class clerk, 21st Jan., 1909, and att'd. to acctt.-gen.'s dept.; higher clerical officer, Apr., 1922; actg. hr. exec. offr., Jan., 1941; ret'd., July, 1943.

COKE-WALLIS, LEONARD GEORGE, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Ind. Political Service (b. 12th March, 1900).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1924; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1924, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and appts. depts., May, 1929; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Jan., 1930; under-secty. to C.C. and agent to G.G., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1930; secty. to A.G.G., Eastern States, April, 1933; vice-presdt., State Council, Cooch-Behar, Nov., 1936; sec. to

resdt., Central India, Nov., 1939; pol. agent, Bhopal, Feb., 1940; sec. and excise asst. to res. C.I., Apl., 1940, and again Apl., 1941; pol. agt., Malwa, Apl., 1940; sec. to res. Hyderabad, July, 1941; pol. agt., Chhattisgarh States, July, 1943; offg. res. for Eastern states, June, 1943, and again, Feb., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1945; dep. sec., pol. dept., Feb., 1945; pol. agt., Punjab Hill States, Sept., 1945.

COLDMAN, REV. ALFRED THEODORE, B.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt., Madras (b. 3rd July, 1884).—Ret'd., July, 1939.*

COLDSTREAM, SIR JOHN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 23rd Dec., 1877).—Ret'd., Dec., 1937.*

COLE, FRANCIS NORMAN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 29th July, 1906).—Educ. at Dulwich College, Trinity College, Oxford, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; pte. secty. to govt., Central Provs., Apr., 1934 to Nov., 1935.

COLE, GEORGE ASHMEAD, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 1st Feb., 1902).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; asst. commr., Kohat, 8th Apl., 1931; asst. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Bahrain, March, 1935; asst. to pol. agent, Kalat, Nov., 1936; pol. agent in Chagai, Oct., 1937; asst. pol. agent in Zhob, Nov., 1939; pol. agent in Zhob (addtl. charge), Oct., 1940; asst. commr., Charsadda Jan., 1941; dy. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Sept., 1941; pol. agt., Khyber, Oct., 1943; dir. agric. prodn., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1944; pol. agt. in Kurram, May, 1946.

COLE, LESLIE PRICHARD, M.B.E., M.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr.) (b. 4th Nov., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Madras, 20th Oct., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1938; M.B.E., Jan., 1941; suptg. engr., Apl., 1946; on depn. ex India.

COLEBROOK, EDWARD HILDER, C.I.E., M.C. Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 16th Oct., 1898).—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1921, as asst. supt. on probn.; confd. Apl., 1925; offg. spl. supt., Malabar spl. police, March, 1928; offg. dist. supt., Aug. 1929; confd., Nov., 1930; awarded King's police medal; Indian police medal, Jan., 1936; confd. as dep. inspr.-gen., Jan., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

COLERIDGE, SIR JOHN FRANCIS STANHOPE DUKE, G.C.B., C.M.G., D.S.O., A.D.C., Lieut.-General, I.A. (b. 25th April, 1878).—Milty. secty., India Office, Nov., 1933 to May, 1936.*

COLLET, ARTHUR LOWE, O.B.E., Judl. Dept., Bengal (regr., etc., high ct., Calcutta) (b. 9th March, 1888).—Apptd. offg. regr. in insolvency, high ct., Calcutta, 23rd Nov., 1931; confd. (provl.), May, 1932; offg. regr., original side, Sept., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1941.

COLLETT, REGINALD ALFRED, O.B.E., M.I.C.E., (b. 14th April 1888).—Joined the service 20th Apl., 1914; served in P.W.D., public health branch, Bombay, from Apl., 1914; cons. p.h. engr., govt. of Bombay, Poona, Dec., 1938; M.B.E., Jan., 1942; p.h. engr. to govt., Poona, Dec., 1944; O.B.E., June, 1945.

COLLIER, REV. ROBERT, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 21st July, 1893).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 7th Oct., 1939; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1941.

COLLINS, SIR GODFREY FERDINANDO STRATFORD, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., O.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (Sind) (b. 3rd Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Christ Church,

Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 21st Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from July, 1916 to Jan., 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; extra dep. dir. of civil supplies, Bombay, 1919; forest settlement officer (temp.), Sept., 1920; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1923; settlement officer, Oct., 1924; addl. dy. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Apl., 1926; confd., June, 1930; actg. regr., co-op. societies, June, 1926; secty. to gov. of Bombay, home and eccl. depts., Aug., 1929; member legisl. council, Bombay, 1929 and 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. pte. secty. to gov. of Bombay, Aug., 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. commr., Sind, Apl., 1935; confd., Nov., 1939; secty. to govt. rev. dept.; member, Sind rev. trib., Nov., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; advr. to gov., Jan., 1942 to Jan., 1946; K.C.I.E., Jun., 1945.

COLLINS, MONTAGUE VIVIAN HEYSHAM, M.B.E. (b. 20th Nov., 1891).—Served in great war, 1914-18; mentioned in despatches; apptd. asst.-sec., legis. assembly dept., Jan., 1946.

COLLINS, TERENCE MOSTYN, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-gen., Central Provs.) (b. 21st March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Dec., 1913; on mil. duty from 10th Nov., 1917 to 21st Oct., 1919; dist. supt., Jan., 1925; prinpl. training school, Saugor, March, 1925; central intel. officer, Central Provs., Nov., 1936; confd. as dep. inspr.-gen., Sept., 1944; offg. inspr.-gen., June, 1946.

COLLISTER, SIR HAROLD JAMES, late Indian C.S. (b. 14th Jan., 1885).—Educ. at South-Eastern Coll., Ramsgate, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 29th Nov., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; addi. sess. and sub-judge, Sept., 1917; jt. mag., Feb., 1918; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), July, 1918 to March, 1919; dist. and sessions judge, March, 1922; addl. judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1933, and July, 1934; judge, ditto, July, 1935; kt., Jan., 1944; retd., 23 Aug., 1944.

COLQUHOUN, SAMUEL, M.B.E., late Survey of India. (b. 23rd Aug., 1883).—Retd., Nov., 1939.

COLTHUP, SIDNEY ROBERT, India Office (hr. executive officer, acct.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 11th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Rochester Math. Sch.; served as sec. div. clerk, Local Govt. Bd., from Feb. to Sept., 1912; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 23rd Sept., 1912, and attd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from Aug., 1915 to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; hr. exec. officer, Sept., 1932; assessor of income tax, from Oct., 1935, to Dec., 1937, and from March, 1941; actg. suptg. exec. offr., Nov., 1945.

COLVILLE, COL. SIR DAVID JOHN, P.C., G.C.I.E. (governor of Bombay).—Apptd., Feb., 1943; held office of Viceroy and govr.-genl., Mar., to June, 1945; Aug. to Sept., 1945, and again Dec., 1946.

COLVILLE, WILLIAM, Indian Police, O.B.E. (supt.) (b. 12th Apl., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 8th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., March., 1928; and again Nov., 1933; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, Home dept., Apl., 1932; pers. asst. to dir., intelligence bureau. March, 1933; asst. to D.I.G. C.I.D., Apl., 1936 to Apl., 1937; supt., Oct., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

COLVIN, SIR CLEMENT PRESTON, Kt., O.B.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th July, 1879).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

COLVIN, ELLIOT JAMES DOWELL, C.I.E.,

Lieut.-Col., I.A. retd.; late Indian Political Service (b. 27th July, 1885).—Retd., July, 1940.†

COLVIN, SIR GEORGE LETHBRIDGE, Kt., C.B., C.M.G., D.S.O., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Apl., 1878).—Agent, E.I. Rly., 30th Sept., 1921 to March, 1933; C.M.G., 1918; C.B., 1919; Kt., Jan., 1933.

COLYER, JOHN ALFRED, late Indian State Rlys., N.W. Rly. (b. 23rd Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

COMPTON, JOSEPH NEILD, A.M.I.Loco.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mech. engineer, G.I.P., Rly.) (b. 25th Sept., 1900).—Asst. mechl. draftsman, junr. scale, Sept., 1922; confd. supt. enr. scale, Nov., 1938; dy. ch. mech. engr., Dec., 1940.

CONDER, STANLEY JOHN, India Office (higher executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 28th June, 1892).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 10th June, 1913, and attd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from July, 1915 to Dec. 1918; exec. offr., Apl., 1922; higher exec. officer, March, 1927; assessor of income tax, Oct., 1936 to Jan., 1946; actg. suptg. exec. offr., Jan., 1941.

CONDON, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.B.E., late Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 22nd Feb., 1886).—Retd., Feb., 1941.

CONLIN, JOHN FRANCIS, M.C., Indian Police (supt., Ajmer-Merwara) (b. 30th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 14th Oct., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1926, and again, Nov., 1932; asst. commr. in Andamans, Oct., 1926; supt., May, 1936; ditto, Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1936.

CONNELL, WALTER LANGHOLM, B.A., Burma Engrg. Serv., (b. 11th Jan., 1904).—Joined the service, 8th Feb., 1928; exec. engr. June 1937; released from mil. serv., June, 1946.

CONNOR, SIR FRANK POWELL, Kt., D.S.O., F.R.C.S., Major-Gen., I.M.S. retd. (b. 16th Sept., 1877).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

CONNOR, JAMES ESSON CLEAL, Burma Civil Service (Cl. I) (dep. commr.) (b. 14th March, 1917).—Educ. at Govt. English High Sch., Maymyo, and Rangoon Univ., appt. on probn., 30th Sept., 1939; joined the service, 24th Aug. 1940; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1940, and served as asst. commr.; addl. judge, 1941; civ. aff. serv. to Dec., 1945; dep. commr., 1946.

CONNOR, WILLIAM CASEY, B.Sc., late Indian Police (b. 29th Nov., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

CONSTANTINE, GEORGE BAXANDALL, B.A., Indian C.S. (Judge, ch. court, Sind) (b. 22nd June 1902).—Educated at Bradford Grammar School, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Feb., 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Feb., 1936; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1936; regr., high ct., Bombay, June, 1937 to Dec., 1938; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1938; chairman, aliens advisory cte., govt. of India, Oct., 1940 to Feb., 1941; offg. commr. of lab. and regr. trade unions, Bombay, var., from Apl., 1942, to Mar., 1945; judge, ch. ct., Sind, Apl., 1945.

CONTRACTOR, KHAN BAHADUR SHERLARI† DADABHAI, M.B.E., B.A., late Bombay Educ. Service (b. 5th Sept., 1878).—Retd., Jan., 1936.* M.B.E., Jan., 1941; associate officer, Order of St. John, June, 1941.

CONTRACTOR, SORAB CURSHEDJI, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd., late Jail dept., Madras (b. 25th Jan., 1887).—First commn., dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ to 25th Sept.,

1925; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confd., June, 1928; insp.-genl. prisons and ch. insp. certified sch., Nov., 1939; on mil. duty, June, 1941; ret'd., Jan., 1942.

COODE, W. J., Indian State Rlys., B.N. Rly. (b. 29th Sept., 1901).—Joined the serv., 22nd Sept., 1921; dep. contr. rly. priorities, Feb., 1944; regl. contr. of rly. priorities, Madras, Jan., 1946 (under War Transport Dept.).

COOK, BERNARD CHRISTOPHER ALLEN, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 20th July, 1906).—Educ. at Radley and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1929; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty. to govt., U.P., genl. bch. (temp.), Apr., 1934; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1935; under secty. fin. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1938; contr. of enemy firms and trading and custodian of enemy property, Sept., 1939; on spec. duty (commr.) coal, Jan., 1942; ditto in gov.-genl. reforms sectt., March, 1942; dep. sugar contr. for India, Apr., 1942; addl. dep. sec. fin., Aug., 1942; dep. ditto, Dec., 1943; mag. and collr. (confd.) Jul., 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1945; offg. jt. sec. fin. dept., Oct., 1945; on spl. duty, comm. dept., June, 1946; offg. Indian trade commr., London, Apr., 1946.

COOK, SIR EDWARD MITCHENER, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S., United Provs.—Retd., June, 1926.*

COOK, JACK MERVYN GERARD, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 22nd March, 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 24th Nov., 1930; confd. Jan., 1933; confd. as supt., June, 1944.

COOK, JAMES HIGH ERNEST, late Indian Posts and Telegraphs (b. 8th June, 1896).—Retd., June, 1941.

COOK, WILLIAM LOUIS CHARLES GERARD, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 21st Feb., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 13th Nov., 1923; offg. addl. supt., Apr., 1929; confd., Feb., 1936; asst. inspr. genl., March, 1938 to March, 1941.

COOKE, CHRISTOPHER HERBERT, C.I.E., B.A., Indian, C.S. (fin. secty. to govt., United Provs.) (b. 29th May, 1889).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 14th Dec., 1922; and served in United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1925; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; offg. dep. secty. to govt., United Provs., Jan., 1934; offg. secty., bd. of rev., United Provs., Oct., 1934; settlement offer., Oct., 1936; mag. and collr., Dec., 1937; rev. secty. to govt., U.P., Nov., 1939; fin., sec., ditto, Mar., 1944; C.I.E., June, 1946.

COOKE, CONRAD REGINALD, O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.E.E., India Posts and Telegraphs (divl. engr.) (b. 31st Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1925; divnl. engr., June, 1932; field service, Dec., 1940; confd. as dir. tels., Jan., 1944; O.B.E., June, 1944.

COOKE, GEORGE HUMPHREY, O.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Pol. Service (b. 23rd Jan., 1903).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1923; in milty. employ to 19th Jan., 1930; asst. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1931; senr. sub-judge, Hazara, Nov., 1933; under-secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1935; asst. commr. Tank, Oct., 1938; dep. commr., D.I. Khan, Jan., 1939; asst. commr. Tank, March, 1939; sec.; to resdt. for E. States, July, 1940; dep. sec. pol. dept. (war), Sept., 1943; O.B.E., Jun.,

1945; jt. sec. in civ. supp. dept., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1946; dir. civ. supp. and jt. sec. to govt., N.W.F.P., April, 1946.

COOKSEY, WILFRID, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Dec., 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

COOPER, CHARLES ROBERT PLANT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 26th Apr., 1882).—Retd., July, 1939.

COOPER, SIR DHANJISHAH BOMANJEE, Khan Bahadur.—Apptd. member, govr's exec. council Bombay, June, 1934, to March, 1937; knighted Feb., 1937.

COOPER, HAROLD HINTON, C.I.E., Ind. State Rlys. (ch. mechl. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 20th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th Dec., 1914; offg. dist., loco. supt., Oct., 1919; offg. works manager, loco. and carr., Nov., 1926; pers. asst. to ch. mechl. engr., Feb., 1927; works manager, loco., Sept., 1927; offg. supt., mechl. workshops, Aug., 1932; and again, Sept., 1935; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1936; and again, March, 1937; provl. perm., Jan., 1939; confd., Jan., 1940; supt. mech. workshops, Nov., 1940; ch. engr. mechl. (provl.) Jan., 1941; C.I.E., Jun., 1945.

COOPER, JOHN WALTER, Office of High Commr. for India (principal exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 22nd Dec., 1879).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Store Dept., 9th June, 1898; supervisor, ditto, Aug., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; principal ditto (temp.), April, 1927; confd., Apr., 1929.

COOREY, CLARENCE OSWOLD, M.B.E., B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 28th Feb. 1910).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Colombo, Univ. of Ceylon and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; joined the service 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934; sub-collr., July, 1936; commr., Madura municip., Apr., 1940; jt. sec. bd. of rev., Aug., 1943; dep. sec., educ., etc., dept., Jan., 1944; M.B.E., Jan., 1946; dep. sec., finl. dept., Mar., 1946.

CORFIELD, SIR CONRAD LAURENCE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.C., M.A. (Cantab.) Indian C.S. (Indian Political service) (b. 15th Aug., 1893).—Educ. at St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Feb., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. from 5th May, 1920; asst. pte. sec. to the Viceroy, May, 1921, to Dec., 1922; sec. to the agt. to the govr.-genl. in the states of Western India, Jan., 1925; asst. to pol. agent, Kalat, Nov., 1926; secty. to agent to govr.-genl., Rajputana, Nov., 1928; pol. agent in Southern States of C.I. and in Malwa, June, 1931; secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1931; vice-presdt., Rewa State council, March, 1932; adviser to delegate for Rewa, Indian round-table conf., 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; jt. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1934; offg. addl. ditto, Apr., 1935; confd., Aug., 1936; jt. secty., F. and P. Dept., Apr., 1935; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; jt. secty., pol. dept., Apr., 1937; offg. secty. to H.E. the Crown Representative, May, 1937; jt. secty., pol. dept., Oct., 1937; resdt. at Jaipur, Nov., 1938; resdt. Rajputana and the ch. commr. Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1939; resdt. Jaipur, Oct., 1939; pol. agent and dep. commr. Quetta Pishin, Dec., 1940; resident, Punjab States, Sept., 1941; confd., Apr., 1942; C.S.I., June, 1942; K.C.I.E., June, 1945; pol. advr. to Crown rep., Aug., 1945.

CORFIELD, WILLIAM FRANCIS, Office of the High Commissioner for India (hr. clerical officer, accis. dept.) (b. 24th Apr., 1882).—Apptd. clerical

officer, 1st June, 1923; actg. hr. ditto, Apl., 1927; confd., March, 1936.

CORIN, JOHN MADAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 9th May, 1907).—Educ. at St. Paul's, and Magdalene College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under-secty., home, pol., etc., depts., Bombay, Aug., 1935; passport officer, Bombay, Nov., 1936; secty. to govr., Sind, Apl., 1937; collr. (confd.) Dec., 1943.

CORMACK, FREDERICK EUSON, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Serv. of Engr. (ch. exec. engr. Assam) (b. 18th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service, Oct., 1913; asst. engr., Feb., 1914; I.A.R.O. from 27th June, 1916 to 7th June, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; temp. suptg. engr., March, 1938, ch. engr. and sec. to govt., P.W.D., Aug., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1945.

CORMACK, HENRY SLATER, M.C., M.B., F.R.C.S.E., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd., late Burma Medl. Dept. (b. 6th May, 1886).—Retd., May, 1941.†

CORNELIUS, ALVIN ROBERT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 1st May, 1903).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., and Oct., 1932; and again, May, 1933; conid., Nov., 1936; attaché, govt. of India, legis. dept., May, 1941; dep. leg. remembrancer, Punjab, Dec., 1942; legal ditto and sec. govt. of Punjab, legis. dept., 1943.

CORNER, COURTNEY JOHN, Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (b. 16th Nov., 1915).—Educ. Portsmouth Gra. Sch., apptd. on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

CORNISH ATHELSTAN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 22nd July, 1888).—Retd., Nov., 1940.

CORNWELL, RICHARD BARRY, late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 20th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service 5th January, 1909; dep. consvr., Oct., 1922; on deputation to Forest Research Institute, Sept., 1924; dist. forest officer, Oct., 1933; on mil. duty, July, 1940 to Nov., 1944; ret'd., July, 1945.

CORY, EDGAR MYRIE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 6th Jan., 1885).—Retd., Jan., 1942.†

COSGRAVE, SIR WILLIAM ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 6th Apl., 1879).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

COSTELLO, SIR LEONARD WILFRED JAMES, C.B.E., Kt., M.A., LL.B., (b. 25th Aug., 1881).—late judge high ct., Calcutta; ret'd., March, 1941; C.B.E., Jan., 1946.

COSTELOE, JAMES GEOFFREY, Burma Police, (Cl. I) (b. 4th Dec., 1920).—Apptd. on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

COTTAM, WILLIAM JOHN, Indian Police (sup't.) (b. 23rd March, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 5th Dec., 1924; addl. supt., June, 1930; spec. supt., intell. bch., July, 1944.

COTTER, EDWARD, C.I.E., M.B., B.Ch., D.P.H., Col. I.M.S. (public health commr. with the Govt. of India) (b. 6th Nov., 1892).—First commn., dated 8th Dec., 1915; in milt. employ prior to 1st July, 1930; asst. dir. pub. health, Burma, July, 1930; offg. dir., May, 1933; dep. public health commr. with the govt. of India, July, 1937; public health commr., March, 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

COTTON, REV. HENRY WILMOT STAPLETON, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (archdeacon of Lucknow) (b. 22nd Feb., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on

probation, 22nd Oct., 1925; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; offg. archdeacon of Lucknow, Apl., 1936; and again, May, 1937; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1937; bishop's commry., Apl., 1938; archdeacon, Apl., 1938.

COTTON, JOHN RICHARD, M.B.E., Capt., I.A., Indian Pol. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1909).—First commn., 31st Jan., 1929; pers. asst. to ch. commr. and resdt., and C.-in-C. at Aden, Nov., 1934; passport offer. (in addn.), Jan., 1935; under trng. in the Punjab, Jan., 1936; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, July, 1937; confd., March, 1938; att'd. officer, Rajputana Agency, Feb., 1939; under sec. to resdt., Rajputana, Feb., 1939; sec. to resdt., Rajputana, in addn., March, 1939; under-sec. to resdt., Rajputana, Apl., 1939; ditto, Hyderabad, June, 1941; M.B.E., Jan., 1944; sec. to resdt., W.I. States, May, 1944.

COUCHMAN, SIR HAROLD JOHN, D.S.O., M.C., Brig., R.E. (ret'd.), late Survey of India (b. 29th July, 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

COUPER, SIR THOMAS, Kt., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 15th March, 1878).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

COUSENS, HENRY JAMES MORRISON, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 4th May, 1883).—Retd., May, 1938.*

COUSINS, EDMUND RICHARD JOHN RATCLIFFE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar) (b. 22nd Apl., 1888).—Educ. at Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1918; offg. dep. commr., March, 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1922; confd., July, 1925; dep. commr., Feb., 1926; offg. commr., Apl., 1934, C.I.E., June, 1938; commr., March, 1937; advr. to the govr., Bihar, Nov., 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1942; ret'd., Jul., 1945.

COVENTRY, JOHN PHILIP CHETWYND, Indian Police (b. 14th Dec., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Dec., 1937; services placed at the disposal of the Govt. of Cent. Provs. and Berar from Oct., 1938; a.d.c. to the govr., Aug., 1941.

COWAN, ALAN CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 7th Sept., 1915).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd., on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

COWARD, DUNCAN CECIL, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 16th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 25th June, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Feb., 1932.

COWGILL, JOHN FELIX, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 11th June, 1903).—Retd., Nov., 1940.†

COWGILL, JOHN HARRY CHRISTOPHER, Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 14th Nov., 1927; offg. addl. supt., March, 1934; supt. confd., Nov., 1940.

COWHAM, REV. ARTHUR GERARD, M.A., Indian Ecccl. Estab. (junr. chaplain) (b. 27th Oct., 1895).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 25th Feb., 1929; junr., chaplain, Feb., 1931.

COWIE, JOHN GRANDISON, M.B.E., Dept. of Industries, Punjab (inspr. of industrial schools) (b. 12th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as vice-princl., Mayo sch. of arts, Sept., 1924; inspr.

of industrial schools, May, 1928; M.B.E., Jan., 1941.

COWLEY, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th Nov., 1915).—Educ. at Middlesbrough High Sch., Jesus Coll., Cambridge, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; a.r.p. offr., Apl., 1942; asst. orgn. nat. war front, Sept., 1942.

COX, ALFRED INNES, O.B.E., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. Medical Dept., Madras (dist. medl. officer) (b. 1st Nov., 1894).—First commn., 27th Sept., 1919; in milty. employ. until 25th Jan., 1932; dist. medl. officer, Madras, Feb., 1932; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

COX, CUTHBERT EUSTACE CONNOR, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service, Central Provs. (b. 9th March, 1885).—Retd., March, 1940.

COX, EDMUND LEONARD, Indian Police (district supt. Madras) (b. 29th January, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, Nov., 1932; confd. Nov., 1934; dist. supt. (confd.) Mar., 1946.

COX, MATHEWS HENRY, Brig., C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., I.A.O.C., (ch. tech. advr. fertilizer project, supply dept.) (b. 12th Aug., 1892).—First commn., Sept., 1916; temp. Col., Dec., 1941; local Brig., July, 1942; apptd. to I.O.S., May, 1938; dep. dir. genl. muns. prodn., dept. of supply, May, 1943; ch. tech. advr., fertilizer project, Feb., 1946.

COX, HUBERT EUSTACE, Indian State Railways (dep. ch. engr., signals, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 14th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service 2nd Jan., 1915; signal and interlocking engr., G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr., signals, July, 1935; confd., Dec., 1935.

COX, VERNON MAURICE HERVALWIL, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Aug., 1898).—First commn., 12th Sept., 1917; in milty. employ. to 1st Nov., 1927; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent in Kalat, Ja., 1930; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat and of Chagai, May, 1932; asst. commr., Peshawar, Feb., 1935; dep. commr., Peshawar, May, 1936; offr. on spec. duty, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1936; pol. agent, ditto, March, 1937; rev. and divl. commr., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1938; home secty., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1939, and again, Nov., 1940; chief secty. ditto, Aug., 1940; dy. commr. Bannu, Feb., 1941; sec. to resdt. for Western India, Oct., 1941; pol. agt., E. Kathiawar and Sabar Kantha agencies, Apl., 1944.

COYAJEE, H. H. C., B.A., B.Sc., Barr.-at-Law, Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 24th Nov., 1897).—Addl. judge high ct., Bombay, March to July, 1943; judge, ditto, July, 1943.

COYAJEE, SIR JEHANGIR COOVERJEE, Kt., B.A. (Cantab.), LL.B. (Bom.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Sept., 1875); retd., Nov., 1931.*

CRACKNELL, ARTHUR JOHN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th May, 1885).—Retd., Nov 1938.*

CRACKNELL, FREDERICK GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 12th Feb., 1910).—Educated at Leeds Grammar School and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1934; civil and sess. judge, Oct., 1941; dep. sec. govt. of India (home), Sept., 1944.

CRAIG, MALCOLM THOMAS, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Central Provs.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as asst.

engr., 6th Apl., 1920; asst. exec. engr., May) 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1926; retd., Apl., 1941.

CRAIK, SIR HENRY DUFFIELD, Bart., G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S.; (b. 2nd Jan., 1876).—Educ. at Eton Coll., Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; regr. of the chief court, March, 1905; asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1908; settlt. offr., Oct., 1910; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1915; dep. commr., and rev. sec. to govt., Feb., 1916; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1919; offg. addl. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1919; offg. chief sec. to Punjab Govt., Apl., 1922; confd., July, 1922; C.S.I., Jan., 1924; offg. finl. commr., Nov., 1927; commr. (provl.), Oct., 1929; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, Apl., 1930; confd., Aug., 1930; K.C.S.I. Punjab, Apl., 1930; confd., Aug., 1930; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1935; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, July, 1934; apptd. govr. of Punjab, Apl., 1938; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1941; retd. Apl., 1941; pol. advr. to H.E. Crown Representative, Oct., 1941, to May, 1943.

CRAKER, PHILLIP WILLIAM, O.B.E., late Indian State Railways (b. Aug., 1887).—Retd., Apl., 1941.†

CRANSTON, WILLIAM PATRICK, Capt., I.A. (b. 5th Oct., 1913).—Apptd. to I.P.S., on probn., from mil. employ., Oct., 1938; under-sec. res. Hyderabad, Feb., 1944; I.P.S. (confd.) May, 1944; asst. pol. agt. and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Oct., 1945; on spec. duty, Baluchistan, Dec., 1945; under sec. to A.G.G. Baluchistan, Jan., 1946; asst. pol. agt. and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, March, 1946. asst. pol. agt., Nasirabad, May, 1946.

CRAW, HENRY HEWAT, K.B.E., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (advr. to sec. of state for India and Burma) (b. 29th Oct., 1882).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., and Jena Coll., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1907, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty, Oct., 1917, to Jan., 1919; offg. depy. commr., May, 1921; dep. commr., Jan., 1923; offg. commr., May, 1931; confd., June, 1935; ch. secty. to govt., Burma, Sept., 1935; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; finl. commr., Feb., 1938; counsellor to the govr. Burma, March, 1939 to Nov., 1941; K.B.E., Jan., 1942; advr. to sec. of state for Burma, Feb., 1942; ditto for India, in addn., June, 1943.

CRAWFORD, CHARLES ANTHONY, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (chief comml. mgr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 14th Apl., 1900).—Joined the service, 14th Jan., 1916, and held non-gazetted appts.; asst. supt., Feb., 1923; asst. comml. manager, March, 1926; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager, June, 1929; and again, Nov., 1935; confd., Dec., 1936; ch. comml. manager, Aug., 1940; reg. contr. rly. priorities, war transp., March, 1943; O.B.E., June, 1944.

CRAWFORD, WILLIAM GLASGOW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 28th Apl., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, on 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., May, 1931; on mil. duty, Nov., 1943 to Dec., 1945.

CRAWLEY, DESMOND JOHN CHETWODE, Indian C.S., Madras (sub. collr. and mag.) (b. 2nd June, 1917).—Educ. at King's School, Ely, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd. 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, sub. collr.,

1940; under-sec., public dept., January, 1944; addtl. under-sec., devel. dept., March, 1944; dir. pol. dept., govt. of India, July, 1946.

CRAWLEY, GEORGE RICHARD, Indian Police (asst. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 22nd Apl., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 26th Nov., 1930; confd., Apl., 1933; A.D.C., to govr., Oct., 1936 to June, 1938.

CREAGH-COEN (see Coen, T. B. C.).

CREANER, THOMAS AMBROSE, B.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers, (exec. engr. Punjab) (b. 24th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service, 20th Oct., 1930; on mil. duty to Oct., 1945; exec. engr., Nov., 1945.

CREED, CLARENCE JAMES, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., Indian Police (insp. gen. Bihar) (b. 19th July, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 29th March, 1920; supt., Sept., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1936; confd. Jan., 1940; O.B.E., June, 1943; inspr.-gen., Oct., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

CREEK, ERNEST GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 11th May, 1910).—Educated at the East Anglian School, Bury St. Edmunds, and St. John's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; mag. and collr., May, 1941.

CREIGHTON, THOMAS GORDON, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., M.I.L.E., Indian State Ry's. (divl. mech. engr., N.W., Rly.) (b. 29th Dec., 1898); asst. loco. supt., junr. scale, 11th Dec., 1922; divl. mech. engr., senr. scale, July, 1937; dep. ch. contrl. of standardisation, rly. board, Apl., 1940.

CRERAR, SIR JAMES, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Sind) (b. 11th Dec., 1877).—Retd., Aug., 1934.*

CRICHTON, GERALD CHARLES LAWRENCE, C.I.E., Maj., I.A. Indian Polit. Serv., (b. 30th July, 1900).—First commn. dated 14th July, 1921; prob. in pol. dept., May, 1926; settlement officer, Peshawar, Jan., 1927; asst. commr., Bannu, July, 1927; secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana and C.C. Ajmer Merwara, June, 1928; pol. asst. to C.C. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1928; asst. commr., Tank, Nov., 1930; offg. asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Oct., 1931; under-secy. to govt., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1933; offg. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transf. depts., Dec., 1933; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Oct., 1934; home secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1937; dep. secty., ext. dept., govt. of India, June, 1938; home sec., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1938; pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1939; ditto, Khyber, Sept., 1942; jt. sec. ext. affairs dept., Aug., 1943; counsellor Brit. legn., Kabul, Dec., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

CRICHTON, WALTER HUGH, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 24th July, 1896).—First commn., 8th Dec., 1924; agency surg., Kurram, Feb., 1933; health officer, Simla, Oct., 1934; asst. dir. pub. health, Delhi, Oct., 1936; chief health officer, Delhi, Apl., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; in tempv. military employ from May, 1941.

CRICKMAY, JOSEPH GEORGE, I.S.O., late India Office (b. 23rd April, 1883).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 17th Jan., 1902; transferred to mil. dept., from Oct., 1914; minor staff clerk, March, 1917; staff clerk (actg.), Nov., 1919; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1922; representative of the India Office on the Navy, Army and Air Force Insur-

ance Fund Advisory Ctee., Apl., 1922; actg. suptg. exec. officer, March, 1935; and again, Apl., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937; actg. principl. fin. dept., Feb., 1943; ret'd., Feb., 1946; I.S.O., June, 1946.

CRITCHELL, HUGH MARTIN, Survey of India, (b. 25th Dec., 1900).—Proby. extra asst. supt., Oct., 1919; confd. Oct., 1922; on mil. duty from Feb., 1942; supt., Nov., 1945.

CRITCHLY, VERNON FAIRBANK, (Punjab Electricity) (b. 22nd September, 1893).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1927; served as exec. and elec. engr. variously up to Dec., 1934; dep. chief engr., Jan., 1936; confd. as ch. engr., Oct., 1940; on l.p. from Oct., 1945.

CROAD, ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Dec., 1885).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

CROCKETT, WILLIAM GORDON, M.B.E., Officer of High Commr. for India (b. 18th Dec., 1880).—Retd., Dec., 1940; re-employed on temporary basis, as controller store dept., Dec., 1940.†

CROEN, RONALD FELIX, Burma Land Records Dept. (b. 20th Dec., 1893).—Asst. supt. land records, Nov., 1915; supt. ditto, July, 1927; asst. comdt. Gurkha evac. camp, Apl., 1944.

CROFT, SIR WILLIAM DAWSON, K.B.E., C.I.E., C.V.O., B.A., India Office (b. 16th Aug., 1892).—Educ. at Winchester Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, military dept., 28th May, 1919, after previous service as temporary junr. clerk since Nov., 1915; apptd. pte. sec. to the perm. under-sec. of state, Jan., 1920; principal, Apl., 1921; establishment officer, July, 1921 to Jan., 1922; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; pte. secty. to secty. of state, Apl., 1931 to Oct., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; asst. secty.
