Aug., 1936; posted to econ. and overseas dept., Oct., 1937; C.V.O., Jan., 1938; actg. asst. under-sec. of state for India, July, 1940; deputy under-sec. for India, 1st Jan., 1942; K.B.E., June, 1943; on loan min. of state, Cairo, resumed duty in India Office, Jan., 1946.

CROFTON, DENIS HAYES, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 14th Dec., 1908).—Educated at Tonbridge School and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secy. to govt., pol. dept., Bihar, June, 1936; attached to govt. of India, dept. of lab., Apl., 1939; under-secy. to govt. of India, dept. of lab., June, 1939; pte. sec. to Ind. rep., E. Group Supply Council, Feb., 1941; M.B.E., Jan., 1943; pte. sec. to govr., Bihar, Feb., 1944.

CROFTON, SIR RICHARD MARSH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. Central Provs.) (b. 6th Apl., 1891).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1915, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from Oct., 1917 to March, 1919; offg. dep. commr., July, 1922; dep. commr. (prov.), and finl. sec. to govt., May, 1926; dep. commr., Dec., 1926; offg. rev. secty. to govt., Dec., 1927; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1931; excise and opium commr. in C. India, Nov., 1931; dir. genl., rev. dept., Hyderabad Feb., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; commr. (contd.), March, 1941; prime minister, Bahawalpur State, Apl., 1942; kt., June, 1945.

CROFTS, FRANK NORMAN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 23rd July,

1906).—Educ. at Rugby and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived 1st Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. collr. and mag.; temp. sess. and sub-judge, May, 1935; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1936; confd., Jan., 1940.

CROMBIE, ALAN DOUGLAS, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 20th Jan., 1894).—Educ. at Wolverhampton Grammar Sch.; joined the service, 25th Aug., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1923; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1925; on foreign service as agent under Rajah of Pariakimedi, Nov., 1926; dep. secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Madras, Dec., 1929; pte. secty. to govr., Madras, June 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; collr. (prov. subs.), Sept., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936; collr. and dist. mag., Sept., 1936; member bd. of rev., Feb., 1946.

CROMBIE, GEORGE EDMOND, M.A., India Office (princl. econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 14th June, 1908).—Educ. at Aberdeen Grammar Sch. and Fettes Coll., Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. asst. principal, 19th Oct., 1931; actg. resdt. clk., July to Aug., 1932; resdt. clk., July, 1934 to Oct., 1934; pte. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state, Oct., 1935; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Aug., 1936; actg., princpl. Apl., 1937; confd., Oct., 1938; retd., May, 1942; reinstated as principal, econ. and overseas dept., May, 1945.

CROMBIE, PETER, M.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 2nd Nov., 1911).—Educ. at Fettes College, University of Aberdeen and Caius College, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1938; dist. supply offcr., Malabar, June, 1945.

CRONE, DESMOND ROE, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., R.E., Survey of India (b. 24th Sept., 1900).—First commn. 16th July, 1920; in mil. employ to Oct., 1924; proby. asst. supt., Oct., 1924; confd. Jan., 1927; supt. Mar., 1936; in mil. employ. from Mar., 1943 to Mar., 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1941.

CROOKSHANK, HENRY, B.A., B.A.I., (b. 13th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt. (geologist), 5th Dec., 1920; supt. (confd.), Nov., 1935; with Indian Army, July, 1940 to Jan., 1943; states liaison offr., Dec., 1945.

CROSSLEY, DONALD, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 22nd July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1929; confd., March, 1934; awarded Indian Police Medal, March, 1937; offg. d.i.g., Apl., 1945.

CROSSLEY, THOMAS BIGHAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Feb., 1910).—Educated at Blackburn Grammar School and King's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

CROSTHWAIT, TIMOTHY LELAND, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 5th Aug., 1915).—Educ. at Wellington and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 19th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bombay and Sind as asst. collr., asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, 12th Feb., 1942 to Apl., 1944; M.B.E., June, 1944; on staff of Cabinet Mission to India, March to June, 1946.

CROSTHWAITE, SIR BERTRAM MAITLAND,

Kt., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Oct., 1880).—Retd., Dec., 1935.*

CROSTHWAITE, SIR HUGH STUART, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 20th Oct., 1879).—Retd., Feb., 1940.†

CROW, CHARLES GEORGE VENANT, Burma Frontier Service, (b. 22nd March, 1902).—Apptd. 13th Jan., 1926.

CROXFORD, ERNEST WILLIAM, India Audit Office (actg. hr. exec. offr.) (b. 18th Jan., 1915).—Apptd. exec. offr., 2nd Oct., 1933; actg. hr. exec. offr., Sept., 1941.

CRUICKSHANK, MARTIN MELVIN, C.I.E., M.D., B.Sc., Ch.M., D.O.M.S., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 22nd March, 1888).—First commn., 17th March, 1919; in milty. employ until 25th Feb., 1931; district medl. officer, Madras, May, 1934; prof. of surgery Madras medl. coll., and surgeon, gen. hospital Madras, Sept., 1934; supt. and surg., gen. hosp., Madras, Oct., 1939; ch. medl. offr., Delhi; March, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; addl. member, medl. bd., India Office, Nov., 1945 to May, 1946.

CRUMP, SIR LOUIS CHARLES, Kt., late Indian C.S.—Retd., Jan., 1929.*

CUFFE, SIR GEORGE, Indian State Rlys. (b. 15th May, 1892).—Joined the service, 26th July, 1940; apptd. gen. mgr., B.B. and C.I. Rly., Dec., 1945; Kt., Jan., 1946.

CUMMING, SIR JOHN GHEST, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 29th Dec., 1868).—Retd., Oct., 1921.*

CUMMING, ROUALEYN CHARLES ROSSITER, C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl. police, and jt. secty., Assam) (b. 2nd Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 7th Dec., 1911; supt., Apl., 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1935; inspr.-genl., and jt. secty. (home dept.), Assam, Dec., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1942; King's police and fire serv. medals, 1944.

CUNLIFFE, SIR JOHN ROBERT ELLIS, Kt., Barr. (late judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 4th March, 1886).—Retd., Nov., 1937.*

CUNNINGHAM, SIR CHARLES BANKS, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 8th May, 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

CUNNINGHAM, SIR GEORGE, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 23rd March, 1888).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam of 1910; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1911, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1914; personal asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Oct., 1915; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1917; asst. commr., Tank, Dec., 1918; pol. asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Feb., 1919; sub-divl. offr., Tank, Apl., 1919; pte. sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., May, 1919; spl. duty on staff of chief pol. offr. with N.W. Frontier Field Force, May, 1919; O.B.E., Aug., 1920; asst. commr., Thal, Nov., 1920; pol. agent, Tochi, Apl., 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; counsellor, British Legation, Kabul, Nov., 1925; pte. sec. to the Viceroy, July, 1926; C.S.I., June, 1931; member, exec. council govr. N.W.F.P., June, 1932 to May, 1936; actg. govr. N.W.F.P., Nov., 1932; offg. agent to govr.-genl., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1932; K.C.I.E., June, 1935; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1937; govr., N.W.F.P., March, 1937; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1946; retd., Nov., 1946.

CURGENVEN, SIR ARTHUR JOSEPH, B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 24th July, 1876).—Retd., July, 1936.*

CURRIE, DONALD McDONALD, B.Sc. (consvr., Madras) (b. 11th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922; confd. as consvr., May, 1944.

CURSETJI, MANEKJI JEHANGIR MANEKJI, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.K.C., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 18th Sept., 1890).—Joined the provl. service as asst. engr., June, 1915; asst. engr. (impl. service), Jan., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., June, 1924.

CURSON, BERNARD ROBERT, India Office (actg. princpl. econ. dept.) (b. 14th Nov., 1913).—Apptd. cler. offer. 23rd June, 1930; actg. exec. offer., May, 1940; actg. asst. princpl., Jan., 1941; actg. princpl., July, 1944.

CURTIS, GERALD COLVILLE SEYMOUR, B.A., O.B.E., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 4th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Westminster, Christchurch Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 8th October, 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served as asst. commr. in the Punjab; pol. asst. and commandant, Border Mily. police, May, 1932; colonisation officer and asst. pol. agent, Nasirabad, May, 1933; and again, Dec., 1934; pol. agent, Sibi, Nov., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Punjab Hill States Oct., 1936; asst. commr. and city mag., Peshawar, Aug., 1938; dep. commr., Mardan, Oct., 1938; dep. commr., Hazara, March, 1939; addl. dy. sec. ext. affairs dept., Oct., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; pol. agt., S. Waziristan, Dec., 1943.

CURTIS, SYDNEY JOSEPH, M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 1st Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1922.

DABHOLKAR, ANANT YASHWANT, M.C., J.B., B.Sc. (Bom.), D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Public health dept., Bombay (dir.) (b. 30th June, 1888).—First commn., 17th March, 1919; in milty. employ prior to 11th Dec., 1929; asst. dir. of public health, Jan., 1930; dir., 1933.

DABOO, ERACH RUTTANJI, M.C., M.B., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S. retd., (b. 9th May, 1888).—Civ. surgeon, Assam, Feb., 1933; on mil. duty, June, 1941; retd.

DADABHOY, SIR MANECKJI BYRAMJI, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E.—Apptd. pres., council of state, 21st Dec., 1932; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1936.

DAFTARY, GAVRISHANKAR DULLABJI, B.E. (Civil), Indian Service of Engrs. (suprg. engr., Bombay) (b. 23rd Jan., 1889).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., March, 1913; asst. engr., March, 1914; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 19; exec. engr., March, 1923; suprg., ditto confd., Feb., 1940.

DAGLISH, JULIAN, Burma Police (dist. suprl.) (Cl. I.) (b. 19th Sept., 1916).—Apptd. asst. suprl. on prob., Dec., 1937; transfd. to mily. dept., Nov., 1940; civ. aff. serv., to Dec., 1945; dist. suprl. 1946.

DAIN, SIR JOHN RUTHERFORD, C.I.E., B.A., late I.C.S. (Orissa) (b. 13th Jan., 1883).—Retd., Oct., 1942.

DALAL, Lalitchandra Ramanlal, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 17th Feb., 1918).—Educ. at Bombay Univ.; apptd. on prob. after examn. of 1942 and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

DALAL, SIR ARDESHIR RUSTAMJI, K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 24th Apl., 1884).—Retd., July, 1931; * planning and development member of Viceroys' exec. cl. Aug., 1944, to Jan. 1946; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

DALAL, BHICAJI PHIROZESHAH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 1st Dec., 1913).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Coll., Coonoor, Bombay Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. on prob., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 17th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

DALAL, SIR DADIBA MERWANJEE, Kt., C.I.E.—Member of comtee. on Indian exchange and currency, 1919; chairman, govt. securities rehabilitation comtee., Bombay, 1921; apptd. member of council of sec. of state for India, 19th Nov., 1921; delegate for India at Genoa Economic Conference, and representative for India at The Indian retrenchment comtee., 1922; high commr. for India, 19th Apl., 1923, to 26th Sept., 1924; delegate for India at Impl. Economic Conference, 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; Kt., Jan., 1924.

DALIP SINGH, KANWAR, SIR, B.A., late Judl. Dept., Punjab (b. 2nd June, 1885).—Apptd. asst. legal remembrancer, Nov., 1922; govt. advocate, Nov., 1924; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, Oct., 1925; judge, ditto, Feb., 1930; Kt., Jan., 1943; retd., Oct., 1943.

DALIP SINGH, SIR M.A., Income-tax Dept., Punjab (asst. commr.) (b. 29th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, Aug., 1920; held non-gaz. appts., Aug., 1921-1921; collr. of Income-tax, Sept., 1921; on special duty, Punjab Secretariat, May, 1930; Income-tax offer., June, 1930; confd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, June, 1936; Kt., Jan., 1943.

DALJIT SINGH, RAJA, SIR, K.B.E., C.S.I.—Member of the legislative council, Punjab, 1913-1915; member govr.-gen.'s legislative council, 1913-1915; C.S.I., June, 1914; member of council of Sec. of State for India, March, 1915, to June, 1917; K.B.E., June, 1919; chief minister, Kashmir, to 1921.

D'ALMEIDA, WILLIAM, M.B.E., late home dept., govt. of India (b. 21st Oct., 1888).—Retd., Aug., 1941.

DALVI, BHUJANGRAE KESHAVRAO, B.A., Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 15th June, 1892).—Joined the serv., 20th Nov., 1933; dist. and sess. judge, (confd.) Jan., 1941.

DALY, SIR CLIVE KIRKPATRICK, C.S.I., C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (retd.), late Indian Political Service (b. 3rd Apl., 1888).—First commn., 18th Dec., 1907; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; pol. agent, Bahrain, Nov., 1925; H.M. Consul, Seistan and Kain, March, 1929; offg. consul-genl., Khorasan, June, 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar agency, June, 1938; dep. resdt., Rajkot, March to June, 1939; dep. resdt., W.I. States Agency, March, 1939; resdt. for Baroda and the Gujarat States, July, 1939; confd. resdt., Cl. II, Dec., 1941; C.S.I., Jan., 1943; retd., 3rd Apl., 1943; Kt., June, 1946.

DALYELL OF THE BINNS, Lieut.-Col. G., C.I.E., name changed from LOCH, P. G. (q.v.).

DALZIEL, WALTER WATSON, C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon.), Barr. Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Orissa) (b. 5th Oct., 1900).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and Corpus Christi, Oxford, joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived 7th Dec., 1924, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settlement offr., Nov., 1927; offg. judl. commr., Apl., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1932; confd., Apl., 1934; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, legisl. dept., Apl. to Oct., 1935; sec. to govt., Orissa, law, comm. and lab. depts., Jan., 1940; again, Nov., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; sec. judl. dept., Feb., 1944; dist. and sess. judge selen.

grade, Mar., 1944; sec. govt. legis. dept., and addl. sec. appt. dept., Aug., 1945; on l.p.r

DAM, SARAT CHANDRA, B.Sc., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 13th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service, 19th Apr., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Apr., 1923; offg. exec. engr., June, 1929; confd., Apr., 1932; p.a. to ch. engr., June, 1939.

DAMLE, KASHINATH RAGHUNATH, Indian C.S. (sec. to high commr. for India to Australia) (b. 3rd Mar., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., King's College, London, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1940; sec. to high commr. for India in Australia, Oct., 1944.

DANDEKER, NARAYAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub collr., Madras) (b. 24th June, 1908).—Educ. at School of Oriental Studies, Lond.; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, sub-collr., and jt. mag., Apr., 1933; asst. collr. of customs, Madras, Jan., 1937; apptd. officer on special duty, income-tax department, Bengal, Nov., 1939; asst. commr. of income-tax, March, 1940; commr. of inc. tax, Punjab, etc., May, 1941.

DANIELS, MARK, O.B.E., B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.) (b. 22nd Feb., 1892).—Educ. at Central Foundation School, London, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge (wrangler; scholar, 1910-14); apptd. temp. junr. clerk, finl. dept., 16th March, 1915; resdt. clerk, (temp.), Sept., 1915, to Dec. 1916; junr. clerk, store dept., 1st Nov., 1915; on mil. service, from Dec., 1916 to Feb., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920, as asst. controller, store dept.; senr. clerk, Dec., 1921; contr., Apr., 1922; O.B.E., June, 1934; officer for defence planning and co-ordin., Sept., 1939; actg. dir. of purchase, July, 1941.

DANTYAGI, VISHWESWAR DAYAL, O.B.E., M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 14th Jan., 1899).—Joined the serv., 13th Jul., 1922; dep. acct. genl., central revenues, Apr., 1936; dep. acct. genl., P. & T., Delhi, Apr., 1937; asst. dir. of audit, defence services, W.C. and Lahore Dist., Oct., 1938; depy. dir. of audit, def. services, Nov., 1940; O.B.E., June, 1946.

DAPHTARY, B.A., Bombay Judl. Dept., apptd. advocate-genl., Bombay, April, 1945.

DAR, JEEWAN NATH, RAI SAHIB, B.A., late Indian Posts and Telegraphs (b. 31st Jan., 1884).—Retd. Jan., 1939.*

DAR, MUKAT BIHARI LAL, B.Sc., LL.B., (export trade contr., Bombay) (b. Jan., 1896).—Joined the service (U.P.) as dep. collr., 1st April, 1921; under sec., govt. of India, supply dept., etc., Aug., 1943; offr. on spl. duty, office of ch. contr. of exports, Mar., 1946; export trde. contr., Bombay, April, 1946.

DAR, SHIAM KISHAN, apptd. actg. judge, Allahabad high court, Nov., 1940; addl. judge, ditto, July, 1941; judge, Dec., 1941; retd., Oct., 1944.

DAR, SHYAM SUNDAR LAL, M.A. (Cantab.), Barr., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Dec., 1895).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1920; arrived, 16th March, 1921, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and jt. mag.; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1925; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. excise commr., July, 1931; inspr.-genl., regstrn., United

Provs., Apr., 1932; mag. and collr., Nov., 1933.

DARBY, GEORGE SCOTT, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 16th April, 1885).—Retd., March, 1939.*

DARGAN, PATRICK ARTHUR, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgeon, Punjab) (b. 1st Aug., 1888).—First commn., 1st Nov., 1920; civil surgeon, Punjab, Apr., 1931.

DARLEY, SIR BERNARD D'OLIER, Kt., C.I.E. A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 24th Aug., 1880).—Retd., March, 1934.†

DARLING, GEORGE KENNETH, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 10th May, 1879).—Retd., Dec., 1938.*

DARLING, HENRY JOHN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 24th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. elec. engr., 13th Oct., 1926; dist. elec. engr., E.I. Rly., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. loco. supt., E.B. Rly., Aug., 1928; confd., Feb., 1930; ch. electrical engr., N.W. Rly., Nov., 1931; offg. ditto, E.I. Rly., Oct., 1935; confd., Nov., 1936; retd., Jan., 1945.

DARLING, SIR MALCOLM LYALL, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Dec., 1880).—Retd., May, 1940.†

DARRINGTON, VICTOR GEORGE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 25th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 12th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1924; senr. instructor, Madras Forest Coll., Dec., 1925; pers. asst. to ch. consvr., March, 1931 to March, 1934, and again, Jan., 1940; consvr. actg. variously from Mar., 1944.

DARSHAN SINGH, SARDAR, F.R.H.S., Barr. Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric. on 23rd Dec., 1913.

DART, REV. THOMAS HENRY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estadt. (junior chaplain) (b. 14th May, 1908).—apptd. chaplain on probn. Calcutta Diocese, 12th Aug., 1942.

DAS, AMARENDRA NATH, Rai Bahadur, O.B.E., M.A., late Bihar C.S. (b. 16th July, 1885).—Retd., July, 1940.†

DAS, AMRITA LAL, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 1st Feb., 1906).—Joined the service as student, 9th May, 1928; asst. exec. engr., May, 1929; offg. exec. engr., May, 1933; confd., May, 1938.

DAS, ARJAN (see Arjan, Das).

DAS, ATUL CHANDRA, RAI BAHADUR, O.B.E., B.A., (b. Jan., 1889).—Joined the service 1st Nov., 1912; retd., June, 1945; re-employed in dept. of works, mines and power, April, 1946.

DAS, CEDRIC PERCY MAYA, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dir. of agric., United Provs.) (b. 8th Aug., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric. on 1st Sept., 1917; prof. agric. coll., Cawnpore, Nov., 1921; princpl. ditto, Dec., 1925; dep. dir. of agric., United Provs., Nov., 1934; confd. in sel. grade, Apr., 1936; dir. of agric., Sept., 1941.

DAS, DEBES CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., home dept., govt. of India) (b. 1st July, 1911).—Educ. at Mitra Institution, Calcutta, University of Calcutta and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the Service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; under-secty., govt. of Assam (home and pol. depts.), Oct., 1938; ditto, home dept., govt. of India, June, 1940; dep. sec., ditto, Apr., 1943.

DAS, DHARM (see Dharm Das).

DAS, GURCHARN (see Gurcharn Das).

DAS, MATHRA (see Mathra Das).

DAS, NABAGOPAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (reg. dir. resettlement etc., Bengal and Assam) (b. 20th Feb., 1910).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., the School of Economics, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934; dy. agric. marketing adviser, July, 1940; sec., post-war reconstrn. cte., May, 1944; on spl. duty, 1943-45; reg. dir. of resettlement and employment, Jul., 1945.

DAS, NIRANJAN (see Niranjan Das).

DAS, PRIA (see Priea Das).

DAS, SUDHANSHU KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S., Orissa (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 3rd Sept., 1898).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London Univ.; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 21st Dec., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1929; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; registrar, high ct., Patna, Feb., 1939; sec., legis. dept., May, 1938; sec. bd. of conciliation, Nov., 1940; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1941; sec., judl. dept., March, 1941 to March, 1944; acting high ct. judge, Patna, Nov., 1944.

DAS, SUNDAR, K. (see Sundar Das, K.).

DAS, SUDHI RANJAN, apptd. judge high ct., Calcutta, Aug., 1944.

DAS GUPTA, KULADA CHARAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 3rd Jan., 1900).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and sub-divl. officer; jt. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1929, and again, Apl., 1933; addl. ditto, Oct., 1932; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1934; regr., high ct., March, 1942.

DAS GUPTA, PRITHWISH CHANDRA, M.A., A.C.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 3rd Aug., 1897).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1925; offg. ch. auditor, Burma Rlys., May, 1935; exr., outside audit dept., Bombay, Oct., 1935; again, Apl., 1937; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, March, 1937; examiner outside audit dept., Bombay, April, 1937; dep. govt. examiner of accts., B.B. & C.I. Rly., Oct., 1938; ch. audtr., rly. clg. accts., Delhi, Nov., 1939; cost accounts offr.; offr. of finl. advr. (supply), Sept., 1940.

DAS GUPTA, SURENDRA NATH, C.I.E., M.A., Ph.D. (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. Oct. 1887).—Retd., Oct., 1942.†

DASH, ARTHUR JULES, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 24th Apl., 1887).—Retd., Sept., 1942.†

DASS, AMARJIT KUMAR, Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 22nd Nov., 1916); apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Feb., 1940.

DASS, KAUSHAL, KUMAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 25th May, 1915).—Educ. at Allahabad University; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, commr. Madera municip., Apl., 1943.

DASTUR, SIR HORMAZDIAR PRIROZ, Kt., B.A., LL.B., Barr., late Bombay, Judicial Dept. (b. 20th March, 1887).—Retd., Apl., 1938.*

DASTUR, JEHANGIR FARDUNJI, M.Sc., D.I.C. late Indian Agric. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 12th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service, 14th May, 1910; mycologist, Dec., 1923; ret'd., Sept., 1941; re-employed as Impl. Mycologist, I.A.R.I., Apl., 1945.

DATT, DEV (see Dev Datt).

DATTA (see also Dutt).

DATTA, ABINASH CHANDRA, late Income-tax dept., Assam (b. 30th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Oct., 1915; income tax officer, Jan., 1927; asst. commr., June, 1934; ret'd., May, 1943.

DATTA, DINES CHANDRA, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) (b. 1st July, 1904).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 15th May, 1928; confd., July, 1930; asst. to pol. officer, Sadiya frontier tract, Apl., 1932; offg. supt., Jan., 1936.

DATTA, GURU SADAY (see Dutt, G.S.).

DATTA, NARENDRA NATH, Babu, B.A., Bihar C.S. (dep. mag. and dep. collr.) (b. 16th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service, 6th Aug., 1904; dep. collr., Oct., 1906; offg. dep. commr., May, 1933.

DATTA, PARES CHANDRA, M.B., Ch.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Railways (ch. medl. offr.) (b. 31st July, 1892).—Apptd. dist. medl. officer on probn., 1st May, 1925; confd., May, 1928; ch. med. offr., Apl., 1942.

DATTA, PROMODE CHANDRA, Rai Bahadur, C.I.E.—Apptd. member, govr.'s exec. council, Assam., 31st March, 1934; vacated office, March 1937; advocate-general, Assam, Aug., 1939 to Oct., 1943.

DATTA, RABINDRA NATH, B.A. (Oxon.), M.Sc. (All.), Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Central Provs., 14th Oct., 1927; dep. consvr., Nov., 1931.

DATTA, SISIR KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 27th Nov., 1911).—Educ. at Govt. High School Silchar, Assam; Calcutta Univ., and London School of Economics; joined the service, 14th Jan., 1937; arrived in India, 19th Feb., 1937, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dir. of indust. etc., Assam.

DATTA, SUDHINDRA MOHAN, B.A., Indian Police (b. 18th March, 1910).—Apptd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn., 15th Feb., 1934; confd., Feb., 1936.

DATTATRAYA VAMAN REGE (see Rege, D. V.).

DAUBENY, RONALD GILES, O.B.E., Indian Police, Indian Political Service (b. 19th July, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 21st Nov., 1926; offg. A.D.C. to Govt., May, 1929; pte. secy. to ditto, Apl., 1932; A.D.C. to the Viceroy, Oct., 1933; under secty. to A.G.G., Eastern States, Apl., 1936; asst. mag. and collr., Moradabad, Nov., 1936; under-secty. to resdt. for Eastern States, Nov., 1937; asst. pol. agent, Orissa States, Nov., 1938; confd. in the Indian Political Service, Feb., 1939; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Dec., 1939; H.B.M.'s vice-consul, Khorramshahr, Dec., 1940; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1941; sec. Brit. legation, Kabul, Feb., 1942; sec. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, June, 1943; O.B.E., Jan., 1945; first asst., to resdt., Kashmir, Jan., 1946.

DAUDPOTA, U. M., M.A., Ph.D., (Sind) (b. 1st June, 1897).—Apptd. prof. of Arabic, Ismail coll., Andheri (on probn.), Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; under govt. of Sind as dir. of pub. instruction (on probn.), March, 1939; confd., March, 1941.

DAUKES, SIR CLENDON TUBERVILLE, Kt., C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd. (b. 24th Dec., 1879).—Retd., Aug., 1935.*

DAVE, ANANDBHAI KARUNASHANKER, B.Sc.

Indian Police, Central Provinces (b. 13th Jan., 1921).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn. after exam. of 1941.

DAVE, KARUNASHANKAR NANDLAL, B.A., LL.B., late Income Tax Dept., Central Provs. (b. Sept., 1885).—Retd., 16th Sept., 1940.†

DAVID, ALAN MICHAEL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (Punjab) (b. 18th Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 11th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924; transf. to def. dept., Sept., 1944.

DAVID, JOHNSON ANTHONY (b. 21st July, 1897).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1916; regr. to ch. commr. Delhi, Nov., 1937; supt., home dept., Aug., 1941; offg. asst. secty. home dept., May, 1945.

DAVIDSON, DAVID ALEXANDER GIBB, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 15th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 19th May, 1922; asst. consvr., 1st Nov., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

DAVIES, ANDREW EVAN, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 24th March, 1914).—Joined the service as proby, asst. supt. of police, Bombay, Nov., 1933; confd., Apl., 1936; asst. cmdt. Sind Police Rifles, Oct.-Dec., 1944; supt. of pol. (confd.) June, 1945; reverted to Bombay, May, 1946.

DAVIES, ARTHUR, Burma Frontier Service, (b. 25th Oct., 1912).—Apptd., 30th Dec., 1936.

DAVIES, ARTHUR CECIL, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 28th May, 1889).—Retd., July, 1939.*

DAVIES, HAROLD ESCOTT, B.A., India Office (actg. princpl., mil. dept.) (b. 5th Nov., 1915).—Educ. at Aldenham School and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. princpl., 11th Oct., 1937; resident clerk, May, 1939; on milty. service from June, 1940; actg. princpl., in absentia, Jan., 1942; rejoined June, 1945.

DAVIES, HENRY MERSON, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.).—Retd. Jan., 1939.*

DAVIES, JOHN HUGH RATHBONE, Indian Police (b. 16th June, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt. United Provs., on probn., Dec., 1937.

DAVIES, LAWRENCE STANLEY RICHARD GILBERT, late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 19th June, 1894).—Retd. June, 1939.*

DAVIES, P. W. WILTON (see Wilton Davies).

DAVIES, ROBERT WILLIAM HOME, B.A., Indian C.S. (judl. commr. for W.I. and for Ajmer-Merwara) (b. 10th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Jan., 1922; arrived, 18th March, 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr. and supt. of stamps, May, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1925; asst. mag. and collr., Apl., 1928; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct. 1932; confd., Oct., 1933; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Oct., 1934; ditto, Kathiawar, Sept., 1936; offg. judl. commr. for W.I., and for Ajmer-Merwara, June, 1938, to Oct., 1938; confd. May, 1939.

DAVIES, ROLAND GWYNNE, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. Sind) (b. 11th February, 1906).—Educ. at the Grammar School, Bradford, and Queen's College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr; collr., (confd.) Sept., 1943.

DAVIES, ROY FREDERICK, M.A., Burma Civil Service (Cl.I) (b. 10th Oct., 1919).—Educ. Worksop Coll. Notts. and St. Catherine's Coll. Cambridge; apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

DAVIES, STUART THOMAS, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Maj. I.M.S. (b. 10th Sept., 1901).—First comm. 15th Aug., 1928; on milty. duty to Dec., 1936; dist. med. offr., Madras, Dec., 1936; on milty. duty May, 1941 to Feb., 1946; dist. med. offr., March, 1946.

DAVIES, VINCENT ELLIS, O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab.) Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Orissa) (b. 3rd June, 1903).—Educ. at St. John's Sch., Leatherhead, and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1926; arrived 20th Dec., 1926, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1933; sec. to govt. of Orissa, Jan., 1941; again July, 1942, confd. as mag. and collr., Apl., 1941; dist. mag. and collr., Bihar, Oct., 1944; sec. fin. dept., Sept., 1945; O.B.E., June, 1946.

DAVIES, WILLIAM FREDERICK MANT, Indian Police (b. 11th July, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 16th Nov., 1927; a.d.c. to govt., Mar., 1935, to Dec., 1936; served with cent. govt. civ. def. dept., Mar., 1942.

DAVIES-LEIGH (see under Leigh).

DAVIS, DAVID, Indian Forest Service (consvr. United Provs.) (b. 11th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1925; instructor, forest coll., Dehra Dun, Oct., 1930 to May, 1933; consvr. (confd.), Apl., 1940.

DAVIS, SIR GODFREY, Indian C.S. (chief judge, Sind) (b. 23rd Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr., asst. rembr. of legal affairs, Jan., 1920; asst. judge and addnl. sess. judge, Dec., 1922; secy. to cttee. on Bombay Stock Exchange, Sept., 1923 to Jan., 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1924; ditto, and agent for sardars in the Deccan, Aug., 1925; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1926; confd., Jan., 1929; secy. to govt., legal dept., Bombay, and rembr., legal affairs, Jan., 1932; member, legisl. council, Feb., 1932, judl. commr., Sind., Dec., 1935; chief judge, chief court of Sind, Apl., 1940; Kt., Jan., 1941.

DAVIS, HERBERT LUDLOW, Capt., C.I.E., R.I.N. (ret'd.) (Nautical Adviser to the Govt. of India) (b. 28th Feb., 1887).—Nautical advr. to govt. of India, Apl., 1940; addl. contr. of Ind. Shipping, Bombay, (in addn.) Mar., 1944. C.I.E., June, 1944.

DAVIS, HAROLD PERCY WHITTON, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Bombay) (b. 7th Dec., 1893).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1921, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1923; instr., forest research coll., June, 1926 to Apl., 1929; offg. consvr., June, 1935; served in Sind as extra consvr., Sept., 1944; consvr. from Nov., 1944.

DAVIS, PERCIVAL WILLIAM, M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 19th July, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926; offg. princpl., Madras forest coll., June, 1933.

DAVIS, PERCY, M.B.E., Stationery and Printing Dept., Punjab (b. 22nd Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, 3rd July, 1924, as works manager, central jail press, on probn.; confd., July, 1925; supt., Govt. printing, May, 1935; M.B.E., Jan., 1935.

DAVIS, WILLIAM JOHN, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1st Dec., 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1940.†

DAVISON, RICHARD GEORGE CHARLES

LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 5th June, 1916).—Educ. at Battersea Grammar School, King's College, London, and Trinity College, Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; spec. offr. legis. dept., Jan., 1946.

DAVY, ANTHONY EDWARD GAINS, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 10th Dec., 1908).—First commn., 30th Aug., 1928; pers. asst. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Oct., 1935; under sec. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Feb., 1936; under trng. in Punjab, Aug., 1936; city magistrate, Peshawar, March, 1938; asst. pol. agent, Zhob, Apr., 1939; confd. in Indian Political Service, May, 1939; asst. pol. agent, Orissa States, Dec., 1939; under sec. to resdt., Rajputana and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1941; under-sec., ext. affairs dept., Nov., 1943; on depn. to Assam, Oct., 1944; sec. to resdt. for central India, Aug., 1945.

DAVY, G. Burma Office apptd. princpl., 17th April, 1946.

DAVY, HORACE FREDERICK, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Stores dept. (contr. of inspn.) (b. 19th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. laby. officer, 2nd Dec., 1913; dep. supt. of local manufactures, Dec., 1918; contr. of inspn. (temp.), May, 1923; confd., Jan., 1925; offg. dir. of inspn., June, 1928; dep. chief controller of purchase (munities), dept. of Supply, June, 1940.

DAVY, PERCY HAROLD, (Civil Aviation Directorate) (b. 20th Aug., 1895).—Apptd. aircraft inspr., Mar., 1938; offer. in ch., mech. training sch., Feb., 1941; on depn. to dept. of supply, May, 1944; aircraft insp. Feb., 1945.

DAWSON, JAMES ALEXANDER, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 4th May, 1880).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

DAWSON, LIONEL EDWARD, B.A., M.A.I. late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 16th Jan., 1887).—Retd., 15th Jan., 1942.†

DAWSON, SIR VERNON, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. and India Office (b. 22nd Feb., 1881).—Retd., March, 1941.†

DAWSON, WILLIAM MAURICE GRANT, O.B.E., M.C., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., United Provs.) (b. 11th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; prof. civil engr. Thomason Coll., Roorkee, Oct., 1935 to Oct., 1936; confd. as suptg. engr., Jan., 1942; ch. engr., Oct., 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

DAY, BINGLEY WALDEMAR, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 2nd Feb., 1908).—Educ. at Stowe and Corpus Christi coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930; sub. collr., Nov., 1932; under-secty. public dept., Oct., 1937; temp. dep. ditto, July, 1940; temp. sec. educ. dept., Jan. to Feb., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1946; dep. commr. civil supplies, Madras, Feb., 1946.

DAY, FRANCIS EDWARD, India Audit Office (exec. offr.) (b. 22nd July, 1915).—Apptd. exec. officer, 23rd Jan., 1936; actg. hr. ditto, March, 1943; serving with H.M. forces to Dec., 1945.

DAY, JOHN THOMAS, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 18th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1941.†

DAYA KRISHNA, KHANNA, RAI SAHIB LALA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 19th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service, 24th Sept., 1915; asst. engr., Sept.,

1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. engr., May, 1940.

DAYAL, BISHAMBHAR, (see Bishambhar Dayal).

DAYAL, DEVI (see Devi Dayal).

DAYAL, HARISHWAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (Indian Political Service) (b. 15th July, 1915).—Educ. at Govt. Intermediate Coll., Allahabad, and Allahabad Univ.; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1940; under-sec. to resdt. for W. India States, Apr., 1942; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar, Sept., 1942; under-sec. to resdt., Rajkot civ. station, Jan., 1943; under-sec. ext. affairs dept., govt. of India, Mar., 1944; I.P.S. (confd.) Mar., 1945.

DAYAL, ISHWAR (see Ishwar, D.).

DAYAL, J. M.B.E., Milty. Accts. Dept. (b. 14th June, 1903).—Joined the service 23rd Apr., 1927; dep. fincl. advr., mily. fin., 1st Nov., 1940.

DAYAL, MADHU SUDAN (see Madhu Sudan, D.).

DAYAL, RAGHUBAR, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 26th Oct., 1900).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1925; arrived, 17th Nov., 1925, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag.; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Jan., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1933; confd., Sept., 1936.

DAYAL, RAJESHWAR (see Rajeshwar Dayal).

DE, INDRA NATH, Indian Police (supth. Orissa) (b. 20th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 24th Apr., 1925; offg. supt., March, 1933; confd., Jan., 1938.

DE, INDU BHUSAN, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 18th Jan., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, Dec., 1926; exec. engr., Dec., 1935.

DE, JNANANKUR, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 21st Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and University Coll., London; apptd. to I.C.S. by nomination; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1917; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dy. collr., Jan., 1922, and sub. divl. officer, May, 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1927; confd., Dec., 1928; first land acquisn. collr., Calcutta, Feb., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1939; offg. sec. judl. dept. and supt. and rememb. legal affairs, Bengal, Sept., 1945.

DE, RAJENDRA NATH, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Assam) (b. July, 1891).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1914; proby. extra asst. consvr., July, 1916; confd., July, 1917; apptd. to I.F.S. as dep. consvr., 3rd Nov., 1928. junr. consvr., Mar., 1943.

DEAN, SIR ARTHUR WILLIAM HENRY, C.I.E., B.Sc., M.C., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., central P.W.D.) (b. 7th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 3rd Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Apr., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; transf. to Indore, Jan., 1933; temp. suptg. engr., aviation circle, Feb., 1935; offg. suptg. engr., construction circle, Apr., 1936; confd., Dec., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1941; addl. ch. engr., Jan., 1942; ch. engr., Sept., 1942; on spl. duty in addn., Oct., 1944; Kt., June, 1946.

DEAN, JOHN MARTIN, Indian Police (b. 9th

July, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1936.

DEANE, DONALD VICTOR, O.B.E., Major R.E. (b. 19th Oct., 1902).—(mint master Calcutta) ; mil. employ up to 21st Dec., 1932 ; dep. mint master on probn., 30th Dec., 1932 ; confd., Oct., 1934 ; offg. mint master variously from Feb., 1934 ; mint master, confd., Aug., 1940 ; O.B.E., Jan., 1941.

DEANE, LANCELOT VICTOR, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 14th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 22nd Nov., 1925 ; asst. commr., Port Blair, Jan., 1930 ; asst. to D.I.G., June, 1939 ; confd. as supt., March, 1942.

DEANE, MAHOMED KHAIRUB, B.Sc., Factory Dept., Bombay (inspr.) (b. 16th Apr., 1891).—Apptd. inspr. of factories, Bombay, 20th Nov., 1923 ; offg. ch. inspr., Feb., 1931.

DEANS, HARRY SCOTT, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (consvr., N.W.F.P.) (b. 29th March, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 15th Dec., 1922 ; on deputation to the Kashmir state from 4th March, 1924 ; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927 ; consvr. N.W.F.P., Jan., 1946.

DEB, BIRENDRANATH, Office of the High Commr. for India (asst. to Indian trade publicity officer) (b. 13th Aug., 1881).—Apptd. to Office of H.C. for I., Apl., 1921 ; clerical officer, Feb., 1924 ; exec. officer, Feb., 1927 ; asst. to Indian trade publicity officer, Nov., 1929.

DEB, N. C., Indian State Rlys.—Jt. dir., rly. bd., Feb., 1946.

DEBENDRA NATH SEN GUPTA (see Sen Gupta, D. N.).

DEBI DATTA MAI, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Service of Engs. (United Provs.) (b. 23rd Feb., 1884).—Retd., June, 1939.*

DEBI LAL SAH, B.A., M.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 7th March, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. working plan officer on 26th Dec., 1925 ; asst. consvr. July, 1926 ; dep. consvr., Feb., 1933.

DE CHAZEL, JOHN ROBERT, Indian Police (b. 19th Feb., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 26th Nov., 1936 ; confd., Jan., 1939 ; on depn., central govt., Oct., 1943.

DE GALE, HUGH OTWAY, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Punjab) (b. 9th Jan., 1891).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1911 ; asst. supt., May, 1913 ; on mil. duty from 15th Nov., 1917 to 28th Feb., 1919 ; King's police medal, Jan., 1921 ; supt., Jan., 1924 ; awarded bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1925 ; princpl. police training school, Phillour, Apl., 1927 ; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1933 ; ditto, (provl. subs.), June, 1935 ; offg. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1935 ; C.I.E., June, 1938.

DEHEJIA, VENILAL TRIBHOVANDAS, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (under-secty., dept. of e. h. and l.) (b. 23rd July, 1908).—Educ. at Bombay University, Rl. College of Science, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London ; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931 ; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and supt., salt and excise ; dep. secty. rev. dept., Sind, Sept., 1939 ; attaché, e. h. and l. dept., govt. of India, March, 1940 ; under-secty., e. h. and l. dept., govt. of India, June, 1940.

DEHLAVI, SAMIULLA KHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Sept., 1913).—Educ. at Anglo-Urdu High Scho., Poona, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford ; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937 ; confd., 1st Oct., 1938 ; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served

in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. ; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1940.

DE KRETSEK, CLAUDE BERTRAM, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 23rd Oct., 1892).—Educ. at University Coll., London, and Wadham Coll., Oxford ; apptd. after exam. of 1916 ; arrived, 17th Jan., 1918, and served in Burma as asst. commr. ; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, offg. asst. collr. of customs, Jan., 1920, to July, 1921 ; services re-placed at disposal of govt. of Burma, July, 1921 ; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1923 ; confd., Sept., 1927 ; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1941 ; on depn. to govt. of India as prest., third spl., trib., Lahore, May, 1945.

DE LA FOSSE, SIR CLAUDE FRASER, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Educl. Dept., United Provs.—Retd., 1923.*

DELAHAY, VEN. WILLIAM, late Indian Eccles. Estab., (Burma) (b. 25th Jan., 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

DE LA NOUGERREDE, FREDERICK ADAIR, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly.) (b. 15th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 16th July, 1915 ; asst. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., July, 1918 ; offg. dist. contr. of stores variously from Jan., 1919 ; confd., March, 1931 ; offg. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., Feb., 1935.

DE LA RUE, CHARLES ARTHUR, M.A., Burma Frontier Service, (b. 13th Oct., 1917).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

DELL, PERCY BERTRAM, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 15th Nov., 1888).—Second class clerk, bd. of trade, Sept., 1908 to Sept., 1909 ; ditto, bd. of edn., Sept., 1909 to June, 1911 ; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 9th June, 1911, and attd. to registry and record dept. ; transfed. to store dept., Jan., 1917 ; on mil. service from Aug., 1914 to July, 1919 ; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920 ; exec. officer, Apl., 1922 ; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1926 ; higher exec. offcr., May, 1938 ; M.B.E., June, 1938 ; temp. prin. exec. offr., Apl., 1941.

DE MELLOO, M. (b. 25th May, 1908).—Joined the service, 9th Apl., 1940 ; exec. eng., cent. P.W.D., Port Blair, Sept., 1945.

DE MONTMORENCY, SIR GEOFFREY FITZHERBEY, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., K.C.V.O., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 23rd Aug., 1876).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

DENCH, WILLIAM GEORGE, C.I.E., B.A., B.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs., engr. (Punjab) (b. 1st Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912 ; exec. engr., Oct., 1921 ; under-secty. to govt., irrigation branch, Punjab, Apl., 1931 ; under-secty. (North), Punjab irrigation sectt., Jan., 1933 ; offg. supt. engr., May, 1933, and again, Feb., 1936 ; sec., p.w.d. irrig., June, 1940 ; C.I.E., Jan., 1944 ret'd., 1st Dec., 1943.

DENNEHY, SIR HAROLD GEORGE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. sec. to the govt. of Assam) (b. 18th Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Clifton and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge ; apptd. after exam. of 1913 ; arrived, 6th Dec., 1914, and served in Assam as asst. commr., ; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1915 to March, 1919 ; under-sec. to govt. of Assam., Apl., 1923 ; offg. chief sec., ditto, Apl., 1924 ; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1925 ; confd., June, 1933 ; offg. secty. to govt. in transferred depts., Assam. June, 1929 ; confd., June, 1933 ; offg. ch. secty., Assam, Sept., 1936 ; C.I.E., Feb., 1937 ; sect, educ. and local self govt. depts., Feb., 1938 ; chief secty. to govt., Assam, Apl., 1939 ; confd.,

Oct., 1940; C.S.I., Jan., 1942; Kt., Jan., 1946.

DENNIS, REGINALD BURTON, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.C.G.I., M.I.E.E., F.R.A.I., F.R.G.S.—Indian p. and t. (dir. of tels.) (b. 25th Apl., 1900).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1923, as asst. divl. engr.; divl. engr., Jan., 1931; dy. ch. engr., Nov. 1940; dir. of tels., May, 1944.

DENT, THOMAS VALENTINE, M.B.E., Indian Forest serv. (b. 4th Apl., 1909).—Joined the serv. as asst. convr. Bengal, 11th Oct., 1929; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

DENT, EDWARD JOHN MACDONALD, Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 6th June, 1918).—Educ. at Wellington and Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in N.W.F.P. as asst. commr.

DEO, SIR RAJENDRA NARAYAN BHANJA, Raja Kt., O.B.E.—Member of govt.'s exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1929 to Jan., 1934; vice-presdnt., ditto, Dec., 1931; O.B.E., June, 1918; knighted, June, 1933.

DERBYSHIRE, SIR HAROLD, Kt., K.C., M.C., Bengal Judl. Dept.; ch. justice high ct., Calcutta, 12th Nov., 1934, to Nov., 1946.

DERRY, ROBERT LAWRIE, Indian Forest Service (b. 17th Dec., 1898) (Bihar).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 17th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930; divl. for. offr., Jan., 1940; on l.p.r. from Apl., 1946.

DESAI, CHANDULAL CHUNILAL, C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 27th Apl., 1900).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Cambridge Univ. (non-collegiate); joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived 26th Nov., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr., offg. dep. commr., June, 1927; and again, Feb., 1931; regr. co-op. society and dir. of indus., March, 1937; dep. commr., Nov., 1937; sec. local self-govt. medical and public health dept., June, 1939; sec. local self-govt. dept. July, 1941; C.I.E., June, 1945.

DESAI, MANILAL JAGDISHBHAI, C.I.E., B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S., (collr. Bombay) (b. 4th Jan., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the serv. 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and serv. in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dir. of land records, etc., Bombay, Oct., 1935; collr., June, 1937; dep. secty., rev. dept., Bombay, May, 1938, to March, 1939; settlement commr. and dir. land records, Jan., 1940 to March, 1942; sec. to govt. rev. dept., Sept., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

DESAI, MANULAL CHUNILAL, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 25th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Cambridge (non-collegiate).—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., March, 1928; jt. mag., Nov., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1933; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1934; collr. and mag. (confd.) July, 1939; dist. and sess. judge (confd.) Sept., 1945; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

DESAI, SHANKAR PANDURANG, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 17th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Bombay and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 7th Dec., 1915 and served in Assam as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt. of Assam, July, 1921, to July, 1923; asst. commr. and settl. officer, Oct., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1929, and again, Dec., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; offg. secty. fin. and rev. depts., June, 1931; and again, March, 1934; offg. secty.,

transf. depts., Assam, Apl., 1933; offg. dir. of land records, etc., Assam, Jan., 1934; and again, Nov., 1934; secty., fin. dept., Assam, Apl., 1936 to May, 1938; again secty. fin. and rev. depts., March, 1939; secty. educ. and local self-govt. depts., Jan., 1940; dir. of land records, etc.; dir. of surveys and commr. of inc.-tax, Assam, Feb., 1941.

DESHMUKH, SIR CHINTAMAN DWARKANATH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (offr. on special duty, fin. dept., govt. of India) (b. 14th Jan., 1896).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1919; arrived, 20th Apl., 1920, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1925; confd., July, 1929; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; offg. rev. sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., Feb., 1932; finl. secty. ditto, June, 1933; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; fin. secty. and secty. p.w.d., Cent. Provs., Nov., 1937; employed under the Cent. govt., Apl., 1939; offr. on special duty, fin. dept., govt. of India, July, 1939; Kt., Jan., 1944.

DESHMUKH, RAMRAO MADHAVRAO, B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.)—Late Finance minister, Gwalior State and member Nat. Defence Council; apptd. high commr. for India in S.Africa, Jan., 1945.

DE SMIDT, GERALD EWALD OVERBECK, D.S.O., late India Posts and Tels. (Burma) (b. 1st June, 1885).—Retd., June, 1940.†

DE SOUZA, F. J., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Railways (govt. inspr. N.W. Rly.) (b. 29th Sept., 1892).—Asst. exec. engr., 24th Oct., 1916; exec. engr., Oct., 1935; ditto senr. scale, Apl., 1939; confd. as govt. inspr. of rlys., Feb., 1944; confd. senr. govt. inspr., July, 1945.

DEV DATT, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (govt. inspt. of rlys., Comm. Dept.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1915; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; ditto, senr. scale, July, 1935; supt., May, 1939; govt. inspr. (snr. scale), Feb., 1944; ch. govt. inspr. rlys., July, 1945.

DEVAOSS, SIR MUTHIAH DAVID, Kt., Barr., late Madras Judl. Dept.—Retd., Dec., 1928.*

DEVASAHAYAM, JACOB, RAO BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 9th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on 7th June, 1923; offg. dist. supt., June, 1929; confd., July, 1936.

DEVENISH, REV. ROBERT CECIL SYLVESTER, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Punjab). (b. 22nd Nov., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 27th Oct., 1919; junior chaplain, Oct., 1921, on mil. duty from 24th Nov., 1921, to 30th Nov., 1922; senr. chaplain March, 1928; offg. archdeacon, Apl., 1933; confd., May, 1934; ret. 28th Feb., 1943.

DEVI DAYAL, B.A., late Indian Service of Engineers (Assam) (b. Oct., 1890).—Joined the service, 15th October, 1914; asst. engr., Feb., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; asst. ch. engr. and under-secty. to govt., p.w.d., Assam, July, 1932 to Apl., 1936; supt. engr., March, 1942; ret. d.

DEVI DAYAL DHAWAN, RAI BAHADUR LALA, M.Sc., LL.B., late Punjab Civil Service, (b. 15th Oct., 1888).—Extra asst. commr., Jan., 1934; sub judge, Nov., 1920; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; judge, high court, Lahore, 29 Jan., 1943; ret. d., Oct., 1943.

DEVI DAYAL SAIGAL, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 10th Dec.,

1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 8th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

DEVI SINGH, M.A., Lala, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th Dec., 1884).—Retd.†

DE VINE, JAMES CHANCELLOR, late Indian Police (b. 9th Aug., 1898).—Retd., June, 1940.†

DEVLIN, REV. THOMAS STAFFORD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 27th May, 1892).

—Apptd. chaplain on probation, Jan., 1923; junior chaplain, 5th Jan., 1925; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1933; retd., June, 1946.

DEY, BIMAN BEHARI, RAO BAHARDUR M.Sc., (Cal.) D.Sc., (Lond.), late Indian Educl. Serv. (b. 1st Nov., 1889).—Joined the serv., 2nd Nov., 1916, and served in Bengal to 9th Apl., 1920; addl. prof. of chemistry, presdy. coll., Madras, Jan., 1924; prof. of chem., Feb., 1937; retd., Nov., 1944; re-empl. as princpl. presdy. coll., Madras, Nov., 1944; dir. of public instrn., Madras, Jan., 1945.

DEY, PROMODE KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (plant pathologist, United Provs.) (b. 25th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as plant pathologist on 1st March, 1920; confd. in sel. grade, May, 1941.

DEY, SUSHIL KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 16th Feb., 1907).

—Educ. at Calcutta University, London School of Economics and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal, as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931; controller, A.R.P., Bengal, Apl., 1944; dir. relief and rehabilitation, Bengal, and ex-officio jt. sec. dept. co-opern. credit and relief Off. 1945.

DHAND, MILKHI RAM, B.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th March, 1892).—Retd. Dec., 1940.†

DHANGRIA, HOTCHAND HARUMAL, RAI BAHADUR, (P.W.D., Sind).—Joined the service, March, 1914; non-gaz. appts. to Jan., 1924; asst. engr., Feb., 1924; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1940; exec. engr., Aug., 1945; on depn. as administrator, Shikahpur municipality, Nov., 1944.

DHANPAT RAI, B.A., RAI SAHIB, Indian Posts and Teleg. (b. 7th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as proby. supt., Aug., 1920; Rai Sahib, 1920; confd. supt., July, 1925; dep. p.m.g., Jan., 1946.

DHANPAT RAI, RAI SAHIB, M.B.E., dept. of E. H. and L. (b. 25th July, 1882).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

DHANVADA ANANDA RAO (see Ananda Rao, D.).

DHAR, DEBABRATA, B.A., Indian Police, Assam (b. 1st March, 1921).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn. after exam. of 1942.

DHAR, NIL RATAN (see Nil Ratzan Dhar).

DHAR, SATYENDRA MOHAN, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. sec. govt. of India, agric. dept.) (b. 25th Mar., 1897).—Educ. at Mayurbhanj Sch.,

Orissa, Univ. Coll. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1921; arrived, 18th Jan., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1928; offg. dist. mag., and collr., Dec., 1931; dir. of industries, Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1935; mag. and collr., Jan., 1936; secty., educ. dept., var., from Feb., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1942; offg. commr. Apl., 1941 and June, 1942; devel. commr., Feb., 1944; jt. sec. govt. India, agric. dept., Mar., 1946.

DHARM DAS, JAINI LALA, R.B., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 16th

March, 1897).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1930.

DHAVLE, SANKARA BALAJI, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Oct., 1882).—Retd., 1942.†

DHAWAN, PURUSHOTTAM LAL, RAI BAHADUR, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Oct., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1941.†

DHIR, RAM DAS, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 29th Dec., 1906).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 3rd, Aug., 1932; exec. engr., Aug., 1941.

DHURANDHAR, JAGANNATH RAVJI, O.B.E., B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 8th March, 1884).—Retd., March, 1941.†

DIAS, ANTHONY LANCELOT, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 13th March, 1910).—Educ. at St. Vincent's High Sch., Poona, Univ. of Bombay, the Sch. of Economics, London, and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; supt. land records, N.C. Ahmedabad, from Nov., 1938 to Feb., 1939, and again May, 1939.

DIBDIN, AUBREY, C.I.E., M.A., (asst. secty., Burma Office) (b. 4th March, 1892).—Educated at Tonbridge and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. junior clerk, 26th January, 1920; war service—served in India with 1/4th Bn. The Queen's (R.W.S.R.), Oct., 1914 to Nov., 1919; asst. principal and pte. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state, Apl., 1921; principal (actg.), Jan., 1924; confd., Apl., 1924; Internatl. labour conf., Geneva, 12th, 15th, 16th, 17th, 18th, 19th and 20th sessions; adviser to Indian Govt. delegation; 21st and 22nd sessions, delegate; jt. secty., R.C., on Indian labour, Apl., 1929 to May, 1931; dep. clerk of the council, Sept., 1934 to July, 1936; asst. secty., reforms dept. (temp.), July, 1936; asst. secty., public and judl. dept., Apl., 1938; ditto, econ. and overseas dept. June 1941; C.I.E., June, 1943; asst. sec. Burma Office, Nov., 1945.

DIBLE, WILLIAM CUTHBERT, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 18th June, 1886).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch., Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1910, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; dep. commr., Nov., 1918; mag. and collr., Nov., 1925; offg. commr., Apl., 1934; confd., Feb., 1940; member bd. of rev.; C.I.E., June, 1938; retd., June, 1946.

DICK, ALAN MACDONALD, C.B.E., M.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S. (prof. King Edward medical coll., Lahore) (b. 30th July, 1884).—First commission, 31st July, 1909; O.B.E., Feb., 1921; apptd. principal, medical sch., Amritsar, Sept., 1922; prof. of ophthalmology, King Edward medl. coll., Lahore, Oct., 1923; C.B.E., Jan., 1936.

DICKENS, CHARLES, M.B.E., A.M. Tech. I., Indian Posts and Teleg. (b. 19th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as proby. supt., Sept., 1919; confd., Nov., 1921; M.B.E., 1935; dep. p.m.g., Jan., 1946.

DICKESON, CHARLES FREDERICK, India Audit Office (higher exec. officer) (b. 23rd Feb., 1893).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 24th Sept., 1914, and attached to store dept.; transf. to India audit office, Jan., 1915; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; higher clerical officer, Aug., 1926; higher exec. officer, Aug., 1930; actg. suptg. exec. officer, Jan., 1941.

DICKINSON, E. C., Punjab Educ. Service (prof. of English, govt. coll.) (b. 12th May, 1892).—Joined the service, 31st Dec., 1927; apptd. prof. of English, govt. coll., from 31st Dec., 1927.

DICKINSON, FRITZ GEORGE, M.C., B.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 11th June, 1891).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

DIKSHIT, HARI KRISHNA, M.B., B.S., L.M. (Dub.), United Provs. Medl. Service, (b. 20th Oct., 1892).—Apptd., 10th Dec., 1919; civil surgeon (confd.) 6th Oct., 1939.

DIKSHIT, KASHINATH NARAYAN, Rao Bahadur, M.A., Archaeological Survey Dept. (dir. genl.) (b. 21st Oct., 1889).—Joined the service, 29th March, 1917; supt., Sept., 1922; dep. dir.-genl., Feb., 1935; dir.-genl., Sept., 1937.

DIKSHIT, MADHAY JAGANNATH, M.A., B.Sc., late Bombay C.S. (b. 31st Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

DILAWAR HUSAIN KHAN, M.B.E., M.A., Indian Forest Service (asst. consvr., Orissa) (b. Apl., 1896).—Joined the service as proby. extra-asst. consvr., 5th Apl., 1922; confd., Apl., 1923; asst. consvr., Jan., 1929; for research offr., Orissa, Nov., 1938; M.B.E., June, 1943.

DILLON, HENRY VERE SHIRLEY, B.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 9th Nov., 1884).—Retd., June, 1939.*

DIMOND, WILLIAM ELLIOT RANDAL, C.I.E.,* Col., I.M.S. (b. 29th Sept., 1893).—First commn., dated 27th Sept., 1919; in mil. employ to July, 1925; asst. dir. of public health, N.W.F.P. (civil), July, 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; inspr. gen. civ. hosp. and prisons, Aug., 1944.

DIN MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, Shaikh, M.A., late Punjab C.S., Judl. Bch. (b. March, 1884).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1907; sub-judge, Oct., 1915; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1924; confd., June, 1933; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, March, 1934; and again, July, 1936; member, Ind. delimitn. ctee., Aug., 1935; judge, high ct., Lahore, March, 1937; on depn. as chairman, air transpt. licensing bd., June, 1946; retd., Dec., 1946.

DIVATIA, SIR HARSIDHBHAI VAJUBHAI, M.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Bombay. —Apptd. addl. judge, high court, Bombay, 19th, June, 1933; judge, ditto, June, 1934; chairman, textile enquiry committee, Bombay, Oct., 1938 to July, 1939; Kt., Jan., 1945; retd., Feb., 1946.

DIVATIA, SHRINIVAS SATYENDRA, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind.) (b. 21st Dec., 1898).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 1st Nov., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1923; offg. exec. engr., June, 1927; confd., Nov., 1932.

DIXON, ALGERNON DREW, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 17th Oct., 1907).—Educated at Bedford School, Corpus Christi College, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Feb., 1934; exec. offcr., Cawnpore improvement trust, Apl. to Oct., 1937.

DIXON, ARTHUR FREDERIC WILLIAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag. Madras.) (b. 11th May, 1892).—Educ. at Christ's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 18th August, 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920; sub-collr. and jt. mag., and education dept., Sept., 1929; offg. collr. and dist. mag. Aug., 1933; confd., March, 1935; officer in

charge, reforms (Orissa) dept., March, 1936; secty., educ. dept., Orissa, April, 1936, and again, Dec., 1936; chief secty., Orissa, March to Nov., 1937; secty., local admin. dept., Feb., 1940; Diwan of Cochin, Oct., 1941; C.I.E. June, 1942; collr., Nov., 1943; member, bd. of rev., Sept., 1944; C.S.I., Jan., 1946; sec. govt. food dept., Mar., 1946.

DIXON, MICHAEL GREY, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 24th June, 1910).—First commn., 27th Aug., 1931; pers. asst. to A.G.G. in Central India, Nov., 1935; under trng. in for. and pol. dept., Jan., 1936; under-sec. to A.G.G., Gujarat States and resdt. at Baroda, March, 1936; under trng. in Punjab, Nov., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Apl., 1938; confd. in Indian political service, Feb., 1939; on special duty in Kashmir, Sept., 1939; under-secty. to resdt. for Punjab States, Apl., 1940; vice-consul, Kabul, Jan., 1944; city mag., Peshawar, Dec., 1944; dep. commr., Mardan, Aug., 1945; asst. commr., Peshawar, Jan., 1946; under sec. ext. aff., dept., July, 1946.

DODD, PATRICK HUBERT FORSTER, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 22nd Aug., 1916).—Educ. at King's School, Canterbury, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 29th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.

DODD, SIR ROBERT JOHN SHERWOOD, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 26th July, 1878).—Retd., July, 1933.*

DODSWOTRH, OWEN ALARIC, I.S.O., late Indian Forest Service (b. 13th Sept., 1883).—Retd. Jan., 1939.*

DODWELL, DAVID WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A., Ph.D. (Columbia), Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag.) (b. 13th Dec., 1898).—Educ. at Banbury County Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922; sub. collr., and jt. mag., Jan., 1924; tutor to minor Maharaja of Travancore, June, 1925; to Apl., 1928; offg. collr. and mag., Sept., 1931; addl. dep. secty., fin. dept., Madras (temp.), Nov., 1935; and again, Aug., 1936; sec. fin. dept., Aug., 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

DODWELL, EDWARD ARTHUR, Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 9th Sept., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

DOGRA, HANS RAJ, C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1919; suptg. engr., Jan., 1937; chief engr., Madras, May, 1938; retd., Dec., 1940; re-apptd. temp. ch. engr., March, 1944, to Feb., 1946; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

DOHERTY, REV. ROBERT BRANDAN, B.A., Indian Eccles., Estabt., Bombay (b. 14th July, 1906).—Apptd. junr. chaplain on probn., 18th Dec., 1939.

DOK, U THA, Burma Engrg. Service (C.I.) (b. 21st Dec., 1906).—Asst. exec. engr. (P.W.D.) Apl., 1931; exec. engr., Apl., 1939.

DONALD, ARTHUR ALEXANDER, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-gen., Burma) (b. 1st Apl., 1899).—Joined the service on 5th June, 1920; offg. dist. supt. Apl., 1928; confd., Dec., 1931; on mil. duty; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; dep. inspr.-gen., 1946.

DONALD, SIR JAMES, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., LL.M., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 26th Dec., 1873).—Retd., Feb., 1929.*

DONALD, JOHN OMMANNEY STEWART, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 10th Oct.,

1905).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1925; in milty. employ until 19th Jan., 1931; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Sept., 1932; ditto, 5th Waziristan, June, 1933; asst. commnr., Mansehra, March, 1936; asst. pol. agt. N. Waziristan, Aprl., 1937; asst. commr., Hangu, March, 1938; ditto, Tank, Aug., 1940; city mag. and asst. commnr., Peshawar, Aug., 1941; pol. agt., Kurram, Sept., 1942; dep. commr., May, 1944; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Jul., 1945; pol., agt., S. Waziristan, Aprl., 1946.

DONALD, SIR JOHN STUART, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian Pol. Dept.—Retd., Aprl., 1920.*

DONALDSON, EION PELY, C.M.G., India Office (actg. asst. sec. ext. dept.) (b. 15th Aprl., 1896).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxfd. (classical scholar); apptd. asst. principal secretary's office, Inland Revenue, 8th Sept., 1920; transf. to India Office, 1st Oct., 1924; pte. secty. to parly. under-secty. of state for India, Dec., 1927; pte. secty. to perm. ditto, Aprl., 1930; principal, July, 1930; on depon. for the course of instn. at Impl. Defence Coll., Jan. to Dec., 1936; posted to Burma Office, Aprl., 1937; war service—served in France with Machine Gun Corps, March, 1916 to March, 1919; Major, Reserve of Officers, June, 1919; on loan to the War Cabinet as actg. asst. secty., Feb., 1940; C.M.G., Jan., 1943; actg. asst. sec. in absentia, Feb., 1942; rejoined India Office as actg. asst. secty. external dept., Aug., 1945.

DONALDSON, JAMES DUFF, late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 9th Jan., 1884).—Retd., Aprl., 1940.

DONALDSON, JOHN COOTE, C.I.E., M.C., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provinces) (b. 24th May, 1895).—Educ. at Felsted, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1920; arrived, 5th Dec., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Feb., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1925; confd., July, 1932; offg. dep. secty. to govt., industries and revenue branches, Oct., 1927; dir. of ind., U.P., Oct., 1930; ditto Malerkotla State, Sept., 1935; secty. to govr. U.P., Aprl., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1939; jt. sec. to the govr.-genl. (public), March, 1940, to Sept., 1942; retd., 1946.

DONNELLY, REV. ARTHUR FERGUSON, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 30th Sept., 1914).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Calcutta Diocese, 16th May, 1944.

DONNISON, FRANK SIEGFRIED VERNON, C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. sec., home and judicial department) (Burma) (b. 3rd Jul., 1898).—Educated at Marlborough College and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; joined the service, 30th October, 1932; arrived, 25th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; warden, oilfields, March, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1929; confd., Jan., 1936; secty. to fin. commrs., March, 1937; secty., judicial dept., March, 1939; C.B.E., Jan., 1943; rep. to govt. of Burma, New Delhi, Sept., 1942; sec. to dep. govr., Sept., 1943; civ. aff. serv., Oct., 1944; dep. commr., Oct., 1945; ch. sec. home and judcl., dept., Feb., 1946.

DONOGHUE, EVANS HERBERT COLERIDGE BAINTYN, (asst. secty. govr. gens' council) (b. 7th Aug., 1896).—On milty. service Aug., 1915 to Oct., 1919, joined the service, 4th June, 1921; supt. office of sec. to exec. council of the govr.-gen., Nov., 1935; asst. sec. ditto, Aug., 1945.

DOOG, JOHN MYLES GREGORY, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., Burma Rlys.) (b. 22nd Aug., 1884).—Joined the service as apprentice engr.,

5th Feb., 1910; asst. engr. (tempy.), Aug., 1910; ditto (perm.), Aug., 1911; on milty. duty from 18th July, 1917 to 3rd Aprl., 1919; dist. engr., Jan., 1927; dep. ch. engr., July, 1937.

DORAB, N. D. KHANDALAVALA (see Khandalavala, D. N. D.).

DORAN, ALFRED JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd March, 1908; asst. supt., Jan., 1922; transpn. supt. (traffic comml.) E.I. Rly, May, 1934.

DORMAN-SMITH, SIR REGINALD HUGH, P.C., G.B.E.—Apptd. Govr. of Burma, 6th May, 1941 to Aug., 1946.

DOSSETOR, REV. FRANCIS ERNEST, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 16th Dec., 1885).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

DOUGLAS, REV. PERCY SHOLTO DOUGLAS, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 13th March, 1887).—Retd., Sept., 1940.

DOW, SIR HUGH, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 8th May, 1886).—Educ. at Aske's School, Hatcham, S.E. and University College, London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1910, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; munpl. commr., Surat, from Oct., 1916; asst. commr. in Sind for civil supplies and recruiting, July, 1918; dep. contr. of prices, Sind, Sept., 1919, to Aprl., 1920; dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1921; in addn., passport officer from Jan. to July, 1923; actg. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Oct., 1923; dep. sec., ditto, Nov., 1923 (tempy.); fin. advr. to P.W.D. in Sind (tempy.), Dec., 1925; offg. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1926; rev. offr., Lloyd barrage project (tempy.), Jan., 1927; C.I.E., June 1932; jt. secty., to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Oct., 1934; addl. secty., ditto (tempy.), June, 1936; offg. secty. ditto, Oct., 1936; confd., March, 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; dir. gen. of supply, govt. of India, Aug., 1939; vice-pres., war supply board, July, 1940; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1940; apptd. govr. of Sind, 1st Aprl., 1941 to Jan., 1946.

DOW, THOMAS MILLER, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th Dec., 1890).—Retd., March, 1941†

DOWLEY, FRANCIS MICHAEL, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 29th Sept., 1885).—Retd., Jan., 1941†

DOWLING, LIONEL EDWARD, late Indian State Railways (b. 30th Aug., 1885).—Retd.

DOWNING, FRANCIS WILLIAM HALL, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 26th March, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., N.W.F.P., on 30th Sept., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1932; confd., Sept., 1933.

DOWNING, HENRY JULIAN, Indian C.S. (sub collr. and jt. mag.) (b. 22nd Mar., 1919).—Educ. at Trowbridge High Sch. and Hertford Coll., St. Andrews' Univ., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn. after seln., in May, 1940; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1943.

DRACUP, ATHELSTANE HAMLEIGH, O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab.), late Bombay C.S. (b. 26th Oct., 1887).—Retd., Oct., 1942†

DRAKE, ANTONY ELLIOT, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (b. 15th June, 1907).—Educ. at Aldenham Sch. and New College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to resdt., Rajputana, March, 1937; asst. pol. agent and colonisation officer Nasirabad, Nov., 1939; asst. pol. offr., Nov.,

1939; offr. in charge of civic guards and A.R.P. contrl. in Baluchistan, Aug., 1941; sec. to resdt., Mysore, Sept., 1943; O.B.E., June, 1946.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, SIR DIGBY LIVINGSTONE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 21st Dec., 1877).—Retd., Dec., 1936.*

DRAVID, NILKANTH KRISHNA, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 24th Sept., 1908).—Educ. at London Sch. of Economics and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, Sept., 1938.

DRAYSON, GEORGE FREDERICK, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 11th Dec., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 8th Aug., 1904; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to Sept., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; pers. asst. to high commr., July, 1927; actg. higher exec. officer, Oct., 1928; confd., June, 1929; actg. princpl. exec. offr., Apl., 1941.

DREDGE, ALLAN LANCELOT AUSTIN, M.B.E., Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 14th Nov., 1907).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1927; in mil. employ up to Oct., 1933; under-secty. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, Oct., 1933; officer on special duty, Peshawar, Dec., 1935; under-secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1936; officer on special duty, civil secretariat, Peshawar, Feb., 1936; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, March, 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; vice-consul, Zahidan, Nov., 1937; asst. commr., Bannu, Apl., 1939; ditto, Nowshera sub. divn., Aug., 1940; vice-consul, Khwramshahr, Nov., 1941; M.B.E., June, 1944; dep. commr., Bannu, July, 1944.

DREWE, GEOFFREY GRABHAM, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to Govr. of Bombay) (b. 3rd May, 1904).—Educ. at Cheltenham, Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, Lond.; joined the serv., 12th Oct., 1928; arrived Nov., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr., March, 1932; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1935; collr. and dist. mag., confd., Dec., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; sec. to Govr. of Bombay, Dec., 1945.

DRING, ARTHUR JOHN, Maj., C.I.E., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 4th Nov., 1902).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1923; in milty. employ. to June, 1927; asst. pte. secty. to govr.-genl., 16th July, 1931; asst. commr., Tank, Feb., 1934; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Feb., 1935; dep. secty. to the govr.-genl.'s exec. council (temp.), May, 1936; pte. secty. to govr., N.W.F.P., March, 1937; secty., ditto, Apl., 1937; pol. agent in Chagai, March, 1940; home secty., N.W.F.P., July, 1940; pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; sec. to govt., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1943; ditto (food), Dec., 1943.

DRUMMOND, ROBERT OLIVE, M.B.E., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., United Provs., 11th Oct., 1929; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934; M.B.E., Jan., 1939.

D'SOUZA, FRANK, C.I.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 6th Dec., 1883).—Retd., July, 1940.†

D'SOUZA, MICHAEL, M.B.E., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 8th Jan., 1887).—Held non-gazetted posts from 3rd Aug., 1903; supt., tel. traffic, July, 1925; ch. supt., Jan., 1936; retd., Nov., 1943; M.B.E., Jan., 1945.

DUBE, ANANT PARSHAO, Rai Bahadur,

I.S.O. (b. 24th March, 1884).—Asst. secty., govt. of India, def. dept., March, 1937; I.S.O., June, 1938; retd., July, 1939; re-appointed asst. secty., govt. of India, defence co-ordn. dept., Sept., 1939; asst. secty., home dept., govt. of India, 1st to 23rd May, 1940; offr. on spec. duty, office of dir.-genl. of supply, Aug., 1940.

DUBOIS, BASIL ALLEN, late Indian Police Bombay (b. 5th March 1887).—Retd., Nov., 1940.†

DUCKFIELD, WILLIAM EDMUND, late Indian Police (b. 28th June 1895).—Retd., Aug., 1940.†

DUGGAN, SIR JAMSHEOJI NASARVANJI, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., L.M. & S., D.O. (Oxon.), F.C.P.S., late Bombay Medical Dept. (b. 8th Apl., 1884).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

DU HEAUME, FRANCIS HERBERT, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 27th May, 1897).—Joined the service, 27th Jan., 1920; asst. dist. officer, frontier constabulary, Apl., 1922; asst. supt., Dec., 1925; officer in charge, intell. branch, N.W.F.P., May, 1926; supt., Feb., 1927; dist. officer, frontier constabulary, May, 1929; O.B.E., Jan., 1932; princpl. trng. sch., Phillaur, Apl., 1934; dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1942; dep. dir., civil def., Oct., 1942, to Jan., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1943; dep. inspr.-genl. again, June, 1945.

DUKE, CHARLES BERESFORD, O.B.E., Indian C.S., Indian Political Serv., (b. 19th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Feb., 1934; asst. pte. secty. to Viceroj., Nov., 1934, to Jan., 1938; confd. in the Indian Pol. Service, Nov., 1937; asst. commr., Bannu, Oct., 1938; asst. commr., Charsadda, Apl., 1939; secty. to govr., N.W.F.P., March, 1940; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Nov., 1941; dep. secty., ext. affairs dept., Oct., 1943; O.B.E., Jan., 1946; dep. sec., ext. aff. dept., Jan., 1946.

DUKE, JAMES ALEXANDER, late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 26th Aug., 1887).—Retd., Aug., 1940.

DULAT, SHAMSHER SINGH, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 3rd Apl., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1934; addl. ditto, July, 1934; dist. and sess. judge, confd., Sept., 1940.

DUMBRECK, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 28th Nov., 1917).—Educ. at Linlithgow Academy, Edinburgh Univ., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 29th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

DUN, U. BA, Barr., Burma Judl. Dept. (b. 21st June, 1884).—Apptd. dep. govt. advocate and secty. to Burma legisl. council, 28th Jan., 1926.

DUNCAN, ARCHIBALD WALLACE, Lieut.-Col., (rettd.), late I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar (b. 11th July, 1884).—Retd., July, 1939.*

DUNCAN, JOHN ELDER, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 30th Jan., 1898).—Retd., Aug., 1940.

DUNCAN, PATRICK THOMAS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 16th March, 1916).—Educ. at Bromsgrove and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 27th Aug.,

1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in N.W.F.P. as asst. commr.

DUNCAN, PERCY RICHARD, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 15th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as forest engr., United Provs., on 1st Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Aug., 1930; retd., 15th Oct., 1944.

DUNCAN, ROBERT SYMON, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 27th Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, on 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1934; on mil. serv. from Sept., 1939, to Sept., 1944; on depn., Bahawalpur State, Sept., 1944.

DUNDAS, AMBROSE DUNDAS FLUX, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S., Indian Pol. Serv. (b. 14th Apl., 1899).—Educ. at Harrow and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 14th Dec., 1922, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., March, 1924; jt. mag., Oct., 1924; off. mag. and collr., May, 1925; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Sept., 1925; pers. asst. to C.C. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1925; asst. commr., Charsadda, Nov., 1927; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1928; ditto, Dir Swat and Chitral, Apl., 1932; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P.
