(transfd. depts.), Nov., 1932; ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1933; dep. commr., Peshawar, Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1936; employed on spl. duty under for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1936; secty. to resdt. for Punjab states, March, 1937; chief secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1937; resdt. in Waziristan, Aug., 1940; ch. secty., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1940; resdt. in Waziristan, Nov., 1941, to Oct., 1943; resdt. and divl. commr., Oct., 1943; C.S.I., Jan., 1946; on depn. as sec. to govt. of India, war dept., Jan., 1946; resdt., cl. II. (confd.) May, 1946.

DUNDON, GEORGE HEWITT, M.C., late Ind. Service of Engineers (Punjab) (b. 11th May 1893).—Joined the service 1st October, 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under-secty., public wks. dept. (irrign. branch), March, 1929; under-secty., Western admin., irrign. branch., June, 1937, to Oct., 1937; suptg. engr. (permt.), Feb., 1941. on l.p.r., April, 1946.

DUNI CHAND, (Survey of India, Supt.) (b. 6th Nov., 1890).—Proby. extra asst. supt., Nov. 1911; confd., Nov., 1914; supt., March, 1944.

DUNKLEY, SIR HERBERT FRANCIS, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Rangoon) (b. 2nd July, 1886).—Educ. at Wellingtonborough Gr. Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; regr., chief court, Lower Burma, Jan., 1919, to Nov., 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; offg. regr., high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1926; acting judge, high ct., Rangoon, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1933; actg. ch. justice, high ct., Rangoon, Oct., 1945; Kt., Jan., 1943.

DUNLOP, SIR R. W. L., Kt., C.I.E., D.S.O., late Indian Legis. Dept.—Retd., July, 1927.*

DUNLOP, SIDNEY WILLIAM CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub collr., Madras) (b. 9th May, 1914).—Educ. at Epsom Coll., and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India Oct., 1937; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1940; under sec., rev. dept. Dec., 1942; dep. sec. P.W.D., May, 1946.

DUNN, ARTHUR GEORGE, Indian State Ryls. (dist. loco. supt., Burma rlys.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1922, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. ditto, Burma rlys., May, 1929; offg. transptn. officer (power)

Jan., 1931; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Burma rlys., March, 1934.

DUNN, JOHN ALEXANDER, D.Sc., D.I.C., F.G.S., Geological Survey Dept. (suplg. geologist) (b. 27th June, 1899).—Apptd. asst. supt., 17th Dec., 1921; curator, geol. museum, Calcutta, May, 1929; offg. supt., Apl., 1931; confd., July, 1939.

DUNN, SYDNEY ERNEST, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 26th Nov., 1899).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 22nd Oct., 1919, after previous service from 25th Apl., 1917; second class clerk, 29th Apl., 1920; transfd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; actg. higher exec. offr., July, 1941.

DUNNETT, SIR JAMES MACDONALD, K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 11th Apl., 1877).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

DUNNICLIFF, HORACE BARRATT, C.I.E., M.A., D.Sc., F.I.C., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 23rd Sept., 1885).—Joined the service as profr., Khalsa Coll., Amritsar, on 7th March, 1914; profr., govt. coll., Lahore, Aug., 1917; chemist, cordite factory, Aruvankadu, Nov., 1918; profr., govt. coll., Lahore, Apl., 1921; spl. chemical adviser for customs, Calcutta, Oct., 1928; princpl., govt. coll., Lahore, May, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; ch. chemist, cent. revs., Nov., 1939; retd., March, 1943; reempld. war transport dept., Nov., 1943 to June 1946.

DUNSDON, ARTHUR CULHAM, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 28th June, 1887).—Joined the service 9th Oct., 1913; resdt. engr. E.I. Rly., Oct., 1921; exec., engr., ditto, August, 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1934; dy. agent (styled dy. gl. mngr.), E.I. Rly., March, 1938; provl. perm. dep. ch. engr., May, 1939; retd., 1945.

DURAI, KANDAPPA, B.Sc., Indian State Ryls. (divl. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 12th May, 1894).—Joined the service, 26th March, 1920; asst. engr., Jan., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1928; exec. engr., Dec., 1937; divl. supt., Aug., 1942; dir., rly. bd., 1946.

DURGA PRASAD ROHATGI, Rai Bahadur, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Feb., 1899).—Joined the service 12th Oct., 1922; apprentice engr., June, 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1932; Rai Bahadur, June, 1939.

DURNO, LESLIE GEORGE, late Indian C.S. (b. 27th July 1892).—Retd., Dec., 1940.†

DURRANT, GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 9th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1914; in mil. employ from 12th Oct., 1914, to 5th Oct., 1922; asst. supt., Oct., 1922; offg. supt., Nov., 1925; supt. (provl. subs.), Jan., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936; awarded King's police medal, Feb., 1937.

DURRELL, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian State Ryls. (elec. engr., Burma rlys.) (b. 6th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1914; offg. asst. elec. engr., Sept., 1919; confd., Jan., 1920; dist. elec. engr., Dec., 1927; offg. dep. loco. supt. (elec.), Oct., 1930.

DURST, THOMAS ORAM, India Office (higher exec. officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 25th Nov., 1889).—Educ. at Emanuel Sch., Wandsworth Comm.; second div. clerk, bd. of edn., Apl., 1909, to Nov., 1911; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 27th Nov., 1911, and attd. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Jan., 1919; higher clerical officer,

Api., 1922; higher exec. officer, Sept., 1926; assessor of inc. tax, Jan., 1928; actg. suptg. exec. offcr., July, 1940.

DUTCH, ROBERT AUSTEN, O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge Bengal) (b. 22nd Oct., 1905).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. coll. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1936; addtl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1936, to Nov., 1937; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1937, to Oct., 1938; census supt., Bengal, Dec., 1939, to 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; addl. sec., home dept., Mar., 1945; offg. sec., health and L.S.G., Mar., 1946.

DUTT (see also Datta).

DUTT, BIRENDRA CHANDRA, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 21st Nov., 1895).—Joined the service as probnr., 28th Feb., 1920; asst. acct. genl., Bengal, Nov., 1922; ditto, Madras, Feb., 1924; ditto, central revs., March, 1926; currency officer, Lahore, April, 1927; ditto, Calcutta, Jan., 1931; exr., local fund acct., Bengal, Oct., 1933; exr., outside audit, Bengal, Apr., 1935; ch. audtr., E.B. rly., and govt. exr. accts., A.B. rly., Oct., 1938; comptr., Orissa, Sept., 1940; acct.-genl., Bihar, Nov., 1941; acct., genl., Cl II. (prov.), Sept., 1944; acct.-genl., Bengal, April, 1946.

DUTT, DEBPATI, Legis. Assembly Dept. (b. 1st Apr., 1884).—Retd., Apr., 1939.*

DUTT, DIVAKAR, B.A., R.B., late Indian State Ry., (b. 3rd Dec., 1886).—Retd., Dec., 1941.

DUTT, NARESH CHANDRA, B.A., late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 7th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

DUTT, NAND LAL, M.Sc., (sugar cane expert) (b. 20th Apr., 1900).—Joined the service as second cane breeding officer, Sugar cane Substation, I.A.R., 3rd Apr., 1926.

DUTT, PAVITRA KUMAR, Office of High Commr. for India (educ. officer, suptg. exec. grade, educn. dept.) (b. 25th March, 1888).—Apptd. educ. officer, 8th Feb., 1929; jt. social secty. to Indian delegation to round-table conf., London, Oct., 1930; and again, Sept., 1931.

DUTT, RABINDRA CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 5th Nov., 1912).—Educ. at Hare Sch., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dy. collr., Dec., 1939; addl. dist. mag., Apr., 1942; mag. and collr., Apr., 1943.

DUTT, SUBIMAL, O.B.E., B.Sc. (Cal.), Indian C.S. (addl. sec. agric. dept.) (b. 13th Mar., 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Calcutta, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1929; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1935; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1936; offg. dep. commr., Apr., 1936; addtl. under-secty., e.h. and l. dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1938; under-secty., ditto, Feb., 1939; addtl. dep. secty., ditto, Apr., 1940; agent of govt. of India in Malaya, Apr., 1941; addl. secty., agric. dept., Jan., 1945; O.B.E., June, 1946.

DUTTA, J. N., B.Sc., (b. 16th Aug., 1906).—Joined the service, 15th July, 1929; held non-gaz. posts in commerce dept. to 1943; held minor gaz. posts to Jul., 1945; ch. supt., comm.

dept., July, 1945; O.S.D. and ex-officio asst. sec., commerce dept., Nov., 1945.

DUTTON, HUGH REGINALD, C.I.E., F.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Medical Dept., Bihar-Orissa (b. 21st Nov., 1875).—Retd., Mar., 1940.†

DYSON, WILLIAM GEOFFREY, M.C., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (chief consvr., Madras) (b. 7th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1915, as asst. consvr.; on mil. duty from 6th Dec., 1915, to 3rd Feb., 1919; dep. consvr., Dec., 1920; instr., Madras forest coll., May, 1923; princpl., ditto, Aug., 1923; dist. forest officer, July, 1924; actg. consvr., variously since Sept., 1929; provl. sylviculturist, July, 1931; consvr., Oct., 1938; chief ditto, Apr., 1940.

EADES, GERALD JOHN, late Indian State Ry., (b. 20th July, 1887).—Retd., Mar., 1941.†

EADES, JOHN HENRY, P.W. Dept., Burma (elec. inspr.) (b. 21st Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as elec. engr., Nov., 1926; elec. inspr., Dec., 1930; on mil. duty, April, 1944 to Mar., 1946.

EADON, ALAN T. (Dir., Civil Aviation, Burma).—Apptd., Jan., 1939.

EAGEN, THOMAS GEORGE ROYAL, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Ry., (dep. ch. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th June, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1926; senr. sc., June, 1935; dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1934.

EARLE, ROBERT JOHN, Indian State Ry., (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 13th Feb., 1898).—Joined service, Oct., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; provl. perm. exec. engr., E.I. Rly., Feb., 1939.

EARLEY, WILFRED PATRICK, A.C.A., (b. 10th Oct., 1901).—Apptd. auditor genl. of Burma, 19th Oct., 1943.

EASTMAN NAGLE, JAMES NICHOLAS, O.B.E., Indian State Ry., (ch. trpt. mgr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 7th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 25th March, 1913; dist. traffic supt., July, 1926; publicity officer, Nov., 1928; dep. ch. trpt. mgr., Feb., 1942; ch. trpt. mgr., (confid.), O.B.E., June, 1946.

EASTMOND, ARCHIBALD, M.C., M.Inst.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 11th Oct., 1889).—Retd., Apr., 1939.*

EASTON, THOMAS WILLIAM ROBERTSON, Indian Service of Engineers (Punjab) (b. 29th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 20th Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1926; confid., Oct., 1929; under-secty. to govt. (constrn.), Burma, Feb., 1936; suptg. engr. (perm.), March, 1944; on mil. serv., Apr., 1942, to June, 1944; on l.p.r., Apr., 1946.

EASTWELL, WILFRED HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer) (b. 1st March, 1898).—Apptd. to India Office, store dept., as clerical officer, 1st Dec., 1919, after previous service in General Post Office from 22nd Oct., 1915; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Oct., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, June, 1922; actg. hr. exec. offcr., Sept.-Nov., 1936; temp. ditto, Dec., 1940.

EATES, KENNETH RAYE, M.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 20th Jul., 1894).—Joined the serv., 10th Feb., 1920, with prev. mil. serv.; Indian police medal, Jan., 1934; King's ditto, Jan., 1942; prom. to I.P. as supt., Apr., 1937; M.B.E., June, 1946.

EAYRES, VERNON JOHN GEORGE, Office of

High Commr. for India (higher exec. offer., store dept.) (b. 17th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Strand Sch., King's Coll.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 4th Sept., 1908; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to Apl., 1917; transf. to office of high commn. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; personal asst. to high commr., Oct., 1928, to Nov., 1936; hr. exec. officer, Nov., 1936; actg. princpl. ditto, May, 1940.

EBBS, JOHN HENRY, India Office (actg. hr. exec. offr., acct-genl.'s dept.) (b. 11th May, 1897).—Apptd. cler. offr., 23rd Jan., 1922; actg. exec. offr., Apl., 1940; actg. hr. cler. offr. Apl., 1940; actg. hr. exec. offr., Apl., 1946.

ECCLESTON, REV. FREDERICK ERNEST, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 3rd March, 1905).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 11th May, 1944.

EDGAR, SAMUEL GARTHWAITE, C.B.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 8th July, 1887).—Retd., Jan., 1942.†

EDGUMBE, GEORGE AUBREY VALLETORT, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 9th Feb., 1931; mil. duty with B.N.V.R.F., Oct., 1940; at British Admiralty, March, 1943; with M.E.S., Aug., 1945; exec., engr. Feb., 1946.

EDGECOMBE, ARTHUR RALPH BROOKS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 14th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 1st Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1930; confd., Oct., 1933.

EDGLEY, NORMAN GEORGE ARMSTRONG, M.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 19th June, 1888).—Educ. at schools in Switzerland, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bengal, Apl., 1912; under-sec., judl. pol. and appt. depts., Bengal, June, 1916; regr., high ct., Calcutta, appellate side, Apl., 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1924; services placed at disposal of govt. of Assam, Oct., 1927; Kaiser-i-Hind medal, 1st class, Jan., 1930; offg. secty. to govt., judl. dept., Bengal, and supt. and remembrancer, legal affairs, March, 1933; confd., July, 1933; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, variously from June, 1934; confd., May, 1939.

EDMONDS, ALBERT CHARLES, B.A., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 2nd Nov., 1895).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, 5th Dec., 1920; retd., Apl., 1939.

EDMONDS, VEN. CANON HERBERT JAMES, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 20th March, 1886).—Retd., July, 1940.†

EDMONDS, FRANCIS FREDERIC COLTON, M.A., B.Sc. (b. 16th Oct., 1901).—Apptd. inspr. of schools, Coorg and Bangalore, Feb., 1930; retd., Nov., 1936; sec., fed. public serv. comm., June, 1940.

EDMUNDS, SIR PERCY JAMES, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., India Posts and Tels. (ch. engr.) (b. 1st Feb., 1890).—Joined the service (wireless branch), 5th Dec., 1919; divl. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. dir. of wireless, Apl., 1916; confd., Apl., 1927; contr. of broadcasting in addn., March, 1935; dep. dir.-genl., wireless, Apl., 1936; del. to internl. conf., Cairo, 1938; dep. chief engr., June, 1938; dir. of tels., Sept., 1939; P.M.G., Sept., 1940; dy. dir.-genl. tels., Jan., 1941; ch. engr., June, 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; kt., Jan., 1946.

EDWARDS, ARTHUR JOHN CHARLES, C.I.E., F.I.A., Office of High Commr. for India (chief accounting officer) (b. 24th July, 1883).—Educ. at Aske's Hatcham Sch.; entered Natl. Debt.

Office, May, 1903; pte. sec. to contr., Oct., 1911, to July, 1913; apptd. junior clerk, India Office, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., 5th July, 1913; senr. clerk, Feb., 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from Apl., 1921; clerk to commrs. of income tax, Apl., 1921, to Apl., 1922, and from Oct., 1922; asst. chief accounting officer, 1st Apl., 1922; deputy ditto, 21st Apl., 1922; apptd. Indian income tax officer, June, 1924 chief accountg. officer, and commnr. of inc. tax, Nov., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1943.

EDWARDS, CHARLES CYRIL ROGERS, D.S.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 15th July, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th Jan., 1921; exec. engr., Burma rlys., March, 1934.

EDWARDS, COSMO GRANT NIVEN, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 13th Aug., 1896).—First commn. dated 19th June, 1915; joined the pol. dept., 8th March, 1921; asst. commt., Kohat, Apl., 1922; ditto, Mardan, Apl., 1926; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Nov., 1926; dist. judge, Peshawar, Dec., 1928; dep. commr., D.I. Khan, Apl., 1929; offg. under-secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Apl., 1930; and again, March, 1931; offg. dep. sec. for. and pol. dept., June, 1930; and again, Sept., 1932; secty. to resdt. in Mysore, Feb., 1934; pol. agent, Kalat, Apl., 1938; ditto, in Bhopal, Apl., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1944; resdt., Kolhapur, Oct., 1942; resdt. for Madras states, May, 1946; resdt., Cl. II (confd.) May, 1946.

EDWARDS, EDWIN NORMAN, Indian Police (b. 1st Oct., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 10th Nov., 1935; confd., Mar., 1938.

EDWARDS, JOHN ASHWORTH, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 17th Feb., 1932).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, 23rd Dec., 1931; confd., Jan., 1934; civ. aff. serv. to Dec., 1945; dist., supt. Jan., 1946.

EDWARDS, OSBORNE GEORGE, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th June, 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1941.†

EDWARDS, WILLIAM CECIL, late Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 19th Sept., 1888).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

EGAN, ERIC MARTIN, Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. offr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th Oct., 1894).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1919; divl. transpn. offr., May, 1935.

EGAN, THOMAS JARLETTE, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (dir. of vety. services, United Provs.) (b. 5th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., March, 1922; supt., Apl., 1925; profr., Punjab vety. coll., Nov., 1933; dir. of vety. services, United Provs., July, 1934.

EGERTON, WILLIAM LE BELWARD, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 1st Dec., 1901).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, March, 1927; asst. pol. agent, Kalat, Dec., 1929; under-secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in C.I., Nov., 1930; secty., ditto, Feb., 1931; offg. pol. agent, Bhopal, in addn., Aug., 1935; offcr. on spec. duty, pol. dept., Apl., 1937; dep. secty., pol. dept., May, 1937; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar Agency, Oct., 1937; sec. to resdt., W.I. States, March, 1938; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar agency, May, 1938; on spec. duty in pol. dept., Nov., 1938; and Jan. to Apl., 1939; officer on special duty under orders of resdt. for States of W. India, Jan., 1939; resdt. in Mewar and pol. agent, S. Rajputana States, Apl., 1939;

addtl. dep. secty., pol. dept., Sept., 1940; secty. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Dec., 1940; pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Nov., 1941; sec. resdt., Mysore, Sept., 1942; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Sept., 1944.

EGGAR, SIR ARTHUR, M.A., Barr., Burma Judl. Dept. (advocate-genl., Burma) (b. 22nd May, 1877).—Apptd. acting asst. govt. advocate, 7th May, 1911; law officer to R.A.F., Middle-East, 1918-19; apptd. dep. govt. advocate and sec. to Burma legisl. council, Dec., 1922; offg. govt. advocate, March, 1924; confd., Dec., 1925; adv.-genl., Apl., 1937; Kt., Jan., 1938.

EGGLESFIELD, LAURENCE ALFRED, Wing Cmr. (Civil Aviation Directorate) (b. 28th Mar., 1900).—Apptd. dep. dir. civil aviation in India, Feb., 1939; on depn. to R.A.F., 3rd Feb., 1942; resumed duties as dep. dir. of civil aviation, May, 1945.

EKAMBARAM, KOLIPAKKAM VENKATISA, Indian Service of Engrs. (supgt. engr., Madras) (b. 2nd June, 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 13th Aug., 1932; exec. engr., Aug., 1941; supgt. engr., April, 1946.

EKANATHA RAJARAM, RAO, B.A. (Hons.), Imperial Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 2nd March, 1901).—Joined the service, March, 1926; asst. collr. of customs, July, 1927.

ELKINGTON, J. R. S. W., Capt., R.E., (b. 14th June, 1904).—In milty. employ to May, 1935; exec. engr., central p.w.d. (temp. aviation circle), to Oct., 1938; apptd. exec. engr., Nov., 1938; reverted to milty. duty, Dec., 1939.

ELLIOT, DAVID CLEPHAN, M.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 17th Sept., 1917).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh, St. Andrew's Univ., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., after seln., in May, 1940, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; confd., Nov., 1941; A.R.P. offr., Aug., 1942; p.a. to dep. commr., Lahore, April, 1943; D.S.O., Aug., 1944; under sec. to govt., pol. dept., July, 1945; on l.p.r., Jan., 1947.

ELLIOTT, REV. FRANK EDMUND, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior Chaplain) (b. 17th March, 1904).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Nagpur Diocese, 12th Aug., 1942.

ELLIOTT, SIR IVO D'OYLY, Bart., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 7th March, 1882).—Retd., March, 1932.*

ELLIOTT, JACK, late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 5th Jan., 1891).—Retd., Jan., 1939.†

ELLIOTT, JAMES STURGESS, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 2nd March, 1914).—Educ. at Alderman Newton's Sch., Leicester, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; cane inspr., Dec., 1940; sub. divl. offr., June, 1941; jt. mag. and dep. collr., regional grain supply offr., Oct., 1943; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

ELLIOTT, REV. THOMAS REGINALD HATCHARD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 5th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bombay, 27th Oct., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; senr. ditto, Oct., 1937; snr. presidency chaplain, Oct., 1938; offg. archdeacon of Bombay, Feb., 1946.

ELLIS, DICK, Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 28th March, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

ELLIS, THOMAS HOBART, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 11th Oct., 1894).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined

the service, 31st Jan., 1919; arrived, 31st March, 1919, and posted to Bengal; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1924; offg. ditto, Feb., 1930; confd., July, 1930; chairman, conciliation board, Aug., 1938; regr., high ct., appl. side, Nov., 1938; spec. offr., A.R.P., Feb., 1942.

ELMES, ARCHIBALD JAMES, India Audit Office (higher exec. officer) (b. 8th Jan., 1893).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 6th Aug., 1914; on milty. service, Jan., 1917, to March, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; transf. to India Audit Office, Feb., 1922; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1933; on loan to min. of war transport, Sept., 1941.

ELMES, JULIUS PAUL JONATHAN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 1st Nov., 1887).—Retd., March, 1939.*

ELMORE, ALBERT EDWARD, late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 24th Jan., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

ELMSLIE, CYRIL STEWART, late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 10th Apl., 1893).—Retd., June, 1940.

ELWIN, DONALD HAMILTON, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (b. 15th April, 1904).—Educ. at Bradfield and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928; under sec., public dept., Madras, May, 1932; offg. under-sec., to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1934; offg. dep. sec. ditto, Sept., 1943; pte. sec. to the govr. of Madras, Sept., 1935; comp. to nephew of Maharaja of Mysore, June, 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1939; supt., census, Madras, Dec., 1939; dep. sec. war transpt. dept., Sept., 1941; jt. ch. contr. imports, Delhi, May, 1945; on l.p.r.

ELWIN, REGINALD BARNES, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th June, 1908).—Educ. at Bradfield and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-secy. to govt., Punjab, pol. and home dept., May, 1933; offg. judge, small cause ct., Simla, March, 1934; attd. home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1937; under-secy., def. co-ord. dept., etc., Sept., 1940; secy. to chairman, E.G. supply cl., up to Oct., 1942; pte. secy. to Indian member, Oct., 1943, to Apl., 1944; dep. secy., govt. of India (indus. and civ. supplies), Apl., 1943; dep. secy. ditto (war transport), June, 1944; offg. jt. sec., govt. of India, dept. war transpt., March to Oct., 1945; dep. sec. ditto, Oct., 1945.

EMERSON, GERALD HERBERT, Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1935; offg. asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1935; secy. to A.G.G., Eastern states, March, 1936; asst. commr., Nowshera sub-div., Nov., 1937; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1939; asst. commr., Mansebra, Apl., 1941; sec. to govr., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1941; pol. agt., Chagai, Sept., 1944; pol. agt., Zohb, Feb., 1945; I.P.S. (confd.), Apl., 1945.

EMERSON, SIR HERBERT WILLIAM, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st June, 1881).—Retd., Aug., 1940.†

EMERSON, RALF BILLING, COL., C.I.E., O.B.E., R.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. commr., rlys.) (b. 3rd July, 1897).—Joined the service, 28th June, 1920; asst. exec. engr., G.I.P. Rly., Feb., 1927; provl. perm. exec. engr., Dec., 1938;

dep. genl. manager, June, 1939; genl. manager, Dec., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1946; ch. commr., rlys., May, 1946.

EMINSON, BASIL FRANKLIN, M.B., B.S. (Lon.), Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (b. 24th Sept., 1887).—First commn dated 27th July, 1912; in milty. employ prior to 27th Nov., 1923; offg. civil surg., May, 1925; civil surg., Hyderabad, May, 1933; K.-in-H. Gold Medal, Jan., 1944; retd., Sept., 1942; re-employed as civil surg., Karachi, Sept., 1942 to March, 1946.

ENGINEER, SIR NOSHIRWAN PHIROZSHAH.—Apptd. advocate-genl. of India, Apl., 1945; Kt., June, 1945.

ENGLAND, FREDERICK SAMUEL EDWARD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 2nd July, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 24th Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935; on mil. serv., Oct., 1944 to Feb., 1946.

ENGLAND, WALTER BRASSINGTON, M.B.E., M.A., Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, public dept.) (b. 4th Apl., 1893).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to India Office, 4th Apl., 1921; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, Apl., 1922; exec. officer, Jan., 1924; higher clerical officer, June, 1927; actg. higher exec. officer, Sept., 1929, to Aug., 1930; hr. exec. officer, (temp.), Apl., 1938; M.B.E., Jan., 1941.

ENGLEFIELD, ARTHUR GUY, Indian State Rlys (dep. ch. mech. engr. N.W. Rly.) (b. 13th Jul., 1893).—Joined the service, asst. loco. supt., Dec., 1919; asst. rolling stock officer, July, 1926; asst. works manager, N.W. rly., Dec., 1932; divl. mech. engr., N.W. rly. (prov.), Aug. 1934; confd., Sept., 1936; dep. ch. mech. engr., Feb., 1945.

ENSOR, ERIC LOVELL, Indian State Rlys. (dep. trans. supt. tels.) (b. 25th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. tel. supt., 4th Oct., 1921; asst. transpn. supt. (tels.), 1st Nov., 1922; offg. dep. ditto, Apl., 1932, and again, Sept., 1935; dep. trans. supt., tels., Jan., 1939.

ENSOR, PETER TEBBETT, B.Sc., Econ., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 7th June, 1915).—Educ. at Colfe's Sch., Lewisham, London Sch. of Economics, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 13th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; a.r.p. offr., Apl., 1942; sub. divl. offr., Apl., 1942; under-sec., govt. of Punjab, July, 1944.

ERACH MANEKSHAH NANAVATI (sz. Nanavati, E. M.).

ERSKINE, CLAUDE ERNEST TORIN, C.I.E., D.S.O., M.C., Col., I.A., retd. (b. 1st May, 1887).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; in mil. employ to Apl., 1938; pers. asst. to secty., mil. dept., India Office, Sept., 1938, to Feb., 1945.

ERSKINE, LORD, JOHN FRANCIS ASHLEY, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E. (b. 26th Apl., 1895).—Educ. at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; asst. pte. secty. to First Lord of Admir., 1920-1921; parly. pte. secty. to P.M.G., 1923; princpl. pte. secty. to Home Secretary, 1924; M.P. for Weston-super-Mare Divn. of Somerset, 1922-1923 and again 1924; asst. govt. whip in Natl. Govt., 1932; G.C.I.E., Aug., 1934; Govr. of Madras, Nov., 1934, to Mar., 1940; G.C.S.I., May, 1940.

EUSTACE, EDWARD ARTHUR RAWLINS, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (commr. Punjab) (b. 18th Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and R.M. Coll., Sandhurst; joined the service,

4th Sept., 1925; arrived, 14th Nov., 1923, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., March, 1925; addl. under-sec. to govt. of India, dept. of educ., etc., Dec., 1927; offg. regr., high ct., Lahore, Jan., 1936; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1931; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Nov., 1931; confd., July, 1935; home sec., govt. of Punjab, March, 1938; sec., (medl. etc. dept.), Oct., 1938, and again, Apl., 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; commr., Dec., 1941; sec. to govt. (medl. etc.), dept. April, 1942.

EUSTACE, JOHN CURTIS WEINHER, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 22nd Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Wellington; Brentwood Coll.; Victoria, British Columbia, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1933; dep. regr., co-op. societies, Punjab, Apl., 1935; offg. regr., ditto, March, 1936, and again Oct., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1945.

EVANS, AIDAN OSWALD, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 26th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 30th Oct., 1909; offg. dist. ditto, Feb., 1915; offg. dep. traffic manager, Aug., 1921; with Indian Rly. conference asscn., Apl., 1922; supt., July, 1925; ditto, operating dept., Oct., 1926; offg. secty. to agent, E.I. Rly., March, 1928; offg. divl. supt., March, 1929; offg. dep. agent, E.I. Rly., Dec., 1929; offg. divl. supt., Oct., 1930; and again, Nov., 1932; confd., Jan., 1936.

EVANS, ARTHUR GARBETT, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 6th May, 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 1st Nov., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933; inspr.-genl., Rampur State, Oct., 1938; June, 1945 (confd.).

EVANS, ELWYN RICHARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 5th Nov., 1914).—Educ. at Brynmawr County Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; served in Sind, Nov., 1943; dep. sec., govt. of Sind, rev. dept. and ex-officio asst. commr., Feb., 1944; regional grain purchasing officer, March, 1946.

EVANS, SIR GEOFFREY, Kt., C.I.E., M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Agricl. Service.—Retd., Feb., 1925.*

EVANS, GEORGE EMLYN THOMAS HULSE, O.B.E., M.C., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 18th June, 1899).—Joined the serv., as asst. supt., Bengal, on 15th Mar., 1921; offg. supt., Mar., 1925; confd., Jan., 1932; dep. commr. police, Calcutta, Mar., 1934, to Feb., 1936; asst. inspr.-genl., March, 1940; King's police medal, 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

EVANS, HUBERT JOHN, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr. United Provs.) (b. 21st Nov., 1904).—Educ. at City of London Sch., Jesus Coll., Cambridge, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived, 27th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1932; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1936; dep. commr., Delhi, March, 1938; asst. settl. offr., sec., 1938; rev. sec., fin. commrs., Feb., 1939; dep. commr., subst. provl., May, 1940; dir. war, infm. and dep. sec., govt. Punjab (home), March, 1942; mag. and collr. (confd.) Dec., 1944.

EVANS, LEONARD GLYDE LAVINGTON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S., and Indian Political Service (b. 9th Nov., 1888).—Retd., March, 1938.*

EVANS, SIR T. J. CAREY (see Carey-Evans, Sir T. J.).

EVANS, VICTOR DONALD CHAPPELL, Indian Police (b. 8th Jan., 1915).—Apptd., asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 11th Nov., 1935; confd., July, 1937; served in Sind from Oct., 1939; a.r.p. contr., May, 1942; enq. and enforcement offr. and dep. sec., food dept., Nov., 1943.

EVANS, WILLIAM HUGH, B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 24th June, 1892).—Educ. at County Sch., Pwlheli, and Univ. Coll., Bangor; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1921; arrived, 30th Nov., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; confd. Sept., 1927; ret'd., 31st Oct., 1943.

EVANS-GORDON, KENMURE ALICK GARTH, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 20th Aug., 1885).—Ret'd., Aug., 1940.†

EVENNETTE, GEORGE BERTRAM CHARLES, M.B.E., (dep. regr. high ct., Lahore) (b. 27th July, 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1915; asst. dep. regr., June, 1933; confd., Aug., 1933; dep. regr. April, 1941; confd., Aug., 1941.

EVERETT, CECIL HOWARD, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 9th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 6th Jan., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1930; supt. (subs. provl.), March, 1936; confd., Oct., 1936; central intell. offr., Lahore, July, 1944.

EVERITT, WILLIAM MICHAEL, Burma Police (Cl. I.) (b. 5th Nov., 1924).—Apptd. on prob. after seln., Aug., 1946.

EVERS, REV. MERVYN SAXELBYE, M.A., M.C., late Indian Eccles. Estab. (b. 8th Sept., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 18th May, 1924; junr. chaplain, May, 1926; senr. ditto, May, 1932; ret'd.

FAIRHALL, CHRISTOPHER HAMLYN, Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 1st Oct., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

FAIRLAMB, RONALD WATER, Burma Fr. S. (b. 18th Feb., 1918).—Apptd. on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

FAIRLEY, FRANCIS NOEL, late Office of High commr. for India (b. 11th Dec., 1883).—Ret'd., May, 1944.†

FAIRWEATHER, SIR CHARLES EDWARD STUART, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 26th Nov., 1909; supt., Apl., 1923; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, June, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1932; confd., March, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offr., Order of St. John, June, 1941; confd. as commr., police, Calcutta, July, 1941; Kt., 1944; ret'd., 16th Jan., 1944.

FAIZMUHAMMAD KHAN, KHAN, late Punjab C.S. (b. 16th Dec., 1899).—Extra asst. commr., Punjab, Jan., 1914; ret'd., Dec., 1944.

FAKHAR-UD-DIN, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad F.).

FALCONER, GEORGE ARTHUR, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 3rd June, 1894).—First commn., dated 28th Aug., 1917; joined pol. dept., 18th June, 1923; under-sec., to pol. resident in Persian Gulf, May, 1924; asst. resident, Aden, Aug., 1927; 2nd asst. to resdt., Kashmir, March, 1929; secty. to A.G.G., Deccan States, Apl., 1933; ditto, Gujerat States, Oct., 1933; offg. A.G.G., ditto, Apl., 1935; consul for Kerman, Nov., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1942; pol. agt., Bhopal, Nov., 1942; H.M. minister, Nepal, Apl., 1944.

FALSHAW, DONALD, Indian C.S. (dist. and

sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 22nd Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Lancaster Royal Gr. Sch., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1932; offg. ditto, Nov., 1933; sub. perm., June, 1937; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1943.

FANN, JOHN MONTAGU GROSSE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 31st Dec., 1904).—Educ. at Latymer Upper Sch., Hammersmith, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. warden, oilfields, Apl., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1932; confd., Dec., 1937; sec., evacuee and welfare dept., 1944; on spl. duty, repr.'s office, govt. of Burma, Calcutta, May, 1942; spl. offr. (Burma), Madras, Aug., 1942 to Mar., 1944; sec., welfare and reconstr. dept., Aug., 1944; civ. aff. serv., Nov., 1944, dep. commr., April, 1946.

FARBROTHER, ERNEST SAMUEL, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (dir., vety. services, Bombay) (b. 16th July, 1890).—Joined the service, Oct., 1913; on milty. duty from July, 1915, to Apl., 1919; offg. supt., Sind, July, 1919; ditto, Bombay Presyd., Feb., 1922; confd., Nov., 1923; dir., vety. services, June, 1932.

FARGUE, DUDLEY GORDON HERIOT DE LA, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Sept., 1897).—First commn. dated 21st Sept., 1916; joined pol. dept., 11th Apl., 1923; under-sec. to agent to G.G. in C.I., Apr., 1925; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1926; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Jan., 1928; offg. under-sec. for. and pol. dept., July, 1930; on foreign service in Gwalior State, March, 1931; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1932; secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Jan., 1933; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1934; pol. agent, Zhob, Nov., 1934; home secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., July, 1938; dep. commr., Bannu, Oct., 1938; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar agency, Aug., 1940; pol. agent, Loralai, Sept., 1941; pol. agt., Kurram, May to June, 1944; rev. and divl. commr., Aug. to Oct., 1944; ch. sec. to govt., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1944.

FARMBROUGH, ALFRED, A.I.C., Office of High Commr. for India (chemist, store dept.) (b. 30th Sept., 1889).—Apptd. junior asst. chemist, India Store depot, 1909; asst. chemist, 1920; chemist, June, 1935.

FARMER, JOHN COTTON, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 18th May, 1886).—Ret'd., Aug. 1940.†

FARMER, PATRICK SCOTT LETHBRIDGE, Indian Police (b. 18th July, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, Nov., 1932; asst. commr., Port Blair, Feb., 1938.

FARNON, JOSEPH ASHLEY ST. JOHN, M.A., late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 23rd July, 1882).—Ret'd., Jan., 1938.*

FARQUHAR, ARTHUR GRAHAM FRANCIS, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 21st Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Glenalmond and Sch. of Economics, London; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1927; and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. under-secty. to govt., Oct., 1929; offg. dep. commr. variously from March, 1931; dep. commr. (provl.), Feb., 1939; confd., March, 1941; civil air recruiting offr., Feb., 1942; O.B.E., June, 1942; addl. sec. to govt., May,

1943; sec., local self govt. dept., Feb., 1944; addl. commr., April, 1946.

FARQUHARSON, FRANCIS ARCHIBALD, C.I.E., M.C., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 20th Jan., 1886).—Retd., 20th July, 1941.†

FARR, C.J., Burma Office.—Apptd. princpl. 12th Aug., 1946.

FARRAN, ARTHUR COURTNEY, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (princpl., Karnatak Coll., Dharwar) (b. 15th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1915; profr. of history and pol. economy, Elph. Coll., Bombay, Jan., 1917; profr. of history and economy, Deccan Coll., Poona, July, 1926; offg. princpl. and profr., Elph. Coll., Bombay, June, 1929, and again June, 1932; princpl., Karnatak Coll., Dharwar, Nov., 1930.

FARRANT, GEORGE YORKE SILVERTHORNE, M.C., Indian Police (supdt., Sind) (b. 23rd June, 1894).—Joined the service, 28th Dec., 1919, as asst. supdt., Bombay, offg. supdt., Feb., 1924; confd., Sept., 1927; King's police medal, Jan., 1942; confd. supdt. (sel. grade), Dec., 1943.

FARUQI, GHUFAN AHMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 1st Aug., 1909).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Sitapur, Allahabad Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Apl., 1939; officer on special duty, local self govt. dept., United Provs., June, 1939; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Aug., 1940.

FAULKNER, TERENCE BERNARD MICHAEL CUNLIFFE, Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 20th Dec., 1910).—Apptd. asst. supdt. on probn., Oct., 1939; confd., Dec., 1941; a.d.c. to govr., Sept., 1942, to Dec., 1943.

FAUQI, MUHAMMAD ZAHURUDDIN AHMAD (see Zahuruddin Ahmad, F. M.).

FAUQUI, NASIR AHMAD, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 15th Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under-secty., rev. dept., Bombay, Aug., 1935; confd., Apl., 1936; under-secty., home and pol. depts., and passport offcr., Nov., 1937; attd. home dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1937; O.B.E., June, 1944; collr. and dist. mag., Sind, May, 1946.

FAWCETT, SIR CHARLES GORDON HILL, Kt., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 28th June, 1869).—Retd., June, 1929.*

FAWCUS, GEORGE ERNEST, C.I.E., O.B.E., V.D., M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educl. Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 12th March, 1885).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

FAWCUS, LOUIS REGINALD, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (advr. to govt. of Bengal) (b. 7th Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Uppingham, Trinity Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlt. offr. transfed. to Bengal, Apl., 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1917; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to March, 1919; mag. and collr., Sept., 1925; offg. secty., bd. of rev., Bengal, Oct., 1927; offg. dir., land records, Bengal, March, 1929; offg. secy. to govt., Bengal agric. and ind. depts., Jan., 1931; confd., July, 1931; offg. secty., rev. dept., Bengal, June, 1935; offg. commr., Feb., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; member bd. of rev. (confd.) Feb., 1941; advr. to

govr. and ex-officio sec. land rev. and fin. depts., Aug., 1945; C.S.I., Jan., 1946.

FAY, ARCHIBALD THOMAS, late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 22nd Aug., 1883).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

FAZAAL-I ILLAHI, Khan Bahadur, B.A. (Allahabad) (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 29th June, 1894).—Joined the service, June, 1917; sub. divl. offr., Oct., 1927; dir. infmn. bur., Mar., 1932; on depn. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1932; under-sec. local self-govt., Dec., 1932; sub. divl. offr., May, 1938; on depn. to govt. of India as spl. press advr., Sept., 1939; dep. commr. (perm.) Dec., 1942.

FAZAL RASUL, Khalifa, Khan Sahib, B.A., Indian Posts and Teleg. (b. 1st July, 1896).—Joined the service as proby. supdt., Nov., 1918; confd., Jan., 1920; Khan Sahib, 1938; dep. p.m.g., Jan., 1946.

FAZL, A. H. A. (see Abul Fazl, A. H.).

FAZL ALI, SIR SAVID, Barr., Judl. dept., Bihar (b. 19th Sept., 1886).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, Feb., 1929; performed duties of Chief Justice, Patna High ct., May to July, 1938; Kt., Jan., 1941; chairman R.I.N. Commn., Apl., to July, 1946.

FAZL MUHAMMAD KHAN, KHAN, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. Jan., 1888).—Apptd. asst. consvr., from 19th July, 1922; dep. consvr., July, 1926; retd., March, 1943.

FAZLUR RAHMAN KHONDKAR, B.A., Indian Police (supdt.) (b. 1st Feb., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supdt., Bengal, 22nd Apl., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; confd., supdt., June, 1942; spec. supdt., intell. branch., Aug., 1943.

FEARN, JOHN MARTIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 24th June, 1916).—Educ. at Dundee High Sch., St. Andrew's Univ., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 24th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

FEARON, LEONARD KEARY, Salt Dept., Madras (dep. commr.) (b. 10th Nov., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 11th Dec., 1901; asst. inspr., Apl., 1905; inspr., Apl., 1910; asst. commr., Oct., 1920; dep. commr., March, 1932.

FELL, DOUGLAS YATES, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Sept., 1910).—Educ. at Charterhouse and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. pol. agent, Baluchistan Agency, Nov., 1938; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1938; offcr. spec. duty, C.I. Agency, May, 1940; under-secty. to the resdt. for Baroda and the Gujarat States and asst. pol. agent, Rewak-antha, Jan., 1941; sec. to resdt., Baroda and Gujarat States, July, 1944; sec. to resdt., Baroda and states of W.I. and Gujarat, Nov., 1944; asst. pol. agt. and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Dec., 1945; offcr. on sp. duty in Baluchistan, May, 1946; pol. agt. in Chagai, July, 1946.

FELL, SIR GODFREY BUTLER HUNTER, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma).—Retd., Sept., 1924.*

FENTON, J. M., Indian State Rlys. (b. 25th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service 9th June, 1922; dep. gen. mgr. E.I. Rly., July, 1945.

FENTON, RICHARD VINCENT M.B.E., Indian C.S. (dep. sec., govt. of India) (b. 17th July, 1915).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938;

confd., 13th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in C.P. as asst. commr.; A.D.C. to govt., C.P. and Behar, Jan., 1943; under sec., govt. of C.P., Apr., 1943; under sec., govt. of India, dept. of I. and S., Aug., 1943; ditto, infmn. and arts dept., Jan., 1946; M.B.E., Jan., 1946; dep. sec., ditto, April, 1946.

FENWICK, EDGAR NORMAN, late Indian Service of Engrs., Punjab (b. 30th Aug., 1892).—Retd., June, 1938.*

FERGUSON, ALISON JEAN, India Office (actg. hr. exec. offr., acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 5th July, 1920).—Apptd. exec. offr., 15th Aug., 1939; actg. hr. ditto, July, 1945.

FERGUSON, DONALD HARRY, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 8th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. transpn. supt., G.I.P. Rly., 16th June, 1924; supt., July, 1930.

FERGUSON, ROLAND EDWARD STUART, late Indian Police, Bihar (b. 22nd Aug., 1899).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

FERMIE, OTTO WILLIAM PATRICK, C.I.E., B.Sc., B.E., India Posts and Teleg. (p.m.g.) (b. 16th July, 1891).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1918; asst. divl. engr., July, 1920; divl. engr. tels., Jul., 1928; supt. of tels., N.W. rly., Jan., 1932; offg. dir., tels., United Provs., March, 1936; confd., Aug., 1936; p.m.g. Jan., 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

FERMOR, SIR LEWIS LEIGH, Kt., O.B.E., D.Sc., A.R.S.M., F.R.S., F.G.S., F.A.S.B., M.Inst.M.M., late Geological Survey of India (b. 18th Sept., 1880).—Retd., Nov., 1935.*

FERRIE, ALLAN, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 12th Apr., 1892).—Retd., March, 1939.*

FFORDE, SIR CECIL ROBERT, Kt., K.C., late Judicial Dept., Punjab (b. 24th June, 1876).—Retd., March, 1931.*

FFORDE, THEOBALD PHILIP FEATHERSTONE-
HAUGH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 25th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, 27th Jan., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; asst. supt., June, 1946.

FFOULKES, GEORGE ROBERT GWYNNE, Indian Police (b. 9th June, 1915).—Asst. supt., Bihar, apptd., on probn., 11th Nov., 1935; confd., Feb., 1940.

FIELD, SIR DONALD MOYLE, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd. (b. 19th Nov., 1881).—Retd., July, 1935.*

FIELD, GEOFFREY GLENNIE, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 3rd June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 22nd Nov., 1915; I.A.R.O. from 8th July, 1918, to 8th Aug., 1919; offg. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Dec., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932.

FIELD, JOHN BERTRAM FINCH, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 15th July, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. supt., Nov., 1933; confd., Dec., 1940.

FIELD, ROBERT EDWIN, C.B.E., India Office (actg. asst. sec., services and genl. dept.) (b. 27th Dec., 1888).—Apptd. second-class clerk, India Office, 22nd Apr., 1910, and attached to the pol. dept.; transf. to acct.-genl.'s dept., Dec., 1911; asst. sec. to cttee. on State technical scholarships, Apr. to Sept., 1912; transf. to dept. of sec. for Indian students, Sept., 1912; sub-warden, 21, Cromwell Road, S.W., Apr. to Oct., 1913; actg. warden, ditto, Nov., 1914, to Feb., 1919; sec. to London advisory cttee. for Indian students, Aug., 1913; minor staff clerk, Feb., 1916; asst. to educt. adviser for Indian

students, Feb., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; asst. to jt. sec. for Indian students, Nov., 1920; re-transf. to India Office as asst. princpl., Apr., 1921; jt. sec. to cttee. on Indian students, 1921-22; reading clerk to council, Nov., 1924; pte. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state, June, 1926; actg. princpl., 1st Dec., 1928; confd., Apr., 1930; O.B.E., June, 1936; actg. asst. secty., services and gen. dept. and estabt. offr., July, 1940; C.B.E., Jan., 1946.

FIELDEN, LIONEL, C.I.E., late All-India Radio (b. 15th May, 1896).—Joined the service, as Controller of Broadcasting, on 30th Aug., 1935; retd., Apr., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

FINDLAY, SIR CHARLES STEWART, Kt., M.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 28th Aug., 1874).—Retd., Jan., 1932.*

FINDLAY, JAMES, M.A., M.B., Ch.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd., late Jail Dept., Burma (b. 19th Aug., 1886).—Retd., Aug., 1941.†

FINLAY, WILLIAM WATERS, M.A. (Edin.), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 8th May, 1899).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1931, and again, Sept., 1933; offg. secty., bd. of rev., United Provs., Jan., 1934; dep. commr., Nov., 1934; confd. mag. and collr., Nov., 1938.

FINLAYSON, ANDREW, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 11th Aug., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, on 17th Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; temp. suptg. engr., Sept., 1943.

FINNEY, PHILIP EDMUND STANLEY, O.B.E., Indian Police Serv. (dep. dir., govt. of India, home dept.) (b. 11th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 5th Dec., 1924, offg. supt. temp. employed as supt., Deoli jail, Dec., 1931; confd., as supt., July, 1937; O.B.E., June, 1934; dep. commr., Calcutta police, Feb., 1938, to March, 1940; supt., centr. intell. bureau, July, 1943; centr. intell. offr., Nov., 1945; dep. dir., intell. bureau, govt. of India, home dept. May, 1946.

FISCHER, C. E., R.E., Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service 27th Feb., 1919; dep. ch. engr. B.B. and C.I. Rly., Dec., 1944.

FISCHER, FELIX CHRISTOPHER, Burma Income Tax Dept. (b. 9th June, 1893).—Joined the service, Aug., 1915; inc. tax. offr., Oct., 1924; asst. commr. income tax, Oct. 1945.

FISH, REV. FREDERIC JOHN, B.A., M.C., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 13th Apr., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, Punjab, 5th Feb., 1930; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1932.

FISHER, GERALD THOMAS, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 27th Aug., 1887).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1908; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, as asst. commr., Kohat, 13th May, 1919; under-sec. to agent to gov.-genl. in Central India, March, 1921; confd. as pol. officer, June, 1922; pol. agent, Chagai, March, 1924; H.M. Consul for Sistan and Kain, Jan., 1925; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Dec., 1927; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Nov., 1928; secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, March, 1932; ch. minister, Kapurthala state, Jan., 1935; resdt. at Gwalior and pol. agent for states of Rampur and Benares, Apr., 1937, again, Oct., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; resdt.

for C. India, Feb., 1940; confd. as resdt., cl. I, July, 1941; C.S.I., June, 1942; ret'd., March, 1943.

FISHER, WILLIAM HUMPHREY WALTER, Indian Police (b. 30th March, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd., May, 1943.

FISHWICK, DAVID, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 29th Apr., 1906).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch. and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., local govt. dept., Burma, March, 1933; asst. collr., Impl. customs service, Rangoon, March, 1934; asst. commr., Oct., 1934; joint registrar, co-operative societies, Oct., 1935; dep. commr., offg., Dec., 1935; confd., May, 1939; dep. commr., 1940-41; interned by Japanese, 1942.

FISK, SYDNEY PATRICK, Indian Police (b. 2nd June, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1938.

FITT, WATKIN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 22nd June, 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 10th Oct., 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct., 1915; on mil. service from Feb., 1916, to Aug., 1919; second-class clerk, 29th Apr., 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from Apr., 1921; exec. officer, Apr., 1922; higher exec. officer, Nov., 1934.

FITZE, SIR KENNETH SAMUEL, K.C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 6th Jan., 1887).—Ret'd., June, 1944; advr. to sec. of state for India, June, 1944.

FITZHERBERT, RICHARD ANTHONY, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 4th March, 1887).—Ret'd., March, 1942.

FITZMAURICE, SIR DESMOND FITZ-JOHN, C.I.E., B.A. Lieut.-Col., R.E., ret'd. (master, security printing) (b. 17th Aug., 1893).—Apptd. dep. mint master, on probn., Nov., 1929; ditto (tempy.), Dec., 1932; offg. master, security printing, Sept., 1934; confd., Oct., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1941; Kt., Jan., 1946.

FLACK, ALAN WILLIAM, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (addl. dist. and sess. judge Bihar) (b. 1st Nov., 1913).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1937; sub-divl. offr., Dec., 1940; supt., cent. jail, Apr., 1943; dist. mag. and collr., Mar., 1944; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1944.

FLATT, LESLIE NEEVE, C.I.E., B.Sc., V.D., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Ryls (b. 26th March, 1889).—Joined the service, 4th Jan., 1913; asst. loco. supt., E.I. Rly., June, 1913; in milty. employ from 22nd Feb., 1916, to 24th Feb., 1919; works manager, G.I.P. Rly, Aug., 1920; dep. ch. mech. engr., March, 1923; dep. carr. and wagon supt., Oct., 1923; dep. ch. mech. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. dep. loco supt., Apr., 1927; offg. loco. and carr. supt., Dec., 1930; offg. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. Rly., Feb., 1934; confd., July, 1935; ditto, N.W. Rly., Jan., 1938; div. mechl. engr., Apr. 1943 to 1945; C.I.E., June, 1943.

FLEETON, ERIC RALPH, Indian State Ryls. (supt.) (b. 24th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt., 1st Sept., 1920; transptn. (power) supt., E.I. Rly., Sept., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. Rly., March, 1936; provl. ch. mechl. engr., Apr., 1940.

FLETCHER, ALAN HENRY SAUL, M.B.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Assam) (b. 12th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1934; confd., Jan., 1942; M.B.E., Jan., 1943; sec. to govt., educ., etc. depts., May, 1946.

FLETCHER, ANTONY LEOCADIA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Dec., 1909).—Educ. at St. Joseph's High Sch., Trivandrum, the Universities of Madras and Nagpur, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offcr., Nov., 1936, to May, 1938.

FLETCHER, EDWARD WALTER, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 23rd July, 1899).—First commn., 23rd Jan., 1919; in milty. employ to Jan., 1928; British trade agent, Gyantse, and asst. to pol. officer in Sikkim, Nov., 1929; secty., Brit. legn., Kabul, Dec., 1932; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, March, 1935; on depn. to Baltic States for 13 mths. from Dec., 1936; secty. to resdt. for W.I., May, 1938; addl. dep. sec. external affairs dept., Dec., 1939; first secty., Brit. legation, Kabul, Aug., 1940; comm. agt., Ahwaz, Apr. 1944; spec. duty, extl aff. dept., Apr., 1945; spec. duty, Brit. consu genl., Pondicherry, Nov., 1945; consul genl. in French Ests. in India, Dec., 1945.

FLETCHER, JOHN HINTON, M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 23rd June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under-secty. to govt., irrign. branch, Punjab, Jan., 1931; ditto (construction), Oct., 1934; offg. ditto (northern admin.), Feb., 1936; supt. engr. (perm't.), July, 1941; jt. sec., govt., of Punjab, (home) (addl. charge), July, 1941 to Feb., 1945.

FLEURY, WALTER WILFRED RONALD, Indian Service of Engrs. (under-sec., P.W.D., Orissa) (b. 7th Apr., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 25th Aug., 1930; under sec., p. w.d., Apr., 1944.

FLEWETT, WILLIAM EDWARD, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Burma) (b. 22nd June, 1893).—Apptd. dep. consvr., Punjab, 16th Dec., 1921; div. forest officer, Andamans, Sept., 1931; offg. ch. forest officer, Andamans, Aug., 1931, to Aug., 1936; chief timber inspcn. offcr., dept. of supply, Sept., 1939; dep. dir., directorate-genl. of supply, July, 1940.

FLINT, HARRY EWART, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Burma) (b. 22nd Aug., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 18th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., May, 1925; O.B.E., June, 1932; served in R.I.N., Aug., 1939 to Jan., 1945; consvr. (confd.), Oct., 1945.

FLOWERDEW, RICHARD EDWARD, C.I.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 26th Sept., 1886).—Ret'd., Apr., 1939.

FLUX, RONALD CECIL, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 2nd June, 1901).—Joined the service on 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; on depn. to U.K., Aug., 1944 to May, 1946.

FORBES, GEORGE FREEMAN MURRAY, O.B.E., M.C., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 5th June, 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1922; asst. supt. (proby.), Sept., 1922; confd., Jan., 1927; offg. supt., govt. rly. police, May, 1927; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1927; confd., July, 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1932.

FORD, WILLIAM HENRY, M.B.E., Burma Office (actg. staff offr.) (b. 26th Dec., 1898).—Apptd. cler. offr., Nov., 1921; hr. ditto, Mar., 1937; M.B.E., Jan., 1941; actg. hr. exec. offr., Apl., 1943; actg. staff offr., Burma Office, Sept., 1946.

FORRESTER, CHARLES, A.H.W.C., F.I.C., Ph.D. (Edin.), F.R.S.E., A.M.I.Chem.E., Indian School of Mines (princl., Indian Sch. of Mines) (b. 6th March, 1895).—Apptd. prof. of chemistry and assaying on probn., 3rd Nov., 1926; confd., 1929; offg. principal, Indian Sch. of Mines, May, 1927; Nov., 1929; and June, 1934; principal, ditto, Nov., 1936.

FORSYTH, JAMES PETERS, B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (actg. dir. of purchase, store dept.) (b. 22nd Apl., 1883).—Educ. at R. Gr. Sch., Lancaster, and Caius Coll., Cambridge (8th wrangler); apptd. junr. clerk, store dept., 19th Oct., 1906; senr. clerk, July, 1912; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; dir. of purchase (temp.), Apl., 1927; confd., Nov., 1932; actg. dir.-genl., stores dept., May, 1940; on spl. duty, July, 1941.

FORTESCUE, VEN. CECIL FRANCIS, L.Th., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Bombay) (b. 14th Oct., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 18th Jan., 1922; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1924; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1930; bishop's commissary (in addition), Apl., 1939; archdeacon of Bombay, Dec., 1939; ret'd., June, 1943.

FORTY, FREDERICK CHARLES, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 17th Sept., 1894).—Apptd. to acct.-genl.'s dept., India Office, 15th Dec., 1919; transf. to office of High Commr. for India, 8th Apl., 1921; clerical officer, June, 1921; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; actg. hr. ditto, March, 1939; progress offr., Apl., 1941.

FOSTER, ESMOND LEWIS PEARCE, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (ch. forest offr., Andamans) (b. 28th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924; divl. forest officer, Sth. Andamans, Aug., 1935; ch. forest officer, ditto, Oct., 1935; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; utilization offr., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1942; O.S.D., home dept., June, 1945; ch. forest offr., Andamans, Oct., 1945.

FOSTER, KENNETH WILLIAM, B.A., Burma C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 13th Aug., 1899).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Dec., 1921; dep. commr., Aug., 1937; secy. to financial commissioner, March, 1939; sec. to govt. of Burma, 1944; sec., ch. sec.'s office, April, 1943; addl. sec., fin. and rev. dept., Oct., 1944.

FOSTER, SIR WILLIAM, Kt., C.I.E., B.A., late India Office.—Ret'd., Nov., 1923.*

FOWLE, FULWAL WILLIAM CRAVEN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 4th March, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. eng., 27th Nov., 1908; resdt. engr., Sept., 1912; in milty. employ from 27th Sept., 1917, to 26th May, 1920; divl. engr., Feb., 1926; ret'd.

FOWLER, ROBERT WILLIAM DOUGHTY, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (dep. commr.) (b. 6th March, 1914).—Educ. at Queen Elizabeth's Gr. Sch., Mansfield, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1937, and served as asst. commr.; on milty. service, Nov., 1940; addtl. under-sec., def. dept., March, 1941; dep. sec. in ch. sec.'s office (Simla), Jan., 1944; civ. aff. serv., Mar., 1944 to Dec., 1945; dep. commr., 1945.

FOX, SIR CYRIL SANKEY, D.Sc., Geological

Survey of India (b. 24th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 2nd March, 1911; supt., Dec., 1930; offg. dir., July, 1936; confd., July, 1939; kt., June, 1943.

FOY, THOMAS ARTHUR WYNESSE, C.I.E., Ind. Service of Engineers (ch. engr.) (b. 20th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1927; under-secy. to govt., irrig. branch, Punjab, May, 1931; ditto (constn.), Punjab irrig. sect., May, 1933; chief engr., irrig., Bikaner State, from Oct., 1935; supt. engr. (permt.), Apl., 1943; served under govt. of India, external affairs, June, 1943; C.I.E., June, 1945; on depn. to U.S.A., May to Oct., 1945; ch. engr. and sec. to govt. Punjab (confd.), Feb., 1946.

FRAMJI, KAVSI KAIKHOSRU, M.B.E., B.A., B.Sc., Ind. Service of Engrs. (b. 21st March, 1908).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 30th July, 1932; M.B.E., Jan., 1943; dir. Indian waterways exp. stn., Poona, Nov., 1945.

FRAMPTON, HENRY JAMES, C.I.E., M.C., B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (ch. sec., govt. United Provs., March, 1945) (b. 14th Aug., 1897).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and St. John's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; asst. mag. and collr., United Provs., 2nd Dec., 1921; jt. mag., Dec., 1923; offg. mag. and collr. Jan., 1926; dep. secy. to govt., United Provs., genl. bch., Oct., 1932; mag. and collr., Nov., 1936; dep. commr., March, 1937; dep. secy., home dept., govt. of India, May, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; ch. sec., govt. United Provs., Mar., 1945.

FRANCE, REV. ALEXANDER, late Indian Ecclesl. Estabt. (b. 24th Oct., 1886).—Ret'd., Oct., 1941.

FRANCE, ROBERT EDWARD, India Office (actg. princpl., econ. dept.) (b. 16th Aug., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 27th Oct., 1919, after previous service from 27th Apl., 1916; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Oct., 1919; clerical offr., 1st Jan., 1920; exec. offr., May, 1926; actg. hr. exec. offr., Oct., 1936; confd., Sept., 1938; temp. asst. admin. offr., March, 1940; lent to Miny. of Supply, Sept., 1940, to Dec., 1943; actg. princpl., econ. and overseas dept., Jan., 1944.

FRANCIS, JOSEPH STANDISH NOEL, Burma Engrg. Serv., (Cl. I) (b. 30th Aug., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 7th Feb., 1929; confd. 6th June, 1932; exec. engr. 5th Feb., 1936; on mil. serv., Mar. 1945 to Jan., 1946.

FRANKLIN, ERIC ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (regr. high ct. Rangoon, Burma) (b. 3rd July, 1910).—Educ. at Govt. English High Sch., Maymyo, Rangoon Univ., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 7th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., Oct., 1939; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1941; dep. sec., ch. sec.'s dept., 1944; on sp. duty comm. and supply dept., Oct., 1945; dist. and sess. judge, 1945-46; regr. high ct., Rangoon, July, 1946.

FRANKLIN, JOHN FORD, Burma Frontier Service, (b. 3rd Sept., 1909).—Apptd. 15th Jan., 1935.

FRANKLIN, MURIEL CURRAN, LL.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Burma) (b. 18th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as head mistress, govt. normal school, Rangoon, on 3rd Jan., 1924; inspectress of schools, Feb., 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1943.

FRASER, DENHOLME DE MONTALTE STUART, C.S.I., C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian

Political Service (pol. a.d.c. to sec. of state) (b. 5th Oct., 1889).—First commn. dated 20th Jan., 1909; first asst. to pol. resident, Persian Gulf, Nov., 1919; under-sec. to agent to govr.-genl. in Central India, Nov., 1922; asst. magr. and collr., Moradabad, Dec., 1924; asst. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1925; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, May, 1926; pol. agent, Eastern Kathiawar States, Dec., 1926; pol. agent in Southern States of C.I. and Malwa, Oct., 1928; on foreign service in Tonk State, Oct., 1930; pol. agent, Malwa, Apl., 1935; offg. secty. to A.G.G. in C.I. in addn., May, 1936; pol. agent, Malwa, Apl., 1937; offg. resdt. for C.I., March to July, 1938; resdt. in Kashmir, Oct., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1938; confd. as 2nd class.resdt., June, 1939; resdt. in Mysore, Nov., 1941; confd., June, 1942; C.S.I., Jan., 1944; ret'd., Apl., 1945; pol. a.d.c. to sec. of State, Apl., 1945.

FRASER, DONALD MACDONALD, Maj., I.M.S., Civil Surgeon, United Provs.—Date of first commn., 18th Jan., 1925; civil surgeon, March, 1934; dir. health services, Delhi.

FRASER, JAMES DUNCAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr. Punjab) (b. 11th Jan., 1915).—Educ. at Haileybury and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 18th Oct., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; a.d.c. to the govt., Nov., 1941; sub-divl. offr., Apl., 1943; dep. commr.

FRASER, SIR STUART MITFORD, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. and Political Dept.—Ret'd., June, 1920.*

FRASER-TYTLER, SIR WILLIAM KERR, K.B.E., C.M.G., M.C., Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 26th Dec., 1886).—First commn. dated 24th Aug., 1910; asst. sec., for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1921; sec., Kabul legation, Apl., 1923; asst. to agent, govt.-genl., Madras States, Dec., 1925; asst. commr., Mansehra, March, 1927; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, Dec., 1927; and again, Oct., 1930; dep. commr., D.I. Khan, June, 1928; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, Oct., 1930; chargé d'affaires, ditto, Apl., 1932; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1933; confd., Sept., 1933; C.M.G., June, 1933; offg. foreign secty. to Govt. of India, May, 1933; offg. pte. secty. to Viceroy, May, 1934; H.B.M.'s minister, Kabul, March, 1935; K.B.E., Jan., 1939; ret'd., Dec., 1941.

FREAK, LESLIE ASHFIELD, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E. Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Punjab) (b. 28th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. engr. (subst.), March, 1942; chief engr. (subst.) and sec., govt. of Punjab, p.w.d., Apl., 1944.

FREDERICK, AMBULLUR EZEKIEL DAVID, B.A., Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 7th May, 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 6th Sept., 1928; confd., Sept., 1930; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; offg. dist. supt., Jan., 1936; confd., Aug., 1941.

FREDERICK, JESUBATHAM Moses, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 19th Nov., 1901).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 8th July, 1925; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1933; confd., Oct., 1935.

FREELAND, SIR HENRY FRANCIS EDWARD, K.C.I.E., C.B., M.V.O., D.S.O., Maj.-Gen., R.E., late Railway Dept.—Ret'd., July, 1920.*

FREKE, CECIL GEORGE, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc.,

late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 8th Oct., 1887).—Ret'd., Sept., 1939.*

FROME, NORMAN FREDERIC, C.I.E., B.Sc. (Eng.), M.I.E.E., D.F.C., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (p.m.g.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1899).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1923, as asst. div. engr.; div. engr., Oct., 1931; dir. Sind and Baluchistan Circle, Nov., 1934; senr. elec. engr., March, 1935; dir. of tels., senr. elec. engr., March, 1935; elec. engr.-in-c., May, 1937; dir. of tels., Apl., 1938; p.m.g., June, 1941; addtl. ch. engr., July, 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

FRY, AUGUSTINE SARGOOD, C.I.E., M.B., Ch.M., F.R.C.S.E., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (Punjab) (b. 4th Nov., 1890).—First commn., 13th March, 1916; civil surg., Punjab, Apl., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; prof., operative surg., King Edward medl. coll., Lahore, Feb., 1942.

FRY, Rev. ERNEST HENRY, late Indian Eccl. Estabt.—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Madras, 16th Oct., 1931; snr. chaplain, Oct., 1943; on mil. duty, May, 1941 to Aug., 1943; ret'd., June, 1946.

FRY, LESLIE ALFRED CHARLES, M.B.E., Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 17th Apl., 1908).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1927; in mil. employ till Nov., 1933; asst. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Nov., 1933; asst. commr., Lucknow, June, 1934; under training in the Govt. of India secretariat, May, 1935; asst. commr., Lucknow, July, 1935; under-secty. to the resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1935; vice-consul Zabul, Dec., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; under sec. to resdt., Punjab States, Feb., 1939; offcr. on spec. duty and 2nd asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Apl., 1940; asst. pol. agent, Chhattisgarh States, Oct., 1940; under-secty., ext. affs. dept., June, 1941; again, Nov., 1943; M.B.E., Jan., 1944; asst. to resdt., Kashmir, March, 1944.

FULLER, EDWIN GERALD, Burma Commn. (collr., Rangoon) (b. 16th Jan., 1893).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 20th Oct., 1914; extra asst. commr., March, 1921; addl. dist. magte., July, 1931; dep. commr., Aug., 1934; collr., Rangoon Town and addl. dist. mag., Rangoon, 1938, also contr., Burma State Lottery; served under govt. of Bihar, Jan., 1943 to July, 1944; civil aff. serv., Oct., 1944; dep. inspr.-genl., prisons, Dec., 1945; collr., July, 1946.

FURLEY, HUGH DENYS, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 7th Jan., 1883).—Ret'd., Jan., 1938.*

FURNESS, CECIL ARUNDEL, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 5th March, 1885).—Apptd. to accent.-genl.'s dept., India Office, 4th Sept., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, 1st Apl., 1921; clerical officer, Oct., 1921; exec. officer, Apl., 1922.

FURNESS, HORACE GEOFFREY, late Indian Police (b. 11th March, 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1909; asst. supt., July, 1912; I.A.R.O., from 6th Aug., 1916, to 28th Feb., 1919; offg. supt., Jan., 1920, and again, Jan., 1923; confd., Oct., 1926; princpl. police training sch., Vellore, June, 1936; ret'd., March, 1945.

FYFE, WILLIAM, late Dept. of Industries, Madras (b. 25th Jan., 1885).—Ret'd., Nov., 1941†

GABRIEL, SIR EDMUND VIVIAN, C.S.I., C.M.G., C.V.O., C.B.E., V.D., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Ret'd., March, 1920.*

GADRE, NARAYAN BALKRISHNA, A.M.I.E. (Ind.), B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 3rd Dec., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 8th Oct., 1923;

asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Nov., 1931; confd., Oct., 1933; under govt. of India def. dept., Nov., 1939.

GAINSFORD, DONALD, late Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 18th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 12th June, 1920; offg. supt., Apl., 1926; confd., Oct., 1931; King's police medal, March, 1940; asst. inspr. genl., Sept., 1942; retd., Aug., 1946.

GAISFORD, SIR PHILIP, Lieut-Col., C.I.E., I.A., Indian Political Serv., (b. 28th Nov., 1891).—First commn. dated 18th Jan., 1911; in mil. employ to 24th June, 1919; asst. commr., June, 1919; sec. to agent to govr.-genl. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Apl., 1925; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., March, 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; on foreign service as guardian to H.H. the Maharajah of Gwalior, March, 1929; secty. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Nov., 1934; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., March, 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; dep. secty., pol. dept., Apl., 1937, and again, Sept., 1937; offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1937; resdt. at Gwalior and pol. agent for states of Rampur and Benares, Apl., 1938; pol. agent in Bhopal, Sept., 1939; resdt. for Kolhapur and the Deccan States, March, 1940; confd. as resdt., cl. I, Jan., 1944; C.I.E., June, 1942; resdt., Mysore, Oct., 1944; Kt., June, 1946.

GAIT, SIR EDWARD ALBERT, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Assam).—Retd., Dec., 1920; member of the Council of India, 20th June, 1922, to 19th June, 1927.*

GALBRAITH, HAROLD ALEXANDER, Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 4th Jan., 1895).—Nongaz. appts. from Sept., 1914; prom. to I.P., Jan., 1942; Indian Police medal, Jan., 1944.

GALE, BERKELEY, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 5th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

GALE, LUCY, late India Office (b. 16th Jan., 1882).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st June, 1921; actg. hr. ditto, July, 1935; confd., Jan., 1936; retd., June, 1944.

GALE, U MAUNG (C1) late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 5th May, 1889).—Apptd. dist. supt. on 7th Apl., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; awarded bar to ditto, Jan., 1932; in enemy hands; retd. and re-employed as dist. supt., 1945-46.

GALES, SIR ROBERT RICHARD, Kt., F.C.H., M.Inst.C.E., M.A.S.C.E., late Railway Dept.—Retd., Oct., 1919.*

GALLAGHER, WILLIAM NORMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 12th Jan., 1913).—Educ. at Royal Academical Institution, Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 18th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

GALLANT, MAURICE NAHUM, O.B.E., B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 24th March, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928; for. econ., Oct., 1934; O.B.E., June, 1944; chairman timber projects bd., 1945.

GALLOWAY, ARNOLD CRAWSHAW, C.I.E., O.B.E., Maj., I.A., Ind. Political Serv., (b. 5th Jan., 1901).—First commn., 24th Dec., 1920; in milty. employ to June, 1928; under-secty. in A.G.G. in the states of Western India, March, 1932; vice-consul, Zahidan, Jan., 1933; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Feb., 1924; secty., ditto, June, 1934; and again, Apl., 1937; secty., British legn., Kabul, Nov., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Bahrein, Nov., 1937;

secty. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Feb., 1938; pol. agent, Kuwait, May, 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; consul-genl., Ahwaz, June, 1943; pol. agt., Muscat, Nov., 1944; offg. resdt., cl. I and pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, May, 1945; pol. agt., Bahrain, Nov., 1945; C.I.E., June, 1946.

GALVIN, JOHN SMART, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Bombay (civil surg.) (b. 4th March, 1892).—First commn., 15th March, 1920; civil surg., March, 1931; offg. presdy. surg., Bombay, Feb., 1935.

GALVIN, PERCY HAROLD, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 6th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1913, as asst. supt., E.I. rly.; transpn. supt. (traffic comml.), E.I. rly., May, 1934.

GANDASING CHEEMA, SARDAR, C.I.E., D.Sc., Indian Agric. Serv., (horticulturist to govt. of Bombay) (b. 2nd Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as horticulturist, 13th June, 1921; for. instr. (in addn.), May, 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

GANDEE, JOHN STEPHEN, M.B.E., India Office, (pte. sec. to parl. under sec. of state) (b. 8th Dec., 1909).—Apptd. cler. offr. 12th May, 1930; actg. exec. offr. June, 1937; confd. Mar., 1938; actg. hr. exec. offr., Nov., 1941; seconded as actg. staff offr., econ. and overseas dept., Oct., 1942; actg., hr. exec. offr. June, 1945; M.B.E., June, 1945; asst. princpl., Nov., 1946.

GANDHI, KAIKHUSRU ARDESHIR, M.B., B.S., Bombay Public Health Dept.—Asst. dir. public health Vaccine Inst., Belgaum, Dec., 1940 to May, 1941; dir. of public health, Bombay, (confd.) June, 1943.

GANGA NATH BABU, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., United Provs.; (b. 11th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 19th May, 1908; sub-judge, June, 1923; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1933; addl. judge, high ct., Allahabad, variously from Oct., 1934; judge, ditto, Apl., 1937; retd., 6th Jan., 1943.

GANGARAM KAULA (see Kaula, G.).

GANGULI, ANIL BEHARI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1933; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1938; addl. dep. sec., rev. dept., Bengal, May, 1942; dep. sec., rev., Sept., 1942.

GANGULI, SANAT KUMAR, B.L., late Bengal Judl. Service, (b. 9th Nov., 1886).—Retd., Nov., 1941.

GANGULY, KESAV LAL, B.E., B.S.C., Indian State Rlys. (govt. inspr. rlys.) (b. 20th Feb., 1898).—Joined the service, Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., E.B. Rly., Oct., 1923; on spec. duty, post and air dept., Nov., 1944; govt. inspr. rlys.

GARBETT, SIR COLIN CAMPBELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.M.G., B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 22nd May, 1881).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 28th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; settlt. offr., Mandi State, 1910; dist. judge, Simla, 1913; lent to mil. dept., 1915; asst. postal censor, Karachi, March, 1915; ditto, Bombay, Sept., 1916; on active service, Mesopotamia, 1916; revenue commr., Mesopotamia, Feb., 1917, and administrator, agricl. development (mil.), June, 1917; twice mentd. in despatches; C.I.E., Aug., 1917;

tempy. empld. in pol. dept., India Office, July, 1919, to Aug., 1920; civil sec. to high commr., Mesopotamia, Sept., 1920, to May, 1922; dep. commr., Dec., 1921; C.M.G., June, 1922; ch. sec. to govt., Punjab, Apl., 1931; commr., Multan, March, 1935; C.S.I., June, 1935; finl. commr. (devel.), Dec., 1937; ditto (rev.), Sept., 1939; K.C.I.E., June, 1941; ret'd., Dec., 1941.

GARDINER, REGINALD THOMAS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 27th Apl., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1929; confd., Nov., 1930; mil. duty to April, 1945; exec. engr., April, 1945.

GARDINER, RICHARD ALYMER, M.A., M.B.E., Capt. (T/Lt.-Col.), R.E., Survey of India (b. 21st June, 1911).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1931; in mil. employ. to Nov., 1936; proby. asst. supt. Nov., 1936; confd., Nov., 1938; in mil. employ. from Oct., 1941; M.B.E., 1944; supt., Dec., 1944.

GARDINER, WILLIAM NORMAN, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 1st June, 1882).—Ret'd., Dec., 1942.†

GARDNER, E., Indian State Rlys. (b. 21st Jan., 1892).—Dep. genl. manager, B.B. and C.I. rly, Jan., 1946.

GARDNER, JAMES CLARK MOLESWORTH, C.I.E., Indian Forest Serv., (b. 15th March, 1894).—Joined the ser. as asst. consvr., on 11th Dec., 1922; systematic entomologist, Aug., 1923; forest entomologist, F.R.I., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1945.

GARDNER, JOHN WILLIAM BERTRAND, M.B.E., Defence Dept., Govt. of India (b. 13th Aug., 1886).—Ret'd., Apl., 1938.*

GAREWELL, SARDAR JAGDEO SINGH, M.R.C.V.S., I.V.S. (b. 18th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as supt., C.V.D. (Punjab and N.W.F.P.), 30th Jan., 1918; confd., Jan., 1921; princpl. Punjab vet. coll., Aug., 1940; on depts. to govt. of India as livestock adviser.

GARLAND, EDWARD ANDREW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 10th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1922, as asst. consvr., Bombay, dep. consvr., Nov., 1925; p.a. to I.G. of forests, Dehra Dun, Aug., 1938; transferred to Sind, Aug., 1942; offg. var. as commr.

GARNIER, CHARLES NEWDIGATE, O.B.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 5th Jan., 1883).—Ret'd., Apl., 1938.*

GARRAD, ERNEST WILLIAM, Indian Police (b. 9th Apl., 1897) (supt.).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 12th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., July, 1929; addl. asst. inspr.-genl., Simla, June, 1935; supt., June, 1937; cmdt., provl. addl. police, Jan., 1946.

GARRETT, SIR JOSEPH HUGH, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 22nd June, 1880).—Ret'd., Nov., 1939.*

GARROD, EDGAR SAMUEL, D.S.O., M.C., D.C.M., late Indian Police (N.W.F.P.) (b. 6th March, 1895).—Ret'd., 1940.†

GARROD, REV. WILLIAM FRANCIS, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 23rd March, 1893).—Appdt. junior chaplain, 10th Feb., 1930.

GARROD, WILLIAM HENRY EDWARD, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., Bombay) (b. 10th May, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; cons. pub. health engr. to govt., Nov., 1937; suptg. engr. (provl. subst.), March, 1942; perm't., Oct., 1942; confd. as ch. engr., roads and bldgs., and sec. to govt. p.w.d., Apl., 1944; C.I.E., June, 1946.

GARSON, WILLIAM AUBREY, M.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd May, 1895).—Ret'd., June, 1938.*
