GARVIN, STEPHEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 2nd Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; appdt. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

GASH, REV. IVER JOHN, A.K.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 26th May, 1890).—Appointed chaplain-on-probn., Bengal, 9th Oct., 1928; transferred to Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1929; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1930; transf. to Central Provs., Oct., 1932; senior chaplain, Jan., 1935; ret'd., May, 1945.

GASKING, REV. CLEMENT AUGUSTINE, late Indian Eccles. Establishment (Punjab) (b. 6th June, 1894).—Appdt. chaplain on probn., 12th Oct., 1926; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1928; ret'd., May, 1946.

GASSON, SIR LIONEL BELL, late Ind. Police (Madras) (b. 9th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1910; asst. supt., July, 1912; supt., Jan., 1926; dep. commr., March, 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; asst. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1930; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1934; supt., spl. branch, C.I.D., Apl., 1935; cent. intell. offcr., Madras Prov., May, 1938; commr. (confd.), July, 1939; Kt., Jan., 1944; ret'd., Aug., 1944.

GASTER, VIVIAN ISAAC, B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (educ. officer, principal exec. grade, education dept.) (b. 11th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; on mil. service, Nov., 1914, to Apl., 1917; asst. principal (tempy.), Home Office, Oct., 1918, to Feb., 1920; asst. to local adviser to Indian students, London, Feb., 1920; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, as asst. to joint secs., Indian students' dept., Oct., 1920; principal exec. officer, educ. dept., Apl., 1925; on loan to Min. of Home Security, May, 1941.

GASTRELL, EVERARD HUDDLESTON, O.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Apl., 1898).—First commn. dated 14th Nov., 1916; joined pol. dept., 8th March, 1922; Brit. Consul for Arabistan, July, 1922; vice-consul, Ahwaz, Nov., 1922; on settlement training in Punjab, Nov., 1926; services placed at disposal of ch. commr., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1927; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, March, 1927; vice-consul, Duzdap, Dec., 1930; secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Dec., 1931; and again, Nov., 1933; pol. agent, Bahrein, July, 1933; consul-genl. for Khorasan, March, 1934; claims commr., Baluchistan, July, 1935; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, March, 1936; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Nov., 1936; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Dec., 1936; pol. agent i/c. of the Bolan Pass and Chagai dist., Feb., 1937; O.B.E., May, 1937; sec. to resdt., Central India, March, 1939; census supt., Baluchistan, Dec., 1939; H.B.M.'s consul-genl. in the French Establishments in India, July, 1941; addtl. counsellor, Brit. legation, Tehran, Nov., 1944.

GATELEY, TERENCE PATRICK, M.B.E. (b. 16th Dec., 1898).—Elec. engr., Central Public Works Dept., Oct., 1933; M.B.E., June, 1941. suptg. engr., elec. circle, Aug., 1942 and July, 1943.

GATES, SIR FRANK CAMPBELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Barr. (1891), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Ret'd., Apl., 1914.*

GATFORD, HARRY, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Ryls. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 6th Apr., 1889).—Joined the service, 10th Apr., 1920; resdt. engr., E.I. rly., Apr., 1924; exec. engr. (junr.), E.I. rly., Apr., 1930; ditto (senr.), Nov., 1933.

GAUNTLETT, SIR MAGER FREDERIC, K.C.I.E., K.B.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 12th Oct., 1873).—Retd., May, 1930.*

GAURI, NATH, P.M.S., late Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 20th Sept., 1884).—Retd., July, 1938.*

GAWAN-TAYLOR, EDWARD, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Sind) (b. 29th Oct., 1889).—Educ. at Durham Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr., supt. land records, Southern Div., Jan., 1921; on depn. as settlt. officer from Dec., 1921, to May, 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1926; ditto and Talukdari settlt. offr., Feb., 1926; confd., Apr., 1931; retd., Aug., 1945.

GAWNE, EWAN MOORE, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 26th March, 1889).—Educ. at Wellington, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived 8th Dec., 1913; sub. collr., and jt. mag. Oct., 1917; under-sec. to govt. Jan., 1919, to Feb. 1921; temp. dep. sec. to govt. of Madras, Apr., 1924, to June, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., July, 1925; confd., Feb., 1932; member, bd. of rev., Jan, 1940; again, Apr., 1944; ch. sec., Aug., 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; civil rep., Southern army, Feb., 1943; advr. to govt., Aug., 1944; C.S.I., Jan., 1945; ch. sec., May, 1946.

GAWTHORNE, ARTHUR RICHARDSON, Indian State Ryls. (exec. engr.) (b. 12th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 15th Apr., 1920; exec. engr., Burma Ryls., July, 1933.

GEE, EDWARD ROWLANDS, M.A., D.I.C., F.G.S., F.N.I., Geol. Survey of India, (b. 1st Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt. (geologist), 12th Dec., 1923.

GEILS, ERNEST DONALD, late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 19th Oct., 1885).—Retd., Oct., 1940.†

GELL, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM MONCREIFF, Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 8th Nov., 1917).—Educ. at Wellington and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., pol. dept., March, 1944.

GELSON, WILLIAM EDWARD, B.Sc., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.Mech.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Ryls. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 5th Feb., 1899).—Asst. exec. engr., bridges, Nov., 1926; provl. perm. bridge engr., Oct., 1937; research officer, rly. board, Jan., 1941; retd., Dec., 1943.

GEMMELL, GEOFFREY WOODBURN, Lieut.-Col., I.A. Survey of India (supt.) (b. 26th Sept., 1897).—First commn., 15th Nov., 1915; asst., supt., geodetic bch., May, 1927; supt., Apr. 1930.

GENNINGS, JOHN FREDERICK, C.I.E., C.B.E., Barr. (b. 21st Sept., 1885).—Retd., Sept., 1940.†

GENTLE, JOSEPH ALFRED HECTOR ROBERTS, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 26th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 2nd Jan., 1925; dep. consvr., Jan., 1929.

GENTRY, EDGAR CHARLES (b. 10th Aug., 1893).—Architect, Central Public Works Dept., Oct., 1933.

GEORGE, FRANK, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 9th Feb., 1907).—Educ. at Priory Sch., Shrewsbury, and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; secty., finl. commr., Sept., 1938; settlement offr., Nov., 1938; dep. sec. reconstrn. dept., Oct., 1943; civ. aff. serv., Feb., 1944; dep. commr., 1945.

GEORGE, HUGH SHAW, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 29th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 6th Dec., 1915; on mil. duty from 9th Aug., 1917, to 8th March, 1919; dep. consvr., Nov., 1921; sylviculturist, C.P., Oct., 1932; consvr. (confd.), Jan., 1939; ch. consvr., Jan., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

GEORGE, JAMES, M.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 17th May, 1905).—Educ. at Priory County Sch., Shrewsbury, Queen's Coll., Cambridge, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Apr., 1931; offg. under-secy. to govt., Bengal, pol. and appt. depts., Sept., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936.

GEORGESON, WILLIAM WARES, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 13th March, 1906).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; sub-collr., Jan., 1933; dist. and sess. judge (confd.) Aug., 1943; dist. and sess. judge, Trichinopoly, Aug., 1944.

GERAGHTY, MICHAEL JOSEPH, Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 19th Dec., 1929; mil. duty, Nov., 1940.

GERRARD, CHARLES ROBERT, Bombay Educ. Service, (b. 31st Jan., 1892).—Joined the service 22nd Feb., 1935; dep. dir. Sir J. J. School of Art, Bombay, Feb., 1935 to Mar., 1937; dir. ditto, on probn., Mar., 1937; confd., Jan., 1940.

GERRARD, MISS JESSIE MARGARET, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., Lady Willingdon training coll., Madras) (b. 9th Jan., 1892).—Apptd. prof., Queen Mary's Coll., Madras, 8th Aug., 1921; princpl., Lady Willingdon trng. coll., Dec., 1925; confd., Jan., 1927.

GHAFFUR, ABDUL (see Abdul Ghaffur).

GHANI, M.A., B.A. (Hons.) (b. March, 1904).—Held non-gaz. posts in commerce dept. to Apr., 1941; held minor gaz. appts. in supply dept., Apr., 1941; asst. sec., ditto, July, 1944; asst. sec. comm. dept., Sept., 1944.

GHANSHYAM, DAS, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., LL.B., late Punjab C.S. (b. 10th Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

GHATAK, LABANYA MOHAN, M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 2nd Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as an offcr., cl. II, May, 1923; dep. mil. acct. genl. (jnr.), March, 1935; ditto (senr.), Apr., 1936; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Apr., 1936; revtd. to mil. accts. dep., July, 1939; prom. cl. I (prov. subs.), June, 1942; promoted commd. contr., May, 1945.

GHATAK, NARENDRANATH, M.B.E., Barr., Judl. Dept., Bengal (b. Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as master and official referee, high ct., original side, Calcutta, 22nd Nov., 1922.

GHATE, BAL GANGADHAR, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 1st Oct., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 27th Apr., 1928.

GHATE, NAMDEV SITARAMPANT, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 4th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

GHATGE, SAMBAJJIRAO APPASAHAB, B.A., LL.B., Bombay Civil Serv. (b. 1st May, 1901).—Joined the service 1st Nov., 1936; collr. and dist. mag., (confd.) Oct., 1942.

GHAZANFAR ALI KHAN, RAJA.—Apptd. health member of governor genl's council, Oct., 1946.

GHAZANFAR ALI NAQVI, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 17th Apr., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 27th May, 1926; offg. supt., March, 1933; confd., March, 1940.

GHOLAP, LAKSHMAN TRIMBAK, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 4th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, Lond., joined the serv. 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. pol. agt. Jawhar, Mar., 1933; collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1936; dep. sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, Apr., 1941; controller of Ind. shipping, Bombay, March, 1944; and ex-officio jt. sec. govt. of India, commerce dept., C.I.E., June, 1946.

GHOSAL, SIR JOSNA, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 13th June, 1871).—Retd., Jan., 1931.*

GHOSE, BIRENDRA NATH, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. traffic-commcl. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 12th Aug., 1900).—Joined the service, 15th Apr., 1919; asst. supt., June, 1922; senr. scale (prov'l.), Feb., 1938; confd., Nov., 1938.

GHOSE, SIR SARAI KUMAR, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 3rd July, 1879).—Retd., July, 1939.*

GHOSH, AJIT KUMAR, Indian Police (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 3rd March, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Feb., 1940; confd., March, 1944.

GHOSH, AJOY KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Apr., 1908).—Educ. at King's Coll. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Nov., 1932; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1935; sec. bd. of rev., March, 1940; addl. dist. mag., Nov., 1940; addl. dep. sec., rev. dept., Sept., 1942; ditto and addl. dep. dir. civ. supplies in addn., Apr., 1943; jt. sec., ch. minister's dept., Mar., 1946.

GHOSH, B. B., Indian Audit and Accts. serv. (b. March, 1905).—Joined the service, Apr., 1930; asst. fin. advr., mil. fin., Nov., 1940; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin. May, 1943; ditto, munitions prodn., April, 1945; ditto, mil. fin., May, 1946.

GHOSH CHAUDHURI, HARI SADHAN, B.Sc., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 3rd Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Bengal, 11th March, 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; confd. supt., June, 1942; dep. comm. police, Calcutta, July, 1942.

GHOSH CHAUDHURI, TAPENDRA KUMAR, Rai Sahib, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1887).—Retd., 1942.†

GHOSH, JOTISH CHANDRA, Indian Police (asst., supt., Orissa) (b. 28th Nov., 1912).—

Apptd. on probn., Jan., 1937; confd., 15th Dec., 1937; transfd. to Orissa, June, 1940.

GHOSH, J. M., Barr., Judl. Dept., Bengal (regr. in insolvency, high ci., Calcutta) (b. 16th Apr., 1884).—Joined the service, 14th May, 1921; asst. regr., high ct., Calcutta, original side, Nov., 1921; dep. regr., ditto, Dec., 1931; offg. regr., in insolvency, original side, Nov., 1932.

GHOSH, MAHIM CHANDRA, M.A. (Cantab.), B.A. (Calc.), late Indian C.S. (b. 20th Jan., 1880).—Retd., Jan., 1940.†

GHOSH, NAGENDRA NATH, late Inc. Tax. Dept. (b. 8th July, 1885).—Retd., 8th July, 1940.†

GHOSH, NIBARAN CHANDRA, B.A., O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (gen. mgr. E.I. Rly) (b. 21st Dec., 1890).—Apptd. to the service, 20th March, 1913; asst. supt., E.I. rly., Jan., 1916; supt., Apr., 1928; offg. divl. supt., E.I. rly., variously, from Apr., 1934; confd., March, 1938; ch. operating supt., Aug., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; genl. mgr., E.I. Rly. (confd.), Apr., 1945.

GHOSH, PRAKRITY, KUMAR, D.Sc., Geol. Survey of India, (b. 26th Oct., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt. (geologist), 4th Sept., 1929.

GHOSH, SACHINDRA MOHAN, B.A., Indian Police (b. 1st Apr., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 1st March, 1935; confd., March, 1937.

GHOSH, SHOTTYENDRA KUMAR, Indian C.S. (contr. of emigrant labour, govt. of India) (b. 21st May, 1896).—Educ. at Scottish Churches Coll., Calcutta; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1927; addl. dist. mag., Apr., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., Apr., 1928; confd., Dec., 1933; contr. of emigrant labour, govt. of India, Nov., 1937.

GHOSH, SUDHIR KUMAR, M.Sc., United Provs. educ. service, Cl. I (b. Feb., 1895).—non-gaz. appt. 1919-21; asst. master interm. coll., educ. serv., Cl. II, July, 1921; princpl. jubilee interm. coll., Lucknow; educ. serv., Cl. I, July 1936, inspr. of sch., Jan., 1941.

GHOSH, SUKHENDRA NATH, B.A., B.Sc. (Glas.), M.Inst.C.E., F.R.San.I., M.I.E. (Ind.), Public Health Dept., Bengal (b. 16th Oct., 1888).—Apptd. asst. sanitary engineer, Bengal, Nov., 1918; executive engineer, public health dept., 1921; chief engr., public health dept., 1939.

GHOSH, TARAK NATH, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 2nd Oct., 1914).—Apptd. on probn., Jan., 1937; confd., 15th Dec., 1937.

GHOSHAL, UMESH KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 25th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Hindu Sch., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1938; emptl. advr. to govt., June, 1941; asst. contr. A.R.P., Dec., 1942; dep. ditto, Apr., 1944; regl. contr. of procurement, Bakarganj.

GHULAM ABBAS, O.B.E., B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 17th May, 1902).—Joined the service, cl. II, March, 1925; asst. mil. acct.-genl., Apr., 1933; secty. Quetta Rec. Cttee., Nov., 1935; dep. mil. acct.-genl. (junr.), Oct., 1936; addl. dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1939; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Aug., 1941 to May, 1945; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

GHULAM AHMAD, MIAN, O.B.E., M.A.,

Indian Police (dep. dir., intell. bureau) (b. 12th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 29th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., June, 1928; and again, Oct., 1932; confid., Apl., 1934; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., variously from Feb., 1934; asst. dir., intell. bureau, home dept., June, 1939 (confid., Oct., 1939), to Jan., 1942; dep. dir., ditto, Feb., 1942; O.B.E., June, 1943.

GHULAM AHMED, MADANI, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 2nd March, 1913).—Educ. at Faiz-I-am High Sch., Meerut, and Aligarh Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

GHULAM, FARUQUE KHAN BAHADUR, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (b. 7th Oct., 1899).—Joined 8th Dec., 1921; transpt. mgr., B.N. Rly., Sept., 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; ch. operg. supt., E.I. Rly., April, 1946.

GHULAM, HUSSAIN KADIRDAD KHAN AGHA (Sind) (b. 8th Aug., 1895).—Joined 31st May, 1918; dept. supt., salt and excise, lower Sind, May, 1924; offg. var. from Apl., 1927; dep. collr. (2nd grade) Apl., 1931; ditto (1st grade), Aug., 1941.

GHULAM MUHAMMAD, SIR, C.I.E., M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (finance officer, communications) (b. 29th Aug., 1895).—Joined the service, 7th March, 1920; asst. audit officer, O. & R. rly., Jan., 1921; govt. exr. of accts., B. & N.W. rlys., Jan., 1925; services placed at disposal of rly. board, Nov., 1925; on foreign service under Bhopal state, June, 1932, to Feb., 1934; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., March, 1934; offg. dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., finance, May, 1934; confid., Jan., 1935; offg. finance officer, posts and tels., July, 1936; confid., Apl., 1937; fin. offer., communications, Oct., 1937; ch. contr. of stores, March, 1940; contr.-genl. of purchase, Sept., 1940; addl. sec. to dept. of supply, March, 1941; C.I.E., June, 1941; on for. service, Hyderabad, May, 1942; Kt., June, 1946.

GHULAM, RASULKHAN GULDAR, KHAN BAHADUR, (Sind) (b. 13th March, 1896).—Temp. service in Bombay medl. serv., from Oct., 1924; supt. and medl. offr. of prisons, Sind, Nov., 1930; inspr.-genl. of prisons Apl., 1946.

GHULUM, RASUL SHER KHAN KEHAR, Khan Sahib, B.A., (Sind) (b. 10th July, 1899).—Joined the serv., 15th Apl., 1925; on for. serv. as returning offr. Shikarpur, Oct., 1936 to Mar., 1937; dep. collr., Oct., 1944; dep. sec. to govt., home pol. and misc. dept., May, 1946.

GIBBS, WALTER FELIX, Indian Police (b. 12th Apl., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Nov., 1938; confid., Dec., 1940.

GIBLING, MORLEY THEODORE, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Sind) (b. 12th March, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; confid., Oct., 1931; secty., cent. bd. of irrign., Simla, May, 1935, to Apl., 1939; suptg. engr. (confid.), Dec., 1943.

GIBSON, SIR EDMUND CURREY, K.C.I.E., M.A. Indian C.S., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 6th July, 1886).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., London, St. John's Coll., Oxford and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; employed temp. under govt. of India; for, and pol. dept., from June, 1915; tutor and guardian to H.H. the Maharaja of Bharatpur; asst. pol.

agent, E. Rajputana States (in addn.), Sept., 1917; addl. asst. master, Mayo Coll., March, 1918; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Apl., 1918; dep. commr., Oct., 1923; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Jan., 1924; pol. agent, Eastern States of Rajputana, March, 1925; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Apl., 1927; offg. resdt. and agent to govr.-genl., Eastern States, Apl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1933; offg. resdt., Gwalior, Nov., 1934; resdt., cl. II, Aug., 1935; resdt. for. States of W.I., Oct., 1937; confid. as resdt., cl. I, May, 1939; K.C.I.E., June, 1941; empld. (temp.) as China relations offer., Calcutta; retd., Mar., 1945.

GIBSON, JOHN CARRINGTON, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 8th March, 1912; dist. loco. supt., Apl., 1918; supt., E.I. rly., July, 1925; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Feb., 1934; confid., Jan., 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), E.I. rly., Jan., 1936; divl. supt., Oct., 1938; retd., 5th Dec., 1943.

GIBSON, JOHN PERCIVAL, M.A., Burma Office (actg. asst. sec.) (b. 2nd June 1898).—Educ. at Royal Academ. Inst., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. asst. princpl., mil. dept., 19th Oct., 1921; transfd. to commerce and revenue dept., June, 1923, and to pol. dept., Apl., 1924; resident clerk, Oct., 1924; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1927; pte. secty. to perm. under-sec. of state, Dec., 1928; princpl., Apl., 1930; actg. asst. sec., July, 1941.

GIBSON, RAYMOND EVELYN, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S., (b. 10th Oct., 1878).—Retd., Dec., 1937.†

GIDLOW, JOHN, Burma Police, (Cl. I) (b. 18th July, 1920).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

GIDNEY, SIR CLAUDE HENRY, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Nov., 1887).—Retd., 27th Sept., 1944.†

GIDWANI, VISHINO LAKHIMAL, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 4th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at N.J. High Sch., Karachi, and Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

GILBERT, CHARLES FREDERICK, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 25th Oct., 1885).—Retd., Oct., 1940.†

GILBERT, LEONARD BURGES, C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 20th July, 1887).—Retd., 20th July, 1942.†

GILCHRIST, ROBERT NIVEN, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 13th Apl., 1888).—Apptd. prof., Presy. Coll., Calcutta, 1911; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, 1914; princpl., Krishnagar Coll., 1916; contr., labour bureau, govt. of India, 1921; labour intellig. offr., Bengal, and dep. sec., commerce dept., 1922; jt. secty. to govt., commerce and marine depts., July, 1930; labour commr., Bengal, March, 1932; C.I.E., June, 1934; reforms commr., Oct., 1934; retd., June, 1941; served in Burma Office from Feb., 1940; posted to India Office (pol. dept.); author of 'Indian Nationality,' 'Principles of Political Science,' 'Conciliation and Arbitration.'

GILDEA, ERIC PERCIVAL, Indian State Rlys. (late N.W. Rly.) (b. 9th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Apl., 1916; temp. asst. loco. supt., Sept., 1916, to Oct., 1919; confid., Nov., 1919; asst. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; offg. variously as divl. rolling

stock officer from Jan., 1925; confd., Nov., 1927; retd., 7th May, 1944.

GILL, FRANK FREDERICK PERCY, late Indian Police (Orissa) (b. 2nd April, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 18th Nov., 1925; confd., Sept., 1929; asst. supt., India home intell., Apl., 1931 to Nov., 1934; p.a. to dir. intell. bureau, to Feb., 1945; transf. to Orissa, Oct., 1937 as asst. inspr.-genl. and supt. C.I.D., to Sept., 1939; King's police medal, June, 1943; on dept. to War Office (hon. Lt.-Col.) March, 1945 to Feb., 1946; supt. (confd.) March, 1938; retd., May, 1946.

GILL, GURDIAL SINGH, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.B., Ch.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail dept., Madras (insp.-gen. of prisons) (b. 21st Mar., 1896).—Date of first commn., 18th Aug., 1926; offg. supt. jail dept., Nov., 1931; confd., Apl., 1932; inspr.-genl. of prisons and ch. inspr. certified schools, June, 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; C.I.E., June, 1946.

GILL, HARRY ANDERSON CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (b. 12th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at Bradfield and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 10th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1935; under-secty., finance dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1937; dep. ditto, Apl., 1938, to Oct., 1939; collr. of cent. excise, Oct., 1942.

GILLAN, SIR GEORGE VAN BAERLE, K.C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 3rd Sept., 1890).—First commn. dated 19th Jan., 1912; in milty. employ to 31st Dec., 1918; under-sec. to govt. of India, for and pol. dept., March, 1924; offg. dep. sec. to ditto, Nov., 1924; H.B.M.'s consul.-genl., Khasgar, July, 1925; 1st asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Nov., 1928; pol. agent, Gilgit, Sept., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; secty. to A.G.G., Punjab states, Aug., 1935; resdt., W. Rajputana states, Apl., 1937; resdt., Jaipur (addtl. charge), Dec., 1937; resdt., Western Rajputana states, March, 1938; resdt. in Mewar and pol. agent, Southern Rajputana states (addl. charge), Apl., 1938; resdt. for Baroda and Gujarat states, Apr., 1939; resdt. at Gwalior and for the states of Rampur and Benares, Feb., 1940; K.C.I.E., June, 1944; confd. as resdt., cl. I, and resdt. for Rajputana, Jan., 1944.

GILLESPIE, JAMES ROSS, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 5th Aug., 1914).—Educ. at Dunfermline High Sch., St. Andrew's Univ., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 9th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; a.d.c. to the govr., Aug., 1940; served under cent. govt. from 1942 to 1944; dep. commr. and dist. mag., June, 1944; spec. offr. poll. and appt. dept., Apl., 1945; dist. mag. and collr., Mar., 1946.

GILLIAT, ALGERNON EARLE, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 7th Dec., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec., rev. sec.'s dept., Apl., 1913; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Apl., 1916; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1921; offg. sec. to govt., fin. and rev. dep., May, 1923; dep. commr., Sept., 1924; ditto and addl. sec., fin. and rev. dept., Dec., 1924; sec. to govt. fin. dept., May, 1927; C.I.E., June,

1928; offg. commr., May, 1932; confd., Jan., 1936; confd. fin. commr., Feb., 1941; retd., Dec., 1943.

GILLIES, PETER, L.D.S. (b. 22nd May, 1897).—Apptd. dental surg. and supt., Punjab dental hosp., Lahore, on probn., 19th March, 1930; confd., Sept., 1932.

GILLIGAN, WILLIAM BOYD, late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Apl., 1893).—Retd., June, 1940.†

GILMORE, EDWARD FITZGERALD GELSTON, B.Sc. (Hons.), M.I.Mech.E. (b. 25th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 12th Feb., 1924, as physicist, Govt. Test House, Alipore; supt., Govt. Test House (prov.), Nov., 1934; subst., March, 1938; dep. dir. of scientific and industrial research, June, 1940.

GIMSON, CHRISTOPHER, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 24th Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Oundle and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. commr.; transf. to Assam, Apl., 1912; settl. offr., July, 1914; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Feb., 1918, to Jan., 1919; presdt., Manipur State Durbar, Feb., 1919, to Nov., 1921; offg. dep. commr., March, 1923; confd., Apl., 1928; pol. agent, Manipur, Feb., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

GITA RAM, GARG, RAI BAHADUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 8th Sept., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; suptg. engr. (permt.), May, 1943; on spec. duty, Punjab, irrign. sectt., Jan., 1944; to March, 1945.

GLADDING, DONALD, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Aug., 1888).—Retd., July, 1939.†

GLAISBY, ARTHUR GEOFFREY TINDALL, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. contr. stores G.I.P. Rly) (b. 12th Apl., 1890).—Joined the serv., 12th Sept., 1914; asst. engr., G.I.P. Rly., Apl., 1917; resdt. engr., Sept., 1921; liaison officer, G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1934; offg. dep. contr. of stores, Nov., 1935; offg. dist. contr. of stores, Mar., 1936; dep. agent, styled dep. gen. mgr. (works), May, 1937; dep. contr. stores, G.I.P. Rly., June, 1939; O.B.E., June, 1946.

GLANCY, SIR BERTRAND JAMES, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S., retd. late Ind. Political Service (Punjab) (b. 31st Dec., 1882).—Educ. at Monmouth Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd., after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and pol. asst.; temp. under-sec. to govt. of India, for, dept., March to Nov., 1913; asst. resdt., Mewar, May, 1914; 1st asst. to the agent to the govr.-genl. in Rajputana, March, 1915; 1st asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Dec., 1918; dep. sec. to govt. of India, pol. dept. Oct., 1921, and again, Apl., 1927; employed under Kashmir durbar from Nov., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; offg. pol. secty. to govt. of India, June, 1928; offg. agent to govr.-genl., Punjab states, Apl., 1929; in foreign service as president, council of state, Jaipur, Oct., 1929; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Punjab states, Apl., 1932; offg. pol. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., July, 1932, and again, July, 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; resdt. and A.G.G. in C.I., June, 1933; member, council of state, variously from July, 1933; secty., Chamber of Princes, Oct., 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1935; secty. to H.E. the Crown Repre-

sentative, Apl., 1937; political adviser to H.E. the Crown Representative (temp.), Dec., 1938; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1940; govr. of the Punjab, Apl., 1941; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1946; retd., Apl., 1946; granted unrestricted permission by H.M. the King to wear decoration of Yun Hin, special cravat conferred by the president of the Chinese republic, Sept., 1946.

GLANVILLE, STEPHEN DE, Burma Front Service, (b. 26th Dec., 1907).—Apptd. Nov., 1929; civ. aff. serv., Feb., 1943 to Oct., 1945; dep. commr., Oct., 1945.

GLASS, GEORGE WILLIAM, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 29th Dec., 1887).—Held non-gazetted appt. from Aug., 1906, to Dec., 1918; offg. asst. accts. officer, posts and tels., Feb., 1919; confd., Dec., 1919; dep. acct. genl., ditto, Aug., 1927; examr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Feb., 1935; dep. acct. genl., Bengal, Apl., 1935; attd. officer, office of the dep. acct. genl., posts and tels., Madras, Feb., 1937; on for service as comptr. of posts and tel. accts., Burma, Apl., 1937; comptr., Assam., Nov., 1939.

GLASS, LESLIE CHARLES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 28th May, 1911).—Educ. at Bradfield, Trinity Coll., Oxford; and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Dec., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. warden, Burma oilfields, Sept., 1937; asst. settlement offr., Feb., 1939; offg. settlement offr., Sept. 1940; Burma propaganda offcr. (Delhi), 1942; civ. aff. serv., Aug., 1944; press and publicity offcr., Nov., 1945; retd., Dec., 1945.

GLEDHILL, ALAN, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 26th Oct., 1895).—Educ. at Rugby Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; offg. ditto, March, 1928; confd., June, 1928; addl. dist. mag., Assam, July, 1942; actg. judge, h. ct., Rangoon, Oct., 1945.

GLENN, JOHN ARTHUR GOULD, Indian Police (b. 31st July, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Dec., 1937, confd.; a.d.c. to govr., Apl., 1941 to 1944.

GLENNIE, EDWARD AUBREY, C.I.E., D.S.O., Brig., R.E., Survey of India (dir.) (b. 18th July, 1889).—First commn., 23rd July, 1910; asst. supt. (proby.), 20th Dec., 1919; confd., Dec., 1921; supt., May, 1928; offg. dir., Nov., 1936; confd., March, 1940; C.I.E., June, 1940.

GLOVER, SIR HAROLD MATTHEW, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Punjab) (b. 29th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1908, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Apl., 1912; offg. consvr., Jan., 1925; confd., Nov., 1929; served in United Provs., Oct., 1932; posted as consvr., Punjab, June, 1933; Kt., Jan., 1942; retd., 30th Jan., 1944.

GODBOLE, YESHWANT ANANT, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (b. 4th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Bombay University and at Cambridge; apptd. after examination of 1912; arrived, 24th Nov., 1913, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Apl., 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; onfd., Dec., 1928; regr. of co-op. societies, Bihar and Orissa, March, 1934; offg. dir. of industries in addn., March, 1935; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bihar, Nov., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; commr., Apl., 1941; advr. to govr.,

March, 1944; C.S.I., June, 1944; memb. bd. of rev., Apl., 1946; sec. to govr.-genl. (public), July, 1946.

GODFREY, REGINALD WILLIAM, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt. Assam) (b. 10th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. 15th March, 1921; confd., March, 1923; offg. supt., Apl., 1930; confd., June, 1930; pol. offcr., Sadiya fron. tract, Apl., 1938; sec. to govr., Oct., 1943; O.B.E., June, 1946.

GODWIN, CHARLES, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, store depot, 2nd May, 1904; transf. to office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; actg. higher exec. offr., Sept., 1939.

GODWIN, WALTER WILLIAM, Indian Police (dep. commr.) (Bombay) (b. 16th Feb., 1892).—Served in Bombay City Police dept., Mar., 1921; to May, 1931; prom. supt. Indian police, May, 1931; awarded I.P. medal, July, 1940; offg. dep. commr., Apl., to Sept., 1941; dep. commr., Sept., 1941.

GOFF, GEORGE HERBERT, late Indian State Ry's (b. 25th Apl., 1885).—Retd., Apl., 1940.

GOIL, DWARKA PRASAD, M.B., F.R.C.S. (Edin.), K.H.P., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S., retd. (b. 1st Aug., 1879).—Retd., March, 1938.*

GOKHALE, BHALCHANDRA KRISHNA, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., India C.S. (sec., govt. of India works etc., dept.) (b. 23rd July, 1882).—Educ. at Bombay and Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived 15th Dec., 1915; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; ditto and asst. settlmt. officer, Nov., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1927; secty. to govt. educn. and dev. dept., March, 1929; mag. and collr. (provl. sub.), May, 1930; confd., Dec., 1931; secty., fin. dept., Bihar, May, 1939; commr. of excise, etc., Dec., 1942, to Apl., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; adv. to the govt. of Orissa, Sept., 1944; C.S.I., Jan., 1946; administrator, Damodar project., May, 1946; sec., govt. of India works, etc., dept., July, 1946.

GOKHALE, VINAYAK GOPAL, L.Ag. (Bom.), late Indian Agric. Service (b. 10th March, 1883).—Retd., March, 1938.*

GOLDSWORTHY, FRANK TRUSCOTT, India Office (actg. hr. exec. off. acct.-genl's dept.) (b. 11th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Bristol Gr. Sch., apptd. 2nd class clerk, 25th Feb., 1913; on mill. service from May, 1915, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; lent to Admiralty, June, 1940; actg. hr. exec. offcr. Dec., 1945.

GOLDSWORTHY, LEONARD JAMES, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 12th Dec., 1890).—Retd., Feb., 1938.*

GOLLAN, DONALD WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 16th Sept. 1886).—Retd., Sept., 1941.

GOODALL, J. W. D., Capt., M.D., Ch.B. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (Edin.), I.M.S. (civil surgeon, Bengal) (b. 10th Dec., 1909).—Date of first commn., Feb., 1934; civil surgeon, Sept., 1938.

GOODCHILD, WILLIAM BADEN, late India Office (b. 19th May, 1900).—Retd., Jan., 1945.

GOODERSON, RICHARD NORMAN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 3rd March, 1915).—Educ. at Northampton Town and County Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; a.r.p. offr., Apl., 1942; judge, small cause ct., Simla, Feb., 1944; retd., 1946.

GOODMAN, REGINALD TERENCE WYNNE, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., N.W.F.P.) (b. 26th July, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 20th Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., May, 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; dep. transp. contr., Sept., 1943.

GOODSIR, DAVID BRODIE, Burma Police Cl. I (b. 28th Mar., 1926).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

GOODWYN, HAROLD PETER, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (under-sec., home dept., Bengal) (b. 19th Jul., 1913).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Peterhouse, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1938; special officer, home dept., Aug., 1939; under-secty., home dept., Sept., 1939; confd., May, 1940; dep. sec. to govr., Feb., 1944; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

GOPAL, NAND (see Nand Gopal).

GOPAL, RAM (see Ram Gopal).

GOPALAKRISHNA RAO, BELTHANGADI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st June, 1909); educ. at Board High Sch., Puttur, University of Madras, and St. John's Coll., Cimbridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1935.

GOPALAKRISHNAN, PANANGATTI ANANTHANARAYANAIYER, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. United Provs.) (b. 20th Dec., 1909).—Educ. at Rajah's High Sch., Kollenbode, Sth. Malabar, Madras Univ., Univsty. Coll., London and the Sch. of Oriental Stud., London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1935; O.B.E., June, 1945.

GOPALASWAMI RAMASWAMI AYYANGAR, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (jt. sec., govt. of India, agric. dept., Madras) (b. 18th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Emmanuel Coll. Camb.; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927; sub. collr. and jt. mag., March, 1930; jt. sec., board of rev., Madras, Feb., 1939; sec. to the National Defence Council, Aug., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; govt. of India, food dept., July, 1944; jt. sec. govt. of India, agric. dept., Sept., 1945.

GORDON, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., and secty. to govt., p.w.d., Sind) (b. 15th June, 1880).—Joined as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; suptg. engr. (subst. temp.), Jan., 1927; ditto (provl. perm.), Dec., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935; ch. engr. and secty. to govt., p.w.d., Sind, May, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

GORDON, SIR ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 14th Apr., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 5th Dec., 1907; supt., Oct., 1915; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Nov., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. commr. of police, Calcutta, Apr., 1935; officer, Order of St. John, June, 1941; Kt., Jan., 1943; retd., 14th Apr., 1943; re-employed to Apr., 1946.

GORDON, CHARLES HOWARD, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd Dec., 1916).—Educ. at Oundle and Trinity Hall Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 11th Oct., 1938; confd., 7th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1941;

pte. sec. to govr., Jan., 1941; addl. dist. mag., Apr., 1944; mag. and collr., Aug., 1944.

GORDON, SIR EYRE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Feb., 1884).—Retd. March, 1943.†

GORDON, GERALD WATSON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Assam) (b. 31st Oct., 1902).—Joined the service, 27th Oct., 1925, as asst. exec. engr.; under-secty., p.w.d., Assam, June, 1937.

GORDON, JAMES, M.B.E., India Office (actg. princpl., E. and O. dept.) (b. 27th Dec., 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, 3rd Oct., 1921; M.B.E., June, 1934; actg. exec. officer, Nov., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935; staff officer for services questions, July, 1939; actg. supt., tel. and mails branch., Aug., 1943; actg. princpl., econ. and overseas dept., July, 1944.

GORDON, JOHN DE LA HAY, C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 30th March, 1887).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; in mil. employ till Dec., 1911, when he was apptd. asst. pol. agent (on probn.); asst. resdt., Aden, Apr., 1913; mil. govr., Basra, Sept., 1916, to Sept., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1918; pol. agent, Eastern Kathiawar states, Jan., 1925; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Dec., 1927; 1st asst. resdt., Aden, Nov., 1931; pol. agent, eastern Kathiawar agency, March, 1932; offg. A.G.G., Deccan states, Aug., 1935; offg. A.G.G., states of W.I., June, 1936; dep. A.G.G., states of W.I., Nov., 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; offg. resdt., Mysore and chief commr., Coorg, Dec., 1937; confd. as resdt., cl. I., and resdt. in Mysore, Jan., 1939; C.S.I., June, 1941; retd., Apr., 1942.

GORDON, RONALD EAGLESON, M.C., Lieut.-Col., R.E., Indian State Rlys. (govt. inspr. of rlys.) (b. 17th Jan., 1889).—First commn. dated 23rd Dec., 1900; asst. engr. March, 1921; exec. engr., June, 1921; offg. divl. supt. (senr.), N.W. rly., Dec., 1932; and again, March, 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), Nov., 1936; provl. perm. dep. ch. engr., May, 1939; transfld. to posts and air dept. as govt. inspr. of rlys., May, 1941.

GORMAN, JAMES HAMILTON, M.B., B.Ch., D.P.H., Maj., I.M.S. (b. 29th Jan., 1904).—First commn. dated 4th Feb., 1930; in mil. employ to 6th Feb., 1939, when he joined the Port Health Dept.; port health officer, Calcutta, March, 1939; in temp. mil. employ from Sept., 1941.

GORRIE, REV. LESLIE MANIFOLD, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 21st March, 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 18th Apr., 1919; junr. chaplain, Apr., 1921; senr. chaplain, Apr., 1929.

GORRIE, ROBERT MACLAGAN, D.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 26th June, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Sept., 1930; served under def. dept., Nov., 1939; timber advr. to contr. genl. of inspn.

GORWALA, ASTAD DINSHAW, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., govt. of Bombay) (b. 7th Dec., 1900).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Cambridge Univ. (Non-Coll.); joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 5th Dec., 1924, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1928; offg. asst. rev. officer, Lloyd barr. and C.S., Nov., 1931; dep. secty., govt. of India, communications dept., Nov., 1937, and again, July, 1938; on spec. duty, Sind, May, 1938; offg. secty., govt. of

India, communications dept., March, 1939; dep. secty., govt. of India, communications dept., May, 1939; sec. to govt., Bombay (fin.), Oct., 1941; supply commr. in addn., Sept., 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; sec., govt. of Bombay fin. dept., and supply commr., Aug., 1944; on depts. to U.K. and U.S.A., Feb. to Mar., 1946; sec., govt. of Bombay civil supply, and supply commr., April, 1946.

GOSCHEN, VISCOUNT, Rt. Hon. GEORGE JOACHIM GOSCHEN, OF HAWKHURST, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., C.B.E.—Govr. of Madras, 14th Apr., 1924, to 11th Nov., 1929; actg. govr.-genl. of India, June to Oct., 1929; P.C., Feb., 1930.*

GOSS, EDWARD SLADE, M.C., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 25th Sept., 1883).—Ret'd., Sept., 1938.*

GOSSET, HUGH HENRY EYRE, M.C., Lieut.-Col., R.E., ret'd., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 3rd Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1912; asst. engr., July, 1913; on milty. duty from 21st Oct., 1914; exec. engr., Jan., 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr., Sept., 1932; provl. permnt., Apr., 1938; govt. inspr. of rlys., May, 1941; ret'd. 3rd Feb., 1944.

GOSWAMI, JITENDRA NATH, (b. 21st July, 1901).—Apptd. project offr. (elec.) central tech. power bd. July, 1945.

GOSWAMI, UPENDRA LAL, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 5th Jan., 1912).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ.; joined the service, 24th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.; depy. regr., genl. dept., high ct., Rangoon, Apr., 1941; jt. contr. of rationing, Hoogly, Nov., 1945.

GOULD, ALFRED JOHN, B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Burma) (b. 28th Feb., 1900).—Ret'd., Jan., 1939.*

GOULD, SIR BASIL JOHN, C.M.G., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 29th Dec., 1883).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 29th Nov., 1907, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and pol. asst.; asst. to agent to govr.-genl. in Central India, May, 1909; asst. sec. to govt. of India for dept., June, 1910; special duty in for. dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1910; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., Oct., 1910; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse, May, 1912; offg. pol. offr., Sikkim, Oct., 1913; asst. commr., Nowshera, Oct., 1914; ditto, and asst. commr., Charsada, May, 1915; dist. judge, Peshawar, Sept., 1916; asst. commr., Mardan, May, 1917; asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, Sept., 1917; on spl. duty under orders of H.B.M.'s Consul, Seistan, from May, 1918; H.B.M.'s Consul for Seistan and Kain, Nov., 1918; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, Apr., 1920; C.I.E., Sept., 1921; first asst. to resident in Kashmir, Dec., 1925; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, June, 1926; and again, June, 1928; chargé d'affaires, Kabul, March, 1927; C.M.G., June, 1929; pol. agent, Kurram, Nov., 1929; ditto, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Jan., 1931; offg. resdt., Waziristan, Oct., 1931; rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1933; and again, Dec., 1934; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Baluchistan, July, 1934; pol. officer, Sikkim, Dec., 1935; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse (in addn.) Aug., 1944; Kl., June, 1941.

GOVIND NARAYAN PAREEKH, B.A., Indian Police (b. 10th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 21st Jan.,

1929; supt. (confd.) June, 1942; under suspension, from July, 1945.

GOVINDARAJA AYYANGAR, NARASIMHA, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.E., late Indian Serv., of Engineers (Madras) (b. 10th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as apprentice engineer, 16th June, 1914; asst. engr., July, 1917; exec. engr., June, 1924; dep. ch. engr., bldgs. and roads, Dec., 1935; suptg. engr., Dec., 1940; ch. engr., March, 1944; on depts. to U.K., Mar., 1946; ret'd., March, 1946; re-empld., as sp. ch. engr., June, 1946.

GOVIND DINANATH MADGAONKAR (see Madgaonkar, G. D.).

GOW, PETER FLEMING, Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (b. 28th June, 1885).—Ret'd., June, 1940.

GOYDER, CECIL WILLIAM, C.B.E., B.Sc., A.C.G.I., All-India Radio (ch. engr.) (b. 1st Apr., 1905).—Joined the serv. as ch. engr., 20th Aug., 1936; C.B.E., Jan., 1946.

GRABHAM, REGINALD GEORGE DENIS, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 23rd Dec., 1922; offg. supt., March, 1926; asst. commr., Port Blair, Dec., 1930; supt., May, 1935; addtl. asst. to I.G., Feb., 1937; supt. of dist. pol., Ajmer-Merwara, Aug., 1940.

GRACE, OLIVER GILBERT, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Police (N.W.F.P.) (b. 10th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1920; asst. supt., Feb., 1922; supt., June, 1925; dist. officer, frontier constabulary, N.W.F.P., Jan., 1929; commdt., ditto, Dec., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1934; commdt. frontier constab., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

GRACIE, CLEMENT FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 29th June, 1907).—Educ. at King Edward VII Sch., Sheffield, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; secty. to finl. commr., Burma, July, 1936, to Dec., 1936; on milty. service, Nov., 1940, to Oct., 1945; dep. commr., Oct., 1945; on mil. service again, Jan., 1946; dep. commr., 1946.

GRAHAM, CYRIL GEORGE, late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 28th May, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st October, 1909; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Nov., 1914; exec. engr., May, 1932; offg. dep. agent, G.I.P. rly., Apr., 1935; offg. dep. ch. engr., maintenance, G.I.P. rly., May, 1936; and again, Sept., 1937; provl. permnt., May, 1939; confd., March, 1940; ch. engr., Oct., 1943; ret'd., 1945.

GRAHAM, FREDERICK ERNEST, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 29th Apr., 1892).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 8th Sept., 1914; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Sept., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apr., 1922; higher clerical officer, Apr., 1926; actg. higher exec. offr., Jan., 1940.

GRAHAM, GEORGE KENNETH, Capt., I.M.S. (b. 30th Apr., 1907).—First commn., 3rd Feb., 1931; in mil. employ to 4th Nov., 1934; agency surgn., N. Waziristan, Nov., 1934; medl. officer, Lady Reading Hosp., Peshawar, March, 1938; in temp. milty. employ, March, 1940.

GRAHAM, HAROLD, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Apr., 1889).—Ret'd., Oct., 1942.†

GRAHAM, HAROLD JOHN, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 1st June, 1886).—Ret'd., March, 1941.†

GRAHAM, HUBERT, late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 7th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Manchester Gr.Sc. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as an asst. mag. and collr.; offg. addl. under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, political dept., Apl., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1923; confd., March, 1931; asst. commr., inc. tax, Bengal, Jan., 1927; commr. of inc. tax, B. & O. (temp.), March, 1929; secty., educn., forest and excise depts., Bengal (confd.), Apl., 1939; retd., April, 1946.

GRAHAM, SIR JAMES DRUMMOND, Kt., C.B., C.I.E., M.B., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S., retd. (b. 21st Jan., 1875).—Retd., Feb., 1934.*

GRAHAM, SIR LANCELOT, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th Apl., 1880).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and pol. agent; asst. judge and sess. judge, from Jan., 1909; asst. membr., legal affairs, and sec. for making laws and regns., March, 1911; judge and pol. agent and judl. asst. in Kathiawar from Aug., 1913; addl. jt. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Nov., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; actg. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Apl., 1923; confd., Dec., 1924; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1930; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1936; govr. of Sind, Apl., 1936 to Apl., 1941.

GRAHAM-SMITH, CYRIL, O.B.E., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 2nd June, 1882).—Retd., Apl., 1938.*

GRANT, CHARLES FREDERICK, C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Sept., 1878).—Retd., Apl., 1939.†

GRANT, JOHN LESLIE, C.I.E., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Serv. of Engrs. (Sind) (b. 12th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; in milty. employ from 1st Aug., 1916, to 18th March, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1942; confd. as jt. ch. engr. and jt. sec., govt. Sind, Dec., 1943; ch. engr. and sec. to govt., P.W.D., March, 1945; retd., 1945.

GRANT, JOHN WILLIAM, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agricl. Service (b. 18th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, on 14th Nov., 1922.

GRANT, LACHLAN COLVILLE PATRICK MAC-ALPINE, O.B.E., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (dir. of tels.) (b. 12th Aug., 1902).—Arrived in India, 27th March, 1925; joined the service as asst. div. engr., 30th March, 1925; div. engr., Sept., 1927; p.a. to chief engr., July, 1935; asst. dep. dir.-genl. (engrg.), June, 1938; confd. as dir. tels., May, 1944.

GRASSBY, CYRIL GEORGE, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 15th March, 1899).—Retd., May, 1940.†

GRAY, JAMES EDWARD, Burma Front. Serv., (b. 23rd Dec., 1923).—Apptd. on probn. after asst. seln. Aug., 1946.

GRAY, JAMES ERNEST, M.B., B.Ch., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., (b. 30th Aug., 1899).—First commn., 8th Jan., 1924; in mil. employ. to Jan., 1934; confd. in civil, Feb., 1936; offr. on genl. duty, St. George's Hosp., Bombay, Feb., 1938; civil surg., Sind, Apl., 1938; inspr.-genl., civil hospitals, Nov., 1942.

GREAVES, SIR WILLIAM EWART, Kt., Barr.—Judge, high ct., Calcutta, 23rd Nov., 1914; retd., Dec., 1927.*

GREEN, SIR ALAN MICHAEL, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 11th Apl., 1885).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

GREEN, ARTHUR EATOUGH, Capt., C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Serv. of Engrs. (chief engr., Bihar) (b. 16th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under-sec., public works dept., Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1926; O.B.E., May, 1937; suptg. engr., confd., Apl., 1938; chief engr., May, 1942; C.I.E., June, 1946.

GREEN, CHARLES WILLIAM TANDY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Bengal) (b. 21st May, 1887).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1909, as asst. engr.; I.A.R.O. from Apl., 1915, to Dec., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1926; pers. asst. to ch. engr. and secty., P.W. dept., March, 1928; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1929; confd., Dec., 1931; awarded Empire Gallantry Medal, June, 1934; ch. engr., Oct., 1940.

GREEN, JOHN MOWBRAY, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 23rd Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 31st Jan., 1921; asst. supt., Sept., 1922; offg. supt., Apl., 1925; confd., Oct., 1930; comdt. of police, Aden, May, 1934; reverted, Oct., 1934; commr. of police, C. and M. Stn., Bangalore, June, 1939.

GREEN, LESLIE BENTON, O.B.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), Dept. of Industries, Madras (dir. of industries) (b. 11th June, 1892).—Apptd. offg. asst. dir., 16th Dec., 1915; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; dep. dir., Feb., 1922; dr., June, 1935; O.B.E., June, 1939; served under cent. govt. as tea controller, 1942.

GREENE, HUBERT EUGENE, late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 10th June, 1883).—Retd., June, 1938.*

GREENEP, DONALD PRESTWICH, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 20th Aug., 1901). Retd., Dec., 1941.†

GREENFIELD, HARRY, C.S.I., C.I.E., Imp. Customs Serv., (b. 2nd Oct., 1898).—Joined the Indian Police service as asst. supt. on 27th Dec., 1919; dep. commr., Calcutta, Oct., 1925; asst. collr. impl. customs service, Bombay, 1927; ditto, Calcutta, Feb., 1930; collr. of salt rev., Madras, Nov., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; dir. of inspn., customs and cent. excises, Sept., 1939; member, cent. bd. rev. and jt. sec., fin. dept., May, 1943; C.S.I., June, 1946.

GREENFIELD, SIR HENRY CHALLEN, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Central Provinces) (b. 8th December, 1885).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr., and under-sec. to chief commr., July to Sept., 1913; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1915; commr. (temp.), ditto, Dec., 1916, to Jan., 1917; 2nd asst. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, July, 1918; dep. commr. (provl.), Feb., 1921; confd., Aug., 1923; rev. sec. to govt., Apl., 1927, and again, July, 1928; secty. in settlts. and land records, etc., depts., Jan., 1929; commr. of settlts. and dir., land records, March, 1933; offg. commr., May, 1933; confd. (provl.), July, 1934; subst., May, 1936; C.I.E., June, 1934; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Central Provs., during March, 1937; advr. to govr., Nov., 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; kt., June, 1944; retd., Dec., 1945.

GREENING, LAURENCE EDWIN, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engineers (Bombay)

(b. 19th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., May, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; dep. secty. to govt., P.W.D. (roads and bldgs.), Bombay, March, 1934; supt. engr. (provl. perm.), Apl., 1939; retd., Oct., 1946.

GREENWOOD, EDWARD JOSEPH BERNAL, M.Sc., M.I.E.E., M.I.E. (Ind.) (supt. engr., elec., electricity dept., Madras) (b. 16th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as elec. inspr. to Govt., 25th Feb., 1913; elec. inspr., Jan., 1933; supt. engr., July, 1939.

GREGORY, ALBERT EDWARD, India Office (hr. clerical officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 12th Feb., 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, 16th Apl., 1920; actg. higher ditto, July, 1935; confd., Aug., 1936; actg. hr. exec. offic., milty. dept., July, 1940.

GREGORY, ERIC VERNON, Stationery and Printing dept., (b. 22nd Jan., 1898).—Joined the service 11th Apl., 1927; asst. contr. printing, Oct., 1932; manager of publcns., Feb., 1937; dep. contr. printing, Aug., 1941.

GREGORY, RALPH, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 11th Dec., 1908).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Jan., 1935.

GREGORY, SIR THEODOR EMANUEL GUGENHEIM, D.Sc. (b. 10th Sept., 1890).—Apptd. econ. advisor to the govt. of India, Jan., 1938; kt., June, 1942.

GREGORY, WALTER GASPER, Barr., Judl. Dept., Bengal.—Acted as addl. judge, high ct., from 27th March, 1925, to 27th Aug., 1925, and from 9th Nov., 1925, to 11th March, 1926; offg. judge, high ct., March to Aug., 1926, Nov., 1926, to Aug., 1927, and again from Nov., 1927.

GREGORY MOSES, RALPH, Indian C.S.—Name changed to GREGORY, RALPH (q.v.).

GREGSON, WILLIAM, late Indian Agricl. Service (Burma) (b. 28th July, 1892).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

GREVAL, SHIVA DEVA SINGH, M.D., Ch.B., D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (imp. serologist, Calcutta) (b. 31st Dec., 1892).—First commn. dated 1st March, 1922; in milty. employ prior to 22nd Sept., 1926, when he joined the central research inst., Kasauli; imp. serologist, May, 1943.

GREWAL, BALBIR SINGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr. Punjab) (b. 28th August, 1912).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Ludhiana, the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Dec., 1936, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. offic., Apl., 1938; judge, small cause ct., Simla, Feb., 1942 to Apl., 1942; dep. commr., Apl., 1942; offr. on spl. duty, office of fin. commrs., Nov., to Dec., 1944.

GREY, CHARLES ROBERT, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (div. mech. engr. and works manager, N.W. Rly.) (b. 20th Oct., 1894).—Joined service, Dec., 1910; asst. mech. engr., junr. scale, N.W. rly., July, 1927; div. mech. engr., provl., senr. scale, Jan., 1939; confd., Jan., 1940; M.B.E., Jan., 1942.

GRIEVE, WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educ. Service (b. 21st March, 1885).—Retd., Mar., 1940.†

GRIFFIN, SIR ARTHUR CECIL, K.C.I.E.,

O.B.E., B.Sc., (b. 30th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1911; on mil. duty from 5th Nov., 1914; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. dep. agent, Apl., 1933; divl. supt. (sen.), Nov., 1934; dep. chief engr. (prov. perm.), Feb., 1938; perm., Oct., 1939; secty., rly. bd., May, 1938; genl. manager, N.W. rly., Feb., 1941; ch. commr. rlys. (confd.) Sept., 1944; temp. member govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Aug. to Oct., 1945; K.C.I.E., June, 1946.

GRIFFIN, FREDERICK CHARLES, M.I.C.E., late Public Health Dept., Bengal (b. 20th Feb., 1883).—Retd., 1939.*

GRIFFIN, LANCELOT CECIL LEPEL, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. Indian Polit. Serv. (b. 5th Jan., 1900).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 17th Dec., 1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Naini Tal, March, 1925; jt. mag., Nov., 1925; under-secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in C.I., Nov., 1927; asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Apl., 1929; secty. to A.G.G., Punjab states, March, 1932; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Apl., 1936; addl. dep. secty., pol. dept., Apl., 1937; pol. agent, Orissa States, Oct., 1939; also resdt. at Jaipur, Nov., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; counsellor, Brit. legation, Kabul, Apl., 1942; sec. to crown representative, Jan., 1944; confd., Aug., 1944; C.S.I., Jan., 1946.

GRIFFITH, ARTHUR LAWRENCE, Indian Forest Service (b. 24th May, 1903).—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930; sylviculturist, forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1940.

GRIFFITH, FRANCIS GORDON, O.B.E., late Indian State Railways (b. 23rd Nov., 1889).—Joined the serv., as asst. traffic supt., 1st Apl., 1913; on spl. duty, rly. bd. Mar., 1920; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; in mil. employ from July, 1924 to Mar., 1926; O.B.E., June, 1926; transpn. offr., Aug., 1926; statist. offr., Apl., 1929; divl. supt., senr., July, 1940; dep. c.c.m., provl., Dec., 1940; divl. supt., regl. contr. rly. priorities, Lahore, Apl., 1944; retd., Nov., 1944.

GRIFFITH, SIR RALPH EDWIN HOTCHKIN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd. (b. 4th March, 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

GRIFFITHS, BRYHMER JONES, Maj., B.Sc., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., United Provs. Medl. Serv.—Date of first commn., 4th Feb., 1929; in civil employ as civil surgeon (confd.) 10th Jan., 1940.

GRIFFITHS, CHRISTOPHER HOWARD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th Nov., 1883).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

GRIFFITHS, DAVID THOMAS, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Burma) (b. 10th Oct., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 1st Jan., 1923; offg. dep. consvr., Aug., 1924; confd., Feb., 1927; offg. consvr., July, 1941; sec. to agt. of govt. of Burma in China, Mar., 1942; served in R.I.N. Nov., 1942 to May, 1945; depn. in U.K., May, 1945 to Aug., 1945; civ. aff. serv., Aug. to Dec., 1945; consvr. (confd.) Oct., 1945.

GRIFFITHS, JOHN COLDWELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 30th Apl., 1913).—Educ. at Swansea Gr. Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937; sub. collr. and jt. mag. Mar., 1939; jt. sec. bd. of rev., Aug., 1945 to June, 1946.

GRIFFITHS, WILLIAM THOMAS, Indian

State Rlys. (dep. agent (staff), G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 20th Aug. 1888).—Joined the service, 1st May, 1916; 3rd. asst. secty., May, 1921; offg. publicity supt., Apl., 1923; asst. traffic manager, Oct., 1923; divl. ditto, Feb., 1929; offg. dep. agent, G.I.P. rly., July, 1933; confd., Dec., 1935; dep. traffic manager, provl., Apl., 1940.

GRIGG, SIR PERCY JAMES, K.C.B., K.C.S.I. (b. 16th Dec., 1890).—Member, govt.-genl.'s exec. council, May, 1934, to Apl., 1939.*

GRIGSON, WILFRED VERNON, C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 11th Oct., 1896).—Educ. at St. John's Sch., Leatherhead, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925; and again, March, 1931; confd., Apl., 1934; on foreign service as adminr., Bastar State, March, 1927; aboriginal tribes enq. offr., May, 1940; jt. sec to govt., Sept., 1940; sec. to govr., Apl., 1941; on depn. to H.E.H. the Nizam's govt., March, 1942; C.S.I., Jan., 1945.

GRILLE, SIR FREDERICK LOUIS, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (chief justice, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 20th Feb., 1889).—Educ. at Harrow, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1913, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from Oct., 1917, to Oct., 1919; sub-judge, Central Provs., Oct., 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1920; confd., Dec., 1926; offg. legal remembr. and secty. to govt., legal dept., Oct., 1927; offg. addl. judl. commr., July, 1931; confd., March, 1934; offg. judl. commr., June, 1933; confd., Jan., 1935; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936; kt., May, 1937; apptd. chief justice, Oct., 1943.

GRINDAL, ERIC BERTRAM WOOD, O.B.E., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 25th Oct., 1885).—Dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1942.

GRINDLEY, ARCHIBALD PERCIVAL REGINALD, Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 18th Sept., 1892).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1914; divl. transpn. officr., May, 1935.

GRIST, FREDERIC EDWIN, C.I.E., late India Office (b. 9th Nov., 1883).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch.; apptd. clerk 2nd class, registry and record dept., 23rd Jan., 1902; attached to mil. dept., May to Oct., 1907; transfed. to mil. dept., Nov., 1912; minor staff clerk, March, 1917; staff clerk, June, 1919; principal, Oct., 1921; actg. asst. secty., July, 1931; secty., capitation rate tribunal, Nov., 1932; asst. secty., finl. dept., Dec., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1938, ret'd.

GRUER, HAROLD GEORGE, M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 6th May, 1886).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, Aberdeen Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Central Provs., as asst. commr.; addl. sr. sub-judge, July to Oct., 1915; sub-judge, June, 1917; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), Nov., 1917, to March, 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. addl. judl. commr., June, 1934; and again, Sept., 1935; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936; ret'd., Oct., 1943.

GRUNDY, ERIC EDMUND LEIGH, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 6th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service as principi., technical sch., Jamalpur, 29th Sept., 1924; O.B.E., June, 1941; ret'd., Apl., 1943.

GUBBAY, MOSES MORRECAI SIMEON, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Ret'd., Apl., 1921; apptd. commr. for I.C.S. Family Pension Fund, Oct., 1937.*

GUERITZ, JOHN ELTON FORTESCUE, Capt. I.A., Indian Pol. Serv. (on probn.).—Apptd. asst. pol. agt. Chhattisgarh States, May, 1946.

GUGGALI, GURUSIDDAPPA HOOVAPPA, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 18th Oct., 1897).—Educ. at Fergusson Coll., Poona, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; confd., Oct., 1933.

GUHA, BRAJA KANTA, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 3rd July, 1900).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to govt. of Bengal, fin. commerce and marine depts., March, 1929; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1931; K.-i-H. medal, 1st class, Feb., 1937; dist. and sess. judge, confd., Jan., 1938.

GUHA, SURENDRA KUMAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 4th May, 1894).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Apl., 1923; exec. engr., Jan., 1930; suptg. engr., March, 1942.

GUHA RAY, SAILENDRA NATH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr. Bengal) (b. 14th Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1930; confd., Jan., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1945.

GUHA-THAKURTA, S.C., M.A., Bengal C.S. (b. 8th Sept., 1901).—Apptd. information officer, home dept., govt. of India, May, 1936.

GUILD, ALEXANDER BROWN, Maj., M.B., United Provs. Medl. Serv., (b. 12th Dec., 1901).—Date of first commn., Feb., 1930; civil surg.

GUL MUHAMMAD KHAN, B.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 8th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., on 30th Jan., 1923; offg. supt., Jan., 1931; confd., June, 1934.

GULAM HUSSAIN-RAHIMTULLA KHAI-RAZ (see Khairaz, G. H. R.).

GULRAJANI, RUPCHAND KARAMCHAND, B.A. (judicial dept., Sind) (b. 7th Apl., 1894).—Joined Feb., 1934; sub. judge (Cl. I) (confd.), 1934; offg. var. from Apl., 1936; prov. subst. city mag., Karachi, June, 1946.

GUMBLETON, JOHN GILLUM MAXWELL, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 13th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 17th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confd., Jan., 1934; dep. inspr.-genl., states of W.I., Oct., 1936 to March, 1943; supt. police, Poona, Oct., 1945.

GUMBRILL, ARTHUR, Indian State Rlys. (publicity officer, E.I. Rly.) (b. 15th June, 1887).—Joined the service as publicity officer (temp.), 15th March, 1927; confd., Dec., 1928; offg. central publicity officer, Indian State rlys., Apl., 1933, and again, Apl., 1937.

GUNDEVIA, YEZDEZARD DINSHAW, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 19th June, 1908).—Educ. at Bombay Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London, joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Un. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settl. offr.,

Oct., 1936; addl. dep. sec. to govt. of India, and sec. to rep. of govt. of India and Burma, May, 1945.

GUNDIL, NARAYAN RAMKRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 22nd Feb., 1885).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1913; asst. judge, Dec., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge variously from Dec., 1931; confd., May, 1938; retd., Feb., 1940; apptd. judicial member, income tax appellate tribunal, Apl., 1941.

GUNJAL, PADMAPPA HIRIGAPPA, B.A., LL.B., Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 17th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1925; served in Bombay judl. dept., from Jan., 1925; judge (confd.) Jul., 1943.

GUNJIKAR, KASHINATH RAMCHANDRA, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Bom.), Indian Educl. Service (prof. R.I. of Science, Bombay) (b. 8th March, 1894).—Joined the service as prof. of maths., Elph. Coll., Bombay, 7th June, 1925; ditto, R.I. of Science, Bombay, Nov., 1926.

GUNN, JOHN PATERSON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 31st Oct., 1889).—Retd., 29th July, 1941.†

GUNNING, CARDWELL SINCLAIR, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S., (commr. Assam) (b. 28th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; acting under-sec. to chief commr., Sept., 1916; confd., Dec., 1916 on mil duty, I.A.R.O., Oct., 1918, to Jan., 1919; offg. dep. commr., May, 1923; confd., Sept., 1928; O.B.E., June, 1942; commr. of divns. (confd.) Dec., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1946.

GUPTA, ANAND SWARUP, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Police (asst. supt. United Provs.) (b. 25th Dec., 1915).—Joined the service as asst. supt. of police, 10th Feb., 1939; confd., Feb., 1941.

GUPTA, ARUN CHANDRA, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 30th Oct., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bengal, 3rd Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

GUPTA, ASHOK CHANDRA, O.B.E., M.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 4th Nov., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

GUPTA, AVADH BEHARI (see Avadh Behari G.).

GUPTA, ATUL KRISHNA, RAI SAHIB, B.A. (asst. sec., legis. dept. govt. of India) (b. 1st Jan., 1892).—Joined the service, Apl., 1915; supt., June, 1933; asst. sec., Mar., 1943.

GUPTA, BINODE BIHARI, late Indian Police (Assam) (b. Apl., 1885).—Retd., Apl., 1940.†

GUPTA, BIRENDRA CHANDRA, A.I.E.E., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 10th Dec., 1881).—Retd., July, 1938.†

GUPTA, CHANDRA PRAKASH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag.) (b. 8th Oct., 1919).—Educ. at Modern High Sch., Delhi, Delhi Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. after seln. in July, 1942, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag.

GUPTA, HARISH CHANDRA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 15th July, 1910).—Educ. at Ewing Christian Coll., Allahabad, University of Allahabad, and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1936; addl. dist. mag., Apl., 1941; addl. land collr., Feb., 1943.

GUPTA, HIRENDRA NATH, B.Sc., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd Nov., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on

16th Aug., 1924; offg. supt., March, 1931; confd., Apl., 1937.

GUPTA, IRESH CHANDRA, M.Sc., A.I.C., His Majesty's Mint, Bombay (b. 11th March, 1898).—Joined the service, 30th Aug., 1921, as dep. assay master; offg. assay master, Apl., 1922; March, 1923, and Sept., 1926.

GUPTA, JYOTISH CHANDRA, B.A., late Income-tax Dept., Bengal (b. Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.†

GUPTA, KULADA CHARAN DAS (see Das Gupta, K.C.).

GUPTA, LAKSHMAN SARUP (see Lakshman, S. G.).

GUPTA, NIRMAL CHANDRA SEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 7th July, 1918).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GUPTA, PANNA LAL, Indian State Railways (ch. engr. E.I. Rly.) (b. 16th Jan., 1896).—Asst. engr., 24th Sept., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr. (prov.), 1937; confd., Oct., 1938; ch. engr., Sept., 1945.

GUPTA, RADHA KRISHNA (see Radha, K.G.).
GUPTA, RAGHAVANSH LAL, O.B.E., M.Sc., (Allbd.) Indian C.S. (jt. fin. advr. food) (b. 7th Aug., 1905).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty. to govt. fin. dept., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1934; and again, Feb., 1936; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Oct., 1936; under-secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1937; fin. sec., bd. of rev., Oct., 1939; dep. sec., fin., Apl., 1940; contr. for trade, Calcutta, May, 1941; dep. finl. advr., supply fin., March, 1942; ditto, food, Jan., 1943; jt. finl. advr., foreign (food), March, 1943; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

GUPTA, RANJIT KUMAR, B.A., Indian Police, Bengal (b. 9th Feb., 1920).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., after exam. of 1941.

GUPTA, RANJIT, Indian C.S. (ch. presdy. mag., Bengal) (b. 1st Feb., 1906).—Educ. at University Coll., Aberystwyth, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1935; addtl. dist. mag., Jan., 1936; ch. presdy. mag., Calcutta (confd.), Apl., 1937.

GUPTA, SAMARENDRA NATH, Dept. of Industries, Punjab (princl., Mayo sch. of arts) (b. 29th March, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. princpl., Mayo sch. of arts, 2nd Jan., 1914; vice-princl., ditto, May, 1928; princpl., ditto, Nov., 1929.

GUPTA, SARAT KUMAR DUTTA, M.A., M.B.E., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 17th June, 1881).—Retd., June, 1936*; re-empld. in dept. of war transport govt. of India, Jul., 1943, to Apl., 1946.

GUPTA, SATYENDRA NATH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., customs, Karachi) (b. 29th July, 1895).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., London, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918; arrived, 10th Jan., 1919, and posted to Bengal; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1921; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta, 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1925; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. dep. Indian trade commr. in London, July, 1928; offg.

Indian trade commr., Nov., 1929; trade commr. Hamburg, March, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1935; collr. of customs, Bombay Oct., 1939; ditto, Karachi, Dec., 1937; and again, Jan., 1940.

GUPTA, SEN (see Sen Gupta).

GUPTA, SHAIBAL KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 26th Feb., 1902).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in Bengal as asst. collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1932; addl. ditto, 24-Parganas, Aug., 1935; dist. and sess. judge, confd., Jan., 1939.

GUPTA, SHAMLAL, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Prov.) (b. 15th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1929; on for service under Delhi municipal ctte., March, 1939.

GUPTA, SHORLINDRA CHANDRA, C.I.E., M.A. (Cantab.), Indian Fin. Dept. (b. 20th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 20th May, 1916; currency officer, Cawnpore, Apl., 1922; under-secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1925; offg. under-secty. for. and pol. dept., Aug., 1930; audit officer, Indian stores dept., Nov., 1931; offg. dep. govt. exr., B.N. rly., Feb., 1933; exr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Dec., 1933; compt. Assam, Feb., 1935; acct.-genl., Cent. Provs. and Berar, Dec., 1939; confd. as acct.-genl., Oct., 1942; dir. of audit, def. services, Oct., 1943; asst. acct.-genl., cl.I (confd.) Sept., 1945; dir. of audit, war and supply; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

GUPTA, SUKAMAR, M.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 1st Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 7th Dec., 1922; addl. supt., March, 1928; offg. supt., March, 1931; confd., Oct., 1932.

GUPTA, SUNNY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th March, 1905).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 14th Apl., 1931; exec. engr., Aug., 1937.

GURBACHAN SINGH, LAMBA SARDAR, Indian Serv. of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 26th Oct., 1925; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935; under sec. to govt., P.W.D., irrign. branch, Feb., 1945.

GURBAKSH SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, B.A., M.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs., Punjab (b. 15th Jan., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

GURCHARN DAS, LALA, MEHTA, Rai Sahib, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 11th Mar., 1887).—Retd., 11th March, 1942.†

GURDIAL SINGH, Indian Police (b. 15th June, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 3rd Feb., 1932; confd., Feb., 1934.

GURDIAL SUNDER SINGH, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 22nd July, 1899).—Joined the service, 9th Nov., 1923; asst. consvr., Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927; personal asst. to insp.-genl. of forests and presd., forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1940.

GURJAR, NARAYAN PANDURANG, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 12th Apl., 1895).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 7th Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1928; confd., Oct., 1929; conslg. p.h. engr. to govt., Sind (in addn.), Apl., 1938; transf. to Bombay, Aug., 1939.

GURMANI, NAWAB MUSHTAQ AHMAD, (dir.-

genl. of resettlmt. and employmt., govt. of India) (b. 25th Oct., 1905).—Educ. at M.A.O. coll. Aligarh; elected member of legis. council, Punjab, 1930; nom. member, ditto and elected member, legis. assembly, 1932-37; parl. sec. to min. of educ., Punjab., 1937-42; dir. of pub. and recruitmt., (tech.) govt. of India, lab. dept., 1942-45; dir.-genl. of resettlement and emplmt. and ex-officio jt. sec. ditto, July, 1945.

GURNER, SIR CYRIL WALTER, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (commissioner, Bengal) (b. 18th Jan., 1888).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., London, Ariel Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll. London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bengal, Apl., 1912; under-secty. to govt.; genl. dept., Sept., 1915; dep. chairman, Calcutta Corpn., July, 1918; dep. sec. to govt., of Bengal, pol. and appt. dept., Sept., 1923; mag. and collr., March, 1925; secty., govt. of Bengal, local self-govt. dept., March, 1929; offg. chairman, Calcutta improvement trust, March, 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; confd. as commr. of a divn., Feb. 1938; spec. offr. (land rev. commn.), rev. dept., Apl., 1940; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; spec. offr., public health dept., Feb., 1943; advr. to govr., Dec., 1945 to Jan., 1946; Kt., June, 1946.

GURNEY, WILFRED ERIC, V.D., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 2nd Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 16th Nov., 1911; on milty. service, 16th Nov., 1914, to 1st Oct., 1919; resdtd. engr., Oct., 1919; divl. engr., Oct., 1930; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), July, 1936.

GWYER, SIR MAURICE LINFORD, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., D.C.L., K.C.—Member of Indian States Enquiry Committee, 1932; late First Parliamentary Counsel to the Treasury; apptd. first Chief Justice of India, Oct., 1937; retd., 25th Apl., 1943.

GWYNN, JOHN PETER LUCIUS, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 22nd June, 1916).—Educ. at St. Columba's Coll., Rathfarnham, Co. Dublin, Trinity Coll., Dublin, and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 10th Oct., 1938; confd., 9th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939; under sec. to govt., rev. dept., Sept., 1945.

GYAN NATH, RAI BAHADUR, Raja, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Political Service (b. Feb., 1885).—Retd., Feb., 1940.†

GYAN SINGH, BHAI, B.A., late Income Tax Dept., Punjab (b. 28th Aug., 1884).—Retd., Aug., 1939.†

GYANI, SUNDRA SINGH, Sardar Bahadur, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. officr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 14th May, 1893).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1914; divl. transpn. officr., May, 1935; dep. ch. opertg. supt. (provl.), Oct., 1942.

GYAW, U, B.A., B.Sc., late Indian Serv. of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 6th March, 1891).—Temp. serv. as agric. engr. from 3rd Jan., 1919; agric. engr., Oct., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1936; confd., May, 1939; retd., March, 1946.

GYAW, U AUNG, Burma Civ. Serv. (cl. I) (b. 16th June, 1895).—Joined the serv., 1st Apl., 1919; dep. commr., 30th Dec., 1939; civ. aff. serv., Apl., 1945; dep. commr., Dec., 1945.

GYAW, U AUNG THA, Burma Judl. Serv. (b. 18th Sept., 1898).—Joined 1st Feb., 1923; dist. and sess. judge, 1st Oct., 1939; on spec.

duty in refugee camps, June, 1942 to Jan., 1943; civ. aff. serv., Jan., 1943; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1945.

GYAW, U ON, O.B.E., late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 8th June, 1887).—Apptd. asst. dist. supt., April, 1920; dist. supt., Jan., 1924; O.B.E., June, 1932; ret'd., June, 1942.

GYI, SIR JOSEPH AUGUSTUS MAUNG, Kt., Barr., Burma Judl. Dept.
