(mag. and collr., Bengal) (b., 28th Apl., 1899).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1923; arrived, 15th Dec., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1929; confd., Dec., 1936; jt. secty to govt., co-op. edn. dept., Bengal, Apl., 1937; spec. offr., fin. dept., Bengal, July, 1940; sec. publ. health dept., Aug., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1944; offg. commr., Dacca divn., Nov., 1945.

HOLLAND, LAWRENCE BERTRAM, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Orissa) (b. 25th Feb., 1885).—Retd., Aug., 1939.*

HOLLAND, SIR ROBERT ERSKINE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.V.O., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th June, 1873).—Retd. from I.C.S., Apl., 1925; member of Council of India, 21st Apl., 1925; to July, 1931.*

HOLLAND, SIR THOMAS HENRY, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., D.Sc., F.R.S., late Berkeley Fellow of the Owens Coll., late Geol. survey of India; member, govr.-genl.'s Council, Apl., 1920 to Sept., 1921.*

HOLLIDAY, GEORGE DARGUE, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 14th May, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 17th Nov., 1926. confd. as supt. Mar., 1942; supt. watch and ward staff, N.W. Rly., Dec., 1945; Indian Police medal, Jan., 1946.

HOLLIS, REV. ARTHUR MICHAEL, M.A., (b. 23rd June, 1899).—Apptd. Bishop of Madras, Oct., 1942.

HOLMAN, THOMAS GEORGE HOATHLEY, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 10th July, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 1st Jan., 1926; offg. addl. supt., variously from March, 1932! offg. princpl., police training coll., Sardah, Apl., 1933; supt. (temp.), June, 1936; confd., June, 1938; princpl. police trng. coll., Sardah, Feb., 1943.

HOLMES, ANDREW GILMOUR, M.A., Indian C.S. jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 5th June,

1915).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1939; addl. mag., Apl., 1942; dep. contr. rationing, Nov., 1943; dep. sec. home dept., and dep. civ. rep. govt., Bengal, Nov., 1945.

HOLMES, ARTHUR MALCOLM, Indian Police (b. 21st July, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 24th Nov., 1928; A.D.C. to govt., C.P., Dec., 1933 to June, 1937; awarded King's police medal, July, 1938; asst. to inspr.-gen., Feb., 1944

HOLMES, FREDERICK GORDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 14th July, 1916).—Educ. at King Edward's School, Bath, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 4th March, 1941; served in United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.

HOLMES, HENRY CHRISTOPHER, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 10th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service, as forest engr., Bombay, 24th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., July, 1928; on active service with R.N. from 1st Nov. 1939.

HOLMES, JAMES EDWARD, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 30th Aug., 1897).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 2nd Feb., 1920; transferred to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; actg. exec. officer, May, 1930; confd., July, 1935.

HOLMES, THOMAS ATKINSON, Burma C.S. (cl. I) (b. 29th May, 1920).—Educ. at Tonbridge School and Pembroke College, Oxford; apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

HOLT, EDWARD HARLAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collector, Sind) (b. 20 Apl., 1907).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar School and Queen's College, Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; served in Sind from April, 1936.

HOLT, JOHN WILLIAM CECIL, Indian State Rlys. (dep. dir. rly. bd.) (b. 22nd July, 1900).—Asst. supt., E.I. rly., Oct., 1925; supt. (senr.), Aug., 1937; divl. supt., Apl., 1944.

HOLT-KEENE, H., Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Sept., 1893).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1921; ch. elec. engr. G.I.P. Rly., Mar., 1945.

HOMAN, ARTHUR KNOX, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. rly.).—Retd., Oct., 1939.†

HOMPRAY, CHARLES KENYON, late Indian Forest Service (Bengal) (b. 19th Apl., 1897).—Retd., May, 1940.†

HONAVAR, MONJUNATH BABU, B.A., B.Sc., LL.B., Bombay Judl. Dept., (b. 14th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service 2nd Dec., 1924; served in Bombay Judl. dept. from Dec., 1924; dist. and sess. judge, (confd.) Mar., 1943.

HOOD, SIR HUGH MEGGISON, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 5th June, 1885).—Educated at Middlesbrough High Sch., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived 23rd Nov., 1909, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Sept., 1916; on mil. duty from Aug., 1916 to Apl., 1919; registrar, co-operative societies, Apl., 1923; and again, Nov., 1926; off. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1924; confd., Aug., 1929; off. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Dec., 1925; chairman, Madras provl. banking enquiry commee., Dec., 1929; off. secty. to govt., finance dept., Apl., 1931; confd. Dec., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; off. secty. to govt.,

Madras, law and educn. dept., March, 1936; secty. to govt., home dept., Apl., 1936; off. second secty. to govt., Apl., 1936; off. ch. secty. to govt., Madras, June, 1936; Aug., 1938 and Aug., 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1939; 3rd membr. bd. of rev. (provl. subst.); adviser to the govr., Oct., 1939; K.C.I.E., June, 1942; ret'd., July, 1945.

HOOD, ROBERT HOOD, late Indian Service of Engineers (United Provs.) (b. 18th Dec., 1891). Retd., 23rd Oct., 1940.†

HOOD, VISCOUNT, SAMUEL HOOD, B.A., India Office (b. Oct. 15th, 1910).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity College, Cambridge; apptd. asst. princpl., Feb. 19th, 1935; resdt. clk., March, 1936; asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state for India, Sept., 1936; ditto for Burma, Apl., 1937; actg. princpl., May, 1939; services loaned to the Ministry of Information, Sept., 1939; on loan to Foreign Office.

HOOD, WILLIAM, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (gen. manager, G.I.P., Rly.) (b. 5th Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. bridge engr. 23rd Jan., 1925; senr. asst. ditto, Aug., 1927; off. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), Nov., 1932; confd., Sept., 1934; off. dep. ch. contr. of standardisation, July, 1936; dep. ch. engr. provl., Apl., 1941; ch. engr. (confd.) July, 1945; gen. mangr., G.I.P. Rly., O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

HOOGWERF, GERALD JAMES, Indian Police (b. 8th Oct., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Nov., 1938.

HOOJA, MADAN MOHAN LALL, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 5th Sept., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., March, 1939; confd., March, 1941.

HOOLE, NORMAN PETER GLEDHILL, Indian Police (dist. supt. Burma) (b. 9th May, 1916).—A ptd. asst. supt. on probn., 6th Dec., 1935; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; dist. supt., 1946.

HOON, PRAN NATH, late Indian State Rlys. (E.B. Rly.) (b. 5th March, 1889).—Joined the service, 12th Jan., 1915; asst. contr. of stores, Sept., 1923; dist. contr. of stores (junr.) E.B. Rly., Sept., 1926; ditto, (senr.) May, 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1944.

HOOPER, ERNEST GEORGE GALE, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 14th Dec., 1899).—Apptd., clerical officer, 3rd Feb., 1924, after service at India Office from 21st May, 1920; exec. officer, 1st Nov., 1932.

HOOPER, GEOFFREY GRANT, B.A., M.C., late Indian Civil Service (Bengal) (b. 7th Dec., 1891).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

HOPE, CAPT. THE HON. SIR ARTHUR OSWALD JAMES, G.C.I.E., M.C., govr. of Madras, March, 1940.

HOPE, JOHN RUTHERFORD, O.B.E., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 14th May, 1894).—Held non-gaz. and sub-gaz. appts. from Apl., 1922 to Apl., 1929; apptd. to class II, Apl., 1929; dep. finl. advr., R.A.F., Oct., 1934; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1941.

HOPE, ROBERT CYPRIAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 25th September, 1915).—Educ. at St. Edward's School, Oxford, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 6th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938; sub. collr. and jt. mag., June, 1941.

HOPKINS, ALAN CECIL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 30th Oct., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Sept., 1926.

HOPKINS, EDWARD ALFRED, Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 4th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 29th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., May, 1928; awarded King's police medal, March, 1929, on l.p.r.

HOPKINS, GEOFFREY MAYNE, O.B.E., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 5th July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 30th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1926; O.B.E., Jan., 1942.

HOPKINSON, ARTHUR JOHN, C.I.E., Indian Civil Service, Indian Political Service, (b. 28th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; arrived, 10th May, 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb. 1924; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1924; British trade agent at Gyantse and asst. to pol. offr. in Sikkim, Jan., 1927; pol. agt., E. Kathiawar states, Nov., 1928; dep. commr., Hazara, Nov., 1930; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transf. depts., Apl., 1932; chief secty., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; serv. in N.W.F.P., again from Oct., 1938; pol. agt., W. Kathiawar agency, March, 1939; secty. to A.G.G., resdt. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Sept., 1940; pol. agent in Kalat and pol. agent in charge of Bolan Pass, Oct., 1941; on specl. duty, Sikkim, Dec., 1944; offg. resdt. cl. II and pol. offr. in Sikkim, Aug., 1945. resdt. cl. II (confd.) May, 1946.

HORMAZDIAR PHIROZ DASTUR (see Dastur, H. P.).

HORNELL, SIR WILLIAM WOODWARD, Kt., C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educatl. Service.*

HORTON, RALPH ALBERT, C.I.E., late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 5th Oct., 1885). Retd., Oct., 1940.†

HORWILL, LIONEL CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 19th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Royal Coll. of Science and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915; on milty. duty July, 1916 to Dec., 1919; sub. collr., Sept., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1925; confd., Feb., 1929; addl. judge, ct. of resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1935; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, variously from July, 1936; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1938; judge, high ct., Madras, Jan., 1940.

HOSALI, SATYENDRANATH, B.A., Indian Police (b. 4th July, 1913) (asst. supt., Bombay).—Apptd. on probn., 3rd Feb., 1937; confd., March, 1939.

HOSE, SIR JOHN WALTER, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. and India Office.—Retd., Jan., 1916; employed in India Office from Jan., 1916, to Oct., 1926.*

HOSEGOOD, PHILIP JAMES, India Office (acct.-gentl's dept.) (b. 9th Sept., 1920).—Apptd. exec. offr. 20th Sept., 1939; on mil. duty to Aug., 1946.

HOSIE, JAMES FINDLAY, M.B.E., M.A., Ind. C.S. (asst. mag. and collr. Bihar) (b. 22nd Aug., 1913).—Educ. at Kilsyth Academy, Glasgow Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 9th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. divl. offr., 1940; under sec., pol. and appts. dept., Apl., 1942; asst. fincl. advr., mil. fin., Dec., 1945; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

HOULTON, JOHN WARDLE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (commr., Bihar)

(b. 28th Jan., 1892).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Feb., 1920; arrived, 12th May, 1920, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; mag. and collr. (provl. sub.), March, 1932; offg. secty. to govt., rev. dept., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1933; mag. and collr., July, 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; commr. of excise, and I.G.R., July, 1940; offg. ch. sec. to govt., Bihar, Apl., 1944; advr. to govr., March, 1945; C.S.I., June, 1945; commr., Apl., 1946.

HOWARD, SIR ALBERT, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., A.R.C.S., F.C.S., F.L.S., late Dept. of Agric.—Retd., Dec., 1928.†

HOWARD, SIR HENRY GEORGE, Maj., C.I.E., M.C., M.I.E.E., M.A.M.Soc.C.E., Electricity Dept., Madras (b. 5th Oct., 1883).—Chief engr. for electricity on agreement from 6th Sept., 1927; consltg. engr. to govt. on contract, Nov., 1939; kt., Jan., 1939.

HOWARD, SIR STANLEY HERBERT, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 17th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1912, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1916; silviculturist, forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Oct., 1919, to Jan., 1926; offg. consvr., Dec., 1929; and again, Apl., 1933; confd., Apl., 1936; ch. consvr., March, 1940; inspr.-genl. of forests and president, forest resch. inst., Dehra Dun, 1940; kt. Jan., 1943; retd., Dec., 1945.

HOWARD, REV. GEORGE JOSEPH, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 12th Feb., 1906).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Madras diocese, Sept., 1938; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1940; on mil. duty, Feb., 1944 to April, 1946.

HOWE, HENRY, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th July, 1883).—Retd., June, 1939.*

HOWE, ROBERT DOUGLAS, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (b. 21st Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Bishop Foy Sch., Waterford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin, apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 12th Sept. 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to mem. for India on the Eastern Gp. Supply Council, Feb., 1942; under sec., govt. of India (war dept.), Apl., 1943 to Apl., 1946.

HOWELL, ALFRED EDWARD, Indian State Rlys. (production engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 31st March, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. works manager, N.W. rly., 6th Apl., 1927; production engr., N.W. rly., Oct., 1930.

HOWELL, DAVID ARNOLD, C.I.E., O.B.E., A.M.I.C.E., Public Works Dept., Punjab (chief engr.) (b. 28th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., 25th Dec., 1920; exec. sany. engr., March, 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1932; confd., Dec., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; ch. engr., p.w.d., public health branch., Jan., 1944.

HOWELL, SIR EVELYN BERKELEY, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov.) (b. 12th Feb., 1877).—Retd., Sept., 1934.*

HOWES, JOHN BARON, O.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 17th Dec., 1907).—First commn., July, 1931; pers. asst. to pol. res., Persian Gulf, Oct. 1935; offg. pol. agt., Bahrain, Aug., 1938; publicity offr., Trucial Coast, Oct., 1939; publicity offr., Persian Gulf, Sept., 1940; M.B.E., 1941; pol. agt., Muscat, Aug., 1941; under-sec., external aff. dept., Mar., 1944; O.B.E., June, 1944; dep. sec., infm. and broadcasting dept., Sept., 1944; offg. jt. sec. to ditto, Dec., 1945 to Feb., 1946 dep. sec. to

govt. of India, dept. of infm. and arts, Feb., 1946.

HOWROYD, ROGER ANTHONY FITZWILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep., commr., Punjab) (b. 20th Dec., 1915).—Educ. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. offr., July, 1942; dep. commr., Apl., 1945; dir. of war publicity etc., Dec., 1945.

HOWSON, WILLIAM ROXBURGH, Capt., I.A. (b. 3rd June, 1914).—Apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., Oct., 1938; vice-consul, Khorramshahr, Oct., 1944.

HOYLE, LEONARD ARTHUR, late Indian State Ry. (G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 4th Feb., 1886).—Retd., Apl., 1941.†

HTAY, MAUNG BA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 3rd June, 1908).—Educ. at the Wesleyan High Sch., Monywa, Rangoon Univ., and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, January, 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; western sub-divl. mag. June, 1938; asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1940.

HUBAN, JOHN PATRICK, C.S.I., O.B.E., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S. (surgn.-gen., Madras) (b. 13th Feb., 1891).—First commn. dated 23rd Sept., 1914; in milty. employ. to 16th Oct., 1922; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; civil surgn., Oct., 1922; ch. medl. officer, Jaipur, Jan., 1932; admin. medl. offcr. in Rajputana and addtl. depy. dir.-gen., I.M.S., 1940; inspr.-gen., civil hos., and d.p.h., N.W.F.P., 1941; surgn.-gen., Madras, July, 1944; C.S.I., June, 1946.

HUBBACK, REV. GEORGE CLAY, B.Sc., D.D., (b. 7th Apl., 1882).—Bishop of Assam, 1924–45; Bishop of Calcutta and Metropolitan of India, Burma, and Ceylon, Sept., 1945.

HUBBACK, SIR JOHN AUSTEN, K.C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Feb., 1878).—Educ. at Winchester and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 6th Dec., 1902; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlmt. offr.; settlmt. offcr., May, 1909; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1910; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1912; special duty, bd. of rev., Dec., 1912; offg. sec., ditto, Sept., 1913, to May, 1914; tempy. empd. in rev. and statistics dept., India Office, from May to Sept., 1915; mag. and collr., March, 1916; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from May to July, 1918; offg. sec. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, rev. and judl. depts., Feb. 1919; confd., Apl., 1919; dir. of land records and surveys, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1923; offg. commr., Feb., 1925; confd., Dec., 1928; offg. member, bd. of rev., May, 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; tempy. member, govr.'s exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, Aug., 1933; and again, Oct., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1936; govr. of Orissa, Apl., 1936; retd., Apl., 1941; apptd. advr. to sec. of state for India, March, 1942.

HUBBARD, ALEXANDER EGERTON, late Indian State Ry. (b. 5th Nov., 1882).—Retd., June, 1939.*

HUBBARD, PHILIP MAITLAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 9th Nov., 1910).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. colonzn. offr., May, 1937, to Oct., 1937; sub. divl. offr., Nov., 1938; on for. serv. under Bahawalpur state,

Nov., 1939; colonzn. offr., Oct., 1941; addtl. dist. and sess. judge, 1944.

HUDDLESTON, SIR ERNEST WHITESIDE, C.I.E., C.B.E., Capt., R.I.N. (ret'd.), late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 18th Aug., 1874).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

HUDSON, SIR WALTER FRANK, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Sind) (b. 22nd Aug., 1875).—Retd., Oct., 1933.*

HUGGINS, HENRY WALTER, Indian State Ry. (late N.W. Ry.) (b. 10th Oct., 1886).—Retd., 9th Oct., 1944.†

HUGHES, ARTHUR, O.B.E., B.A. (Man.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 25th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at City and County Sch., Chester, Victoria Univ., Manchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1933; offg. addl. dep. secty. to the govt. of Bengal, fin., comm. and marine depts., Nov., 1936; dep. secty., ditto, Apl., 1937; jt. secty., com. and lab. dept., Bengal, May, 1938; offcr. on spec. duty, dept. of lab., govt. of India, May, 1940; O.B.E., June, 1943; chairman, labour tribunal, Nov., 1943, on l.p.r. Jan., 1947.

HUGHES, DEWDYD WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 7th Apl., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 19th Dec., 1912; dep. consvr., March, 1915; retd., Feb., 1945.

HUGHES, HENRY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 18th June, 1885).—Retd., Sept., 1940.†

HUGHES, THOMAS LEWIS, C.B.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th July, 1897).—Educ. at Birkenhead Sch.; on mil. service from Sept., 1915, to Dec., 1922; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offices, Jan., 1925; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1932; ditto (provs. subs.), March, 1935; secty. to finl. commr., Burma, Dec., 1936; retd., Aug., 1939; re-empld. as sec. to govr., Burma, Sept., 1942; C.B.E., Jan., 1943.

HUGHES-HUGHES, EDWARD ESSEX, Indian Police (addl. supt., Assam) (b. 11th Aug., 1915).—Apptd. on probn., Nov., 1936; confd., Nov., 1938; asst. to the pol. offr., Sadiya Frontier Tract, Nov., 1938 to Dec., 1939; addl. supt., Jan., 1945.

HUGHFF, REGINALD GEORGE, O.B.E., Indian State Ry. (wks. mgr. E.I. Ry) (b. 30th Oct., 1892).—Joined the serv., 11th Feb., 1921; works manager, E.I. ry., Sept., 1923; offg. pers. asst. to dep. ch. mechl. engr., senr. scale, June, 1925, and again, Feb., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. ry., Jan., 1936; confd., Apl., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

HUFFTON, REV. HARRY VALENTINE, B.A., B.D., Indian Ecclesl. Estabt. (b. 14th Feb., 1905).—Apptd., Apl., 1946.

HUKUM SINGH, THAKUR, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late United Provs. C.S. (b. March, 1884).—Retd., March, 1939.*

HULKOTI, CHANAPPA CHANVIRAPPA, LL.B., late Bombay C.S. (Judl. Branch) (b. 27th Dec., 1887).—Retd., 27th Dec., 1942.

HULLAND, WALTER VEORGE, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 28th Jan., 1908).—Educ. at the Grammar Sch., Barnstaple, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; O.B.E., June, 1946.

HUME, ANDREW PARKE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Unit. Provs.) (b. 24th Nov.,

1904).—Educ. at University Sch., Hastings, Lausanne, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr.; jt. mag., Aug., 1929; offg. dep. commr., March, 1932; offg. addl. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, June, 1936; offr. on spl. duty, ditto, Sept., 1936; chairman, Delhi improvement trust, March, 1937; dep. secty., dept. of supply, May, 1941; confd. as mag. and collr., July, 1941; jt. sec., supply dep. Aug., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

HUME, ROBERT C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Police, (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 1st Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1920; asst. supt., Aug., 1923; supt., May 1930; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1925; O.B.E., June, 1931; dep. commr. for town of Madras, Feb., 1935; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1938; confd. as dep. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1941; commr. police, Madras City, May, 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

HUMPHREY, CECIL ALBERT, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 17th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1936; dep. sec. (home), Jan., 1942; dep. commr. (confd.), Dec., 1943.

HUMPHREY, WILFRED, Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 11th June, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on probn., 5th Dec., 1924; confd., Oct., 1929; asst. dir. intell., G.I. rly., March, 1937; ditto, under govt. of India, Peshawar, Apl., 1938.

HUMPHRYS, SIR FRANCIS HENRY, G.C.M.G., G.C.V.O., K.B.E., C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 24th Apl., 1879).—Retd., Nov., 1929.*

HUNT, EDWIN WILLIAM, O.B.E., Indian Police (b. 1st Feb., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 19th Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Nov., 1932, and again, March, 1933; awarded Indian police medal; supt. (confd.), Feb., 1940; O.B.E., June, 1943.

HUNT, GEOFFREY HOWARD, B.Sc., M.C., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 7th Nov., 1891).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

HUNT, HENRY CARTER, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 19th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 26th Nov., 1909; supt., June, 1920; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Feb., 1925; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1933, and again, Oct., 1936; confd., July, 1937; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938; ret'd., 19th Sept., 1944.

HUNT, ROLAND CHARLES COLIN, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 19th March, 1916).—Educ. at Rugby and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 14th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct. 1939; sub. collr. and jt. mag., jt. sec. bd. of rev., civil supplies, Madras, Feb., 1946.

HUNT, THELMA GWENDOLEN, India Office (mil. dept.) (b. 28th May, 1901).—Apptd. cler. offr. 7th July, 1921; actg. hr. ditto, July, 1939; actg. hr. exec. offr., May, 1944; resdt. clerk, Feb., 1942.

HUNTER, CHARLES FREDERICK, late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 12th Feb., 1887).—Retd., Nov., 1940.†

HUNTER, DOUGLAS CAMPBELL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th Aug., 1886).—Educ. at Felsted Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov.,

1910, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. offr.; jt. mag., Dec., 1916; on mil. duty, July, 1918, to March, 1919; offg. as dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1921; sess. and sub-judge, May, 1925; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1925; judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1939; ret'd., 19th Jan., 1944.

HUNTER, GUY GUNNING RATCLIFFE, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., Cent. Provs.) (b. 16th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as prof'r., Jubbulpore coll., 28th Dec., 1912; prof'r., Morris coll., Nagpur, July, 1924; offg. princpl., ditto, July, 1931; offg. dir. of public instrn. and educ. secty., Cent. Provs. and Berar, June, 1938; ret'd., Aug., 1945.

HUQUE, KHAN BAHADUR SIR MUHAMMAD Azizul, C.I.E. (see Azizul Huque).

HURLEY, THOMAS JOSEPH, O.B.E., M.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M., Indian Vetry. Serv. (dir., vet. serv., Madras) (b. 4th May, 1895).—Apptd. supt., I Circle, Civil Vetry. dept., Vizagapatam, March, 1922; offg. as princpl., Madras vetry. coll., June, 1926; and again, Sept., 1928; prof'r. of surg., ditto, Aug., 1928; offg. dir. of vetry. services, Madras, variously from June, 1929, to Sept., 1939; confd. as dir., Sept., 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

HURN, REV. EDWARD LIDDELL ARTHUR, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (junr. chaplain) (b. 4th Sept., 1905).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 11th Nov., 1935; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1937.

HURRELL, IAN MURRAY, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 1st Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

HUSAIN, AKHTAR, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st March, 1902).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., Oct., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1932; confd. (subs. provl.), March, 1933; addl. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1935; junr. secty. to finl. commrs. and dep. secty. to govt. revenue dept., May, 1937; dep. commr. (confd.), July, 1937, and settl. offcr., Gurgaon, Oct., 1938; sec. to govt., post-war reconstrn. dept., July, 1944, O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

HUSAIN, AKHTAR, Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 10th Jan., 1907).—Educ. at the Universities of Allahabad and Agra and at Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. Sept., 1934; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1937; dep. sec. (supply), govt. of India, Dec., 1943; Cawnpore devel. bd. Apl., 1946.

HUSAIN, ASHFAQUE, B.A. (Oxon), Bar.-at.-Law (dep. educ. advr. govt. of India) (b. 21st Mar., 1905).—Apptd. infm. offr., bureau public infm., govt. of India; educ. offr., Nov., 1942 to Dec., 1944; asst. educ. advr., Jan. to May, 1945; dep. educ. advr., May, 1945.

HUSAIN, BADR-UD-DIN TYABJI (see Tyabji, H. B.).

HUSAIN, CHAUDHRI AKBAR (see Akbar Husain, C.).

HUSAIN, HAFAZAT (see Hafazat Husain).

HUSAIN, ZAHID (see Zahid Husain).

HUSAIN, ZAKIR (see Zakir Husain).

HUSSAIN, BUNYAD (see Bunyad Hussain).

HUSSAIN, HAFAZAT (see Hafazat Hussain).

HUSSAIN, IQBAL (see Iqbal Hussain).

HUSSAIN, KHAN BAHADUR, JAVAD, Madras C.S. (b. 11th Feb., 1893).—Joined Sept., 1916; dep. collr., Apl., 1926; sub. collr., Nov., 1930; commr., Madras Corp., Jan., 1934; collr., July, 1938; member, Madras public serv. commn., Nov., 1942.

HUSSAIN, MIAN MUHAMMAD TASDIQUE (see Mian, M. T. H.).

HUSSAIN, MUHAMMAD AFZAL (see Muhammad, A. H.).

HUSSAIN, MUHAMMAD HADI, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 15th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; under-secty., office of chief secty., govt. of Assam, May, 1937, to March, 1938; dir. of indus. and regr. of co-ops., Feb., 1941; sec., post-war reconstr. May, 1945.

HUSSAIN, MUZAFFAR (see Muzaffar Hussain).

HUTCHINGS, SIR ROBERT HOWELL, K.C.I.E., C.M.G., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (b. 11th March, 1897).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Trinity Coll. Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and collr., Apl., 1922; under-sec., govt., of Bengal, pol. and appt. dept., Oct., 1924; offg. supt. central jail Alipore, Oct., 1926; addl. mag. and collr., March, 1928; addl. dep. secty., pol. dept., govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1929; offg. pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, Feb., 1930; member, mining board, Bengal, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Apl., 1933; and again, June, 1934; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1935; mag. and collr., May, 1935; jt. secty., home dept., Bengal, Oct., 1937; addl. secty., home dept., Bengal, Feb., 1939; agent of the govt. of India in Burma, Sept., 1939; addtl. sec., govt. of India (war), July, 1942; C.M.G., Feb., 1943; sec., govt. of India (food), Sept., 1943 and again, 1946 member govr.-gen'l's. exec. cl. July to Sept., 1946; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

HUTCHINSON, FREDERICK HEAP, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1914; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; agric. engr., Sept., 1929; under-secty. to govt., p.w.d., United Provs., Dec., 1931; on depn. to govt. Mauritius, 1938; suptg. engr. (confd.), Jan., 1940; ch. engr., Aug., 1943.

HUTTON, JOHN HENRY, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 27th June, 1885).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

HUTTON, LT.-GEN., SIR THOMAS, K.C.I.E., C.B., M.C., i.d.c., p.s.c. (sec. P. and D. Dept., Govt. of India) (b. 27th Mar., 1890).—Col. comdt. R.A. from 1942 to Dec., 1944.—Educ. Rossall, R.M.A., Woolwich; 2nd Lt. R.A. 1909; Capt., 1915; Bt. Maj. 1918; Maj. 1927; Bt. Lt. Col. 1927; Col. 1930; Maj.Gen. 1938; Lt.-Gen. 1941; served Europ. War 1914-1918; Bt. Maj. Legion of Hon. French and Italian War Crosses, M.C. and Bar; Palestine, 1936; G.S.O.I., I Divn. 1936-38; G.O.C., W. Ind. dist., India 1938-40; dep. ch. of gen. staff A.H.Q., India, 1940-41; chief ditto, 1941; G.O.C. Burma 1941; sec. war resources and reconstr. coms. of council (India), 1943-43; sec. planning and devel. dept., 1944.

HYDARI, M. S. A. (see Muhammad Saleh Akbar Hydari).

HYDE, EDGAR STANLEY, Indian C.S. (asst.

commr., Central Provs.) (b. 6th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Sedbergh and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1928; arrived, 30th Nov., 1928, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; admr., Baster state, March, 1934; supt., Lushai Hills, May, 1942, to June, 1944.

HYDE, HENRY ARMROID, C.I.E., M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Jan., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1924; confd., June, 1929; ch. engr., and secty. to govt. p.w.d., Central Provs., Nov., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1936.

IBBOTSON, SIR ARCHIE WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.B.E., M.C., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 25th Jan., 1886).—Educ. at King Edward's School, Birmingham, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; on milty. service (I.A.R.O.), from May, 1915 (Military Cross, Feb., 1917), to June, 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; jt. mag., Oct., 1916; offg. dep. commr., May, 1920; settl. officer, Nov., 1923, to May, 1924; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1935; confd. as mag. and collr., Aug., 1926; offg. commr., May, 1929; special commr., Alwar durbar, Jan., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1934; dep. commr., Oct., 1935; confd. as commr., May, 1942; kt., Jan. 1944; actg. advr. to the govr., Sept., 1943; ret'd., Nov., 1945.

IBRAHIM, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Ibrahim).

IDDON, COLWELL, O.B.E., India Office (administrative officer for service questions) (b. 1st Oct., 1893).—Apptd. clerical officer, 27th March, 1922; asst. to jt. editors, India Office List, 1927-29; acting higher clerical officer, June, 1930; confd., Sept., 1933; M.B.E., June, 1933; apptd. staff officer for services questions, Jan., 1936; apptd. administrative officer, July, 1939; actg. princpl., Jan., 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

IENGAR, HARAVU VENKATANARASINGHA VERADA RAJ, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 23rd Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Mysore Univ., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., revenue dept., Bombay, March, 1931; confd., July, 1931; passport officer to govt., Bombay, Oct., 1933; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Bombay, June, 1934, and again, Oct., 1936; secty., ditto, May, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; sec., rev. dept., Oct., 1941; addtl. sec., home dept., Sept., 1942; sec., ditto, Sept., 1943; jt. sec., govt. of India (planning and devel.) Oct., 1944.

IFE, DOUGLAS WALTER STANTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr. and dist. mag., Bihar) (b. 17th Apl., 1915); educ. at Watford Gr. Sch., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Dec., 1938; arrived in India, Jan., 1939, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1941; dep. commr. and dist. mag., Apl., 1944; dist. mag. and collr. (Orissa).

IKRAM, SHEIKH MOHAMMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 10th Sept., 1908).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Lyallpur, the Punjab Univ., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1933; arrived in India,

Nov., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; dep. regr., co-op. societies, Apl., 1937; offg. regr., ditto, var. from June, 1938.

IKRAMULLAH, MOHAMMAD, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. sec. supply dept.) (b. 15th Jan., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad and Nagpur, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, 13th Dec., 1927, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., 1931; special duty, forest divisions, 1931-32; under-secty. to govt., Central Provs., May, 1933; under-secty., govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Oct., 1933; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Jan., 1936; and again, July and Oct., 1936; secty., coal mining cttees, Nov., 1936, to Apl., 1937; dep. Indian trade commr., May, 1937; offg. Indian trade commr., May, 1938; advr. and secty., 24th and 25th Labour Conferences; dep. secty., supply dept., govt. of India, Feb., 1940; it. ditto, Jan., 1944. to Oct., 1945, advr. to Indian rep. to U.N.O. commn., Nov., 1945; C.I.E., Jan., 1946; memb. Indian coalfield cttee, Mar., 1946.

ILAHI, NUR (see Nur Ilahi).

ILM-UD-DIN, MOHAMMAD, M.A., Imperial Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 14th Nov., 1895).—Joined the service, March, 1923; asst. collr. of customs, Nov., 1935.

IMAM, JAFAR, Bihar Judl. Dept.—Apptd. Advocate-General, Sept., 1942.

INAM-UL, MAJID (see Majid Inam-ul).

INAYAT ULLAH KHAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th Feb., 1907).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. offr., Apl., 1935; addtl. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1938; dep. regr. co-ops., Sept., 1940; addtl. ditto, July, 1943; addtl. dir., civil supplies and dep. sec. supplies, Nov., 1943; dir., ditto, etc., Dec., 1944; dir., food purchases etc., Jan., 1945.

INDARJIT RAI, TANDAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 10th Nov., 1905).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 19th Nov., 1927; proby. asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1928; confd., Oct., 1931.

INDARJIT, SINGH, Indian Audit and Accts. Service.—Apptd., under sec. govt. of India, fin. dept., March, 1945.

INDER, ROBERT WILFRID, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 7th June, 1885).—Retd., July, 1939.*

INDRANARAYAN BRIJMOHANLAL, LL.B., Bombay Judicial Dept. (judge, small causes ct., Bombay) (b. 31st July, 1885).—Officiated as addl. judge and as judge, small causes ct., Bombay, from 9th Feb., 1925, to 30th Sept., 1928; confd., Oct., 1928; offg. ch. judge, June, 1935.

INDRAVADAN NARAYANBHAI MEHTA (see Mehta, I. N.).

INDUBHUSAN MUNDLE (see Mundle, I. B.)

INGLIS, SIR CLAUDE CAVENDISH, C.I.E., B.A.I. (Trin. Coll., Dub.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (Poona) (b. 3rd March, 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st October, 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1927; confd., Nov., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; retd., Apl., 1939; dir., central irrigation and hydrodynamic research station, Poona, Nov., 1939; kt., Jan., 1945; retd., May, 1945.

INGLIS, SIR HUGH ARBUTHNOT, late, Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 28th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov.,

1910; supt., July, 1923; princpl., police training sch., Moradabad, March, 1931; inspr., genl., July, 1940; kt. Jan., 1942; retd., Dec., 1945; Kings Police and Fire Service Medal, Jan., 1946.

INGOLDBY, ERIC, C.I.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. mechl. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 7th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1921; asst. mechl. engr., Nov., 1922; works manager, May, 1935; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., March, 1925; works manager, June, 1928; offg. dir., mech. engrg., Nov., 1928; on spl. duty, rly. bd., Nov., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., March, 1930; confd., Apl., 1933; dep. ch. contr. of standardisation (temp.), Dec., 1932; confd., Feb., 1935; offg. ch. ditto, Apl., 1935; confd., dir., rly. bd., Oct., 1935; confd., Jan., 1936; ch. mechl. engr., G.I.P. rly. (provl.), Feb., 1938; confd., Aug., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; ch. contr., standardization cent. standards office for rlys., Apl., 1943.

INGRAM, REV. JAMES WILLIAM, late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 10th Apl., 1890).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 21st Aug., 1919; Junr. chaplain, Aug., 1921; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1929; offg. presdy. senr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Madras, Nov., 1930; and again, Feb., 1935; confd., Aug., 1937; retd., Aug., 1943.

INGRAM, LESTER CHARLES, Indian Police (b. 7th Feb., 1914).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, United Provs., Nov., 1933; confd., Apl., 1936.

INKSON, NORMAN LEOPOLD, D.S.O., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 31st Oct., 1882).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

INNES, SIR CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Oct., 1874).—Retd., Aug., 1933.*

INNES, FERGUS MUNRO, B.A., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (ch. contr. of imports) (b. 12th May, 1903).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec., pol. dept., Punjab, Dec., 1927; registrar, high ct., Lahore, Dec., 1930; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Oct., 1932; confd., Oct., 1936; collr. of customs, Calcutta, Apl., 1939; offg. ch. contr. of imports, Oct., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

IQBAL AHMAD, SIR, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. dept., United Provs. (Allahabad).—Apptd. addl. judge, Allahabad high ct., Oct., 1932; judge, ditto, July, 1933; actg. chief justice, ditto, Feb., 1941; chief justice, ditto, June, 1941 to Sept. 1946; kt. June, 1941.

IQBAL HUSSAIN, KHAN SAHIB, MIAN, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 9th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., on 1st Oct., 1912; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1933; retd., 9th March, 1944.

IRVINE, GILBERT MARSHALL, Maj., I.M.S., (Burma Estabt.) (b. 1st July, 1898).—First com., Aug., 1923; civ. surgeon, Apl., 1933; on foreign serv. with medl. coll., Rangoon, June, 1937; on mil. duty, Dec., 1941; dean of medl. faculty, Rangoon Univ., 1946.

IRVING, SIR MILES, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 1st Aug., 1876).—Retd., March, 1935.*

IRWIN, BARON (see Halifax, Viscount).
IRWIN, HERBERT WILLIAM, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (A.I.R.O.) (b. 11th July, 1906).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 18th Feb., 1930.

IRWIN, JOSEPH BOYD, C.I.E., D.S.O.,

M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (addl. sec., govt. of India (war)) (b. 6th March, 1895).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1920; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1920, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; pers. asst. to commr. in Sind and Sindhi translator to govt., Dec., 1924; asst. commr. in Sind and ditto, March, 1927; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Feb., 1929; and again, March, 1932; confd., Nov., 1935; offg. secty., home and eccl. depts., Bombay, Feb. 1936; confd., May, 1936; secty. to govr., Bombay, Aug., 1938 to Sept., 1943; addt. sec., govt. of India (war), Sept., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1942; chairman post-war pay cttee., war dept., Jan., 1946.

IRWIN, REGINALD DE VERE, Indian State Ry's. (ch. mechl. engr., E.B. Ry.) (b. 5th Apl., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 3rd March, 1915; dist. loco. supt., March, 1924; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1934; confd., Feb., 1937; ch. ditto, Aug., 1944.

ISAAC, PALATHUNKAL VARKI, B.A., M.Sc., D.I.C., Indian Agricl. Service (2nd entomologist) (b. 6th June, 1891). Joined the service as 2nd entomologist 26th June, 1922.

ISAR, RAYMOND FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag.) (b. 19th Jan., 1916).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Naini Tal, St. George's, Mussoorie, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

ISHAQUE, HAFIZ SAYED MUHAMMAD, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 6th Aug., 1906).—Educ. Aligarh Univ., Lucknow Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1933; dir., rural constrn., Oct., 1940; ch. contr., jute, Oct., 1940; spec. offr., dept. of civil supplies, May, 1942; O.B.E., June, 1946.

ISHWAR, DAYAL, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 16th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service, 18th March, 1921; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, July, 1923; divl. audit offcr., O. & R. rly., March, 1925; accts. offcr., N.W. rly., Apl., 1929; asst. contr. of rly. accts., Apl., 1932; asst. auditor-genl. (personnel), March, 1933; audit officer, Indian stores dept., March, 1936, and again, Jan., 1937; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Nov., 1936; audit offr., Indian stores dept., Jan., 1939; comptr., Sind, Nov., 1938; ditto, N.W.F.P., Oct., 1939; ch. accts. offcr. (American purchases) dept. of supply, Aug., 1941; acct.-genl., U.P., March, 1944.

ISLAM AHMAD, Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 27th Jan., 1936.

ISLAM, KHAN SHAMSUL, M.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag.) (b. 16th Aug. 1915).—Ed. at Govt. High School, Hathrao, Allahabad Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 21st Oct., 1939; confd., 30th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940; transf. to govt. Bengal, Mar., 1944; dep. contrl., civilsupp., Faridpur, Feb., 1945.

ISMAIL, IBRAHIM CHUNDRIGAR. —Apptd. commerce member of govt.-gen.'s council, Oct., 1946.

ISMAIL, PARVEZ MUHAMMAD, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 27th May, 1908).—Educ. at Central Model Sch., Lahore, Punjab Univ., and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.;

sub-divl. offcr., Nov., 1936; judge, small cause ct., Simla, March, 1940; dep. regr., co-op. societies, June, 1943; regr., ditto, Mar., 1944; dep. commr., Sept., 1945.

ISMAIL, SHAH ABUL HASANAT MUHAMMAD (see Shah, A. H. M. I.).

ISSERLIS, PAUL, Burma C.S. (cl. I).—(b. 25th June, 1919).—Educ. Ilford County High Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

ISVARAN, VENGUAYYAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 30th June, 1908).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; served in Sind from June, 1942; dir. of agric. educn., May, 1943.

IVEY, RONALD CHURCHILL, O.B.E., Indian State Ry's. (dep. transpn. supt., G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 18th June, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., G.I.P. rly., 8th July, 1919; transpn. (traffic comml.) supt., May, 1929; offg. divl. transpn. supt., May, 1933; offg. dep. transpn. supt., Jan., 1935; confd., July, 1935; O.B.E., June, 1943.

IYENGAR, A. S. (b. 28th Oct., 1894).—Apptd. Principal Information Officer, Press Infm. Bureau, dept. of infm. and arts, 16th Feb., 1946.

IYENGAR, KOMBAR RAMASWAMI KRISHNASWAMI, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., (b. 5th July, 1983).—Retd., July, 1838.* C.I.E., June, 1945.

IYENGAR, MANDAYAM ANNADHORAI THIRUMALAI (see Thirumalai Iyengar, M.A.).

IYENGAR, SESHADRY AMARENDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 12th Feb., 1911).—Educ. at La Martiniere Coll., Lucknow, Bishop Cotton's Boys' High Sch., Univ. of Madras, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr., Apl., 1938; public relations officer, food dept., Madras, March to Apl., 1946.

IYENGAR, VANKIPURAM RANGASWAMI, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 29th Apl., 1884).—Retd., March, 1939.*

JACK, ALEXANDER PURVIS, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 21st Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Nov., 1909; dist. supt., Jan., 1922; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932.

JACK, SIR ROBERT ERNEST, late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 20th Dec., 1878).—Retd., Dec., 1938.*

JACKETS, CECIL HECTOR, Indian Police (Madras) (b. 19th March, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th November, 1923; asst. commr. of police, Madras, July, 1930; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1931; confd., Jan., 1938, on l.p.r., June, 1945.

JACKS, WILLIAM HENRY, M.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and mag.) (b. 20th Oct., 1915).—Educ. at La Martiniere Coll., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., Edinburgh Univ., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 23rd Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940; sub. collr., and jt. mag., Oct., 1945.

JACKSON, DONALD, Utke, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 19th May, 1918).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Peterhouse, Cambridge; apptd. on probn., after seln., in May, 1940, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; comdt., civil pioneers, June, 1943; served under

govt. of Assam, Oct., 1944; apptd. on probn. to I.P.S., Apl., 1945; on depn. to Assam govt., Apl. 1945; under training in the N.W.F.P., Oct., 1945; sec. Brit. Legation, Kabul, May, 1946.

JACKSON, EUGENE WENTWORTH DISNEY, O.B.E., M.C., B.A., B.A.I., M.A.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 1st July, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr. Oct., 1926; O.B.E., Feb., 1937; pers. asst. to ch. engr., Sept., 1940 to Apl., 1941; on mil. service, July, 1944 to Nov., 1945.

JACKSON, RIGHT HON. SIR FRANCIS STANLEY, Lieut.-Col., P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E. (b. 21st Nov., 1870).—Govt. of Bengal, March, 1927, to March, 1932.

JACKSON, SIR GILBERT HOLLINSHEAD BLOMFIELD, Kt., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 26th Jan., 1875).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

JACKSON, GORDON NOEL, M.B.E., Indian Police (b. 25th Dec., 1913).—Joined the serv. as proby. asst. supt. of police, Punjab, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935; apptd. to Indian Political Service subs. on probn., May, 1939; personal asst. to resdt., Rajputana, May, 1939; under trng. in Ajmer, Oct., 1939; o.-in-c., civic guards and a.r.p. in Baluchistan, July, 1940; asst. pol. agt., Bahrain, Jan., 1941; ditto, Muscat, May, 1942; pol. agt., Kuwait, June, 1943; under-sec. to resdt., Persian Gulf, Dec., 1943; pol. agt., Kuwait, June, 1944; M.B.E., June, 1945; under sec. to resdt. at Baroda, and for the states of W.I. and Gujarat, Nov., 1945.

JACKSON, HERBERT GUY, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 30th April 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Madras, 1st Oct., 1915; asst. exec. engr., July, 1922; offg. exec. engr., Nov., 1924; confd., Aug., 1926; suptg. engr., Dec., 1941; retd., 1946.

JACKSON, LAURENCE HUNGERFORD, Col., I.A., Survey of India (dir.) (b. 27th Sept., 1889).—First commn. dated 8th Sept., 1909; asst. supt. (proby.), 7th Sept., 1914; in milty. employ. from 18th Sept., 1914, to 18th Dec., 1919; asst. supt., May, 1921; supt., Dec., 1926; offg. dir., June, 1934; dir., Sept., 1938.

JACKSON, REV. LAURENCE SEPTIMUS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Dec., 1933; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1935; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1946.

JACOB, SIR CLAUD WILLIAM, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., Field-Marshal, I.A. (b. 21st Nov., 1863).—Sec., mil. dept., India Office, Apl., 1926, to May, 1930.*

JACOB, KORATHU GEORGE, M.A., Imp. Customs Service (b. 9th May, 1900).—Joined the service, 14th Apl., 1924; asst. collr., Bombay, April, 1926; second secty., central bd. of rev. Oct., 1938; ex officio dep. secty., govt. of India fin. dept. (cent. revs.), Apl., 1940; collr., cent. excise, Madras, Dec., 1943.

JACOB, MAZHUANCHARRY CHAKKA, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 8th May, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Ascam, 9th Nov., 1930; dep. consvr., Nov., 1935; in charge. wkg. plan divn., Shillong, Sept., 1937.

JACOBSON, ERIC JOHN HART, M.B.E., late Indian State Ryis. (b. 23rd March, 1894).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1913; transptn. supt., E.I. Ry., March, 1926; M.B.E., Jan., 1939; dep. ch. opertg. supt., Apl., 1941; divl. supt., Aug., 1941; retd., Nov., 1945.

JAFAR, ALI KHAN G. AGHA (Sind) (b. 6th Nov., 1902).—Joined the serv., 29th Apl., 1928

as supt. of excise, Bombay; under govt. of India from July, 1940; dep. commr., salt and excise, Sind, March, 1944.

JAFAR, IMAM SAIYID (b. 18th Apl., 1900).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, Oct., 1943.

JAFAR, MOHAMMED, O.B.E., M.B., B.Sc., M.R.C.S., D.T.M.&H., D.P.H., Maj., I.M.S. (air- port hth. offr., Karachi) (b. 5th Jul., 1901).—First commn. dated 23rd Oct., 1930; in mil. employ. to July, 1935; in the medical research dept. to Jan., 1937, when he joined the port health dept.; airport health offr., Karachi, Nov., 1937 to Dec., 1943; ch. health offr., Delhi prov., Dec., 1943 to Sept., 1944; dir. public health, Bengal, Sept., 1944; O.B.E., June, 1946.

JAFAREY, MOHAMMAD AON, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 10th Apl., 1893).—First commn. dated 25th Aug., 1917; perm. 25th Feb., 1921; in mil. employ. to Sept. 1928; Burma jail dept. Sept., 1928 to Feb., 1930; supt. jails, U.P. from Feb., 1930; on mil. duty, 8th July, 1941; inspr.-gen. prisons, U.P., 5th Feb., 1946; dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Jan., 1947.

JAFRI, SACHED SAIYID, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 7th Nov., 1916).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., Aligarh Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 21st Oct., 1939; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

JAFRI, ZAFAR HUSAIN, M.B., United Provs. Medl. Serv. (b. May, 1892).—Apptd. 4th Apl., 1918; civil surgeon (confd.) 16th Oct., 1937.

JAGAN NATH, BHAGAT, Rai Bahadur, M.A., LL.B., late Punjab C.S., Judl. bch. (b. 1st Sept., 1886).—Retd., Sept., 1941.

JAGANNATH, JUNEJA LALA, A.M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 27th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service 5th Oct., 1911; asst. engr., Oct., 1912; exec. engr. Oct., 1920; prom. cl. I, Feb., 1945.

JAGANNATHAN, RAGAVIENGAR, M.A., B.L., mil. accts. dept. (b. 26th Feb., 1898).—Joined the service as offcr. in cl. II, Aug., 1923; finl. advr. and sec., Quetta reconstr. cttee. June, 1940; retd., 4th Sept., 1943; prom. cl. I, Feb., 1945.

JAGANNATHAN, SARRUKAI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. sec. finl. dept.) (b. 18th May, 1914).—Educ. at Christian Coll. School, Madras, Madras Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr. to Feb., 1938; sub. divl. offr., Feb., 1938; spec. offr., pol. dept., Apl., 1940; under sec., ditto, May, 1940; addl. dist. mag., Apl., 1942; on spec., duty, Office of A.G., Bombay, May, 1942; attaché, mil. fin. dept., New Delhi, Oct., 1942, attaché supply fin., Nov., 1942; asst. fin. advr., ditto, Jan., 1943; ditto mil. fin. dept., Apl., 1943; dep. sec. co-ordn. cttee. of council, Oct., 1945; dep. sec. exec. council, Jan., 1946; dep. sec. fin. dept., Apl., 1946.

JAGAT NARAYAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 14th Feb., 1911).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Sitapur, Queen's Intermediate Coll., Benares, Thomason Civil Engineering Coll., Roorkee, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1936.

JAGDEO SINGH, GAREWAL, SARDAR, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (supt., civil

vety. dept., Central Provs.) (b. 18th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1918; supt., May, 1919; offg. dir. of civil vety. dept., B. and O., Apl. to Nov., 1930; offg. princpl., Punjab vety. coll., Apl., 1935; offg. dir., vety. services, Central Provs., June, 1937.

JAGDISHWAR DAYAL, M.B.E., M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 14th June, 1903).—Joined the service as officr., cl. II, Apl., 1927; M.B.E., Jan., 1939; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Nov., 1940 and again Oct., 1944.

JAGDISH PRASAD, KUNWAR, SIR, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th June, 1880).—Member, council of state, Apl., 1935 to March, 1940.†

JAGTAP, BALKRISHNA PIRAJIRAO, RAO BAHADUR, L.C.E., M.R.San.I. and C., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 30th June, 1889).—Retd. 30th June, 1942.†

JAI LAL, SIR RAI BAHADUR, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Punjab (b. 17th Aug., 1878).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

JAI RAJ SINGHA, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 19th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., United Provs., on 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1929.

JAIN, LAKSHMI CHANDRA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 30th June, 1910).—Educ. at Christ Church Coll., Cawnpore, Allahabad Univ., Imperial Coll. of Science, London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1936; dir. of civil indus., United Provs., Jan., 1944.

JAKEMAN, ARTHUR REGINALD, Indian Police (dep. commr. Madras) (b. 1st June 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 18th Nov., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; commr. of police and comdt. of state forces, Pudukkottai, Aug., 1932; reverted to Brit. serv., May, 1933; offg. dist. supt., March, 1934; offg. asst. comdt. of police, Aden, June, 1936; reverted, Dec., 1936; confd. as dist. supt., Oct., 1939; on depn. to Assam, Dec., 1942 to Nov., 1944; dep. commr., police, Madras.

JAKUB, SHAH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 2nd Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1920; asst. auditor, O. and R. Rly., Oct., 1924; divl. audit officer, N.W. rly., Jan., 1926; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Jan., 1929; offg. ch. accts. officer, N.W. rly., Sept., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, Nov., 1933.

JAMAL-UD-DIN, M.B., Ch.B., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab) (b. 22nd May, 1891).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1914; civil surg., May, 1925.

JAMES, ANTHONY SAMUEL, O.B.E., M.C., Indian Posts and Tels. (dir. of tels.) (b. 28th Apl., 1891).—Joined the serv. as asst. supt., 18th Dec., 1919; asst. divl. engr. Feb., 1921; div. engr. Mar., 1927; dir., posts and tels., Sind and Baluchistan circle, May, 1933; offg. dir. tel. engrg., Bombay, March, 1934; ditto. Punjab, Apl., 1935; dir. of tels., confd., Aug., 1939; O.B.E., June, 1946.

JAMES, ARTHUR HULBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 12th March, 1893).—Apptd. second-class clerk, India Office, 26th June, 1912, and attd. to stores dept.; on mil. service from Oct., 1915, to Nov., 1917; transfid. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer,

Apl., 1922; higher clerical officer, Apl., 1926 actg. higher exec. offr., Jan., 1940.

JAMES, BASIL REGINALD, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 1st Aug., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Jan., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936; confd., Jan., 1940.

JAMES, CLAUDE HUBERT, Indian Fin. Dept. (b. 3rd Oct., 1882).—Offg. ch. auditor, G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1931.†

JAMES, SIR JOHN FRANCIS WILLIAM, M.A. (Oxon), Barr., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 3rd June, 1879).—Retd., June, 1939.*

JAMES, JOHN FERDINAND, Burma Marine Service (b. 31st July, 1900).—Joined as engr. supt. govt. launches, Sept., 1935; princpl. engr. and ship surveyor June, 1939; on reconstrn. duty July, 1944; civ. aff. serv., Apl., 1945 to Jan., 1946.

JAMES, J. N. A., Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Nov., 1891).—Joined 15th Oct., 1920; traffic supt., B.B. and C.I. Rly., June, 1945.

JAMES, LEWIS EDWARD, Legisl. Dept., Govt. of India (asst. secty.) (b. 3rd May, 1900).—Joined legisl. dept., Nov., 1918; supt., Apl., 1932; asst. secty., Jan., 1938.

JAMES, PETER LAWRENCE SETON, Indian Police (Assam) (b. 4th Apl., 1920).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939; confd., Nov., 1941; asst. pol. offr., Sadiya, May, 1942.

JAMES, REV. RHYS, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 10th Oct., 1889).—Retd., May, 1942.†

JAMES, SIDNEY HAMILTON, Dept. of Industries, Burma (chief inspr. of factories, expl. and mines) (b. 14th Dec., 1891).—Apptd. asst. inspr. of factories, 19th Oct., 1920; inspr., Oct., 1924; offg. ch. inspr., variously from Feb., 1925; confd., Apl., 1936; chief inspr. factrs., expl., and mines, Apl., 1937.

JAMESON, GERALD GREY, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 4th July, 1898).—Joined the service, 19th July, 1916; in mil. employ from 19th July, 1916, to 14th Nov., 1921; asst. supt., Nov., 1921; offg. dist. offr., frontier constabulary, Jan., 1925; supt., Jan., 1927; asst. inspr.-genl. govt. rly. police, Apl., 1940, to Oct., 1943.

JAMESON, WILLIAM JAMES, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bengal) (b. 29th Oct., 1915).—Apptd. on probn., 21st Nov., 1935; confd., Jan., 1939.

JAMUAR, BRAHMDEV PRASAD, Barr., Bihar C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 22nd Sept., 1898).—Offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937.

JAN, MUHAMMAD, (b. 29th June, 1895).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1918; supt. home dept., govt. of India, July, 1940; offg. asst. sec., ditto, Oct., 1945.

JANAKIRAM SINGH, BANAPHAR, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 15th Oct., 1899).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

JANARDHANA RAO, KARASAGOD PATNA-SHETTI, RAO BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 15th March, 1892).—Joined the service, 4th June, 1915; apptd. to I.P. as dist. supt., Madras, Aug., 1932; offg. asst. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1936; dep. commr., crimes branch, Nov., 1936; awarded Indian police medal, July, 1940; supt. (sel. grade),

Aug., 1944; confd. as dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1944.

JANES, RONALD ARTHUR, O.B.E., India Off. (acting acct.-genl.) (b. 28th Sept., 1888).—Educ. at Thanet Coll., Margate, and Taunton Sch.; apptd. 2nd-class clerk, 4th July, 1907; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to Feb., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; acting staff clerk, Apl., 1921; acting higher exec. officr., Apl., 1922; confd., July, 1922; supt. exec. officr., Sept., 1932; actg. asst. acct.-genl., Nov., 1937; assessor of inc. tax, Dec., 1937; confd., as asst. acct.-genl., Jan., 1938; actg. dep. acct.-genl., Jan., 1941; commr. of inc. tax, Feb., 1941; O.B.E., June, 1945; actg. acct.-genl., Nov., 1945.

JANG, RANA LOKE SHAMSHER, (see Rana, L. S. J.).

JANVRIN, JULIUS VYVIAN BREESE, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 16th July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 7th Dec., 1922; offg. addl. supt., March, 1929; offg. supt., Jan., 1930; awarded Indian police medal, 1932; confd., Feb., 1934; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, May, 1940, to Nov., 1943; Retd., Apl., 1945.

JÂQUES, COLIN CAMPBELL, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 11th June, 1905).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1925; in training to Oct., 1927; proby. asst. supt., Oct., 1927; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. supt., rly. police, Feb., 1937; supt. of police, confd., 1938; asst. to the I.G. police; addtl. supt. police, K. & J. hills; asst. pol. officr., Khan states, Feb., 1940; asst. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1940; again supt., May, 1945.

JARDINE, LIONEL WESTROPP, C.I.E., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 15th Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1921; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1921, and served in United Provs. as asst. commr.; city mag., Nov., 1923; pol. officer on probn., Jan., 1925; asst. commr., Nowshera, Nov., 1926; ditto, Mardan, Aug., 1927; offg. dep. commr., Peshawar, May, 1928; pol. agt., Khyber, Nov., 1928; sec. to ch. commr., N.W.F.P., May, 1930; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1930; 1st asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, May, 1931; minister, Kashmir, Feb., 1932; pol. agt., Bundelkhand, Apl., 1934; officr. on spec. duty, Peshawar, Feb., 1936; dep. commr., Peshawar, March, 1936; offg. rev. and divl. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1936; again dep. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1936; rev. and divl. commr., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; resdt. for Baroda and Gujarat states, Feb., 1943; pol. agt., Jaipur, Nov., 1945; offg. resdt. cl. II and rev. and judl. commr., Baln, Jan., 1946; offg. resdt. cl. II and rev. and divl. commr., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1946.

JASPER, LESLIE DURHAM, India Office (princl.) (b. 22nd Feb., 1914).—Apptd. acting princpl. 4th Mar., 1946.

JATAR, NILKANTH SHIRIAM, SIR, C.I.E., D.S.O., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut-Col., retd., late I.M.S., Jail Dept., Central Provs. (inspr.-genl.) (b. 26th May, 1887).—In milty. employ prior to 21st Jan., 1922; supt., central jail, Nagpur, Jan., 1922; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Feb., 1930; confd., Feb., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1938; retd., and re-empd. from July, 1942; retd., Nov., 1945; kt. Jan., 1946.

JATHAR, GANESH BHASKAR, B.A. (Cantab.), M.A. (Edin.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 10th July, 1887).—Retd., 10th July, 1942.†

JAYAKAR, MUKUND RAMRAO, P.C., M.A., LL.B. (b. 13th Nov., 1878). Member, Bombay legis. cl., 1923; member, legis. assembly, 1928; delegate, Indian R.T.C., 1930-1933; judge, federal court of India, Oct., 1937, to Feb., 1939; P.C., Feb., 1939; member of judl. committee of privy cl., Feb., 1939 to Apl., 1941.

JAYAKAR, MOTABHAI SUNDARRAO, C.I.E., M.A., late Bombay C.S. (b. 1st Dec., 1884).—Retd. Dec., 1939.†

JAYARATNAM, THOMAS COOKE SAMUEL, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 10th Oct., 1893).—Educ. at London Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 20th Feb., 1919; under-secty., govt. of Central Provs. in addition, July, 1923; under-secty., govt. of India, home dept., June, 1924; dep. commr., Aug., 1927; jt. secty., Indian franchise cttee., Jan., 1932; offg. dep. sec., dept. of industries and lab., govt. of India, July, 1932, and again Apl., 1933; offg. commr. of settlements and dir. of land records, etc., Central Provs., Oct., 1933; confd., June, 1934; commr. of settlements, to March, 1937; chairman, textile enq. cttee., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; offg. ch. sec., March, 1942; C.S.I., Jan., 1945; advr. to govr., Dec., 1945 to Jan., 1946.

JAYASANKAR, S. Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 31st Oct., 1904).—Joined the service, 21st Mar., 1928; asst. finl. advr. milty. fin., Oct., 1939; dep. fin. advr., ditto, Mar., 1942.

JAYAVANT, ANANT RAMKRISHNA, Indian Police (b. 20th June, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 20th Feb., 1932; confd., Nov., 1933.

JEEJEEBHOY, F., Chief Justice, Court of Small Causes, Rangoon.—Offg., May, 1937; confd., Jan., 1938; legal advr. to govt. of Burma in India, 1945-6.

JEFFCOCK, JOHN PARKYN, A.C.G.I., M.I.E.E., A.F.R.Ae.S., (Civil Aviation Directorate) (b. 17th Feb., 1910).—Apptd. dir. of communs., Jan., 1946; on loan from Min. of Civ. Aviation, London.

JEFFERIS, CHARLES EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 1st Dec., 1885).—Retd., Dec., 1940.

JEHANGIR, SIR COWASJI, Bart., G.B.E., K.C.I.E.—Temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, 8th Dec., 1912, to 12th July, 1922; confd., 23rd June, 1923; O.B.E., Jan., 1918; K.C.I.E., June, 1927; retd., June, 1928; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd session, Nov., 1932; member, legis. assembly, Jan., 1931; member of Ind. delegation to monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; member, Ind. res. bank cttee., July, 1931; member, nat. def. council, July, 1941; G.B.E., Jan., 1944.

JELLY, JOHN STEUART OSBORNE, B.A., Maj., R.E., Survey of India (b. 27th Oct., 1906).—First commn., Oct., 1926; in mil. employ. to Apl., 1931; proby. asst. supt., Apl., 1931; confd. Apl., 1934; supt., Mar., 1940; in mil. employ. from May, 1940 to Jan., 1942.

JENKIN, JAMES LESLIE, O.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (acting controller, store dept.) (b. 23rd Aug., 1888).—Educ. at King Edward VI. Gr. Sch., Grantham; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 10th June, 1907; transfed. to office of high commissioner for India, Oct., 1920; asst. controller, store dept., Apl., 1921; princpl.

exec. offcr., 1922; actg. contr., Jan., 1940; O.B.E., June, 1946.

JENKIN, REGINALD TREVOR, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (Central Provs.) (b. 25th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1912; asst. postal censor, Bombay, from 27th Apr., 1915; dep. consvr., Sept., 1919; offg. consvr., variously from May, 1931; ret'd., March, 1942.

JENKIN, WILLIAM NORMAN PRENTICE, C.S.I., C.I.E., Ind. Police (dep. inspr.-gen. Punjab) (b. 11th Aug., 1899).—Joined the serv. as asst. supt., Punjab, on 27th Dec., 1919; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1925; offg. supt., Apr., 1927; confd., May, 1930; C.I.E., June, 1931; cent. intell. offcr., Punjab, Delhi and Sind, Nov., 1935; dep. dir., intell. bureau, March, 1939; confd. Apr., 1941; C.S.I., Jan., 1946; dep. inspr.-gen., Punjab, May, 1946.

JENKINS, SIR EVAN MEREDITH, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., Indian C.S. (govr. Punjab) (b. 2nd Feb., 1896).—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the serv., 25th Oct., 1920; arrvd., 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1925; ditto (prov.) Oct., 1927; ditto (perm.) Jan., 1932; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Nov., 1933; offg. jt. secty., ditto, June, 1935, and again, Jan., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1936; ch. commr., Delhi, March, 1937; secty., supply dept., govt. of India, July 1940; secty. to Viceroy and secty. to govr.-genl. (personal), Oct., 1943; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1944; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1945; govr., Punjab, Apr., 1946.

JENKINS, JOHN LEWIS, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 8th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 27th Jan., 1920; addl. supt., Apr., 1926; supt., Nov., 1929; asst. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1934, to March, 1938; dep. inspr.-genl. (confd.), June, 1944; Indian police medal, 1939.

JENKINS, WALTER ALLEN, C.I.E., D.Sc. (Sheffield), Indian Educ. Service (dir., public instrn.) (b. 1st Apr., 1891).—Joined the service as prof. of Dacca Coll. on 2nd March, 1916; services placed at disposal of Univ. of Lacca from July, 1921, to July, 1926; offg. inspr. of schools, Dec., 1926; confd., Jan., 1927; offg. asst. dir., public instrn., Jan., 1931; princpl. Rajshahi Coll., Mar. 1932; offg. special offcr. for primary educn., March, 1933; princpl., David Hare training coll., Nov., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1942; dir., public instrn., March, 1943; spec. offr. fin. dept., Aug., 1944; on depn., Aug., 1944.

JENKINS, SIR WILLIAM JOHN, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dir. of agric., Poona) (b. 27th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; dep. secty., Indian central cotton cttee.; offg. dir. of institute of plant industry, Indore, May to Nov., 1927; dep. dir. of agric., Sind, June, 1930; offg. dep. dir., Bombay, Nov., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1939; dir. of agric., Poona, July, 1939; held in addn. post of dir., vet. services, Jan. to March, 1944; kt. June, 1945. agric. comr. (temp.) Jan., 1946.

JENNER, ERIC JOHN, M.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 11th Feb., 1910).—Joined the service as proby., asst. supt., Bombay, 25th Nov. 1930; confd., July, 1932; supt., confd., Oct., 1941; M.B.E., June, 1943; dep. commr., C.I.D., Sept., 1943 to Jan., 1946; Indian police medal.

JENNETT, STANLEY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr.) (b. 6th Aug., 1904).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1928; confd., Sept., 1930; under-secty., govt. of Punjab, p.w.d., irrign., Oct., 1942 to July, 1945.

JENNEY, REGINALD CHARLES NAPIER, B.A., Maj., R.E., Survey of India (b. 24th Jan., 1906).—First commn. 3rd Feb., 1926; in mil. employ. to 1st Jan., 1930; proby. asst. supt., Jan., 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; supt., May, 1939; in mil. employ., Aug., 1940 to Dec., 1942.

JENNINGS, ALFRED KICKWEED, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 30th March, 1890).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 2nd Feb., 1914; on mil. service from June, 1915, to June, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. offcr., Apr., 1922; higher clerical offcr., May, 1926; actg. higher exec. offcr., Oct., 1939.

JENNINGS, EDGAR, Indian Police (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 18th March, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 19th Feb., 1921; dist. supt., March, 1929; civ. aff. serv. to Jan., 1946; served with fron. areas dept., Jan., 1946; dep. commr., 1946.

JEPHSON, MAURICE KEARLEY, O.B.E., Ind. Office (hghr. exec. offr., mil. dept.) (b. 2nd Apr., 1893).—Apptd. sec.-class clk., 26th Feb., 1912, and attd. to registry and record dept., transf. to rev. and statistics dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from March, 1917, to Sept., 1919; exec. offcr., Apr., 1922; actg. higher exec. offcr., June, 1924, to June, 1925; confd., Aug., 1927; actg. suptg. exec. offcr., June, 1940; editor, India Office and Burma Office List, March to Nov., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1946; actg. ch. exec. offr. milty. dept., Jan., 1946.

JERATH, HARBANS LAL, B.A., M.S.P., F.R.E.S., Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 29th July, 1899).—Joined the service as proby. supt., Dec., 1919; confd., Aug., 1922; dir. posts and tels., Karachi, Feb., 1945.

JERROM, JOHN HENLEY GRIMLE, M.V.C.V.S., late Indian Veterinary Service (Sind) (b. 6th December, 1886).—Joined the service as supt. civil veterinary dept., Sind, March, 1921; offg. dir., vety. services, Bombay, Apr., 1933; dir., vety. services, Sind, Apr., 1936; ret'd., 1946.

JESSON, ARTHUR WILLIAM MOSLEY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 12th Aug., 1884).—Ret'd., Aug., 1939.*

JETLEY, BRIJ BHUSHAN SHARAN (see Brij, B. S. J.).

JHA, ADITYA NATH, M.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Aug., 1911).—Educ. at Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Oct., 1936; apptd. to Ind. pol. serv. (subst.), Nov., 1939; under-secty. to resdt. for E. States, Nov., 1939.

JHA, AMAR NATH, M.A., United Provs. Educ. Service, Cl. I (b. 25th Feb., 1897).—Joined the service as prof., Muir Cent. Coll., Alld., 1st Aug., 1917; on for. serv. with Alld. Univ. from July, 1922; apptd. vice-chancellor, Allahabad Univ., Dec., 1938; on depn. as member Ind. natl. war memorial cttee., Sept., to Dec., 1945.

JHA, BHOLA NATH, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr. United Provs.) (b. 23rd Feb., 1902).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Exeter Coll.,

Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, 27th Nov., 1926, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., 27th Oct., 1929; secty., bd. of rev., Dec., 1937; dep. secty., agric. and rural devel., Dec., 1939; mag. and collr. (confid.), Feb., 1940; rev. sec., Feb., 1942; C.I.E., June, 1946.

JHA, CHANDRA MOHAN, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 11th Feb., 1898).—Apptd. dep. supt. of police, 1st Aug., 1922; to selection grade of dep. supt. of police, 1st July, 1937; offg. supt., 12th June, 1938; confid., 11th Nov., 1938.

JHA, CHANDRA SHEKHAR, O.B.E., M.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. sec. govt. of India, commonwealth relations dept.) (b. 20th October, 1909).—Educated at the Watson High English Sch., Madhubani, Patna Univ., and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 3rd Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., fin. dept., Bihar, Oct., 1936; dep. commr., Apl., 1939; contr. and sec., supply and transp., Apl., 1943; O.B.E., Jan., 1945; dep. sec. govt. of India, commonwealth relations dept., Mar., 1946.

JHA, LAKSHMI KANT, M.B.E., B.A., Ind. C.S. (dep. sec. govt. of India, commn. dept.) (b. 22nd Nov., 1913).—Educ. at Barari H.E. Sch., Bhagalpur, Benares Univ. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the serv., 20th Sept., 1937; arr. in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and collr., May, 1940; under-sec., local self govt. dept., Nov., 1940; ditto, govt. of India, supply dept., June, 1942; fin. and comm. dept., May, 1944; dep. sec., supply dept., Sept., 1944; M.B.E., Jan., 1946; dep. sec., commn., dept., May, 1946.

JHUNNA SINGH, M.B.E., B.Sc., Indian For. Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 31st July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 2nd Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926; dir., fuel circle, Punjab, March, 1944; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

JIA RAM, Indian Police (b. 21st Nov., 1912).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Feb., 1937.

JIVA NAND PANDE, B.A., Indian Police (b. 19th Dec., 1883).—Retd., Dec., 1938.*

JIWAN AMRIK SINGH, B.A., Dip. of Forestry, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 10th July, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

JOG, DATTATRAYA PARASHURAM (see Parashuram Jog, D.).

JOGENDRA NATH MANDAL, HON.—Apptd. legislative member of govt.-genl.'s council, Oct., 1946.

JOGENDRA SINGH, SIRDAR SIR, K.C.S.I., (b. 25th May, 1877).—Minister of Agric., United Provs., 1926-37; served on Indian sugar cte., Ind. taxation enq. comm., and Skeen cte., Ind. army indianisation cte., member of govr.-genl.'s exec. council, educn., health and lands, July, 1942 to July, 1946; K.C.S.I., June, 1946.

JOHN, JORDAN CONSTANTINE, O.B.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar (civil surg.) (b. 22nd Sept., 1886).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1912; O.B.E., June, 1919; in mil. employ up to 30th Apl., 1923; civil surg., Sept., 1924; ret'd. and re-empld., 22nd Sept., 1943.

JOHN, RICHARD STANLEY THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Feb., 1909).—Educ. at Haverfordwest Gr. Sch., University Coll.,

Cardiff, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1934; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Dec., 1936; asst. pol. agt., Chitral, Feb., 1937; under-secty. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, June, 1938; vice-consul, Bushire, Feb., 1939.

JOHNSON, ERIC ALEXANDER, Burma Front. Serv. (b. 27th June, 1913).—Apptd., 30th Dec., 1936.

JOHNSON, FRANK, India Office (higher clerical offcr., mil. dept.) (b. 10th Dec., 1883).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 17th Oct., 1921; acting higher ditto, Sept., 1933; confid., Dec., 1934.

JOHNSON, JAMES RUSSELL, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 15th May, 1891).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 22nd May, 1911; dep. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1913; proby. asst. supt. of police, March, 1920; confid., June, 1921; asst. supt., May, 1920; addl. supt., Apl., 1921; offg. supt., Apl., 1924; confid., Aug., 1926; supt., rly. police, Oct., 1939, to Oct., 1944.

JOHNSON, SIR JOHN NESBITT GORDON, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th Feb., 1885).—Retd., July, 1939.†

JOHNSON, LESLIE JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 8th Sept., 1914).—Educ. at St. George's Coll., Mussoorie, Punjab Univ., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 3rd Oct., 1938; confid., 8th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

JOHNSON, NORMAN, Indian State Rlys. (b. 31st July, 1894).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1920; asst. rolling stock offr., Dec., 1924; asst. works manager, Nov., 1927; asst. loco. supt., Nov., 1932; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., March, 1933; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., March, 1933; confid., Dec., 1934; ch. exec. offr., Burma rlys., Aug., 1942; dep. dir.-genl. (munitions), supply dept., Aug., 1943.

JOHNSON, STANLEY MERRY, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (bridge engr.) (b. 5th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1923; asst. bridge engr., July, 1928; exec. engr. (bridges), N.W. rly., March, 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), N.W. rly., March, 1936; (provl.) Apl., 1940.

JOHNSON, DAVID PERCIVAL, A.R.C.Sc.I. late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 23rd Jan., 1890).—Retd., Feb., 1940.†

JOHNSTON, DAVID SETON, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 7th Aug., 1886).—Retd., Sept. 1942.†

JOHNSTON, SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Nov., 1872).—Retd., March, 1931.*

JOHNSTON, REV. CANON GEORGE FREDERICK, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Sind) (b. 17th Apl., 1884).—Retd., June, 1938.*

JOHNSTON, JAMES HALCRO, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 8th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1914; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; ret'd., Dec., 1941.

JOHNSTON, JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 13th July, 1904).—Educ. at Anderson Educational Institute, Lerwick, Univ. of Edinburgh, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March,

1932; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1933; confd., Apl., 1941.

JOHNSTON, JOHN PIM WATSON, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 20th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th March, 1921; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., June, 1938; O.B.E., June, 1938.

JOHNSTON, MALCOLM KENNETH, C.I.E., M.B.E., Ind. Police (asst. dir., intell. bureau) (b. 22nd Apl., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 17th Nov., 1926; offg. supt., Jan., 1933, and again, May, 1933; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1936; awarded King's police medal, 1938; asst. dir., intell. bureau, home dept., March, 1938; supt., confd., May, 1940; M.B.E., Jan., 1941; dep. dir. intell. bureau, Nov., 1945; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

JOHNSTON, ROBERT LENG, late Indian Police (v. 4th March, 1888).—Retd., July, 1939.*

JOHNSTON, RONALD HILARY GRAVDS, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 26th Jan., 1898).—Educ. at Aldenham Sch.; joined the service, 4th Sept., 1923; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1929; services placed at disposal of govt. of Bengal, Apl., 1935; mag. and collr., Feb., 1938.

JOHNSTON, WILFRID, M.C., B.A., Burma Office (acting asst. secty., Burma office) (b. 9th Oct., 1892).—Educ. at Methodist Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (scholar); war service—served in R.F.A., Nov., 1915, to Apl., 1919 (M.C., Sept., 1917); apptd. junr. clerk, 20th Jan., 1920, and attd. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept., India Office; asst. princpl., Apl., 1921; act. princpl. clerk, Jan. to March, 1922; resdt. clerk, March, 1922; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state for India, Sept., 1924; actg. princpl., Aug., 1927; confd., June, 1929; actg. asst. secty., Burma Office, Feb., 1939.
