LA BOUCARDIERE, MAURICE PRINCE, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 3rd Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 22nd March, 1920; offg. supt., Apl., 1923; confd., Nov., 1927; additional labour officer, Bombay, March, 1939 to Nov., 1942; King's police medal, June, 1942; confd., supt. (sel. grade) March, 1944; d.i.g. of police, southern range, May, 1945; dist. supt., May, 1946.

LACAMP, ADRIAN. Survey of India (asst.

supt.) (b. 19th Nov., 1893).—Joined the service, 19th Jan., 1923; asst. supt., mathematical instruments office, March, 1926.

LACEY, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th Dec., 1889).—Retd., July, 1939.*

LACEY, GERALD, C.I.E., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engineers (United Provs.) (b. 26th July, 1887).—Retd., 25th July, 1942.†

LACEY, WALTER GRAHAM, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr. Bihar) (b. 17th July, 1894).—Educ. at Norwich and Bedford Gr. Schs., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 1st Dec., 1919; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; ditto and under-sec. to govt., finl. dept., May, 1922; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925, and March, 1926; addl. judl. commr., Feb., 1926; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1926; offg. secty., finl. dept., Apr., 1930; offg. supt. of census, Nov., 1930; offg. secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Nov., 1933; mag. and collr. (prov. subs.), Aug., 1933; confd., Sept., 1935; offg. secty. to govt., finl. dept., May, 1935; secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Oct., 1935; addl. secty. to govt., pol. and appts. dept., Bihar, Apr., 1936; dep. commr., March, 1938; secty. to H.E. the Govt. of Bihar, Apr., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1939; advr. to govr. of Bihar, Nov. 1945; commr., Jan., 1946.

LACHHMAN PRASAD BHARGAV, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 15th Oct., 1927; confd., Oct., 1937.

LAD, PURUSHOTTAM MANGESH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 29th March, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1936; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1946.

LADLI PERSHAD, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., Commerce Dept., Govt. of India (b. 12th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service 26th Aug., 1913; supt. dept. of commerce, June, 1925; asst. secty. ditto, May, 1932; dep. dir. comml. intell., Sept., 1940; dep. ch. contr., imports, May, 1943; jt. ch. contr. imports, Mar., 1946.

LAHARRY, N., Indian State Ry's. (b. 27th June, 1892).—Joined 1st Mar., 1921; commnl., supt. B.N. Ry., July, 1945.

LAHIRI, BISHWA NATH (see Bishwa, N. L.).

LAHIRI, KUMAR, B.E. (Eng.), M.I.M.C.E., A.M.I.S.E., A.M.I.C.E., (b. 10th Dec., 1909).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1941; engr.-in-ch., lighthouse dept., and ch. insp. lighthouses in Brit. India, Jan., 1946.

LAHIRI, SACHCHIDANANDA, Rai Bahadur, Indian Audit Dept. (b. Oct., 1889).—Joined the service 5th Apr., 1909; asst. acct.-genl., Central revenues, May, 1936; dep. ditto, Bihar, Oct., 1936; Rai Bahadur, June, 1940; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Apr., 1941.

LAINE, SIR ABRAHAM JAMES, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 26th Aug., 1876).—Retd., Dec., 1935.*

LAITHWAITE, SIR JOHN GILBERT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., Burma Office (dep. under sec. of State for Burma) (b. 5th July, 1894).—Educ. at Clongowes and Trinity Coll., Oxford; served in France with 10th Lanc. Fus., May, 1917, to July, 1918; specially employed, War Office, March to Sept., 1919; apptd. junr. clerk, mil. dept., 16th July, 1920, after previous service

as temp. junr. clerk since 1st Oct., 1919; asst. principal, Apr., 1921; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, March, 1922; on depn. to India with Lord Winterton, Aug. to Nov., 1922; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1923; principal, services and genl. dept., Sept., 1924; pol. dept., May, 1927; personally attached to prime minister for Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept., Dec., 1931; secty., Indian Franchise cottage, Jan.-June, 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; secty., Ind. delimitn. ctee., Aug., 1935 to Jan., 1936; pte. sec. to Viceroy, Apr., 1936 to Oct., 1943; secty. to govr.-genl. (in addn.), Apr., 1937 to Oct., 1943; C.S.I., Jan., 1938; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1941; acting asst. under sec. of state, India Office, Oct., 1943; under sec. (civil) to the War Cabinet, Jan., 1944-Sept., 1945; sec. British Commonwealth Meeting, London, Apr., 1945; dep. under sec. of state for Burma, Oct., 1945.

LAKHPAT RAI, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 8th July, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Feb., 1928.

LAKSHMANAN, CHERUVARI KOTTIETH, L.M. & S., M.R.C.S., D.T.M. & H., D.P.H., Maj., I.M.S. (chief health offcr., Delhi) (b. 5th Apr., 1898).—First commn. dated 30th Apr., 1928; in milty. employ prior to 16th Dec., 1935, when he joined the port health dept.; dep. public health commr. with the govt. of India, March, 1939; ch. health offcr., Delhi, May, 1941; D.P.H., Bengal, Nov., 1943; addl. dir. of A.I.I. of P.H., Sept., 1944.

LAKSHMAN SARUP GUPTA, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 26th Oct., 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

LAKSHMANA RAO, SIR KASARAGOD PATANASHETTI, Diwan Bahadur, Madras C.S. (Judicial Branch) (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 15th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. prof., law coll., Madras, 28th Aug., 1916; prof., ditto, Oct., 1920; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1921; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, variously from Aug., 1930; addl. ditto, Jan., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935; kt., June, 1946.

LAL, BANWARI (see Banwari Lal).

LAL, BASANT (see Basant Lal).

LAL, BEHARI (see Behari Lal).

LAL, BIPEN BEHARI, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag.) (b. 30th Jan., 1917).—Educ. at Delhi and Allahabad Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in United Provs. as asst. mag.

LAL, CHAMPA (see Champa Lal).

LAL, CHHUTTAN (see Chhuttan Lal).

LAL, CHUNI (see Chuni Lal).

LAL, DONALD CHANDU (see Chandu Lal, D.).

LAL, DHAWAN PURUSHOTTAM (see Dhawan Purushottam Lal).

LAL, HARBANS (see Harbans Lal).

LAL, HARIVANSH (see Harivansh Lal).

LAL HUSSAIN, KHAN MIAN SULTAN, B.A. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Joined the service, 14th April, 1917; sub. divl. offr., July, 1930; dep. commr. (perm.) May, 1942.

LAL, JAI (see Jai Lal).

LAL, KUNDAN (see Kundan Lal).

LAL, KUNK BEHAR MOHAN (see Mohan Lal, K.B.).

LAL, MOHAN, BATRA (see Mohan Lal, Batra).

LAL, MOTI (see Moti Lal).

LAL, MUTSADDI (see Mutsaddi Lal).

LAL, PANNA (see Panna Lal).

LAL, RAM (see Ram Lal).

LAL, RANG BEHARI (see Rang Behari Lal).

LAL, RANGI (see Rangi Lal).

LAL, SARDARI (see Sardari Lal).

LAL, SHADI (see Shadi Lal).

LAL SHAH BUKHARI, KHAN SAHIB, SAYAD, B.A. (Hons.), M.A. (b. 13th Jan., 1906).—Appdt. to Punjab C.S., Mar., 1934; Indian vice-consul, Jeddah, Otc., 1937 to Dec., 1942; exec. offr., municl. cttee., Sialkot, March, 1943; haji. offr. govt. of India, commonwealth relations dept., 2nd June, 1945; Khan Sahib, June, 1942.

LAL, SHAM (see Sham Lal).

LAL, SHAVAX ARDESHIR, C.I.E., M.A., LL.B. (b. Nov., 1899).—Joined the service, June, 1930; 2nd class sub. judge, Bombay, June, 1930, to Jan., 1933; legal dept., Bombay, Jan., 1933, to Apl., 1936; dep. secty., legislative dept., Apl., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; jt. sec., legis. dept., Aug., 1943.

LAL SINGH, Sardar Bahadur Sardar, B.Sc. (Hons.), M.Sc., (b. 16th Mar., 1896).—Joined July, 1926; appdt. Punjab agric. serv., cl. I, Sept., 1931; appdt. fruit devel. advr. to govt. of India, Sept., 1944; depn. to Australia, Feb., 1946.

LALKAKA, MINOCHAR DORABJI, B.A., LL.B., Barr., Judicial Dept., Bombay (b. 3rd Aug., 1894).—Appdt. judge, small cause ct., Bombay, 15th Apl., 1929.

LALL (see also under Lal).

LALL, A. S., M.B.E., I.C.S.—Appdt. addl. dep. sec., govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1944.

LALL, ARTHUR SAMUEL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 14th July, 1911); educ. at Central Model Sch., Lahore, Univ. of the Punjab, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offcr., May, 1937; under-secty., pol. dept., Punjab, Apl., 1940; offcr. on spl. duty, fin. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1941; attaché, fin. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1942; dep. secty., fin., Apl., 1944.

LALL, JANARDHAN SHANKAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Sept., 1914).—Educ. at Central Model Sch., Lahore, Punjab Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; appdt. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

LALL, KRISHEN BEHARI, M.A., Indian C.S. (under sec., govt. of India, commerce dept.) (b. 30th May, 1915).—Educ. at A.S.V.J. High School, Delhi, Delhi University and London School of Oriental Studies; appdt. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 6th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938; and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; dep. sec., food dept., 1944; under sec. govt. of India, commerce dept., Oct., 1945.

LALL, SHAMALDHARI (see Shamaldhari Lall).
LALLUBHAI, MANEKLAL, Rao Bahadur, O.B.E., late Salt Dept., Bombay (b. 9th Nov., 1879).—Retd., June, 1939.*

LALVANI, LALCHAND VARUMAL, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 14th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1921; offg. exec. engr., May, 1926; confd., Oct., 1930.

LAMARQUE, WALTER GEOFFREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 12th Feb., 1913).—Educ. at Marl-

borough and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Dec., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1940; under-secty., govt. of India, dept. of labour, March, 1941; supply dept., June, 1944.

LAMB, GEORGE HAMISH, Indian State Rlys. (works manager, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 15th March, 1887).—Joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1921; production engr., G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1929; asst. works manager, G.I.P. rly., Feb., 1931; works manager, Oct., 1932.

LAMB, JOHN CHARLES, Indian State Rlys. (mech. engr. and transportation (power) supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 27th Oct., 1899).—Asst. transportation supt., G.I.P. Rly., Jan., 1926; transportation supt., senior scale, provl., E.I. rly., Nov., 1938; confd., Aug., 1939.

LAMBERT, D. P., Maj., I.M.S. (civ. surg.) (b. May 25, 1902).—Date of first commn., Oct. 15, 1926; civil surg., United Provs., Aug., 1934.

LAMBERT, ERIC THOMAS DRUMMOND, O.B.E., Indian Police (b. 3rd Nov., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Assam, 25th Nov., 1929; asst. pol. offcr., from Dec., 1939; pol. offr. from Mar. to Aug., 1942; cent. intell. offr., Aug., 1942; King's police and fire serv. Medals, Jan., 1942; O.B.E., Apl., 1946.

LAMBERT, E. W., M.A., Barr., Burma Judl. Service (govt. advocate) (b. 7th Apl., 1890).—Appdt. offg. asst. govt. advocate, Burma, 25th March, 1926; confd., June, 1934; govt. advocate, May, 1937; dir., crown office, Burma, Nov., 1945.

LAMBERT, OSCAR WILFRED, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. offcr., accis. dept.) (b. 2nd March, 1894).—Appdt. clerical officer, 8th July, 1923, after previous service from 7th Oct., 1919; actg. exec. offcr., June, 1931; confd., Apl., 1933.

LAMBRICK, HUGH TREVOR, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 20th Apl., 1904).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. asst. commr. in Sind, Apl., 1931; tempy. dep. secty. to govt., pol. and reforms dept., Bombay, March, 1935; offg. collr. June, 1936; confd., Dec., 1936; supt., census operations, Sind, Oct., 1939; spec. commr. for Sind, Karachi, June, 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

LAMECH, G. S., Rao Bahadur, B.Sc., Burma Serv. of Engrs. (b. 5th Dec., 1899).—Joined as engr. P.W.D., Oct., 1921; asst. engr. Mar., 1923; on mil. service, July, 1945 to Jan., 1946.

LANCASTER, HAROLD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 30th June, 1883).—Appdt. colliery supt., 1st July, 1922; ret'd., Apl., 1939.*

LANDER, PERCY EDWARD, M.A. (Cantab.), D.Sc. (London), A.I.C., late Dept. of Agric., Punjab (b. 8th May, 1889).—Dept. dir. of agric., 4th Dec., 1920; offg. agricl. chemist to govt., March, 1921; confd., Dec., 1923; ret'd.,

LANE, A. S., M.B.E., B.Sc., Dept. of Industries and Labour, Govt. of India (b. 22nd May, 1900).—Appdt. aircraft and engine inspr. on the staff of the dir. of civil aviation in India, 29th March, 1929; M.B.E., Jan., 1935; ret'd.

LANE, BASIL WILLIAM, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 5th Aug., 1905).—Joined the service, 13th Jan., 1926; offg. dist. supt., Dec., 1932; and again, March, 1933; confd., July, 1939; dep. commr., traffic and licensing, Madras, June, 1941.

LANE, HERBERT ALLARDYCE, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Aug., 1883).—Retd., May, 1940.†

LANE, HUGH THOMPSON, M.B.E., B.A., Ind. C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 26th Apl., 1914).—Educ. at Rossall Sch. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the serv. 24th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

LANE, RICHARD GEORGE McLAREN, late Indian Police (b. 29th June, 1902).—(Bihar and Orissa).—Retd., June, 1938.*

LANG, LIONEL EDWARD, C.I.E., M.C., Lieut.Col., I.A., late Indian Political Service (b. 1st Nov., 1885).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

LANG, WILLIAM GORDON, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bombay) (b. 23rd Nov., 1916).—Apptd. on probn., 27th Oct., 1936; confd., Sept., 1938.

LANG-ANDERSON, Col. W. G., O.B.E., R.E. (ch. engr. and sec. govt. N.W.F. Prov., P.W.D.).—After prev. serv. in Army apptd. exec. engr. from Jan., 1934 to Mar., 1938; suptg. engr., Mar. 1938 to Nov., 1940; reverted to mil. duty Nov., 1940 to Feb., 1945; ch. engr. and sec. govt. N.W.F. Prov., P.W.D., Jan., 1946.

LANGDON, FREDERICK GUY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 5th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th June, 1911; offg. resdt. engr., Nov., 1914; confd., Sept., 1919; divl. engr., Dec., 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr., contrn., May, 1935; and variously from May, 1936; provl., Nov., 1937; confd., May, 1939; chief ditto, provl., May, 1941.

LANGFORD, JOHN BRIAN, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (b. 21st June, 1907).—Educ. at City and County School, Chester, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs., as asst., mag. and collr.; dep.-secty. rev. dept., United Provs. (temp.), Apl., 1937; dep. sec. dept. of supply, Jan., 1942; O.B.E., June, 1944; offg. jt. sec., May to July, 1944; and again Aug. to Nov., 1944; member of Hydara mission to U.K., Jan. to Mar., 1945; dep. sec. supply, Apl., 1945; joint sec. ditto, Aug., 1945.

LAO HTIN WAH, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 26th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 30th Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., March, 1932.

LARKIN, ALFRED SLOANE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commissioner, Bengal) (b. 11th June, 1894).—Educ. at High Sch., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. under reconstruction scheme; arrived, 2nd May, 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. mag. and collr., March, 1926; offg. ditto, March, 1928; confd., Nov., 1930; offg. commr. of excise and salt, May, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; div. commr. (confd.), Dec., 1945.

LARMOUR, EDWARD NOEL, Burma C.S. (cl. I) (b. 25th Dec., 1916).—Apptd. asst. commr., 8th Apl., 1942; mil. trng., Sept. 1942 to Jan., 1943; civ. aff. serv., Nov., 1944; sub. divl. offr., Feb., 1945; under sec. (home and judl.) Mar., 1946.

LARWILL, REV. GUTHRIE JAMES, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 26th Dec., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 24th Oct., 1926; junior chaplain, Oct., 1928; snr. chaplain, Oct., 1938; retd., May, 1945.

LASRADO, EDWARD ALOYSIUS, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 21st June, 1902).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1926 dep. consvr., Nov., 1930; senior timber supply offr., Sept., 1942; dep. commr., civil supplies (fuel), Jan., 1946.

LASRADO, JOSEPH PATRICK, Indian C.S. (name changed to SHENOY, J. P. L. [q.v.]).

LATHAM, HUGH DALLAS, O.B.E., Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 5th Feb., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 5th Nov., 1925; offg. dist. supt., Nov., 1931; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1939; confd. as dist. supt., March, 1938; O.B.E., June, 1942.

LATHAM, KENNETH REGINALD, Burma C.S. (cl. I) (b. 6th Jan., 1917).—Educ. at Repton Sch.; apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

LATIFI, ALMA, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., LL.M. (Cantab.); LL.D. (Dublin), Barr. (1902), late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 12th Nov., 1879).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

LATIF-UR-RAHMAN, NAWABZADA, A.S.M., M.A. (Cantab.), Bengal Judl. Dept. (chief judge, Calcutta small cause ct.).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1918; judge, Calcutta small cause ct., Oct., 1920; chief judge, ditto, 1937; judge, Calcutta high ct., Apl.-Aug., 1944; and again, Apl. to Aug., 1946.

LATIMER, COURTENAY ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 13th July, 1911).—Educ. at Rugby and Christ Church Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; apptd. subs. on probn. to Indian political service, Jan., 1939; on foreign service as guardian to H.H. the Minor Maharaja of Bharatpur, Jan., 1939; vice-consul, Bushire, Dec., 1939; on depn., Aug., 1941; asst. commr., Bannu, Dec., 1941; asst. pol. agt., N. Waziristan, Jan., 1943; asst. commr., Hangu, Apl., 1943; confd. in the I.P.S., May, 1943; sup. asst. commr., Peshawar, Dec., 1944; pol. agt. N. Waziristan, Jan., 1945; under sec. to govt. N.W.F.P., post-war planning, Aug., 1945; dir. civil supplies and jt. sec. to govt. N.W.F.P., rev. dept., Aug., 1945; sec. to govt. N.W.F.P., Apl., 1946.

LA TOUCHE, JAMES FLEMING DIGGES, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 18th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., June, 1930.

LAUGHTON, FREDERICK EASTFIELD, Maj., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 29th Apl., 1900).—Retd., July, 1943.†

LAUGHTON, GEORGE CHRISTIAN, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. eng.) (b. 26th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; divl. engr., Oct., 1925; offg. divl. supt., Nov., 1926; offg. dep. dir., programme, rly. bd., March, 1929; offg. dir., civil engrg., Apl., 1931; offg. secty., rly. bd., June, 1932; offg. dep. dir., ditto, Oct., 1932; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1932; confd., Dec., 1935; on loan to B.B. & C.I. rly., agent and genl. manager, ditto, Apl., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

LAURENCE, REV. GEORGE, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (archdeacon of Lahore) (b. 12th Nov., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 26th March, 1926; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1927; snr. ditto, Nov., 1937; archdeacon of Lahore, May, 1944.

LAURIE, MALCOLM VYVYAN, O.B.E., Indian Forest Service (b. 30th August, 1901).—

Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1925; forest economist Apl., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930; offg. silviculturist, forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1935; confd., Oct., 1936; dep. dir., directorate-genl. of supply, Nov., 1940; O.B.E., June, 1946, on l.p.r.

LAW, HENRY RUSSEL MACKAY, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 16th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

LAWIE, WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 22nd Jan., 1895).—Date of first commn., 29th Nov., 1923; civil surgeon, 3rd July, 1933.

LAWLEY, WILFRED, O.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 2nd June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1934; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

LAWRANCE, REV. NEVILLE ANTHONY HENRY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 20th July, 1909).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Nagpur Diocese, 1st Sept., 1940.

LAWRENCE, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 8th June, 1900).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 27th Oct., 1919; clerical officer, Jan., 1920; transferred to office of high commr., Oct., 1920; actg. exec. offcr., Feb., 1927; confd., June, 1929.

LAWRENCE, SIR HENRY STAVELEY, K.C.S.I. late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Retd., Sept., 1926; member of Royal commn. on agric. in India, 1926-7.*

LAWSON, REGINALD GEORGE BATEMAN, late Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 15th Dec., 1914 Rangoon, March, 1935, and again, Oct., 1936; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Jan. to Oct., 1936; asst. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936; commr., Rangoon city police, Jan., 1938; retd., 1st Apl., 1943.

LAWSON, WILFRED CHARLES, Indian Police (b. 7th June, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd., Nov., 1940.

LAYARD, AUSTEN HAVELOCK, C.I.E., M.A., Barr.-at-Law, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Rugby and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 29th Dec., 1920, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; dep. commr., Delhi, March, 1932; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

LAYTON, GEORGE EDWARD, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 12th Oct., 1879).—Apptd., 1st July, 1925, in connection with the G.I.P. Rly. Co.

LAZARUS, MISS THEODORA, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Madras) (b. 9th Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

LEACH, SIR ALFRED HENRY LIONEL, (Madras).—apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, 15th Feb., 1933; ch. justice, high ct., Madras, July, 1937; kt., Jan., 1938; retd., Feb., 1947.

LEAKE, JONES INCHIQUIN, late Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 18th March, 1895).—Joined the service, 8th Apl., 1924; asst. supt., Jan., 1925; retd., 6th Nov., 1945.

LE BAILLY, WILLIAM FRANCIS GRAHAME, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Wellington and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1932, and Oct., 1933; ditto (subs. provl.), March, 1935.

LE BROCO, EDWARD HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 25th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 6th Jan., 1923; offg. supt., Apl., 1926; confd., Aug., 1934; princpl., trng. coll., Sardah, Apl., 1939, to Feb., 1943.

LE BURN, LEONARD EDWIN, M.M., India Office (actg. staff offr., econ. dept.) (b. 29th Sept., 1897).—On mil. serv. 1916-1919; apptd. cler. offr., 2nd Mar., 1922; act. hr. ditto, May, 1937; actg. staff offr., July, 1942.

LE FEVRE, JOHN REGINALD, Indian Police (b. 15th Aug., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd., Nov., 1940.

LECKY-THOMPSON, THOMAS EDWARD, B.Sc., Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 7th Nov., 1902).—Joined the service, as asst. dist. supt. June, 1926; offg. dist. supt., June, 1934; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; dist. supt., 1946.

LECKY - THOMPSON, WILLIAM, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 4th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 10th Sept., 1922; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1930; confd., Feb., 1936; supt. of police supplies, March, 1938; confd., dist. supt. police, May, 1940; civ. aff. serv. to March, 1946; again dist. supt., 1946.

LEDGARD, Maj. H. A., M.B., Ch.B. (Leeds), I.M.S. (b. 7th Dec., 1903).—Date of first commn. 24th Apl., 1933; ch. med. offr. and civil surg., Delhi, 1943; civil surg., March, 1944; ch. med. offr. and inspr.-genl. of prisons, Baluchistan, May, 1946.

LEDGER, LLOYD KIRKWOOD, O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil and agency surg.) (b. 27th May, 1895).—First commn. dated 15th March, 1920; in milty. employ up to 30th June, 1925; offg. agency surg., Peshawar, July, 1925; offg. medl. offcr., Seistan; agency surg., Bundelkhand, May, 1929; ditto, Gilgit, Oct., 1932; civil surg., Peshawar, Oct., 1936; O.B.E., June, 1938.

LEDWIDGE, WILLIAM BERNARD JOHN, B.A., Burma Office (actg. principal) (b. 9th Nov., 1915).—Educ. at Cardinal Vaughan School and King's College, Cambridge; held Commonwealth Fund Fellowship at Princeton University, U.S.A., Sept., 1937, to June, 1939; apptd. asst. principal, 2nd Oct., 1939; served with H.M. Forces, from March, 1940 to Dec., 1945; pte. sec. to under sec. of state, Jan., 1946; actg. princpl. Aug., 1946.

LEE, REV. ARTHUR OLDFIELD NORRIS, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 6th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, 30th Nov., 1921; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1931; retd., June, 1942.

LEE, DIONYS JOHN NORRIS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th Oct., 1892).—Retd., Sept., 1940.†

LEE, EDWARD OWEN, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. of excise, etc., Bihar) (b. 3rd Nov., 1891).—Educ. at Malvern and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1915, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; asst. commr., Assam, Apl., 1922; services replaced at disposal of govt. of Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1925; mag. and collr. (provl. sub.), Jan., 1929; confd., May, 1930; offg. dep. commr., Delhi, Apl., 1929; chairman, Assam labour bd., Apl., 1933; contr. of emigrant lab. in

Assam, Oct., 1933; commr. of excise, etc., Bihar, Oct., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1944.

LEE, GEORGE HENRY, M.C., late Indian Police (b. 19th Jan., 1898).—Retd., March, 1940.†

LEE, JOSEPH STANDISH SEARCHFIELD, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 8th Oct., 1883).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

LEEDHAM, JOHN WALTER, Burma Front. Serv. (b. 18th Mar., 1905).—Apptd. asst. supt., 7th Nov., 1927; civ. aff. serv. June, 1943 to Oct., 1945; resdt. Oct., 1945.

LEEPER, WILLIAM CLUFF, Maj., O.B.E., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Nov., 1899).—First commn., 15th Apr., 1919; in milty. employ to 21st Aug., 1928; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1929; pers. asst. to ch. commr. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., May, 1930; asst. commr., Peshawar, May, 1931; ditto, Kohat, March, 1933; ditto, Nowshera, June, 1933; ditto, Charsadda, Oct., 1933; ditto, Mardan, Feb., 1935; asst. commr., Manshera, May, 1937; pol. agent, Kurram, Oct., 1937; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., devel. depts., March, 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., home dept., Feb., 1943; dep. commr., Mardan, Jan., 1946.

LEES, LEONARD MARTYN, Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 2nd Dec., 1898).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 21st Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt. (Edn.), June, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1927; dep. commr., p.s., Jan., 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; secty., fin. dept., Oct., 1939; secty., L. & R. dept., May, 1940; on spl. duty. Burma evac. welfare dept., Aug., 1942; sec. ditto, Dec., 1942; jt. sec. transpt. dept. and commns. dept., 1945; commr., July, 1946.

LEES, ROGER EDWARD ROWLEY, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 14th Aug., 1914).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 22nd Nov., 1934.

LEGATE, ROBERT MOORHEAD DE CONLAY, M.Sc., A.M.I.E.E., (b. 25th Oct., 1900).—Apptd. project offr., central tech. power bd., Oct., 1945.

LEIGH, REV. ARTHUR GEORGE DAVIES, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senior chaplain) (b. 3rd Apr., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 26th Dec., 1926; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1928; senior chaplain, Dec., 1938.

LEIGHTON, ERIC, Indian Police (cent. intell. offcr., Orissa) (b. 11th May, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 7th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1928; cent. intell. offcr., Orissa, Apr., 1940.

LEITCH, ROLAND FRANK, M.B.E., Burma Front. Serv. (b. 21st Aug., 1904).—Apptd. asst. supt. Jan., 1930; M.B.E., June, 1932; civ. aff. serv., Feb., 1943 to Oct., 1945; dep. commr., Oct., 1945.

LE MESURIER, ALGERNON PAUL, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 28th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Rugby, Lausanne, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr., June, 1936; confd., June, 1939; dir. of settlements, Aug., 1940; ch. sec. to govt. of Sind, March, 1946.

LEO, HARRY WALLER, Burma Serv. of Engrs. (b. 16th Mar., 1892).—Apptd. to P.W.D. Burma, 16th Oct., 1922; exec. engr. 1st Oct., 1928; on mil. duty, Oct., 1942 to Mar., 1946.

LESSLIE, DAVID FINDLAY, M.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 31st March, 1897).—

Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Jan., 1920; offg. supt., March, 1925; confd., Aug., 1929; asst. to dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936, to Dec., 1941.

LESTER-GARLAND, REV. GEORGE HENRY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 9th May, 1895).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., N.W.F.P., 26th March, 1926; junr. chaplain, Apr., 1926; senr. chaplain, Apr., 1936.

L'ESTRANGE, ALBERT PERCIVAL PORTER, Land Records Dept., Burma (asst. dir.) (b. 13th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as probr., 22nd Sept., 1903; supt., Aug., 1907; asst. dir., Aug., 1930.

LETHBRIDGE, MONTAGU HENRY BOWATER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 22nd March, 1889).—Educ. at Westminster, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. setilt. offr., Oct., 1915; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Apr., 1918; dist. and sess. judge and commr. for workmen's compensation, Nov., 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Nov., 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Assam, legisl. dept., Oct., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; dist. and sess. judge, Bengal, Nov., 1935; confd. in selection grade, Jan., 1939.

LEVINGE, SIR EDWARD VERE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa).—Retd., Aug., 1918.*

LEWIS, SIR CLINTON GRESHAM, Brig., O.B.E., R.E., late Survey of India (b. 25th Nov., 1885).—Retd. Aug., 1941.†

LEWIS, GEORGE ALBERT, Burma Front. Serv. (b. 25th May, 1922; )Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

LEWIS, ELWYN BYRON, M.B.E., Cent. Excises and Salt Dept., Northern India (chief mining engr., Khewra) (b. 3rd Jan., 1900).—Joined the service, 5th Nov., 1935; chief mining engr., Khewra, Nov., 1935; M.B.E., Jan., 1944.

LEWIS, GEORGE ALBERT, Burma Frontier Service (b. 11th April, 1904).—Apptd. asst. supt., 7th Nov., 1927; O.B.E., June, 1945; dep. sec., fron. areas adminstrn., Jan., 1946.

LEWIS, LUCIEN ALEXANDER, M.I.E.E., Office of High Commr. for India (dep. dir. of inspection, store dept.) (b. 3rd May, 1882).—Educ. at Brighton Gr. Sch., and City and Guilds Inst., Finsbury Coll.; apptd. asst. inspr. of scientific supplies, store depot, 19th Nov., 1906; electrical engr., ditto, Aug., 1917; dep. dir. of inspection, store dept., July, 1936; actg. dir., ditto, Dec., 1939.

LEWIS, REV. OLIVER GEORGE, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 11th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 5th Oct., 1929; junior chaplain, Oct., 1931.

LEWIS, SYDNEY ALLAN MAYBURY, M.R.San.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Sind) (b. 23rd Sept., 1886).—Retd., 29th Sept., 1941.†

LEWIS, WALTER ALAN SCOTT, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st July, 1907).—Educ. at Winchester and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1933; chairman, nat. serv. lab. trib., Bengal, Jan., 1942; contr., purchase, dept. of civ. supplies, Calcutta; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; dir. rice procurement, dept. civil supplies, Mar., 1946.

LEWIS, SIR WILLIAM HAWTHORNE, K.C.S.I.,

K.C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (govr. of Orissa) (b. 29th June, 1888).—Educ. at Oundle, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on censor duty, Bombay, from Apl., 1915, to March, 1916; under-sec. to govt., rev. and judl. depts., Bihar and Orissa, June, 1918; jt. mag., March, 1921; offg. dep. commr., March, 1923; offg. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Oct., 1925; confd. as mag. collr., Oct., 1926; on spl. duty, home dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1927; jt. secty., reforms office, May, 1930; jt. secty. to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; jt. secty., reforms office, Jan., 1932; offg. reforms commr., govt. of India, May, 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; addl. secty., reforms, govt. of India, Feb., 1935; reforms commr., July, 1936; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938; K.C.S.I., 24th Dec., 1940; govr. of Orissa, Apl., 1941.

LEWIS, WILLIAM HOWARD, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 7th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 14th Nov., 1927; offg. supt., Feb., 1934; offg. dep. commr., Calcutta, Feb., 1936; on depn. to Orissa state from Feb., 1939; confd., supt., March, 1941.

LEWISOHN, LEON HORNBLY, Indian Police (b. 24th Dec., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bengal, Nov., 1932; confd., March, 1935; a.d.c. to govr., Orissa, Feb., 1938; on depn. under Crown repres. police, Nectmch, Dec., 1939, to May, 1942; King's Police Medal, Nov., 1946.

LEWYS-LLOYD, IDRIS WYN, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 7th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Colwyn County Sch., Univ. of Wales, and Paris; joined the service, 10th Dec., 1928; arrived, 12th Jan., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1934; settlt. offcr., Nov., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; mag. and collr. (confd.), Feb., 1944; sec. to govr., Sept., 1944.

LEYDEN, JOHN LAMB, O.B.E., Burma Front. Serv. (b. 11th Apl., 1904).—Apptd. asst. supt., 7th Nov., 1927; O.B.E., June, 1945; dep. sec., fron. areas adminstrn. Jan., 1946.

LIAQUAT ALI KHAN, MAWABZADA.—Apptd. fincl. memb. of govr.-genl.'s council, Oct., 1946.

LIAQAT HAYAT KHAN, SIR, NAWAB, KHAN BAHADUR, K.B.E., late Indian Police Service (Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1884).—Retd., June, 1931.†

LIDIERTH, JAMES EUGENE, I.S.O., M.B.E. (b. 12th Oct., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st June, 1908, to 15th Oct., 1919; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1921; offg. under-sec. to agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1922, and again, Aug., 1929; awarded K.-i.-H. medal (2nd class) in 1925; registrar, A.G.G.'s office, Quetta, Apl., 1929; asst. secty. to A.G.G. and C.C. in Baluchistan, Aug., 1929; offg. under-sec., ditto, Dec., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Apl., 1934; offg. under-sec. to A.G., Baluchistan, July, 1935; I.S.O., May, 1937.

LIFTON, WILFRID JOHN, Capt. (E.), C.I.E., R.I.N. (b. 27th Jan., 1890).—Apptd. princpl. offr., mercantile marine dept. Apl., 1940 and ch. surveyor, govt. of India, Sept., 1942.

LIGHTFOOT, GORDON SHELLEY, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 18th Aug., 1897).—

Served in the Indian Army from 12th May, 1915, to 18th May, 1923; proby. asst. supt., 19th May, 1923; confd., May, 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; supt., May, 1930; pol. officer, Balipara frontier tract, Oct., 1934; ditto Charduar, Oct., 1938, supt., Mar., 1943.

LIGHTOWLER, IVOR HARRISON, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. optg. supt.) (b. 21st Dec., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. storekeeper, 20th May, 1912; asst. contr. of stores, Apl., 1919; dist. ditto, March, 1931; dep. ch. optg. supt., Aug., 1941.

LILLIE, CECIL JAMES WINGATE, Indian C.S. (dep. secty., govt. of India, home dept.) (b. 6th July, 1897).—Educ. at Clifton, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1921; arrived, 1st Dec., 1921; and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925; under-sec. to govt., Dec., 1931; offg. dep. commr., March, 1932; offg. commr. of settlements and dir. of land records, etc., May, 1933; offg. secty., rev. dept., Central Provs., Nov., 1936; dep. commr., March, 1937; dep. secty., govt. of India, home dept., Nov., 1938; and again, Oct., 1939; on depn. to H.E.H. the Nizam's govt., June, 1942 to July, 1944; offg. commr., Apl., 1946.

LILLIE, HORACE, late Indian Police (b. 2nd Apl., 1886).—Retd., 1940.†

LIN, MAUNG SAN, Burma C.S. (cl. I) (dep. commr.) (b. 16th May, 1916).—Educ. at Govt. English High Sch., Maymyo, and Rangoon Univ.; apptd. on probn. 30th Sept., 1938; W. sub. divl. mag., Rangoon, 1945; dep. commr., Oct., 1945; dep. sec., comm. and supply dept., Feb., 1946.

LINCKE, SYDNEY HOWARD PHIPPS, late Indian State Railways, (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 7th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. storekeeper, 8th Nov., 1912; dist. contr. of stores, Nov., 1926; dep. ditto, Nov., 1928; contr. of stores, variously from Nov., 1929 to Apl., 1945.

LINCOLN, EDWARD HENNING, V.D., late Punjab C.S. (b. 16th March, 1889).—Joined the service, 14th Aug., 1905; extra asst. commr., March, 1934; dep. commr., Nov., 1933; offg. secty., elec. and indus. depts., Punjab, Apl., to July, 1936; ret'd., 1944.

LINDOP, KENNETH JAMES HULME, C.B.E., M.C., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 13th July, 1898).—Educ. at Denston Coll., Staffs. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the serv., 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. settlt. offr., March, 1923; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1928; confd., Jan., 1936; commr. of settlements and land records, July, 1939; C.B.E., Jan., 1943; civ. aff. serv., 1943; commr., Dec., 1946.

LINDSAY, DAVID KENNETH LLEWELLYN, O.B.E. Maj., I.M.S. (b. 9th Aug., 1904).—Date of first commn., 1st Feb., 1933; apptd. to Burma medl. dept., Dec., 1935; on mil. serv., Sept., 1939 to Nov., Mar., 1946.

LINDSAY, SIR HARRY ALEXANDER FANSHAW, K.C.I.E., C.B.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 11th March, 1881).—Retd., Feb., 1937.*

LINDSAY, ROBERT WILLIAM (b. 20th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service, 16th July, 1909; held non-gaz. appt. in mil. dept.; services placed at disp. of govt. of Burma, p.w. dept., from Sept., 1921, as asst. engr.; employed as exec. engr., Port Blair, variously from Apl., 1923, and from Oct., 1929; again asst. engr. (B.E.S.), Burma, Dec., 1928, to Sept., 1929.

LINDSAY, REV. W. T., M.A., Ind. Eccles. Estabt. (b. 2nd Jan., 1897).—Apptd. junior chaplain, Bombay, on probn., 8th Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

LINES, ROY NICHOLAS, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 11th Sept., 1914).—Educ. at Royal Latin Sch., Buckingham, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1937; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

LINGWOOD, FREDERICK, late Office of the High Commr. for India (b. 1st March, 1901).—Retd., Apl., 1944.*

LINLITHGOW, MOST HON. MARQUESS OF, VICTOR ALEXANDER JOHN HOPE, P.C., K.G., K.T., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., O.B.E., D.L., T.D. (b. 24th Sept., 1887).—Educ. at Eton; served European War, 1914-1918 (despatches); civil lord of the admy., 1922-24; presdt., navy league, 1924-31; chairman, departl. ctee. on distribn. and prices of agricl. produce, 1923; chairman, Rl. commn. on Indian agriculture, 1926-28; chairman, jt. select ctee. on Indian constittl. reform, 1933; G.C.S.I., Apl., 1936; viceroy and govr.-genl. of India, Apl., 1936, to Oct., 1943; Kt. of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, June, 1936.

LISTER, REV. JOHN GEORGE, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 8th March, 1885).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, 2nd Feb., 1914; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1927.

LISTER-JACKSON, ARTHUR, late Indian State Rly. (b. 18th Sept., 1891).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

LITTLE, EDWARD PYBUS, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 28th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1935; under govt. of India, def. dept., Oct., 1939.

LITTLEWOOD, GLADYS ELIZABETH, M.B.E., Indian Educl. Service (N.W.F.P.) (b. 1st Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, 16th June, 1918; inspectress of schools, Ambala, Nov., 1919; ditto, N.W.F.P., May, 1920; K.-i.-H. Medal, 1st class, June, 1934; retd.,

LITTLEWOOD, ROWLAND WHITELAW, late Indian Agric. Serv. Madras (b. 19th June, 1888).—Apptd. Jan., 1921; retd., June, 1943.

LITTLEWORTH, MICHAEL CHARLES GEORGE, Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 19th May, 1919).—Apptd. on probn., Nov., 1938; O.S.D., home dept., Aug., 1945; comdt. mill. police and dist. supt., Andamans, Oct., 1945; supt. Port Blair.

LLEWELLYN, JOHN LISTER, O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 10th Apl., 1906).—Educ. at Nottingham High Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1935; O.B.E., June, 1944; secr., Bengal admin. enq. ctee., Nov., 1944.

LLOYD, SIR ALAN HUBERT, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Burma, attd. Impl. Customs Service (establishment offcr., govt. of India) (b. 30th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. asst. collr. of customs, impl. customs service, Rangoon, March, 1910; confd., Nov., 1911; transf. to Bengal as asst. collr. of customs, June, 1916; on depn. under commercial

intell. dept., India, Dec., 1916, to June, 1917; offg. collr. of customs, June, 1917; confd., Dec., 1919; apptd. first member of central board of rev., govt. of India, Dec., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, June, 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; member, council of state, Apl., 1935; kt., Jan., 1939 temp. addtl. secty. to govt. of India, commerce dept., May, 1939; secr., ditto, Aug., 1939; retd., Nov., 1942; estabt. offr., govt. of India Nov., 1942; ditto, and examr. of capital issues, May, 1943.

LLOYD, BARON, RT. HON. GEORGE AMBROSE LLOYD OF DOLOBRAN, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., D.S.O., F.R.G.S.—Govt. of Bombay from 16th Dec., 1918, to 9th Dec., 1923.

LLOYD-JONES, CYRIL MICHAEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 1st Nov., 1914).—Educ. at Oundle and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 22nd Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

LOADER, WALTER GERALD, Burma Front. Serv. (b. 16th Feb., 1897).—Joined as cadet inspr. of police, July, 1919; asst. supt., B.F.S., Aug., 1926; comdt., Gurkha evac. camp, Mohipan, Mar., 1946.

LOBB, JOHN CHALICE, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1897).—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1919; supt., Nov., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., variously from Apl., 1935; senr. supt., police, Delhi, Dec. 1942; O.B.E., June, 1943; again dep. inspr.-genl., May, 1945.

LOBO, CHARLES MANUEL, LL.B., Judl. Dept., Bombay (judge, chief ct., Sind) (b. 11th Aug., 1884).—Apptd. govt. pleader and public prosecutor for Sind, 1st Apl., 1929; offg. addl. commr. for Sind, July, 1929; and again, Jan., 1936; confd., March, 1936; addl. judl. commr., Sind, May, 1938; judge, chief ct. of Sind, Apl., 1940.

LOBO-PRABHU, JOSEPH MATHIAS, Indian C.S. (b. 26th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at University of Madras and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the United Provs. as asst. collr. and mag.; offr. on spec. duty at govt. h.q. (fin.); confd. as mag. and collr., May, 1942; dep. sec., govt. of India (food), July, 1944; and again, Aug., 1945.

LOCH, PERCY GORDON (name changed to DALYELL of the BINNS), C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 16th Jan., 1887).—Retd., May, 1938.*

LODGE, RONALD FRANCIS, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 8th Apl., 1889).—Educ. at Liverpool Institute High Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1925; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Apl., 1935, and again, Aug., 1936; regr. high ct., 1937; confd. in sel. gr., May, 1939; apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Apl., 1941.

LOGAN, FRANCIS HUGH late Indian C.S., (United Provs.) (b. 1st July, 1904).—Retd., June, 1938.*

LOGAN, ROBERT ANCEL, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond), Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Central Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 13th

Nov., 1889).—First commn., 10th Oct., 1919; civil surgeon, Apl., 1932.

LOKUR, NARAYAN SWAMIRAO, B.A., LL.B., Bombay C.S. (judge, Bombay) (b. 21st Apl., 1887).—Held various judl. appts. from 22nd Oct., 1918, to 31st Dec., 1925; judge and sess. judge, Bombay, Jan., 1926; judge, high ct., ditto, Dec., 1942.

LONE, MAZIR SHAN, O.B.E., M.C., Burma Front. Serv., (b. 17th May, 1912).—Apptd. 24th Jan., 1946.

LONG, CECIL EUSTACE, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Feb., 1882).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

LONG, ATHELTON CHARLES ETHELWULF, M.A., Burma C.S. (cl. I) (b. 2nd Jan., 1919).—Educ. at Westminster Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

LONGDEN, ARTHUR JOHN ERIC, Burma Police (cl. I) (b. 18th June, 1922).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

LONGRIGG, JOHN HEMSLEY, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 6th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1913; dep. consvr., Dec., 1918; offg. consvr., Aug., 1928; princpl. for coll., Coimbatore, Jan., 1937; dep. consvr., July, 1939; retd., May, 1945.

LONSDALE, CHRISTOPHER EDWIN, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 18th Aug., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, 19th Nov., 1931; confd., Jan., 1934; dist. supt. of police (confd.) Mar., 1945; princpl. Police Training Coll., Dec., 1945.

LORT-WILLIAMS, SIR JOHN ROLLESTON, Kt., late Judl. Dept., Bengal (b. 14th Sept., 1881).—Retd., June, 1941.†

LOTHIAN, SIR ARTHUR CUNNINGHAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 27th June, 1887).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. mag. and asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1915; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1917; temp. offg. as asst. to pol. agent, Bunkelkhand, from Nov., 1915; first asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Dec., 1920; sec. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Apl., 1924, and again, Nov., 1927; dep. sec., govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., July, 1926; secty. to resdt., Mysore, May, 1928; resdt., Jaipur, Apl., 1929; on spl. duty, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1931; resdt., Baroda, July, 1932; pol. agent, E. Rajputana states, March, 1933; resdt., Jaipur, June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Rajputana, Apl., 1934; addl. secty. to the govt. of India, f. and p. dept., Apl., 1935; employed as spl. representative of the Viceroy, Oct., 1936; addtl. secty. to govt. of India, f. and p. dept., Feb., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; resdt. for Rajputana and C.C. in Ajmer Merwara, Oct., 1937; K.C.I.E., June, 1941; resdt. at Hyderabad, offg., Oct., 1942; confd., Feb., 1944.

LOVATT, ARTHUR WILLIAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 1st Sept., 1912).—Educ. at Dunfermline High Sch., St. Andrew's Univ., Vienna Univ., and London Sch. of Economics; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. sec. to govr., Bombay, Jan., 1941; under-sec., Eastern group supply cl., Oct., 1942; under-sec., govt. of India (home), Apl., 1943.

LOVELAND, FRANK, Indian State Rlys.

(dist. traffic supt.) (b. 14th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 23rd July, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Aug., 1932.

LOVETT, SIR HARRINGTON VERNEY, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.).—Retd., Oct., 1919.*

LOW, ALASTAIR PETRIE, O.B.E., Indian C.S. Ind. Political Serv. (b. 30th Jan., 1908).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., pol. and home depts., Punjab, March, 1934; under-secty. to A.G.G., states of W.I., Nov., 1935; secty. to A.G.G., Gujerat states, May, 1936; asst. commr., Charsadda, Apl., 1938; addtl. asst. pol. agt., S. Waziristan, Dec., 1939; asst. commr., Hangu, Oct., 1940; dy. commr., Bannu, Oct., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; finl. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1944.

LOW, PETER DUNSTAN, O.B.E., A.M.I.E.E., Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as dist. elec. engr., E.I. Rly., 7th Apl., 1927; dep. ch. mech. engr., elec., B. & A. rly., March, 1940; retd., Oct., 1945.

LOW, THOMAS RALPH, M.C., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (addl. direct. of agric.) (b. 21st Dec., 1893).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 4th Dec., 1920; princpl., agric. coll., Cawnpore, May, 1934; offg. dir. of agric., United Provs., Oct., 1935; dir. inst. of plant industry, Indore, July, 1936; confd. in sel. grade, Aug., 1940; dep. dir., Aug., 1941; on depn. to defence dept., Nov., 1941; addl. dir. of agric. and war prodn., Apl., 1943; addl. dir. of agric., Jan., 1946.

LOWE, MISS IRENE H., M.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Madras) (b. 4th May, 1890).—Apptd. inspectress of schools, 29th March, 1922; dep. directress of public instrn., Aug., 1926; retd., May, 1945.

LOWIS, ROSS HENRY DONALD, O.B.E., Indian Police, Indian Political Service (b. 5th Feb., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1924; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Feb., 1936; under trng., Punjab, Dec., 1937; M.B.E., Jan., 1938; asst. pol. agt., Chitral, Dec., 1939; confd. in Ind. pol. service, Dec., 1939; asst. commr., Hangu, Oct., 1941; pol. agt., N. Waziristan, Jan., 1943; dep. commr. Kohat, Jan., 1945; and again, Aug., 1946; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

LOWNDS, EDWIN ALBERT, late India Office (b. 24th Apl., 1878).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

LOWTHER, ERNEST HERBERT NEWTON, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 19th May, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 10th June, 1911; dist. supt. (s.p.t.), July, 1923; supt. (s.p.t.), Feb., 1925; confd., July, 1925; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., July, 1932; offg. dep. agent, personnel, E.I. rly, March, 1936; divl. supt., Oct., 1940; retd., Apl., 1945.

LUBY, THOMAS, M.A., V.D., late Indian C.S. (Bihar) (b. 12th Nov., 1882).—Retd., 28th Nov., 1942.†

LUCAS, E. S. S., Capt., I.M.S. (civ. surg.) (b. 1st Nov., 1897).—Date of first commn., 20th Aug., 1928; civil surgeon, United Provs., Nov., 1934.

LUCAS, HENRY CHARLES EDWIN, Indian Police (b. 27th Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 25th Nov., 1925; offg. dist. supt., June, 1934; confd., March, 1940; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; dist. supt., 1946.

LUCAS, LIONEL JOHN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 5th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at St. Xavier's Coll., Calcutta, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, 30th Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Dec., 1928; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1935; dep. secty., education, etc., dept., Bihar, Apr., 1939; dep. commr., Port Blair, March, 1940-July, 1941; dep. sec. educn., etc., dept., Apr., 1944; retd., 1946.

LUCE, GORDON HANNINGTON, M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 20th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as prof., Rangoon Coll., 1st Nov., 1912; on mil. service from 24th July, 1918, to 9th Feb., 1919; lecturer in English, May, 1922; lecturer in Burmese and Far Eastern history, Sept., 1929; retd., 1st Nov. 1943.

LUCKMAN, REV. SYDNEY, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 3rd Nov., 1888).—Retd., Nov., 1944.†

LUMBY, ESMOND WALTER RAWSON, M.A., India Office (princl., pol. dept.) (b. 27th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; asst. princpl., customs and excise dept., 23rd Oct., 1933, to 14th Oct., 1934; asst. princpl., India Office, 15th Oct., 1934; asst. sec., Ind. delegns. to assembly of League of Nations, 1935-37; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1937; actg. princpl., Oct., 1938; confd., May, 1939; on staff of Cabinet Mission to India, Mar., to June, 1947.

LUMLEY, SIR (LAWRENCE) ROGER, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., J.P., D.L. (b. 27th July, 1896).—Govr. of Bombay, Sept., 1937, to March, 1943; G.C.S.I., June, 1943.

LWIN, MAUNG SAW, B.A., Indian C.S. (addl. dep. commr., Burma) (b. 17th Dec., 1911).—Educ. at Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 18th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; western sub-divl. mag., Rangoon, Dec., 1939; under-sec., educn. dept., Jan., 1941; addl. dep. commr., 1945; dep. sec., agric. and rural econ. dept., Aug., 1946.

LWIN, U SAN, Burma Audit dept. (b. 10th May, 1906).—Joined Ind. audit dept., 6th Apr., 1929; dep. acct.-genl. (jr.), Burma, 17th May, 1938; left behind in Burma; served in civ. aff. serv.; dep. acct.-genl. (sr.), Burma, 1st Feb., 1946.

LYALL, JOHN HENRY, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Bihar) (b. 16th Aug., 1885).—Retd., Aug., 1940.†

LYDALL, EDWARD FRANCIS, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 4th Aug., 1907).—Educ. at Marlborough and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. and pers. asst. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, Nov., 1935; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, May, 1936; offer. on spec. duty, Kalat agency, Aug., 1936; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, March, 1938; asst. commr., Peshawar, Feb., 1938; sec., Kabul legation, Nov., 1939; confd. in Ind. pol. serv., Sept., 1940; asst. commr., Nowshera, Sept., 1942; presdt. Manipur State Durbar, May, 1943; sec. to resdt. at Baroda and for States of W. India and Gujarat, May, 1946.

LYDE, LIONEL GILDEA, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 13th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Rugby, Wadham Coll., Oxford, and

Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived, 30th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1932; mag. and collr. (confd.), Feb., 1945.

LYLE, ARTHUR NEVIN (late R.E.), O.B.E., B.A., B.A.I., A.M.I.C.E. (supgt. engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; confd., Oct., 1925; supgt. engr. (confd.), May, 1941.

LYLE, THOMAS McELDERRY, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 24th May, 1886).—Retd., 24th May, 1941.

LYSTER, ANTHONY ST. GEORGE, C.S.I., B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 13th March, 1888).—Retd., 13th Apr., 1943.†

LYTTON, EARL OF, RT. HON. VICTOR ALEXANDER GEORGE ROBERT LYTTON, K.G., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., P.C.—Parly. under-sec. of state for India, 1st Oct., 1920; govr. of Bengal from March, 1922, to March, 1927; viceroy and actg. govr.-genl. of India, 10th Apr. to 5th Aug., 1925.

MACALPINE, ROBERT IAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th March, 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928; on mil. serv. May, 1940.

MACAULAY, DONALD, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr.) (b. 4th June, 1895).—Joined the service, 11th May, 1920, as asst. loco. supgt.; offg. works manager, E.B. rly., Dec., 1930; dist. loco. supgt., E.B. rly. (prov.), Aug., 1934; dep. ch. mechl. engr. (elec.), March, 1940.

MCBRIDE, J. B., O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E., late Bengal Factories Service (b. 9th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1923; inspr. of factories, Sept., 1924; chief inspr. factories, Bengal, Sept., 1931; O.B.E., June, 1939; retd., Aug., 1944.

MCALL, ANTHONY GILCHRIST, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 7th Jan., 1895).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and R.M.A., Woolwich; joined the service, 5th Aug., 1921; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to Assam, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1930; dep. commr., Oct., 1935; confd., Aug., 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

MCCARTHY, DENIS ARTHUR MACKAURA, Indian Police (b. 11th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supgt., United Provs., 5th Dec., 1924; offg. supgt., Apr., 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; comdt. and dist. supgt., Port Blair, Dec., 1936; supgt., confd., Apr., 1939.

MCCLENAGHAN, HERBERT ERIC ST. GEORGE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr.) (b. 5th Aug., 1896).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 14th Nov., 1922; arrived, 8th Jan., 1923, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apr., 1926; March, 1931, and again, Apr., 1933; confd., March, 1934; collr., central excise, Bombay, Apr., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

MACCOLL, ALEXANDER MALCOLM, Indian Police (supgt., river divn.) (b. 28th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supgt., 26th Jan., 1920; dist. supgt., Nov., 1924; supgt., river divn., Nov., 1936; interned by Japanese, Jan., 1942 to Sept., 1945.

MACCOLL, HUGH HERBERT, M.C., late

Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 7th Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 28th Nov., 1921; offg. dep. consvr., June, 1923; confd. Apl., 1927; retd., Sept., 1947.

MCCONAGHEY, RONALD ALLEN, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 21st May, 1912).—Apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., June, 1937; vice-consul, Bunder-Abbas, Apl., 1944; and consul, Kerman (in addn.), Oct., 1944; under sec. to res'd. for Rajputna, Dec. 1945; asst. pol. agt., Orissa States, June, 1946.

MCCORKELL, DAVID ROBERT ANDREW, Indian Police (b. 22nd Apl., 1916).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Dec., 1936; transfed. mil. dept., Nov., 1940; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; dep. commr., Rangoon, Oct., 1945.

MCCRACKEN, ANDREW, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 21st Oct., 1890).—Ed. at Ayr Academy and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to Sept., 1919; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept.; asst. rice commr., Rangoon, Feb., 1920; under-sec. to govt., May, 1921; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, reforms office, June, 1930, to Jan., 1932; secty., home dept., govt. of Burma, Feb., 1938; commr., (subst.), Apl., 1942; retd., Aug., 1945.

MCCREA, KENNETH IAN, M.B.E., Indian Police (b. 15th Dec., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., 16th Nov., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; offg. dep. commr., Kyautpyu, Apl., 1941; M.B.E., Jan., 1941.

MACDERMOT, PATRICK JOSEPH, India Office (b. 2nd Feb., 1885).—Apptd. clerical officer, 23rd Aug., 1921; higher clerical officer, Oct., 1931; asst. supt., tel. and mails branch, March, 1942; actg. asst. supt., record dept., Aug., 1943.

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER, M.A. (Glas.), B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. 13th Nov., 1883).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER MACLEOD, Indian Police (b. 10th Oct., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 11th Nov., 1935; confd., Mar., 1937.

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER RANALD HUME, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 3rd Feb., 1909).—Edu. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., Assam, March, 1934; confd., Aug., 1934; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1934; ditto, S. Waziristan, Nov., 1935; presdt., Manipur State Durbar, Feb., 1937; dep. sec. (home), Nov., 1941; dep. commr. (provl.), Jan., 1942; supt., Luskin Hills, June, 1943.

MACDONALD, REV. ALISDAIR JOHN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Madras) (senr. chaplain) (b. 12th June, 1900).—Apptd. chaplain on probn. Church of Scotland, Oct., 1933; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1935; mil. service, Dec., 1937 to April, 1942; presdy. senr. chaplain, July, 1944.

MACDONALD, ANGUS ALEXANDER, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., Ind. C.S. (dep. commr. Punjab) (b. 12th Nov., 1904).—Edu. at Univ. of Edinburgh, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928, and arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1942;

confd. as dep. commr., Nov., 1942; home sec. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

MACDONALD, REV. DONALD, M.A., B.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 22nd Sept., 1891).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, 27th Feb., 1927; apptd. senr. chaplain, Feb., 1937; retd. Feb. 1947.

MACDONALD, DONALD CHARLES, late Indian Forest Service (Central Provs.) (b. 28th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as proby. extra asst. consvr., 7th May, 1912; confd., Oct., 1916; in milty. employ from 29th March, 1918, to 20th Feb., 1919; apptd. to I.F.S., 25th June, 1929; dep. consvr., June, 1929; employed under Rewa Durbar, Feb., 1933; retd., March, 1944.

MACDONALD, Lieut.-Col. D.P., M.B., B.Ch., F.R.S.C., I.M.S., retd.—Apptd. addl. member, medl. bd., India Office, 3rd May, 1946.

MACDONALD, HECTOR IAN, O.B.E., late Military Accounts Dept. (b. 10th Aug., 1885).—Retd., Feb., 1940.†

MACDONALD, IAN HAY, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., Orissa) (b. 19th July, 1915).—Edu. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 16th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Orissa, May, 1939; sub. divl. offr., Apl., 1940; jt. mag. and collr., Oct., 1943.

MACDONALD, THOMAS, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 16th Jan., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Central Provs., 11th Oct., 1929; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934; served under def. dept., govt. of India.

MCDOUGALL, JAMES CURRIE, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agricl. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 5th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1925; offg. princpl., agric. coll., Nagpur, Nov., 1928; confd., Jan., 1934; dir. of agric. (provl.), Central Provs., Oct., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; retd., 5th Jan., 1945.

MCDOUGALL, SIR RAIBEART MACINTYRE, K.C.M.G., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (counsellor to govr. of Burma) (b. 30th Apl., 1892).—Edu. at Greenock Academy and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1914, and posted to Burma; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919; under-sec. to govt., Sept., 1920; regr., high ct., Rangoon (offg.), March, 1923; confd., Nov., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1926; confd., June, 1930; secty., local govt. dept., March, 1931; secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma, March, 1934; and again, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1935; secty., home dept., Oct., 1939; commr., confd., Apl., 1941; counsellor to govr., Nov., 1941; K.C.M.G., Jan., 1945.

MCDOWALL, ROGER GORDON, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. Nov., 1886).—Retd., June, 1941.†

MACDOWELL, KENNETH MACLEAN, O.B.E., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Burma) (b. 16th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. engr. Jan., 1940; ch. engr. (confd.) Dec., 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1945; l.p.r. from May, 1946.

MACEDWARD, REV. LACHLAN, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 25th Apl., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 15th Feb., 1926; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1928; senr. ditto, Feb., 1938; retd., Aug., 1944.

MELHINNY, GEOFFREY WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (Sind) (b. 16th December, 1900).—Educ. at Rugby and Corpus Christi, Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 5th Dec., 1924, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., May, 1928; under-secty. to govt., home, etc., depts., Sept., 1929; member, legisl. council, Feb., 1932; offg. collr., Sept., 1933; temp. addl. dep. secty., home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1934; collr., Sind., Nov., 1937; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1939; sec. to govt., Bombay, gen. and educn. dept. June, 1944; ch. sec. to govt. of Sind, Apl., 1945; rev. commr. and sec. to govt. (rev. dept.) March, 1946.

MACEVOY, HENRY BRIAN, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., (b. 19th Sept., 1899).—Date of first commn., 3rd Feb., 1931; joined Burma medl. dept., Nov., 1934; on mil. serv. Dec., 1941; inspr.-genl. prisons, 21st Feb., 1946.

MACFARQUHAR, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (director-general, disposals) (b. 6th Nov., 1903). Educ. at Royal Academy, Inverness, Univ. of Aberdeen, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Feb., 1933; confd., Sept., 1936; rev. sec. to finl. commrs. and dep. sec. to govt., Punjab, Dec., 1940; dep. sec., dept. of supply, govt. of India, Apl., 1941; dep. dir. ditto, Dec., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1945; dir.-genl., disposals, Apl., 1946.

MACGREGOR, ANDREW DAWSON, F.R.C.V.S. Indian Vety. Service (princpl. veterinary coll., Bengal) (b. 5th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1914; I.A.R.O. from 19th Feb., 1916; offg. supt., civil vety. dept., Jan., 1919; confd. July, 1921; princpl., vety. coll., Bengal, Apl., 1922; also veterinary adv. to govt. of Bengal (in addition) from March, 1938.

MACGREGOR, ROBERT ANDERSON, C.I.E., M.I.Mech.E., M.I.S.I., M.I.E.I., F.R.S.A., M.M.G.I. (metallurg. inspr.) (b. 9th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service as officer on special duty, 25th Feb., 1937; offg. metallurgical inspr., Apl., 1937; confd., June, 1937; dir., munitions prodcn., dept. of supply, July, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

MACGREGOR, ROBERT FORRESTER DOUGLAS, C.I.E., M.C., M.B., Lieut.-Col., ret'd., late I.M.S. (b. 5th Jan., 1885).—Retd., Jan., 1942.†

McGREGOR, WILLIAM MALCOLM, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys (b. 22nd Jan., 1889).—Joined the service, 26th Oct., 1908; dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., Aug., 1919; offg. dep. ditto, Oct., 1928; offg. contr., March, 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; contr. of stores, N.W. rly., Apl., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; ret'd., 1946.

McGUIGAN, HUGH SUTHERLAND, Indian Police (b. 28th May, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt. Bombay, on probn., 23rd Nov., 1936; confd., Sept., 1938.

McGUINNESS, JOHN WILLIAM, M.B.E., Burma Front. Serv., (b. 23rd Feb., 1914).—Apptd. 16th Jan., 1936.

McGUIRE, ROBERT ELY, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 22nd Aug., 1901).—Educ. at the High School, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., fin. dep. Nov., 1930; warden, oil fields, Sept., 1932; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1932; provl., Jan., 1937; confd. Apl., 1937; warden, oil-

fields, July, 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; addtl. sec. reconstr. dept., 1944; sec. transpt. and commns. dept., 1945; offg. commr., 1946.

MCINERY, EDWARD FOLLET, M.B.E., B.A., Ind. C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1910).—Ed. at Swansea Gram. Sch. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, March, 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1938; addl. mag., Dec., 1941; M.B.E., June, 1946.

MCINTOSH, ROBERT, M.B.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., Madras) (b. 23rd May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; M.B.E., Feb., 1937; suptg. engr.; Oct., 1941; ditto confd., May, 1943; ch. engr. June, 1945.

MACINTYRE, JOHN MACLACHLAN, M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Dec., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1928; asst. exec. engr., Baluchistan, Aug., 1935; M.B.E., Jan., 1939; on deprn. under govt. of India, May, 1945.

MCINTYRE, THOMAS, M.B.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (bridge engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 4th March, 1898).—Asst. exec. engr., bridges, Dec., 1926; bridge engr., E.B. rly., Nov., 1938; M.B.E., Jan., 1944.

MACK, EDMUND ELMAR, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 2nd May, 1896).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived, 5th Jan., 1921; sub collr. and jt. mag. July, 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1929; and again Oct., 1932; confd. Jan., 1933; and again Oct., 1942; addl. judge, Hyderabad, June, 1941.

MACKARNES, CUTHBERT GEORGE MILFORD, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service, (Assam) (b. 15th Apl., 1890).—Joined the serv. as asst. consvr., 7th Dec., 1912; in mill. employ. from 23rd Nov., 1915 to 14th March, 1919; dep. consvr., June, 1919; pers. asst. to consvr., Assam, Apl., 1932 senr. consvr., Feb., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; ret'd., Mar., 1945.

MACKAY, THOMAS IAN SUTHERLAND, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 25th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; suptg. ditto, Aug., 1940; ret'd., Aug., 1944.

McKEEVER, RONALD FRASER, Indian Police (b. 20th Aug., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Central Provs., on probn., 14th Nov., 1935; confd., Apl., 1938; supt., Eastern states, jt. police force, Sept., 1943.

MacKEITH, ALEXANDER ADAM, Indian Police (b. 19th March, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Dec., 1937; confd., Dec., 1939; King's Police Medal, Mar., 1940; a.d.c. to govr., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1941 to Apl., 1942.

McKELVIE, GEORGE MAURICE, O.B.E., B.Sc., Ind. Serv. of Engrs. (suft. engr., Central (p.w.d.)) (b. 21st Feb., 1896).—Joined the service (Central Provs.) as asst. exec. engr., 8th Dec., 1920; offg. exec. engr., and under-secty. to govt., July, 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1928; under-secty., p.w.d., Apl., 1937; on spec. duty with govt. of India, Nov., 1937; exec. engr., cent. p.w.d., Apl., 1938; suptg. engr., July, 1940; consltg. engr., govt. of India (roads), posts and air, Nov., 1942; ditto, war transport, July, 1944; O.B.E., Feb., 1945; ditto (roads) Nov., 1945.

McKELVIE, JOHN NORMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Aug., 1914).—Educ. at Malvern and Corpus Christi Coll.,

Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; a.r.p. offr., Sept., 1940; sub-divl. offr., May, 1943.

MACKENZIE, REV. DONALD FRANCIS, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 31st March, 1885).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 30th Sept., 1919; junr. chaplain, Church of Scotland, Sept., 1921; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1927; presdty. senr. chaplain, Church of Scotland, Bengal, Aug., 1937; retd., 10th Aug., 1942.

MACKENZIE, REV. DONALD STEWART, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senior chaplain) (b. 2nd March, 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 24th Apl., 1919; junr. chaplain, Apl., 1921; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1930.

MACKENZIE, SIR DUNCAN GEORGE, K.C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 1st July, 1883).—Educ. at Winchester, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 29th Nov., 1907, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, June, 1912; asst. commr., Ajmer, March, 1918; city mag., Peshawar, Nov., 1914; asst. commr., N.W.F. Prov., Dec., 1915; asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, Nov., 1918; first asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1919; sec. to the agent to the govr.-genl., Punjab states, Nov., 1921; pol. agent, southern Rajputana states, Nov., 1926; on foreign service in Bharatpur state, Feb., 1928; C.I.E., June 1930; pol. agent in Bhopal, Nov., 1930; resdt. Jaipur, Apl., 1931; ditto and in western states of Rajputana, Jan., 1932; pol. agent, E. Rajputana states, March, 1933; resdt., Hyderabad, July, 1933; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1937; retd., Feb., 1941.†

MACKENZIE, JAMES MACAULAY, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 20th June, 1887).—Retd., 20th June, 1942.

MACKCOWN, JOHN AINLIE, C.I.E., Indian C.S., (commr., Punjab) (b. 27th Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Radley and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1925; arrived, 6th Dec., 1925 and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; deputed to Bahawalpur state, Nov., 1929; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Oct., 1932; dep. sec. govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Feb., 1936; offg. jt. sec., ditto, July, 1936; and again Oct., 1936; addl. dep. sec. legisl. dept., govt. of India, March, 1937; dep. sec. dept. of indus. and lab., April to July, 1937; addl. dep. sec., legislative dept., Aug. to Oct., 1937; sec. dep. communs. dept., Nov. to Dec., 1937; addl. dep. sec., legislative dept., Jan. to March, 1938; dep. sec. communs. dept., May to July, 1938; addl. dep. sec., legislative dept., July, 1938; dep. sec., dept. of supply, Sept., 1939; jt. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of supply, Apl., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1942; commr., Nov., 1945.

MACKIE, JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 1st Jan., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 10th Feb., 1928; exec. engr., Feb., 1937.

MACKINNON, JOHN, C.I.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 30th Jan., 1886).—Retd., Jan., 1941.†

MACKINTOSH, CHARLES CALDER, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th May, 1886).—Retd., June, 1941.

MACKINTOSH, REV. KENNETH, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 10th Jan., 1903) (junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland).—Apptd. chaplain on

probn., ch. of Scotland, 27th Apl., 1928; junr. chaplain, Apl., 1930.

MACKLIN, SIR ALBERT SORTAIN ROMER, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 4th March, 1890).—Educ. at Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 1st Dec., 1913, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; judge and asst. sess. judge, Apl., 1922; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, June, 1923; offg. judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1924; regr. high ct., appellate side, Nov., 1926; judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1929; judl. asst. and addl. sess. judge, Aden, June, 1929; offg. secy. to govt., legal dept., June, 1931; judl. commr. in the states of western India, July, 1932; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1934; confd., June, 1935; kt., June, 1946.

MACKNEY, HERBERT HODDY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st May, 1886).—Educ. at Liverpool Institute High Sch., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from June to July, 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; acting judge, high ct., Rangoon, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1933; retd., 11th June, 1943.

MACLACHLAN, DUNCAN, C.S.I., C.I.E., Ind. C.S. (commr., Bombay) (b. 9th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Mountjoy Sch., Dublin, and Univ. Coll., Galway; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 21st Nov., 1913, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on mily. duty from Aug., 1916 to Nov., 1919; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindhi translator to govt., Apl., 1920; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept. (tempy.), May, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Sept., 1928; confd., Jan., 1931; pol. agent, Bhor, Apl., 1932; offg. commr., Apl., 1935; confd., 2nd gr. commr., Nov., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1941; confd., 1st gr. commr., Dec., 1943; C.S.I., June, 1945; advr. to govr. of Bombay, to Apl., 1946.

MACLAGAN, SIR EDWARD DOUGLAS, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Retd., Dec., 1924.*

MACLEAN, CHARLES ALLAN, C.B.E., M.C., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Bihar) (b. 22nd May, 1892).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 5th Dec., 1920; cane commr., edc. and jt. regr., co-ops., Bihar, Nov., 1939; C.B.E., Jan., 1941; off. dir. agric., Bihar, Apl., 1935; again March, 1943.

MACLEAN, EVAN VICTOR, late Indian State Rlys., E.I. Rly. (b. 6th Apl., 1887).—Retd., July, 1938.*

MCLEAN, ANGUS, B.Sc., late Indian Agricul. Service (Burma) (b. 27th Apl., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir., 16th Dec., 1921; retd., Apl., 1946.

MCLEAN, ARTHUR ERROL JOHN, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (inspr. of schools) (b. 28th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service as headmaster govt. high school, Maymyo, 30th Jan., 1922; offg. asst. dir. of public instrcn., Aug., 1933; inspr. of schools, Nov., 1933; offg. asst. dir. of public instrcn., Apl., 1935; inspr. of schools, Rangoon, Jan., 1937; asst. dir. of pub. instrcn., June, 1938; confd., Apl., 1939; retd., Dec., 1945.

MCLEAN, REV. LAUGHLAN, M.A., B.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Bombay) (b. 18th Feb., 1886).—Retd., March, 1939.*

MCLEAN, SIR ROBERT, Kt., B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 3rd Feb. 1884).—Retd., July, 1929.*

MCLELLAN, REV. DUNCAN TAIT HUTCHISON, M.A., B.D., late Ind. Eccles. Estabt. (Madras) 29th Nov., 1893).—Apptd. chaplain on probn. [ch. of Scotland, 11th Feb., 1922; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1923; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1933; presdy., senr. chaplain, Madras, Apl., 1938; confd., July, 1939; retd., July, 1945.

MACLEOD, ARCHIBALD CORRIE, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), (name changed from MacNabb, A. C.), Indian C.S. (commr., Punjab) (b. 1st Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; city mag., Delhi, Apl., 1914; sec., muncl. cttee., Delhi, Sept., 1915; again, city mag., Delhi, Oct., 1916; spl., duty on staff of lieut.-govr., Punjab, from Oct., 1917 to March, 1918; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1923; dep. commr. (prov.), Nov., 1924; senr., secty. to finl. commrs., Punjab, Nov., 1928; offg. dep.; sec. to govt., Punjab, dev. dept., Apl., 1933; offg. commr., May, 1934; admin. Lahore municity. and chrnm., Lahore impr. trust, Apl., 1937; commr. (perm.), May, 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; fin. commr. (devel.) Dec., 1945.

MACLEOD, SIR NORMAN CRANSTOUN, Kt., B.A., Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept.—Retd., June, 1926.*

MCLEOD, WILLIAM NORMAN, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs., (Punjab) (b. 15th June, 1885).—Retd., June, 1940.†

MCINTIC, JOHN FRANCIS, Indian Police (b. 29th Aug., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1936; confd., Nov., 1938.

McMAHON, SIR ARTHUR HENRY, G.C.M.G., G.C.V.O., K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Col., Indian Army, retd., late foreign dept., govt. of India.—Retd. from Indian Army, March, 1915.*

MACMILLAN, JOHN NIVEN, Indian State Rlys. (dist. signal engr., E.I., Rly.) (b. 20th Oct., 1901).—Asst. signal engr., Oct., 1936; dist. ditto, Nov., 1935.

MACMILLAN, NEIL BOYD (commercial offr., electricity bch., Punjab) (b. 14th Jan., 1880).—Joined the service, 23rd Apl., 1928, as sales manager; asst. comml. manager, Sept., 1931; comml. manager, Apl., 1933; comml. offr., Jan., 1934.

MACNABB, ARCHIBALD CORRIE, C.I.E., C.S. (name changed to Macleod, A. C.) [q.v.].

MCNAIR, SIR GEORGE DOUGLAS, M.B.E., late Judl. Dept., Bengal (Calcutta).—Apptd. actg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 28th Apl., 1933; confd. Apl., 1934; kt. Jan., 1943; retd., Nov., 1945.

MCNAIR, SIR ROBERT HILL, Kt., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1877).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

MCNALLY, SIDNEY JAMES, India Office (actg. hr. exec. offr., acctnt.-gen'l's dept.) (b. 11th June, 1902).—Apptd. cler. off., Mar., 1932; actg. exec. offr., Apl., 1940; actg. hr. ditto, Apl., 1942.

MCNEIL, JOHN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 2nd Dec., 1906).—Ed. at Rangoon Univ. and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., genl. dept., Rangoon, Apl., 1936 to Apl., 1937; asst. dist. judge, Oct., 1937; dist. and sess. judge, 1940-41, and again, 1945.

MCNEILL, KENNETH JOHN, O.B.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly) (b. 13th July, 1980).—Joined the service as traffic

probrn., 17th Feb., 1911; asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1914; dist. traffic supt., June, 1918; divl. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; offg. supt., watch and ward June, 1930; offg. dep. traffic manager variously, from May, 1933; divl. transportn. supt., Dec., 1934; dep. traffic man., Apl., 1940; regr. contr. rly. priorities under war transpt. dept., July, 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1945; retd., July, 1945.

MACONACHIE, SIR RICHARD ROY, K.B.E., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Sept., 1885).—Retd., July, 1937.*

MACPHERSON, DONALD, C.I.E., M.A., Ind. C.S. (mag. and collr. Bengal) (b. 22nd March, 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Rl.
