Burma Office, Feb., 1943 to Apl., 1944; dep. commr., Oct., 1945; sec. to govr., Burma, July, 1946.

NASIM ALI, Sir SYED, M.A., B.L. (late Bengal Judl. Dept.)—Apptd. addl. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 13th Nov., 1933; apptd. judge, ditto, Oct., 1936, to Dec., 1946; kt., Jan., 1945.

NASIM HUSAIN, M.A., M.B.E., Meteorological Dept., (b. 21st Oct., 1906).—P.C.S. exec. (Punjab), Nov., 1929; serv. lent to govt. of India, Dec., 1931; private sec. to High Commr. for India, London, July, 1937; M.B.E., June, 1941; returned to Punjab, Dec., 1941; admin. offr. cent., P.W.D., Jan., 1942; dir. of admin. and co-ord., ind. and supplies dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1946; ch. admin. offr., Indian meteorological dept., June, 1946.

NASIRUDDIN SAHIB, Khan Bahadur Saiyed, B.A., Indian Police, Madras (b. 4th July, 1903).—Promoted to the Indian police as supt., June, 1943.

NATARAJAN, JAGADISH, B.A., Hons. (Bombay) (b. 17th May, 1902).—Apptd. dep. principl. information offcr., home dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1938; principl. ditto, dept. of inform. and broadcasting, Nov., 1941.

NATH, AJUDHIA (see Ajudhia Nath).

NATH, AMAR (see Amar Nath).

NATH, GAURI (see Gauri Nath).

NATH, GYAN (see Gyan Nath).

NATH, JAGAN BHAGAT (see Jagan Nath, B.).

NATH, JOGENDRA CHANDRA, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 22nd Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Jan., 1925; dep. consvr., Jan., 1930.

NATH, PREM (see Prem Nath).

NATH, SURATH (see Surath Nath).

NATU, W. R., B.Sc., (b. 16th Sept., 1920).—Joined the service in 1938 as asst. to econ. advr. govt. of India; Bombay Educ. Serv., 1939; prof. commerce, prof. adv. banking, prof. econ., Sydenham Coll. of Commerce, Bombay; sec. post-war devel. cttee., Bombay, Co-op. Inst.; statisl. advr. govt. of India, dept. agric., 1944; sec., prices sub-committee, 1945.

NAUBAT RAI, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 7th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 1st Apl.,

1922; asst. engr. (prov. service), Apl., 1926; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; offg. exec. engr., May, 1930; confd., Jan., 1936.

NAWAB SINGH, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 9th Apl., 1907).—Educ. at University of Nagpur and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1935 to Apl., 1937; ditto, sub-permt., Oct., 1938; legal remembrancer, May, 1946.

NAWABZADA, AMINULLAH KHAN, A.Inst.T., late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 16th Aug., 1893).—Asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1915; divl. transpn. offcr., May, 1935; retd., 25th Nov., 1944.

NAYAK, PANGAL MANJAPPA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 27th Dec., 1916).—Ed. at Christian High Sch. Udipi, South Kanara, Madras Univ., Imp. Coll. of Science and Technology, London, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd., on probn., 29th Sept. 1939; confd., 24th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Apl., 1942; on div. sec. (public) May, 1946.

NAYAK, PANGAL RAMANATHA, M.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 29th March, 1911).—Educ. at Christian High Sch., Udipi, Univ. of Madras, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies London; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; dep. sec. (supply), govt. of India, Jan., 1944; M.B.E., Jan., 1945; sec., coalfields cttee., Dec., 1945.

NAYAR, CHETTUR, Govindan, B.A., Bar-at-Law, Judl. dept. Madras (law sec. to the govt. of Orissa) (b. 23rd Dec., 1881).—Apptd. dist. munsif, June, 1910; offg. dist. judge, June, 1933; confd., Aug., 1933; sec., govt. of Orissa, law and comm. dept., Apl., 1936.

NAYAR, SIR CHETTUR MADHAVAN, late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. Jan., 1879).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

NAYAR, KRISHNAPILLAI KARUKAIL KARUNAKARAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 11th Sept., 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930; and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; judge, small causes ct., Dec., 1936.

NAYAR, MANAKAMPAD KESAVANUNNI, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 25th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927; per. asst. ch. consvr. of forests, Apl., 1945.

NAYAR, PADUNHARANKUNNATH THAZATHAYIL RAMAN, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1910).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Boys' High Sch., Calicut, Univ. of Madras, and St. Catherine's Coll. Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934; sub-collr., Jan., 1937; city mag. (1st cl.), Feb., 1938; sub-collr., March, 1939; judl. trng., July, 1942 to Jan., 1944; addtl. dis. and sess. judge, Feb., 1944; again, Nov., 1944; regr., high ct., Madras, Jul., 1945.

NAYLOR, LEONARD BROWN, C.B.E., late Burma Frontier Service (b. 19th March, 1887).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., March, 1914; asst. supt. (B.F.S.), July, 1923; dep. commr. (permt.), July, 1939; C.B.E., June, 1943; retd., Dec., 1944.

NAYUDU, BANDI VENKATA SRI HARI RAO (see Sri Hari, R. N. B. V.).

NAYUDU, BHASKARA RAO, T. (see Bhaskara Rao Nayudu, T.).

NAYUDU, BUNDY SRIRAMULU (see Sriramulu Nayudu B.).

NAYUDU, PAKALA VENKATARAMANA RAO, Rao Bahadur (see Venkataramana Rao Nayudu, Pakala, Rao Bahadur).

NAZIR, AHMED, Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 10th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd., on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 28th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Sind as asst. collr.; offg. var., July, 1943; dep. sec., govt. of Sind (food) and civ. supplies, Oct., 1945.

NAZIR, ALAM, SHAH, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.) (b. 4th March, 1906).—Joined the service as dep. supt. (prob.), 2nd Jan., 1929; confd., Jan., 1929; apptd. to I.P. as asst. supt., 31st Aug., 1930; offg. supt., Apl., 1931; and again, May, 1933; confd., May, 1943.

NAZIRUDDIN, KAMALUDDIN, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 31st Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 26th June, 1922; asst. exec. engr., June, 1923; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; confd., June, 1932; transfed. from Madras to Bihar and Orissa, jt. cadre, Apl., 1936; ex. engr., P.W.D., Bihar.

NAZR-UL-BAKAR, SHEIKH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 21st Dec., 1913).—Educ. at Denny's High Sch., Rawalpindi, Govt., High Sch., Murree, and Punjab Univ.; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1939; emplt. advr. to govt., Aug., 1942; addl. dist. mag., Apl., 1943; dep. dir. civ. supplies, Dec., 1943.

NEALE, REGINALD FRANCIS, Indian Police (b. 8th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 5th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Apl., 1933.

NEALE, REGINALD KINGSLEY, Lieut.-Col., Mily. Accts. Dept. (b. 19th July, 1897).—First commn., Apl., 1915; joined mily. accts. dept. as offcr., Cl. II, July, 1935.

NEALE, SIR WALTER GORDON, Lieut.-Col., K.C.V.O., C.I.E., I.A. (retd.) late India Office (b. 2nd Oct., 1880).—Retd., July, 1930; pol. A.D.C. to sec. of state, Sept., 1933 to Apl., 1945.†

NEEDHAM, JOHN CLISSOLD, Comdr., R.N.R., Burma Marine Service (b. 20th Feb., 1907).—Prev. serv. with colonial marine 1934-39; nautical surveyor, Rangoon, Sept., 1939; ditto and sea transpt. offr., Nov., 1945.

NEEDHAM, SIR RICHARD ARTHUR, Kt., C.I.E., D.S.O., M.D., B.Sc., F.R.C.P., D.P.H., Col., I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 31st July, 1877).—Retd., Oct., 1930.*

NEGI, THAKUR SINGH, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 30th Aug., 1903).—Joined the serv. as dep. supt. of police, 1st Jan., 1927; confd., Dec., 1928; supt. of police (confd.), June, 1942.

NEHPAL SINGH, B.A., M.R.A.S., Indian Educl. Service (asst. d.p.i., United Provs.) (b. 14th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as princpl. jubilee inter. coll., Lucknow, 4th July, 1922; hd. master, govt. high schl., Bareilly, Apl., 1924; princpl., govt. inter. coll., Almora, Oct., 1927; offg. inspr. of schools, June, 1928; confd., July, 1932; asst. dir. of public instrn., variously, from Apl., 1935; confd., Oct., 1937.

NEHRU, BRAJ KUMAR, M.B.E., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (b. 4th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at the Govt. Intermediate College, Allahabad,

University of Allahabad, the School of Econ. London, and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service 25th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. offcr., Apl., 1937; offcr. on spl. duty, fin. dept., Nov., 1939; under-sec., ditto, Sept., 1940; dep. ditto, Apl., 1944; M.B.E., Jan., 1945; dep. sec. govt. of India, fin. dept., Apl., 1944; jt. sec., ditto, May, 1946.

NEHRU, BRIJLAL, M.A., Barr., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 5th May, 1884).—Retd., May, 1939.*

NEHRU, JAWAHARLAL, PANDIT (b. 1889).—External Affairs and Commonwealth Relations member of Govt.-Genl.'s Council, Sept., 1946.

NEHRU, RATAN KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S., Impl. Customs Service (commr. salt rev. (b. 10th Apl., 1902)).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1925; arrived, 5th Dec., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1929; Apl., 1932; and again, March, 1933; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, March, 1934; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1935; offg. collr., June, 1936; first secty. to central bd. of rev., etc., Feb., 1937; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1937; first secty., cent. bd. of rev., March, 1938; temp. addl. dep. secty. to govt. of India, commerce dept., Jan., 1939; commr., cent. excises and salt, N.W. India, from 1st to 10th Dec., 1939; collr., ditto, Dec., 1939; jt. sec., dept. of supply, G.I., July, 1942; ditto, commerce, Oct. 1944.

NEHRU, SHRI SHRIDHAR, M.A., Ph.D., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Agra Coll. Sch., India, Allahabad; Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, and Heidelberg; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 1st Dec., 1913, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., March, 1918; under-sec. to govt., Apl., to July, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1923; confd., June, 1930; sec. to govt., industries and educn. dept., June, 1931; dir. of publicity and reforms offr., Jan., 1932; member, Impl. council of agricl. research, June, 1933; mag. and collr. again from Dec., 1933.

NEIL, JOHN FREDERICK, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 12th March, 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, store dept., India Office, Nov., 1919; clerical offcr., Jan., 1920; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. offr., March, 1926.

NELSON, SIR ARTHUR EDWARD, K.C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A. late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 17th Apl., 1875).—Retd., Nov., 1934.*

NELSON, WILLIAM HENRY, C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th June, 1880).—Retd., Jan., 1940.†

NEOGI, PANCHANAN, M.A., Ph.D., F.C.S. (Lond.), late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. 4th Oct., 1883).—Retd., Dec., 1938.*

NEOGI, PROBHA CHANDRA, O.B.E., Indian Serv. of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 31st July, 1900).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 22nd Jan., 1926; offg. exec. engr., May, 1929; confd., Jan., 1935; O.B.E., June, 1945.

NETER PARKASH MOHAM, M.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 12th June, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927; M.B.E., Jan., 1942; fuel contr., Mar., 1943; dep. sec. to govt. (civil supply dept.), March, 1946.

NETHERSOLE, MICHAEL HENRY BRADDON,

C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., D.S.O., Indian Civil Service (commr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; arrived, 17th Dec., 1919, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1925; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927 to Nov., 1933; sec., inform. dept. and price contr., Nov., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1942; commr. (confd.), Nov., 1945; C.S.I., Jan., 1946.

NEVILLE, COLLIN MAXWELL WRIGHT, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 19th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st May, 1921; supt., Apl., 1926; offg. dep. inspr.-genl. of police, Orissa, Aug., 1942; D.I.G. (confd.) Oct., 1944.

NEWELL, REV. CHARLES JOHN CUTHBERT, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 29th Jan., 1909).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bombay Diocese, 2nd June, 1944.

NEWMAN, CARL DAMIEN, M.B., B.S., Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as dist. medl. offcr., E.B. Rly., 3rd Dec., 1924; offg. ch. medl. offcr., variously, from June, 1931; confd. B. & A. rly., Feb., 1940.

NEWSAM, WILLIAM OSWALD, Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 13th March, 1892).—Educ. at Jesus College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mily. duty from Nov., 1917 to March, 1919; sub-collr., Oct., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1926; offg. regr., high court, July, 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Chittoor, and addtl. judge, court of resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1935; ditto, Mysore, Jan., 1936 to July, 1937; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1938; high ct., Madras, July to Oct., 1937, and Feb. to May, 1939; dist. and sess. judge, Chittoor and addtl. judge, court of resdt. and judl. commr. of Coorg, 1939; dist. and sess. judge Chittock, June, 1945.

NEWTON, BERNARD ST. JOHN, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (Central Provs.) (b. 13th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; ditto and under-sec., p.w.d., Nov., 1925; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1931; confd., 1937; ch. engr., March, 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1945; retd., Apl., 1945.

NIAZ MUHAMMAD KHAN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 12th June, 1907).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1933; under-secty., fin. dept., Apl., 1937; K.-i.-H. Medal, 1938.

NIAZ, MAQBUL MOHAMMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 5th Nov., 1917).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1940, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.

NIBLETT, L. H., B.A., (mag. and collr., Un. Provs.) (b. 10th June, 1894).—Joined the U.P. civil (exec.) serv., 1st May, 1916; mag. and collr. (confd.) Nov., 1943.

NICHOLAS, GEORGE ATHANAS, B.A., Burma Land Records (b. 18th June, 1894).—Apptd. asst. supt. land records, 1st Nov., 1914; supt. ditto 7th Nov., 1918; left behind in Burma; asst. dir. land records, 16th Oct., 1945; confd., 4th Sept., 1945.

NICHOLL, REV. ESMOND MCKILLOP, M.A., M.C. late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Punjab) (b. 16th March, 1891).—Retd., Sept., 1941.†

NICHOLSON, JOHN PATRICK, O.B.E., United Provs., C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 22nd June, 1907; dep. collr., Apl., 1911; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1931; confd., Sept., 1934; O.B.E., June, 1941.

NICHOLSON, SIR JOHN WILFRED, B.A., C.I.E., Ind. Forest Serv. (consvr., Orissa) (b. 13th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 3rd March, 1916; provl. sylviculturist, Dec., 1921; on depn. to Kenya for 3 years from Sept., 1927; dep. consvr., Feb., 1931; offg. consvr. variously, from Sept., 1931; consvr., Nov., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; kt. Jan., 1946.

NICHOLSON, MARK ALLEYNE, C.I.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (C.M.O., Central India) (b. 10th June, 1885).—First commn. dated 31st July, 1909; civil surgn., Kurram, Oct., 1922; M.O., S.W. Scouts, Dec., 1923; offg. resdy. surgn. in Persian Gulf, Sept., 1924; agency surgn., Hazara dist., Oct., 1928; ditto, and C.M.O., Rajputana, March, 1931; C.M.O., Central India, and resdy. surgn., Indore, Apl., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

NICHOLSON, PETER FRANCIS COBHAM, Capt. I.A. (b. 11th July, 1914).—Apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., Oct., 1939; under-sec. to resdt., Baroda and Gujarat states, May, 1944; under-sec. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1944.

NICOL, CAMERON MACDONALD, C.I.E., M.A., M.D., D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Dept. of Public Health, Punjab (dir.) (b. 20th July, 1891).—First commn., 9th Feb., 1923; asst. dir. of public health, Rawalpindi, 18th Feb., 1934; dir. of public health, Aug., 1936; on depn. var., July, 1937 to Oct., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1941; on mil. duty, Apl., 1941 to Jan., 1946; dir. of pub. health, Jan., 1946.

NICOLSON, JOHN FRANCIS HUNTER, O.B.E., M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 28th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Apl., 1920; offg. exec. engr., June, 1925; confd., Oct., 1927; ch. public works offcr., F.S.S., Apl., 1935; O.B.E., June, 1943; on mil. serv., Apl., 1945 to Jan., 1946; ch. engr. bldgs. and roads, br., 1946.

NIGAM, BISAN NARAIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 4th Apl., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1942.

NIGEL JONES, M. E. (see Jones, M. E. Nigel).

NIGHTINGALE, ARTHUR WINFIELD, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th Nov., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939 and re-employed, Jan., 1941.

NIGHTINGALE, WILFRED ANDREW, B.A. (Mech. Sc.) Cantab., Indian State Rlys. (mech. engr. and transp. (power) offcr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 22nd Feb., 1901).—Asst. works manager, junior scale, E.I. Rly., Feb., 1926, mech. engr. and transp. (power) offcr., senior scale, Nov., 1938.

NILAKANTA MAHADEVA AYYAR, C.I.E., M.A. (Mad.), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 14th May, 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived in India, 2nd Jan., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; and sub.-divl. offcr., June, 1924; at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dept. (cent. rev.), March, 1925, as offg. asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta; confd., Dec., 1925; asst. collr. of customs, Madras, June, 1926; offg. mag. and

collr., Bengal, July, 1928; and again, March, 1932; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Nov., 1928; offg. ditto, Bengal, Oct., 1930; mag. and collr., July, 1935; addl. dep. secty., dept. of indus. and lab., govt. of India, Apl., 1936; dep. secty., dept. of labour, Nov., 1937; chairman, coal mines stowing bd., Nov., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; confd. mag. and collr., Aug., 1942; dir. and sec., civil supplies, Apl., 1943.

NILAKANTAN, V., Rao Bahadur, Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Sept., 1902).—Joined, 13th Apl., 1926; sec. rly. bd., Mar., 1946.

NILKANTH SHIRAM JATAR (see Jatar, N. S.).

NIL RATAN DHAR, M.Sc. (Cal.), D.Sc. (Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S., Indian Educl. Service (b. 2nd Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as prof'r., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, 19th July, 1919; with Allahabad Univ. from July, 1922; dep. dir. of public instruction, Apl., 1938; confd., Sept., 1939.

NIND, WILLIAM WALKER, C.I.E., B.A.; late Impl. Customs Service (b. 26th Sept., 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1937*; served in India office from Feb., 1940.

NINNIS, ROBERT DOUGLAS KIALLMARK, late Indian Police (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 30th Apl., 1897).—Retd., 10th March, 1940.†

NIRANJAN DAS, GULHATI LALA, Indian Service of Engrs (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Nov., 1904).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., Oct., 1926; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936; under-sec., P.W.D. (irrign.), Dec., 1941; personal asst. to sec., ditto, Jan., 1945; under-sec. ditto, Feb., 1945; on depn. to central bd. of irrign., Aug., 1945.

NIRINJAN PRASAD, GOYAL, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th Apl., 1903).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 28th Oct., 1905; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

NIVAS, HARI KRISHNA, Ind. Serv. of Engrs. (b. 9th May, 1905) (exec. engr., P.W.D., Bihar).—Joined the serv. as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 14th Nov., 1928; exec. engr., Oct., 1937 under-sec., P.W.D., Bihar, March, 1939.

NIX-JAMES, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 22nd March, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 28th March, 1920; asst. execut. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1927.

NIXON, SIR JOHN CARSON, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 21st Sept., 1887).—Retd., Feb., 1941.†

NIYOGI, SIR MACHRAJ BHAWANI SHANKAR, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Cent. Provs. (b. 30th Aug., 1886).—Apptd. offg. addl. judl. commr., June, 1930; confd., Sept., 1930; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1936; kt. June, 1944; retd., Aug., 1946.

NIZAM-UD-DIN, KHAN BAHADUR, Indian State Railways (N.W. Rly.) (inspr.) (b. 3rd July, 1893).—Asst. engr., 17th Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Aug., 1935; sup. of works, Sept., 1938; confd. as inspr., Jan., 1944 (ports and air dept.); senr. govt. inspr. rlys. (confd.), Jan., 1945.

NOBLE, REV. REGINALD HARRY, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Punjab).—Retd., Oct., 1942.

NOBLE, THOMAS ALEXANDER FRASER, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 29th Apl., 1918).—Educ. at Rose's Academical Instn., Nairn

and Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. on probn. after seln. iq. Aug., 1940, and served in N.W.F. Prov. as asst. commr.

NOEL, EDWARD WILLIAM CHARLES, C.I.E., D.S.O., Lieut.-Col., I.A., late Indian Political Service, (b. 14th Apl., 1886).—Retd., Apl., 1941.†

NOON, SIR FIROZ KHAN, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E.—Educ. at Chief's Coll., Lahore, and Wadham College, Oxford; advocate of Lahore high court and member of the Punjab legislative council from 1921; minister for local self-govt., Punjab, 1927; educ. minister, Punjab 1930; high commr. for India, 1st July, 1930; K.C.I.E., May, 1937; K.C.S.I., Oct., 1941; labour member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Oct., 1941; defence member, ditto, July, 1942 to Aug., 1945.

NORBURY, HARRY CARTER, J.P., M.Inst.T., late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 18th Oct., 1883).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

NORMAN, CHARLES ARCHIBALD KENSIT, O.B.E., B.A., late India Office (b. 12th March, 1884).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; solicitor, supreme court, 1909; served on the legal staff, public trustee's office, and H.M. customs and excise, Jan., 1910 to Feb., 1914; apptd. clerk, legal adviser's dept., 2nd Feb., 1914; asst. solicitor, Aug., 1933; O.B.E., June, 1941; rettd., Mar., 1944.

NORMAN, DOUGLAS REAY, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th Apl., 1890).—Retd., July, 1940.†

NORMAN-WALKER, HUGH SELBY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 17th Dec. 1916).—Educ. at Sherborne and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

NORMAND, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM BLYTH, C.I.E., M.A., D.Sc., late Indian Meteorological Dept. (b. 10th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1913, as impl. meteorologist; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), Feb., 1916 to Apl., 1919; offg. dir.-gen. of observatories from June to Oct., 1924; and from March 1927; rettd., 10th Sept., 1944; kt. Jan., 1945; re-employed on spec. duty Sept., 1944 to May, 1945.

NORONHA, MARK STANISLAUS, B.A., LL.B., Judicial dept., Bombay (b. 19th July, 1889).—Apptd. judge, small cause ct. Bombay, 30th Dec., 1928; ch. judge, ditto, Oct., 1943.

NORONHA, RONALD CARLTON VIVIAN PIEDADE M.B.E., B.A. B.Sc. Indian C.S. (asst. commr., C.P.) (b. 14th May, 1916).—Educ. at St. Aloysius European High Sch., Vizagapatam, Madras Univ., University Coll., London, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 8th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in C.P. as asst. commr.; contr. of food supplies, June, 1943; dep. dir. food (distrbn.) May, 1944; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

NORTH, CORNELIUS WILLIAM, M.B.E., Bur. Front. Serv., (b. 15th Feb., 1917).—Apptd. 11th Jan., 1939; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

NOTTAGE, JOHN BOWTELL, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Feb., 1891).—Retd., July, 1939.*

NOYCE, SIR FRANK, K.C.S.I., C.B.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—(b. 4th June, 1878). Retd., Nov., 1937.*

NUR ILAHI, KHAN BAHADUR, Shaikh, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Punjab) (b. 8th July, 1884).—Retd., July, 1939.*

NUR, KHWAJA MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Nur, Khwaja).

NUR MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, Shaikh, O.B.E., M.A., late Punjab C.S. (b. 26th Apl., 1886).—Retd., †

NUR, MUHAMMAD T. SHAIK, Khan Sahib, B.A. (Sind) (b. 12th Dec., 1898).—Joined Oct., 1922; held non-gaz. and gaz. appts.; dep. collr., Nov., 1938; gen. asst. to rev. commr. and asst. sec. to govt. of rev. dept., Mar., 1944; addl. dist. mag., June, 1946.

NYO, JOSHUA POO, K.S.M., Burma Front. Serv., (b. 3rd Jan., 1899).—Apptd. asst. supt., June, 1926; civ. aff. serv. up to Oct., 1945; dep. commr., Oct., 1945.

NYUN, MAUNG, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. of customs, Burma) (b. 30th Dec., 1909).—Educ. at A. V. High Sch., Thaton, Rangoon Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Stud., Lond.; joined the serv., 10th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Dec., 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec., com. and indus. dept., Burma, March, 1937; asst. collr. of customs, July, 1941; dep. ditto, Aug., 1940; addl. ditto, Jan., 1941; collr. (confd.), Feb., 1941; civ. aff. serv. to Jan., 1946; collr. of customs, 1946.

NYUN, MAUNG SOE, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 6th March, 1906).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., Rangoon Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 3rd Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Apl., 1938; under trng. in office of acct.-genl., Burma, Apl., 1941; sec., pub. wks. and rehab. dept., Jan., 1946.

NYUN, U BA, Burma Civ. Serv. (cl. I).—Apptd. 8th Apl., 1942; civ. aff. serv. Sept., 1945; under sec., commerce and supply dept., Jan., 1946.

OAK, VIDYADHAR GOVIND, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 18th May, 1909).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1934; confd. as dist. and sess. judg., Oct., 1941.

OAKDEN, SIR RALPH, Khv C.S.I., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 13th July, 1871).—Retd., Dec., 1930.*

OAKLEY, FRANK STUART, Indian Police (asst. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 8th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 22nd Nov., 1923; offg. dist. supt., 1927; confd., July, 1939; cent. intell. offcr., Central Provs. and Berar, Apl., 1939, to Jan., 1944.

OBAIDULLAH, ABU ZAFAR, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 28th Feb., 1911).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 1st July, 1931; confd., Sept., 1933; confd., supt., July, 1943.

O'BRIEN, J. A., late Civil Aviation Directorate (b. 1st Feb., 1881).—Joined the service as chief inspr. of aircraft (temp.), Nov., 1936; rettd. 12th May, 1944.

O'BRIEN, MICHAEL, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1st May, 1883).—Retd., May, 1938.*

O'BRIEN-SMITH, EDWARD QUINTON, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 20th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

ODDIN-TAYLOR, HARRY WILLOUGHBY, C.I.E., C.B.E., A.C.G.I., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Ind. Serv. of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 12th July, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; resigned the service from 26th Sept., 1920; re-apptd. as

exec. engr., 7th Dec., 1926; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1931; O.B.E., Jan., 1933; jt. secty. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, in the p.w.d., Jan., 1936; C.B.E., May, 1937; retd., July, 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

ODELL, FRANCIS DAVID, O.B.E., M.A., Indian Agric. Service (b. 20th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. dir. of agric., Feb., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; dir. of agric. (confd.), Feb., 1941.

ODGERS, SIR CHARLES EDWIN, Kt., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 17th Jan., 1870).—Retd., Jan., 1930.*

ODLING, RICHARD ERNEST, O.B.E., late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 14th June, 1886).—Retd., June, 1942.†

O'DONNELL, THOMAS FRANCIS, Maj., M.B., B.Ch.(Dub.) United Provs. Medl. Serv.—Date of first commn., 24th Apl., 1933; civil surgeon.

O'DOWD, GERALD RONEY, B.A., late Mil. Accts. Dept. (1st Oct., 1885).—Retd., June, 1940.

O'FLYNN, ERIC MORTIMER, Indian Police (suplt., Sind) (b. 28th Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, 11th Apl., 1930; confd., March, 1933; supt., confd., Apl., 1942; p.a. to inspr.-genl., Sind, June, 1944; provl. motor transport. offr., etc., Nov., 1944.

OGDEN, LIONEL ROY, O.B.E., Burma Fron. Serv. (b. 6th Mar., 1903).—Apptd. asst. supt., Nov., 1927; O.B.E., 11th May, 1937; resdt.'s grade, Feb. 1946; dep. sec., frontier areas adminstrn.f.(devel.), 1946.

OGILVIE, SIR CHARLES MACIVOR GRANT, C.S.I., C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt. of India, defence dept.) (b. 6th May, 1891).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 4th Dec., 1915, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. administrator, Nabha state, July, 1923; asst. ditto, Sept., 1923; offg. dep. commr., May, 1925; provl., ditto, June, 1926; senr. secty. to finl. commrs., Apl., 1928; C.B.E., June, 1928; home secty. to govt., Punjab, May, 1929; and again, June, 1933; addl. secty. to govt., Punjab, fin. dept., Sept., 1933; offg. secty., ditto, Oct., 1933; confd., Oct., 1934; on depn. for course at Impl. Defence Coll., Jan., 1936; secty. to govt. of India, defence dept., Apl., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; kt., Jan., 1944; retd.

OGILVIE, SIR GEORGE DRUMMOND, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 18th Feb., 1882).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

OGLE, JOHN WILFRED, Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 25th March, 1913; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; divl. transptn. offr., July, 1925; publicity offr., March, 1931; dep. ch. comml. mgr., April, 1940; divl. supt., senr., Dec., 1940 to Feb., 1945.

O'GORMAN, FREDERICK WILLIAM, C.E., O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bombay) (b. 8th July, 1891).—Joined the service, 2nd July, 1913, as asst. supt.; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; supt. of police, Jan., 1925; awarded bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1930; cent. intell. offcr., Bombay Prov., Aug., 1938; confd. as supt. (sel. grade), Oct., 1941; dep. inspr.-genl. (confd.), March, 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1944.

O'HAGAN, REV. CHARLES GILBERT late Ind. Eccles. Estabt., Punjab (b. 17th Sept., 1901).—

Apptd. junr. chaplain on probn. March, 1940; retd., 1946.

OHN, MAUNG, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 16th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at Government High Schs., Thaton and Rangoon, Rangoon Univ., and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Jan., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1940; on milty. service, Nov., 1940; dep. commr., Thayetmgo, March, 1941; civ. aff. serv., July, 1945; collr. (Rangoon) Nov., 1945; addl. sec. P.W.D.; dir. of welfare and rehab., July, 1946.

OKA, DATTATRAYA SITARAM, LL.B., late Bombay C.S. (Judicial Branch) (b. 20th June, 1883).—Retd., June, 1938.*

OLIVER, HENRY ARTHUR, Indian Police (b. 14th Apl., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Dec., 1937; confd., Dec., 1939; addl. supt., Jan., 1945.

OLIVER, PETER RICHARD, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 3rd June, 1917).—Educ. at Felsted and Trinity Hall Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 7th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; on depn. to Bahawalpur State, Aug., 1944.

OLIVIER, OF RAMSDEN, 1st Baron, Rt. Hon. SYDNEY HALDANE OLIVIER, P.C., K.C.M.G., C.B., B.A., LL.D.—Secty. of State for India, 23rd Jan. to 6th Nov., 1924.

OLLEY, WILLIAM JOHN, Burma Civil Serv. (cl. I) (b. 21st Dec., 1919).—Educ. at Lismore High Sch. New South Wales; apptd. on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

OLPHERTS-FORRESTER, SHIRLEY WYBRANDTS GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 2nd Sept., 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad, Univ. of Nagpur, University Coll., London, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 15th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; in exec. charge, central jail, Nagpur, Sept., 1942, to Jan., 1943.

OLVER, SIR ARTHUR, C.B., C.M.G., F.R.C.V.S., Col., R.A.V.C., retd., late animal husbandry expert.—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

OLVER, STEPHEN JOHN LINLEY, Indian Police (b. 16th June, 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 1st Nov., 1935; apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., Sept., 1944; asst. pol. agt., Quetta-Pishin, Sept., 1944; under sec. ext. aff. dept., Mar., 1945; Brit. Trade Agent at Gyantse, Aug., 1945.

OMAR, ZAFAR (see Zafar Omar).

OMAR, MOOS AKBANI (dir. of public health, Sind) (b. 15th June, 1898).—Joined 1st Apl., 1937; offr. in charge, chemico-bact. lab. Karachi Apl., 1937; asst. dir. of pub. health, Sind, July, 1939; dir., ditto, Oct., 1943.

OMMANNEY, FREDERICK ROY, Indian Police (suplt., Sind) (b. 12th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 15th Dec., 1922; offg. supt., March, 1926; confd., Jan., 1933; dist. supt., March, 1946.

O'NEILL, RT. HON. SIR HUGH, Bart., M.P., parly. under-sec. of state for India and for Burma, Sept., 1939, to May, 1940.

O'NEILL, JOHN CONNELL, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 27th May, 1888).—Retd., Jan., 1943.†

O'NEILL, NIALL O'HAGAN, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 14th Nov., 1914).—Educ. at Beaumont and

Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; a.d.c. to the govr., Nov., 1939, to July, 1941; served with East. Group supply cl. Feb., 1942; under sec., govt. of India, I. and C.S., dept., Apl., 1943; M.B.E., Jan., 1945; dep. sec. ditto, Nov., 1945; offg. dep. sec. I. and S. dept., Jan., 1946.

O'NEILL, PATRICK LAWRENCE, Maj., I.M.S. (b. 18th Nov., 1906).—First commn., 5th Aug., 1930; in mil. employ to Aug., 1939; spec. surg., K.G. Hosp., Vizagapatam, Sept., 1939; dist. medl. offr., North Arcot, May., 1940; on mil. duty, Mar., 1941.

O'NEIL SHAW, JOHN CHARLES LIDDELL, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 5th March, 1896).—Joined the service, 5th May, 1920; asst. supt., Feb., 1922; offg. supt., March, 1925; confd., Aug., 1926; O.B.E., June, 1941.

O'NEILL, REV. WILFRED STEPHEN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 23rd Nov., 1886).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Lahore, 24th March, 1927; ditto, N.W.F.P., Apl., 1927; senr. chaplain, March, 1937.

ORAM, ARTHUR, C.I.E., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (N.W.F.P.) (b. 20th Oct., 1885).—Retd., Apl., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.†

ORANGE, SIR HUGH WILLIAM, K.B.E., C.B., C.I.E.—Retd., 30th Nov., 1910.*

ORDE, SIR PERCY LANCELOT, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 19th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1907; asst. supt., Apl., 1908; supt., July, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1927; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., May, 1928; confd. (provl. subst.), May, 1930; confd., July, 1932; offg. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1935; inspr.-genl. and jt. secty. to govt., Punjab, home dept., confd., Sept., 1936; King's police medal, Feb., 1937; kt., Jan., 1941; retd., 18th Jan., 1943.

ORGILL, TYRREL CHURTON, C.I.E., M.A., F.R.G.S., late Indian Educ. Service (N.W.F.P.) (b. 7th June, 1884).—Retd., June, 1939.*

O'RIORDAN, KEVIN MORAN, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 6th March, 1887).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

ORMEROD, CHARLES MAURICE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 27th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Rugby and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., Punjab, l.s.g. dept., Nov., 1930; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936; served under govt of India, March, 1944 to Jan., 1946.

ORMEROD, RICHARD CATON, B.A., Burma Office (actg. princpl.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1915).—Educ. at Winchester Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. princpl., India Office, 11th Oct., 1938; posted to Burma Office, Jan., 1939; resdt. clerk, June, 1940, to Nov., 1941; on mil. service, Nov., 1941 to Nov., 1945; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, Dec., 1945; actg. princpl., Aug., 1946.

ORR, ARCHIBALD, Indian State Rlys. (supt. of collieries) (b. 27th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. mining engr., 28th Apl., 1920; asst. colliery-supt., June, 1929; offg. ch. mining engr., July, 1931; offg. manager, Jerangdih colliery, March, 1933; confd. as supt. collieries, June, 1935.

ORR, CILCIL BRUCE, Indian Police, (dep. inspr. genl. Burma) (2nd Mar., 1902).—Joined the serv. as asst. dist. supt. Burma, 30th Nov., 1921; offg.

dist. supt., May, 1929; confd. May, 1933; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; dep. inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Dec., 1945.

ORR, JOHN WARD, M.A., Indian C.S. (under sec. home and fin. depts., Orissa) (b. 1st Apl., 1916).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., St. Andrews' University and Queen's College, Oxford; apptd. on probn. 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 29th August, 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., serving in Orissa; under sec. home and fin. depts., Orissa, Aug., 1945; sec. to govr., June, 1946.

ORTCHESENE, JOHN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Perth Academy, Edinburgh Univ., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., March, 1936; spcl. commr., and engrg. offr., July, 1939; dist. and sess. judge (sub perm.), Aug., 1939; spcl. enq. commr., Nov. to Dec., 1941.

OSBORN, JACK DASHWOOD BRETON, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 16th June, 1902).—Asst. traffic supt., E.B. rly., Jan., 1926; dist. traffic supt., provl. perm., July, 1938.

OSBORNE, RAYMOND HARRY, India Office (actg. hr. exec. offr. acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 26th May, 1899).—Apptd. cler. offr., 15th Feb., 1922; actg. exec. offr. Apl., 1940; actg. hr. ditto, Dec., 1945.

OSMAN ALI, SHUJAT, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 1st July, 1912).—Educated at St. Joseph's College, Coonoor, Madras, University of Madras, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935; sub-collr., Feb., 1938; employd. in ch. sec. Oct., 1939, to March, 1940; city mag. (1st cl.), Madwa, Apl., 1942; ch. presdy. mag., Sept., 1944; group test offcr., civil selectn. bd., govt. of India; Oct., 1945.

OSMASTON, BERTRAM HUTCHINSON, Indian Forest Service (consvr., United Provs.) (b. 30th March, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., United Provs., 9th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926; offg. asst. forest economist, Dehra Dun, May, 1928; pers. asst. to forest economist, Apl., 1931; confd. commr., July, 1941.

OSMASTON, FITZ WALTER COMPLYON, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Orissa) (b. 3rd July, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Feb., 1923; instr., forest research institute, Apl., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927; served in Orissa; under govt. of India, def., Oct., 1940.

OSMASTON, GEORGE HUTCHINSON, M.C., Lieut.-Col. R. E., Survey of India (dir.) (b. 26th Sept., 1898).—First commn. dated 26th May, 1916; in milty. employ to 30th June, 1921; proby. asst. supt., July, 1921; confd., Oct., 1925; supt., Aug., 1929; director, Dec., 1944.

O'SULLIVAN, DENNIS REIL (judge, chief ct. of Sind) Apptd., Jan., 1943.

O'SULLIVAN, HUGH PATRICK, late Indian Police (b. 26th Jan., 1889).—Retd., Oct., 1940.

OULSNAM, SAMUEL HARRISON YARDLEY, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.C., B.A. (Cantab.), Ind. C.S. (dep. secty., dept. of educn., health and lands, govt. of India) (b. 17th Jan., 1898).—Educ. at Newcastle-under-Lyme High Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1921

arrived, 2nd Dec., 1921, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., March, 1926; offg. dep. commr., March, 1928; Sept., 1932; and again, Apr., 1933; offg. reforms officer., Jan., 1935; dep. commr., Oct., 1937; C.I.E., May, 1937; addtl. dep. sec., dept. of educn., health and lands, May, to Nov., 1938; jt. sec., supply dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1940; dep. secty., dept. of educn., health and lands, Apr., 1940; jt. sec., ditto, Apr., 1943; C.S.I., June, 1946.

OUNG, TUN HLA, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma).—Joined the serv. as asst. dist. supt., 23rd May, 1927; confd., dist. supt., May, 1941; left in Burma, 1942-45; resumed duty under govt. of Burma, Nov., 1945; dist. supt., 1946.

OWDEN, JOHN SCAMBLER, late Indian Forest Service (b. 28th Feb., 1887).—Retd., Jan., 1941.†

OWEN, LEONARD, M.A., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 6th Oct., 1890).—Retd., July, 1941.†

OWEN, LLEWELLYN GORDON, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service, Burma (b. 5th March, 1885).—Retd., March, 1940.†

OWEN, MORRIS, M.Sc., F.Inst.P.H., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 16th Feb., 1885).—Retd., Feb., 1940.†

OWENS, JOSEPH HUBERT, C.I.E., M.B.E., late Indian Posts and Telegraphs (b. 18th Feb., 1887).—Retd., Feb., 1942.†

OXBURY, HAROLD FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S., Impl. Customs Service (dep. commr.) (b. 11th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at King Edward VI Sch., Norwich, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 4th Oct.; 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1931; ditto, local govt. dept., March, 1932; ditto, home and pol. dept., March, 1933; dep. secty., fin. dept., Burma, Dec., 1934; asst. collr., impl. customs service, May, 1935; asst. commr., Feb., 1938; secty. to finl. commr., Nov., 1938; dep. commr. (provl.), March, 1938; confd., July, 1938; collr. of customs, Rangoon, Feb., 1940; civ. aff. serv. April., 1945 to March, 1946; addl. sec. fin. and rev., March, 1946; sec. ditto, 1946.

OXLEY, HUMPHREY LESLIE MALCOLM, India Office (asst. solr., legal adviser's dept.) (b. 9th Oct., 1909).—Apptd. clerk, legal adviser's dept., 23rd Sept., 1933; asst. solr., March, 1944.

OZANNE, ROBERT DE LA CONDAINE, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 26th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 13th Oct., 1923; offg. disc. supt., Apr., 1926; confd., Aug., 1937.

PACKMAN, KENNETH CHALMERS, C.I.E., Lt. Col., I.A., Ind. Polit. Service (b. 14th May, 1899).—First commn. dated 15th Apr., 1919; in mil' employ to March, 1924; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent in Kalat, May, 1925; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan Sept., 1928; 2nd addl. ditto, June, 1930; pol. agent, Dir, Swat, and Chitral, Oct., 1930; ditto, N. Waziristan, Apr., 1931; secty. to A.G.G., Deccan states, March, 1935; A.G.G., Deccan states, and resdnt. at Kolhapur (in addn.), May, 1936; consul-genl., Kashgar, Oct., 1936; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, March, 1939; resdnt. in Waziristan, May, 1941; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Nov., 1941; on depn. Nov., 1944; resdnt. in Waziristan, Jan., 1945; and again, Aug., 1945; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

PADDON, ERROL GRAHAM EVANS, B.Sc., A.C.G.I., Indian State Rlys. (ch. elec. engr. E.I. Rly.) (b. 21st June, 1898).—Asst. elec. engr.,

Aug., 1921; dist. ditto, May, 1938; ch. ditto, March, 1945.

PADDON, SIR STANLEY SOMERSET WREFORD, Kt., C.I.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 28th March, 1881).—Retd., May, 1940.†

PADDONROW, LINDSAY ARTHUR, Indian Police (asst. supt., Sind) (b. 18th Feb., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; confd. as supt., Feb., 1943; dep. commr., Bombay, Feb., 1943; I.P. medal, 1945.

PADHI, RAMA KRISHNA, Indian Police, Bihar and Orissa (b. 25th Jan., 1919).—Apptd. sub. dep. collr. on probn., 11th Dec., 1939; apptd. asst. supt., police, on probn., 28th Apr., 1941; confd., Apr., 1943.

PADMANABHA AYYAR, NARAYANA, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suprg. engr., Madras) (b. 10th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 11th June, 1923; asst. exec. engr., July, 1924; offg. exec. engr., May, 1928; confd., July, 1933; suptg. engr., Apr., 1943.

PADMANABHAN, DORAI SWAMIER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 15th March, 1909).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Apr., 1934; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1941.

PAGE, SIR ARTHUR, Kt., K.C., Barr. (1901), late Judicial dept., Bengal (b. 9th March, 1876).—Retd., Apr., 1936.*

PAI, AMMEMBAL VITTAL, C.I.E., O.B.E., Ind. C.S. (jt. sec. commonwealth relns. dept.) (b. 11th Oct., 1901).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., March, 1928; under-secty. to govt., p.w. and labour dept., Feb., 1931; agent of the govt. of India in Ceylon, Apr., 1936; O.B.E., June, 1939; addl. dep. secty., educn., health and lands dept., govt. of India, Feb., 1941; dep. secty., dept. of Indians overseas, Oct., 1941; contr.-genl. of emigrn. and jt. secty. commonwealth relations dept., Sept., 1944; C.I.E., June, 1946.

PAI, K. RAMA (see Rama Pai, K.).

PAI, MANGALORE PURUSHOTHAM, Indian C.S. (jt. sec. dept. industries and supplies) (b. 1st Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras, King's Coll., London, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1919; arrived, Nov., 1929; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1930; diwan of Sivaganga, July, 1938; contr. of coffee, Dec., 1940; yarn commr., March, 1942; contr. of supplies, Nov., 1942; dep. dir. supplies New Delhi, June, 1945; dep. dir. genl., ditto, July, to Oct., 1945; jt. sec. dept. of indus. and supplies, Nov., 1945.

PAINE, CRISPIN BERNARD CHITTY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 2nd March, 1906).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., Assam, Apr., 1933; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1935; presdnt. Manipur State Durar, Mar., 1936; dep. sec. (home), Oct., 1939; sec. (educn. etc.), Feb., 1941; dep. commr. (provl.), Mar., 1942.

PAING, MAUNG, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep.

commr., Burma (b. 7th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Jan., 1932, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma, June, 1936; dep. commr., July, 1938; under trng., income-tax dept., June, 1940; asst. commr. of income tax, May, 1941; civ. aff. serv., July, to Aug., 1945; asst. commr., inc. tax, Oct., 1945; addl. sec., home and judl. dept. July, 1946.

PAISLEY, JOHN FREDERICK, M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 18th June, 1906).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, Oct., 1928; exec. engr. Oct., 1937; M.B.E., June, 1946.

PAKENHAM-WALSH, WILFRED PAKENHAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 2nd June, 1898).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., Rangoon, March, 1928; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1929; regr., high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; transf. to govt. of Punjab, 1938; commr. under publ. servants enq. act, Feb., 1940; legal remembrance to govt., Punjab, June, 1940; regr. high ct., Apl., 1941; actg. judge, high ct., Rangoon, May, 1946.

PAL, NIRANJAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 26th Oct., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

PALIT, ANATH NATH, O.B.E., F.R.C.S.E., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar (b. 14th Oct., 1883).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

PALMER, FRANCIS EDWARD CHETWYND, late Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 6th May, 1887).—Retd., 6th May, 1942.†

PALMER, REGINALD WILLIAM VAUGHAN, Indian Forest Service (consvr., Burma) (b. 2nd May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 23rd Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., July, 1923; consvr. Nov., 1939; joined civ. aff. serv., March, 1945.

PALMER, TREVOR EDWARD, O.B.E., Capt., I.M.S. (b. 17th Apl., 1904).—First commn. 2nd Aug., 1932; on mil. duty to Nov., 1939; dist. med. offr. Tanjore, Mar., 1940; on mil. duty, Mar., 1941 to Feb., 1946.

PALMER, WILLIAM JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S., (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 25th Apl., 1909).—Educ. at Cheltenham Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1940; dep. sec., home dept., Apl., 1940; addl. dist. mag., May, 1941; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1944.

PAMPHILON, ALFRED HART, India Office (exec. officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1890).—Served as asst. clerk, customs house, from June, 1910, to July, 1912, and as sec. div. clerk, foreign office, from July to Nov., 1912; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 11th Nov., 1912; and attd. to registry and record dept.; transf. to mil. dept., Jan., 1917; on service with Royal Navy from Nov., 1916, to Feb., 1919; exec. offcr., Apl., 1922; transf. to acct.-genl.'s dept., Feb., 1923; actg. hr. exec. offr., Jan., 1946.

PANANDIKAR, DR. SATTYASHRAYA GOPAL, M.A. (Bom.), D.Sc. (Lon.), Indian Educl. Service (b. 18th July, 1893).—Joined the service as prof. of history and pol. economy, Elph. Coll.,

Bombay, 24th Oct., 1924; at Gujarat Coll., Ahmedabad, Nov., 1938, to June, 1939; Karnatak Coll., Dharwar, June, 1939.

PANCHAPAGESAN, TIRUVALUR RAMASWAMI SASTRIAR, M.A., Finance Dept., govt. of India (b. 4th Nov., 1887).—Ch. audr. G.I.P., rly. and govt. exr. accts., B.B. & C.I. rly., Sept., 1939.†

PANCHAPAKESA AYYAR, AIYLAM SUBRAHMANYA AYYAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 26th Jan., 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Oxford Univ. (non-collegiate); joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, Dec., 5th 1922; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; confd., March, 1935.

PANDALAI, KRISHNAN GOPINATH, M.B., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (Madras) (b. 20th May, 1885).—Retd., May, 1939.*

PANDE, BHAIARAB DATT, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 17th March, 1917).—Educ. at Government Intermediate Coll., Allahabad, Allahabad Univ., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 9th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr.; regional grain offr., Oct., 1943; dep. sec. and food contr., Bihar, Dec., 1945.

PANDE, BHUVANESHWAR PRASHAD, RAI BAHADUR, late Bihar C.S. (Judl. Branch) (b. 1st Match, 1887).—Retd., March, 1942.†

PANDE, GAURI DATT, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 10th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1928; suptg. engr., May, 1941; ret. d., July, 1944.

PANDE, JIVA NAND (see Jiva Nand Pandé).
PANDE, PITAMBAR DUTT, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th Dec., 1896).—Joined the service, 22nd March, 1921; services placed at the disposal of Finl. Commr., Rlys., Dec., 1935; on for. service under govt. of Burma, Oct., 1937; dep. ch. accts. offr., G.I.P. rly., Oct., 1938; dir., R.C.A. office (confd.), Sept., 1944.

PANDIT, ANAND DATTATRAYA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (b. 1st Apl., 1909).—Educ. at Patwardhan High School, Nagpur, Universities of Nagpur and Allahabad, and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service 5th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933; and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1936; exec. offr. devel. bd., Cawnpore, Sept., 1945.

PANDIT, GOVIND NARAYAN, B.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 28th March, 1906).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 11th Aug., 1931.

PANDRANG ROW, VOMBATHKERE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 11th June, 1881).—Retd., June, 1941.†

PANDYA, ISHWARRAI NARBHERAM, late Income Tax Dept., Bombay (b. 21st Sept., 1883).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

PANIKAR, ITTEKOTE ACHUTA, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 11th July, 1900).—Joined the service, 21st Feb., 1923; dep. acctnt.-genl., Burma, Sept., 1935; on for. service under the govt. of Burma, Apl., 1937; dep. acctnt.-genl., Bombay, May, 1940.

PANJABI, KEWALRAM LALCHAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magt., Bombay) (b. 29th Dec., 1899).—Educ. at St. Patrick's Sch., Karachi, and Bombay and London Univs.;

apptd. after exam. of 1923; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1923, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; offg. collr. and pol. agent, Surat, March, 1927; offg. regr., co-op. societies, Poona, Nov., 1931; collr. and magte., Oct., 1936; sec. govt., Bombay (gen. and educ. dept.), Jan., 1941; jt. sec., govt. of India (food), Dec., 1943.

PANNA LAL, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc., LL.B., Barr., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1883).—Retd., Dec., 1944.†

PANNIKAR, S. K., Indian State Rlys. (b. 2nd Jan., 1895).—Joined 1st Jan., 1919; dep. ch. engr., B.N. Rly., Jan., 1945.

PANT, PANDIT CHINTAMANI, Rai Sahib, late Central Public Works Dept. (b. 11th May, 1885).—Retd., May, 1940.

PANT, PARASHURAM MADHAVRAO, B.Sc., Indian Police, Bombay (b. 27th Apr., 1920).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn. after exam. of 1941.

PANT, PITAMBAR, Rai Bahadur, P.M.S., late Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 1st Oct., 1883).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

PAPWORTH, HAROLD CHARLES, O.B.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 16th Dec., 1888).—Retd., Dec., 1943.†

PARANJOTI, ROBERT CHARLES, B.E., B.Sc. (Eng.), London, A.M.I.L.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 20th Oct., 1894).—Asst. loco. supt., Jan., 1926; dist. loco. supt., Feb., 1938.

PARANJPE, DHUNDIRAJ VISHWANATH, Rao Suhadur, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 31st Dec., 1884).—Rao Suhadur, July, 1940; retd., Dec., 1939.*

PARANJPE, GOPAL RAMCHANDRA, O.B.E., M.Sc., Indian Educl. Serv., (b. 30th Jan., 1891).—Apptd. profr. of physics, Royal Inst. of Science, Bombay, 26th Oct., 1920; offg. princpl., ditto, June, 1933; confd., May, 1939; O.B.E., June, 1946.

PARANJPYE, SIR RAGHUNATH PURUSHOTTAM, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Bom.), D.Sc. (Cal.) (b. 16th Feb., 1876).—Educl. Maratha H.S., Bombay; Fergusson Coll., Poona; St. John's Coll., Cambridge (Fell); Indian govt. scholar; princpl. and profr. of maths., Fergusson Coll., Poona, 1902-26; Fellow of the Univ. of Bombay, 1904-27; sometime vice-chancellor of the Indian Women's Univ., Poona; member, Bombay legisl. council, 1913; awarded K.-i-H. medal, 1st class, 1916; minister, Bombay govt., 1921-23, 1927; member of council of India, Apr., 1927, to Apr., 1932; member, Indian taxation inquiry ctee., 1924-25; member, reforms inquiry ctee., 1924; delegate of the govt. of India to internatl. labour conferences, Geneva, 1927-1930; member, int. conf. on treatment of foreigners, 1929; int. conf. for formation of int. relief union, 1927; int. conf. on limitation of manufacture of narcotic drugs, 1931; kt., Jan., 1942; high commr. for India in Australia, Oct., 1944; author of lives of Gopal Krishna Gokhale and Dhondo Keshar Karve, and "The Crux of the Indian Problem."

PARASHURAM JOG, DATTATRAYA, Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 9th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 1st Oct., 1931; confd., June, 1933; exec. engr., Oct., 1940; supt. engr., Apr., 1946; on other duty in highways dept.

PARE, JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 22nd July, 1914).—Educl. at County Sec. Sch., West Bridgeford,

Nottingham Univ., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 6th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag. Feb., 1944; asst. commr., civil supplies, Salem, March, 1946.

pareEKH, GOBIND NARAYAN (see Gobind, N. P.).

PARIJA, PRANKRISHNA, O.B.E., B.Sc., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. 1st Apr., 1891).—Apptd. profr. of botany, Ravenshaw Coll., Cuttack, 4th Aug., 1921; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; chancellor, Uktal univ., dir. of agric.

PARIKH, HIMATLAL BAPALAL, M.I.E. (Ind.), L.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Sind) (b. 24th May, 1888).—Joined the provl. service as asst. engr., 29th March, 1911; exec. engr., March, 1921; spl. road engr., Bombay, Nov., 1935; ditto, Sind, Apr., 1936; retd., 24th May, 1943.

PARISH, FREDERICK FRANK, Indian Railway Accts. Service (dep. ch. accts. offr.) (b. 5th May, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and acctnt., G.I.P. Rly., 1st May, 1919; services permanently placed at the disposal of finl. commr., rlys., Oct., 1929; confd. as accts. offcr., gr. I, May, 1935; confd. as dep. ch. accts. offcr., Jan., 1938.

PARISH, JAMES EDWARD, Office of the High Commr. for India (asst. engr., mechanical transport, store dept.) (b. 21st March, 1887).—Apptd. to Office of H.C. for I., July, 1921; asst. inspr. of stores, Sept., 1922; asst. engr., mechanical transport, March, 1926.

PARKER, HARDING NEVILLE, B.A., late Indian State Railways (b. 20th July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 2nd Dec., 1912; in mil. employ from 12th May, 1919 to 14th Aug., 1920; dist. traffic supt., Dec., 1921; offg. asst. agent, E.B. rly., Jan., 1928; dep. traffic supt., Oct., 1930; offg. dep. traffic manager, comml., E.B. rly., March, 1933; ditto, transpn., July, 1935; offg. dep. agent personnel, E.B. rly., July, 1936; dep. traffic manager (provl.), Dec., 1939; ch. transpn. manager, Feb., 1942; retd., July, 1945.

PARKER, HERBERT, M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 17th Apr., 1889).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

PARKER, RICHARD HENRY, late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 21st July, 1898).—Retd., March, 1939.*

PARKER, RICHARD NEVILLE, F.C.H., late Indian Forest Service (Punjab) (b. 4th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.†

PARKES, RODERICK WALLIS, O.B.E., B.A., Ind. C.S., Ind. Polit. Serv. (b. 2nd Apr., 1909).—Educl. at St. Paul's Sch., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after the exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addl. asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1935; asst. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1935; secty., British legation, Kabul, Aug., 1936; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, Oct., 1938; asst. pol. agent, Chhattisgarh states, Nov., 1939; under-secty. to resdt. for states of W. India, Sept., 1940; and again, Apr., 1942; pol. agt., W. Kathiawar agency, March, 1942; pol. agent to E. Kathiawar agency, Nov., 1942; natl. war front offcr. for W. region, Dec., 1942; secty. to resdt., Rajputana, Nov., 1943; sec. to resdt., Baroda, Feb., 1944; secty. to resdt., Kolhapur and Deccan states agency, Apr., 1944; sec. to resdt.

for Punjab States, Apl., 1945; liaison commr. (states), govt. of India (food) Apl., 1946; O.B.E., June, 1945.

PARKIN, GEORGE DEVEREUX, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 30th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 29th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; supt. of police, Benares State, Apl., 1931; supt., Nov., 1932; on depn. as dep. inspr.-genl., Rampur state, Dec., 1934, to Nov., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1941.

PARKINSON, JOSEPH ERNEST, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educ. Service (b. 13th April, 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1938; re-employed as educ. commr. with govt. of India, Apl., 1938, to Jan., 1939.†

PARLBY, JOSHUA, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.Sc., late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 3rd March, 1889).—Joined theserv. as mil. acct., 2nd cl., Nov., 1913; finl. asst., I.E.F., Apl., 1917, to Dec., 1918; O.B.E., 1919; dep. secty., high commrs. sect., Iraq, Dec., 1918; advr. and secty. to Iraqi acct.-genl., Jan., 1921; fin. secty. to high commr., Iraq, Apl., 1922; spec. duty for closing of "Accts. of Occ. Territories," July, 1922, to May, 1923; aud.-genl. and inspr.-genl. of accts., Iraq, March, 1924, to May, 1925; on dep. to London and Geneva as jt. rep. of Iraq govt., arbitration proceedings, League of Nations, Jan., 1925; secty. to finl. commn., Iraq, Apl. and May, 1925; compt. and aud.-genl., Iraq, May, 1925, to May, 1935; awarded the "Order of the Two Rivers" by H.M. the King of Iraq, 1935; confd. as cl. I offer., Apl., 1937; spec. duty in mil. acctnt.-genl.'s office, Nov., 1937; offg. mil. acctnt.-genl., Apl., 1938; dep. mil. acctnt.-genl. (senior), Sept., 1938; offg. mil. acctnt.-genl. again, Oct., 1939; promoted command contr., July, 1940; mil. acctnt.-genl., Aug., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1942; retd., Sept., 1946.

PARPIA, YUSUF RAHIM, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 25th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; administ., Kurla municipality., Feb., 1936; services placed at disposal of govt. of Sind, March, 1939; confd. as collr. Nov., 1939; controller of import trade, Bombay, May, 1940, to Feb., 1943; served in Sind from March, 1944 to March, 1946.

PARR, CLIFFORD HARLEY, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (b. 15th July, 1892).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., United Provs., 1st Jan., 1920; apptd. impl. agriculturist, impl. agric. research inst., Delhi, Aug., 1940.

PARRY, CLAUDE FREDERICK, O.B.E., Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 9th March, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1919; dist. supt., Oct., 1925; principl., pol. training sch., Saugor, Feb., 1933; on depn., crown representative police, Neemuch, June, 1941; O.B.E., June, 1942; 'King's Police medal, June, 1946.

PARSONS, SIR ALFRED ALAN LETHBRIDGE, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Oct., 1882).—Retd. from I.C.S., Nov., 1934; member of council of India, Nov., 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1935; advr. to the sec. of state for India, Apl., to Nov., 1939.*

PARSONS, SIR ARTHUR EDWARD BROADBENT, K.C.I.E., C.B.E., D.S.O., Lieut.-Col., I.A. retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 5th Aug., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

PARSONS, CYRIL GORDON, M.C., Indian

Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 7th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., March, 1923; confd., Apl., 1927; confd. in seln. grade, Sept., 1944.

PARSONS, FRANK THOMAS, Indian Police (b. 9th Sept., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Nov., 1936; confd., Apl., 1942; aide-de-camp to H.E. the govt. of Orissa.

PARSONS, RUDOLPH ELLIOT REBSCH, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) (b. 17th Jan., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Assam, 14th Oct., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; asst. to pol. offer., Sadiya frontier tract, May, 1933, sec. provl. trpt. authy., etc., Oct., 1941.

PARTAB SINGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1896).—Educ. at Khalsa Coll., Amritsar and Punjab Univ.; arrived, 11th Jan., 1922, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (provl.), August, 1928; ditto (perm.), Apl., 1932.

PARTAP SINGH, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (b. 10th Jan., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consrv., Punjab, on 9th Nov., 1923; transf., N.W.F.P., March, 1932.

PARTHASARATHI AYYANGAR, SRINIVASA AYYANGAR, B.A., M.L., Ind. Pol., Madras (b. 1st May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 1st May, 1927; offg. supt., Apl., 1931; confd., July, 1940; inspr.-genl., police, Travancore, Jan., 1944.

PARTO, ANNEPU VASUDEVA, M.B.E., B.A., Indian Police, Madras (b. 3rd Aug., 1907).—Joined the service, 11th June, 1930; confd. June, 1932; M.B.E., June, 1944; dist. supt. (confd.), Dec., 1943.

PARTRIDGE, EDWARD HENRY WILLIAM, late Office of the High Commr. for India (b. 22nd Apl., 1879).—Apptd. asst. engr., mechanical transport, Oct., 1924; engr., ditto, March, 1926; retd., Apl., 1944.

PASCOE, SIR EDWIN HALL, Kt., M.A., D.Sc., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 17th Feb., 1878).—Retd., June, 1932.*

PATANJALI SASTRI, MANDUKULATHUR.—Apptd. judge, high court, Madras, March, 1939.

PATCH, CHARLES JAMES LODGE, M.C., Lieut.-Col., retd., late I.M.S. (b. 31st Oct., 1887).—First commn. dated 15th Dec., 1919; in milty. employ to 5th Nov., 1922; supt., Punjab lunatic asylum, Nov., 1922; supt., Punjab mental hosp., July, 1924; retd.,

PATEL, BHAGVATPRASAD RAOJIBHAI, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 24th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Proprietary High Sch., Ahmedabad, Bombay Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Sind as asst. collr.; dir. of civ. supplies and ch. contr. prices, Nov., 1944.

PATEL, BHAILAL KHUSHALDAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Sind) (b. 12th Apl., 1906).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; confd. as collr., Sept., 1939; dep. sec., supply dept., May, 1943; offg. jt. sec., Sept. to Oct., 1943; and again Jan. to Apl., 1945, and Nov., 1945.

PATEL, BHAILAL SHANKARBHAI, B.Ag., N.D.A., N.D.D., C.D.A.D., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 29th Sept., 1887).—Retd., 29th Sept., 1942.†

PATEL, BHOGILAL PRABHUDAS, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 16th Oct., 1912).—Educ. at Shree-Sayaji High Sch., Baroda, and

Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PATEL, HIRALAL MULJIBHAI, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (b. 27th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. collr., Jan. and Dec., 1932; attd. central bd. of rev., June, 1934; offg. dep. sec. to govt., Bombay, fin. dept., March, 1936; Indian trade commr., Hamburg, July, 1937; dep. Indian trade commr., Jan., 1940; dep. sec., govt. of India, dept. of supply, Sept., 1940; services loaned to Eastern Group Supply Council from Mar., 1941; dep. dir.-genl. (supply); July, 1942 to Apl., 1943; dep. sec. indust. and civil supplies dept., Apl., 1943 to Jan., 1944; jt. sec. ditto, Jan., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1946; jt. sec. co-ord. cttee. of Council, Jan., 1946; jt. sec. exec. council, Jan., 1946.

PATEL, RANCHHODBHAI BHAIBABHAI, C.I.E., M.A., LL.B., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 18th Dec., 1880).—Retd., Apl., 1941.

PATEL, VALLABHBHAI JHAVERBHAI SARDAR (b. 1876).—Home member of Governor-General's Council, Sept., 1946.

PATERSON, GEORGE FREDERICK JOSEPH, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., Lieut.-Col. (local Col.), I.A. dir., mily. lands and cantonments, def. dept.; (b. 26th March, 1885).—First commn., Jan., 1904; in milty. employ until Sept., 1919; joined the late cantonments mag.'s dept. (now contonments dept.), Sept., 1919; apptd. dir., military lands and cantonments, defence dept., July, 1935.

PATERSON, HUGH JAMES, O.B.E., B.Sc., Ind. Serv. of Engrs. (suptg. engr. Cent. p.w.d.) (b. 18th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., 18th Dec., 1928; confd., June, 1930; transf. to central p.w.d., Jan., 1934; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1935; confd. Dec., 1939; suptg. engr., May, 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

PATERSON, NOEL KENNEDY, O.B.E., M.A., Ind. C.S. (ch. commr., And. and Nicobar Islands). (b. 25th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh, Univ. of Edinburgh, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1929; arrived, 25th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Central Provs., March, 1934; under-sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, May, 1937; dep. commr., Port Blair, Apl., 1938, to March, 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; ch. commr., Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Feb., 1946.

PATERSON, ROBERT HAND, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 21st June, 1912; asst. contr. of stores, N.W. rly., Apl., 1917; dist. contr. of stores, March, 1931; offg. dep. contr. of stores, Aug., 1936; confd., March, 1941.

PATERSON MORGAN, JOHN ANTHONY, see Morgan, J. A. P.

PATHAK, VINAYANAND (see Vinayanand, P.).

PATIL, RAMRAO KRISHNA RAO, B.Sc., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 13th Dec., 1907).—Retd., Apl., 1942.†

PATKAR, SIR SITARAM SUNDERRAO, B.A., LL.B., late Judicial Dept., Bombay (b. 16th May, 1873).—Retd., May, 1933.*

PATON, WILLIAM CALDER, M.C., M.B., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals and prisons, N.W.F.P.) (b. 27th Jan., 1886).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1912; resdt. medl. offcr., gen. hosp., Madras, Jan.,

1924; offg. civil surg., Coonoor, July, 1924; offg. D.M.O., Sept., 1927; confd., June, 1929; profrr., medl. coll., Madras, May, 1932; chief medl. offcr. and civil surg., Delhi, Jan., 1934; specialist in obstetrics with gynaecology, Madras, Oct., 1938; inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals and prisons and dir. of public health, N.W.F.P., Sept., 1939.

PATRICK, SIR PAUL JOSEPH, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., Ind. Office. (actg. asst. under-sec. of state) (b. 6th Oct., 1888).—Ed. at Rugby and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. to sec's off., G.P.O., Jan. to Sept., 1913; apptd. junr. clk., Ind. Office, 1st Oct., 1913, and attd. to mil. dept.; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., March, 1916; tempy. asst. sec., army dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1917, to May, 1918; served with 4/11th Gurkha Rifles in Palestine, May, 1918, to Jan., 1919; resumed duty at India Office, Feb., 1919; private sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, July, 1920, to Sept., 1920; addl. pte. sec. to govr. of Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1920, to Apl., 1921; senior clerk, March, 1921; princpl., Apl., 1921; establishment offcr., Feb., 1922; delegate of govt. of India to perm. cttee. of intl. bureau of public health, Paris, 1922; admin. advr. to Indian delegation, internl. sanitary conf., Paris, 1926; sec. to Indian delegs. to 5th, 6th and 7th assemblies of League of Nations, 1924, 1925 and 1926; actg. secty., pol. (internl.) dept., Apl., 1930; jt. sec. to British delegn., Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931, 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; jt. sec., Indian states enquiry cttee., Jan.-June, 1932; asst. sec., pol. (internal) dept., Dec., 1933; C.S.I., June, 1934; actg. asst. under sec. of state, July, 1941; commr. income tax, Dec., 1945; K.C.I.E., June, 1946.

PATRO, ANNEPU VASUDEVA, M.B.E., B.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, 11th June, 1930; confd., June, 1932; M.B.E., June, 1944; dep. commr. of police, crime branch, Feb., 1942.

PATTENSON, ARTHUR ERIC TYLDEN, C.S.I., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 15th Nov., 1888).—Retd., June, 1941.†

PATTERSON, BRIAN WALTER, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. contr., stores, E.B. Rly.) (b. 24th Jan., 1899).—Asst. traffic supt., Apl., 1918; dist. ditto (provl.), Aug., 1936; confd., July, 1938; dep. ch. contr., stores, March, 1941.

PATTERSON, DAVID CLARKE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 5th May, 1879).—Retd., May, 1939.*

PATTIPATE HANIMIREDDIGARI RAMA REDDI (see Rama Reddi, P. H.).

PATTLE, ERNEST GODFREY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th Aug., 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1941.†

PATTON, ARNOLD GORDON, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sec., fin. and rev. dept., Assam) (b. 2nd Dec., 1892).—Educ. at City of London Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1916, and served in Assam as asst. commr. on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Jan., 1918, to March, 1919; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1925; and again, Feb., 1932; confd., Feb., 1934; sec., fin. and rev. dept., Apl., 1937; and again, Jan., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1943.

PATWARDHAN, RAMKRISHNA PURUSHOTAM, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (profrr., Elph. coll., Bombay) (b. 8th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1921; profrr. of Indian history and economics, Deccan Coll., Poona, Feb., 1921;

profr., Elph. Coll., Bombay, July, 1926, Oct., 1937, and June, 1940; profr. of history, Deccan coll., Poona, June, 1932.

PAVRI, KEKI SHIAVAX, Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 4th June, 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., March, 1940.

PAW, MAUNG SAN, Indian State Ryls. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 6th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 6th March, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Ryls., Dec., 1932.

PAWSEY, CHARLES RIDLEY, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.C., B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 14th July, 1894).—Educ. at Berkhamsted Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; served in Assam as asst. commr. from 4th Dec., 1919; offg. dir. of land records, inspr.-genl. of registrn., etc., Assam, Sept., 1932; and again, March, 1934; dep. commr. (prov. subs.), Apl., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; C.I.E., Dec., 1943; C.S.I., Feb., 1945.

PAYMASTER, BARJOR BEHRAMJI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 10th Jan., 1912).—Educ. at Parsi High Sch., Panchgani, Univ. of Bombay, Univ. Coll., London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Sind as asst. collr.; from Dec., 1935, to Sept., 1940; served in Bombay as asst. judge, Oct., 1940, to June, 1942; served in Sind as asst. judge, July, 1942.

PAYTON, WILFRED HUGH, C.M.G., B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. sec. govt. of Burma) (b. 7th Apl., 1892).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to Sept., 1919; asst. supt., S. Shan states, Apl., 1921; under-sec. to govt., Feb., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1925; confd., June, 1930; offg. secty. to govt., educn. dept., Apl., 1928; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept.; addl. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Oct., 1930; secty., ditto, March, 1932; lab. commr., Burma, Apl., 1935; secty., home dept., Apl., 1937; commr., confd., June, 1941; on spec. duty, ch. sec.'s office, 1944; C.M.G., Jan., 1945; ch. sec. to the Govt. of Burma, Dec., 1944; fin. commr., Dec., 1945.

PE, MAUNG WIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 5th May, 1912).—Educ. at New College, Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under sec. fin. dept., Sept., 1940; civ. aff. serv. from Sept., 1945; dep. commr., Jan., 1946.

PEACE, GORDON, O.B.E., F.I.C., F.R.A.S., Dept. of Explosives (ch. inspr.) (b. 4th Aug., 1890).—Apptd. ch. inspr. of explosives in India, 10th Oct., 1934; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

PEACOCK, DAVID HENRY, M.A., D.Sc., F.I.C., F.C.S., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 9th Dec., 1889).—Retd., Dec., 1942.†

PEARCE, CHARLES FREDERICK BYRDE, C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 13th June, 1892).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Aug., 1920; arrived in India, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under sec. to govt., June, 1923; offg. dep. sec., ditto, Dec., 1924; asst. settl. offcr., March, 1925; settl. offer., July, 1927; dep. commr., Oct., 1930; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Burma, June, 1934; secty., ditto, Nov., 1934; and again, Feb., 1936; apptd. represen-

tative of the govt. of Burma, applicn. cttee. for the Indo-Burma finl. award, Jan., 1936; secty. to govr. of Burma, Feb., 1939; C.B.E., June, 1941; commr., Fed. Shan States, Nov., 1941; admnr. refugees areas, N. Assam, June, 1942; C.C.A.O., Burma (rank Maj.-Genl.) Feb., 1943; commr. on spec. duties, Frontier areas dept., June, 1945; dir. Frontier areas admin., Feb., 1946; counsellor to govr. of Burma, Mar., 1946.

PEARCE, CYRIL, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Ryls. (dep. ch. mech. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 26th Sept., 1892).—Joined the serv., 23rd Nov., 1923, as asst. dist. loco. supt., E.I. rly.; asst. supt., Dec. 1929; transptn. supt. (power), Aug., 1934; dep. ch. mech. engr., Feb., 1944.

PEARCE, GEORGE ALFRED, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 18th Oct., 1920; offg. supt., May, 1923; addl. supt., Dec., 1926; supt., Aug., 1928; O.B.E., June, 1938; confd. in sel. grade, July, 1944; awarded King's police medal, June, 1946.

PEARCE, ROGER RAMSDEN, B.A., Indian, C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 28th Oct. 1914).—Educ. at Bishop's Stortford Coll., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; served in Sind from Nov., 1938; offg. var. from Dec., 1941; dep. sec., govt. of Sind, food and civil supplies dept. and addl. dir., civil supplies, March, 1945.

PEARL, JOHN HERBERT, Indian Audit Office (actg. hr. exec. offr.) (b. 15th Mar., 1918).—Apptd. exec. offr. 17th Aug., 1937; actg. hr. exec. offr., June, 1946.

PEARMAN, JAMES EDGAR, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 18th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 10th Oct., 1921; offg. supt., Jan., 1924; confd., June, 1926; awarded King's police medal; O.B.E., June, 1935; asst. to I.G. and supt., C.I.D., May, 1937.

PEARSE, J. I. Indian State Ryls. (b. 21st Aug., 1903).—Joined 7th Aug., 1925; dep. traffic mgr., G.I.P. Rly., July, 1944.

PEARSON, FRANCIS FENWICK, M.B.E., Capt., I.A. Ind. Polit. Serv. (b. 13th June, 1911).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1931; apptd. to pol. dept., Oct., 1935; subst. on probn. and seconded as a.d.c. to Viceroy; under trng. in United Provs., Sept., 1936; under-sec., resd. for Western India states, Sept., 1938; confd. in Indian political service, June, 1939; asst. pol. agent, Punjab hill states, Sept., 1940; pol. agent, ditto, Oct., 1941 (addnl. charge); on depn. under civil defence dept., Feb., 1942; under sec., pol. dept., Feb., 1943; M.B.E., June, 1945; under sec. ext. aff. dept., Oct., 1945; presdt., Manipur State Durbar, Nov., 1945.

PEARSON, SIR HERBERT GRAYHURST, Barr. (b. 5th Aug., 1878).—Late legal adviser and solicitor to the sec. of state, June, 1933; retd., Nov., 1938.*

PEARSON, SIR RALPH SNEYD, C.I.E., F.L.S., late Indian Forest Service, Dehra Dun. —Retd., Aug., 1927.*

PEDDIE, KENNETH, M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian State Ryls. (b. 17th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Nov., 1940.†

PEDLEY, JOHN EDWARD, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.C., B.A., Ind. C.S. (commr. United Provs.) (b. 4th Dec., 1891).—Educ. at Repton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in

United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. regr., high ct., Allahabad, March, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1930; confd., Jan., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1939; chairman, sugar commn., United Provs.; advr. to the govr., Nov., 1945; commr. (confd.), Nov. 1945; C.S.I., June, 1946.

PEEBLES, ROBERT WILLIAM HORSBURGH, Burma C.S. (cl. 1) (asst. commr.) (b. 14th Dec., 1916).—Educ. at Glasgow Academy and St. Andrew's Univ.; apptd. on probn., 5th Oct., 1939; joined service, 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1940; served as asst. commr.

PEEK, THOMAS ARTHUR, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, general dept.) (b. 14th Aug., 1884).—Apptd. to India Office, 27th June, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Apl., 1922; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1924; held non-gaz. posts to March, 1946; offg. dep. Indian trade commr., London, Apl., 1946.

PEEL, EARDLEY GARFORTH BRYAN, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 12th March, 1888).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

PEERS, ELWIN VICTOR AUSTIN, O.B.E., Barr., Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 17th March, 1921; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., June, 1928; sec., provl., transpt. authy., Nov., 1940; jt. sec. to govt., March, 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

PEJAVAR MOHAN RAO (see Mohan Rao, Pejavar).

PELLY, CORNELIUS JAMES, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 8th Apl., 1908).—Educ. at Congowes Wood Coll., Co.
