Kildare, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on for. serv. as colon. offcr., Bahawalpur govt., Nov., 1937; asst. pol. agent, W. Kathiawar agency, May, 1940; pol. offcr., Trucial coast, Sharjah, Apl., 1941; pol. agt and consul, Muscat, May, 1942; pol. offcr., Kuwait, Oct., 1943; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; pol. agt., Bahrain, Mar., 1945; sec. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Nov., 1945.

PELLY, HUTCHISON RAYMOND, Lieut.-Col., retd., late I.A. (Burma) (b. 22nd Sept., 1887).—Retd., Sept., 1942.†

PEMBERTON, FREDERICK, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 7th Sept., 1890).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., 18th Dec., 1929; offg. exec. engr., March, 1931; confd., Feb., 1938; retd., Aug., 1945.

PEMMANDRA, K. MONAPPA, Rao Bahadur, B.A., Indian Police, Madras (b. 15th Dec., 1901).—Promoted to the Indian Police as supt., June, 1942.

PENMAN, DAVID, C.I.E., D.Sc., late Dept. of Mines in India (b. 9th Apl., 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1938.*

PENNELL, Maj. GEORGE ERNEST, Burma Frontier Serv., (b. 29th March, 1913).—Apptd., 29th May, 1946.

PENNELL, KENNETH EUSTACE LEE, C.I.E., B.A., M.C., late Indian Service of Engineers, (Assam) (b. 26th July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1912; in mil. employ. from 6th Dec., 1915, to 16th July, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1922; exec. engr., Dec., 1925; offg. under-secty., p.w.d., Assam, Jan., 1928; asst. ch. engr. and under-secty. to govt. (p.w.d.), Assam, Nov., 1934; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; chief engr.

and secty. (p.w.d.), June, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; retd., Aug., 1945.

PENNELLS, SIDNEY GILBERT, India Office (actg. princpl., Burma Office) (b. 4th May, 1896).—Apptd. clerical officer, 20th Oct., 1921; exec. officer, May, 1927; actg. hr. ditto, May, 1938; conf., June, 1939; actg. asst. admin. offcr., Apl., 1940; pte. sec., to under sec. of state, June, 1942, to Jan., 1944; actg. princpl., Jan., 1944.

PENNEY, THOMAS MAXWELL MONCRIEFF, Maj., R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 28th March, 1893).—First commn. dated 19th July, 1912; asst. supt. (proby.), 13th Oct., 1920; confd., Oct., 1922; supt., Dec., 1928; confd. as director, Aug., 1941.

PENNY, CYRIL CUNNINGHAM, Indian State Rlys. (asst. transpn. (power), G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 19th March, 1891).—Asst. transpn. supt., junr. scale, Sept., 1924; senr. scale (provl.), June, 1936; confd., Aug., 1940.

PENNY, SIR JAMES DOWNING, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (finl. commr. to govt. of Punjab) (b. 25th May, 1886).—Educ. at Marlborough Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. regr., co-operative credit societies, Punjab, March, 1914; pol. asst. and commdt., B.M. police, July, 1916; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Punjab, Oct., 1917; on mil. duty, June, 1918, to March, 1919; supt., Simla, hill states, Apl. to May, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1923; confd., Oct., 1933; offg. dep. sec., govt. of India, commerce dept., June, 1926; ditto, fin. dept., Dec., 1926; confd., March, 1927; sec., fin. dept., Punjab, Nov., 1927; offg. commr., variously from June, 1932; on special duty, Punjab civil sec., Aug., 1933; chief sec. to govt., and C.I.E., May, 1937; C.S.I., June, 1939; finl. commr. (rev.), Nov., 1941; finl. commr. (dev.) and sec., dev. dept., Nov., 1941; finl. commr. (confd.), Dec., 1941; K.C.I.E., June, 1943.

PEREIRA, LEO VINCENT, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 28th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service, Feb., 1920; asst. engr., Feb., 1922; exec. engr., Jan., 1936.

PERKIN, EMIL ATHOL OWEN, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 25th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1909; supt., July, 1920; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1934; deputed as inspr.-genl., Orissa, Apl., 1936; inspr.-genl., July, 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; retd., 30th Sept., 1944.

PERROTT, ARTHUR FINCH, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 13th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1912; asst. supt., May, 1914; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1919; supt., March, 1924; awarded bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1925; commdt. frontier constab. Apl., 1926; pol. agt., N.Waziristan, Oct., 1928; dep. inspr.-genl., pol., Feb., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; awarded Ind. Pol. medal, Jan., 1945; provol. motor transpt. contr. etc., May, 1943; retd., Dec., 1945.

PERRY, EDWARD WILLIAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Ind. C.S. (sec., rev. dept., Bombay) (b. 12th June, 1891).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 9th Dec., 1915, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty, Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., genl., etc., Dept. (tempy.), Oct., 1922; addl.

dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept. (temp.), June, 1923; offg. collr., Bombay, sub. dist., and Salsette dev. offcr. (in addn.), May, 1924; offg. collr. and supt. of stamps, Apl., 1926; member, legisl. council, Feb., 1927; pte. sec. to govr., Apl., 1927; asst. sec., Indian statutory commission, Nov., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1930; secty., re-organisation cttee.; collr., Jan., 1933; offg. dir. of information and commr. of labour, Bombay, May, 1933; offg. jt. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Apl., 1934; sec. govt., Bombay, Dec., 1936; prime minister, Kolhapur state, from Oct., 1941; reconstrn. commr. and sec. reconstr. dept., Bombay, and sec. to govt., Bombay (revenue), Nov., 1944; C.S.I., Jan., 1946; commr., cent. divn., March, 1946.

PERRY, THOMAS VICTOR, B.A., Indian Eccles. Dept. (junr. chaplain) (b. 16th Aug., 1908).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 8th Nov., 1935; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1937.

PERRY, WILLIAM ERIC, M.C., Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Survey of India (b. 2nd Jan., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1935.*

PERSHAD, LADLI (see Ladli, P.).

PETCH, DONALD BURMAN, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 16th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1923; offg. dep. commr. and settlt. offcr., July, 1924; under-sec. to govt., educn., local govt. and public health dept., Nov., 1925; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; ditto, (provl. subs.), Dec., 1934; sec. to fin. commr., Burma, Nov., 1934; addl. sec., rev. dept., Burma, July, 1936; sec., com. and indus. dept., May, 1937, again, Oct., 1939; dep. commr., Nov., 1938; sec. govt. of Burma, 1944; sec., comm. and supply dept., and agric. and rur. econ., 1945; jt. sec. ditto, Jan., 1946; sec., agric. and rur. econ. 1946; commr. (confd.), July, 1946.

PETERS, CHARLES ROBERT, late Indian Police (b. 26th Sept., 1886).—Retd., May, 1939.*

PETERS, GERALD HENRY, Burma C.S. (cl. I) (dep. commr.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1917).—Educ. at Diocesan Boys' High Sch., Rangoon, and Rangoon Univ.; apptd. on probn., 26th Sept., 1939; joined service, 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1940, and served as asst. commr.; on mil. duty, 1941; civ. aff. serv., Dec., 1943; dep. commr., Oct., 1945.

PETERSON, IVAN EMILLE KERR, late Central Excises and Salt Dept., Northern India (b. 13th Sept., 1885).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

PETRIE, SIR DAVID, Kt., C.I.E., C.V.O., C.B.E., M.A., late Indian Police (b. 9th Sept., 1879).†

PETRIE, HARRY, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 4th Apl., 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, store dept., India Office, March, 1920; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. offcr., Jan., 1926; actg. higher exec. offcr., Apl., 1941.

PETTIGREW, GUY IRVINE, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 2nd Nov., 1909).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1929; on special duty, Western India states agency, Oct., 1935; under trng. in Punjab, Apl., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Punjab hill states, Oct., 1937; asst. commr., Hazara, Dec., 1938; confd. in Indian political service, June, 1939; asst. commr., Kohat,

March, 1940; addtl. asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1940; city magte., Peshawar, June, 1941; asst. commr., Charsadda, Nov., 1941; sec. Brit. legation, Kabul, May, 1943; consul, Bushire and vice-consul ditto (addl. charge), March, 1946.

PETTY, JOHN, O.B.E., M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Sind) (b. 24th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1921, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1923; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

PHADKAR, ANANT KESHAV, (b. 12th Sept. 1893).—Joined the service 23rd Sept., 1923; served in Bombay judl. dept., dist. and sess. judge (confd.), June, 1942; jt. judge and addl. sess. judge, Belgaum, June to Nov., 1943.

PHARASKHANEWALA, BALKRISHNA SHAMRAO, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 10th July, 1884).—Retd., July, 1939.*

PHATAK, SHANKAR DIWAKAR, B.A., LL.B., late Judicial Dept., Central Provs. (b. 14th Nov., 1887).—Retd., 14th Nov., 1942.†

PHELIPS, DENIS CHARLES PRESCOT, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 14th Dec., 1899).—Educ. at Gresham's School, Holt, Norfolk and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. settlt. offcr., Dec., 1924; warden, oilfields, May, 1926, and again, March, 1932; off. dep. commr., variously from June, 1929; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1936; confd., Jan., 1937; warden, oilfields, Jan., 1939; addl. secty., home dept., Feb., 1939; secty., defence dept., March, 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; served with Indian army, Aug., 1942 to May, 1944; dep. commr., Jan., 1945; commr., 1946.

HELPS, JOHN MURRAY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 7th Nov., 1916).—Educ. at Winchester and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bombay and Sind as asst. collr; offg. var. from May, 1943; sec. to govt. of Sind, Apl. 1946.

PHEROZE MERWAN KHAREGAT, Sir, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt. of India, dept. of agric.) (b. 29th May, 1890).—Educ. at Bombay and Clare Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam of 1913; arrived in India, 9th Dec., 1914; served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1922; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, June to Nov., 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1923; confd., Apl., 1931; offg. regr., co-op. societies, Dec., 1926; secty. to govt., United Provs. dept. of industries and educn., March, 1932; and again, Sept., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1935; labour commr., July, 1938; vice-chairman, impl. council of agric. research, Apl., 1939; kt., June, 1943; addl. sec., dept. of educ. h. and l., May, 1944; sec., ditto, Apl., 1945; sec., dept. of agric., Sept., 1945.

PHILIP, MODEIL MANI (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 13th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Sept., 1928; arrived, 20th Oct., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1935; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936; dep. sec., educ., etc., dept., Oct., 1938; regr., co-op. socs., Dec., 1940; mag. and collr. (confd.) July, 1945; dir. of resettmt., govt. of India, Sept., 1945.

PHILLIPPE, WILLIAM AUGUSTINE, Income Tax Dept., Bengal (asst. commr.) (b. 17th Nov.,

1886).—Joined the service, 11th July, 1904; asst. commr., Aug., 1934.

PHILIPS, ALAN ANDREW, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 18th Aug. 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 18th Nov., 1912; dist. traffic supt., 2nd July, 1924; divl. comml. officr., N.W. Rly., Feb., 1927; offg. comml. instructor, rly. staff coll., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1929; divl. supt. (junr.), Oct., 1939; ch. comml. mgr. (provl.), Oct., 1940; ch. contr. rly. priorities, March, 1942; retd., Aug., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

PHILLIPS, ARTHUR GORDON, C.I.E., Barr., late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 20th Oct., 1885).—Retd., Oct., 1940.†

PHILLIPS, JAMES ROLAND, O.B.E., B.Sc., Ind. Serv. of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 1st Nov., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., March, 1927; confd., Sept., 1933; dep. secty. to govt., Bombay, p.w.d. (irrign. and rlys.) Nov., 1933; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

PHILLIPS, JOHN, India Office (exec. officr., acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 8th Feb., 1896).—Apptd. clerical officr., 18th July, 1921; exec. officr., May, 1927; actg. hr. ditto, Apl., 1940; lent to min. of shipping, June, 1940.

PHILLIPS, JOHN ALFRED STEELE, C.I.E., M.B., C.H.B., D.P.H., Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 6th Oct., 1882).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

PHILLIPS, KEITH DAVID, Indian Police (b. 5th Aug., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Dec., 1937.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM HENRY, Factory Dept., Bombay (inspr.) (b. 22nd May, 1892).—Apptd. inspr. of factories, Bombay, 15th Oct., 1923, after non-gazetted service from 12th May, 1922; offg. ch. inspr., May, 1930.

PHIPPS, ALBERT HENRY, Indian Police (dep. insp.-genl., Burma) (b. 22nd Feb., 1899).—Joined the serv. as asst. dist. supt. Burma, 14th Jan., 1922; offg. dist. supt., March, 1928; confd., July, 1932; Lt.-Col. on mil. duty, Oct., 1942; resumed civ. duty, Dec., 1945; dist. supt., Dec., 1945; dep. inspr.-genl., armed civil pol., 1946.

PHIPSON, EDWARD SELBY, C.I.E., D.S.O., M.D., B.S., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Col., late I.M.S. (b. 10th March, 1884).—Retd. and re-employed; health offr., Simla, Apl., 1941.†

PHOOLKA, PURSHINDER SINGH, B.A., Indian Police, Punjab (b. 15th Apl., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn. after exam. of 1940.

PICK, S. G., Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th Nov., 1893).—Joined 22nd Aug., 1919; ch. traffic mangr. B.B. and C.I. Rly., 23rd May, 1945.

PILDITCH, SIR DENYS, C.I.E., Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 6th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1912; supt., July, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; addl. dep. dir., intell. bureau, Nov., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; dir., intell. bureau, Dec., 1939; confd. as dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; kt., Jan., 1944.

PILLAI, GOBIND KRISHNA, Public Works Dept., United Provs. (b. 10th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., 25th Dec., 1920; exec. engr., July, 1922; on depn. to Allahabad municipal bd., July, 1925.

PILLAI, SIR N. RAGHAVAN, K.C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A. (Mad.), M.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (secty., commerce dept., govt. of India) (b. 24th July, 1898).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Nov., 1922; arrived, 16th Jan., 1923, and served in

Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; asst. collr. customs, Madras, Dec., 1927; dep. dir. of comml. intell., Calcutta, May, 1929; dep. sec. to govt. of India, commerce dept., March, 1932; temp. jt. secty., ditto, June, 1934; offg. ditto, Feb., 1936; collr., Karachi, Apl., 1936; C.B.E., Feb., 1937; offcr. on special duty, govt. of India, commerce dept., Apl., 1937; dep. commr., confd., July, 1937; joint secty., govt. of India, commerce dept., Apl., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1939; addtl. sec., commerce dept., Feb., 1941; sec., ditto, Oct., 1942; on depn to U.K., Oct., 1944 to Apl., 1946; K.C.I.E., June, 1946; on depn. Paris peace conf., Aug., 1946.

PILLAY, K. T., JANID, Indian Store Dept., purch. offr., Aug., 1940; regl. commr., Calcutta, under indus. and supplies, dept., Mar., 1946.

PILLAI, T. G. P. S. (see Sanjivi Pillai, T. G. P.).

PILLAI, VELLAYAMBALAM KRISHNA BALAKRISHNA (see Balakrishna Pillai, V. K.).

PILLAY, THILLAINAYAKAM SIVARAMA SETU, O.B.E., M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 14th Apl., 1899).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1923; attd. officr., office of auditor-genl., Feb., 1936; asst. auditor-genl. (personnel), March, 1936; 2nd secty., cent. bd. of rev., May, 1938; 1st secty. to the cent. bd. of revenue and ex-officio dep. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept. (cent. revenues), Oct., 1938; dep. secty., govt. of India (commerce), and secty. econ. resources bd., Oct., 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; jt. secty., dept. of comm., govt. of India, March, 1943.

PIM, SIR ALAN WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 17th May, 1871).—Retd., July, 1930.*

PIMPUTKAR, MORESHWAG GAJANAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1919).—Educ. at Sarvajanik High Sch., Surat, Bombay Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. on probn. after seln. in Aug., 1940, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PINHEY, LOUIS ALEXANDER GORDON, O.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Oct., 1901).—First commn., 14th July, 1921; in milty. employ to Nov., 1925; undersecty. to A.G.G.; and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Apl., 1929; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Aug., 1929; ditto, Sibi, Dec., 1929; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, July, 1930; vice-consul, Zahidan, Nov., 1931; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Feb., 1933; secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Oct., 1934; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1934; addl. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Apl., 1936; offg. pol. agent, ditto, in addn., Nov., 1936; secty. to A.G.G., resdt. and C.C., Baluchistan, Sept., 1937; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta-Pishin, June, 1938; again, Sept., 1940; sec. to A.G.G., resdt., and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Nov., 1938; wazir-i-azam, Kalat state, Oct., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; pol. agt. E. Rajputana States, Feb., 1946; sec. to resdt. for Rajputana (addl. charge), June, 1946.

PINNELL, LEONARD GEORGE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 10th June, 1896).—Educ. at City of London Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; manager, Dacca Nawab estate, Nov., 1925; addl. dep. secty., fin., commerce and marine depts., Feb., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1930; confd., July, 1933; offg. lab.

commr., Bengal, Apl., 1934; pte. secty. to govt. of Bengal, Aug., 1935; secty. to the govr., Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; supervisor, I.C.S. probrs., Nov., 1940; pte. sec. to viceroy, Feb., 1942; dir., civ. supplies, Aug., 1942; princpl., officers trng. centre, Darjeeling, Oct., 1944.

PINK, ARTHUR WILFRID, Burma Police (cl. I) (b. 8th Aug., 1917).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

PIR, AHSAN-UD-DIN, B.A., Indian C.S.—Name changed to AHSAN-UD-DIN (q.v.).

PITAMBAR PANT (see Pant Pitambar).

PITAMBERDAS LAVJI THACKER (see Thacker, P. L.).

PITAM CHAND AGRAWAL, RAI BAHADUR, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., United Provs.) (b. Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1929; Rai Bahadur, Jan., 1931; sec., irrign. cttee., Jan., 1939; confd. as suptg. engr., Aug., 1943; p.a. to ch. engr., Nov., 1944.

PITKEATHLY, SIR JAMES STOTT, K.C.I.E., C.M.G., C.V.O., C.B.E., D.S.O., Indian Stores Dept. (chief contr. of stores) (b. 10th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1909, as electrical inspr.; electrical engr., Apl., 1911; C.V.O., Dec., 1911; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Sept., 1918; asst. dir. of works, electrical and mechanical sections, Mesopotamia, from Aug., 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; chief contr. of stores, Indian Stores Dept., 1st Jan., 1922; on foreign service under Ceylon govt., June, 1928; C.M.G., Jan., 1930; kt., June, 1933; on spl. duty under govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Nov., 1936, to Jan., 1937; offcr. on special duty, defence co-ordn. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1939; K.C.I.E., June, 1939; dep. dir.-genl. of supply, govt. of India, Aug., 1939.

PLANT, JOHN FREDERICK, LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 30th May, 1918).—Educ. at Hanley High Sch., and Birmingham Univ.; apptd. on probn. after seln. in June, 1941, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PLATT, ARTHUR JAMES, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sub.-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 13th Aug., 1908).—Ed. at Liverpool Collegiate Sch., and Trin. Hall, Camb.; apptd. after exam of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932; sub.-collr., March, 1934; under sec., rev. dept., March, 1940; dep. ditto, May, 1942; pte. sec. to govr., Dec., 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

PLATT, SYDNEY ERNEST, O.B.E., B.Sc., late Public Health Circle (engrg. bch.), Bihar (b. 17th Aug., 1884).—Retd., May, 1939.*

PLATTS, MATHIEU GEORGE, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., B.Sc., M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 17th Dec., 1886).—Retd., Dec., 1941.†

PLAYNE, HAROLD FRANCIS, B.A., V.D., late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 14th Jan., 1885).—Retd., 16th Dec., 1939.*

PLEW, SIDNEY FERDINAND, Indian Police (b. 8th May, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Nov., 1936, confd., Apl., 1939.

FLOWDEN, CHARLES TERENCE CHICHELE, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Political Service (b. 6th Feb., 1883).—Retd., Feb., 1938.*

FLOWDEN, PHILIP PETER MEREDYTH CHICHELE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at Eltham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 13th Dec., 1913, and served in the United Provs.

as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Aug., 1919, to Nov., 1919; joint mag., Apl., 1921; addl. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1922; sess. and sub-judge, Feb., 1927; and sess. judge, Oct., 1927; confd., June, 1929; offg. judl. secty. to govt., United Provs., May, 1933, to Oct., 1934; ret'd., 1946.

FLOWMAN, ALBERT CHARLES, M.B.E., late India Office (b. 16th June, 1883).—Retd., and re-employed.†

PLUMB, WALTER CYRIL, C.I.E., Indian Pol. (dep. inspr.-genl., Punjab) (b. 14th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 26th Dec., 1915; asst. supt., June, 1919; asst. dist. offcr., Oct., 1921; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; supt., Oct., 1924; dist. offcr., frontier constabulary, Sept., 1925, to March, 1937; confd. as dep. inspr.-gen., June, 1942; C.I.E., June, 1946; offr. in ch. S.I. agency, Punjab, June, 1946.

PLUMPTRE, CYRIL MACDONALD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 27th Dec., 1884).—Retd., March, 1939.*

PLUNKETT, FRANK BLENNERHASSETT, O.B.E. (Sind) (b. 17th Sept., 1886).—Joined 1st Aug., 1905; supt. of prisons, Bombay and Sind, Apl., 1921 to Sept. 1941; O.B.E., June, 1930.

POLLARD, RICHARD CHARLES, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1908).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 26th Nov., 1929; confd., Jan., 1933; on spec. duty for a.r.p., Sept., 1939; confd. supt., Nov., 1943.

POLLARD, STANLEY CLAUDE, Burma Front. Serv. (b. 24th Jan., 1917).—Apptd. 11th Jan., 1939.

POLLOCK, JOHN DOUGLAS ALEXANDER, Indian Police (b. 28th June, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, United Provs., Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

POLLOCK, RONALD EVELYN, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 17th Apl., 1891).—Educ. at Harrow and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 11th Dec., 1915, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; ditto, and under-sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1920; sub-judge, Nov., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1924; confd., Dec., 1928; secty., legal dept., Central Provs., March, 1930; offg. addl. judl. commr., June, 1932, and again, May, 1933; conjd., Jan., 1935; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936.

PONT, LEON VICTOR, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 7th Dec., 1883).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

POPE, ALFRED HENRY GEORGE, M.B.E., India Office (actg. supt., tel. and mails bch.) (b. 24th March, 1904).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 14th Oct., 1921; actd. higher ditto, Nov., 1932; confd., Dec., 1934; M.B.E., May, 1937; actg. asst. supt., tel. and mails bch., Aug., 1943; actg. supt., ditto, and clerk of codes, July, 1944.

PORTEOUS, JOHN BRIAN, India Office (exec. offcr., acctnt.-genl.'s acpt.) (b. 29th Aug., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 13th Oct., 1919; clerical offcr., Jan., 1920; exec. offcr., Dec., 1926; actg. hr. ditto, Mar., 1940; assessor of income tax, Jan., 1946.

PORTER, ALFRED ERNEST, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (addl. secty., govt. home dept., Bengal) (b. 2nd Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the

service, 28th Oct., 1922; arrived, 14th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. dir. of comml. intell. dept., govt. of India, Aprl., 1925; offg. under-secty., govt. of Bengal, pol. and appt. dept.; confd., March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1927; supt., census operations, Bengal, Aprl., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1933; confd., June, 1933; jt. secty., agric. and indus. dept., Bengal, July, 1935; addtl. secty. to govt., home dept., Bengal, Oct., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1942; sec. to govr., Feb., 1944; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Nov., 1945; sec. exec. council, July, 1946.

PORTER, ARTHUR WILLIAM, O.B.E., late Burma Front. Serv. (b. 23rd Dec., 1887).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Jan., 1911; asst. supt. (B.F.S.) Dec., 1922; O.B.E., June, 1930; dep. commr., March, 1938; ret'd., May, 1945.

PORTER, REV. JOHN, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senr. chaplain) (b. 7th Aprl., 1889).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 2nd Oct., 1921; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1923; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1931.

POTTER, ARTHUR KINGSNOTE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 7th Aprl., 1905).—Educ. at Charterhouse and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., home and pol. dept., Aug., 1931; dep. secty., fin. dept., Burma, March, 1934; offg. dep. commr., June, 1934; contr. of fin. (defence), Aprl., 1937; dep. commr. (confd.), March, 1938; contr. fin. (def.), Jan., 1941; contr. mil. accts. and fin. 1945; contr. of prices, Rangoon, 1945.

POULTON, HENRY MORTIMER, C.I.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Aprl., 1898).—First commn., dated 18th June, 1917; joined the pol. dept., 16th May, 1922; supy. asst. commr., Bannu, March, 1924; asst. commr. ditto, Aprl., 1944; asst. commr., Kohat, March, 1925; asst. pol. agent, Zhob, Dec., 1927; pol. agent, Chagai, and dep. commr., Shorarnd, Aug., 1928; pol. agent, Zhob, Nov., 1928; ditto, Kalat, June, 1929; ditto, Chagai, Aug., 1929; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, Aug., 1930; pol. agent, Kalat, Nov., 1931; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, May, 1932; ditto, Haroati and Tonk, Oct., 1932; ditto, E. Rajputana States, Sept., 1934; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar agency, Aug., 1935; pol. agent, Bunkelkhand, Feb., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; pol. agent, Jaipur, Aprl., 1942; pol. agent, E. Rajputana States (in addtn.), Aug., 1942; resdt., Gwalior and States of Rampur and Benares, Aprl., 1944; offg. resdt. cl. II, and resdt. at Gwalior and for States of Rampur and Benares, Feb., 1945; pol. agt. E. Rajputana States (addl. charge), Feb., 1945; offg. resdt. cl. I, and agt. to govr.-genl., resdt. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, May, 1946.

POULTON, JOHN CECIL, Burma Front. Serv., (b. 18th Dec., 1901).—Apptd. 13th Nov., 1924; civ. aff. serv. Mar., 1943 to Aprl., 1946.

POUNDE, ERIC LLEWELLYN (b. 2nd Aprl., 1908), Office of high commr. for Ind., apptd. Jul., 1931; p.a. to liaison offcr., Ind. Armments prodn., Jul., 1941; gen. asst. to H.C., Oct., 1945; pte. sec. to ditto May, 1946.

POUNDE, HERBERT RICHARD GILDER, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 31st Jan., 1905).—Apptd. exec. offcr., 10th Dec., 1935, after previous unestabd. service from 8th Aug., 1927.

POWELL, ARTHUR BARRINGTON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 24th Aprl., 1918).—Educ. at Cowbridge Gr. Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; a.d.c. to govr., Nov., 1941 to Nov., 1943.

POWELL, EDWIN VALENTINE MURRAY, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. offcr., Burma Rlys.) (b. 14th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 23rd July, 1920; offg. variously as dist. loco. supt., from March, 1921; transpn. offcr., Aprl., 1929; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Oct., 1936.

POWELL, REV. LLEWELLYN MONTAGUE SAXON, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 8th Feb., 1906).—Joined the service, Feb., 1936; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1938.

POWELL, RICHARD, late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 4th Aprl., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1908; in mil. employ from 19th Aprl., 1915, to 4th June, 1916; asst. to inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Aug., 1918; supt., May, 1922; princpl., P.T. sch., Moradabad, Dec., 1925; asst. to inspr.-genl., Jan., 1937; dep. inspr.-genl., Aprl., 1939; ret'd., 4th Aprl., 1944, and re-empld. in dept. of war transport, Aprl., 1944 to Feb., 1946.

POWELL-PRICE, JOHN CADWAGAN, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (United Provs.) (b. 11th June, 1888).—Ret'd., Sept., 1943.†

POWER, DAVID LLOYD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 20th March, 1919).—Educ. at University Coll. Sch., Hampstead, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. after seln. in May, 1940, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., addl. under sec., home dept., June, 1944; under sec., ditto, Aug., 1944; (confd.) July, 1945; under sec., home and ch. minister's dept., Oct., 1945.

POWER, ROBIN TURENNE, late Indian State Rlys., Burma (b. 9th Oct., 1883).—Ret'd., June, 1940.†

POWIS, LEONARD JOHN PEARSON, M.I.St.E., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 9th Dec., 1888).—Ret'd., Dec., 1943.†

PRABHU DATTA SHASTRI, M.A., B.Sc., Ph.D. (Kiel), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th March, 1885).—Ret'd., March, 1940.†

PRADHAN, DWARKANATH RAMCHANDRA, B.A., B.Sc., Ind. C.S. (reg. dir. resettlmt. etc., Bombay) (b. 8th June, 1906).—Ed. at Bombay Univ. and Fitzwilliam House, Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Dec., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. var.; collr. (confd.) Oct., 1944; chairman, N.S. lab. trib. and reg. dir., resettlmt. and employmt., in addition, Nov., 1945.

PRALL, SAMUEL REGINALD, M.D., B.C., M.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Bombay (supt., St. George's hosp.) (b. 18th Feb., 1891).—Date of first commn., 25th Feb., 1921; in milty. employ until 23rd June, 1930; civil surgn., June, 1932; offg. supt., St. George's hosp., March, 1934; confd., July, 1934.

PRANCE, GEOFFREY BERNARD SANDEMAN, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 28th July, 1896).—Joined the service, 29th Aprl., 1919; asst. supt., May, 1921; addl. supt., Aprl., 1925; supt., Aug., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; awarded bar to ditto, Jan., 1928; dist. officer, frontier constaby., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1944; ret'd., Feb., 1945.

PRASAD, BIND BASNI, M.A., LL.B., Rai

Bahadur (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.).—Joined the U.P. civil (Judl.) serv., 7th Nov., 1921; dist. and sess. judge (confd.), Apl., 1944.

PRASAD, JYOTI, B.A., Rai Bahadur, (mag. and collr. United Provs.).—Joined the U.P. civil exec. serv., 2nd Oct., 1916; mag. and collr. (confd.), Dec., 1943.

PRASAD, KUNWAR JAGDISH (see Jagdish Prasad, Kunwar).

PRASAD, KANCHI (see Kanchi Prasad).

PRASAD, NIRINJAN, GOYAL, LALA (see Nirinjan, P. G. L.).

PRASAD, DR. RAJENDRA (b. 1884). Agriculture and Food member of govr.-genl's. Council, Sept., 1946.

PRASAD, RAJENDRA, Rai Bahadur, M.B., B.S., B.Sc., P.M.S., United Provs. Medl. Serv., (b. March, 1893).—Apptd. 15th Nov., 1920; civ. surg. (confd.).

PRASAD, RAM (see Ram Prasad).

PRASAD, RAMESHWARA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 6th Sept., 1914).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Colombo, Colombo Univ., King's Coll., London, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 8th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939; sub-collr. and jt. mag. Sept., 1943; under sec. home dept., Apl., 1945.

PRASAD, SHYAM SUNDER (see Shyam Sunder Prasad).

PRASADA, KRISHNA (see Krishna Prasad).

PRASADA, SHANKAR, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 11th March, 1905).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Apl., 1935.

PRASAD, SHANTI (see Shanti Prasad).

PRASAD, SITAL (see Sital Prasad).

PRASANNA KUMAR ACHARYA, M.A., D.Litt., Ph.D., late Indian Educ. Service, (b. 21st Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as prof'r. Muir central College, Allahabad, 1st Dec., 1920; with Allahabad Univ. from 1st July, 1922; ret'd., Apl., 1945.

PRASHAD, BAINI (see Baini Prashad).

PRASHAD, BALESHWAR, Indian Police, Bihar and Orissa (b. 1st July, 1902).—Apptd. dep. supt., Nov., 1923; prom. to I.P. as supt., July, 1939; King's Police and Fire services medal, Jan., 1946.

PRASHAD, DURGA (see Durga Prashad).

PRASHAD, KAMTA (see Kamta Prashad).

PRATAP, RAM (see Ram Pratap).

PRATT, EDWARD BROUGHTON, Indian Police (b. 22nd Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 26th Nov., 1926; offg. comdt. of police, Aden, Apl., 1933; a.d.c. to the govr., Apl., to Oct., 1936; supt., customs intell., W. India, Feb., 1941, to March, 1942; confd. as dist. supt., Sept., 1944.

PRATT, SIR HENRY SHELDON, Kt., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 26th Jan., 1873).—Ret'd., July, 1930.*

PREM CHAND, PURI, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service, 24th Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

PREM NATH, LALA late Indian Forest Service (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Apptd. asst. consvr., Punjab, 19th July, 1922; on deptn. to Chamba state from 19th Apl., 1926, to Jan., 1931; dep. consvr., Jan., 1931; ret'd.,

PREMSWARUP, Indian Police (b. 16th Aug., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Feb., 1938.

PRENDERGAST, W. H., Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Jan., 1895).—Dep. ch. engr., B. & A. rly., Nov., 1945.

PRESCOTT, RICHARD GORDON BATHGATE, C.M.G., O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Burma) (b. 30th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 21st Dec., 1915; dist. supt., Oct., 1923; awarded King's police medal, June, 1932; dep. commr., Rangoon police, May, 1937; commr., March, 1939; confd. dep. inspr.-genl. of police, May, 1941; O.B.E., June, 1941; C.M.G., Jan., 1943; civ. aff. serv. to Dec., 1945; on deptn. to U.K., Dec., 1945.

PRIA DAS, TANDAN, Rai Sahib, jala., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 6th Dec., 1884).—Ret'd., Dec., 1939.*

PRICE, ARCHIBALD MILNER, Dept. of Indus. and Labour, Govt. of India (b. 18th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service, 8th Dec., 1906; supt., dept. of indus. and lab., July, 1927; asst. secty., Oct., 1934; ditto, lab. dept., Nov., 1937; admin. offcr., cent. p.w.d., July, 1940.

PRICE, EARDLEY EDWARD CARNAC, C.I.E., A.C.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 11th March, 1892).—Joined the service, 11th June, 1919; asst. acctnt.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1920; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Aug., 1925; asst. acctnt.-genl., Bombay, Nov., 1928; asst. dir., comml. audit, Bombay, Nov., 1928; dep. acctnt.-genl., Bombay, May, 1932; services lent to Jodhpur Durbar, Jan., 1933; services lent to the govt. of N.W.F.P., Apl., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1942; confd. acctnt.-genl. (cl. II), Oct., 1943; addl. sec. govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1945.

PRICE, ERNEST, M.B.E., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 14th Dec., 1879).—Ret'd., Dec., 1939.*

PRICE, GLADYS MAUD, India Audit Office, (hr. exec. offcr.) (b. 22nd May, 1898).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 20th Jan., 1922; exec. offcr., Oct., 1926; hr. ditto, Sept., 1938.

PRICE, MURRAY ROKEBY, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 29th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 23rd May, 1924; offg. supt., May, 1929; confd., Feb., 1934; princpl., police trng. sch., Nasik, Apl., 1943, to Sept., 1944; served in Sind from Oct., 1944.

PRICE, WILLIAM ALAN BOURNE, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 28th Apl., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., Apl., 1925; confd., May, 1929.

PRICHARD, HENRY MONTAGUE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 29th May, 1887).—Ret'd., Aug., 1938.*

PRIDEAUX, EDWIN TERREY, O.B.E., B.A., (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (collr., Bihar) (b. 16th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Latymer Upper Sch., Hammersmith, and Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1935; O.B.E., June, 1942; sec., local self-govt. dept., Oct., 1943; collr. (confd.), Oct., 1945; again sec. local self-govt. dept., Sept., 1946.

PRIDMORE, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Ajmer Merwara) (b. 17th Aug., 1910).—Educ. at Rugby and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United

Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magt., Nov., 1936; dep. commr., Ajmer Merwara.

PRIESTLEY, SIR GERALD WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., Ind. C.S. (1st member, bd. of rev. Madras) (b. 12th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at West Monmouthshire Sch., Pontypool, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; supt., Pudukkottai state, Aug. to Sept., 1915; spl. asst. settl. officer, Nov., 1916; sub-collr. and jt. mag. and ditto, Oct., 1917; special settl. officer, July, 1919, to March, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1927; confd., Aug., 1930; commr. of Coorg, Nov., 1937; commr. of labour, Nov., 1936, to Oct., 1937; member, bd. of rev., Aug., 1939; chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1942, to Dec., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1944; advr. to the govr., July, 1944; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1946; 1st member, bd. of rev., May, 1946.

PRING, NOEL GRATTAN, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr. of forests N.W.F.P.) (b. 30th Dec., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 6th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Dec., 1926; served under def. dept., Sept., 1941; consvr. of forests, N.W.F.P., Mar., 1946.

PRINGLE, IVAN TERENCE ST. CLAIR, M.A., late Indian State Rlys. (E.B. Rly.) (b. 20th Feb., 1887).—Retd., Feb., 1942.†

PRINGLE, REGINALD JAMES, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 23rd March, 1910).—Educ. at Felsted Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. pte. secty. to govr., Bengal, Feb., 1936; apptd. to fin. and comm. provl., March, 1940; under-secty., dept. of commerce, govt. of India, Sept., 1940; M.B.E., Jan., 1944; sec., cent. bd. of rev., Oct., 1944.

PRIOR, SIR CHARLES GEOFFREY, K.C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 9th Dec., 1896).—First commn. dated 14th July, 1915; joined the pol. dept., 24th Aug., 1923; under-secty. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Oct., 1924; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Aug., 1926; sec. to ditto, Apl., 1927; under-secty. to A.G.G., Punjab states, March, 1929; pol. agent, Bahrein, Apl., 1929; offg. dep. secty., for. and pol. dept., June, 1933; confd., May, 1934; offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1934; and again, Apl., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1936; prime minister, Alwar state, Nov., 1936; pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Sept., 1939; member, Middle East war council, as repr. of govt. of India, Nov., 1941; K.C.I.E., June, 1943; resdt. cl. I and pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Nov., 1945.

PRIOR, SIR HENRY CARLOS, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar) (b. 6th Jan., 1890).—Educ. at Eton, and King's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 30th Nov., 1914; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. to army dept., Oct., 1915 (commndt., 3rd (Naga Hills) Bn., Assam Rifles) to Oct., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1921; confd., Jan., 1929; manager, Bettiah wards estate, June, 1926; addl. secty. to govt., rev. dept., July, 1929; secty., ditto, Oct., 1931; secty., fin. dept., B. and O., Apl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1936; temp. addtl. secty. to govt. of India, commerce dept., Apl., 1940; secty., dept. of lab., Feb., 1941; C.S.I., June, 1943; sec., dept. of labour, June, 1944; govt. of India del. to Int. lab. conf., 1944 and 1945; member, coun.

of state, 1941-46; sec. works, mines and power, dept., Apl., 1946; K.C.I.E., June, 1946; reverted to Bihar, July, 1946; ret'd.

PRITAM SINGH, BRAR, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 17th Nov., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 7th Aug., 1921; offg. supt., Feb., 1928; confd., Nov., 1933.

PRITCHARD, JOSEPH WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (sec., home dept., Madras) (b. 5th August, 1903).—Educ. at Newport (Salop) Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived Dec., 1927; sub-collr., July, 1932; offg. mag. and collr., var. from May, 1936; commr. of Coorg, Nov., 1939; ch. commr., July, 1940; collr. and dist. mag., Feb., 1943; sec., home, dept. Feb., 1946.

PROCTOR, ERNEST, M.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., Burma Rlys.) (b. 3rd July, 1894).—Joined the service, 15th Apl., 1920, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Burma rlys., July, 1933.

PROMODE KUMAR DEY (see Dey, P. K.).
PROPHULLA CHANDRA KANJILAL, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1886).—Ret'd., 2nd Mar., 1941.†

PROTHERO, EDWIN LYNDON, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; addl. secty. to govt., rev. dept., July, 1929; under-secty. to govt. (estab.), Punjab, Feb., 1935; on for. serv., Bawalpur state, Aug., 1938; suptg. engr. perm't., July, 1941; on spl. duty, Nov., to Dec., 1945.

PROUDLOCK, ROBIN, Indian State Rlys. (divl. comml. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th June, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 8th Nov., 1914; asst. dist. ditto, Jan., 1922; offg. dist. ditto, Oct., 1923 (lent to Q.M.Q.'s branch, Jan., 1927); divl. transpn. officer, July, 1927; divl. comml. officer, Oct., 1930; dep. ch. comml. manager, provl., Dec., 1940; divl. supt. (jr.), May, 1942; ch. comml. mgr. N.W. Rly. (confd. provl.), Feb., 1945.

PRU, U SAW HLA, B.A., B.L., late Burma C.S. (b. 24th Nov., 1886).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., 22nd Feb., 1915; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1928; confd. Oct., 1930; sec. to govt., judl. dept., Burma Oct., 1933; excise commr., Burma, March, 1936; ret'd., 1942.

PRUTHI, DR. HEM SINGH, (b. 23rd Feb., 1897).—Joined the service, 25th Aug., 1920; apptd. demonstrator in zoology-entomology, Punjab Agric. Coll., Lyallpur, Aug., 1920; supt. prof. entomology, ditto, Oct., 1920; asst. supt., zoological survey of India, July, 1925; confd. July, 1927; Impl. Entomologist, Imp. Agric. Research Inst., May, 1937; dir. ditto, 1944; plant protect. advr. govt. of India, and dir. of locust control, dept. of agric., Oct., 1945.

PRYDE, ARTHUR WALTER, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 3rd Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, 26th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confd., Oct., 1932; lab. officer, Bombay, Nov., 1935; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1939; transferred to Sind, Oct., 1939; offg. var., May, 1941 to Oct., 1945.

PUCKLE, SIR FREDERICK HALE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th June, 1889).—Educated at Uppingham, and King's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1913, and served in

the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from May, 1915, to Apl., 1919; dep. commr. (prov.), Oct., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1930; fin. secty. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1930; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1933; confd., Feb., 1935; employed on spl. duty in the home dept., govt. of India, May, 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938; sec., home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1938; again, Oct., 1939; dir.-genl. of information, Dec., 1939; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1942; ret'd., Apl., 1946.

PUDDEN, HEDLEY HERBERT CRAWFORD, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 1st Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Oct., 1928; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; civ. aff. serv., Dec., 1944 to Dec., 1945.

PUGH, MAURICE DOUGLAS, Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 9th Nov., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 12th Dec., 1923; asst. dist. offcr., frontier constab., Nov., 1929; offg. supt., Sept., 1933.

PULLA REDDI, OBULREDDIGARI, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag. Madras) (b. 11th July, 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr., July, 1930; under-secty. to govt., dev. dept., March, 1931; Diwan of Jeypure estate, Jan., 1932, to Dec., 1935; dep. commr., excise, Aug., 1937; commr., corp. of Madras, Jan., 1940; first minister to govt. Mysore, Nov., 1944.

PULLEYNE, GEOFFREY BERNARD, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 12th May, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1910; dist. supt., March, 1924; supt., rly. police, Hyderabad state, Oct., 1929.

PURANIK, WASUDEO RAMCHANDRA, B.A., LL.B. (Central Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1886).—Govt. advocate, Jan., 1936; advocate-genl., Apl., 1937; judge, Nagpur high ct., 22nd June, 1942; puisne judge, Oct., 1943; ret'd., Sept., 1946.

PURDY, JOSEPH WILLIAM, Indian Police (b. 29th Nov., 1912).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt. of police, Bengal, Nov., 1933.

PURI, AMAR NATH, RAI BAHADUR B.A., LL.B., Com. Dept., Govt. of India (asst. sec.) (b. 3rd May, 1897).—Joined the service, 10th June, 1918; supt., dept. of comm., Jan., 1929; asst. secty., govt. of India, dept. of comm., Apl., 1935; offg. asst. collr. customs, Sept., 1939; confd., Jan., 1940; dep. ch. contr., imports, June, 1944.

PURI, BALDEO DAS, M.A. (b. 26th March, 1876).—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1922, as prof. of maths., Thomson Civ. Engrg. Coll., Roorkee; confd., May, 1926.

PURI, B. D., RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 23rd June, 1883).—Ret'd., June, 1938.*

PURI, BALWANT SINGH, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (cent. P.W.D.) (b. 16th July, 1894).—Joined the service in Burma, 7th Oct., 1918; asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1929; services placed at disposal of central p.w.d., March, 1935; O.B.E., June, 1944; addl. ch. engr., July, 1944; second ditto, Oct., 1944.

PURI, INDRA SINGH, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Aud. Dept. (b. 17th May, 1895).—Joined the serv. 1st March, 1919; dep. dir., rly. audit, Apl., 1929; offg. contr., rly. accts., March, 1931; accts. offcr., rly. clearing accts., Feb., 1933;

offg. dep. dir., ditto, March, 1933; accts. offcr., N.W. rly., Nov., 1933; offg. dep. ch. accts. offcr., N.W. rly., July, 1934, and again, June, 1937; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accounts office, Oct., 1935; dep. ch. accts. offcr., N.W. rly., June, 1937; fin. offcr. and ch. accts. offcr., E.B. rly., Nov., 1940; offg. dir. of fin., rly. bd., May, 1941; dir., ditto, May, 1943; confd. as acct.-genl., Aug., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

PURI, NEHCHAL DAS, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Jail Dept., Punjab (b. 5th Dec., 1888).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; supt., central jail, Jan., 1924; dep. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Feb., 1927; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, March, 1932, June, 1933; and again, Feb., 1935; confd., May, 1939; ret'd.

PURI, PREM CHAND (see Prem Chand Puri).

PURI, TARA CHAND, M.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (dist. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 1st Apl., 1914).—Educ. at D.A.V. High Sch., Lahore, Punjab and Nagpur Univs., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr.; under-sec., educn., etc., dept., Aug., 1942; addtl. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1944; dist. and mag. collr., Mar., 1946.

PURI, YOGENDRA KRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 25th July, 1916).—Educ. at D.A.V. High Sch., Lahore, Pun. ab Univ., Univ. Coll., London, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 7th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; under sec. (home), June, 1943—July, 1944; dir. of supply, Oct., 1945.

PURSELL, RICHARD STANLEY, C.I.E., O.B.E., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 5th July, 1882).—Ret'd., May, 1938.*

PUTTICK, HAROLD WALTER, M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. ret'd., (N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. elect. engr., 4th Oct., 1921; offg. dist. elect. engr., Oct., 1928; confd., Nov., 1936; ch. elec. engr., N.W. rly., June, 1940 to 1946.

PYNE, WILLIAM HENRY JOHN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 30th June, 1882).—Ret'd., May, 1939.*

PYPER, JOHN CLARK, O.B.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (b. 18th March, 1889).—Ret'd. and re-employed.†

PYU, MAUNG KHIN MAUNG, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 19th March, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Rangoon and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1938; provi. dep. commr., Apl., 1941; confd., June, 1941; civ. aff. serv. from July, 1945; sec. to govt., indust. and lab. dept., 1945; dep. commr., 1946.

QADIR, ABDUL (see Abdul Qadir).

QAIYUM, A. (see Abdul Qaiyum).

QAMAR UL ISLAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 18th Jan., 1919).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1942; served in Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Mar., 1946.

QARNI, ABSAR HUSAIN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 31st March, 1917).—Educ. at Aligarh Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-divl. offr., Jan., 1944; under sec. fin. dept., July, 1946.

QUAYLE, THOMAS, C.I.E., D.Litt., M.A., Office of High Commr. for India (sec., educn. dept.) (b. 13th Sept., 1884).—Apptd. jt. sec. for Indian students, July, 1921; joint sec., educn. dept., Apl., 1925; asst. ditto, Aug., 1926; designation changed to secty., ditto, Oct., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1936.

QUILTER, CHARLES FREDERICK GRANT, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 13th Dec., 1883).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

QUIN, THOMAS MICHAEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Sept., 1911).—Educ. at Stonyhurst, New Coll., Oxford, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

QUINLAN, PATRICK BAZOT, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (chairman, public service commission, Burma) (b. 12th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service as princpl. of govt. high and normal sch., Moulmein, 16th Feb., 1915; on mil. service from 1st Sept., 1916, to 2nd March, 1919; inspr. of schs., Aug., 1919; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; dir., ditto, Feb., 1935; chairman, public service commission, Apl., 1939.

QUINN-YOUNG, FREDERICK CHARLES STEPHEN (dist. supt.), Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Central Provs., 27th Nov., 1922; offg. dist. supt., June, 1925, Feb., 1926, and again, Oct., 1932; confd., Aug., 1937.

QURAISHI, ABUL HASAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st July, 1908).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Ballia, Queen's Intermediate Coll., Benares, Allahabad Univ., and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Oct., in Bengal, as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and sub-divl. offcr., July, 1935; spec. offcr., fin. dep., Nov., 1937; mag. and collr., Jan., 1942; dep. sec., commerce, etc., dept., central govt., Oct., 1944.

QURAISHI, FAZAL ELAHI, Khan Bahadur, B.A., (b. 15th May, 1892).—Joined the serv., Feb., 1915, and held non-gaz. appt. to Apl., 1936, dep. sec. Fed. Pub. Serv., Commn., Aug., 1945; Khan Bahadur, Jan., 1946.

QURAISHI, MANZOOR ALAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag.).—Apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in United Provs. as asst. mag.

QURBAN ALI KHAN, Mir, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 11th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 8th Nov., 1921; asst. supt., Nov., 1923; supt., July, 1932; dep. inspr.-genl., in addn., May, 1935; served under govt. of India, def. dept., as d.i.g. spec. staff, June, 1941.

QURESHI, SALAH-UD-DIN., Indian Police North West Frontier Prov.) (b. 15th Oct., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Feb., 1940.

RAB, SAIYID MUJIBUR (see Saiyid Mujibur, R.).

RACHHPAL SINGH, Rai Bahadur THAKUR, late United Provs. C.S. (b. 20th Sept., 1880).—Retd., Sept., 1940.†

RACHPAL SINGH, late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 9th June, 1887).—Retd., June, 1942.

RADFORD, JOHN EDWARD DICEY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 12th Feb., 1916).—Educ. at Winchester and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1928; confd., 12th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

RADFORD, RONALD WALTER, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 28th Feb., 1916).—Educ. at High Sch. for Boys, Southend-on-Sea, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., under sec., fin. dept., Oct., 1943; dist. mag. and collr., July, 1945.

RADHA KRISHNA AIYAR, TARURAI SESHUIYAR, Rao Bahadur, B.A., late Indian Audit Dept., (b. 15th Feb., 1886).—Retd., Feb., 1940.†

RADHA KRISHNA GUPTA, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 18th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933; under-sec. (genl.), p.w.d., irrign., Aug., 1944.

RADHAKRISHNAN, KOMARALINGAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 15th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at the P.S. High Sch., Mylapore, Madras, the Madras Univ., the Sch. of Economics, London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Central Provs., Jan., 1937; under-sec. to govt., Aug., 1940, to July, 1941; admin., Baster state, Jan., 1942, to Sept., 1942; sub-judge under trng., Dec., 1943.

RADICE, PETER WOLFE, Indian C.S. (sec. local self-govt. and public health depts., United Provinces) (b. 18th June, 1908).—Educ. at Blundell's School, Tiverton, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1933; dep. commr., Port Blair, Sept., 1941; sec. local self-govt., and public health depts., Dec., 1945.

RAE, BERTRAM LANGFORD DENIS, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 28th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 12th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., May, 1933; confd., Feb., 1939; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; dist. supt., 1946.

RAE, CECIL JAMES, M.Eng., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd June, 1899).—Retd., Dec., 1940; re-employed under crown in Sierra Leone, to May, 1946.

RAFI, MIAN MUHAMMAD, B.A. (Oxon.), Barr. (secty., legislative assembly dept.) (b. 1st Sept., 1889).—Apptd. dep. secty., legisl. assembly dept., 1st Aug., 1931; secty., ditto, Sept., 1933.

RAFIQAHMAD, SAIYID, B.A., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 14th July, 1901).—Joined the service as dep. supt. of police, 1st Dec., 1924; confd. Dec., 1926; supt. (confd.), Apl., 1938.

RAGHAVACHARI, NEMAN ECHAMBADI SRINIVASA, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 10th June, 1912).—Educ. at Town High Sch., Guntur, Andhra Univ., Madras Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 17th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1938; addtl. under-sec., public dept., Jan., 1942; under-sec., ditto, May, 1942; under-sec. P.W.D., Oct., 1942; textile cmmr., Aug., 1945; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

RAGHAVAN, NARAYANA IYER VIRA, B.A., LL.B., (Sind) (b. 7th Feb., 1895).—Joined, 1st Dec., 1918; held non-gaz. appts. to March,

1936; asst. to sec. to govt. fin. dept., Apl., 1936; passport offr. (in addn.), June, 1936; examr., local fund accts., Apl., 1946.

RAGHAVAN, TANDALAM NARASIMHACHARI SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 1st Oct., 1901).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 30th Nov., 1925; sub-collr., Aug., 1927; under-sec. to govt., revenue dept., Madras, July, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1936; secty. to commr. of excise, Madras, July, 1936; dep. secty. to govt. fin. dept., Madras, May, 1938; agent to the govt. of India in Ceylon, Sept., 1940, to June, 1943; sec., bd. of rev., Dec., 1943; spec. offr. Themgabadra Project, Feb., 1945; sec. fin. dept., Apl., 1946.

RAGHAVENDRA RAO, PATTADAKAL VENKANNA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (b. 19th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at Maharaja's High School, Mysore, Universities of Mysore and Calcutta, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933; and served in Bombay, as asst. collr.; under sec. secretariat of govt.-genl. (public), Nov., 1941; dep. sec. govt. of India, legis. dept., Apl., 1944; dep. sec., govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1944.

RAGHUNATH RAI, HANDA, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 9th Nov., 1899).—Joined the service. 11th Oct., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1932; confd., Oct., 1932; offg. under-secty., local self govt. dept., Punjab, Jan., 1933; under-sec (constrn. and gen.) and dir., central designs, Punjab, May, 1943.

RAGHUNATHA RAO, S., B.A., Barr., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 10th May, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Feb., 1924; dep. consvr., Feb., 1928.

RAHA, KSHETRA MOHAN, Civ. Aviation Dir. (b. 7th Nov., 1905).—Joined the service as aerodrome offr. (temp.) Feb., 1931; confd. Mar., 1934; offr. on spec. duty (post-war planning), Nov., 1943; on depn. empire conf. civil aviation and radio tech., Jan., 1944 to Mar., 1944; on spec. duty with C.E.C.R.C.A., July, to Sept., 1945; on spec. duty p.w. planng. on provl. intl. civ. aviation org., Montreal Canada, as council member for India, Jan., 1946.

RAHAMAN, Q. F., B.Sc., (Eng.) (b. 30th Jan., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1925; confd. Oct., 1927; exec. engr. (junr.) July, 1938.

RAHIM, JALLALUDDIN ABDUR, Indian C.S. (India govt. trade commr., Alexandria) (b. 27th July, 1906).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge and Munich Univ.; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., March, 1935; asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Nov., 1936; dep. sec. (supply), govt. of India, Apl., 1944; on spec. duty, commerce dept., Aug., 1945; Indian govt. trade commr., Alexandria, Oct., 1945.

RAHIM, KHWAJA ABDUR, B.A., Indian C.S.; name changed to Abdur Rahim (q.v.).

RAHIM, MUHAMMAD INAMUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 1st Jan., 1898).—Educ. at Morris Coll., Nagpur, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. by nomination in India; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep.

commr., variously from March, 1927; under-sec., govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., June, 1927, to Oct., 1930; dep. commr. (confd.), March, 1937; sec. to govt. (comm. and indus.), Apl., 1941; commr. of settlts., May, 1942.

RIHIMTOOLA, SULEMAN JAFFERBHOY, B.A., LL.B., Bombay Judl. Dept., (b. 13th March, 1903).—Joined the service 1st Apl., 1931; offg. assoc. high ct. Bombay, Nov., 1930 to March, 1931; ditto, confd. Apl., 1931; offg. asst. taxing master, Bombay, var. from Mar., 1934 to Mar., 1935; offg. regr., Apl., 1935 to Oct., 1936; ditto (confd.) Nov., 1936; senr. master, high ct., Bombay, Nov., 1940; (confd.), Apl., 1941.

RAHIMTULLA, SIR IBRAHIM, G.B.E., K.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Member of govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, from 1st March, 1918, to 17th March, 1923.*

RAHIMUDDIN AHMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Police (Assam) (b. 22nd Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

RAHMAN, ABDUR (see Abdur Rahman).
RAHMAN, ABUL FAEZ MUJIBOR (see Abul Faez Mujibor Rahman).

RAHMAN, AZIZUR (see Azizur Rahman).
RAHMAN, MOWDUDUR (see Mowdudur Rahman).

RAHMAN, MUHAMMAD ABDUR, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 22nd Oct., 1881).—Retd., Oct., 1936.*; member, centl. legis. Aug., 1937; member, Sandhurst Indianisation cttee. and def. cons. cttee., 1939; spl. offr. (recruitment), supply dept., Feb., 1942; member, fed. pub. serv. comm., May, 1942.

RAHMAN, MUHAMMAD EHSANUR (see Muhammad E. R.).

RAHMAN, SHAIKH ABDUR, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 4th June, 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 29th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Apl., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935; ditto, sub perm., Dec., 1937.

RAHMATULLAH, SHAHABUDDIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 9th June, 1913).—Educ. at Arrah Zillah Sch., Arrah, and Patna Univ.; joined the service, 1st Dec., 1937; arrived in India, Dec., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1939; addl. dist. mag., June, 1943; mag. and collr., Apl., 1944.

RAI, AJILIDAY NARAYANA (see Narayana Rai, A.).

RAI, C. B. (see Roy, C. B.).
RAI, HAKIM (see Hakim Rai).
RAI, HAKUMAT (see Hakumat R.).
RAI, INDARJIT (see Indarjit Rai).
RAI, LAKHPAT (see Lakhpai Rai).
RAI, MANGAT (see Mangat Rai).
RAI, NAUBAT (see Naubat Rai).

RAISMAN, SIR ABRAHAM JEREMY, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., B.A., Ind. C.S. (b. 19th Mar., 1892).—Educ. at Leeds Central High Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, 5th Dec., 1916, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., finl. dept., Oct., 1920; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, Oct., 1922; ditto, Calcutta, June, 1926; commr. of inc. tax, Punjab and N.W.F.P., Feb., 1928, and again, Sept., 1929; on spl. duty, central bd. of rev., Aug., 1929; temp. dep. secty., govt. of India, commerce dept., Feb., 1931; offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1931; confd., March, 1932; C.I.E.,

Jan., 1934; offg. member, central bd. of rev., June, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; addl. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept. (temp.), June, 1936; offg. secty., ditto, Apl., 1937; director, Reserve Bank of India, Oct., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Apl., 1939; to Apl., 1945; kt., June, 1939, K.C.S.I., June, 1941; G.C.I.E., June, 1945.

RAJAMANNAR, P. V., Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 10th May, 1901).—Apptd. advocate-genl., 10th July, 1944.

RAJ NARAYAN BRAHMAWAR, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; divl. forest offcr., Apl., 1926; dep. consvr., Oct., 1931.

RAJ NARAYAN SINGH, M.B.E., B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., May, 1926; M.B.E., Jan., 1941.

RAJA RAM, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E. (b. 28th June, 1891).—Retd., May, 1939.†

RAJADHAKSHA, ANANT GANESH, Indian Police (b. 2nd Feb., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Feb., 1939.

RAJADHYAKSHA, GANPAT SAKHARAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Bombay) (b. 9th Sept., 1896).—Educ. at Bombay Univ., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1920; arrived, 18th Jan., 1921, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Bombay, Aug., 1921; doing duty as asst. judge, Apl., 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1926; offg. dep. secty. to govt., legal dept., Jan., 1928; confd., May, 1929; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; offg. secty. to govt., legal dept., Bombay, July, 1935, and again, Apl., 1936; confd. in seln. grade, Dec., 1942; addtl. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1944; judge, high ct., Aug., 1944; on duty as adjudicator, Post and Tels. dept., and rly. dispute, Apl., 1946.

RAJAGOPAL, POORAEPTI NADAMUNI, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 19th Apl., 1896).—Joined the service, 2nd March, 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Madras, Jan., 1922; currency officer, Madras, May, 1924; examr., local fund accts., Punjab, June, 1928; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Apl., 1931; currency officer, Bombay, Sept., 1931; asst. dir., army audit, northern cmd., Jan., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Nagpur, Apl., 1935, and again, March, 1937; offg. dep. dir.-genl., fin., posts and tels., June, 1936; attd. offcr., office of contr., Orissa, Nov., 1937; contr., Orissa, Jan., 1938; govt. examr. accts., M. & S.M. and S.I. rly., Apl., 1940; ch. auditor, G.I.P. rly., and govt. examr., B.B. & C.I. rly., Apl., 1941; govt. examr., M. & S.M. rly., Apl., 1944; acct.-genl., (cl. II) (prov.), Sept., 1944; acct.-genl., Bengal, Apl., 1946.

RAJAGOPAL AIYAR, CHIDAMBARA AIYAR, O.B.E., late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 11th July, 1888).—Held non-gaz. and sub-gaz. appts., Aug., 1908, to July, 1920; M.B.E., 1919; apptd. as mil. acctnt., 2nd cl., July, 1920; A.M.A.G., July, 1922; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Apl., 1925; revtd. to mil. accts. dept., Aug., 1928; promoted cl. I, Aug., 1941; O.B.E., June, 1943; ret'd., May, 1945.

RAJAGOPALA RAO, KARADIBAVI LAKSHMAN, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th Oct., 1897).—Joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921;

curr. offcr., Madras, Aug., 1933; serv. placed at disposal of Res. Bank of India, Apl., 1935; examr., loc. fund accts., Central Provs., Apl., 1938; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, July, 1938.

RAJAGOPALACHARI, SHRI CHAKRAVANTI, (b. 1879). Educn. and Arts—Member of GOVT.-Genl.'s Council, Sept., 1946.

RAJAGOPALAN, KUMMAYA MANAKADAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub.-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b., 26th Sept., 1912).—Educ. at Christian College Sch., Georgetown, Madras, and Madras Univ.; joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub.-collr. and jt. mag. Feb., 1942; agt. to govt. of India, Ceylon, June to Nov., 1943; sub.-collr., Nov., 1943.

RAJAGOPALAN, PUNDI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 29th Apl., 1901).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 15th Nov., 1924; sub.-collr., Feb., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1933; confd. July, 1936.

RAJAGOPALAM, TANJORE KRISHNA, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 10th Oct., 1885).—Retd., Oct., 1940.†

RAJAMANNAR, P. V., Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 10th May, 1901).—Apptd. advocate-genl., 10th July, 1944; puisie judge, high ct., July, 1945.

RAJAN, ARUNACHALA TYAGA, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 6th Oct., 1883).—Educ. at Amalapuram High Sch., Madras Univ., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 1st Jan., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dist. judge, July, 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922.

RAJAN, SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 10th Jan., 1911).—Educ. at American Coll., Madura, Univ. of Madras, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Aug., 1939, to Aug. 1940.

RAJAN, VISWANATHA NATA, M.Sc., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 22nd Oct., 1907).—Educ. at the Sch. of Economics and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1932; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1937; contr. of distrn., civil supplies, June, 1944.

RAJARATNAM, VAIYAVUR RAJAGOPALA, B.A., (supt.), Indian Police, Madras, (b. 8th Oct., 1902).—Promoted to Indian Police as supt., June, 1944.

RAJESHWAR DAYAL, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 12th Aug., 1909).—Educ. at Kayastha Pathahala Intermediate Coll., Allahabad, Allahabad Univ., and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 7th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1936.

RAJU, MANTHENA TIMMA, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. sec. govt. of India, dept. of lab.) (b. 5th Oct., 1911).—Educ. at P.S. High Sch., Madras, Govt. Model Second. Sch., Benares Hindu Univ., University Coll., London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936; sub.-collr.,

Aprl., 1938; dep. sec. govt. of India, dept. of lab. July, 1945; govt. delegate to int. cttee. on coal mining and Inland transp. Dec., 1945.

RAJU, VADREVV BHADIR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 28th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge (under trng.), Nov., 1937; dep. sec., govt. of Sind, Aprl., 1944.

RAJWADE, LAKSHMANRAO GANPATRAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 14th Sept., 1912).—Educ. at V.C. High Sch., Gwalior, and Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; dep. sec. govt. of Sind, Aprl., 1944; sec., govt. of India, fin. dept. Aprl., to June, 1946.

RAKHA, RAM (see Ram Rakha).

RAKSHIT, JITENDRA NATH, RAI SAHIB, F.I.C., F.C.S., (b. 15th June, 1887).—Late opium chemist, govt. of India; retd., 8th March, 1940.

RAM, AMOLAK (see Amolak Ram).

RAM, BASHI (see Bashi Ram).

RAM CHANDRA, C.I.E., M.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (fin. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st Mar., 1889).—Educ. at Central Model School, Lahore, Punjab, and Trinity College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 17th Nov., 1913, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; coln. offer., Upper Chenab canal, Nov., 1915, to Jan., 1917; M.B.E., June, 1919; temp. under-sec. to govt., Punjab, June, 1919; dep. commr. (prov.), Dec., 1925; sec. to govt., transferred depts., Punjab, Jan., 1926; dep. secty. to govt. of India, educn. dept., May, 1928; offg. jt. secty., ditto, Aug., 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; offg. secty. to the govt. of India, educn. dept., Sept., 1935; secty., fin. dept., Punjab, Jan., 1936; ch. contr. of imports, Nov., 1942; fin. commr. (rev.), Punjab, and addl. sec. rev. dept., Aprl., 1946.

RAM CHANDRA SAHAI, KUNWAR, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 19th March, 1900).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1923; apprentice engr., May, 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., July, 1927; confd., Oct., 1933.

RAM, GITA (see Gita, R.).

RAM GOPAL, C.I.E., M.Sc., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. Dec., 1894).—Joined the service, 21st March, 1921; dep. acct.-genl., United Provs., Oct., 1936; ch. auditor, E.B. rly., June, 1938; audit offer., Indian Stores Dept., Oct., 1938; ch. audr., ditto, Oct., 1939; contr. of supply accts., Sept., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1944; on spec. duty, rail-road co-ordin., Oct., 1944; dir. fin. rly. bd., Jan., 1945; Indian Aud. and Accts. serv. cl. I (confd.), Sept., 1945.

RAM, KESHO, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 17th Dec., 1914).—Educ. at Ramjas High Sch., Daryaganj, Delhi, Delhi Univ., King's Coll., London, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1940; dep. contr., rationing, May, 1944.

RAM KISHAN, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st May, 1884).—Retd., May, 1939.†

RAM LAL, DIWAN, B.A., Judl. Dept., Punjab (high ct. judge).—Apptd. offg. asst. legal remembrancer, 17th May, 1925; asst. legal

remembrancer (conveyancing), Feb., 1930; admr. genl. and offi. trustee, June, 1931; govt. advocate, Oct., 1933; offg. secty. to govt., Punjab, legisl. dept., Oct., 1936; advocate-genl., Aprl., 1937; judge, high ct., Lahore, Feb., 1938.

RAM LAL, KHANNA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th Dec., 1905).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 16th Jan., 1929; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1930; on spec. duty, irrign. bch., Aprl., 1944.

RAM LAL, LALA RAI BAHADUR, M.B.E., Dept. of Industries, Punjab (dir. of industries) (b. 18th Aug., 1888).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 2nd May, 1912; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1918; M.B.E., Jan., 1921; under-secty. to govt., rev. dept., Punjab, Jan., 1927; dir. of industries, Aug., 1930.

RAM LAL SETHI (see Sethi, R. L.).

RAM LAL, SONDHI, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. to consulting engr., p.w. bch., govt. of India) (b. 17th Oct., 1895).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Burma, 10th Feb., 1921; transferred to Punjab, July, 1929; exec. engr., Feb., 1930; asst. to consulting engr., dept. of indus. and lab., govt. of India (p.w. bch.), Jan., 1935.

RAM, MOTI (see Moti Ram).

RAM PRASAD, BURMAN, LALA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 18th Jan., 1900).—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., May, 1925; exec. engr., Aprl., 1935; on depn. to cent. P.W.D., June, 1945.

RAM PRATAP, VASISHTH Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 21st Oct., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 25th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933; under govt. of India as project offr., Nov., 1945.

RAM RAKHA, RAI BAHADUR LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 10th June, 1883).—Retd., June, 1938.*

RAM, SADHU (see Sadhu Ram).

RAM, SHRI JIGJIVAN.—Labour member of Govr.-Genl.'s council, Sept., 1946.

RAM SAHGAL, BASHI (see Bashi Ram, S.).

RAM SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th Dec., 1886).—Retd., Dec., 1941.†

RAMA AYYAR, KOLLINGODE, RANGA IYER, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 28th Feb., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Nov., 1921; asst. dir. of audit, United Provs., Aprl., 1926; dep. dir., ditto, Sept., 1926; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, June, 1929; ditto, Madras, Nov., 1932; ditto, posts and tels., Madras, May, 1935; ditto, Bombay, Aprl., 1937; dep. ch. accts. offer., N.W. rly., June, 1938; ditto, E.B. rly., Aprl., 1939; dep. ch. accts. offer., E.I. rly., Dec., 1940, again, March, 1943.

RAMA IYER, NARAYANSWAMI IYER, Indian Police (b. 20th Nov., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay on probn., Feb., 1939.

RAMA, KANT, RAI Bahadur, M.A., LL.B., (b. 16th Feb., 1894).—Joined U.P. C.S., Feb., 1918; mag. and collr. (confd.), Dec., 1945.

RAMA PAI, KRISHNA, M.A., Patents and Designs (contr.) (b. 15th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as proby. asst. examr., 3rd Sept., 1920; confd., Sept., 1922; contr., Feb., 1926.

RAMA RAU, SIR BENEGAL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (b. 10th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 17th Nov., 1913,

and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Nov., 1917; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1918; offg. under-sec. to govt., development dept., July, 1920; ditto, local self-govt. dept., Sept., 1920; confd., July, 1921; temp. dep. sec. to govt., local self-govt., Oct., 1922; offg. dep. sec. to govt., finl. dept., Apr., 1926; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., June, 1926; confd., March, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1930; on depn. to Indian Statutory Comm., 1929-30; offg. jt. secty., dept. of indus. and labour, govt. of India, Oct., 1930; jt. secty., British Indian delegation to Indian round table conf., 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; collr., Sept., 1932; secty. to British Indian delegation to jt. ctee. on Indian Constitutional Reform, May-Nov., 1933; dep. high commr. for India, Dec., 1934, to March, 1938; agent-genl. for India, S. Africa, May, 1938, to May, 1941; kt., June, 1939; chairman, Bombay port trust, Sept., 1941; on spec. duty with govt. of India, Mar., 1946.

RAMA REDDI, PATTIPATE HANIMIREDDIGARI, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (Madras) (b. 15th Feb., 1888).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, 17th Jan., 1919; vice-princl., agricl. coll., Coimbatore, June, 1928; offg. princpl., ditto, Sept., 1929; secty., Indian central cotton ctee., Dec., 1932; dep. dir. agric., Madras, Sept., 1937; dir., agric., Dec., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; retd., July, 1944.

RAMABHADRAN, TIRUVALLYANGUDI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 25th Aug., 1910).—Educ. at Hindu High Sch., Triplicane, Delhi Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1936; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1943.

RAMACHANDRA AYYAR, VENKATARAMA, Rao Bahadur, M.A., late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 13th Jan., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

RAMACHANDRAN, COMAL SUNDARAM, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 13th Dec., 1916).—Educ. at Madras Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1940, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.

RAMACHANDRAN, THIAGARAJA SADASIVA, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 12th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras, London Sch. of Economics, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1928; offg. collr. and dist. mag., June, 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apr., 1936, to Aug., 1941; on special duty, E. States Agency, Aug., 1941; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1946; spec. offr. with bd. of rev., Apr. to July, 1946.

RAMADHYANI, RANGASWAMY KALKUNTE, B.S., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Feb., 1908).—Educ. at Mysore Univ., University Coll., London, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1934; employed under cent. govt. as provl. supt. of census ops., Dec., 1939.

RAMAKRISHNA AYYAR, PERUMALAYYAR, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 5th Feb., 1908).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India,

Nov., 1932; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1936, again, Aug., 1940; under-sec. to govt. development dept., Madras, July, 1939; judl. trng., June, 1941 to Dec., 1942; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1946.

RAMAKRISHNAN, CHITTUR ANANTAKRISHNA, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 9th Nov., 1910).—Educ. at P.S. High Sch., Mylapore, Univ. of Madras, University Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934; and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1937; estate collr. Vizianagram, June, 1945; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1945.

RAMAKRISHNA, VELGAPUDI, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 4th Apr., 1896).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 10th Jan., 1922; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1923; offg. under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and sec. to govt., rev. dept., June, 1925; dewan to Maharaja of Jeypore, Nov., 1925; dir. of indus., Madras, Nov., 1931; offg. dep. sec., dept. of indus. and lab., govt. of India, June, 1935; commr. of labour, Madras, May, 1939; chairman, natl. serv. labour tribunal, Jan., 1942; Ind. advr. to govt. of Madras, July, 1945; chairman N.S. lab. tribunal and reg. dir. of resettlmt. and employment, Jan., 1946; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

RAMALINGAM, ALAMPADI SUBBARAYA, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 26th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th Feb., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; retd., March, 1944.

RAMALINGAM, PONNAMBALAM, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 1st July, 1897).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Colombo, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1921; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1922; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1931, and again, March, 1933; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; dist. and sess. judge (confd.), July, 1934; served temp. with govt. of Orissa, Apr., 1936, to Dec., 1936; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1941.

RAMAMURTI, SIR SONTI VENKATA, K.C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S.
