(advr. to govr., Madras) (b. 1st Aug., 1888).—Educ. at London Mission High Sch., Vizagapatam, Madras Univ., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Apr., 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. sec. to govt. dev. dept., July, 1929; dir. of agric., Madras, Feb., 1932, to June, 1935; commr. of lab., July, 1938; member, bd. of rev., May, 1939; ch. sec., Nov., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; advr. to govt., var. from Aug., 1942, to July, 1944; served with central govt. from July, 1944. C.I.E., Jan., 1942; K.C.I.E., Jun., 1945; depn. to U.K. and U.S.A., Feb. to Mar., 1946; advr. to govt., Apr., 1946.

RAMAN, AGNESWARA VENKATA, late Public Works Dept., Madras (b. 12th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service as offg. asst. sany. engr., 22nd Jan., 1921; confd., March, 1922; dep. sany. engr., Apr., 1927; retd., March, 1944.

RAMAN, A.V., RAO Sahib (b. 22nd Jan., 1898).—Joined the service 20th Apr., 1922; supt. civ. def. dept., Sept., 1944; asst. sec. home dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1946.

RAMAN, KODAGANALLUR SRINIVASA VENKATA, India C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa)

(b. 15th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 7th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., govt. of Orissa, home and rev. dep., March, 1937; dep. dir.-genl., finance, posts and tels., and officio asst. finance offcr., communications dept., Api., 1939; offcr. on spl. duty, fin. dept., Aug., 1940; asst. finl. alvr., mily. fin., Oct., 1940; dep. ditto, Apl., 1941; sec. supply and P.C. dept. and ch. contr. prices and supplies, Bihar, Sept., 1945.

RAMAN, KRISHNASWAMI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (b. 28th Oct., 1911).—Educ. at Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936 and served in Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., govt. of Orissa, home and fin. depts., Apl., 1940, to May, 1942; transf. to Bihar; addl. dep. commr. May, 1942; dist. mag. and collr. Apl. 1943; dep. sec. supply and price contr. dept. and cloth contr. Feb., 1946.

RAMANAN, COIMBATORE SUBRAHMANYA VENKATA, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Madras (sup.).—First commn., 11th Jan., 1921; in milty. employ to 22nd June, 1931; jail supt., Madras, July, 1933; supt. cent. jail, Vizagapatam, (confd.), Oct., 1931; supt. cent. jail, Cannanore, Oct., 1936; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1937.

RAMANATHAN, VENKATARAMA, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (dep. commr. and dist. mag., Orissa) (b. 4th Sept., 1911).—Educ. at Board High School, Namakal, University of Madras, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935; and served in B. and O. as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. fin. dept., Bihar, March, 1938 to May, 1940; dep. sec. Orissa, home fin. dept., Apl., 1943; dep. commr. and dist. mag. Jan., 1945.

RAMAPPA, UCHIL, B.A., B.L., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 6th July, 1882).—Retd., 1940.†

RAMASOMAJULU, Rao Sahib, Bulusu, A.M.I.Mech.E., A.M.I.E.E. (Elec. Dept., Madras) (b. 8th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. engr. P.W.D., Oct., 1929; confd. as exec. engr., elec. dept., July, 1937.

RAMASUNDARAM, UNJALUR SUBRAHMANYA AYYAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 28th Dec., 1901).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 2nd July, 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1925; offg. exec. engr., May, 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; confd., Oct., 1934; served under central p.w.d. from June, 1941 to Dec., 1945; on other duty in highways dept., Apl., 1946.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, RANGASWAMI IYER, B.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 24th Aug., 1920; dep. govt. examr. of accts., S.I. rly., Apl., 1936; dep. acctnt.-genl., P. and T., Nagpur, Oct., 1937; ditto, Madras, March, 1939; dep. ch. accts. offr. G.I.P. rly. June, 1940; offg. var., Dec., 1940 to May, 1945; Indian aud. and accts. serv. cl. I (prov.) Sept., 1944; dir. of fin., rly. bd. 1946.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, BATLAGUNDU SITARAMA, Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 18th Apl., 1889).—Retd., Apl., 1944.†

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, SIR CHETPAT PATTABHERAMA, K.C.I.E.—Apptd. advocate-genl., Madras, Jan., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; member of govr.'s exec. council, Madras, Feb.,

1923, to Apl., 1928; K.C.I.E., June, 1925; on depn. to League of Nations from 8th Aug. to 9th Oct., 1926; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; temp. member of govr.-genl.'s council, Aug., 1931; and again, June, 1932; delegate of India to monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; member, govt. of India cttee. on secretariat procedure, 1935-1936; member of govr.-genl.'s exec. council, July, 1942, to Aug., 1942.

RAMASWAMI, NAIDU, M., B.A., Ph.D.—Apptd. dir. of fisheries, Sind, Aug., 1944.

RAMASWAMI, PALAMANERI NARAYANASWAMI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 17th May, 1900).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924; sub-collr., Feb., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; confd., Sept., 1937.

RAMASWAMI MUDALIAR, Arcot (see Mudaliar, Sir Ramaswami).

RAMASWAMI, VAIDYANATHIER, M.Sc. (Allbd.) Indian C.S. (dist. and sess., judge, Bihar) (b. 30th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras, Univ. of Allahabad, Imperial Coll., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 29th Nov., 1929, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; actg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936; confd., Oct., 1939.

RAMAYYAR, M.S. Indian Audit and Accts. Service (b. 29th Nov., 1899).—Joined the serv., 17th July, 1920; asst. fincl. advr. mil. fin., May, 1943.

RAMCHANDANI, RANJIT SINGH V., Indian Rlys. Accts. Service (b. 19th June, 1888).—Held non-gaz. appts. from June, 1911; confd. as asst. contr. of stores, E.I. rly., June, 1926; confd. as asst. audit offcr., May 1927; asst. ditto, A.G. rlys., Aug., 1927; transf. to Dehra Dun Coll., Nov., 1929; accts. offcr., gr. I (provl.), July, 1933; on special duty, rly. bd., Nov., 1937; depy. contr., rly. accts., March, 1938; depy. chief accts. offcr., E.I. rly., July, 1938; dep. contr. of rly. accts., Feb., 1939; dep. ch. accts. offr., Sept., 1939 to 1946, on i.p.r.

RAMESAM, SIR VEPA, Kt., B.A., B.L., late Madras Judicial Service (b. 27th July, 1875).—Retd., July, 1935.*

RAMESHWAR, SAHAI, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., U.P.) (b. 13th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 11th Nov., 1929; offg. dist. forest offcr., Aug., 1933; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934; transf. to Orissa, Apl., 1936; ditto, Bihar, Jan., 1939; ditto, U.P., Jan., 1940.

RAMPLING, HAROLD, late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 18th Apl., 1895).—Retd., March, 1940.†

RAMSAY, NORMAN, M.B.E., Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 11th Nov., 1907).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1927; in milty. employ prior to 20th Nov., 1933; 2nd-in-command and adjt., Kolhapur Inf., June, 1933; p.a. to resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1933; under training in the Punjab, July, 1934; under-secty. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Apl., 1935; again under training in the Punjab, Jan., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Punjab hill states, Oct., 1936; under secty. to the resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; asst. pol. agent, Punjab hill states, Nov., 1938; under-secty., pol. dept., Sept., 1940; M.B.E., Jan., 1942; dep. secty. (war) pol. dept., Feb., 1943; on foreign service

as Wazer-i-Azam. Kalat states, Nov., 1943; sec. to resdt. of Mysore, May, 1946.

RAMUNNI MENON, P. K., B.A., B.L., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 27th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

RAMSDEN, GEOFFREY CHARLES FRESCHEVILLE, C.I.E., M.A. (Cantab.) Indian C.S. (commr., Central Provs.) (b. 21st Apl., 1893).—Educ. at Haileybury and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Sept., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Nov., 1923; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; confd., July, 1927; excise commr., and supt. of stamps, Jan., 1929; offg. commr., Feb., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1942; commr. (confd.), Nov., 1945.

RAMZAN, ALI, Khan Bahadur, late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 14th Aug., 1883).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

RANA LOKE SHAMSHER JANG, late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 4th Oct., 1887).—Retd., July, 1940.†

RANA, UDAYASINHJI HARMIRSINHJI, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 3rd Nov., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 20th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1932; confd., July, 1936; ; exec. offcr., R.T.A., Sept., 1944.

RANADE, DATTATRAYA DAMODAR, Rai Bahadur, late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Retd., March, 1938.*

RANCE, Maj-Gen. SIR HUBERT ELVIN, G.B.E., C.B.—Apptd. Govt. of Burma, Aug., 1946.

RANCE, HUGH DUNSTAN HOLWELL, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 19th May, 1912).—Apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., Nov., 1936; confd., May, 1944; asst. for Mekran to pol. agt. in Kalat, Sept., 1944; asst. pol. agt., Bahrain, 1946.

RANDHAWA, MOHINDAR SINGH, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (sec. Imp. Council of Agric. Research) (b. 2nd Feb., 1909).—Educated at Khalsa High Sch., Huktsar, Univ. of the Punjab and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; services placed at the disposal of govt. of India, educn., health and lands, Apl., 1945.

RANDLE, HERBERT NIEL, M.A. (Oxon.), D.Phil., India Office (librarian) late Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service, 31st July, 1905, and officiated as prof. of philosophy, Queen's Coll., Benares; inspr. of schools, Apl., 1906; profr., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, Nov., 1906; ditto, Queen's Coll., Benares, Apl., 1909; ditto, Muir Central Coll., Sept., 1918; actg. prinpl., Queen's Coll., Apl., 1919; Allahabad Univ., from July, 1925; asst. librarian, India Office, Sept., 1927; librarian, Sept. 1933, to Sept., 1945; ret. Sept., 1945; and re-employed.

RANDOLPH, REV. ROY BEVERLEY, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 5th Jan., 1909).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 11th Jan., 1944.

RANDOLPH, REV. THOMAS BERKELEY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 15th March, 1904).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bengal; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1934.

RANG BEHARI LAL, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 19th Mar., 1889).—Acct.-genl., United Provs., Aug., 1941.†

RANGACHARI, M.V., Indian audit and

accts. service,—apptd. dep. sec., govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1945.

RANGANATHAN, CONDRAMANICKAM RAGHUNATHA, M.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 15th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927; dir. forest coll., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1938; princpl Madras Forest Coll., Coimbatore, Oct., 1945.

RANGANATHAN, MAYAVARAM KRISHNASWAMI, Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.E., A.M.I.C.E., Ind. Serv. of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Madras) (b. 16th Sept., 1892).—Joined the serv. as appr. engr. on 10th June, 1916; asst. engr., June, 1917; asst. exec. engr., July, 1922; exec. engr., June, 1926. suptg. engr., June, 1943.

RANGANATHAN, SIR SAMUEL E. (see Runganathan, Sir S. E.).

RANGANATHAN, SAURIRAJAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 12th Nov., 1908).—Educated at Madras Univ., Sch. of Oriental Studies, London, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Feb., 1935; asst. settlement offcr., Nov., 1936; collr. and dep. mag., Apl., 1939; apptd. to fin. and comm., provl., Oct., 1939; under-secty., fin. dep., Nov., 1939; dep. ditto, March, 1942; dep. dir. of inspn. customs, Nov., 1942; sec., cent. bd. of rev., Dec., 1943.

RANGANATHAN, SUBRAHMANYA, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 2nd Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived 1st Jan., 1918; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1919; sub-collr., Dec., 1922; emigration agent, Ceylon, Sept., 1923; offg. dep. secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Apl., 1928; O.B.E., Jan., 1930; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1930; ditto (provl. subs.), Dec., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935; inspr., mun. councils etc., Madras, Oct., 1938; collr., Apl., 1941 to Feb., 1945; bd. of rev. Mar., 1945.

RANGASWAMI AYYANGAR, GIYARPURAM NADATHUR late Indian Agricultural Service (Madras) (b. 19th May, 1887).—Retd., May, 1942.†

RANGASWAMI AYYANGAR, S., B.A., B.L., Judl. dept., Madras (b. 21st July, 1889).—Apptd. administrator-genl. and offi. trustee, 16th July, 1929.

RANGNEKAR, SIR SAJBA SHANKAR, B.A., LL.B., Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 20th Dec., 1878).—Retd., Dec., 1938.*

RANJIT RAI, Rai Sahib, LALA, B.A., LL.B., (dep. registrar, highct. Lahore) (b. 30th Jan., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dep. regr., 5th May, 1930; asst. ditto, June, 1933; dep. ditto, April, 1941; Rai Sahib, 1945.

RANKIN, R. W. R., Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st May, 1894).—Joined, 17th Feb., 1922; dep. ch. engr., M. and S.M. Rly., Dec., 1945.

RANSFORD, SIR ALISTAIR JOHN, Col., C.I.E., late R.E. (mint master, Bombay) (b. 5th Jan., 1895).—In military employ up to Jan., 1924; dep. mint master, Feb., 1926; offg. mint master, May, 1927; again, May, 1929; confd., Sept., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1936; kt. Jan., 1946.

RAO, BELTHANGADI GOPALAKRISHNA (see Gopalakrishna, R. B.).

RAO, CHIKBALLAPUR VENKATA SRINIVASA (see Srinivasa Rao, C.V.).

RAO, CHIRRAVOORI BALKRISHNA (see Chirra-voori, B. Rao).

RAO, DHANVADA ANANDA (see Ananda Rao D.).

RAO, EKANATHA RAJARAM (see Ekanatha Rajaram Rao).

RAO, GHANTA VENKATA (see Venkata Rao, G.).

RAO, KASARAGOD PATANASHETTI LAKSHMANA (see Lakshmana Rao, K.P.).

RAO, KAIWAR SETHU (see Kaiwar, S.R.).

RAO, KRISHNASWAMI ANANDA (see Ananda Rao K.).

RAO, KRISHNA KARNAD (see Krishna Rao).

RAO, MUTTA VENKATASUBBA (see Venkatasubba Rao).

RAO, MYSORE SUBBA (see Mysore Subba Rao).

RAO, NANDALIKE DEVARAO KRISHNA (see Krishna Rao N. D.).

RAO, NEELAMANGALA KESHAVA (see Keshava Rao, N.).

RAO, PEJAVAR MOHAN (see Mohan Rao, P.).

RAO, BENEGAL SANJIVA (see Sanjiva Rao, B.).

RAO, P. H. SESHAGIRI (see Seshagiri Rao.).

RAO, S. RAGHUNATHA (see Raghunatha Rao, S.).

RAO, VALLURI KAMESWARA (see Valluri K. Rao.).

RAO, VYAKARANA NARAHARI (see Narahar Rao, V.).

RAO, V. SURYAPRAKASA (see Suryaprakasa Rao, V.).

RAPER, SIR JOHN HUGH FRANCIS, Indian State Rlys. (member, rly. bd.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 28th June, 1912; asst. traffic supt., June, 1915; dist. ditto, March, 1920; asst. transpn. supt., Nov., 1922; asst. traffic mang., Nov., 1926; offg. dep. ditto, Nov., 1926; confd., July, 1929; offg. ch. traffic mang., June, 1931; and again, Jan., 1933; confd., March, 1933; offg. agent, G.I.P., rly. May, 1936; memb. rly. bd., May, 1939 to Feb., 1945; kt. June, 1942.

RASHEED KHAN, Abdul, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 28th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Lucknow Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

RASHID, ABDUL (see Abdul, R.).

RASHID, MIAN ABDUL, Khan Bahadur, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. traffic commcl. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 3rd May, 1894).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1919; provl. in senr. scale, Aug., 1937; on depn. to Gaekwar, Baroda state rly., from Apl., 1935; confd. in senior scale, Dec., 1937.

RASHID-UZ-ZAMAN, SAIVID, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 28th March, 1895).—Educ. at Patna Coll., Presidency Coll., Calcutta, and London Univ.; joined the service, 14th Dec., 1921; arrived, 19th Jan., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag., and collr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., Aug., 1925; confd., Jan., 1926; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1929; offg. addl. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Apl., 1933; secty., public dept., office of high commr. for India, March, 1934; actg. dep. trade commr., June, 1934; adviser to govt. of India delegation to internatl. labour conf., Geneva, June, 1935; reverted to Bihar as mag. and collr., Nov., 1937; dep. sec. to govt. of India (supplies), July, 1940; jt. sec., commerce dept., govt. of India, March, 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; on depn. to Copenhagen, Nov., to Dec., 1945; on depn. to U.K., May, to July, 1946.

RATCLIFF, GEORGE MASSEY, B.A., Indian

C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 8th Aug., 1906).—Educ. at King Edward VII Sch., Lytham, Lancs., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1933; addtl. dist. mag., Oct., 1939; addtl. dist. judge, Feb., 1940; sec. legisl. dept., Bengal, Jan., 1940.

RATHBONE, PATRICK WARRE, Burma C.S. (CI. I) (dep. commr.) (b. 17th Dec., 1917).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 5th Oct., 1939; joined service, 23rd Aug., 1940; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1940, and served as asst. commr.; on mil. serv., 1941; civ. aff. serv. Sept., 1944; dep. commr., 1945.

RATNAM, S., Indian audit and accts. serv.,—Apptd. dep. sec. govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1945.

RATNASWAMI, M., C.I.E., Barr. (member, Madras services commn.) (b. 15th Aug., 1885).—Apptd. offg. princpl., law coll., Madras, 3rd Jan., 1928; confd., June, 1928; member, Madras services commn., Jan., 1930; offg. chairman, ditto, June, 1935.

RAU, BANTWAL GOPALAKRISHNA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 10th Nov., 1912).—Educ. at Carana High Sch., Mangalore, Madras Univ., City and Guilds (Engineering) Coll., London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

RAU, SIR BENEGAL NARSINGA, C.I.E., B.A. (Camb.), late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 26th Feb., 1887).—Educ. at Canara High Sch., Mangalore, Madras and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1916; ditto, Assam, May, 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1922; offg. sec., legisl. dept., supt. and membr., legal affairs, March, 1925; confd., Dec., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; addl. jt. secy., legisl. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1934; offg. jt. secty., ditto, Jan., 1935; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, May, 1935; on spec. duty, reforms office, govt. of India, Sept., 1935; on depn. to India Office, Oct.-Dec., 1936; knighted, June, 1938; judge, high ct., Bengal, Dec., 1938; confd., 1938; judge, high ct., Bengal, Dec., 1938; confd., Jan., 1939; chairman Hindu law cttee., 1940-41; chairman, Indus. Waters Commn., 1941-42; retd., Feb., 1944; prime minister, Jammu and Kashmir, 1944-45; on spec. duty in the govr.-genl's sectte. (reforms) Nov., 1945.

RAU, B. RAMA (see Rama Rau, Benegal).

RAU, KALLE RAMA RAU SADASHIVA (see Sadashiva Rau, K. R. R.).

RAU, P. K., Indian audit and accts. service.—Apptd. dep. sec., govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1945.

RAU, PENDYALA SATIANARAINA, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (advr. to Govr. Central Provs.) (b. 15th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918; served in Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 27th Jan., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1924; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. commr. of settlements and dir. of land records, etc., C.P., Feb., 1936; offg. finl. secty. to govt., C.P., March, 1936 to March, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; on denn. to govt. of India (food), Dec.,

1942 to May, 1943; chairman, indus. cttee., 1944; advr. to govr., Dec., 1945.

RAW, WILLIAM GEOFFREY, Capt. I. A., Ind. Polit. Service (b. 6th Oct., 1910).—Apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., Nov., 1936; confd., Jan., 1942; consul Bushire, Oct., 1944; city mag., Peshawar, Dec., 1944; asst. commr., Hangu, Aug., 1945; home sec. to govt. N.W.F.P., Oct., 1945.

RAWLEY, RATAN CHAND, M.A., M.Sc., D.Sc. (Lond.), late Dept. of Industries, Punjab.—Apptd. dir. of industries, 7th Nov., 1927; adviser and secty. to the Indian delegation to the internatl. labour conf., Geneva, May–June, 1928.

RAY (see also Roy).

RAY, ANNADA SANKAR, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 14th March, 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Patna and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Aug. 1936; ; addl. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1939.

RAY, SIR ASOKE KUMAR (see Roy, Sir A.K.).

RAY, BIMALENDU, B.A., Indian Police (supt.) Bihar, (b. 11th Oct., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt. Bihar and Orissa, 15th Apl., 1926; offg. supt., Oct., 1936; confd., Aug., 1938; supt., C.I.D., Mar., 1945.

RAY, GERALD GEORGE, O.B.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl., Sind) (b. 14th June, 1895).—joined the service, 6th Dec., 1915, as asst. supt.; on mil. duty from 20th June, 1917 to 17th Feb., 1919; offg. supt., Apl., 1922, Bombay; confd., June, 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; confd., supt., selection grade, Nov., 1942 O.B.E., June, 1943; inspr.-gen. (confd.), Sind, Dec., 1943.

RAY, GORDON MEREDITH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 2nd Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Caterham Sch. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Dec., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dpy. collr., Dec., 1937; supt., Bhagpal garh, Oct., 1942; group testing offr., civil seln. bd., govt. of India, Oct., 1945.

RAY, JAMINI PRASANNA, Rai Bahadur, M.A., Bengal C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1892).—Joined the service, Dec., 1916; dep. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1918; mag. and collr., Feb., 1937; jt. sec., agric. dept.,

RAY, JITENDRA NATH, Rai Bahadur, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd Aug., 1892).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1917; dep. supt., Apl., 1928; apptd. to I.P. as supt., June, 1934; Rai Bahadur, June, 1939; awarded King's police medal, June, 1946.

RAY, KARUNAMAY, B.A., late Indian Police, (b. 10th Nov., 1885).—Retd., Nov., 1940.†

RAY, KIRAN CHANDRA, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 27th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 2nd Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

RAY, KUMUD BHUSON, late Indian State Rlys. (E.B. Rly.) (b. 30th Jan., 1891).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 15th Jan., 1915; asst. engr., Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1925; exec. engr., July, 1937; dep. ch. engr., Jan., 1942 to 1945.

RAY, LALIT KUMAR, L.M.S. (Punjab), D.P.H. (Cantab.) Public Health Dept., United Provs.

(asst. dir. of public health) (b. Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as health offcr., 1st March, 1914; asst. dir. of public health, Nov. 15th, 1934.

RAY, LEWIS JAMES, Burma Front. Serv., (b. 10th Dec., 1915).—Apptd. on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

RAY, NAGENDRA NATH, late Bihar C.S. (b. 1st July, 1885).—Retd., June, 1940.†

RAY, NIKUNJA KISHORE, M.Sc., Indian Police, Bihar (b. 10th Feb., 1920).—Apptd. asst. supt., on probn., after exam. of 1941.

RAY, NIKUNJA KISHORE, Indian Police, Bihar and Orissa (b. 10th Feb., 1920); Asst. supt., on probn., 24th Feb., 1942; confd., Feb., 1944.

RAY, PHILIP BICKNELL, Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 10th July, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., on probn., Oct., 1939.

RAY, PRABHAT CHANDRA, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs (Bengal) (b. 23rd Feb., 1888).—Joined the service, 15th Feb., 1913; asst. engr., Feb., 1914; exec. eng., June, 1926; retd., Feb., 1945.

RAY, RAJESVAR PRASHAD, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 15th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 20th June, 1920; transferred to I.P. as supt., May, 1934.

RAY, RANAJII KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1944; addl. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1939; mag. and collr., Apl., 1941; dep. dir., distrbn., spec. offcr., rice scheme; offg. dep. commr., Jalpaiguri, Mar., 1946.

RAY, REGINALD EDWARD ANTHONY, C.I.E., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 15th May, 1891).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 16th Dec., 1910; confd., June, 1912; addl. supt., Nov., 1924; supt., Aug., 1925; awarded Indian police medal, 1933; dep. inspr. genl., var. from 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; commr. of police, Calcutta, Oct., 1943.

RAY, SAILENDRA NATH GUHA (see Guha Ray, S. N.).

RAY, SANAT KUMAR, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Orissa) (b. 6th Sept., 1887).—Retd., 1941.†

RAY, SATISH CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th May, 1888).—Apptd. inspr. of schools, Assam, 12th Nov., 1923; offg. princpl. Cotton Coll., Ganhati, variously from July, 1933; confd., May, 1939; princpl., Murarichand Coll., Sylhet, June, 1940.

RAY, SATYENDRA NATH, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 25th June, 1902).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd Apl., 1925; arrived, 4th May, 1925, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1934; and again, Feb., 1936; confd., March, 1937; dep. secty., fin. dept., Bengal, Oct., 1937; mag. and collr. (confd.), Oct., 1938; import trade contr., Bengal and Orissa, May, 1940; jt. sec., commerce dept. govt. of India, March, 1943; finl. advr., civil supplies dept., Dec., 1943.

RAY CHAUDHURI, NIRMALKANTI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dpy. collr., Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India,

Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag., and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931; addtl. dist. judge, from June, 1937.

RAYNEAU, CYRIL ALEXANDER, Indian State Rlys. (coal supt.) (b. 8th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. coal supt., 1st May, 1916; offg. coal supt., Apl., 1928; confd., Apl., 1930.

RAYNER, ADRIEN BOOKER, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service, Oct., 1919, as asst. engr.; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. engr. (perm.), May, 1940.

RAYNER, WILLIAM RUFFELL, India Office (admin. officr. for statistical questions, economic and overseas dept.) (b. 16th March, 1890).—Educ. at King Charles' Sch., Tunbridge Wells; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 25th Jan., 1909, and attached to acctnt.-genl's dept.; transfed. to pol. dept., March, 1912; services lent to Colonial Office from March, 1921 to Jan., 1922; transfed. to judl. and public dept., Jan., 1922; higher clerical officer, Apl., 1922; dep. reading clerk to Council, Feb., 1923; asst. princpl., Aug., 1924; jt. editor, India Office list, Oct., 1932 to May, 1934; admin. officr. for statistical questions, economic and overseas dept., May, 1934.

RAYNES, CHARLES HENRY, M.C., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Burma) (b. 10th Aug., 1898).—Joined the serv. as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 19th Feb., 1921; offg. dist. supt., May, 1927; confd., May, 1933; crimes asst. to D.I.G. Rlys., and C.I.D., June, 1937; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; dep. inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Oct., 1945.

RAYNOR, ERIC WALTER, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 20th Sept., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 12th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Sept., 1925.

RAYNOR, FRANK HENRY CHARLES, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 18th Jan., 1902).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1921; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Oct., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1941.

RAZA, AGHA GHULAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 4th July, 1917); apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. colonization offr., Dec., 1945.

RAZA ALI, SYED, SIR, Kt., C.B.E., B.A., LL.B. (b. 29th Apl., 1882).—Member, public services commn., Oct., 1926, to Nov., 1931; agent-genl. for govt. of India in S. Africa, Jan., 1935.*

RAZA, SAIVID HASHIM (see Hashim Raza, S.).
READ, JAMES late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Jan., 1898).—Retd., 10th Feb., 1940.†

REBELLO, ALBIN RICHARD, O.B.E., F.S.S., F.R.E.S., late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 3rd Apl., 1886).—Joined the service, 25th March, 1908; supt., fin. dept., March, 1922; asst. secty., taxation enquiry cttee., Nov., 1925; asst. acct. genl., Punjab, March, 1927; asst. dir., army audit, May, 1927; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, March, 1933; ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Nov., 1935; on foreign service under Govt. of Burma, Apl., 1937; re-transferred to India, Oct., 1938; under Jodhpur Durbar, Nov., 1938; retd. Nov., 1941.

REDCLIFT, STANLEY WHITLOCK, M.B.E., (b. 9th Oct., 1887).—Apptd. electric adviser and electric inspr., Bengal and Assam (on probn.), 1st July, 1926; confd., July, 1928; electrical

advr. and ch. electrical inspr., Bengal, Dec., 1928; M.B.E., Jan., 1941; retd., Oct., 1943.

REDDI, OBULREDDIGARI PULLA (see Pulla Reddi, O.).

REDDI, P. H. RAMA (see Rama Reddi, P. H.).

REDHA, KANTA SARAN, Income-tax Dept., Bihar and Orissa (asst. commr.) (b. 29th June, 1886).—Asst. commr., Sept., 1921.

REDLEY, WILLIAM GORDON, Burma Civil Service (cl. 1) (b. 6th Jan., 1916).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

REDPATH, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 4th Sept., 1909).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1929; pers. asst. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Nov., 1934; under trng. in Punjab, July, 1935; under-secty. to the resdt. for C. India and dep. opium agent, Malwa Apl., 1937; confd., March., 1938; asst. pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1939; sec. to resdt., E. States, Apl., 1944.

REED, WILLIAM HENRY GRIST, M.A., M.R.C.S., (Eng.) Maj. I.M.S., (b. 29th June, 1906).—First commn., 2nd Aug., 1932; in mil. employ. to Jan., 1940; reserve duty to Coimbatore, Feb., 1940; mil duty May, 1940 to June, 1946; dist. med. offr. N. Arcot, July, 1946.

REES, STANLEY VERNON LLOYD, late Indian Police (Assam) (b. 1st May, 1907).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1926; in training to May, 1928; proby. asst. supt., May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1928; offg. supt., rly. police, March, 1935; spec. duty, Shillong, Nov., 1939, on l.p.r.

REGE, DATTATRAYA VAMAN, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 18th Sept., 1897).—Educ. at Bombay Univ., and Cambridge (non-collegiate); joined the service, 14th Nov., 1921; arrived, 7th Jan., 1922, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1925; and again, May, 1931; confd. (provl.), July, 1934; confd., May, 1936; regr. co-operative societies and dir. of industries Apl., 1939; chrmn., lab. investgn. cttee., labour dept., govt. of Ind., Jan., 1944.

REGE, SHANTARAM BALKRISHNA, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 24th Oct., 1890).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., 30th March, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., March, 1925.

REHMAN, MUHAMMAD BAZLUR, M.A., Ph.D., Bombay Educl. Service (princpl., Ismail Coll.) (b. 11th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service as princpl., Ismail Coll., Andhari, on probn., 7th Apl., 1930; confd., Apl., 1932.

REID, ARTHUR BEATSON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 16th March, 1888).—Retd., Aug., 1939.*

REID, DOUGLAS FERNIE PATON, Indian Police (b. 4th March, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 15th Oct., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1930; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., W.I. States Agency, March, 1932; supt., Sept., 1935; comdt., Aden, July, 1936; under Col. govt., Aden, Apl., 1937; principal, police training school, Nasik, June, 1939 to Apl., 1943; Indian police medal, Jan., 1942; served in Sind from June, 1943; asst. to inspr.-gen. police, Bombay, Oct., 1944; dist. supt., Apl., 1946, on l.p.r.

REID, JAMES EDWARD, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-gen., Assam) (b. 5th Oct., 1894).—Joined the serv. as asst. supt., 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., Nov., 1927; confd. June, 1928; pers. asst. to inspr.-genl., Sept., 1929 to Nov., 1933; dep. inspr. genl., Nov., 1930; dir. civil def. Assam, March, 1942; dep. inspr.-genl., Feb.,

1945; ditto, admin., Nov., 1945; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

REID, REV. JAMES POTTER, M.A., K.-i.H., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 8th July, 1885).—Retd., Dec., 1940.

REID, JOHN STUART, O.B.E., I.S.E. (suptg. engr., Bombay). (b. 30th Aug., 1904).—Joined as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1927; confd. exec. engr., Oct., 1936; dep. dir., cent. irrgn. research sta., Dacca, Dec., 1941 to Jan., 1944; suptg. engr., Apl. 1945; O.B.E., June, 1945.

REID, SIR ROBERT NEIL, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th July, 1883).—Retd., 1st Nov., 1943.†

REILLY, SIR BERNARD RAWDON, K.C.M.G., C.I.E., O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 25th March, 1882).—Retd., March, 1937.*

REILLY, SIR HENRY D'ARCY CORNELIUS, late Indian C.S. (b. 15th Jan., 1876).—Retd., July, 1934.†

REMINGTON, JAMES BOILEAU, V.D., late Indian State Railways (b. 12th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1910; asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1913; dist. ditto, June, 1917; asst. transpn. supt. Nov., 1922; offg. divl. trans. supt., Jan., 1925; confd. Apl., 1926; offg. ch. transpn. supt. May, 1933; confd. May, 1942; retd., Feb., 1946.

RENNICK, DENIS KINGSTON, M.B.E., late Survey of India (b. 25th Apl., 1884).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

RENNIE, REV. JOHN YULE, M.A., B.D., B.Litt., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Bombay) (b. 10th Dec., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probn. 1st Dec., 1920; junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Dec., 1922; senr. ditto, Dec., 1932; presdy. senr. ditto, Oct., 1937; confd. March, 1929; retd., Apl., 1946.

RENNISON, REV. ERIC DAVID ROBERT, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 7th Oct., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 14th Oct., 1922; junior chaplain, March, 1923; senr., ditto, March, 1929.

RENWICK, HENRY PERCIVAL, late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 6th January, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., Nizam's G.S. Rly., 3rd Aug., 1912; dist. loco. and carr. supt., June, 1918; dist. loco. supt., G.I.P. Rly., Aug., 1920; asst. trans. supt., Dec., 1922; supt., Feb., 1929; offg. divl. transpn. supt., March, 1933; offg. dep. transpn. supt., Oct., 1933; confd., Oct., 1936; ch. mech. engr., provl., Aug., 1940; retd., Jan., 1946.

REUBEN, DAVID EZRA, Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Patna) (b. 3rd Sept., 1893).—Educ. at Bombay and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918; arrived in India, 23rd Jan., 1919, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1925; offg. settlement officer, Feb., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1928; confd., Oct., 1932; sec. to govt., judl. dept., Apl., 1938; addtl. judge, Patna ct, Aug., 1943; judge, ditto, March, 1944; chrnm., mica enquiry cttee., Nov., 1944.

REYNOLDS, FRANK ARMSTRONG ABBOTT, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 30th May, 1913).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Burma, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; supt., Jan., 1936.

REYNOLDS, HENRY FORSYTH, late Indian Police (b. 11th Nov., 1889).—Retd., 11th May, 1941.†

REYNOLDS, HENRY OSBORNE, C.M.G.,

B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 11th Oct., 1883).—Retd., Nov., 1942.†

RHIND, DONALD, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (economic botanist, Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service as mycologist, Burma, 21st March, 1923; economic botanist, Dec., 1927.

RHODES, SIR CAMPBELL WARD, K.C.I.E., C.B.E. (b. 17th Jan., 1874).—Member of cl. of India, 12th May, 1925 to May, 1935.†

RHODES, MALCOLM CROSSLEY, Burma Serv. of Engrs. (b. 28th Feb., 1905).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr. P.W.D., Burma, Feb., 1929; confd. Feb., 1932; exec. engr., Feb., 1938; on mil. serv., July, 1945 to Jan., 1946.

RIAZ AHMAD, MIAN, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (b. 24th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 22nd Jan., 1929; p.a. to ch. commr., Oct., 1941.

RIAZUL, HASAN, B.A., LL.B., (b. 1st Mar., 1892).—Joined the U.P. judl. service, 25th June, 1921; dist. and sess. judge (confd.), Jan., 1946.

RICE, SIR EDWARD ARTHUR BEACKTON, K.B.E., C.B., M.C., Air Vice Marshal, R.A.F. (retd.) Civil Aviation Directorate (b. 19th Dec., 1893).—Apptd. dep. dir., genl. (aircraft ops.) 11th Feb., 1946; K.B.E., Jan., 1946; C.B., Jan., 1944; C.B.E., Jan., 1941; M.C., June, 1917.

RICE, JOHN LESLIE, Burma Civil Service (cl. I) (b. 6th July, 1916).—Educ. at Oundle. Apptd. on probn. after seln. in Aug., 1946.

RICH, WILLIAM HAROLD ADRIAN, Indian Police. —Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab 20th Nov., 1928; asst. dir. intell. govt. of India, Oct., 1939; on depn. with def. dept., June, 1940; supt., Oct., 1941; asst. D.I.G., Punjab, 1944; again supt., Apl., 1945.

RICHARDS, CECIL JOHN, Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 8th March, 1894).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Aug., 1920; arrived, 5th June, 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. settl. officer, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1927; confd., Apl., 1931; civ. aff. serv. Sept., 1943; on spec. duty in ch. sec.'s off. (Simla) Nov., 1943 to June, 1944; civ. aff. serv. (Delhi), Sept., 1944; dep. commr., Oct., 1945; commr. (confd.) July, 1946.

RICHARDS, REV. GEORGE HENRY, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 24th Sept., 1892).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 26th March, 1930

RICHARDS, PHILIP BERNARD, A.R.C.S., F.E.S., Indian Agric. Service (entomologist to govt., United Provs.) (b. 31st May, 1886).—Joined the service as entomologist to govt. on 15th March, 1921; offg. dir. of agric., United Provs., May, 1934, and again, May, 1936.

RICHARDSON, ALAN, late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 9th Nov., 1882).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

RICHARDSON, CHARLES RICHARD SUGDEN, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Oct., 1889).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

RICHARDSON, FRANCIS ALBERT ILFORD, Impl. Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 26th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service, 17th March, 1921; asst. collr. March, 1923; collr. (confd.) May, 1945.

RICHARDSON, HUGH EDWARD, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. Indian Pol. Service (b. 22nd Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Glenalmond and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served

in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1932; offg. asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1934; British trade agent, Gyantse, July, 1936; pol. offcr., Sikkim, in addn., from May, 1937 to Nov., 1937; asst. commr., Charsadda, June, 1940; confd. in Ind. Pol. Serv., Sept., 1940; dy. commr., Hazara, Nov., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; jt. sec. to govt. of India, external dept., Dec., 1944; and again, Nov., 1945; Br. Trade Agt., Gyantse, Jan., 1946.

RICHARDSON, MAURICE ROBERT, C.I.B., B.A., B.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

RICHARDSON, SIR THOMAS WILLIAM, Kt., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Retd., Nov., 1924.*

RICHMOND, SIR ROBERT DANIEL, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 29th Oct., 1878).—Retd., Nov., 1932.*

RICKERBY, LEONARD WILLIAM ROBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offcr., accs. dept.) (b. 1st March, 1901).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 1st March, 1920, after previous service from 23rd Apl., 1919; exec. offcr., Nov., 1934.

RICKETTS, ALFRED ARTHUR HICKMAN, M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 5th Jan., 1885).—Retd., Jan., 1940.†

RICKETTS, THOMAS CHARLES DONALD, O.B.E., M.C., (b. 3rd July, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. Burma, 17th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr. Dec., 1934; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; retd., Dec., 1946.

RIDLEY, SIDNEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 26th March, 1902).—Educ. at Royal Grammar School, Lancaster, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 27th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug. 1929; offg. dep. commr., U.S. frontier, Oct., 1931; offg. dep. secty. to govt., Bombay, fin. dept., Jan., 1935; secty. to govt., fin. dept., Sind, Apl., 1936; secty. to agt.-genl. for India in South Africa, Oct., 1936 to Oct., 1940; collr. and mag. variously, from Jan., 1941; ch. sec., govt. of Sind, June, 1944; offg. commr., Dec., 1945.

RIEU, SIR JEAN LOUIS, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 23rd Nov., 1872).—Retd., Oct., 1929.*

RIGG, REV. ARTHUR CECIL PIETRONI, M.A., Ecces. Dept., United Provs. (senr. chaplain) (b. 27th Feb., 1889).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 16th Apl., 1917; junr. chaplain, Apl. 1919; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1929.

RIGG-STANSFIELD, REV. HUBERT, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 22nd Sept., 1897).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 25th Dec., 1931.

RIJHWANI, JETHANAND MANDHANDAS, M.B.E., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 4th June, 1903).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 21st Apl., 1928; offg. exec. engr., March, 1933; confd., Apl., 1937; dep. sec. govt. Sind., Nov., 1941, to July, 1943; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

RILEY, PERCY BROWN, M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Veterinary Service (b. 13th Aug., 1887).—Retd., June, 1940.†

RIPDAMAN, SINGH, Ind. Service of Engrs., (b. 6th Oct., 1908).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st May, 1933; confd. as exec. engr., Sind, May, 1942.

RIPPON, STEWART ROSS, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (dir., Burma) (b. 29th July, 1895).—Joined the service as supt., Burma, 18th Feb.,

1924; dep. dir., Dec., 1929; offg. dir., July, 1932; confd., March, 1940.

RISHWORTH, HENRY RICHARD, C.B.E., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Rlys. (ch. medl. offcr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 8th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service as dist. surg., G.I.P. Rly., 22nd June, 1923; dist. medl. offcr., Apl., 1929; O.B.E., June, 1939; ch. medl. offcr. (confd.), Apl., 1942; C.B.E., June, 1946.

RITCHIE, JOHN HALL, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1891).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

RIVETT-CARNAC, HERBERT GORDON, Lieut.-Col. (ret'd.), late Indian Pol. Service (b. 13th Feb., 1892).—Retd., Feb., 1938.*

RIZAWI, SAIYID MUHAMMAD SHUAIB, Khan Bahadur (see Saiyid, M. S. R.).

RIZVI, AMIR HAIDER, B.A., LL.B., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 26th March, 1895).—Joined the service as dep. supt. of police, July, 1921; confd. Jan., 1924; supt. (confd.), March, 1940.

ROBERTS, DAVID EDWARD, M.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Assam) (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—Retd., May, 1939.*

ROBERTS, SIR ERNEST HANDFORTH GOODMAN, Kt., Barr., Burma, Judl. Dept. (ch. justice, high court, Rangoon).—Apptd. ch. just., high ct., Rangoon, 28th Apl., 1936; knighted, June, 1936.

ROBERTS, E. L., Indian State Rlys. (b. 18th May, 1890).—Joined, 5th June, 1919; ch. optg. supt., M. and S.M. Rly., 12th Nov., 1944.

ROBERTS, COL. VIVIAN GODWIN ROBERT, M.B.E., M.C., Burma Frontier Service, (b. 25th Feb., 1893).—Date of first commn., Oct., 1914; apptd. asst. supt. Burma Front. serv., Sept., 1923; on for. serv. as adminstr. Kengtung State.

ROBERTSON, ANTHONY WILLIAM, C.I.E., O.B.E., D.F.C., late Ind. Police (United Provs.) (b. 1st Dec., 1896).—Joined the serv. 17th Apl., 1919; offg. supt., Sept., 1923; confd., Nov., 1932; asst. commr. in the Andamans from 16th Oct., 1923 to 7th Feb., 1927; Diwan, Bundi State, Sept., 1936; retd., March, 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1945.

ROBERTSON, SIR BENJAMIN, K.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., C.I.E., LL.D., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.).—Retd., Feb., 1920*; member of the Council of India, Nov., 1922 to Nov., 1927.

ROBERTSON, CHARLES ROBERTSON OVENS, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 10th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 22nd Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Apl., 1920; confd., June, 1934.

ROBERTSON, DONALD MURRAY SLADEN, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Oct., 1882).—Retd., May, 1939.*

ROBERTSON, EDWARD GEORGE DONALD, Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1916).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Dec., 1936; A.R.P. instr. Dec., 1940; on mil. duty, March to Oct., 1945; supt. pol., supplies Burma, 1945-46.

ROBERTSON, FRANCIS CALDER FORD, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 19th March, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 21st Feb., 1924; dep. consvr., April, 1929.

ROBERTSON, SIR FREDERICK WYNNE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Feb., 1885).—Retd., March, 1937*; chairman, pub. serv., commn., Bengal, Apl., 1937; C.S.I., June, 1941; ditto, fed. pub. serv. comm., March, 1942; kt. June, 1945.

ROBERTSON, JAMES PERCY WYNNE, late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 17th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 18th Apr., 1920; dist. supt., Apr., 1924; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937; asst. inspector-genl., Dec., 1937; ret'd., 9th Sept., 1944.

ROBERTSON, LEO CAYLEY, M.A. (Hons.), Barr., Burma Judicial Dept. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 19th June, 1891).—Apptd., June, 1938; on spec. duty in ch. sec.'s office (Simla), July, 1943; civ. aff. serv. Apr., 1945; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1945; and again, 1946.

ROBERTSON, MURRAY, Indian State Rlys. (junr. dep. agent, E.I. Rly.) (b. 15th June, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. secty. to agent, 20th Aug., 1920; jt. secty. to agent, Aug., 1924; secty. to agent, Jan., 1925; offg. junr. dep. agent, Oct., 1930; confd., Nov., 1931.

ROBERTSON, THOMAS HENRY, Office of High Commr. for India (executive officer, store dept.) (b. 8th June, 1895).—Apptd., 2nd class clerk, 4th Feb., 1915, and attached to store dept.; on mil. service from May, 1915 to Aug., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apr., 1922; actg., higher exec. officer, Jan., 1940.

ROBINS, LESLIE CYRIL FREDERICK, Indian Police (b. 4th Dec., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 24th Nov., 1928; confd., Nov., 1930; offg. supt., June, 1935; police asst. to A.G.G. in C.I., March, 1937; confd. as supt., March, 1942.

ROBINSON, DOUGLAS HARRY, M.I.E.E., (P.W.D., Punjab) (b. 17th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service 29th March, 1934; confd., March, 1937; exec. engr., July, 1937.

ROBINSON, EDWARD CHARLES, Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., United Provs.) (b. 5th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1912; supt., July, 1925.

ROBINSON, HAROLD WOOD (see Wood-Robinson, Harold).

ROBINSON, STEPHEN ERIC, Indian Police (asst. supt.).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 24th Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

ROBINSON, SYDNEY HERBERT, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 31st May, 1876).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

ROBINSON, SIR SYDNEY MADDOCK, Kt., Barr. (1888), late Judl. Dept., Burma.—Retd., Dec., 1925.*

ROBINSON, THOMAS MIDDLETON, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mech. engr., N.W., Rly) (b. 4th Feb., 1894).—Asst. carriage and wagon supt., junr. scale, 28th Feb., 1922; divl. mech. engr., sent. scale, Nov., 1936.

ROBINSON, WILLIAM DOUGLAS, O.B.E., Indian Police (b. 5th Nov., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 16th Nov., 1927; cent. intell. offcr., Punjab and Delhi, March, 1939; asst. dir. intell. bureau, May, 1941; supt. (confd.), June, 1942; senr. supt., Delhi, May, 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

ROCH, ERNEST TWINING, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bombay) (b. 24th May, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1923; confd., Oct., 1924; suptg. engr. (confd.), May, 1943.

RODGER, SIR ALEXANDER, Kt., O.B.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 11th Aug., 1875).—Retd., Feb., 1931.*

RODGER, JOHN, O.B.E., M.C., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 19th Dec., 1891).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1920; in mil. employ to Jan., 1929; resdy. surg., Mewar, 7th Jan.,

1929; civil surg., Sibi, Oct., 1932; offg. resdy. surg. and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Feb., 1933; and again, Sept., 1935; civil surg., Sibi and Loralai, in addn., Apr., 1924, and again, Oct., 1936; O.B.E., May, 1937; legation surg. and H.M. Chargé d'Affaires, Nepal, June, 1938; civil surg., Hayara, June, 1939.

RODGÉR, ROBERT CRUDEN, M.C., D.C.M., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 7th June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 11th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1928; confd., Apr., 1935; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Poona, Apr., 1937 to Apr., 1938; awarded Indian Police Medal, 1942; cent. intell. offcr., Bombay, Jan., 1943.

RODGERS, REV. ARTHUR HAROLD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (jun. chaplain) (b. 13th June, 1907).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 21st Nov., 1942.

RODGERS, E. W. F., late Dept. of Industries and Labour, Civil Aviation Dir. (b. 21st Nov., 1889).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

RODRIGUES, LEO FRANCIS BORGIA, B.A., LL.B., (b. 10th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1935; offg. admin.-genl. and offi. trustee, Bombay, Sept., 1935, ditto, confd., Sept., 1936.

ROGERS, ERIC MAXFIELD, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 2nd Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Apr., 1926; confd., Jan., 1934; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Oct., 1936 to Mar., 1940.

ROGERS, REV. ERIC WITHAM, Indian Eccles. Estabt., United Provs. (junr. chaplain) (b. 17th Apr., 1898).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 31st March, 1935; junr. chaplain, March, 1937.

ROGERS, REV. GEORGE THOMAS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 18th Nov., 1899).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bengal; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1935.

ROGERS, KENNETH DAVID, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 22nd Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Roundhay Sch., Leeds, and Leeds Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offcr., Apr., 1941; rationing contr., Rawalpindi, 1944.

ROGERS, ROBERT THOMAS LEONARD, B.A., Capt., R.E., (Survey of India, Supt.) (b. 9th Apr., 1909).—First commn., 18th May, 1930; in mil. employ to Apr., 1938; proby. asst. supt., Apr., 1938; confd. Mar., 1940; in mil. employ from Aug., 1940 to Jan., 1946; supt., Oct., 1944.

ROGERS, THOMAS EDWARD, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 28th Dec., 1912).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; H.B.M.'s vice-consul, Bushire, Aug., 1941; asst. pol. agt., Bahrain, Aug., 1944; consul, Bushire, Nov., 1944; confd. in the I.P.S., Mar., 1945; M.B.E., June, 1945; sec. to pol. resdt. Persian Gulf, July, 1945; asst. pol. agt. Quetta-Pishin., Apr., 1946.

ROHATGI, D. P. (see Durga Prasad Rohatgi).

ROLFE, CATHERINE IVY, India Office (b. 26th June, 1901).—Apptd. cler. offr., 1st July, 1922; actg. hr. ditto, Dec., 1937; confd., May, 1938; actg. asst. princpl., Apr., 1940; actg. princpl., Aug., 1944; served with U.N.O. from Apr., 1946.

ROLLO, JAMES FREDERICK, late Northern India Salt Rev. Dept. (b. 3rd Nov., 1884).—Retd.,†

ROMESH CHANDRA, B.Sc., C.E. (hons.),

M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service Of Engrs. (sup. engr., Assam) (b. 17th March, 1897).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1921, as asst. exec. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1930; prof. civil engrg., Roorkee, Oct., 1937 to July, 1938; exec. engr. Nov., 1938; supt. engr., Dec., 1944.

RONALDSHAY (EARL OF) see under Zetland (Marquess of).

RONSON, JOHN LANCASTER, Indian Police (b. 11th Jan., 1918).—Apptd. asst. sup., Madras, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd., Nov., 1940; S.D.O., Jan., 1943; jt. mag. and dist. collr., Apl., 1943; addl. under sec. home dept., Mar., 1944; S.D.O., June, 1945.

ROPER, WILLIAM EDWARD CHARLES, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 26th June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 1st Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1931; on nil duty, Nov., 1943 to Jan., 1946.

ROSE, HUGH, Indian State Ry. (dept. sup. of stores) (b. 7th May, 1892).—Joined the service, 4th March, 1920, asst. supt. of catering, Burma ry., May, 1924; dep. supt. of stores, Burma ry., Oct., 1935.

ROSE, HUGH ELLISHA, late Indian State Ry. (b. 1st May, 1883).—Retd., July, 1937.*

ROSE, K. H., Indian State Ry. (b. 31st Aug., 1891).—Joined, 13th March, 1919; dep. ch. engr. B.B. and C.I.Ry., Aug., 1945.

ROSE, ROBERT FREDERICK, late Indian Service of Engineers (b. 19th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

ROSE, REV. THOMAS PERCIVAL, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. Lahore Diocese (senr. chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Nov., 1931; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1933; senr., chaplain, Nov., 1943.

ROSE MEYER, PATRICK MAURICE, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 9th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service, 11th March, 1912; asst. dir., army audit., S. Cmd., Jan., 1929; dep. govt. exr., B.B. and C.I. ry., March, 1935; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. ry., Apl., 1935; dep. govt. examiner of accts., B.N. ry., Nov., 1938; ch. auditor, E.B. ry., Apl., 1941; ditto, rly. clearing accts., Delhi, Aug., 1941; dy. chief auditor, E.I. ry., Dec., 1941; ch. auditor, E.B. ry. and govt. exmr., A.B. ry., Jan., 1942.

ROSEVEARE, MAJOR WILLIAM LEONARD, M.C., R.E., late Indian Service of Engrs., Burma (b. 11th Dec., 1895).—Retd., Mar., 1938.†

ROSS, GEORGE MABYN, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 30th Apl., 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1938.*

ROSS, HERBERT STEWART, B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 27th Dec., 1885).—Educ. at Christ Coll., Brecon, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; jt. mag., Sept., 1916; on nil duty from Aug., 1918 to Jan., 1919; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1922; offg. supt., ditto, Apl., 1922; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1925; confd., July, 1926; secty., bd. of rev., Jan., 1927; offg. commr., Feb., 1935; bd. of rev., Jan., 1927; offg. commr., Feb., 1935; member, Madhe Sahaba enqy. cttee., Apl., 1937; commr., Apl., 1939; confd., Nov., 1941; retd., Nov., 1945.

ROSS, ROBERT, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 25th Jan., 1917).—Educ. at Hamilton Academy, St. Andrew's Univ., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn. after

selection, in May, 1940, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

ROSSINGTON, MAURICE HERBERT, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 31st July, 1908).—Educ. at the Royal Academical Institution, Belfast, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. home dept., Apl., 1937; dep. commr., from Apl., 1939; dep. sec. to govt., March, 1942; sec. to govt. Apl., 1942; asst. commr., front. areas, June, 1942; civ. aff. serv., Jan., 1944; dep. commr., Feb., 1946.

ROSSITER, EDWARD MORGAN, Civil Aviation Directte. (b. 23rd Oct., 1895).—Joined the serv. as tech. offr. (temp.) Apl., 1936; confd. July, 1940; on spec. duty, Apl., 1945; dir. on spec. duty, Dec., 1945.

ROUGHTON, NOEL JAMES, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th Dec., 1885).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1909, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; regr., judl. commr.'s court, July, 1913; under-sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1918 to March, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), Dec., 1920; confd., Nov., 1921; dir. of indus. and regr. of co-op. societies, Central Provs., July, 1923; dep. sec. to govt. of India, commerce dept., June, 1925; finl. secty. to govt., C.P., Apl., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; offg. commr., Feb., 1933; confd., July, 1934; offg. ch. secty. to govt., U.P., May, 1933; member govr.'s exec. council, Central Provs. (temp.), Nov., 1934, and again, May, 1936; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Nov., 1936; member-secty., tariff bd., May, 1938; C.S.I., June, 1938; addl. temp. member, Fed. public serv. commn., retd., Mar., 1941; chairman, Bihar C.P. and Berar and Orissa, jt. pub. Serv. Commn., Apl., 1941; addl., temp. member Fed. pub. Serv. Commn., Sept., 1945 to May, 1946.

ROUPELL, KATHLEEN ZELIA, India Audit Office (higher clerical offcr.) (b. 11th March, 1890).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 6th Jan., 1922; higher clerical offcr., July, 1935; actg. hr. exec., Oct., 1941.

ROUSE, SIR ALEXANDER MACDONALD, Kt., C.I.E., F.C.H., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Sept., 1878).—Retd. Aug., 1933.*

ROUSE, WILLIAM PERCY MEADOWS, Indian Police (b. 24th Feb., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., 17th Nov., 1926.

ROUT, REGINALD ALAN, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th Sept., 1884).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

ROUTLEDGE, BRYAN HOWARD, Indian Police (sup. Assam) (b. 14th Apl., 1903).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1922; deputed to Sardah Police training coll. in Bengal to 16th May, 1924; proby. asst. supt., May, 1924; confd., Nov., 1924; offg. supt., Oct., 1932; confd., Feb., 1933; asst. to d.i.g. Mar., 1936 to Apl., 1938.

ROW, KODIKAL SANJIVA (see Sanjiva, Row, K.).

ROW, PANDRANG V. (see Pandrang Row).

ROWE, ALBERT JOHN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. offcr., accts. dept.) (b. 23rd Oct., 1892).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 12th Nov., 1913, and attd. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.; services lent to high commr. for India, from Oct., 1920; exec. offcr., Apl., 1922;

actg. higher exec. offcr., Apl., 1925; confd., Apl., 1926.

ROWE, BERTRAM ALFRED, Indian Police (supt. Sind) (b. 14th Jan., 1918).—Apptd. Nov., 1938; supt. (Bombay) Sept., 1945; transfdr. to Sind, Apl., 1946.

ROWLAND, FRANCIS GEORGE, B.A., late Indian C.S., Bihar. (b. 14th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 29th Nov., 1906, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfdr. to Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Apl., 1913; attaché, legisl. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1917; dist. and sess. judge, and ditto, July, 1917; offg. dep. sec., legisl. dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1917; addl. legal rembr., Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1919; judl. commr., Nov., 1923; offg. judge, high ct., Patna, variously, from June, 1929; addl. judge, ditto, variously, from March, 1931; judge, high ct., Patna, Aug., 1936; judge, fedl. ct., 25th May, to 4th June, 1943; ret'd., Feb., 1944; on spl. duty, govt. of India (home), Apl., 1944.

ROWLAND, SIR JOHN EDWARD MAURICE, Indian State Rlys. (chief rly. commr., Burma) (b. 30th Apl., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 24th May, 1904; ditto (perm.), July, 1909; dist. engr., March, 1919; offg. variously, as ch. and dep. ch. engr., from June, 1927; confd. as dep. ch. engr., June, 1929; offg. ch. engr., May, 1933; and again, Dec., 1934; confd., March, 1935; offg. agent, Burma Rlys., Feb., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; chief rly. commr., Apl., 1937; kt. June, 1941; on l.p.r.

ROWLAND, THEODORE, M.B.E., India Office (higher exec. offcr., military dept.) (b. 20th July, 1892).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 6th March, 1912, and att'd. to registry and record dept.; transfdr. to mil. dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from Dec., 1915 to March, 1919; exec. offcr., Apl., 1922; dep. reading clerk to council, Feb., 1926 to Feb., 1932; actg. hr. exec. offcr., Jan.-July, 1932; confd., March, 1933; actg. suptg. exec. offcr., Nov., 1941; M.B.E., Jan., 1942.

ROWLANDS, SIR ARCHIBALD, K.C.B., M.B.E.—Apptd. finl. advr. (mil. fin.), govt. of India, July, 1937; K.C.B., 1941; advr. to Viceroy on war admin., Oct., 1943; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. cl. (fin.) Apl., 1945 to July, 1946.

ROWLANDSON, EDMUND JAMES, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 27th Oct., 1882).—Ret'd., Dec., 1935.*

ROWLEY, JOHN HEWITT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 25th Apl., 1917).—Educ. at Llanfyllin County Sch., Mont., Univ. Coll. of North Wales, Bangor, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd., on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 29th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.

ROWNTREE, HAROLD KIRKBY, M.C., M.B., Lieut.-Col. (ret'd.), late I.M.S. (b. 27th Oct., 1887).—Ret'd., Sept., 1938.*

ROWNTREE, JOHN BATEMAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Assam) (b. 5th Apl., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. consvr., 11th Oct., 1929; confd., Oct., 1930; dep. consvr., Jan., 1938.

ROXBURGH, THOMAS JAMES YOUNG, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 2nd March, 1892).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; apptd., after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.

and collr.; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916 to Feb., 1919; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Apl., 1920; offg. chief presy. mag., Calcutta, June, 1923; confd., May, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; offg. secty. to govt., judl. dept., Bengal, Feb., 1935; judl. secty., Bengal, Apl., 1937; addtl. judge, Calcutta high ct., Feb., 1940; judge, ditto, March, 1943.

ROY (see also Ray).

ROY, SIR ASOKA KUMAR, M.A., B.L., Barr-at-Law (b. 9th Sept., 1886).—Memb. of standing cl., Bengal, 1929; Kt., Feb., 1937; advocate-genl. of Bengal, 1934-43; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council (law) June, 1943 to July, 1946.

ROY, BEJOY KUMAR, M.A., Indian Forest Service, M.A., B.Sc. (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 4th March, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1920; dep. consvr., Dec., 1924; actg. consvr. Mar. to June, 1945.

ROY, CHANDRA BALI, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 9th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Nov., 1924; confd., Oct., 1926; confd., Feb., 1938.

ROY, HIMADRI NARAYAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 6th Dec., 1918).—Educ. at Victoria Sch., Kurseong, Calcutta Univ., and King's Coll., London; apptd. on probn., after selection in Aug., 1940, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. S.D.O., Feb., 1943.

ROY, JAMES LEISHMAN, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 29th Apl., 1893).—Joined the service, as asst. engr., Oct., 1915; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; under-sec. to govt., irrign. bch., Aug., 1932; under-sec. (construction), Punjab irrign. sect., Jan., 1933; ditto (north), May, 1933; suptg. engr., May, 1940.

ROY, KEITH COTTON, M.B.E., B.A., Ind. C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 26th July, 1912).—Educ. at Wyggeston Gr. Sch., Leicester, University Coll., London, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, Dec., 1935; arrived in India, Jan., 1936, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; spl. duty, fin. dept., govt. of India, June, 1941; under-sec., fin. dept., Apl., 1942; M.B.E., June, 1945.

ROY, KANANBENARI SEN, Rai Bahadur, M.B.E., B.Sc., M.B.B.S., United Provs. Medl. Serv.; (b. 30th Dec., 1891).—Apptd. 10th Sept., 1918; civil surg. (confd.), 3rd July, 1938; M.B.E., June, 1946.

ROY, PARESH LAL, M.A., Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 20th Dec., 1893).—Asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1919; dist. ditto, Jan., 1937.

ROY, SIR SATYENDRA NATH, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Ind. C.S. (b. 23rd Sept., 1888).—Educ. at Govt. Sch., Bankura, Bengal, Calcutta Univ., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 25th Nov., 1913, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; sec. to prov. cttee. to consider proposals of pub. serv. commn., Nov., 1917, to Jan., 1918; offg. under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, gen. dept., Jan., 1918; ditto, finl. dept., Feb., 1918; employed under Indian def. force, Feb. to June, 1918; on spl. duty, genl. dept. of govt. of Bengal, June, 1918; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, genl. dept., July, 1918; dep. chairman, Howrah municipality, Oct., 1919, to Nov., 1920; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1921; offg. dep. sec., govt. of Bengal, pol. and appt. dept., Nov., 1925; mag. and collr., Sept., 1927; dep.

sec., Indian central cttee., Oct., 1928; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Apr., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; joint sec., Indian round table conf., London, 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; offg. addl. sec. to govt., Bengal, pol. dept., Apr., 1933; jt. sec. to the govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., July, 1936; offg. sec., ditto, Oct., 1936; sec., dept. of communications, Nov., 1937; C.S.I., June, 1938; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. cl., March to Apr., 1942; secy., govt. of India (war transport), July, 1942; kt., June, 1942; retd., Mar., 1945; K.C.I.E., June, 1945; coal commr. with the govt. of India, Mar., 1946.

ROY, SATYENDRA NATH (see Ray.)

ROY, SUHRID KUMAR, B.Sc., Ph.D., F.G.S., Indian Sch. of Mines (b. 1st Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as prof. of geology, 3rd Jan., 1928.

ROY, SURESH CHANDRA, M.Sc., D.Sc. (Lond.), F.N.I., Meteorological Dept., (b. 1st Mar., 1899).—Apptd. Meteorologist, 7th Mar., 1927; confd., Mar., 1929; dir., Colaba and Alibag Observatories, Sept., 1933 to Mar., 1936; dir. Burma Meteorological Dept., Apr., 1937 to Aug., 1942.

ROYDS, THOMAS, D.Sc., late Meteorological Dept. (b. 11th Apr., 1884).—Retd., Apr., 1939.*

RUDDELL, REV. J. W. F., B.A., Ind. Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 8th Aug., 1896).—Apptd. chaplain, Bombay, on probn., 1st Oct., 1934; junior chaplain, Oct., 1936.

RUMBOLD, HORACE ALGERNON FRASER, B.A. Ind. Off. (actg. asst. sec., econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 27th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Wellington and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. asst. principal, Jan., 1929; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Apr., 1930; ditto to perm. under-sec. of state, Dec., 1933; actg. princpl., Sept., 1934; confd. Apr., 1937; actg. asst. sec., July, 1943.

RUNDETT, LESLIE ST. CLARE, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th March, 1891).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

RUNGANADHAN, SIR SAMUEL E., Diwan Bahadur, M.A., I.E.S. (ret'd.) (b. 30th Dec., 1877).—Apptd. advr. to sec. of state for India, May, 1940; kt., Jan., 1943; high commr. for India, 10th June, 1943.

RUPCHAND BILARAM, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 15th May, 1878).—Retd., May, 1938.*

RUSBY, LIONEL STUART, late Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 6th Nov., 1896).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

RUSE, VICTOR HAROLD, B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 12th Oct., 1915).—Educ. at Wanstead County High Sch., London, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; retd., 14th Jan., 1943.

RUSHTON, HUGH DARRELL, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 6th Nov., 1903).—First commn., 30th Aug., 1923; in mil. employ until 20th May, 1929; pers. asst. to rest. at Hyderabad, 25th May, 1929; ditto to ch. commr., N.W.F.P., May, 1931; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, May, 1932; asst. commr., Nowshera, Oct., 1933; dist. and sess. judge, Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1935; add. ditto, Peshawar, July, 1936; asst. commr., Nowshera, Sept., 1938; sec. to pol. rest. in the Persian Gulf, March, 1939; under-sec. to rest. at Hyderabad, Dec., 1939; asst. pol. agent, Sibi,

Apr., 1941; dep. commr., Hazara, Sept., 1944.

RUSSELL, ALEXANDER ALFRED, M.C., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 5th Oct., 1898).—First commn. dated 30th Jan., 1917; in mil. employ prior to 20th Apr., 1924; supy. asst. commr., Peshawar, March, 1925; sec., Kabul legation, Apr., 1925; supy. asst. commr., Mardan, Dec., 1926; under-sec. to ch. commr., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1927; asst. commr., Kohat, Apr., 1928; under-sec. to rest., Persian Gulf, May, 1928; offg. sec. to ditto, May, 1929; British trade agent, Gyantse, etc., Nov., 1931; sec. to A.G.G. for Deccan states, Apr., 1934; ditto, states of W.I., March, 1935; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1936; ditto, Hazara, Dec., 1937; sec. to the rest. for Rajputana, Apr., 1939; pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana States, March, 1941; and again, Oct., 1944; offg. resdt. Cl. II and resdt. for the Madras States, Oct., 1945; resdt. at Gwalior and for states of Rampur and Benares, May, 1946.

RUSSELL, SIR (CHARLES) LENNOX (SOMERVILLE), Kt., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Retd., Aug., 1926.*

RUSSELL, EDWARD WILSON, A.M.I.C.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 24th Oct., 1886).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

RUSSELL, HAROLD GEORGE, O.B.E., Indian Police (Supt., Punjab) (b. 24th Feb., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 25th Nov., 1922; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; offg. supt., Nov., 1932; confd., May, 1937; O.B.E., June, 1936; asst. inspr. gen., May, 1937; senr. supt., Lahore, March, 1940; served under govt. of India (comms.), June, 1942, as motor transport offr.; dep. contr., road transport (war transport dept.), Aug., 1942; contr., Oct., 1944.

RUSSELL, JAMES MCCONNELL, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 28th July, 1909).—Educ. at Stranraer High Sch., Glasgow Univ., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec., fin. dept., Burma, Feb., 1935; asst. settlement offr., Oct., 1937; on mil. service, Nov., 1940; on mil. duty to Aug., 1942; served with def. dept. Sept., 1942; addl. dep. commr., March, 1943; civ. aff. serv., July, 1943; dep. commr., (confd.), July, 1946.

RUSSELL, JAMES WILLIAM, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 2nd May, 1899).—Joined the service, 25th Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., May, 1927; confd., Oct., 1931.

RUSSELL, JOSHUA FORBES, O.B.E., India Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., Bengal) (b. 4th Feb., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th Apr., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1925; exec. engr., Oct., 1926; confd. as suptg. engr., Nov., 1942; ch. engr., Aug., 1944.

RUSSELL, LAWRENCE WALTER, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 6th Jan., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 10th Nov., 1931; confd., Apr., 1933.

RUSSELL, SIR ROBERT EDWIN, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (advr. to govr. of Bihar) (b. 21st Apr., 1890).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 13th Dec., 1913, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service from Oct., 1915, to Apr., 1919; addl. under-sec. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, educn. and munpl. depts., Apr., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1922; offg. sec.

to govt. rev. dept., March, 1927; confd., July, 1928; mag. and collr., Nov., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offg. ch. sec. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, Aug., 1934, to Sept., 1936; ch. sec. to govt., Bihar, March, 1938; advr. to the govr., Bihar, Nov., 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; kt. Jan., 1946.

RUSSELL, ROBERT TOR, C.I.E., D.S.O., F.R.I.B.A. (b. 2nd July, 1888).—Consulting architect, central p.w.d., Oct., 1933.

RUSSELL, STEVEN NOEL, O.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. sec. govt. of India, war dept.) (b. 24th Dec., 1908).—Educ. at King's Sch., Ely, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., pol. and appts. depts., Jan., 1936; O.B.E., June, 1941; under-sec., defence/war, govt. of India, Aug., 1941; addl. dep. sec. (war) Apl., 1943; dep. sec., war dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1943; jt. sec. ditto, Apl., 1946.

RUSSELL, THOMAS BRIAN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 15th Feb., 1888).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

RUSSELL, SIR THOMAS GUTHRIE, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Jan., 1887).—Retd., Jan., 1942.†

RUSTAMJEE, K. F., B.Sc., (Hon.), Nagpur, Indian Police (b. 22nd May, 1916).—Apptd. asst. dist., supt., Cent. Provs. and Berar, on probn., 7th March, 1938.

RUSTOMJI, NARI KAIKHOSRU, Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 16th May, 1919).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., after seln. in Aug., 1941, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Oct., 1945.

RUTHERFORD, SIR THOMAS GEORGE, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th September, 1886).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll. (Edinburgh), Edinburgh and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1910, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Sept., 1916; on mil. duty, from Nov., 1917, to Dec., 1919; special commr., agency operations, Apl., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1925; collr. and dist. mag. (provl.), Aug., 1929; confd., Feb., 1930; offg. sec., p.w. and l. dept., Madras, Apl., 1934; offg. commr. of labour, Madras, March, 1935; offg. sec., home dept., Madras, June, 1936; offg. second sec. to govt., Madras, June, 1936; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Madras, Nov., 1936; commr. of lab., Nov., 1937; sec. to govr., Apl., 1938; C.S.I., June, 1939; advr. to the govr., Oct., 1939; actg. govr., Bihar, Jan., 1943; govr., March, 1943; K.C.S.I., Mar., 1943; actg. govr., Bengal, Sept., 1943 to Jan., 1944, govr., Bihar, Apl., 1944; retd., Aug., 1946.

RUTHNASWAMY, MARIA DOSS, C.I.E., Barr.-at-Law, Principal, Law Coll., Madras, June, 1928; Member, Madras Public Service Commn., Jan., 1930.

RUTLEDGE, REV. JAMES WILLIAM RENWICK, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 26th Aug., 1896) (principal chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Ch. of Scotland, Bombay, 10th Feb., 1928; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1930; senr. ditto, Feb., 1940; offg. presy. senr. chaplain, Bombay, Mar., 1943; ditto, Bengal, Dec., 1943; principal chaplain, Ch. of Scotland, Apl., 1946.

RUTNAM, DONALD ROSS, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b.

19th Sept., 1901).—Educ. at Ceylon Univ. Coll., Colombo, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1925; arrived, 31st Dec., 1925, and served in Bengal as asst. collr.; asst. commr., Central Provs., Aug., 1926; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1929; on foreign serv. as admin., Bastar State, March, 1931; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1937; confd., Feb., 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; jt. sec. to govt. police, etc., depts., March, 1941; dep. sec., govt. of India (war transport), March, 1944; jt. sec., ditto, Oct., 1944.

RYAN, CECIL CRAWFORD LINDSAY, Maj., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 13th Dec., 1897).—Retd., Nov., 1941.†

RYAN, G. C., Indian Police (b. 21st July, 1914).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 22nd Nov., 1934.

SAADAT-UL-LAH KHAN, Indian Agricl. Service (Madras) (b. 14th April, 1898).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Madras, on probn., 24th Jan., 1922; confd., Nov., 1922; agricl. offr., Quetta, Aug., 1932; dep. dir., agric., Madras, Dec., 1934, on l.p.r.

SAADULLA, SIR MUHAMMAD, MAULVI SAITYID, K.C.I.E., M.A., B.L., Membr. govr's. exec. counc. Assam, 1st Apl., 1929 to Mar., 1934; chairman of cttee. on public accts., 1933-34; chief minister, Assam, Apl., 1937, to Sept., 1938; prime minister from Nov., 1939 to Dec., 1941, and from Aug., 1942; member, nat. def. council, July, 1941; K.C.I.E., June, 1946.

SABHARWAL, LAKHPAT RAI, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (consvr. Bihar) (b. 18th Aug., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th March, 1919; pers. asst. to presdt., forest research inst., March, 1936; consvr. of forests, Bihar, 1938.

SABIHUDDIN, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., Barr.-at-Law, late Indian Posts and Tels. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 16th May, 1886).—Retd., July, 1941.†

SABINE, HAROLD EDWARD, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 12th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1920, as proby. asst. supt.; confd., May, 1923; addl. supt., Jan., 1926; supt., Aug., 1926; asst. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1943; to Feb., 1944; Indian police medal, 1941.

SABNIS, TRIMBAK SITARAM, M.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (United Provs.) (b. 8th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 19th May, 1923; economic botanist, June, 1923; princpl. agric. coll., Sept., 1941; confd. in sel. grade, Apl., 1943.

SABPIO, MOHAMED MOOSA, (P.W.D., Sind).—Joined Dec., 1923; asst. engr. (confd.), Dec., 1925; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1940; exec. engr., Aug., 1945; addl. dep. sec. to govt., P.W.D., Nov., 1945.

SACHCHIDANAND, AKHAURI, M.Sc., Indian Police (see Akhauri, S.).

SACHDEV, MULK RAJ, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., Ind. C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; joined the serv., 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1933; O.B.E., June, 1942; dep. dir.-genl., (disposals) Oct., 1943; offr. on spec. duty, office of fin. commr., Feb., 1945; C.I.E., June, 1946.

SACHSE, SIR FREDERIC ALEXANDER, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 27th Feb., 1878).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

SADAGOPAN, K., Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Sept., 1901).—Joined 10th May, 1926;

Fincl. advr. and ch. accts. offr., S.I. Rly., Jan., 1946.

SADASHIVA RAU, KALLE RAMA RAU, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 16th July, 1884).—Retd., July, 1939.*

SADASIVAM, PILLAI, DORAIWAMI, Rao Bahadur, B.A. (Madras), B.Sc. (Tech.), Manch., A.M.C.I. (Manch.) (dep. dir. purchase) (b. 15th June, 1886).—Joined the service, 10th June, 1920, as asst. dir. of industries, Madras (temp.); apptd. asst. dir. of purchase, Indian Stores Dept., Dec., 1922; dep. ditto, July, 1935.

SADASIVAM, TUMULURU RAMAKRISHNIAH, B.A., Finance Dept., Govt. of India (b. 27th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service as prob., 9th Feb., 1915; asst. contr., paper currency, Nov., 1916; asst. acct. genl., posts and tels., Madras, 24th June, 1921; asst. acct.-genl., Cent. Provs., Oct., 1921; as st. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Calcutta, March, 1922; dep. ditto, Oct., 1924; asst. dir. of army audit, W.C., March, 1926; ditto, S.C., May, 1927; audit offr., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1930; asst. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Delhi, Jan., 1931; dep. ditto, Jan., 1931; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Apl., 1931; attd. offr., office of dir., rly. audit, May, 1932; offg. dir., rly. audit, Jan., 1933; offg. audit offr., L.B. and C.C., Apl., 1934; comptr., Sind, Apl., 1936; dep. dir.-genl., finance, posts and tels., Jan., 1937; acct. genl., Punjab, Apl., 1939; acct.-genl., Bihar, Sept., 1939; on for. serv. (govt. of Burma), June, 1940, to June, 1942; acct.-genl. (confd.), cl. II, Nov., 1941; acct.-genl., Madras, March, 1946.

SADDLER, JAMES, M.B.E., M.A., Ind. C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 13th Sept., 1913).—Educ. at Forfar Academy and St. Andrews Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 9th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1940; under sec., home dept., Nov., 1941; under govt. of India infm. dept., Nov., 1942; dep. sec. civ. supps., Dec., 1944; M.B.E., June, 1946.

SADHU RAM, CHAUDHRI, B.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 24th July, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 6th Oct., 1922; offg. supt., Feb., 1928; confd., March, 1934; King's police and fire service medal (bar to medal), Nov., 1940; asst. inspr.-gen., rly. police, Oct., 1945.

SADIQ ALI KHAN, Aga, late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 21st Jan., 1886).—Retd., July, 1940.†

SADULLAH, ABUL MUZAFFAR, M.A., Indian Police (b. 28th Feb., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 11th May, 1938; confd., May, 1940.

SAGREIYA, KAMPTA PRASAD, B.A. (Oxon), B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 10th Feb., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Central Provs., 27th Oct., 1927; dep. consvr., Dec., 1931; silviculturist, March, 1937, to May, 1943.

SAH, DEBI LAL (see Debi Lal Sah).

SAHA, BHUPENDRA NATH, M.Sc., Survey of India, (b. 12th Nov., 1896).—Proby. extra asst. supt., Oct., 1919; confd., Oct., 1922; supt., Dec., 1944.

SAHAI, RAM CHANDRA (see Ram Chandra Sahai).

SAHAI, RAMESHWAR (see Rameshwar Sahai).

SAHAY, BHAGWAN, O.B.E., Ind. C.S., (b. 15th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad and Sch. of Oriental Stud., London; joined the serv., 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 10th Nov., 1929, and

served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1931; dep. sec. to govt., United Provs., indus. bch. (temp.), May, 1936, and again, May, 1937; supt. of census oper. (provl.), Dec., 1939; sec., imperial council of agricl. research, July, 1941; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dept. educn., health and lands, Jan., 1944; O.B.E., June, 1945; mag. and collr. (confd.), July, 1944.

SAHAY, JAYADEV, M.A., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 2nd Jan., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 30th Apl., 1923; offg. addl. supt., Feb., 1932; supt., May, 1936.
