(D.M.O., Madras) (b. 3rd Dec., 1899).—First commn., 29th Feb., 1924; in milty. employ until 3rd Feb., 1934; dist. medl. offr., March, 1934; confd. in civil dept. with effect from June, 1936; princpl., medical coll., etc., Vizagapatam, May, 1939; on mil. duty, Feb., 1941 to Feb., 1946; dist. med. offr. and supt. h.q. hosp. and mental hosp. Calicut, Feb., 1946.

SHEPPARD, ARTHUR LEWIN, M.B., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Burma Medl. Dept. (b. 30th Apl., 1882).—Ret'd., Apl., 1939.*

SHEPPARD, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS BERRILL, O.B.E., M.B.B.S. (U. Melb.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), Major, I.M.S., Madras Medical Dept., (b. 21st Apl., 1904).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1932; in mil. employ until Aug., 1936; dist. medl. offr., Sept., 1936; O.B.E., June, 1941; supt. govt. genl. hosp. Madras, Apl., 1945.

SHERIDAN, A. M., Major, I.M.S. (civil surg., U.P.).—Date of first commn., Apl. 29th, 1929; civil surg., Sept., 1936.

SHERIDAN, SIR PHILIP CAHILL, Kt., C.M.G., late Indian State Rlys.—Ret'd., June, 1929.*

SHERRY, WALTER CAMPBELL, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offr., store dept.) (b. 12th July, 1885).—Educ. at Dorchester Gr. Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 31st Aug., 1904; transfld. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. offr., Apl., 1922.

SHETH, GUNENDRA LALBHAI, B.Sc.Econ., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 10th Nov., 1915).—Educ. at New High Sch., Ahmedabad, Univ. Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

SHIELDS, ALFRED EDWIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 7th May, 1916).—Educ. at Stockton Sec. Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

SHIELS, DR. T. DRUMMOND, M.C., M.P., Parly. Under-Secty. of State, June-Dec., 1929.

SHILLIDY, GEORGE ALEXANDER, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 7th March, 1886).—Ret'd., Feb., 1941.†

SHINDE, PANDURANO KRISHNARAO, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Dec., 1883).—Ret'd., Dec., 1938.*

SHINDE, RAMCHANDRA DHARMAJI, B.A., LL.B. (Bom.), (b. 1st May, 1896).—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1941; dist. and sess. judge (confd.), Dec., 1942.

SHIRLEY, GEOFFREY STANHOPE, late Indian Forest Service, Burma (b. 19th July, 1888).—Ret'd., Nov., 1939.*

SHIROLE, DINKARRAO ABURAO, B.A., Indian Police, Bombay (b. 1st March, 1903).—Joined April, 1928, as dep. supt.; Indian police

medal, 1939; prom. to I.P. as supt., Jan., 1940, regl. transp. authority, Mar., 1945.

SHIRRAS, GEORGE FINDLAY, M.A., F.S.S., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 16th July, 1885).—Ret'd., July, 1940.†

SHIRREFF, ALEXANDER GRIERSON, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Apl., 1883).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived 9th Dec., 1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; regr., ct. of judl. commr., Oudh, Feb., 1913 to July, 1915; jt. mag., Jan., 1916; under-sec. to govt., United Provs., Apl., 1916; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), July, 1918 to Apl., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1919 to Nov., 1921; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1921; confd., Dec., 1923; offg. commr., March, 1935; provl. subst., Feb., 1938; advr. to the govr., Oct., 1942; confd. member, bd. of rev., Nov., 1942; ret'd., Dec., 1944.

SHIVDASANI, HIRANAND RUPCHAND, O.B.E., B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in the United Provs. as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1933; confd., Nov., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Dec., 1944.

SHIVDASANI, RAMCHAND TEKCHAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 14th Nov., 1898).—Educ. at Bombay University and London University, (School of Economics); joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 11th Dec., 1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., May, 1927; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1932; offg. excise commr., Oct., 1933; offg. inspr.-gen. of regn. (in addn.), 1933; offg. dir. of indus., U.P., Feb., 1935; mag. and collr. (provl. subst.), Feb., 1938; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1941; regnl. food commr. (dept. of food), May, 1945.

SHIVESHWARKAR, SADANAND WAMAM, M.B.E., Ind. C.S. (dep. sec., govt. of India, industries and civil supplies dept.) (b. 1st March, 1912).—Educ. at Savantvadi High Sch., Bombay University and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Jan., 1937; arrived in India, Feb., 1937, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. commerce dept., May, 1943; under sec. govt. of India, indust. and civ. supplies dept., May, 1944; dep. sec. ditto, May, 1946; M.B.E., June, 1946.

SHONE, SAMUEL, CAPT. I.M.S., (b. 22nd Feb., 1906).—Spec. (junr.) in med. govt. hosp., Madras, Jan., 1939; recalled to mil. May, 1940; reverted to civil, June, 1945; spec. in med., etc., Vizagapatam, June, 1945.

SHOOBERT, SIR WILFRED HAROLD, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dir.-gen., posts and tels.) (b. 7th June 1896).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; services placed at disposal of Assam Labour Board from Apl., 1923 to March, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1925; confd., Nov., 1933; provl. supt., census operations, Apl., 1930; offg. commr. of settlements, etc., C.P., March, 1934; and again, Apl., 1936; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, indus. and lab., March, 1937; p.m.g., U.P. circle, May, 1938; senr. dep. dir.-gen., posts and tels., Aug.,

1940; dir.-gen., ditto, March, 1912; kt., June, 1946.

SHORT, REV. GEORGE MURRAY DAVIDSON, M.A., late Indian Ecclesl. Establ. (Madras) (b. 21st Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, 19th Dec., 1924; in mil. employ. from Feb., 1925 to March, 1926; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1935; retd., Jan., 1944.

SHORTT, NORMAN ROBERT, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th July, 1883).—Retd., July, 1938.*

SHOVELLER, HAROLD LANGLEY, A.C.A., late income-tax dept., Bombay (b. 16th Jan., 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

SHRI SHRIDHAR NEHRU (see Nehru, S. S.).

SHRINAGESH, JAYAVANT MALLANNAH, C.I.E., Indian C.S., (b. 1st January, 1905).—Educ. at West Buckland School, Barnstaple, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1932, and again, March, 1933; addl. ditto, July, 1933; dep. sec., govt. of India, dept. of supply, Aug., 1941; ditto, labour, June, 1944; dep. sec. shipbuilding and repairs, Aug., 1944; jt. sec. munitions prodn., June, 1945; C.I.E., June, 1946.

SHRIVASTAVA, NIRMAL CHANDRA, B.Sc., Ind. C.S. (dep. sec., govt. of India, dept. of lab.) (b. 1st Sept., 1913).—Educ. at Patwardhan High Sch., Nagpur, Nagpur Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt. of Cent. Provs., Apl., 1939; under sec., govt. of India, dept., of labour, Feb., 1942; dep. sec., ditto, June, 1944.

SHROFF, SARVA DAMAN, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 6th Nov., 1887).—Retd., Nov., 1942.†

SHUKLA, JANARDAN DATT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 1st July, 1915).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Lalitpur, Allahabad Univ., Queen Mary Coll., London, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

SHUKUL, BADRI PRASAD, B.A., late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 2nd May, 1885).—Retd., May, 1938.*

SHYAM SUNDER PRASAD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bihar) (b. 1st March, 1903).—Joined the service as dep. consvr., Bihar and Orissa, 3rd Dec., 1926; divl. for offr., Bihar, May, 1944.

SI, U KA, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 4th April, 1902).—Educ. at Calcutta and Rangoon Univs., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 29th, Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., local govt., dept., Nov., 1929; offg. dep. commr., July, 1932; confd., Feb., 1937; supt., census operations, Apl., 1940; civ. aff. serv., Aug., 1945; commr., Nov., 1945; elections commr., July, 1946.

SIDDI, YAR MOHAMMAD, United Provs. Medl. Serv. (b. 15th March, 1894).—Apptd., 13th Oct., 1920; civil surgeon (confd.), 1st July, 1940.

SIDDIQI, SHUJAAT ALI, M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 9th Nov., 1902).—Joined the service as offr., cl. II, March, 1928.

SIDESHWARI PRASAD VARMA, C.I.E.,

M.Sc., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 28th March, 1890).—Retd., Oct., 1941.†

SIFTON, SIR JAMES DAVID, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon.), late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 17th Apl., 1878).—Retd., Nov., 1937.*

SILVER, CECIL HERBERT, late India Office (b. 28th Jan., 1886).—Educ. at Whitgift Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 15th March, 1907, and employed in the registry and record dept.; transf. to judl. and public dept., May, 1914; resdt. clerk, Dec., 1916 to Aug., 1920; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1919; joint editor, India Office List, Nov., 1920 to May, 1926; actg. staff clerk, Apl., 1921; admin. offcr., Apl., 1922; sec. to Indian delgn. to 4th Internl. Labour Conf., Geneva, Oct., 1922; sec. to Indian delgn. to Internl. Passport Conf., Geneva, May, 1926; admin. offr. for pol. intell., June, 1937, rtd., Nov., 1946.

SILVESTER, COLIN NORMAN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch., mechl. engr.), E.B. rly. (b. 25th Sept., 1895).—Rolled the service, 10th Apl., 1920; asst. rolling stock offr., Dec., 1925; works mang., E.B. rly. (prov.), Aug., 1934; confd., Sept., 1936; dep. ch. mechl. engr., Jan., 1942.

SIMCOCK, ERIC, Office of High Commr. for India (actg. suptg. exec. offr., gen. dept.) (b. 15th July, 1895).—Apptd. 2nd cl. clerk, India Office, 5th Aug., 1914; on mil. service, from Oct., 1915 to Jan., 1919; exec. offr. Apl., 1922; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. offr., June, 1927; on loan to min. of lab. and nat. serv., March, 1943 to Sept., 1943 as actg. first cl. offr., and dep. appts. offr. southern region, Mar., 1945; suptg. exec. offr. (actg.), Oct., 1945.

SIMMONS, CHARLES EDWARDS, late Indian Forest Service (b. 29th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 20th Dec., 1913; in mil. employ from 20th Oct., 1915 to 11th Jan., 1921; dep. consvr., Assam, Jan., 1921; on depn. to the forest coll., Dehra Dun, as instr., from Dec., 1921; offg. consvr., Assam, Sept., 1936; consvr., Bengal, Jan., 1938; retd., 28th Jan., 1944.

SIMMONS, EDWARD CLARE, Indian Forest Service (b. 26th Jan., 1899). (dep. consvr., Burma)—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., May, 1928; on l.p.r. from Dec., 1945.

SIMMONS, JESSE JOHN, late Indian Police, Bombay (b. 12th Apl., 1886).—Retd., May, 1945.†

SIMMONS, R. M., Indian State Rlys. (b. 21st Oct., 1900).—Joined, 16th Jan., 1920; dep. ch. traff. mangr., B.B. and C.I. Rly., Sept., 1945; traff. supt. B.B. and C.I. Rly., May, 1946.

SIMMS, JOHN GERALD, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 6th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. coll. collr., Nov., 1939; under-sec., govt. of India, home dept., Nov., 1938 to March, 1943; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1944; offg. commr. June, 1945; offg. sec. to govt. of Bombay, home dept., Dec., 1945.

SIMPSON, CHARLES EDWARD BYRNE BROWN, Burma Front. Serv. (b. 25th Feb., 1920).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

SIMPSON, DAVID WILLIAM, Burma Front. Serv. (b. 9th Nov., 1917).—Apptd. 7th Jan., 1940.

SIMPSON, EDWARD SKINNER, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 31st Oct., 1897).—Joined the service, 3rd Sept., 1923; arrived in India, 2nd Nov., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1928; confd., Nov., 1932; regr., high ct., Calcutta, appellate side, March, 1935 to July, 1937.

SIMPSON, JOHN ALEXANDER, C.I.E., M.A., India Office (jt. sec., mil. dept.) (b. 25th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. junr. clerk, 15th Sept., 1919, and attd. to pol. dept.; resdt. clerk, Apl., 1920 to March, 1921; served with 14th Bn. co. of London Regt. (London Scottish), Sept., 1915 to Feb., 1916; 4th Bn. Gordon Hlrs., March to Aug., 1917; and 124th Baluchistan Infy., Sept., 1917 to Aug., 1919; prinpl., Apl., 1921; dep. clerk of council, June, 1931; actg. joint sec., mil. dept., Jan.–July, 1932; and again, Sept., 1934; confd., Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

SIMPSON, SIR JOHN HOPE, K.B.E., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh)—Retd. Dec., 1916.*

SIMPSON, REGINALD HUGH, Indian Police (cent. intell. offr., govt. of India) (b. 14th Aug., 1912).—Joined the serv. as proby. asst. supt. of police, Bombay, Nov., 1933; confd. Apl., 1936; served in Sind from Oct., 1938; Indian police medal, 1942; asst. comdt., Sind police rifles, Oct., 1943; under govt. of India, as cent. intell. offr., Apl., 1945.

SIMPSON, SYDNEY, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 3rd May, 1885).—Retd., June, 1940.*

SIMS, ARTHUR MITFORD, C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 31st March, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1911; on mil. duty from 30th March, 1916 to 8th Nov., 1918; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), July, 1934; offg. dep. agent, N.W. rly., Apl., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937; dep. chief engr. (provl. perm.), Apl., 1938; ch. engr., provl., Oct., 1941; confd., Oct., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; retd., Mar., 1945.

SIMS, FREDERICK, M.B.E., Public Works Dept., Bihar (b. 4th Feb., 1894).—Joined the service as electrical engr. and elec. inspr., 17th Oct., 1927; elec. engr. and elec. inspr. (confd.), Oct., 1939; M.B.E., June, 1941.

SINCLAIR, MALCOLM CECIL, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 25th Sept., 1899).—First commn., dated 1st Oct., 1918; in mil. employ to 7th Sept., 1920; asst. resdt., Aden, Oct., 1924; and asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Apl., 1927; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Mar., 1930; asst. commr., Manshera, Apl., 1931; dep. commr., Hazara, Apl., 1932; dep. commr., Bannu, Sept., 1932; civil sec. to ch. commr., Aden, Sept., 1935; civ. sec., dist. mag. and p'port. offr., Aden, Apl., 1937; again, Oct., 1937; O.B.E., June, 1938; Brit. trade agt., Gyantse, and asst. to pol. offr., Sikkim, July, 1940; pol. agt., Western Kathiawar agency, Sept., 1941; consul-gen., Pondicherry, Nov., 1944; H.M.'s consul-gen. in the French Ests. in India, Aug., 1945; and again, June, 1946.

SINCLAIR, MAURICE REVELT, O.B.E., MAJ., I.M.S., (civ. surgeon, U.P.) (b. 26th June, 1906).—Date of first commn. 1st Aug., 1928; in civ. employ (confd.), 26th June, 1937.

SINGH, AJIT (see Ajit Singh).

SINGH, BADAN (see Badan S.).

SINGH, BAKSHI (see Bakshi S.).

SINGH, BALWANT SHIHN (see Balwant Singh Shihn).

SINGH, BANAPHAR JANAKIRAM (see Janakiram Singh, B.).

SINGH, BAWA KARTAR (see Bawa Kartar Singh).

SINGH, BHAN (see Bahan Singh).

SINGH, BRIJ BHUSHAN (see Brij Bhushan, S.).

SINGH, BUDH (see Budh S.).

SINGH, DALIP (see Dalip Singh).

SINGH, DALJIT (see Daljit Singh).

SINGH, DARSHAN (see Darshan Singh).

SINGH, DEVI (see Devi Singh).

SINGH, GURBACHAN (see Gurbachan, Singh).

SINGH, GURBAKHSH (see Gurbakhsh, S.).

SINGH, GURDIAL SUNDER (see Gurdial, S. Singh).

SINGH, JAGDEO, GAREWAL S. (see Jagdeo, S. G. S.).

SINGH, JHUNNA (see Jhunna Singh).

SINGH, JIWAN AMRIK (see Jiwan, A. S.).

SINGH, JOGENDRA (see Jogendra Singh).

SINGH, KAPUR (see Kapur, S.).

SINGH, KARAN (see Karan Singh).

SINGH, KARTAR (see Kartar Singh).

SINGH, KHARAK (see Kharak Singh).

SINGH, KUNWAR BALBIR (see Balbir Singh, Kunwar).

SINGH, KUNWAR SHAMSHER (see Shamsher Singh, Kunwar).

SINGH, LAL RAM SHARAN (see Sharan Singh, Lal Ram).

SINGH, LALLAN PRASAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 1st July, 1912).—Educ. at Queen's Intermediate Coll., Benares, Allahabad Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. cane commr., Nov., 1940.

SINGH, MAHARAJ KUNWAR (see Mahara Kunwar Singh).

SINGH, MALIK ANUP, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bengal (insp.-gen.) (b. 22nd Sept., 1891).—First commn., 1st Nov., 1920; supt., central jail, Midnapore, Oct., 1929; ditto, presdy. jail, Jan., 1931; offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons, Bengal, Apl., 1934; and again, Nov., 1936; confd., May, 1937.

SINGH, MAN (see Man Singh).

SINGH, MOHAN (see Mohan Singh).

SINGH, NAWAB (see Nawab Singh).

SINGH, NEHPAL (see Nehpal Singh).

SINGH, PARTAB (see Partab Singh).

SINGH, PRABH (see Prabh Singh).

SINGH, PRITAM, BRAR (see Pritam Singh, B.).

SINGH, RACHPAL (see Rachpal S.).

SINGH, RAJ NARAYAN (see Raj Narayan S.).

SINGH, RAM (see Ram Singh).

SINGH, REGINALD, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 5th July, 1889).—Joined the service, May, 1909; gazetted postmaster, Jan., 1924; presdy. postmaster, Madras, Sept., 1936; presdy. postmaster, Bombay, Jan., 1938; retd., July, 1944.

SINGH, RIPDAMAN (see Ripdaman, S.).

SINGH, SADAGAR, Burma Engrg. Serv. (b. 23rd May, 1892).—Apptd. as asst. engr. P.W.D., Feb., 1920.

SINGH, SANT PRAKASH (see Sant Prakash, S.).

SINGH, SANT (see Sant, Singh).

SINGH, SARDAR (see Sardar Singh).

SINGH, SARUP (see Sarup Singh).

SINGH, SHAM (see Sham Singh).

SINGH, SHEO ADHAR (see Sheo Adhar Singh).

SINGH, SITAL (see Sital Singh).

SINGH, SUCHA, Indian C.S. (name changed to S. S. Khera, q.v.).

SINGH, TARA (see Tara Singh).

SINGH, TARLOK (see Tarlok Singh).

SINGH, TEJA (see Teja, Singh).

SINGH, TRIBENI PRASAD, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 21st Jan., 1918).—Educ. at Patna Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. pol. and appts. dept. July, 1946.

SINGH, TRIBHUBAN PRASAD (see Tribhuban Prasad Singh).

SINGHA, JAI RAJ (see Jai Raj Singha).

SINGHAL, MAHABIR SINGH, M.A., LL.B., Rai Bahadur (mag. and collr. U.P.) (b. 17th May, 1893).—Joined the U.P. civil (exec.) service, 29th Sept., 1917; mag. and collr. (confd.), July, 1944.

SINHA, ALAKH KUMAR, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., late Indian Police (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 29th June, 1884).—Joined the service, 21st Feb., 1908; asst. supt., Jan., 1920; supt., Apl., 1924; O.B.E., June, 1926; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1936; awarded King's police medal, Feb., 1937; dep. inspr.-gen., Bihar, Aug., 1938; ret'd., 23rd Jan., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; dep. pres. civil selectn. bd., govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1945.

SINHA, AVADH KISHOR PRASHAD, Rai Bahadur, C.I.E., late Ind. Police (b. 16th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1921; promoted to I.P. as supt. Feb., 1935; ret'd., and re-employed as dep. pres., civil sectn. bd., govt. of India, Oct., 1945.

SINHA, BABU NIRSU NARAYAN, M.A., B.L.—Apptd. prest., Bihar and Orissa legisl. council, 21st Jan., 1931; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1934 to March, 1937; actg. vice-prest., ditto, Oct., 1934.

SINHA, BHUVANESHWAR PRASHAD (b. 1st Feb., 1899).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, Oct., 1943.

SINHA, HARNANDAN PRASAD, Indian Serv. of Engrs. (b. 3rd Jan., 1906).—Apptd. as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., Jan., 1929; transfed. to the central public works dept., 1932; exec. engr., July, 1941.

SINHA, MADAN MOHAN (see Madan, M. S.).

SINHA, MITHILESH KUMAR, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 30th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 23rd Apl., 1928; supt., March, 1940.

SINHA, RAM NARAYAN, R.B., Indian Police, Bihar and Orissa (b. 1st Feb., 1900).—Apptd. dep. supt., Nov., 1923; prom. to I.P. as supt., Jan., 1940; awarded Indian police medal.

SINHA, RAMA AKHANDALESHVAR PRASHAD, Indian Police (b. 29th Jan., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar, on probn., 27th Jan., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937.

SINHA, RAMANI MOHAN, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bengal) (b. 19th Jan., 1886).—Ret'd., Jan., 1941.†

SINHA, ROMANI MOHAN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr. (Signals)), (b. 22nd March, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., 1st Nov., 1919; dist. signal engr., E.I. Rly., March, 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr. (signals), E.I. Rly., Nov., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935.

SINHA, RAMESHWAR PRASAD, (b. 12th March, 1904).—Joined Indian Sch. of mines as prof. of mining engng., 1st Feb., 1937; on depn. under govt. of Cent. Provs. and Berar from Apl., 1945.

SINHA, SHIVA PRASAD.—Apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, Sept., 1944.

SINHA, SUDHIR KUMAR, Indian Police (b.

Jan., 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 1st March, 1932; confd., Dec., 1935.

SINHA, HON. TARU, B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 9th Feb., 1899).—Joined the service as offir. in cl. II, Oct., 1922; prmted, cl. I, Nov., 1941; promoted comm. contr., Feb., 1945.

SINTON, JOHN ALEXANDER, V.C., O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 2nd Dec., 1884).—Ret'd., Aug., 1938.*

SIRAJUDDIN AHMAD, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 1st Nov., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 29th June, 1925; offg. supt., May, 1929; and again, March, 1933; confd., Sept., 1939.

SIRCAR, SIR NRIPENDRA NATH, K.C.S.I., Barr., Bengal Judicial Dept.—Member, govr.-gen.'s exec. council, May, 1934 to Apl., 1939.*

SIRKAR, SATYA CHARAN, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. offr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 1st Dec., 1897).—Joined service, Nov., 1919; asst. dist. traffic supt., junior scale, Nov., 1921; divl. transpn. (traffic commercial) offr., senior scale (prov.), Jan., 1939; confd., July, 1939.

SITAL PRASAD, B.Sc., late Inc. Tax Dept. (b. 7th May, 1892).—Apptd. asst. commr., United Provs., June, 1934; offr. on spec. duty cent. bd. of rev., Jan., 1940; dir. of insp., inc.-tax, Sept., 1940; ret'd., Jan., 1943.

SITAL SINGH, M.A., Sardar Bahadur, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 10th March, 1886).—Ret'd., March, 1941.†

SITZLER, EDWARD ALBERT.—Name changed by Deed Poll to SEATON, G.V.

SIVARAMA KRISHNAN, SESHIAH, B.S., Indian Audit Department (b. 16th Apl., 1895).—Joined the service, 7th Aug., 1916; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, Sept., 1936; special telephone rev. acctg. offr., Jan., 1937; asst. acct.-gen., Central Provs., Aug., 1938; dep. acct.-gen., Central Provs., Nagpur, March, 1939; Rao Bahadur, June, 1939; dep. acct.-gen., posts and tels., Madras (in transit), Feb., 1942.

SIVARAMAN, BALARAM, O.B.E., B.A., Ind. C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 10th Jan., 1912).—Educ. at Christian Coll. Sch., Madras Univ. of Madras, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; posted to Orissa, Aug., 1937; served under govt. of India, pol. dept. at Ranpur Darbar, Feb.-March, 1939; O.B.E., June, 1945; sec. to govt. of Orissa, supply and transpt. dept., Mar., 1946; inspr. of supply offices, Oct., 1944; dep. sec. and cloth contr. Sept., 1945; sec. dept. of supply and transp. and contr., ditto, March, 1946.

SIVA RAMAN, MANAKKAL SUNDARALINGAM IYER, Indian C.S. (dep. dir. resettlmt. and emplmt. Madras) (b. 9th May, 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; sub-collr., Sept., 1932; spec. settlement offr., Apl., 1937; sub-collr. and jt. mag. Aug., 1937; collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1942; dep. dir. resettlmt. and employmt., Feb., 1946.

SIVASANKAR, TUMKAR, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and dist. mag. Madras) (b. 14th Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Mysore, Victoria Univ., Manchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1929; offg. dep. commr. of excise, Madras, Apl., 1934; sec., cent. road traffic bd., Apl., 1938; dep. sec.,

devel. dept., March, 1941; addtl. sec., ditto, March, 1943; sec., ditto, June, 1943.

SIVASANKARA MENON, KUMAR PADMA, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Oct., 1898).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to resdt., Hyderabad, Feb., 1925; under-sec. to C.C. and A.G.G. in N.W.F.P., May, 1927; dist. judge, Peshawar, Apl., 1929; agent to the govt. of India in Ceylon, Oct., 1929; dist. and sess. judge, Peshawar, Aug., 1934; offg. addl. dep. sec. to the govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, June, 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. dep. sec., ditto, Sept., 1935; addl. dep. sec. to the govt. of India for. and pol. dept., Apl., 1936; ditto, external affairs dept., Apl., 1937; relief commr. for Baluchistan in addn., Apl., 1936; pol. agent, Zhob, Nov., 1937; Dewan, Bharatpur State, Apl., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; pol. agt., W. Rajputana States, May, 1943; on spec. duty, govt. of India, external affairs dept., July, 1943; agt.-genl. for India, in China, Sept., 1943; again, July, 1944 and again, Sept., 1945.

SIVASWAMI AIYAR, SIR PAZHAMANHERI SUNDARAM AIYAR, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., B.L., member of exec. council, Madras, Feb., 1912 to Feb., 1917.*

SKEMP, FRANK WHITTINGHAM, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 13th Dec., 1880).—Retd., July, 1941.†

SKIDMORE, DAVID LITTLE, India Office (actg. hr. exec. offr. acct.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 14th March, 1920).—Apptd. exec. offr. 20th Sept., 1939; on mil. duty to Aug., 1946; actg. hr. exec. offr. (in absentia), April, 1946.

SKINNER, ARTHUR FREDERICK, Office of High Commr. for India (higher executive offr.—store dept. (b. 28th May, 1887).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 22nd Sept., 1906; on mil. service from Dec., 1916 to Feb., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. offr., Apl., 1922; actg. higher exec. offr., June, 1929; confd., Nov., 1932; actg. princpl. ditto, Jan., 1940; actg. contr., July, 1941.

SKRINE, SIR CLARMONT PERCIVAL, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Feb., 1888).—Educ. at Winchester, New Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 25th Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, Oct., 1915; H.B.M.'s vice-consul at Kerman, Oct., 1916; H.B.M.'s consul for Kerman and Persian Baluchistan, Dec., 1917; prob., pol. dept., Jan., 1918; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Jan., 1920; pol. agt., Quetta, Apl., 1921; H.M.'s consul-general, Kashgar, July, 1922; sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Punjab States, July, 1926; H.B.M.'s Consul for Sistan and Kain, Apl., 1927; pol. agt. and dep. commr., Sibi, March, 1929; pol. agt., Kalat, March, 1932; and again, Dec., 1934; rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, Aug., 1934; O.B.E., Nov., 1935; offg. resdt., Madras States, Nov., 1936; confd. as 2nd class resdt., Nov., 1938; on depn., 1941; consul-gen., Khorasan, Oct., 1944; kt. Jan., 1946; addl. counsellor, Brit. Embassy, Tehran, Mar., 1946.

SLADE, MEAD, C.I.E., late Indian C.S., (b. 25th Jan., 1894).—Educated at Ilminster

School and University College, London; on mil. service from Sept., 1914 to Oct., 1923; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; and asst. to commr. of settlmnt. and land records, May, 1925; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1927; confd., July, 1932; asst. collr., customs, Calcutta, Apl., 1928; offg. collr., ditto, May, 1930; ditto, Rangoon, Nov., 1932; ditto, Bombay, Feb., 1934; offg. jt. sec. to the govt. of India, dept. of commerce, June, 1936; temp. jt. sec., ditto, March, 1937; member, council of state, March, 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938; member, central bd. of rev. and ex-officio jt. sec. to the govt. of India, finance dept. (central revs.), Oct., 1938; ret'd., 1946.

SLADEN, JOSEPH MAURICE, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 22nd Feb., 1896).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 27th October, 1920; arrived, 2nd December, 1920, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. asst. pte. sec. to govr., May to Nov., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., and pol. agent, March, 1925; asst. commr. in Sind and Sindhi translator to govt., March, 1928; jt. sec., Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; minister, Khairpur State, May, 1932; collr., March, 1936; sec., home dept., Bombay, March, 1938; offg. ch. sec., govt. of Sind, Jan., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; ret'd., Oct., 1946.

SLARK, J. W. A., (b. 23rd June, 1896).—Joined U.P. civil service, March, 1918; mag. and collr. (confd.), Jan., 1946.

SLATER, ALFRED, M.A., late Mil. Accts., Dept., (b. 25th June, 1887).—Retd., 2nd Jan. 1942.†

SLATER, OWEN, M.C., Col. R.E., Survey of India (dir.) (b. 19th June, 1890).—First commn. dated 23rd July, 1910; asst. supt. (proby.), 20th Apl., 1920; confd., Oct., 1922; supt., May, 1928; dir., May, 1939.

SLATER, RICHARD MERCER KEENE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 27th May, 1915).—Educ. at Eton, Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 13th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr. under sec., pol. dept., Apl., 1942; sub-divl. offr., Apl., 1944.

SLATTERY, JOHN, C.B.E., late Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 28th June, 1886).—Retd., May, 1937; chrmn., pub. serv. commn., Punjab and N.W.F. Prov., May, 1937; C.B.E., Jan., 1942.

SLEEM, M., Barr. (advocate-general, Punjab) (b. 14th Jan., 1892).—Apptd. advocate-general, 11th Feb., 1938.

SLOAN, SIR TENNANT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th Nov., 1884).—Educ. at Glasgow Academy and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 25th Nov., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1915; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, April, 1917; city mag., Lucknow, July, 1917; temp. addl. under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Sept., 1917; spl. duty, home dept., Nov., 1917; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., May, 1918; on depn. to England, May to Oct., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., United Provs., March, 1921; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., March, 1924; jt. sec., ditto, Apl., 1925; sec. to govt. rev. and public wks. dept. (B. and R.), U.P., March, 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; services placed at

disposal of govt. of India, home dept., May, 1932; jt. sec. to the govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1933; offg. sec., ditto, June, 1935; C.S.I., Jan., 1936; settlmt. commr., Oct., 1936 to July, 1939; commr., Jan., 1939; confd., May, 1942; advr. to the govr., United Provs., Nov., 1939; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1942; retd., May, 1945.

SMALL, GERALD AGNEW, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Assam) (b. 31st Aug., 1886).—Retd., 1st Sept., 1941.

SMALLWOOD, ROBERT, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (b. 25th Aug., 1893) (mil. accts. dept.).—Service in the Indian Army up to 25th July, 1935; joined mil. accts. dept. as an offr. in Class II, July, 1935; fin. sec., F.O.C., R.I.N., July, 1940; reverted to mil. accts. dept., Sept., 1941.

SMART, ARTHUR, India Office (actg. hr. exec. offr., mil. dept.) (b. 16th March, 1897).—Apptd. cler. offr., Sept., 1921; actg. exec. offr., Jan., 1939; actg. hr. cler. offr. Apl., 1939; actg. hr. exec. offr., Sept., 1942.

SMART, COLIN N. B., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly) (b. 18th Feb., 1885).—Retd., Feb., 1942.†

SMART, ROBERT BROUGHTON, B.A., Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 9th Apl., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 17th Jan., 1930; confd., Jan., 1932; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; dist. supt., 1946.

SMELLIE, BENJAMIN ALEXANDER, O.B.E., B.A., LL.B., late Central Provs. C.S. (b. 26th March, 1890).—Retd., Mar., 1943.†

SMITH, ARCHIBALD CLIFFORD, Indian Police (princl., p.t. school, Burma) (b. 9th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 7th Feb., 1921; dist. supt., June, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Feb., 1937; princpl., p.t. sch., Dec., 1936; transf. to mil. dept., Dec., 1939.

SMITH, BERTRAM GILBERT, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 4th Apl., 1883).—Retd., March, 1938.*

SMITH, CECIL CURTIS HAROLD, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Aug., 1898).—First commn. dated 26th Nov., 1915; in mil. employ up to 5th Apl., 1925; asst. pol. agent, Chilas, July, 1926; asst. commr., Thal, Sept., 1930; asst. commr., Thal sub-divn., Apl., 1931; dep. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1932; pol. agent, Kurrum, June, 1935; dep. commr., Peshawar, March, 1938; pol. agt. in Zhob, Nov., 1940; sec. to resdt., Punjab States, Oct., 1941; ch. min., Nabha State, Sept., 1944; ditto Rewa State 1945.

SMITH, CHARLES HAROLD, O.B.E., M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret.) (b. 19th June, 1883).—Retd., March, 1938.*

SMITH, DANIEL RICHARD, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Jan., 1897).—First commn., dated 4th June, 1915; in mil. employ prior to May, 1923; asst. pol. agt., Bahrein, June, 1924; vice-consul, Duzdap, Nov., 1924; asst. for Mekran to pol. agt. in Kalat, Feb., 1927; pol. agt., Chagai, May, 1929; British trade agt. at Gyantse and asst. to pol. offr. in Sikkim, Sept., 1929; under-sec. to agt. to govr.-gen., Rajputana, March, 1930; offr. on special duty, Mirpir, June, 1932; pte. sec. to Maharajah of Rewa, Oct., 1932; pol. agt., Harasti and Tonk, Oct., 1933; asst. to resdt., Kashmir, March, 1934; pol. agt., Sabar Kantha Agency, Oct., 1937; pol. agt. and dep. commr., Sibi, Nov.,

1938; consul, Zahidan, Oct., 1944; offg. consul-gen., Meshed, Aug., 1946.

SMITH, EDWIN CHARLES ALLAN, L.R.F.P.S., L.R.C.P. & S., D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Bombay) (b. 16th Nov., 1887).—First commn., 1st July, 1921; offg. asst. dir. of public health, Rangoon, Aug., 1926; offg. supt., central mental hosp., Yeravda, Nov., 1928; confd., Aug., 1929.

SMITH, EDWIN DENSHAM, late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1886).—Retd., Feb., 1940.†

SMITH, SIR ERIC CONRAN, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Corpus Christi, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1915; and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; temply. employed in judl. and public depts., India Office, from April to June, 1919; pte. sec. to govr. of Madras, Apl., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1924; on for. serv. as commr. for Corp. of Madras., Oct., 1928; sec., local self-govt. dept., Madras, Oct., 1931; collr (provl. subs.), Feb., 1933; addl. jt. sec. to govt. of India in the reforms office, Oct., 1934; collr., Dec., 1934; offg. pte. sec. to Viceroy, June, 1935; addtl. jt. sec., govt., of India, reforms office, Aug., 1935; member, council of state, Apl., to May, 1936; jt. sec., govt. of India, reforms office, March, 1937; addtl. ditto, home dept., Feb., 1938; confd. as jt. sec., home dept., Apl., 1938; jt. sec. to G.G. (public), June, 1938; sec., ditto, Oct., 1939; sec., home dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1942; on spec. duty, home dept., Apl., 1945; sec. govt. of India, def. dept., July, 1945; reforms commr. and sec. to govr.-gen. (public), Aug. 1945; sec. govt. of India, war transport dept., Sept., 1945; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1946; member exec. council govr.-gen., July to Sept., 1946; sec. trspt. dept., Sep. 1946.

SMITH, FRANCIS ARTHUR, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 12th Jan., 1903).—Asst. loco. supt., Jan., 1926; dist. loco. supt., July, 1938; M.B.E., June, 1946.

SMITH, FRANCIS WILLIAM HEAD, C.I.E., Burma Office (asst. under-sec. of state for Burma) (b. 18th Aug., 1886).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. junior clerk, India Office, 2nd Nov., 1909, and attached to fin. dept.; resdt. clerk, Dec., 1911, to Dec., 1912; asst. sec. to Rl. Commn. on Indian fin. and currency, May, 1913 to Feb., 1914; reading clerk to council, July, 1913 to Apl., 1914; transf. to public works dept., Apl., 1914; senr. clerk, May, 1917; services temply. lent to mil. dept., Dec., 1914; actg. asst. sec., public works dept., Jan., 1920; confd., March, 1921; actg. joint sec., mil. dept., May, 1923; dep. clerk of the council, Apl., 1924; sec. to Rl. commn. on agric. in India, 1926-28; actg. sec., services and general dept., 1st Dec., 1928; confd., June, 1929; C.I.E., March, 1929; actg. asst. under-sec. of state, Jan., 1940; ditto for Burma, Dec., 1943; lent to min. of lab., July, 1940 to Dec., 1943, asst. under-sec. of state for Burma, Nov., 1945.

SMITH, FRANK LOVELL, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Perse Sch., Cambridge, and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and ccllr.; dep. supt. of census oprns., U.P., March,

1931; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; confd., Jan., 1940.

SMITH, FREDERICK WILLIAM JAMES, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offr., store dept.) (b. 25th Dec., 1893).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store depot, 6th Aug., 1914; on mil. service from Jan., 1916 to Feb., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. offr., Apr., 1922; actg. higher exec. offr., June, 1940.

SMITH, G. CORLEY, M.B.E., Commerce Dept., Govt. of India (asst. sec.) (b. 12th June, 1895).—Joined the service, 1st Apr., 1913; supt., commerce dept., Nov., 1928; offg. asst. sec., commerce dept., Sept., 1930; confd., June, 1932; offg. admin. offr., central p.w.d., Apr., 1933; and again, Apr., 1935; asst. sec., comm. dept., Nov., 1935; offr. on special duty in Bombay Customs House, Dec., 1938, to Apr., 1939.

SMITH, GEOFFREY WILLIAM JOHN, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 30th March, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Assam, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; asst. to dep. commr., Naga Hills, Sept., 1937 to March, 1939; asst. comdt., 4th bn., Assam rifles, Apr., 1940, to Sept., 1941; asst. to d.i.g., Jan., 1944.

SMITH, HARRY PERCIVAL, C.I.E., B.A., Ind. Forest Service (consvr., Assam) (b. 26th May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 22nd March, 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1923; on depn. to Andamans from Jan., 1926 to May, 1929; p.a. to consvr. of forests, Assam, Oct., 1935; timber dir., govt. of India, Apr., 1942, to Oct., 1943; senr. consvr., Nov., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

SMITH, SIR HENRY MONCRIEFF, Kt., C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian C.S. (N.W. Provs.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1873).—Retd., Dec., 1932.*

SMITH, SIR HENRY SCOTT, Kt., Indian C.S. (ret'd.) (Punjab).—Retd., May, 1925.*

SMITH, HENRY WRAY SANDES, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th Apr., 1884).—Retd., Apr., 1939.*

SMITH, HERBERT, CECIL, C.B.E., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 27th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 21st Dec., 1915; dep. consvr., Jan., 1922; offg. consvr., Feb., 1936; confd., Feb., 1938; ch. consvr. (confd.) Dec., 1942; C.B.E., June, 1945; on I.P.R. from June, 1946.

SMITH, IAN WILLIAM KNIGHTON, B.A., B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Oct., 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1939.

SMITH, SIR JAMES COWLISHAW, Kt., C.I.E., B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 14th Apr., 1873).—Retd., Dec., 1931.*

SMITH, JOHN EDWARD, Indian Police (b. 14th Oct., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 14th Dec., 1927; offg. supt., May, 1934; confd., Oct., 1940.

SMITH, SIR JOSEPH BENJAMIN GEORGE, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Feb., 1878).—Retd., Feb., 1933.*

SMITH, MERVYN FRANK, Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 4th Dec., 1891).—Joined the service, Oct., 1908; asst. supt., Oct., 1941; asst. dep. dir. genl., Jan., 1944; ch. supt., March, 1946.

SMITH, NORMAN PERCIVAL ARTHUR, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Police (dir. intell. bureau, home dept., govt. of India) (b. 30th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service, Nov., 1912; supt. of police, June, 1924; offg. dep. dir., intell. bureau June, 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Nov., 1935; cent. intell. offr., Bombay Presyd., March, 1936; awarded King's police medal,

Jan., 1938; dep. commr. police, Bombay, Aug., 1938; jt. sec., home dept., Bombay, Oct., 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; dir. intell. bureau, home dept., govt. of India, 1945.

SMITH, RAPHL JEANRENAUD, M.B.E., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 7th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1923, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924; M.B.E., June, 1943.

SMITH, REV. RICHARD MARMADUKE LANGDALE, B.A., late Indian Ecclesi. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 29th Oct., 1884).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

SMITH, ROBERT GEORGE, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th July, 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 8th Feb., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933; exec. engr., Feb., 1940.

SMITH, THOMAS ROBERT, India Office, (actg. hr. exec. offr., mil. dept.) (b. 20th July, 1898).—Apptd. cler. offr. 22nd March, 1920; actg. hr. ditto, May, 1938; confd., July, 1938; actg. hr. exec. offr., Nov., 1945.

SMITH, WALTER ROBERT GEORGE, C.I.E., Indian Police (member, fed. pub. serv. commn.) (b. 5th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1908, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, March, 1921; dep. commr. of police, Bombay, Feb., 1932; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1932; offg. commr. of police, Bombay, July, 1933; confd., Apr., 1935; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; C.I.E., May, 1937; memb., fed. pub. serv. commn., Feb., 1942.

SMITH, WILLIAM ROSE, B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys., (b. 19th May, 1886).—Retd., Dec., 1942.

SMITH-PEARSE, THOMAS LAWRENCE HOWARTH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., Rajkumar Coll., Raipur) (b. 15th July, 1893).—Educl. at Marlborough and St. John's Coll., Oxford; vice-princl., Rajkumar Coll., Raipur, Feb., 1921; princpl., ditto, July, 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

SMITHERMAN, FRANK, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 13th Oct., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Burma, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935; civ. aff. serv., Nov., 1945; dist. supt., 1946.

SMITZ, WILLIAM BENNETT, Burma Engrg. Serv. (b. 16th July, 1899).—Apptd. asst. engr. P.W.D., Jan., 1924; apptd., to Burma Engrg. Serv. (cl. I), Oct., 1930; exec. engr. Oct., 1936.

SMYTH, ARCHIBALD ADAMS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 12th June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Madras, Sept., 1925; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1931; confd., Sept., 1934; awarded K.-I.-H. medal, cl. II, Jan., 1936; served with army dept. from Dec., 1939.

SMYTH, DAVID ALEXANDER, C.I.E., C.B.E., late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 24th Nov., 1886).—Retd., Nov., 1941.†

SMYTH, JOHN WILLIAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Sind) (b. 13th May, 1880).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

SMYTH, SIR SAMURL ANDREW, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 18th March, 1877).—Retd., March, 1931.*

SMYTH, SAMUEL, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 10th June, 1899).—Date of first commn., 4th Aug., 1927; apptd. civil surgeon (offg.), Apr., 1934; O.S.D., Mayo Hosp., Lahore, March, 1937.

SMYTHIES, EVELYN ARTHUR, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 19th Mar., 1885).—Retd., Mar., 1940.†

SNELL, OF PLUMSTEAD, BARON, HENRY SNELL, C.B.E., Parly. under-sec. of state for India, March-Aug., 1931; delegate to Indian

round table conf., 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931.

SNELSON, EDWARD ALEC ABBOTT, O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Central Provs.) (b. 31st Oct., 1904).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch., Caius Coll., Cambridge, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 9th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; sub-judge, Dec., 1931; O.B.E., Jan., 1946; legal remember. to govt., Apl., 1946.

SOARES, ALEXANDER DUARANTE, M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Ryls. (dist. medl. offr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 25th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service, 8th June, 1923; dist. medl. offr., G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1929.

SOBHAN, KONDKAR FAZLE (see Khondkar Fazle Sobhan).

SOBHISING, SADHURAM REEJHSINGANI, Rao Sahib, M.A. (Sind) (b. 1st Feb., 1892).—Joined 22nd May, 1916; held non-gaz. appts. to Sept., 1930; dep. collr. Apl., 1941; spec., offr., rev. dept., May to July, 1943; asst. to rev. commr., Aug., 1943; dir. of settlements, etc., Apl., 1945; inspr.-genl., registration, June, 1945.

SODHI, HARBHAJAN SINGH, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 24th May, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

SODHI, NARENDRA SINGH, M.C., L.R.C.P., D.T.M., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 9th Aug., 1882).—Retd., Aug., 1939.*

SOE, MAUNG AUNG, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 27th Mar., 1906).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec., fin. dept., Burma Apl., 1936; dep. commr., March, 1938, again, Apl., 1946; confd., July, 1946.

SOHONI, SHRIDHAR VASUDEV, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. sec. supply and transport dept.) (b. 18th May, 1914).—Educ. at Bombay Univ.; joined the serv., 8th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., asst. cane commr., Apl., 1942; addtl. dist. mag., Sept., 1943; dep. sec. supply and transport dept., July, 1945.

SOHONI, VISHVANATH VISHNU, B.A., M.Sc. (Bombay), Meteorological Dept. (b. 6th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service as meteorologist, 12th March, 1922; suptg. ditto, Aug., 1942; dep. dir.-genl., June, 1946.

SOKHEY, SIR SAHIBSINGH, M.A., B.Sc., M.D. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 15th Dec., 1887).—Joined, 26th July, 1913; in mil. employ to 7th Aug., 1925; apptd. supernumerary offr., Hafikine Inst., Bombay, Aug., 1925; offg. asst. dir., ditto, Feb., 1926; offg. dir., ditto, March, 1928; dir., ditto, Oct., 1932; kt., Jan., 1946.

SOLOMAN, SAMUEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 20th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1933; offg. publicity offr., Bihar and Orissa, March, 1935; dir. of devel., Orissa, Apl., 1940; mag. and collr., July, 1941; addl. sec. poll. and appts. dept. and provincial press advr., Bihar, Oct., 1943; dep. commr. and dist. mag., May, 1945.

SOM NATH, KAPUR LALA, Indian Service

of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 10th Oct., 1908).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 17th July, 1931; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., July, 1932; exec. engr., July, 1941; offr. on spl. duty, Dec., 1944 to July, 1945; under sec. to govt. P.W.D. (irrign.), Oct., 1945.

SOMAYRA, SRI BODUGURU.—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, Feb., 1939.

SONDHI, GURU DATT, B.A. (Cantab.), M.A. (Pb.), Barr., Indian Educl. Service (b. 10th Dec., 1890).—Apptd. prof. of economics, govt. coll., Lahore, 17th Sept., 1917; princpl., ditto, Sept., 1940.

SONDHI, RAM LAL (see Ram Lal, S.).

SONDHI, SUNDAR DAS, M.C., M.B., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S., Jail Dept., Punjab (supi.) (b. 25th Apl., 1890).—Retd., July, 1942.†

SONDHI, VED PALL, M.B.E., M.Sc., Geol. Survey of India, (b. 10th March, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt. (geologist) 4th Nov., 1946.

SOOD, MANOHAR LAL (sse Manohar Lal Sood).

SOOFI, MAHMOOD ALI, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 20th March, 1901).—Educ. at Punjab Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, variously, from May, 1932; confd., Nov., 1936; addtl. judge, ct. of judl. commr., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1940 to June, 1941; dist. and sess. judge, Punjab, Feb., 1942; under govt. of India from Feb., 1945.

SORBY, WILLIAM JOHN WOODCRAFT, Indian State Ryls. (divl. supt., N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Apl., 1893).—Asst. loco. supt., junr. scale, 2nd Oct., 1920; divl. mech. engr., senr. scale, Oct., 1936; divl. supt., Nov., 1944.

SORLEY, HERBERT TOWER, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. sec. to govt. of Bombay) (b. 12th Apl., 1892).—Educ. at Gr. Sch., Aberdeen, Aberdeen Univ., Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 9th Dec., 1915, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dep. commr. of salt and excise, June, 1923; criminal tribes settlt. offr., Bombay Presy. (offg.), March, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1926; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; offg. collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Feb., 1929; offg. prov. supt. of census, Bombay presdy.; collr., Apl., 1933; sec. to govt., gen. and educt. depts., Bombay, March, 1936; C.I.E., June, 1939; offg. ch. sec. Nov., 1939; offg. ch. sec. pol. and services dept., govt. of Bombay, June, 1945; C.S.I., June, 1946.

SOTHERS, DONALD BEVAN, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Sind) (b. 11th March, 1889).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1912, as asst. consvr., Bombay; on duty as asst. censor from 2nd Dec., 1914 to 11th Jan., 1919; I.A.R.O., from 23rd Apl., 1915; asst. contr., famine fodder, Jan. to Apl., 1919; dep. consvr., Apl., 1919; offg. consvr., May, 1928; and again, March, 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; offg. ch. consvr., Bombay, June, 1935; transf. to Sind, Nov., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; ret'd., Apl., 1946.

SOUTER, SIR CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 13th June, 1877).—Retd., Apl., 1937.*

SOUTHERN, REV. GERALD HOLTE BRACE-BRIDGE, M.A., late Indian Ecclesi. Estabt.

(United Provs.) (b. 1st Nov., 1889).—Retd., Feb., 1938.*

SOUTHORN, ANDREW HERBERT, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and dist. mag. Madras) (b. 2nd Feb., 1909).—Educ. at St. Edwards Sch., Oxford, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Sept., 1934; under-sec., public dept., Oct., 1940; dep. ditto, May, 1942; M.B.E., June, 1943; O.B.E., June, 1946.

SPACKMAN, WILLIAM COLLIS, M.B., Col., I.M.S. (b. 23rd Sept., 1889).—First commn., 26th July, 1913; offg. civil surg., March, 1924; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. prof'r. G.M. Cell., and supt., Bai Motlibai and Sir D. M. Petit hospitals, Bombay, July, 1927, March, 1930, and again, Nov., 1932; inspr.-gen., civ. hosp., June, 1941.

SOUTHERN, PETER STERLING, Indian Police (Bihar) (b. 28th May, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Jan., 1941.

SPARGO, FREDERICK WILSON, B.A., B.Sc., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 30th Oct., 1888).—Retd., Sept., 1940.*

SPENCE, SIR GEORGE HEMMING, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (sec., govt. of India, legis. dept.) (b. 6th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1917; addl. dist. mag., Delhi, Jan. to Nov., 1918; temp. under-sec. to govt., May, 1919; spl. duty under for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, from June, 1919; under-sec. to govt. of India, reforms office, Dec., 1919; addl. dep. sec. to govt. of India, legislative dept., Dec., 1920; offg. dep. sec., ditto, Apl., 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; sec., council of state, Nov., 1933; and Jan., 1935; offg. sec. to the govt. of India, legis. dept., June, 1934, and again, Nov., 1935; confd., Apl., 1936; sec. to the govt.-gen.'s exec. council, in addn., Aug., 1936; C.S.I., Feb., 1937; kt., June, 1939; K.C.I.E., June, 1943; member govt.-gen.'s exec. council, July, 1946 to Sept., 1946.

SPENCER, FRANK, M.B.E. (revision offr., govt. of India, def. dept.) (b. 26th Sept., 1888).—Served with reg. army from 18th Nov., 1908, to 11th March, 1924; held minis. appts. at A.H.Q. up to Dec., 1930; offr. supervisor, general staff branch, A.H.Q. (offg.), Jan., 1931; confd., March, 1931; M.B.E., Jan., 1932; apptd. revision offr., def. dept., Apl., 1938.

SPENS, SIR WILLIAM PATRICK, O.B.E., K.C. (chief justice of India) (b. 9th Aug., 1885).—Apptd., Apl., 1943.

SPILLER, THOMAS RUEBEN, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 9th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1920; princpl., New Coll., Patna, Oct., 1923; headmaster, Zila Sch., June, 1927; offg. princpl., Patna training coll., variously from May, 1933; inspr. of schs., Bihar and Orissa, variously from Apl., 1933; headmaster, Ranchi Zila Sch., again, Oct., 1933; princpl., Patna trng. coll., Apl., 1937; retd., 9th Dec., 1944.

SPITTELER, ARTHUR ERNEST, Indian Police Madras (b. 29th Oct., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt. Madras, on probn. 24th Jan., 1936; confd., Jan., 1938.

SPOONER, EDWIN GEORGE, C.I.E., (b. 12th Sept., 1898).—Sales mangr. M.S. Steel Corpn. Bengal; services lent to govt. Aug., 1941; dep.

iron and steel contr. Aug., 1941; offg. contr., Mar., 1944.

SPRATT, NOEL NEWTON, M.B.E., India Office (higher clerical offr., mil. dept.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1891).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 9th March, 1914; on mil. service from Nov., 1916, to July, 1919; higher clerical offr., Apl., 1922; actg. hr. exec. offr. Apl., 1940; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

SPRAWSON, SIR CUTHBERT ALLEN, Kt., C.I.E., M.D., M.R.C.P., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S., retd. (b. 1st March, 1877).—Retd., March, 1937.*

SPRAY, WILLIAM ALFRED, Indian State Rlys. (supt. of collieries) (b. 27th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 13th Aug., 1919; offg. variously as colliery manager from Oct., 1919; confd., Sept., 1926; supt. of collieries, July, 1938.

SPRINGFIELD, ERNEST, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 3rd Nov., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 7th Dec., 1922; offg. supt., May, 1929; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; supt., July, 1934.

SQUIRE, SIR GILES FREDERICK K.B.E., C.I.E., B.A.(Oxon.), Indian C.S., Indian Pol. Serv. (b. 12th Oct., 1894).—Educ. at Marlborough and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Aug., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, on probn., as first asst. to resdt. of Baroda, Apl., 1924; confd., March, 1925; sec. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Apl., 1926; under-sec. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Nov., 1926; sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Punjab States, Dec., 1927; ditto, W.I. States, Oct., 1929; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Nov., 1933; pol. agent, Kalat, Apl., 1934; ditto, Sibi, Dec., 1935; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, March, 1936; consul-gen. for Khorasan, Nov., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1941; on depn., 1941; minister, Kabul, July, 1943; kt., June, 1946.

SQUIRES, HOWARD JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 3rd Sept., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 21st Oct., 1928; exec. engr., Oct., 1937; dep. sec., reconstr. dept., 1944; on mil. duty, Oct., 1945 to Jan., 1946; exec. engr., Feb., 1946.

SREEKANTESWARAN, KOLAR VENKATSWAMIER, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 11th March, 1903).—Joined the service, 8th March, 1926; asst. acct.-gen., Burma, Apl., 1929; curr. offr., Bombay, Dec., 1933; dep. acct.-gen., Punjab, June, 1935; accts. offr., E.I. rly., April, 1936; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. rly., Nov., 1938; examiner, outside audit dept., Bombay, March, 1939; dep. fincl. advr., supply fin., April, 1941.

SRIHARI RAO NAYUDU, BANDI VENKATA, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.L., late inspr. gen. of Registration, Madras (b. 19th Feb., 1887).—Retd., Feb., 1942.†

SRINIVASA RAO, CHIKBALLAPUR VENKATA, C.I.E., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service, 17th March, 1914; asst. acct.-gen., Burma, Nov., 1915; asst. compt., Assam, Dec., 1915; asst. compt., India treasuries, Nov., 1916; currency offr., Rangoon, May, 1919; asst. acct.-gen., Burma, July, 1919; dep. acct.-gen., posts and tels., Madras, Jan., 1924; on spl. duty in fin. dept., March, 1925; offg. dep. contr. of rly. accts., March, 1933; addl. dep. dir. (fin.) rly. bd., Jan., 1935; offg. finl. advr., posts and tels., May, 1935; offg. dep. ch. accts. offr., Burma rlys., March, 1936;

offg. ch. ditto, Apl., 1936; on for. service as acct.-gen., Burma, Apl., 1937; auditor-gen., Burma (subst.), Feb., 1940; acct.-gen., Burma, May, 1940; dir. audit, def. serv., June, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

SRINIVASA RAO, H. (b. 16th July, 1894).—Apptd. asst. supt. Zoological Survey of India, May, 1924; in charge, research Andaman fisheries, Port Blair, Apl., 1932 to July, 1935; dep. fish. devel. advr. (marine), to govt. of India, Nov., 1944.

SRINAVASAN, KALYANA CHENNAKESAVA, M.A., F.C.S., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 26th Apl., 1896).—Joined the service, 24th March, 1921; asst. auditor, E.B. rly., Aug., 1922; on deptn. with govt. of India in connection with accounts for dairy farms, Feb., 1923; sec., rly. rates advisory cttee., May, 1926; divl. audit offr., Karachi, June, 1928; govt. exr. of accts., B.N. rly., March, 1929; dy. ch. auditor, G.I.P. rly., May, 1930; dep. govt. exr., B.B. and C.I. rly., Apl., 1931; on spl. duty under contr. of rly. accts., 1936; dep. ch. accts. offr., Jan., 1939; dep. dir. of fin., Apl., 1939; offr. on spl. duty, rly. bd., June, 1939.

SRINIVASAN, KUPPUSWAMI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 1st May, 1907).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, sub-collr., Jan., 1934; dist. and sess. judge June, 1941; ditto, (confd.) Oct., 1945.

SRINAVASAN, MANDAYAM MUDEMBAL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 15th Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 10th Nov., 1931; dep. consvr. Nov., 1936.

SRINIVASAN, SUBRAMANIA IYER, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th June, 1903).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1926; currency offr., Karachi, Sept., 1932; asst. acct.-gen., Madras, May, 1935; examiner, local fund accts., Central Provs., March, 1937; dept. acct.-gen., Central Provs., July, 1938; accts. offr., G.I.P. rly., March, 1938; ditto, E.I. rly., May, 1940; ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Dec., 1941.

SIRAMULU NAYUDU, BUNDY, late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 4th July, 1885).—Retd., July, 1940.*

SRIVASTAVA, SIR JWALA PRASAD, K.C.S.I., K.B.E., (b. 16th Aug., 1889).—Member, United Provs. legis. cl., 1926-36; legis. assemb., 1932-42; min. of educ., United Provs., 1931-36; min. fin. and indus., 1937; ch., United Provs. Simon cttee., 1928; ch., Cawnpore improve. trust, 1928-31; member, nat. def. cl., 1940-42; member, govr.-gen.'s exec. council (food), July, 1942 to July, 1946; K.C.S.I., June, 1946.

SRIVASTAVA, RAMCHANDRA, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.Sc., Industries Dept., United Provs. (dep. dir.) (b. 10th Sept., 1891).—Apptd. dep. dir. of industries, United Provs., 1st Sept., 1928; services placed at disposal of Impl. Council of Agricultural Research, for employment as sugar tech., July, 1930; dir., impl. inst. of sugar tech., Cawnpore, Oct., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

SRIVASTAVA, SHYAM MOHAN, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (b. 13th July, 1910).—Educ. at K.P. Intermediate Coll., Allahabad, Univ. of Allahabad, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., jt. mag., Nov., 1936; sec., Imp. counc. of agric. res. Feb., 1944; dep. sec.,

govt. of India, Educ. H. & L., Feb., 1945; ditto, agric. dept., Sept., 1945.

STAFFORD, PAUL, Indian Police (b. 12th Dec., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd., Sept., 1940; served in Sind from Aug., 1944, on l.p.r.

STAGG, MONTAGU, Col., R.E., O.B.E., late Mint Dept., Calcutta (b. 4th Nov., 1885).—Retd., July, 1940.*

STAGGS, MALCOLM JAMES ALLAN, M.B.E. (asst. sec. to govt. of India, defence dept.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1900).—Joined the service, June, 1919; apptd. asst. sec. to the govt. of India, defence dept. (offg.), March, 1939; confd., July, 1939; M.B.E., Jan., 1942.

STAIG, SIR BERTIE MUNRO, C.S.I., Indian C.S., attd. Indian Finance Dept. (b. 14th Aug., 1892).—Educ. at St. Andrews, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, 18th Dec., 1917, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., May, 1918, to Jan., 1919; offg. under-sec., govt. of Bengal, pol. and appts. dept., March, 1921; ditto, fin. dept., Nov., 1921; transf. to Indian audit and accts. list, Nov., 1922; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, May, 1924; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1924; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1925; finl. advr., P.W.D., and jt. sec. to govt. of Punjab, fin. dept., March, 1926; sec., hydro-electric branch enquiry cttee., Oct., 1929; sec. to govt., P.W.D. (elec.), Punjab, Apl., 1933; addl. finl. advr., mil. fin., July, 1934; finl. advr., mil. fin., March, 1935; also relief commr., Baluchistan, and jt. sec., govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1935; C.S.I., June, 1936; on spl. duty in for. and pol. dept., Sept., 1936; member, frontier watch and ward cttee., Nov., 1936; finl. commr., rlys., Apl., 1937; dep. auditor gen. in India, Aug., 1940; finl. advr., Iraq commd., Nov., 1941; kt., Jan., 1942; addtl. sec., finl. dept., June, 1942; on spl. duty, war transp., Sept., 1942; auditor-gen. of India, Oct., 1944; and again, Apl., 1945.

STALEY, HENRY SAMBLE, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 20th May, 1886).—Retd., May, 1941†

STALLARD, WILLIAM LEONARD O'BRIAN, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 29th Aug., 1908).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 28th Dec., 1928; confd., Apl., 1930; A.D.C. to govr., Orissa, Apl., 1936; supt., July, 1940.

STAMPE, SIR WILLIAM LEONARD, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 31st Oct., 1882).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

STAMPER, THOMAS HENRY GILBORN, C.I.E., F.S.I., M.C. (b. 27th Oct., 1884).—Late surveyor to govt., Bombay; retd., Oct., 1939.*

STANDEN, SIR BERTRAM PRIOR, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.).—Retd., June, 1926.*

STANFORD, JOHN KEITH, O.B.E., M.A., M.C., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 29th Apl., 1892).—Retd., March, 1938.*

STANIFORTH, WALTER HARLAND, O.B.E., Ind. Office (suptg. exec. offr. acct.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 21st June, 1886).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 15th Aug., 1907; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; actg. staff clerk, Apl., 1921; acting higher exec. offr., Apl., 1922; confd., Oct., 1925; also assessor of income tax; suptg. exec. offr., Oct., 1936; assessor of inc. tax, May, 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1946; actg. asst. acct.-gen., Nov., 1945.

STANSFIELD, REV. HERBERT RIGG, RIGG-STANSFIELD (q.v.).

STANLEY, CHARLES BENJAMIN, Burma Police (cl. I) (b. 19th Jan., 1926).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

STANLEY, RAYMOND MORTON, Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

STANGATE, VISCOUNT (formerly BENN, THE Rt. HON. W. WEDGWOOD), D.S.O., D.F.C.—Secty. of State for India, June, 1929, to Aug., 1931. Created Viscount, Jan., 1942.

STANTON, WILLIAM THOMAS, Indian State Rlys. (b. 16th May, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 15th Aug., 1919; colliery mgr., Sept., 1931; ret'd., 1944.

STAPYLTON, JOHN MILES, late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 13th Nov., 1896).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

STARTE, HORACE WILDERSPIN, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Bombay) (b. 13th March, 1887).—Retd., May, 1942.†

STAVRIDI, A.G., Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th Dec., 1899).—Joined 21 Aug., 1919; divl. supt. E.I. Rly., Oct., 1944.

STEAD, SIR CHARLES, Kt., C.B.E., M.V.O., late Indian Police Service (Punjab) (b. 30th Aug., 1877).—Retd., Aug., 1932.*

STEADMAN, JOHN WILLIAM, O.B.E., Off. of High Commr. for India (asst. ch. acctg. offr.) (b. 3rd July, 1885).—Clerk to Rl. commn. on Indian decentralisation, June, 1908, to March, 1909; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 28th March, 1909, and attached to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; clerk to royal commn. on public services, May, 1913, to Sept., 1915; asst. sec. to Jails Cttee. from July, 1919, to Sept., 1920; minor staff clerk, Nov., 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from Apl., 1921; higher exec. offr., Apl., 1922; suptg. exec. offr., June, 1927; asst. ch. acctg. offr., Nov., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

STEEN, HUGH BARKLEY, M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1895).—Apptd. examr. of surgical instruments, India Store Dept., and addl. member, medl. bd., India Office, May, 1934, to May, 1941.*

STEIGER-HAYLEY, THOMAS THEODORE (see Hayley, T. T. S.).

STEIN, ARCHIBALD HYNDMAN, O.B.E., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 11th Jan., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 21st Feb., 1924; dep. consvr., Feb., 1928; O.B.E., June, 1942; utilization offr., Apl., 1943.

STEIN, GEORGE LESLIE HAMILTON, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 4th March, 1890).—Retd., Mar. 1941.†

STEIN, JOHN ALAN, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bengal) (b. 31st Oct., 1888).—Retd., 1st May, 1944.†

STENT, PERCY JOHN HODSOLI, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 24th Nov., 1888).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

STEPHENS, CHARLES VIVIAN, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 28th Apl., 1887).—Retd., Apl., 1942.

STEPHENS, JAMES, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 6th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

STEPHENS, JOHN, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 22nd July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt., 3rd Apl., 1920; asst. transpn.

supt., Apl., 1943; transpn. supt., July, 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

STEPHENS, STANLEY DONALD, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 31st Aug., 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 3rd Dec., 1931; confd., Jan., 1934.

STEPHENS, STUART ROBERT, Burma Police (cl. I) (b. 5th Jan., 1923).—Apptd. on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

STEPHENSON, HUGH SOUTHERN, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sec. to govr., United Provs.) (b. 29th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Winchester and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. under-sec., fin. dept., govt. of India (temp.), Jan., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; asst. sec., Niemeyer finl. enquiry, Jan., 1936; addl. under-sec., home dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1936; offr. on spec. duty, ditto, Dec., 1936; under-sec. ditto, Apl., 1937; sec. to govr., United Provs., March, 1940; O.B.E., June, 1944.

STEPHENSON, REV. WILLIAM, B.A., Indian Ecclesi. Establ. (b. 4th March, 1898) (junr. chaplain, Punjab).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 15th Oct., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1929.

STEVENS, HAROLD SAMUEL EATON, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.C., M.A., Indian C.S. (ch. sec. to govt., home dept., Bengal) (b. 29th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 23rd Aug., 1920; arrived, 7th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. mag., Nov., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1926; dep. sec. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and apptt. depts., March, 1930; mag. and collr., June, 1931; offg. sec. to govt., Bengal, agric. and indus. p.w. and irrign. depts., July, 1935; confd., Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1938; spec. offr. (jute), agric. and indus. depts., May, 1940; dir., civil def., Dec., 1941; sec., home dept. and jt. ch. sec. and ch. civ. def. commr., Apl., 1942; ch. sec. and ditto, Oct., 1944; C.S.I., June, 1944; advr. to govr., Aug., 1945; ch. sec. govt. home and ch. minister's dept. (confd.), Jan., 1946.

STEVENSON, ARCHIBALD MCCORKELL, O.B.E., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 11th Aug., 1897).—Joined the serv. as offr. cl. II., Feb., 1921; A.M.A.G., July, 1929; dep. fin. advr. to sec. fin., Dec., 1940; revtd. to mil. accts. dept., Apl., 1941; promoted cl. I., Sept., 1941; comd. contr., June, 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

STEVENSON, FREDERICK, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 1st June, 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

STEVENSON, REV. GEORGE, M.A., late Indian Ecclesi. Establ.—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Burma, Dec., 1932; ret'd., July, 1940.

STEVENSON, HENRY NOEL COCHRANE, O.B.E., Burma Front. Serv. (b. 14th Nov., 1903).—Apptd. July, 1926; O.B.E., 8th June, 1944; sec. to govr., May, 1945; dir. Frontier areas administration, Mar., 1946.

STEVENSON-MOORE, SIR CHARLES JAMES, K.C.I.E., C.V.O., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Retd., Sept., 1921.*

STEWART, ANDREW CHARLES, O.B.E., Maj. I.A., Indian Polit. Serv., H.M.'s consul, Khorram-shahr, June, 1943; vice-consul, ditto (addl. charge), Sept., 1945.

STEWART, ALEXANDER DRON, C.I.E., M.B., F.R.C.S.E., Lieut.-Col. (ret'd.), late I.M.S. (b. 22nd June, 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

STEWART, CLYNE GARDEN, O.B.E., M.A., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-gen., Burma) (b. 6th July, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 4th Dec., 1909; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1926; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1932; O.B.E., June, 1932; offg. asst. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1933, to March, 1936; dir., defence bureau, govt. of Burma, Apl., 1937; dep. inspr.-gen. (confd.), Nov., 1937.

STEWART, DONALD, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 14th March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 28th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Apl., 1925; O.B.E., May, 1937; jt. timber advr. offr., fly. and def. depts., Sept., 1939, to May, 1940; dep. dir. (timber directorate), supply dept., May, 1940; dir., ditto, Aug., 1940; consvr. (confd.), July, 1942; dir.-gen. (supply) Apl., 1943 to June, 1945; apptd. presdt. for research inst. and colls., Nov., 1945; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

STEWART, SIR DOUGLAS LAW, Bart., late Indian Police Service (b. 1st June, 1878).—Retd., Nov., 1931.*

STEWART, ERNEST HENRY JAMES, M.C., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 5th June, 1893).—Retd., Sept., 1940.†

STEWART, FRANCIS WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.C., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 26th Feb., 1885).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 24th Nov., 1909, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Sept., 1916; on mil. duty from Jan., 1916, to Nov., 1919; sub-collr. and asst. commr., Oriya agency, May, 1922; offg. variously as collr. and dist. mag., Apl., 1924, to Apr., 1928; collr. and dist. mag. (prov.), Oct., 1928; confd., Aug., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; 3rd member, bd. of rev., Jan., 1938; confd., Apl., 1940; retd., 23rd Aug., 1944.

STEWART, GEORGE EDWARD, STANFORTH, M.B.E., Capt., I.M.S., (b. 2nd Oct., 1910).—First commn., 23rd Apl., 1934; in mil. employ to Jan., 1940; reserve duty, Bellary, Jan., 1940; recalled to mil. May, 1940; on mil. serv., May, 1940 to June, 1946; reserve duty, genl. hosp., Madras, June, 1946.

STEWART, GERALD PAKENHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 5th March, 1906).—Educ. at Portora Royal Sch., Enniskillen, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; president, Manipur State Durbar, Feb., 1933, to Feb., 1936; pol., agt., Manipur, Mar.-Nov., 1938; p.o.w. in Japan, Sept., 1941.

STEWART, SIR HERBERT RAY, C.I.E., F.R.C.Sc.I., D.I.C., N.D.A., (Dir. of Agric.) (b. 10th July, 1890).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; prof. of agric., Punjab Agric. Coll., Dec., 1923; actg. princpl., ditto, March, 1927; offg. dir. of agric., May, 1927; on spl. duty, dept. of agric., Dec., 1927; asst. dir. of agric., Nov., 1928; offg. dir. of agric., Apr., 1931; confd., Dec., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1939; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, educn., health and lands dept., May, 1943; agric. commr., govt. of India, May, 1943; vice-chairman, Impl. Counc. of Agric. Research, May, 1944; kt., Jan., 1946.

STEWART, JOHN AMYOT, M.B.E., Capt., I.A., Indian Pol. Serv.—Asst. commr., Charsadda, Oct., 1944; dep. commr., Mardan, Jan., 1945; under sec. to govt. post-war planning,

N.W.F.P., Aug., 1945; I.P.S. (confd.), Feb., 1946.

STEWART, PETER MCARTHUR, Barr., late Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 21st Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1907, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, Oct., 1920; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1933; confd., supt., sel. grade, July, 1933; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; princpl., police trng. sch., July, 1936, to June, 1938; ditto, pre-cadet sch., Poona, Feb., 1942; retd., Aug., 1945.

STEWART, SIR SAMUEL FINDLATER, G.C.B., G.C.I.E., C.S.I., LL.D., late India Office and Burma Office (b. 22nd Dec., 1879).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. junior clerk, 19th Oct., 1903, and served in mil. dept. to Jan., 1910; reading clerk to Council, May, 1906; pte. sec. to permt. under-sec. of state, Jan., 1910; senr. clerk, fin. dept., May, 1911; on duty in mil. dept. from Nov., 1911, to Feb., 1912, and from Nov., 1914; asst. sec., mil. dept., June, 1918; C.I.E., June, 1919; dep. sec., mil. dept., Apl., 1919; joint sec., mil. dept., July, 1920; sec. to royal commn. on the public services in India, May, 1923, to March, 1924; C.S.I., June, 1924; asst. under-sec. of state and clerk of the council, Sept., 1924; sec., Indian Statutory Commission, Nov., 1927; K.C.I.E., June, 1930; perm. under-sec. of state for India, 11th July, 1930; K.C.B., Jan., 1932; G.C.I.E., June, 1935; perm. under-sec. of state for Burma, Apl., 1937; G.C.B., June, 1939; dir.-gen. of the Ministry of Information, Sept., 1939, to Nov., 1939; sec. for spl. duties, June, 1940; retd., Dec., 1945.

STEWART, SIR THOMAS ALEXANDER, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Feb., 1888).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; on mil. service from July, 1918; actg. asst. collr., Imperial customs service, Jan., 1919; dep. commr. of rice, Rangoon, Jan., 1920; rice commr., July, 1921; offg. collr. of customs, Rangoon, Dec., 1923; collr. of customs, Madras, Oct., 1925; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1928; collr. of salt revs., May, 1932; offg. adtl. sec., commerce dept., Oct., 1932; offg. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, March, 1933; confd., March, 1934; C.S.I., June, 1935; member, council of state, Apl., 1936; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1937; member, govr.-gen.'s exec. council, Apl., 1937; chairman, cent. adv. cl. for rlys., Nov., 1937; actg. govr., Bihar, May to Sept., 1938; K.C.S.I., June, 1939; govr., Bihar, Aug., 1939; kt. of the Order of St. John, Dec., 1940; retd., 9th June, 1945.

STEWART-WILSON, SIR CHARLES, K.C.I.E., B.A., Barr. (1886), late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Retd., Aug., 1913.*

STILL, R. M. L., MAJ., Burma Medl. Serv., (b. 1st June, 1900).—Date of first commn. 17th Nov., 1930; in civ. employ Sept., 1934 to Dec., 1941; supt., mental hosp., Insein, Jan., 1946; ditto, Lahore (India), May, 1946.

STIRLING, ALEXANDER GILCHRIST, India Office (exec. offr., mil. dept.) (b. 19th May, 1916).—Apptd. exec. offr., 10th Feb., 1936; on mil. service from May, 1940 to Nov., 1945; actg. hr. exec. offr., Nov., 1945.

STIRLING, REV. JAMES CLERK PAUL, M.A., B.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt.—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Church of Scotland, May, 1931;

Madras diocese, senr. chaplain, May, 1941; on mil. duty, Nov., 1940, to Sept., 1943; presdy. senr. chaplain, Nov., 1943; retd., July, 1946.

STOCKWELL, FREDERICK RANDOLPH, Indian Police (United Provs.)—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 1st Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936, on l.p.r.

STODART, JAMES CARLYLE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th Feb., 1880).—Retd., Feb., 1940.†

STOKES, SIR HOPETOUN GABRIEL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 1st Aug., 1873).—Retd., March, 1935.*

STOKES, RICHARD CROSBY, Indian Police (supt. Bihar) (b. 20th Apr., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 26th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; offg. supt., Sept., 1933; supt. (confd.), Jan., 1942.

STONE, BERNARD SIDNEY, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offr., store dept.) (b. 23rd Jan., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 30th July, 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct., 1915; second class clerk, 29th Apr., 1920; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. offr., Apr., 1922; actg. higher exec. offr., Oct., 1939.

STONE, SIR GILBERT, Barr. (b. 6th June, 1886).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, 30th Nov., 1930; ch. justice, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936; kt., Jan., 1936; retd., 1943.

STONE, JULAND CHETWYND TALBOT WATKINS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Central Provs.) (b. 17th June, 1886).—Retd., Feb., 1941.

STONE, SIR JOHN LEONARD, O.B.E. (chief justice, high ct., Bombay).—Apptd. 1st Oct., 1943; presdt., Bombay disaster enq. commn., May to Sept., 1944; ch. justice, high ct. Bombay, Oct., 1945.

STONE, WILFRED JOHN, Indian State Ryls. (bridge engr., Burma rlys.) (b. 5th May, 1884).—Joined the service, 28th Aug., 1925; bridge engr., Jan., 1928.

STOOKS, H. J., Madras C.S. (b. 19th Oct., 1902).—Joined the service, May, 1926; dep. collr., June, 1935; on foreign service, Dec., 1938; dep. collr. July, 1938; dep. commr., Aug., 1945; offr. on spec. duty and ex-officio dep. sec. govt. of India, home dept., May, 1946.

STORK, HERBERT CECIL, C.I.E., B.A., Ind. C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 28th June, 1890).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; regr., high ct., Calcutta, appellate side, Jan., 1925; offg. first land acquiss. offr., Calcutta, Nov., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, Assam Valleys Districts, March, 1937; remembr. of legal affairs, etc., Assam, Apr., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1945.

STORR, NORMAN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Holgate Gr. Sch., Barnsley, and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; regr., high ct., Allahabad, Feb., 1939; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1940; regr. fed. ct. of India, Dec., 1943; sec. Bombay explosion inquiry commn., Apr. to Sept., 1944.

STORRS-FOX, REV. EDWIN AUBREY, M.A., late Indian Ecclesl. Establ. (Punjab) (b. 27th Jan., 1888).—Retd.†

STOTT, HUGH, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.D., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S. (surgn.-genl., Madras) (b. 18th July, 1884).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1908; apptd. surg. to H.E. the Govr. of Madras, 5th Feb., 1913; on mil. duty from Nov., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1918; profir., King George's med. coll., Lucknow, Nov., 1922; offg. princpl., ditto, Aug., 1925, and again, Aug., 1927; employed under Lucknow Univ. from Nov., 1927; inspr.-gen., civil hosps., Bihar, Nov., 1937; comdr. of the Order of St. John, Dec., 1940; surgn.-gen., Madras, Feb., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1941.

STOTT, RAYMOND, Home Dept., Govt. of India (govt. examr. of questioned documents) (b. 26th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 31st May, 1915; on mil. duty up to 17th Sept., 1920; asst. examr. of questioned documents, Sept., 1920; offg. govt. examr. of questioned documents, March, 1923; confd., June, 1926.

STOW, VINCENT AUBREY STEWART, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (principl., Mayo Coll.) (b. 27th July, 1883).—Educ. at Winchester, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; asst. master, Marlborough Coll., 1906; asst. master, Daly Coll., Indore, Feb., 1907; services placed at the disposal of the ch. commr., Cent. Provs., from 24th Aug., 1912; princpl., Rajkumar Coll., Raipur, Aug., 1912; on mil. service, 1918-19; princpl., Mayo Coll., Ajmer, July, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934.

STRACEY, ERIC LEWIS, Indian Police (Madras) (b. 28th Nov., 1920).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn. May, 1943; confd., May, 1945.

STRACEY, HUGH AUBREY ST. CLAIR, Indian Police (asst. supt.).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., 27th Jan., 1934; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938; asst. supt., Crown representative's police, Neemuch.
