STRACEY, PATRICK DONALD, Indian Forest Service (b. 31st Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 9th Nov., 1930; dep. consvr., June, 1937.

STRACEY, RALPH AUGUSTINE ST. CLAIR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 30th Aug., 1909).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Coll., Bangalore, Madras Univ., and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. pte. sec. to govr., Bengal, Feb., 1936; mag. and collr., May, 1942; dep. sec., public health, etc., dept., Dec., 1943.

STRANGE, FRANK HORACE LUMSDEN, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 12th Aug., 1884).—Retd., Aug., 1939.*

STRANGMAN, SIR THOMAS JOSEPH, Kt., B.A., LL.B., Barr.—Late advocate-genl., Bombay retd., May, 1922.*

STRATHIE, SIR DAVID NORMAN, K.C.I.E., M.A., Indian Civil Service (Madras) (b. 31st Oct., 1886).—Educ. at Glasgow Academy, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 22nd Jan., 1911, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., July, 1917; under-sec. to govt. of India, comm. and ind. dept., 1918; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras, May, 1919; commr. of inc. tax, Madras, June, 1923; offg. collr. and sec. to govt., rev. dept., June, 1928; offg. regr., co-operative societies, Dec., 1929; collr., March, 1930; confd., Oct., 1931; inspr., mun. cls. and local boards, May, 1937; commr. of labour, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; member, bd. of rev., July, 1938; ch. sec., var. from Feb.

1940; for. service under Palestine govt., Jan., 1941, to Apl., 1942; liaison offr. with Southern Army, Aug., 1942, to Jan., 1943; advr. to govr., Madras, Jan., 1943; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

STRATTON, REV. BASIL, M.A., Indian Ecclesi. Establ. (United Provs.) (junr. chaplain) (b. 27th Apl., 1906).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 15th Sept., 1935; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1937.

STREATFIELD, REV. SIDNEY FREMLYN, B.A., Ecclesi. Dept., Central Provs. (junr. chaplain) (b. 11th Oct., 1887).—Apptd. garrison chaplain on probn., 25th June, 1930; junr. chaplain, June, 1932 on l.p.r.

STREATHFIELD, TERENCE ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 18th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Sutton Valence Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Bombay, Jan., 1934; confd., June, 1934; offg. collr., June, 1936; confd., July, 1939; backward cl. offr., Sept., 1943, to Sept., 1944; and again Jan., 1945; offg. commr., excise Mar., 1945; ch. inspr. certfd. sch., June, 1945.

STREETEN, PETER GARRETT, Burma Police (Cl. I) (b. 7th Oct., 1924).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

STRELLEY, RICHARD COLLIN, late Indian Police (b. 30th June, 1905).—Retd., 1st Sept., 1941.†

STUART, SIR LOUIS, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 12th March, 1870).—Retd., March, 1930.*

STUART, MALCOLM MONCRIEFF, O.B.E., Indian Civil Service (mag. and collr. Bengal) (b. 21st May, 1903).—Educ. at Sedbergh, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Jan., 1927; arrived, Feb., 1927, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., variously from Apl., 1932; on spec. duty, rev. dept., Bengal, Jan., 1938; addtl. dep. sec., home dept., ditto, Sept., 1939, to March, 1940; mag. and collr. (confd.), Apl., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

STUART, SIR ROBERT CHARLES OCHILTREE, K.C.S.I., Maj.-Gen., R.A. (retd.).—Retd., Aug., 1919.*

STUBBS, JOHN LAWRENCE COMMERE, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. United Provs.) (b. 23rd May, 1908).—Educ. at Radley and New Coll., Oxford; joined the serv., 9th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

STUBBS, SHIRLEY GRAHAM, O.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 14th Sept., 1884).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

STUBBS, STANLEY SEVERS, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th Oct., 1891).—Retd., Oct., 1941.†

STUDDERT-KENNEDY, REV. HENRY GERALD, M.A., Indian Ecclesi. Establ. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Punjab, 7th March, 1932; junr. chaplain, March, 1934.

STUDDY, HENRY, Indian Police (supt. Bihar) (b. 20th Apl., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 22nd Dec., 1914; supt., Oct., 1923; persl. asst. to inspr.-gen., March, 1925, to Oct., 1926.

STUMPENHUSEN-PAYNE, ERNEST GEO., B.Sc., Burma C.S. (cl. I) (b. 18th June, 1917).—Educ. Westminster City Sch. and Birbeck Coll., and London Univ.; apptd. on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

STURGIS, GERALD CLARE, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 4th Sept., 1892).—Retd., Apl., 1938.*

SU, U THEI, Burma Agric. Serv. (b. 2nd Jan., 1903).—Apptd. Oct., 1928; dep.-dir. agric., May, 1938; civ. aff. serv. June, 1945; dep. dir. agric., Sept., 1945.

SUBBA RAO, MYSORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 25th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Sch. of Economics, London, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; mag. and collr., Orissa, March, 1937; dir. of devel., etc., May, 1939; provl. press advr., Sept., 1939.

SUBBARAYAN, VIRARAGHAVA, Indian Police Madras (b. 28th Nov., 1902).—Joined the serv. as asst. supt., Madras, 12th Apl., 1926; offg. dist. supt. Apl., 1931; confd. 1942; asst. supt., Dec., 1943.

SUBBAYANAYUDU, RAMISETTI, Diwan Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. March, 1885).—Retd., Mar., 1940.†

SUBERWAL, BANKE LAL, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bengal) (b. 14th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1913; asst. engr., Dec., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. supt. engr., June, 1933; confd., Jan., 1936; retd., Aug., 1944; re-apptd. temp. ch. engr., Aug., 1944.

SUBHEDAR, LAKSHMIKANT SADASHEO, B.A., LL.B., late Income Tax Dept., Central Provs. (b. 2nd Dec., 1889).—Retd., July, 1938.*

SUBHEDAR, TRIMBAK RAGHUNATH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 22nd Nov., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 29th Jan., 1931; confd., Apl., 1933.

SUBRAHMANYA AYYAR, CHANDRASEKHARA, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 8th Oct., 1885).—retd., Oct., 1940.†

SUBRAMANYA AYYAR, TRIPUNITTURA SUBRAMANI, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 11th June, 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1922; asst. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, Oct., 1926; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, March, 1927; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. rly., June, 1930; asst. ch. auditor, E.I. rly., Nov., 1930; dep. govt. exr., B. and N.W. and R.K. rlys., July, 1933; ditto, S.I. rly., Oct., 1937; dep. acct.-gen., Madras, Apl., 1936; dep. govt. examiner of accts., S.I. rly., Nov., 1937; govt. examiner of accts., M. & S.M. rly., Feb., 1938; dep. acct.-gen., Madras, Sept., 1938; dep. ch. accts. offr., N.W. rly., Nov., 1940; offg. var., June, 1943 to 1945; gen. list. cl. I (prov.), Nov., 1945; Indian Audit and Accts. serv., cl. I (prov.), Sept., 1945.

SUBRAHMANYAM, ATHAMANAKOTTAI, M.B.E., M.A. (b. 26th June, 1901) (military accounts dept.).—Joined service as an offr. in cl. II, Apl., 1926; M.B.E., June, 1941; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1942.

SUBRAHMANYAM, NARAYANASWAMY, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 20th Sept., 1912).—Educ. at Municipal High Sch., Mayavaram, and Madras Univ.; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., March, 1939; asst. commr. civil supplies, Trichinopoly, Mar., 1946; dep. ditto, June, 1946.

SUBRAHMANYAN, MANGUDY, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 5th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1910; asst. acctnt.-genl., May, 1911; dep. ditto, July, 1924;

offg. dep. auditor-gen., March, 1929; acctnt.-gen., March, 1929; acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1932; ditto, Bengal, March, 1933; ditto, Cent. Provs., Apl., 1938; acctnt.-gen., Cent. revs., Apl., 1938; acctnt.-gen., Posts and Tels., Oct., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1943.

SUBRAHMANYAN, NATESA, B.A., Indian C.S. (name changed to MANI, N. S. (q.v.)).

SUBRAMANIAN, KAVASSERI NARAYANA IYER, O.B.E., M.A., Ind. C.S. (asst. commr. Cent. Provs.) (b. 4th June, 1909).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec., July, 1938; dep. sec. to govt., Aug. 1st, 1944; O.B.E., June, 1946.

SUBRAMANIAN, P. R., M.A., (b. 25th June, 1910).—Held non-gaz. posts in commerce dept. and on foreign service with Ministry of War Transport to 1944; held minor gaz. appts. in comm. dept. to Nov., 1945; temp. ch. supt. comm. dept., Nov., 1945; on depn. to U.K., and U.S.A., May, 1946 to July, 1946; asst. sec., govt. of India, comm. dept., July, 1946.

SUBRAMANIAN, S., M.A.—Apptd. statstn. under the econ. advr. to the govt. of India, Apl., 1939.

SUBRAMANIAN, VAIDYANATHIER VENKATA, Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 17th May, 1908).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; sub-collr., July, 1932; addl. under-sec., public dept., Sept., 1939; dep. sec., educn. dept., Feb., 1941; dep. sec., fin. dept., Apl., 1944; collr. and dist. mag., Feb., 1946.

SUBRAMANYAN, MINAKSHISUNDARAM VENKATA, B.A., Indian Civil Service (collr., Madras) (b. 8th July, 1900).—Educ. at Madras University, and Christ's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924; sub-collr., Aug., 1926; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1931; sec. educn. and public health dept., Apl., 1942; again Apl., 1943.

SUD, AMAR NATH, Rai Bahadur, B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th July, 1890).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 9th Sept., 1913; asst. engr., March, 1915; exec. engr., 9th March, 1924; dep. ch. engr. (prov.), July, 1940; ret'd., Jan., 1946.

SUDHINDRA KUMAR HALDAR (see Haldar, S. K.).

SUFTI, MOHAMMED HUSAIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 13th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Pindi Bhatian, Punjab Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 6th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., and in Sind as ditto and mag.; offg. var. from Aug., 1944; regional grain purchasing offr., upper Sind, March, 1946.

SUHRAWARDY, SIR ZAHHADUR RAHIM ZAHID, Kt., Barr.-at-law, late Judicial Dept., Bengal (b. 27th Nov., 1870).—Ret'd., Nov., 1931.*

SUJAN SINGH, BEDI, Sardar Sahib Sardar, M.A. (asst. sec. govt. of India, dept. agric.) (b. 2nd Feb., 1895).—Educ. at St. Stephen's Coll., Delhi, joined the service, Jan., 1918; held non-gaz. appts. from Jan., 1928 to Oct., 1938; supt., dept. of educn., health and lands, Oct., 1938; confd. Jan., 1941; asst. sec., govt. of India, March, 1944; confd.

SUKH ANAND, Rai Sahib, Diwan, B.A., (Punjab) (sub-divl. offr. Punjab) (b. 17th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service, Jan., 1915; Munsi, Apl., 1921; sub-judge, Jan., 1923; extra asst. commr., May, 1927; sub-divl., offrs., Jan., 1940; offg. var. as dep. commr.

SUKHDEO PRASAD, TANDON, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 10th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1914; asst. engr., Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1923; confd., Sept., 1924; ret'd., Aug., 1945.

SUKTHANKAR, YESHWANT NARAYAN, C.I.E., B.A., LL.D., Barr., Indian C.S. (secty., commerce dept., govt. of India) (b. 24th Aug., 1897).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and at Cambridge Univ. (non-collegiate); joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 16th Dec., 1922, and served in Cent. Provs. as asst. commr. offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1927; under-sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., Oct., 1932; offg. rev. secty. to govt., Cent. Provs., June, 1933; dep. India trade commr., July, 1934; actg. Indian trade commr., Oct., 1934; and again, Sept., 1935; dep. secty., commerce dept., govt. of India, July, 1937; dep. commr. (confd.), Aug., 1937; tea contr. for India, Nov., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1941; jt. sec., commerce dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1943; offg. sec., ditto, Dec., 1945, and again, Mar., 1946; addl. sec., ditto, Apl., 1946; sec., ditto, Aug., 1946.

SULAIMAN, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Sulaiman).

SULE, RAMCHANDRA GOVIND, C.I.E., L.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supt. engr., Bombay) (b. 17th May, 1888).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., 1st Apl., 1912; asst. engr., Apl., 1913; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., Apl., 1922; supt. engr., Apl., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1943.

SULLIVAN, BASIL MARTIN, C.I.E., O.B.E., F.R.I.B.A., A.M.T.P.I. (b. 30th Aug., 1882).—Late architect to the Punjab govt.; ret'd., 1st Sept., 1938.*

SULLIVAN, MICHAEL JOHN KENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Andaman and Nicobar Is.) (b. 22nd Mar., 1913).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Sept., 1941; offg. excise commr., Nov., 1945; dep. commr., Andaman and Nicobar Ist.

SULTAN ALI, NOON, MALIK, Indian Agric. Service (dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. 23rd March 1895).—Joined the service as supy. dep. dir. of agric., 2nd Sept., 1918; dep. dir. of agric., June, 1924; dir., ditto, May, 1943.

SUMER CHAND, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., United Provs.) (b. 14th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as tempy. engr., 9th Sept., 1918; asst. engr. (provl. service), Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1923; offg. exec. engr., May, 1926; confd., Oct., 1930; offg. suptg. engr., July, 1942; confd., Oct., 1944.

SUNDAR DAS, KHANGAR, Rai Bahadur, Lala, B.A., Ind. Serv. of Engrs. (exec. engr. Punjab) (b. 24th Nov., 1897).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1930; suptg. engr., Nov., 1944; Rai Bahadur, June, 1945; offr. on spec. duty, March to Nov., 1945; and again from Dec., 1945.

SUNDAR SINGH, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 7th March,

1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 8th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Apl., 1932.

SUNDARA RAJ, B., M.A., Ph.D. (b. 25th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1912, as piscicultural asst., ditto expert 12th Apl., 1917; asst. dir. of fisheries (sub. pro. tem.), 7th Feb., 1918; asst. dir. of fisheries, inland, 1st Jan., 1919; actg. dir. of fisheries, 7th Aug., 1923; dir. of fisheries (perm.), 1st Nov., 1924.

SUNDARA RAJAN, K.S., Mil. Accts. Dept., (b. 17th Dec., 1914).—Joined the service 30th Aug., 1938; asst. fincl. advr., mil. fin., Sept., 1944.

SUNDARACHARI, RANGASWAMI, Dewan Bahadur, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 7th Apl., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

SUNDARAM, RAO BAHADUR GANGADARA, M.A., A.M.I.E.E., (ch. engr. elec. dept., Madras (b. 14th Nov., 1896)).—Joined the service, 4th Jan., 1926, as elec. asst.; confd. as exec. engr., Apl., 1933; suptg. engr. (confd), Feb., 1940; ch. engr., Dec., 1944.

SUNDARAM, KUTHER VAIDYANATHA KALYAN, B.A. (Hons.) (Mad.), late Indian C.S. Cent. Provs.) (b. 1st Jan., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, 15th Dec., 1927, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr., sub judge, Dec., 1931; regr. judl. commr.'s court, June, 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1934; ditto (subs. provl.), Jan., 1936; offg. regr., high ct., Jan., 1936; attd. legis. dept., govt. of India, May, 1936; on spec. duty, reforms office, govt. of India, Sept., 1936, to Oct., 1938; addl. dep. secty., legis. dept., Oct., 1938; dep. ditto, Oct., 1939; addtl. ditto, Jan., 1940; retd., Nov., 1940; dep. sec., govt. of India (legis.), Aug., 1943.

SUNDARAM, RAMASWAMY MEENASKI, O.B.E. Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 22nd May, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1931; Diwan of Sivaganga, July, 1934 to Nov., 1938; addl. sec. devel. dept., June, 1943; O.B.E., June, 1945; addl. charge of sec. to govt., May, 1946.

SUNDARAM, VADUVUR SHRINIVAS, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 24th Nov., 1888).—Retd., Nov., 1941.†

SUR, HRISHIKESH, O.B.E., B.A., late India Posts and Tels (b. 2nd Jan., 1885).—Dep. dir.-genl. tels., Apl., 1938.†

SUR, NOLINI KANTA, R.B., D.Sc., Met. Dept. (b. June, 1891).—Apptd. meteorologist, 31st Oct., 1927; confd., Oct., 1929; Rao Bahadur, 1st Jan., 1945.

SUR, SACHINDRA NATH, M.B., D.P.H., Dept. of Public Health, Bengal (asst. dir. of public health) (b. 4th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as offg. dep. sany. commr., Dec., 1916; asst. dir. of public health, Dec., 1918.

SURATH NATH, CHANDHURI, RAI BAHADUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Orissa) (b. 30th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1910, and held non-gazetted appt. prior to 12th Apl., 1924; asst. engr., Apl., 1924; apptd. to I.S.E., 1st Jan., 1930, as asst. exec. engr.; offg. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1930; confd., Apl., 1935; under-secty., govt. of Orissa, p.w.d., Sept., 1939.

SURENDRA NATH SEN, M.A., Ph.D., B.Litt. (Oxon.), F.R.Hist.S. (dir. of archives, govt. of India) (b. 29th July, 1890).—Educ. at

Dacca Coll., Calcutta and Oxford Univs.; prof. of history and English literature, Robertson Coll., Jubbulpore, 1916-17; lecturer in Maratha history and Marathi literature, Calcutta Univ., 1917-1931; Ashutosh prof. of mediaeval and modern Indian history, Calcutta Univ., 1931; honorary advr. on records, govt. of Bengal, 1931; keeper of the records of the govt. of India, June, 1939; dir. of archives, ditto, March, 1944.

SURENDRA NATH SEN, M.A., LL.D., Judicial Dept., United Provs. (judge, high ct., Rangoon).—Apptd. offg. judge, high ct., June, 1927; addl. judge, ditto, March, 1928; confd., Oct., 1930.

SURENDRA NATH SEN, Barr., late Judicial Dept., Burma.—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

SURYAPRAKASA RAO, V., Indian Forest Service (b. 10th March, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bengal, 8th Nov., 1930.

SUTCLIFFE, FREDERICK COWAN, Indian Police (b. 7th June, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Nov., 1938.

SUTHERLAND, JOHN WILLIAM ROSS, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 11th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 17th Dec., 1922; lecturer in forestry, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, Nov., 1924, to Apl., 1926; dep. consvr., Apl., 1926.

SUTTON, NOEL DUDLEY TAVLER, Indian Police (asst. supt., Punjab) (b. 20th Dec., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of Police, Punjab, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

SUTTON, STANLEY CECIL, M.Sc. (Econ.), India Office (b. 23rd Sept., 1907).—Educ. at Tottenham County Sch., London Sch. of Econ. (Graham Wallas prizeman), and Univ. Coll., London (Sir John Macalister Medallist); senr. asst., Brit. lib. of pol. and econ. science, 1931-35; asst. keeper, library, India Office, 17th June, 1935; on loan to E. and O. dept., from 1939.

SWAIN, LEO HARRY, Indian State Rlys. (dir., rly. bd.) (b. 31st Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as bridge engr., 28th March, 1920; asst. contr. of standardisation, July, 1932; offg. dep. ch. contr., central standards office, Apl., 1934; confd., Apl., 1935; dep. ch. engr. (bdgs.), Aug., 1935; dir., rly. bd. (civ. engrg.), Nov., 1938.

SWAMINATHAN, G., O.B.E., Indian audit and accts. dept.—Apptd. dep. sec. govt. of India, fin. dept., Jan., 1946.

SWAMINATHAN, M. R., Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Nov., 1901).—Joined, 16th Apl., 1931; dep. ch. accts. offr., E.I. Rly., Aug., 1944.

SWAMINATHAN, THINNIAM SUBRAHMANYA, ndian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 23rd Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, sub-collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1931; sub-collr. and jt. mag. to Jan., 1944; addl. jt. sec. bd. of rev., July, 1945; asst. sec. bd. of rev. (addl. ch.), May, 1946.

SWAMINATHAN, THIRUMALRAYA, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 18th June, 1912).—Educ. at Pachaiyappa's High Sch., Chidambaran, Madras Univ., Univ. Coll., London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

SWAMINATHAN, VIRUDI SRINIVASAIYAR, Rao Sahib, B.A., (asst. sec. to the govt. of India, commonwealth relations dept.) (b. 2nd Oct., 1898).—Joined the service in 1924; supt. Dec.,

1941; asst. sec. from Nov., 1942; Rao Sahib. June, 1944; offg. under sec. govt. of India, commonwealth relations dept., Dec. 1943 to Feb., 1944; Apl. to May, 1944; and Jan., 1945 to Sept., 1945.

SWANN, ROBERT SWINNEY, M.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to govr. of Orissa) (b. 17th Nov., 1915).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh Univ., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 16th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939; and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Orissa, Nov., 1939; jt. mag. and dep. collr.; dep. sec., govt. of Orissa, home dept., sec. to govr., Orissa, July, 1945.

SWANZY, ROBERT EDWARD, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (Bihar) (b. 17th July, 1888).—Educ. at Rugby, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service, Oct., 1915, to Apl., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1924; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. commr., March, 1936; confd., Oct., 1940; spec. offic., pol. dept., Apl., 1941; offg. member, bd. of rev., Apl., 1944; commr. Chota Nagpur, Sept., 1945, on l.p.r.

SWEET, JACK MCMURDO, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Madras) (b. 20th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1923; confd. as consvr., March, 1943.

SWIFT, GERALD ARTHUR, Indian Police (b. 13th July, 1895) (supt., United Provs.)—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Nov., 1920; offg. supt., Sept., 1923; confd., June, 1930.

SWINNERTON, ALBERT RICHARD, India Office (actg. princpl., milty. dept.) (b. 6th Apl., 1896).—Apptd. clerical offic., 3rd Aug., 1921; exec. offic., Jan., 1929; actg. hr. ditto, Aug. to Oct., 1938; confd., July, 1939; asst. to P.S.V. in London June-Oct., 1938; actg. asst. admin. offic., Apl., 1940; actg. princpl., Feb., 1942.

SWINSTEAD, NORMAN HILLYARD, O.B.E., M.A. (Cantab.), Indian Posts and Tels. (dir. of tels.) (b. 21st March, 1891).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1920; divl. engr., wireless, 3rd October, 1921; asst. dir.-genl., wireless, May, 1930; offg. dir., Aug., 1932; asst. dir.-genl., Oct., 1935; div. engr., Feb., 1936; dir. of tels., Aug., 1940; confd., dep. dir.-genl., tels, May, 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

SWITHINBANK, BERNARD WINTHROP, C.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Oct., 1884).—Retd., Aug., 1942; advr. to sec. of state for Burma, Aug., 1942 to Sept., 1946, †

SYED ABDUL VAHID, B.A., B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (Central Provs.) (b. 20th Jan., 1898).—Retd., 4th Aug., 1942.

SYED ISKANDER ALIMIRZA, SAHIBZADA, C.I.E., O.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 13th Nov., 1899).—First commn., 16th July, 1920; asst. mag. and collr., Aligarh, Aug., 1926; supy. asst. commr., Hazara, March, 1927; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Apl., 1928; ditto, Tonk, May, 1928; ditto, Bannu, Apl., 1930; ditto, Nowshera, Apl., 1931; dep. commr., Hazara, May, 1933; asst. commr., Marden, Oct., 1936; dep. commr., ditto, Jan., 1937; pol. agent, Khyber, Apl., 1938; O.B.E., June, 1939; dep. commr., Peshawar, March, 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1945; pol. agt., Orissa states, May, 1945; jt. sec. govt. of India, war dept., May, 1946.

SYKES, THE RT. HON. SIR FREDERICK HUGH,

P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., G.B.E., K.C.B., C.M.G., Maj.-Gen. (ret'd.).—Gouv. of Bombay from 8th Dec., 1928, to Dec., 1933.

SYKES, JOHN FRANCIS GASKELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Aug., 1909).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. asst. and commt. Border Mily. Police, Apl., 1936; sub. div. offic., June, 1937; asst. colonization offic., Oct., 1938; famine offic., March, 1940; on for. service, Bahawalpur state, Jan., 1941 to 1944; dep. commr., Sept., 1944; development sec. to fin. commrs., Oct., 1945.

SYLVESTER, HAROLD FRASER, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 15th Dec., 1885).—Retd., Dec., 1940.

SYMINGTON, DAVID, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govr., Bombay) (b. 4th July, 1904).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and supt. of stamps, May, 1929; offg. collr., Jan., 1933; backward class offic., Bombay presdy., Nov., 1937; confd. as collr., Dec., 1936; jt. sec. and sec. to govt., Bombay (home) and a.r.p. contr., var. from Apl., 1942, to Aug., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1943; sec. to govr., Sept., 1943 to Dec., 1945.

SYMON, ALEXANDER COLIN BURLINGTON, O.B.E., India Office (b. 13th May, 1902).—Apptd. clerical offic., 22nd April, 1920; asst. secty., Indian deleg. to the disarmament conf., 1932-33; exec. offic., March, 1933; asst. princpl., Nov., 1934; secty., Indian deleg. to London naval conf., 1935-36; pte. deleg. to permit under-secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1938; actg. princpl., Feb., 1939; services loaned to the Ministry of Information, Sept., 1939, to Nov., 1939; on loan to govt. of India, Aug., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; actg. asst. sec. econ. and overseas dept., Mar., 1946; on loan to Treasury as dep. high commr. for U.K. in India, Sept., 1946.

SYMONS, NOEL VICTOR HOUSMAN, C.I.E., M.C., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 27th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at King Edward VI Sch. Bath; joined the service, 26th Aug., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. mag., March, 1926; offg. magte. and collr., Apl., 1927; offg. dep. commr., March, 1929; offg. secty. to bd. of rev., Bengal; confd., May, 1932; offg. pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, Sept., 1934; offg. pte. collr., July, 1935; secty., rev., etc. dept. Bengal; confd., Aug., 1938; a.r.p. contr. Dec., 1940; confd. mag. and collr., Aug., 1941; addl. sec., p.h. dept., Oct., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; ret'd., Sept., 1946.

SYMONS, RONALD STUART, C.I.E., Ind. C.S. (jt. sec., fin. dept. govt. of India) (b. 24th July, 1904).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927 and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dept., Dec., 1937; dept. secty. fin. dept., Apl., 1938; offic. on fin. duty. dept. of supply, Aug., 1940; jt. spl. advr. (m.p.), Oct., 1940; addtl. finl. advr., mil. fin. and ex-officio joint sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, March, 1943; jt. sec. govt. of India, fin. dept., Apl., 1945; C.I.E., June, 1945.

SYMONS, SIR THOMAS HENRY, K.B.E., C.S.I., K.H.S., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 17th May, 1872).—Retd., Sept., 1930.*

SYNGE, GEORGE BERNARD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 11th Feb., 1903).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and apptt. depts., Jan., 1930; confd., March, 1930; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1933; dist. and sess. judge (confd.), Apl., 1939; dist. judge, Midnapore, Nov., 1945.

SYNNOTT, NEVILLE WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 31st July, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 14th Jul., 1909; supt., July, 1925; offg. dep. ch. optg. supt. (movement), March, 1935; dep. ch. contr. movements, Apl., 1941; ch. comm. mgr. ret'd. and re-employed as dep. contr. rly. priorities, Nov., 1945.

TAE LA, PADAM CHAND, T.C.E., Coll., Roorkee, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr. (prov.), March, 1938.

TAIT, DOUGLAS WALTER, India Office (supgt. exec. officr., acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 12th Aug., 1889).—Served as 2nd divn. clerk (customs), from Nov., 1907; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 6th July, 1909, and attached to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.; transf'd. to library, March, 1913; on milty. service from March, 1917, to Nov., 1919; higher clerical offr., Apl., 1922; higher exec. officr., Apl., 1926; assessor of inc. tax, Oct., 1936 to Jan., 1946; actg. suptg. exec. offr. Nov., 1937; confd. Jan., 1938.

TAJAL ISLAM MUHAMMAD NURANABI CHAUDHURI, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 8th Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Presidency Coll., Calcutta; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1927; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933; dir., rural reconstrn., Bengal, March, 1938; sec., public health, etc., dept., Oct., 1940, to Dec., 1943.

TAKLE, GANESH GOVIND, O.B.E., B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Serv. (dep. consvr. Cent. Provs.) (b. 14th Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 4th Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Feb., 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

TALEYARKHAN, KAIKHUSRO MANEKSHAH, Barr., late Judl. Dept., Bombay.—Retd., Apl., 1938.*

TALLENTS, PHILLIP CUBIT, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 13th Apl., 1886).—Retd., Aug., 1939.*

TALLON, WILLIAM JOHN (b. 5th Jan., 1910).—Joined dept. of supply, Feb., 1942; dep. dir.-genl. (disposals) July, 1946.

TALUKDAR, JATINDRA NATH, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 22nd July, 1901).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Wadhram Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 1st Dec., 1925, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1932; addl. secty. to govt., pol. dept., Bengal, Feb., 1935; dep. secty., home dept., govt. of India, 1938 to July, 1940; mag. and collr. (confd.), Apl., 1939; contr. of emigrant

labour, Nov., 1940; spec. officr., home dept., Feb., 1945.

TAMBLING, REV. FRANCIS GORDON HILL, late Indian Ecclesl. Estabt. (Punjab) (b. 5th Jan., 1885).—Retd., July, 1939.*

TAMHANE, VINAYAK ATMARAM, M.Sc., M.Ag., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 4th May, 1884).—Retd., May, 1939.*

TAMPOE, ALFRED McGOWAN COOMARASWAMI, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 25th June, 1881).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

TANDAN, BALWANT RAI, C.I.E., Barr., Ind., C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 8th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1933; and again, Feb., 1936; ditto (prov. subs.), Dec., 1936; dep. secty., rev. dept., govt. of Punjab, Oct., 1939; dep. commr. (confd.), May, 1941; addl. commr., Nov., 1944; commr., Jan., 1945; sec. elec. and industries dept., Apl., 1945; sec. fin. dept., Nov., 1945; C.I.E., June, 1946.

TANDON, RAJ KRISHNA, B.A.—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Punjab, June, 1939; under sec. govt. of India, infm. and broadcastg. dept., June, 1942; offr. on spec. duty, comm. dept., Feb., 1946; Indian Trade commr., for Ceylon, May, 1946.

TANDON, SUKHDEO PRASAD (see Sukhdeo, P. T.).

TANDY, SIR EDWARD ALDBOROUGH, Kt., Brigadier, R.E. (ret'd.), late Survey of India Dept.—Retd., Dec., 1928.*

TANDY, MAURICE PATRICK O'CONNOR, Maj., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 14th May, 1912).—First commn., 28th Jan., 1932; under training in Rajputana, 8th Nov., 1936; ditto, pol. dept. sect., May, 1937; under-sec. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, June, 1937; under trng. Punjab, Nov., 1937; H.B.M.'s vice-consul, Zabul, June, 1939; confd. in Indian Pol. Serv., Dec., 1939; asst. pol. agt., Bahrein, Nov., 1944; pol. agt., Kuwait, Apl., 1945, again, May, 1946.

TANNAN, MOHAN LAL, O.B.E., M.Com. (Bir.), Barr., late Ind. Educn. Serv. (b. 2nd May, 1885).—Retd., Feb., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.†

TANNER, ANTONY THOMAS, Burma Police, (cl. I) (b. 5th Dec., 1923).—Apptd. on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

TANNER, JOCELYN THEODORE, late Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 6th June, 1890).—Joined the service 18th Nov., 1910, as asst. supt.; on milty. duty from July, 1917, to Apl., 1919; supt. of police, Jan., 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1932; confd., supt., sel. grade, Dec., 1940; confd. dep. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1943; ret'd., June, 1945.

TANNER, MAURICE OSBORNE, O.B.E., Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 3rd Dec., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 29th Nov., 1913; dist. supt., May, 1918; awarded King's police medal, March, 1929; O.B.E., Jan., 1935.

TANNER, Capt. RALPH ESMOND SELBY, Burma Frontier Serv., (b. 24th Aug., 1921).—Apptd. 24th June, 1946.

TANNER, RICHARD LIONEL, Indian State Rlys. (production engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 22nd Oct., 1888).—Apptd. production engr., E.I. rly., 1st Nov., 1927.

TARAK, NATH GHOSH, Indian Police (b. 1st Oct., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Jan., 1937.

TARAKAD GOPALAKRISHNA NARAYANA AYYAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess.

judge, Bihar (b. 15th Feb., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras, and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 18th Oct., 1926; arrived, 7th Dec., 1926, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936; sec. judl. dept. and remember. legal affairs, Aug., 1945.

TARA SINGH, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 1st June, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

TARGETT, SIR ROBERT WILLIAM, C.I.E., (dir.-genl. of disp.) (b. 23rd Feb., 1891).—Joined the service, 22nd May, 1918; asst. contr. clothing, Indian Munitions Bd. (temp.); employed in I.M.B. and surplus stores organs., up to Dec., 1921; contr. of leather and textile purchase, Indian stores dept. (which post was subsequently converted to dep. dir. of purchase), Jan., 1922; ch. contr. of stores, Apl., 1939; dep. dir.-genl. of supply, March, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; addtl. dir.-genl., supply, July, 1942; dir. gen. supply and disp., Oct., 1943; kt., Jan., 1945; dir. genl. of disp., Jan., 1946; on spec. duty, Apl., 1946.

TARLOK, SINGH, B.A., B.Sc. (Econ.), Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 26th Feb., 1913).—Educ. at St. Vincent's High Sch., Poona; Bombay Univ., and London Sch. of Economics; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. finl. advr., supply fin., Dec., 1943; under-sec., fin. dept., Jan., 1944.

TASKER, SIR THEODORE JAMES, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 20th Jan., 1884).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 24th Nov., 1908, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., Apl., 1913, to March, 1915; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1916; dist. mag. and collr., Bangalore, Nov., 1917; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; commr., Coorg, Dec., 1923; on for. service under Nizam's govt., Jan., 1927; collr., March, 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; kt., May, 1937; third member, board of rev., Madras, March, 1938; on for. service under Nizam's govt., March, 1939; supervisor, I.C.S. prob., July, 1942; retd., Nov., 1944.

TATE, THOMAS BAILEY, C.S.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 11th Dec., 1882).—Retd., Dec., 1938.*

TAUNTON, SIR IVON HOPE C.I.E., B.A., Ind. C.S. (advr. to govr., Bombay) (b. 19th Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Sind as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service, Nov., 1917, to Apl., 1919; offg. collr. and dis. mag., May, 1923; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1925; chairman, cattle theft commn., Dec., 1925; offg. collr. and supt. of stamps, Jan., 1926; offg. dep. sec. to govt., home and eccl. depts., Apl., 1926; offg. dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept., June, 1927; in foreign service as finl. and rev. member, Khairpur State Exec. council, Oct., 1927; offg. collr., Sholapur, and pol. agent, Akalkot, Nov., 1932; collr., Dec., 1932; municip. commr. for city of Bombay, July, 1934; ch. secty. to govt. of Sind, home, legal, etc., depts.; member, Sind rev. tribunal, Jan., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; advr. to govr., Bombay, Dec., 1942; kt., Jan., 1946.

TAVENER, EDWARD SYDNEY BEAUMONT, M.A., Dip. of Forestry, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 29th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1923, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Jan., 1924.

TAWNG, Capt. KUMJE, M.C., B.G.M., Burma Frontier Serv. (b. 29th Sept., 1917).—Apptd. 7th May, 1946.

TAYLOR, ALFRED GEORGE TUTTON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 1st Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933; on depn. to U.K., Aug., 1944 to March, 1946.

TAYLOR, BASIL CHURTON, late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 19th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1910; dist. supt. and princpl., police trng. sch., Saugor, Jan., 1923; on depn. to Indore as inspr.-genl. from Apl., 1926, to Oct., 1930; and again, March, 1932; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1936; on depn. to Jaipur State as inspr.-genl. of police, June, 1939; retd., 19th Aug., 1944.

TAYLOR, BRUCE HENRY, Indian Police, Bombay (b. 18th Feb., 1911).—Joined the service as proxy. asst. supt., Bombay, 19th Nov., 1931; confd., Feb., 1934; confd. as supt., Aug., 1942; dep. commr., C.I.D., Aug., 1942, to June, 1943; asst. comdt., Sind police rifles, Jan., 1945; comdt. ditto, June, 1946, on l.p.r.

TAYLOR, ERNEST BERTRAM NEAVE, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 2nd Oct., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 20th Oct., 1910; exec. engr., May, 1919; offg. supt. of works, Dec., 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1930, and again, Dec., 1932; offg. senr. govt. inspr., Oct., 1934; dep. ch. engr. (prov.), Nov., 1935; confd., Apl., 1938; ch. engr., prov., Oct., 1941; confd., June, 1941; retd., Oct., 1943.

TAYLOR, E. MCKENZIE, M.B.E., Ph.D. (Cantab.), D.Sc. (Durham), F.I.C., late Public Works Dept., Punjab—Apptd. dir., irrig. research, Punjab, 17th Nov., 1930; retd., Nov. 13th, 1944.

TAYLOR, FRANCIS ERNEST ALFRED, B.A. (Oxon), Ind. C.S. (dist. mag. and collr., Orissa) (b. 16th Sept., 1903).—Educ. at Glenalmond, Univ. of St. Andrews, and Merton Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927, arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1929; sec. to govr., Orissa, July, 1940; sec., rev. and dev. depts., March, 1942; mag. and collr., July, 1942; dist. mag. and collr. and agt. to provl. govt., Apl., 1943; sec. to govt. rev. and labour dept., Bihar, Nov., 1945.

TAYLOR, FRANK HERBERT GRAHAM, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Central Provinces) (b. 17th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., March, 1910; dist. supt., Nov., 1921; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1936; dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1941; on depn. to H.E.H. the Nizam's govt., Jan., 1942; retd., Dec., 1945.

TAYLOR, HUGH GEORGE, India Office (actg. hr. exec. offr. acct.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 17th Nov., 1919).—Apptd. exec. offr., 24th Sept., 1938; actg. hr., ditto, Jan., 1946.

TAYLOR, HENRY JOHN BOSANQUET, O.B.E., B.Sc., Ind. C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 23rd Apl., 1909).—Educ. at the Northern Secondary Sch., Portsmouth, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge;

apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub divl. offcr., Apl., 1934; adminstr., Lahore municipal cttee, etc., Aug., 1940; dep. commr., Oct., 1943; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

TAYLOR, SIR JOHN, C.I.E., D.S.O., V.H.S., M.D., D.P.H., Col. (actg. Maj.-Gen., I.M.S.) (retd.) (dir., central research inst., Kasauli) (b. 14th Feb., 1884).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1906; arrived in India, 26th Feb., 1907; in milty. employ from 26th Feb., 1907, to 31st Aug., 1909; in civil employ. in Bombay from 1st Sept., 1909, to 15th Oct., 1914; reverted to milty. duty from 16th Oct., 1914, to 5th Aug., 1920; offg. sanitary commr., Assam, Aug., 1920; offg. asst. dir., Pasteur Inst., Rangoon, March, 1923; offg. dir., ditto, March, 1925; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. dir., Haffkine inst., Feb., 1932; dir., central research inst., Kasauli, Oct., 1932; C.I.E., May, 1937; kt., June, 1942.

TAYLOR, ROBERT WILLIAM, late Indian State Rlys. (E.B. Rly) (b. 16th March, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st October, 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., March, 1932; and again, Sept., 1935; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1936; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Oct., 1935; confd., Nov., 1937; ch. engr., Dec., 1940; retd., Dec., 1944.

TAYLOR, SAMUEL, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd Feb., 1883).—Retd., Feb., 1938.*

TAYLOR, STANLEY GRISWOOD, C.I.E., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 19th May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 2nd Dec., 1913; addl. supt., March, 1919; offg. supt., Nov., 1923; confd., Aug., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; princpl. police training coll., Sardah, Jan., 1936; confd. in sel. gr., Apl., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1946.

TAYLOR, WALTER, India Office (actg. princpl. fin. dept.) (b. 29th October, 1895).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 5th Aug., 1914, and attached to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.; on mil. service from July, 1915, to Jan., 1919; exec. offcr., Apl., 1922; jt. editor, India Office List, Nov., 1934; actg. hr. exec. offcr., Sept., 1935; confd., Oct., 1936; attd. finl. dept., Dec., 1935; editor, India Office and Burma Office List, Nov., 1937, to March, 1940; attd. Oct., 1938, to expert cttee on the defence of India, 1938-39; asst. administrative offcr., June, 1939; lent to Air Ministry, March, 1940; actg. princpl., Air Min., May, 1940; actg. princpl., India Office (in absentia), Nov., 1943; returned to India Office, Jan., 1945.

TAYLOR, WALTER, D.V.H., M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Veterinary Service (Punjab) (b. 25th Sept., 1886).—Retd., Aug., 1940.†

TAYLOR, WILFRED REGINALD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., late Indian State Rlys. (Burma) (b. 18th March, 1881).—Retd., March, 1938.*

TEJ NARAYAN MULLA, M.A., LL.B. (judge, high ct., Allahabad) (b. July, 1887).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1921, as addl. dist. and sess. judge; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1923; offg. judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1936; judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1940.

TEJA SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, Sardar, B.A., LL.B., Punjab C.S., Judcl. Bch. (judge, Lahore high ct.) (b. 1st Jan., 1889).—Joined the service, 8th July, 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; addtl. judge, Sept., 1943, to beginning of summer vacation, 1944; judge, high ct., Lahore, Apl., 1944.

TEJASWI PRASAD BHALLA, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 11th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 24th May, 1923; offg. supt., July, 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; supt., June, 1935; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D. (Gen.), Apl., 1936; O.S.D., at govt. hdqtrs., Nov., 1937; to Jan., 1940.

TEK CHAND, SIR BAKHSHI, M.A., LL.B., late Ludh. Dept., Punjab.—Apptd. judge, high ct., Lahore, 27th Jan., 1927; retd., 20th Aug., 1943.

TELFER, GEORGE HENRY, late Indian State Rlys., Burma (b. 11th May, 1883).—Retd., 11th Nov., 1940.*

TEMPLE, JOHN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th May 1882).—Retd., March, 1938.*

TEN-BROEKE, BERTRAM ST. LEGER, C.I.E., M.C., Ind. Police (dep.-inspr. genl. Bihar) (b. 8th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th March, 1921; supt., July, 1925; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937; dep. inspr.-genl., July, 1940; C.I.E., June, 1946.

TENNANT, WILLIAM ROBERT, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (b. 26th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ.; arrived, 30th Nov., 1919, and served in the United Provs. as asst. magr. and collr.; dep. supt. of census, March, 1921; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1923; dep. acctnt.-genl., Bombay, Nov., 1924; ditto, United Provs., Nov., 1925; addl. dep. sec., govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1927; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Dec., 1929; confd., March, 1930; offg. finl. advr., milty. fin., Apl., 1933; acctnt.-genl., Bombay, Oct., 1934; dir. of audit, def. services, Oct., 1937; dep. aud.-genl., India, provl., Aug., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; aud.-genl. of India (substantive), Aug., 1942; dep. ditto, Oct., 1942; dir. of rly. audit, Dec., 1944; dep. auditor-genl. of India, May, 1946.

TERRY, J. E., Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Sept., 1894).—Joined 26th Nov., 1931; dep. gen. mangr., B.N. Rly., Nov., 1945.

TERRY, DOUGLAS EDGAR, India Office (hr. clerical offcr.) (b. 15th May, 1899).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 1st June, 1920, after previous service from 2nd May, 1917; hr. clerical offcr., Nov., 1935; actg. staff offcr. for services questions, Aug., 1943.

TEULON, OLIVER HENRY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 29th June, 1883).—Retd., June, 1938.*

TEWARI, BHOLA DATT, M.A., LL.B., (b. 26th Aug., 1903).—Joined the service 17th Aug., 1927; supt. home dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1939; offg. asst. sec. home dept., Apl., 1945.

THA, MAUNG CHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (offg. dep. commr., Burma) (b. 14th Feb., 1910).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Minbu, Rangoon Univ., and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., dept. of comm. and indus., June, 1904; offg. dep. commr., July, 1941; admin. of state colonies, Burma, Dec., 1945.

THACKWELL, ALBERT HENRY, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Jan., 1885).—Retd., Apl., 1941.

THADHANI, GOPALDAS THARUMAL, O.B.E., (b. 19th May, 1892).—Joined India Stores Dept. Nov., 1925; dir. of purchase, 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; dep. ch. contr. purchase, July, 1942; ch.

contr. ditto, Jan., 1945; dep. dir.-genl. indus. and supplies, Jan., 1946.

THADANI, NENUMAL POHOOMAL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar) (b. 10th Sept., 1891).—Educ. in India and at Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to I.C.S. by nomination; arrived, 4th Jan., 1917, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. officr.; jt. mag., July, 1922; addl. dep. commr., June, 1925; magte. and collr. (provl. sub.), Dec., 1931; confd., Oct., 1933; retd., March, 1943.

THADANI, THAKURDAS VASANMAL (judge, ch. ct., Sind).—Apptd. Aug., 1944.

THAKUR, KESHAR SADASHIV, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Bengal) (b. 2nd Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 31st July, 1909; in milty. employ up to 22nd Feb., 1921; offg. civil surgeon, Bengal, Sept., 1921; ditto, 24th Parganas and protector of emigrants, Calcutta, Feb., 1934.

THAMBAN, VENGANAD KOVILAGAM RAJAGOPALA (see Rajagopala Thamban, V. K.).

THANG, THAWNG CHIN, Burma Front. Serv. (b. 16th Apl., 1914).—Apptd. 10th June, 1946.

THAPAR, PREM NATH, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 13th Apl., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1933; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), July, 1935; ditto, perm., May, 1940; dep. sec., govt. of India (infm. and broadcasting), Nov., 1941; jt. sec., ditto, Sept., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1944; offg. sec. govt. of India, infm. and broadcasting, June, 1944; jt. sec. to ditto, Oct., 1944; on depn. to U.K., and U.S.A., Aug. to Nov., 1945; jt. sec. to ditto, Nov., 1945; offg. sec. govt. of India, infm. and broadcasting, Dec., 1945 to Feb., 1946; jt. sec., dept. of infm. and arts, Feb., 1946; sec. civ. supplies, Punjab, May, 1946.

THAW, ALFRED BA, M.B., Maj., I.M.S., Burma Medl. Dept. (inspr.-gen. hosp.) (b. 13th Mar., 1892).—First commn. dated 15th Jan., 1918; in mil empl. prior to 29th May, 1924; asst. medl. supt., Rangoon genl. hosp., June, 1924; civil surg., March, 1926; on mil. duty (Burma), Dec., 1941; again medl. supt., Rangoon Gen. Hosp., Jan., 1946; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., June, 1946.

THEIN, MAUNG KYAW, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., customs) (b. 10th June, 1910).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Akyab, Rangoon Univ., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. collr., customs, Nov., 1937; dep. sec. of agric. and rural econ., Oct., 1945.

THEIN, U BA HLA, late Burma Judl. Service (b. 24th Nov., 1885).—Retd., Dec., 1940.†

THICK, R. W. (b. 25th Sept., 1892).—Project engr. (elec.) Punjab, 1933 to 1944; project offr. (elec. utilization) cent. tech. power bd. (temp.), Apl., 1946.

THIRUMALAI IVENGAR, MANDAYAM ANNADHORAI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 6th Nov., 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934; dist. judge, Aug., 1938; mag. and collr., July, 1943.

THIRUVEN KATA CHARI, R., Indian C.S., name changed to R. T. CHARI (q.v.)

THOM, HERBERT JAMES, C.I.E., M.C., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 24th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., May, 1923; confd., May, 1929; services lent to H.H. the Maharaja Bahadur of Benares from Feb., 1927; supt., Dec., 1931; princpl., police trng. sch., Apl., 1936, to Dec., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; asst. inspr.-gen., July, 1940.

THOMAS, ALAN ROBERT, late Indian Forest Service (Assam) (b. 26th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 22nd Dec., 1911; in mil. employ from 24th Sept., 1915, to 11th Oct., 1919; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; offg. consvr., Jan., 1933, to Feb., 1933; retd., Jan., 1943.

THOMAS, ANTHONY RYLANDS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 8th Dec., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1938; dep. dir., cent. irrig. and hydro. research stn., Poona, May, 1940.

THOMAS, ARTHUR, Burma Fron. Serv. (b. 27th March, 1922).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug. 1946.

THOMAS, BERNARD GORDON BROTHERO, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 25th Nov., 1906; supt., Apl., 1920; asst. inspr.-genl., rly. police, March, 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1933; O.B.E., Jan., 1934; dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936.

THOMAS, D. O., Indian State Rly. (b. 29th Oct., 1897).—Joined, 5th Dec., 1919; dep. ch. optg. supt., M.S.M. Rly., Jan., 1946.

THOMAS, EDWARD FRANCIS, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 30th June, 1880).—Retd., Feb., 1938.*

THOMAS, GEOFFREY BENION, O.B.E., M.B., Ch.B., M.Sc. (Sheffield), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), M.R.C.O.G. (Eng.), Maj., I.M.S., Madras Medical Dept. (b. 28th Jan., 1905).—First commn. dated Feb., 1933; in mil. employ. until June, 1938; specialist in obstetrics with gynaecology, June, 1938; supt., govt. hosp. for women and children, Aug., 1942; O.B.E., June, 1946.

THOMAS, GEOFFREY DAVID IVOR, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 21st Aug., 1905).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; settl. officr., Nov., 1937; M.B.E., Jan., 1927; settlmt. offr., Aug., 1939; civ. aff. serv., Dec., 1943; dep. commr., Oct., 1945.

THOMAS, SIR GEORGE HECTOR, Barr.-at.-Law, late Judicial Dept., United Provs., (b. 23th June, 1884).—Apptd. govt. advocate, ch. ct., Lucknow, Nov., 1925; judge, ch. ct., Oudh, May, 1934; ch. judge, ditto, July, 1938; kt., Jan., 1941; retd., June, 1946.

THOMAS, JOHN HERBERT, C.I.E., F.L.A., govt. of India, Commerce Dept. (supt. of insur.) (b. 21st Feb., 1895).—Apptd. to home civil service, 1914; services loaned to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, as supt. of insurance, June, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

THOMAS, VINCENT EUSTACE, A.M.I.Struct.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 16th Nov., 1888).—Retd., Aug., 1942.†

THOMPSON, AIDAN KENNETH, Burma Front. Serv. (b. 21st Dec., 1897).—Apptd. Aug., 1919; on depn. in India, March, 1943 to Feb., 1946.

THOMPSON, Miss AMY, B.A., late Indian

Educl. Service (b. 5th Nov., 1883).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

THOMPSON, AVERIL FULLER, India Office (actg. asst. princpl., mil. dept.) (b. 21st Apl., 1907).—Apptd. asst. Librarian, 30th Aug., 1937; actg. asst. princpl., May, 1943.

THOMPSON, BRIAN BELL, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 16th June, 1898).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; supt. of police, Central India agency, Apl., 1927; supt., Nov., 1932; awarded Indian police medal, July, 1936.

THOMPSON, CLAUDE MATTHEW, Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Survey of India (b. 21st Sept., 1883).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

THOMPSON, EDWARD HENRY, M.C., Office of High Commr. for India (hr. exec. officr., store dept.) (b. 30th Oct., 1889).—Apptd. second class clerk, 30th June, 1908; on mil. service from Jan., 1916; transfid. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officr., Apl., 1922; attached Indian del., Imp. econ. conf., July to Sept., 1932; hr. exec. officr., Dec., 1935; actg. princpl., ditto, Apl., 1940; progress officr. for medl. stores, Apl., 1941.

THOMPSON, ERIC BERTRAM, M.B.E., late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 21st July, 1887).—Joined as proby. supt., 25th Apl., 1908; confid., Dec., 1909; on depn. to I.E.F., Aug., 1914, to Apl., 1919; dep. p.m.g., July, 1935; p.m.g., Madras circle, Dec., 1940; ret'd., March, 1943.

THOMPSON, REV. FREDERICK ARTHUR, B.A., Indian Ecclesl. Estabt., Bombay (b. 11th Nov., 1911).—Apptd. junr. chaplain on probn., 18th Dec., 1939.

THOMPSON, HENRY WALTER, Indian Police (Sind) (b. 10th Dec., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939; a.d.c. to govr., Sind, Dec., 1942, to Dec., 1943; asst. supt., Karachi, Nov., 1944; offg. var. from May, 1945.

THOMPSON, JOSEPH HERBERT, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 9th March, 1898).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1923; arrived, 12th Dec., 1923, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1924; offg. asst. commr., D.I. Khan, July, 1926; city mag., Peshawar, Dec., 1926; dist. mag., ditto, Jan., 1928; asst. commr., Nowshera, Sept., 1929; divl. and sess. judge, Peshawar, Aug., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, Peshawar and Hazara, Aug., 1931; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1932; secty. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1934; resdt., Jaipur, March, 1938; pol. agent, E. Rajputana States, Dec., 1938; resdt., Jaipur (in addn.), May, 1939, to Oct., 1939; addtl. dep. secty., pol. dept., March, 1941; dep. secty., ditto, Sept., 1941; resdt., Kolhapur and Deccan States, Sept., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1945; resdt., Punjab States, Feb., 1946.

THOMPSON, MALCOLM STUART HAUGHTON, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Madras) (b. 20th March, 1885).—Retd., March, 1940.†

THOMPSON, MICHAEL JOSEPH, Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 11th Oct., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

THOMPSON, SAMUEL PERCY, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 16th Dec., 1902).—Educ. at St. Olave's and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived Dec., 1926, sub.-collr. and jt. magte., Nov., 1928; asst. secty., Indian franchise cttee., Jan., 1932; dist. and sess. judge (confid.), Feb., 1940; regr., high ct., Dec., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1945; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1945.

THOMPSON, WILLIAM PERCY, M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Jan., 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

THOMSON, ALEXANDER WELSH, O.B.E., late India Office (b. 26th Dec., 1878).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 12th Nov., 1900; temp. attached to mil. dept., from Dec., 1915; minor staff clerk, April, 1918; staff clerk, (actg.) Nov., 1919; dep. reading clerk to council, Feb., 1920 to Feb., 1923; higher exec. offr., June, 1922; suptg. exec. offr. June, 1924; asst. acct.-genl., Dec., 1934; assessor of income tax, from Dec., 1934, to Dec., 1937; actg. ch. exec. offr., mil. dept. Nov., 1937; confid., Jan., 1938; O.B.E., June, 1938; actg. princpl., mil. dept., June, 1940; ret'd., Jan., 1946, and re-employed.

THOMSON, ERIC SCOTT, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Apl., 1926; confid., July, 1933.

THOMSON, GEORGE, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 3rd July, 1882).—Rét'd., Jan., 1938.*

THOMSON, JAMES, O.B.E., M.M., India Office (actg. princpl. mil. dept.) (b. 1st Apl. 1895).—Apptd. exec. offr., 16th Aug., 1927; on loan to infm. dept., June, 1933 to Aug., 1933; actg. hr. exec. officr., March, 1935; confid., Feb., 1936; actg. infm. offr., Oct., 1936 to Dec., 1937; sec. admin. side, mil. dept., Feb. to Oct., 1938; attchd. to Burma Office, Jan., 1939; actg. princpl., Feb., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

THOMSON, MURRAY FORBES, Punjab serv. of engrs. (b. 29th Jan., 1902).—Joined the serv. 27th March, 1933; confid. March, 1936; asst. resdt. engr., Jogindarnagar from March, 1933; resident engr., ditto, (confid.), Sept., 1940.

THOMSON, RONALD ORD CAMPBELL, O.B.E., A.M.I.C.E., M.C., Indian State Rlys. (contr. of stores, N.W. Rly.) (b. 11th Dec., 1892).—Asst. exec. engr., 27th Nov., 1919; exec. engr., Apl., 1936; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; dep. ch. engr., Jan., 1945; contr. of stores.

THON, U Do, M.Sc., Burma Educ. Serv. (b. 27th July, 1899).—Apptd. asst. lecturer, June, 1924; inspr. of schools, 1st Feb., 1939.

THORNBURGH, DAVID GILL, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 7th Feb., 1911).—First commn., 27th Aug., 1931; under trng. in Rajputana, Oct., 1935; pers. asst. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Nov., 1935; under trng. in Punjab, Jan., 1936; under trng. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1936; under trng. in Punjab, March, 1937; asst. pol. agent, Mekram, Sept., 1937; 2nd asst. pol. agent, Mekram (in addn.), June, 1938; under sec. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, Oct., 1938; confid. in Indian Political Service, Feb., 1939; asst. commr., Peshawar, Oct., 1939; Brit. trade agt. at Gyantze and asst. to pol. officr., Sikkim, Feb., 1940; asst. commr., Bannu, Aug., 1940; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Apl., 1941; ditto, Chitral, Oct., 1941; vice-consul, Zabol, Dec., 1944; H.M. consul, Zahidan (addl. charge), March, 1946.

THORNE, HAROLD LIONEL, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, Sept., 1905; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1919; divl. transpn. officr., May, 1935; ret'd., 6th July, 1944.

THORNE, SIR JOHN ANDERSON, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub.-collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; sec. to board of revenue, land

revenue, March, 1920; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1922; ditto, May, 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1931; secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, Nov., 1931; offg. jt. secty. to govt. of India, home dept., June, 1935; addl. ditto, Oct., 1935 jt. secty., ditto, Apl., 1936; again, Nov., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938; secty. to govt.-genl. (public), Apl., 1938; temp. member, govt.-genl.'s exec. council, June to Oct., 1939; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1942; temp. member, govt.-genl.'s exec. cl. Apl., to July, 1944; home member, ditto, Oct., 1945; retd., Sept., 1946.

THORNTON, FRANCIS RUTHVEN, M.C., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 5th Aug., 1884).—Retd., Aug., 1939.*

THORNTON, NORMAN, M.I.E.E., Public Works Dept., Punjab (ch. engr.) (b. 5th March, 1894).—Joined the service, 22nd Feb., 1934; exec. engr., March, 1934; offg. dep. ch. engr., Mar., 1935; confd., Oct., 1940; ch. engr. Oct., 1945.

THOULESS, KENNETH JAMES, O.B.E., M.A., (Mech. Sc. Hons., Cantab.), Ind. Posts and Tels. (dir. of tels.) (b. 13th May, 1900).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1923, as asst. div. engr., tels.; div. engr., Oct., 1932; dir., S. and B. circle, Aug., 1935; temp. transf. to def. dept., June, 1940; divl. engr., June, 1938; dir. of tels., Sept., 1940; on mil. service, June, 1940, to Aug., 1942; offg. p.m.g., var., 1945-46; O.B.E., June, 1946.

THURSFIELD, REV. GERALD ARTHUR RICHARD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt., Burma (b. 13th Apl., 1886).—Retd., Feb., 1938.*

TIKA RAM MISRA, RAI BAHADUR, PANDIT, M.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., United Provs. (b. 1st Feb., 1885).—Retd., Feb., 1940.

TILNEY-BASSETT, REV. HUGH FRANCIS EMRA, M.A. (Oxon), Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 16th Jan., 1901).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bengal, 12th Aug., 1929; confd., Nov., 1931.

TIMS, BASIL DUNCAN, M.B.E., India Office (actg. princpl., finl. dept.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1898).—War service: R.N. Air Service and R. Navy, 1916-1919; apptd. clerical offer., 19th Sept., 1921; on depn. to India in connection with Rl. commn. on agric. in India, Oct., 1927, to May, 1928; hr. clerical offer., Apl., 1930; staff officer for currency duties, finl. dept., Dec., 1937; actg. admin. offer. for currency duties and asst. govt. dir., rly. coys., July, 1943; M.B.E., June, 1943; actg. princpl., Feb., 1944.

TIN, MAUNG (A), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 5th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., Burma, educn. dept., Dec., 1934; dep. secty., agric. and for. dept., Nov., 1937; jt. regr., co-ops., Sept., 1938; offg. regr., ditto, Feb., 1941; dep. commr., jt. sec. agric. and rural econ., 1945; sec. ditto, Jan., 1946.

TIN, MAUNG THET, Burma Police.—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., from 4th Apl., 1941.

TIN TOON, MAUNG A. (see Toon, Maung Tin).

TIN TUT, MAUNG (see Tut, Maung Tin).

TIN, U PE MAUNG, M.A., B.Litt., Indian Educ. Service (b. 24th Apl., 1888).—Apptd. to the E.I.S. as lecturer in Oriental Studies, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, from 1st Sept., 1924; retd., Apl., 1943.

TIN, U BA, Burma Engrg. Serv. (b. 8th June, 1889).—Apptd. asst. engr., P.W.D., Feb., 1920; san. engr., Oct., 1922.

TINSLEY, WILLIAM STEWART, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th Aug., 1890).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

TINT, MAUNG BA, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-secty., fin. dept., Burma) (b. 6th Nov., 1908).—Educ. at St. Peter's High Sch., and Kelly High Sch., Mandalay, Rangoon Univ., and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., fin. dept., Nov., 1937; asst. import trade contr., Burma, June, 1940; asst. collr., customs, Aug., 1940; dep. sec. comm. and supply dept., dep. dir. of supply, ditto, Jan., 1946.

TINT, SOE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 5th Nov., 1910).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Rangoon, Rangoon Univ., and Wadharn Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr. on mil. service, Nov., 1940; hdqtrs. asst., Insein, March, 1941; sec., enquiry cttee. (soc. serv. dept.), Mar., 1946; dep. sec., ind. and lab., dept., Aug., 1946.

TIPPER, GEORGE HOWLETT, M.A. (Cantab), F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 26th Feb., 1881).—Apptd. asst. supt., geological survey, 6th Dec., 1903; curator, geological museum, Calcutta, Aug., 1910; apptd. to I.A.R.O., May, 1915, to Dec., 1919; supt., Jan., 1920; retd., Sept., 1929; mineral advr. to High Commr. for India, Nov., 1928; loaned for service under Min. of Economic Warfare, Sept., 1939, to July, 1940; on spl. duty, Apl., 1941.

TIPSON, KENNETH GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 29th May, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 15th Nov., 1925; offg. supt., March, 1933; services placed at disposal of Benares State, Apl., 1937; supt. (confd.), Nov., 1939.

TIRUMALAI AYYANGAR, MALUR SRINIVASA AYYANGAR, RAO Bahadur, B.E., Ind. Serv. of Engrs. (supig. engr., Madras) (b. 1st Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 1st June, 1920; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1921; exec. engr., Jan., 1936; suptg. engr., June, 1945.

TODD, HERBERT JOHN, C.I.E., Indian Political Service (b. 15th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Burma, 4th Dec., 1913; confd., Nov., 1914; in military employ from 1st March, 1917, to 8th Jan., 1919; under civil administration, Bagdad, from Jan., 1919, to July, 1921; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, Oct., 1921; ditto, Kalat, 5th July, 1922; pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1927; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Oct., 1932; pol. agent. E. Rajputana states, Oct., 1935; presdnt., Council of State, Bharatpur (in addn.), from May, 1936, to Aug., 1936; prime minister and vice-presdnt., council of state, Jaipur, Apl., 1939; rest. in Mewar, and pol. agent, S. Rajputana states, Dec., 1940; secty. to agent to govt.-genl., resdnt. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; resdnt., Eastern states, Dec., 1944.

TODD, JAMES McDOUGALL, B.Sc., Burma Engr. Serv. (cl. I) (b. 7th March, 1903).—Apptd. to Burma P.W.D., 8th Feb., 1928; apptd. to B.E.S. (cl. I) Feb., 1933; exec. engr., Feb., 1936; released from army, June, 1946.

TODHUNTER, SIR CHARLES GEORGE, K.C.S.I., F.R.Hist.S., F.S.S., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Retd., May, 1925.*

TOLLEMACHE, HAROLD VINCENT, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 14th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Feb., 1927; ret'd., 7th Sept., 1944.

TOLLER, GEORGE GORDON TAYLOR, O.B.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd Feb., 1885).—Ret'd., Dec., 1941.†

TOLLINTON, HUGH PHILLIPS, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 9th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Radley, Balliol Coll., Oxford, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1932, and again, Apr., 1933; asst. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1934; ditto, Tank, Feb., 1935; ditto, Charsadda, Nov., 1936; home secty., govt., N.W.F.P., June, 1938; secty., ditto devel. depts., July, 1938; confd., Sept., 1938; sent sub-judge, Peshawar, March, 1940; civ. a.r.p. officr., N.W.F.P., July, 1940; settlt. officr., Hazara, Apr., 1941.

TOLSON, KENNETH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 11th Nov., 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 3rd Dec., 1931; confd., Jan., 1934; awarded Indian police medal, 1935; a.d.c. to govr. of Bengal, May, 1938.

TOMKINS, THOMAS LESLIE, India Audit Office (exec. officr.) (b. 4th Dec., 1889).—Apptd. clerical officr., 29th March, 1922; exec. officr., Oct., 1926; actg. hr. exec. officr., Apr., 1941.

TOMKINS, JAMES, D.S.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 25th Jan., 1918).—Educ. at Cardiff High Sch., and Univ. of Wales; apptd. on probn. after seln. in Aug., 1940, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

TOMKINS, WILLIAM DOUGLAS, C.I.E., O.B.E., late India Office (b. 29th July, 1882).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 24th Jan., 1902; transf. to rev. and stat. dept., March, 1906; minor staff clerk, May, 1914; staff clerk, June, 1919; sec., Indian delegations at Intl. Labour Conf., Genoa, 1920, and Intl. Conf. on Transit and Communications, Barcelona, 1921; adminis. officr., Apr., 1922; pte. sec. to delegate for India at Intl. Economic Conf., Genoa, 1922, and Hague Conf., 1922; delegate for India at Intl. Conf. on Customs Formalities, Geneva, Oct., 1923; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Aug., 1924; actg. principl., Dec., 1927; confd., July, 1929; secty. to Indian delegations to assembly of League of Nations, 1934 to 1939; O.B.E., May, 1937; actg. asst. secty., econ. and overseas dept., Jan., 1940; sec. to Min. of Supply mission to India, Aug., 1940 to Aug., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1941; ret'd., Apr., 1946.

TOMS, FRAZER WOODLAND, late Indian Police, Punjab (b. 22nd March, 1888).—Ret'd., July, 1938.*

TOOGOOD, GEORGE WATTS, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt.) (b. 20th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1920, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., E.R. rly. (prov.), Aug., 1934.

TOOKE, COLIN, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 30th Sept., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Burma, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; dist. supt., 1945 to 1946.

TOON, J. CHAN (see Chan Toon, J.).

TOON, MAUNG TIN, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1896).—Educ. at Norris Coll., Rangoon, and Cambridge Univ.; joined the service, 1st July,

1922; arrived, 7th Sept., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1925; confd., May, 1932; dist. and sess. judge, again 1945 to 1946.

TOOP, REV. WILLIAM JOHN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 20th June, 1907).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 11th May, 1944.

TORICK, SIR AMEER ALI (see Ameer Ali, Sir T.).

TORRY, Capt. SAW, Burma Fron. Serv. (b. 23rd Oct., 1920).—Apptd. 8th Mar., 1946.

TOSTEVIN, FRED PERCY, O.B.E., M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 29th Oct., 1886).—Ret'd., Apr., 1942.

TOTTENHAM, SIR ALEXANDER ROBERT LOFTUS, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 31st July, 1873).—Ret'd., June, 1933.*

TOTTENHAM, SIR GEORGE RICHARD FREDERICK, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Indian C.S. (b. 18th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Harrow and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1914; asst. to resdt. Travancore and Cochin, Sept., 1917; sub. collr. and jt. mag., June, 1919; under-sec. to govt., Feb., 1921; offg. dep. sec. to govt., March, 1924; offg. dep. sec., govt. of India, army dept., 1924; addl. ditto, Aug., 1926; offg. ditto, Sept., 1926; dep. sec., ditto, Nov., 1926; C.I.E., June, 1930; addl. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Madras; secty. to govt. of India, army dept., Apr., 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1936; member, council of state, Oct., 1936; kt., May, 1937; presdt. of council, Bharatpur, March, 1938; divan, Bharatpur State, Oct., 1939; addtl. secty., home dept., govt. of India, June, 1940; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Apr., 1945; on spec. duty, home dept., Nov., 1945; K.C.I.E., June, 1946.

TOWNEND, HERBERT PATRICK VICTOR, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. March, 1887).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., finl. dept., Aug., 1917; jt. mag. and dep. collr. and dir. of civil supplies, Oct., 1918; offg. dir.-genl. of comml. intellig., Jan., 1920; dep. sec. to govt. of Bengal, fin., comm. and marine depts., Apr., 1923; sec., Indian coal cttee., Oct., 1924, to Apr., 1925; mag. and collr., May, 1925; dep. commr., Apr., 1926; offg. secty., agric. and indus., and p.w. depts., Bengal, Nov., 1930; offg. sec., local self-govt. dept., Apr., 1932; confd., Sept., 1932; offg. rural dev. commr., Feb., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; spec. officr., home dept., govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1937; confd. as commr. divn., March, 1938; ret'd., Dec., 1944.

TOWNSEND, REGINALD STEPHEN, M.C., M.D., Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 10th Nov., 1882).—Ret'd., Nov., 1939.*

TOWNSHEND, EDMUND GEORGE, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 5th Dec., 1886).—Ret'd., Dec., 1942.

TRACY, RALPH GEORGE, India Office (actg. hr. exec. offr., acct.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 29th Apr., 1912).—Apptd. cler. offr. 6th Aug., 1929; actg. exec. offr. May, 1938; confd. Apr., 1939; actg. hr. ditto, Oct., 1942.

TREANOR, JAMES STANLEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 5th Feb., 1916).—Educ. at Methodist Coll., Belfast, Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd. 31st Aug.,

1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

TREASURE, EDWARD SHEPHERD, M.B.E., Ind. Police (asst. supt. Madras) (b. 4th Jul., 1914).—Appdtd. asst. supt. on probn., Madras, 22nd Nov., 1934; confd. Jan., 1937; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

TREASURE, PATRICK MOORE, Indian Police (b. 21st Aug., 1916).—Asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, apptd. on probn., Dec., 1936; confd., Apl., 1938.

TREGENZA, EDWARD LESLIE, late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 15th May, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 11th March, 1922; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., March, 1928; princpl., police trng. coll., March, 1936, to June, 1941; comdtd., Crown Representative police force, March, 1943; rettd., Jan., 1946.

TRENCH, REV. ALBERT CHARLES, M.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 25th Jan., 1884).—Rettd., Oct., 1938.*

TRENCH, CHENEVIX (see Chenevix-Trench).

TRESHAM, CECIL HENRY, V.D., late Survey of India, (b. 3rd Apl., 1884).—Rettd., Apl., 1939.†

TRESTON, MAURICE LAWRENCE, C.B.E., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Burma Medl. Dept. (civil surg.) (b. 9th Feb., 1891).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1914; civil surg., June, 1923; asst. medl. supt. and police surg., Rangoon genl. hosp., March, 1926; supt., Dufferin hosp., Rangoon, Feb., 1928; gynaecologist, genl. hosp., Rangoon, June, 1934; inspr.-genl., civil hosps., Burma, June, 1941; C.B.E., Jan., 1944; again inspr.-genl., civil hosps. 1945 to 1946; under govt. of India from Feb., 1947.

TREVELYAN, HUMPHREY, O.B.E., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Lancing Coll. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served as asst. collr. and mag., Madras; sub. collr. and jt. mag., March, 1931; under-secty. to A.G.G. in Central India, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. opium agent in Malwa, Apl., 1933; under-secty., pol. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1934; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Apl., 1935; guardian to Maharajah of Gwalior, Nov., 1935; secty. to Resident and C.C., Coorg, March, 1937; dep. commr., Bawalpur state, May, 1940; O.B.E., June, 1941; pol. agent in Mewar and Southern Rajputana states, Oct., 1941; secty. to agt.-genl. for India in U.S.A., Apl., 1944; under govt. of India, pol. dept.

TREVOR, SIR CHARLES GERALD, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (Punjab) (b. 28th Dec., 1882).—Rettd., Apl., 1938.*

TRIBHUMAN PRASAD, SINGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 13th May, 1913).—Educ. at the Banka H.E. Sch., Bhagalpur, Patna Univ., and London Sch. of Economics; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; cane commr., Apl., 1943; regr. co-op. soc., Nov., 1945; dep. sec. educn. dev. and employmt. dept., Jan., 1946.

TRIGG, CHARLES THOMAS, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 15th March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; on depn. to Forest research institute, Dehra-Dun, from 14th July, 1922; dep. consvr., Jan., 1932; on depn. to civ. pioneer force as spec. offcr.

TRILOK NATH, Indian Police, Bihar and

Orissa (b. 1st Dec., 1916).—Asst. supt. on probn., 10th Feb., 1939; confd., Feb., 1941.

TRIMMING, GEORGE AUGUSTUS RODERICK, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 24th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 8th Oct., 1913; works manager, Apl., 1923; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Sept., 1928; confd., March, 1934; offg. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. rly., March, 1935; confd., July, 1935; ch. contr. of standardisation, Apl., 1940; dep. dir.-genl. munitions prod. dept., dept. of supply, Jan., 1941; rettd., Sept., 1944.

TRIMURTI, NARAYANASWAMI, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 15th July, 1900).—Arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1925; transfed. from Central Provs., 27th June, 1926; asst. consvr., Madras, July, 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1929.

TRIPATHI, JAGANNATH, M.Sc., Rai Bahadur (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 16th Mar., 1893).—Joined the U.P. civil (exec.) serv., 1st Oct., 1917; mag. and collr. (confd.), March, 1944.

TRIPATHI, SHYAMA CHANDRA, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Educ. Service (princpl. Ravenshaw Coll.) (b. 1st March, 1893).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1920; profsr. of physics, Feb., 1921; offg. dep. dir. of public instn., Oct., 1930; confd., Aug., 1933; offg. princpl., Ravenshaw Coll., Feb., 1936; confd., Aug., 1936; O.B.E., June, 1946.

TRIVEDI, SIR CHANDULAL MADHAVLAL, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., Ind. C.S. (govr. of Orissa) (b. 2nd July, 1893).—Educated at Bombay Univ. and at St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, Dec., 1917, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1924; offg. regr. co-op. societies and dir. of industries, Central Provs., June, 1925; confd., Nov., 1926; dep. commr., March, 1927; O.B.E., Jan., 1931; dep. sec., home dept., govt. of India, May, 1932; offg. jt. secty., ditto, Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1935; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Cent. Provs. Oct., 1937; C.S.I., June, 1941; addtl. sec., govt. of India (commns.), March, 1942; sec., ditto (war) July, 1944; kt. June, 1945; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1945; govt. of Orissa, Apl., 1946.

TRIVEDI, HAR GOVIND, (supt. engr., P.H. engr. dept. U.P.) (b. 23rd July, 1893).—Joined the serv., Sept., 1919 as temp. asst. engr.; confd. Nov., 1925; offg. exec. engr. from Sept., 1933; confd. ditto, Nov., 1934; addl. Supt. engr., P.H. dept., Aug., 1942; supt. engr. U.P., (confd.), Sept., 1945.

TRIVEDI, NARAYAN VISHWANATH, Rao Bahadur, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 5th Sept., 1886).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 18th Jan., 1909, to 26th Aug., 1914; dep. supt., Aug., 1914; employed under dir. intell. bureau, from Oct., 1923, to Jan., 1929; promoted to I.P. as supt. from 24th Jan., 1929; dep. commr. of police, C.I.D., Bombay, March, 1939.

TRIVEDI, RAMANLAL SHANKERLAL, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 20th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1932; addtl. dist. mag., June to Nov., 1937; addtl. dist. judge from Apl., 1942.

TRIVEDI, RATILAL VITHAL DAS, M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit dept. (b. 13th Dec., 1895).

—Joined the service, 11th Apr., 1921; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, Aug., 1922; asst. dir. of audit, United Provs., Sept., 1928; currency offer., Cawnpore, Dec., 1928; dep. acctn.-genl., Bombay, Sept., 1931, and again, June, 1937; examr., outside audit dept., Bombay, March, 1935; examr., local audit dept., Bombay, Feb., 1936; dep. acct.-genl., Central rev., Oct., 1937; ditto, Punjab, March, 1941.

TRIVEDI, V. C. M.A., (b. 12th Feb., 1916).—Joined Bombay govt. (B.E.S.) 22nd Nov., 1940; fin. dept. (supply) Bombay govt., Mar., 1942; asst. export tr. contr. Bombay, Mar., 1944; under sec. govt. of India, dept. of comm., Nov., 1945.

TROTMAN, EWART WASHINGTON, C.I.E., Ind. C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 10th July, 1895).—Educ. at the Rich Sch., Gloucester; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1923; arrived, 28th Oct., 1923, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dep. secty. to govt., home and eccl. depts., June, 1927; offg. under-secty. to ditto, Aug., 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; offg. secty. to ditto, June, 1929; offg. collr. and mag., Apr., 1931; confd., Nov., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1946; commr. N.D. to May, 1946; commr., sales tax in addn., Mar., 1946.

TROTMAN, REV. LIONEL WILLIAM, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 12th Aug., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probn., Bengal, 19th June, 1930; junr. chaplain, June, 1932.

TROTTER, HUGH, C.I.E., Indian Forest Service (asst. forest economist, forest research institute and coll., Dehra Dun) (b. 16th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 30th Dec., 1913; on mil. service from 28th Nov., 1914, to 9th March, 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1918; asst. forest economist, Sept., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1943; retd., Aug., 1945.

TROTTER, REV. R.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (b. 13th June, 1906).—Apptd. prob. chaplain, Oct., 1937; junior, ditto, Oct., 1939.

TROUSDELL, HENRY FRANK, Printing and Stationery Dept.
