(manager, govt. of India press) (b. 14th July, 1891).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Nov., 1916; asst. manager, govt. of India press, Jan., 1926; offg. dep. contr., forms, Nov., 1927; confd., Dec., 1930; offg. manager, govt. of India press, Nov., 1931; confd., Oct., 1932.

TRUSCOTT, ROBERT RODGER BATE, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 12th Nov., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 14th Nov., 1935; confd., July, 1938.

TRUTWEIN, PERCY WALLACE, B.A., late Burma Commission (b. 30th Jan., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

TUCK, HOWELL ARTHUR, late Indian State Ryis. (N.W. Ry.) (b. 26th Feb., 1888).—Asst. signalengr., 1st Nov., 1917; dist. ditto, 1st July, 1937; dep. ch. ditto, March, 1941; retd., Oct., 1943.

TUCKER, REV. GORDON ELIJAH, B.Sc., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (archdeacon of Calcutta) (b. 8th Oct., 1896).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probn., 22nd Feb., 1929; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1931; senr. ditto, Feb., 1941; archdeacon of Calcutta, Oct., 1, 1943.

TUCKER, OWEN RUFUS, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Ryis. (ch. optg. supt.) (b. 27th March, 1897).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1923; asst. supt., E.I. rly., July, 1925; trans-

ptn. supt. (power) June, 1935; ch. optg. supt., July, 1945; rly. liaison offr.

TUCKETT, ROBERT HENRY, India Office (hr. exec. offcr., acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 17th Jan., 1891).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 1st July, 1913; on mil. service from Dec., 1916, to Feb., 1919; exec. offcr., Apr., 1922; actg. hr. ditto, Sept., 1938; confd., June, 1939; assessor of income tax, March, 1939.

TUCKWELL, CHARLES ADOLPHUS, O.B.E., late India posts and tels. (b. 20th Jan., 1884).—Retd., May, 1938.*

TUFAIL, MOHAMMED CHAJU KHAN RAGPUT, B.A. (Sind) (b. 27th Sept., 1895).—Joined, 6th March, 1920; held non-gaz. appts. and gaz. to Nov., 1934; criminal tribes settlmnt. offr., Jan., 1936; mukhtiarikar (seln. gr.) June, 1937; dep. collr. (2nd gr.) (confd.), Nov., 1938; ditto, (1st gr.) Feb., 1943; dep. commr., crim. tribes, Oct., 1944.

TUFFIELD, GEORGE WILLIAM, Indian State Ryis. (transpn. supt. [senr. scale], G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 19th March, 1889).—Asst. transpn. supt., (junr. scale), July, 1926; ditto (senr. scale), June, 1937.

TUFNELL-BARRETT, HUGH, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 13th Jan., 1900).—Educ. at St. John's Sch., Leatherhead, and Wellington Cadet Coll., India; joined the service, 10th Sept., 1923; arrived, 16th Nov., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1924; offg. under-secty., pol. and appt. dept., March, 1927; confd., July, 1927; addl. dep. secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. dept., June, 1930; again, Apr., 1931; addtl. mag. and collr., Nov., 1931; offg. addl. dep. secty., pol. dept., Bengal, Apr., 1931; again, Nov., 1933; K.-i.-H. gold medal, June, 1938; mag. and collr. (confd.), Jan., 1939; dep. sec. govt. of Bengal home, Apr., 1939; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dep. of lab., Nov., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; jt. sec. to govt. of India (lab.) Apr., 1943; member cl. of state, July, 1943; civ. rep., Bengal govt., with H.Q., E. Cmd., 2nd addtl. sec., Bengal (home) and provl. press advr., Dec., 1943; dir.-genl., food, Calcutta, Jan., 1945.

TULL, THOMAS STUART, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 11th Oct., 1914).—Educ. at Rossah and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 20th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

TULLIS, J., B.Sc., late Dept. of Industries, Bihar (b. 20th May, 1888).—Joined the service, 4th Apr., 1924; offg. princpl. Appl., 1935; princpl. Appl., 1937; retd., Sept., 1945.

TUN, MAUNG SEIN NYO, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 24th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; civ. aff. serv. Aug.-Oct., 1945; sec. educn. dept., Nov., 1945.

TUN, U TIN, B.A. (Hons.), Burma Aud. and Accts. Serv. (cl. 1).—Apptd. on probn., 4th Jul., 1939; confd., 4th July, 1941; left in Burma, served with civ. aff. serv. from May, 1945; asst. acct.-genl., July, 1946.

TURNBULL, FRANCIS, FEARON, C.I.E., B.A. India Office (actg. asst. sec., pol. dept.) (b. 30th April, 1905).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Trinity Hall, Cambridge (Scholar); asst. clerk, House of Commons,

1928-30; apptd. asst. princpl., 12th Aug., 1930; asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state, Jan., 1932; pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, Sept., 1934; actg. princpl., July, 1935; confd., Apl., 1937; pte. secty. to secty. of state for India and for Burma, Nov., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1946; on staff of Cabinet Mission to India, Mar.-June, 1946; actg. asst. sec., political dept., Aug., 1946.

TURNBULL, GILBERT EDWARD, M.B.E., Burma Front Serv. (b. 1st Oct., 1908).—Apptd., 15th Jan., 1935.

TURNER, ALFRED CHARLES, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.B.E., M.A., Ind. C.S., (sec. fin. dept.) (b. 12th March, 1892).—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 11th May, 1920, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; M.B.E., June, 1919; jt. mag., Sept., 1921; offg. mag. and collr. from Feb. to Oct., 1924, and from March, 1925; confd., Feb., 1932; provl. supt. of census opcrns., United Provs., Apl., 1930; offg. dep. secty., United Provs., fin. dept., Nov., 1933; offg. secty. to govt., United Provs., rev. and p.w. depts., Feb., 1935; ditto, fin. dept., Apl., 1936; commr. of inc. tax, Bengal, Nov., 1938; ditto, Bombay Presidency, Sind and Baluchistan. Apl., 1939; jt. secty., fin. dept., May, 1941; C.I.E., June, 1941; addl. sec., Apl., 1944; C.S.I., Jan., 1946.

TURNER, ALFRED ELLIS, late Indian Police (Cent. Provs.) (b. 6th Aug., 1893).—Retd., July, 1938.*

TURNER, ERNEST JAMES, C.B., C.B.E., late India Office (b. 26th Feb., 1877).—Retd., Oct., 1937*; re-empld. temp. as asst. secty., Aug., 1940, to July, 1941.

TURNER, JOSHUA EDGAR CARRINGTON, late Indian Forest Service (b. 15th Sept., 1883).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

TURNER, LEONARD GEORGE HENRY, late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 15th Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

TURNER, SIR SIDNEY, C.B.E., B.A., F.I.A., late India Office (b. 18th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at Sherborne School, and Caius Coll., Cambridge (10th Wrangler); apptd. junr. clerk, acct.-genl.'s dept., 6th Oct., 1905; sec. to Lord Welby's commn. on Indian Mil. Service Family Pension Regns., 1913; senr. clerk, July, 1915; acct.-genl., Dec., 1921; also contr. of pension funds; C.B.E., June, 1927; commr. for inc. tax, Nov., 1930; apptd. chairman, applicn. cttee. for the Indo-Burma finl. award, Jan., 1936; apptd. commr. for the I.C.S. Family Pension Fund, Indian Military Service Family Pension Fund, Indian Military Widows' and Orphans' Fund, Superior Services (India) Family Pension Fund from Oct., 1937; kt., Jan., 1942; retd., Nov., 1945; and re-employed as contr. of pension funds.

TURNER, WILLIAM HENRY, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., Madras) (b. 2nd Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., May, 1920; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1925; confd., Oct., 1927; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1942; temp. ch. engr., Dec., 1945.

TURNER, WILLIAM HOVELL, M.A., M.C., India Office (auditor) (b. 10th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Perse Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to March, 1919; apptd. junr. clerk, p.w. dept., 29th Aug., 1919; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Oct., 1920; actg. princpl., acct.-genl.'s dept., March, 1922; transf. to finl. dept., June, 1923; confd. as princpl., Jan., 1924;

posted to econ. and overseas dept., Apl., 1932; actg. dep. clk. of council, Jan.-July, 1932; secty., Indian del. to Assembly of League of Nations, 1932 and 1933; asst. to auditor, March, 1934; sec. to Burma Office as asst. secty. Aug., 1940; and to Min. of supply, Feb., 1943; auditor, Oct., 1943.

TUT, MAUNG TIN, M.A., Barr. late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Dulwich, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 16th Jan., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt. agric. dept., July, 1924; jt. regr., co-op. socs., June, 1927; under-secty., govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Apl., 1928; dep.-secty., ditto, June, 1929; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1933; confd., Dec., 1935; dep. secty., fin. dept., govt. of Burma, May, 1935; secty., forest dept., Burma, Dec., 1935; ditto, fin. dept., June, 1938; commr. on spl. duty, premier's office, Jan., 1941; advr. and secty. reconstr. dept., 1944; author of essays on 'The Kingdom of Pagan in Legend and History', 'Burma's Part in the War', 'Tales of Burma Labour Corps, etc.,'; C.B.E., Jan., 1946; retd., Apl., 1946.

TWELLS, Miss HAZEL VIOLET, M.B.E., B.A. (Lond.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 7th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as inspectress of girls'schs. in Bombay, 1st Oct., 1922; M.B.E., June, 1942.

TWINBERROW, JOHN DENIS, M.C., Indian Police (ch. supt.) (b. 14th February, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on probn., N.W.F.P., 22nd Feb., 1924; confd., Apl., 1927; supt. Jan., 1941; ch. supt. N.W. Rly., watch and ward, May, 1946.

TWYNAM, SIR HENRY JOSEPH, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A. (Man.), Ind. C.S. (b. 24th Apl., 1887).—Educ. at Pensionat de la Salle, Rouen, Manchester and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Bengal, Apl., 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1916; I.A.R.O., June, 1915, to Jan., 1919; sec., Bengal employment and labour board, Jan., 1919; vice-presdnt., Cooch Behar state, Feb., 1920, to Apl., 1921; mag. and collr., June, 1924; secty. to govt. of Bengal, rev. and irrign. depts., March, 1929; offg. ch. sec. to govt. of Bengal, July, 1932; offg. commr., Oct., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; temp. addl. member, bd. of rev. Bengal, June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, variously from Apl., 1936; C.S.I., May, 1937; actg. govr. of Assam, Feb., 1939, to Oct., 1939; actg. govr., Central Provs. and Berar, June, 1940, to Sept., 1940; govr. Central Provs. and Berar, Oct., 1940 to Sept., 1946; K.C.S.I., Oct., 1940.

TYABJI, BADRUDDIN FAIZ HASAN BADRUDDIN, B.A., INDIAN C.S. (dep. sec. govt. of India, P. and D. dept.) (b. 12th November, 1907).—Educ. at Bombay University and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., under-secty., home and pol. depts., Oct., 1936; secty., Punjab and N.W.F.P., jt. pub. serv. commr., May, 1937; under-secty. to govt. of India, defence dept. (offg.), Dec., 1938; attache, defence dept., March, 1939; under-secty. to govt. of India, defence dept., Apl., 1939; under-secty., govt. of Punjab, Sept., 1939; dep. commr., Apl., 1940, to Nov., 1941; contr. of

supply, Karachi, Sept., 1942; ditto, Bombay, Sept., 1943; dep. secty., govt. of India (planning and devel.), Nov., 1944.

TYABJI, HATIM BADRUDDIN, Judl. Dept., Sind, apptd. judge, court of judl. commr., Sind, Feb., 1940; judge, ch. ct., Sind, Apl., 1940.

TYABJI, SALMAN BADR-U-DIN, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 6th Nov., 1883).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

TYAGARAJA, AYYAR, P.C., B.A., B.L., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. Jan., 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

TYDD, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian Police (Burma) (b. 12th November, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 3rd Dec., 1929; confd. Dec., 1931; offg. supt., Dec., 1935; asst. commr. of police, Rangoon, May, 1938; transf. to customs dept. as asst. collr. (preventive) Feb., 1940; civ. aff. serv. to 1945; dist. supt., 1946; on I.p.r., Jan., 1947.

TYLER, SIR HENRY HEWEY FRANCIS MACDONALD, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 13th Feb., 1877).—Retd., May, 1931*.

TYMMS, SIR FREDERICK, C.I.E., M.C., Civil Aviation Directorate (b. 4th Aug., 1889).—Apptd. dir. of civil aviation in India, 23rd Feb., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; kt., June, 1941; dir.-genl., civil aviation, Sept., 1945.

TYNDALE, ARTHUR CARRINGTON, O.B.E., M.A., late Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (b. 20th Nov., 1895).—Retd., Nov., 1941.

TYSON, JOHN DAWSON, C.S.I., C.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (b. 14th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Aldenham Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Aug., 1914; joined the service, 14th Feb., 1920; arrived, 11th May, 1920, and posted to Bengal; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, fin. and commerce dept., Nov., 1922, to March, 1924; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1924; offg. ch. presdy. mag., June, 1926; secty. to agent of govt. of India in South Africa, May, 1927; pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, June, 1930; C.B.E., Jan., 1933; mag. and collr., Sept., 1935; advr. to H.H. the Maharajah of Cooch Behar, March, 1936; commr., Dacca divn., March, 1938; secty. to govr., Bengal, May, 1938; jt. secty., dept. of e., h. and l., govt. of India, Nov., 1939; secty., ditto, Apl., 1940; confd. July, 1940; sec. to govr., Oct., 1945; C.S.I., June, 1945.

U (see also Maung).

U BA AYE, B.A., Burma Police (asst. supt.).—Apptd. on probn., Jan., 1939; transf. to milty. dept., Oct., 1940.

U BA U, SIR, M.A., Barr., Burma Commn. (judge, hg. ct. Rangoon.)—Apptd. govt. prosec., Apl., 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; confd., Jan., 1928; offg. judge, high ct., Rangoon, Feb., 1930; confd., 1932; judge again 1945; kt., Jan., 1946.

U BA ZAN, A.T.M., B.A., late Burma C.S. (b. 25th Feb., 1892).—Retd., Dec., 1942.

U HLA (1) A.T.M., B.A., Burma C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 14th Sept., 1892).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., June, 1922; dep. commr., from Apl., 1938; A.T.M., 1937.

U KYAW (3) O.B.E., A.T.M., B.A., Burma C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 12th July, 1896).—Apptd. extra asst. commr. May, 1921; dep. commr., June, 1934; sec. to govt. educ. dept., Oct., 1937; jt. sec. home. def. and forests dept., press and publicity offr., July, 1941; spec. duty offr., home

and judl. dept., Feb., 1946; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; again dep. commr., Aug., 1946.

U KYAW DIN, A.T.M., B.A., Burma C.S. (sec. ind. and lab. dept.) (b. 7th Mar., 1894).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Apl., 1922, under secty. to govt., judicial dept., Jan., 1930; ditto, local govt. dept., March, 1934; dep. commr., Apl., 1938; addl. secty. to govt. educn. dept., Apl., 1938; secty., educn. dept., Aug., 1940; dep. commr., Oct., 1945; sec., ind. and lab. dept., Feb., 1946.

U KYAW U, Barr., Burma Judicial Dept. (b. 9th Nov., 1890).—Dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1934; on I.p.r.

U MYA (3), A.T.M., late Burma Judl. Dept., (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 11th March, 1886).—Joined the service, Dec., 1909; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1936; retd.

U PO HAN, Barr.-at-law, Member, Public Service Commn., Feb., 1938.

UP PO SA, B.A., A.T.M., F.R.E.S., late Burma C.S., (b. 13th Aug., 1890).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Dec., 1922; dep. commr., June, 1938; jt. regr. co-op. socs., Burma, Nov., 1939; land commr., Rangoon, May, 1941; retd., Aug., 1945.

U THA TUN KHINE, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 22nd Apl., 1913).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Feb., 1937.

U THAN TIN, O.B.E., B.A., Burma C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 1st March, 1893).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Jan., 1922; dep. commr., June, 1935; dep. secty., agric. and forests dept., Aug., 1937; secty., agric. and forests dept., Sept., 1937; ditto, fin. dept., Feb., 1941; dep. commr., 1945; O.B.E., 1940.

U TUN MRA, U, A.T.M., Burma Police (cl. I) (supt.) (b. 8th June, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1914; promoted to Burma Police (cl. I), June, 1937.

UBAIDULLAH, KHAWAJAH, KHAN BAHADUR, O.B.E., Indian Railway Accts. Service (asst. dir. of fin. in the rly. bd.) (b. 30th Oct., 1900).—Joined the service, Feb., 1923; accts. offcr. Aug., 1935; asst. dir. of fin. in the rly. bd., Oct., 1936; under-secty. to govt. of India, dept. of supply, June, 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1945; dep. dir.-genl. (disposals) Apl., 1946.

ULLAH, CHAUDHRI NIAMAT (see Niamat Ullah, Chaudhri).

ULLAH, H. M. SALAMAT (see Salamat Ullah, H. M.).

ULLAH, MUHAMMAD SANA ULLAH (see Muhammad, S. U.).

UMAR HAYAT KHAN, Hon. Maj.-Gen. NAWAB MALIK, Sir, Tiwana, of Kalra, G.B.E., K.C.I.E., M.V.O. (b. 1874).—Educ. at Aitchison Chief's Coll., Lahore; served in Somaliland; joined Tibet expedition; was attached to late Amir of Afghanistan; M.V.O., Dec., 1911; served in France and Mesopotamia in Great War; K.C.I.E., June, 1916; represented Punjab, Delhi War Conference, 1918; served in 3rd Kabul war; C.B.E., June, 1919; member, Esher cottage, 1920; member, council of State, 1926; member of Council of India, June, 1929, to June, 1934; Hon. A.D.C. to King, Nov., 1930; G.B.E., June, 1934; hon.-col., 19th (K.G.O.) Lancers, Oct., 1935.

UNDERWOOD, FREDERICK LEWIS, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 16th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 10th Apl., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Sept., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1934;

cent. intell. offer., Madras presdy., Feb., 1936, to May, 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

UNGER, OSCAR RAMSEY, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bihar (inspr.-genl. of prisons) (b. 12th Sept., 1889).—First commn. dated 16th July, 1915; in milty. employ to 13th Apl., 1924; supt., central jail, Gaya, Jan., 1925; ditto, Hazaribagh, Feb., 1925; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933.

UNITT, ALFRED, Indian Police (suprt., Punjab) (b. 22nd June, 1902).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1921; comdt., Baluchistan levy, May, 1924; offg. supt., March, 1928; confd., Aug., 1932; asst. inspr.-genl., civic guards, Apl., 1942; senr. supt., Quetta, Oct., 1944; again supt., Nov., 1945.

UNNITHAN, KRISHNAN MADHAVAN, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 13th Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 17th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, sub-collr. and jt. magte., Aug., 1930; dep. commr. of excise, Jan., 1940; ditto, commercial taxes, Jan., 1944; inspr. of municipal cls., etc., Apl., 1944.

UNWIN, RICHARD, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 6th Dec., 1887).—Retd., Dec., 1942.†

UPTON, SIR THOMAS EVERARD TICHBORNE, Kt., late Legislative Dept., Govt. of India.—Retd., Apl., 1932.*

U'REN, CECIL WILLIAM EDWARD, Indian Police (suprt., Bombay) (b. 9th Nov., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 4th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Feb., 1932, Jan., and Sept., 1933; offg. dep. commr., Bombay, Feb., 1936; confd. as supt., Oct., 1939; Indian police medal, 1941; dep. commr. (special bch.), June, 1943 to May, 1944; supt. police, Belgaum, Sept., 1944.

USMAN, SIR MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Usman).

USMANI, ISHRAT HUSAIN, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag. Madras) (b. 15th Apl., 1917).—Apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1945.

UTTAM SINGH, SARDAR SAHIB (exec. engr., central p.w.d.) (b. 24th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1922; exec. engr., 10th Jan., 1934.

UZIELLI, HERBERT REX, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st Aug., 1890).—Retd., July, 1940.†

VACHHA, JAMSHEDJI BEJANJI, Khan Bahadur, C.I.E., B.A., B.Sc., late Bombay S.C. (b. 26th May, 1879).—Retd., May, 1939.*

VAGHAIWALLA, RUSTOM BEHERAMSHAH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 24th Jan., 1919).—Educ. at Bombay Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1942; served in Assam as asst. commr.

VAHID, SYED ABDUL (see Syed Abdul Vahid).

VAIDIANATHAN, V. I.—Apptd. physicist, irrign. research inst. Punjab, Apl., 1932; on depts. to Madras, as dir. irrign. research, July, 1945 to March, 1946.

VAIDYA, JAGANNATH BALKRISHNA, M.R.C.S., Maj., I.M.S., retd., late Medical Dept., United Provs. (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—First commn., Jan., 1914; civil surgeon, United Provs., Apl., 1930; retd.

VAIDYANATHAN, PERUVEMBA PARASURAMAIYER, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. judge, Bengal) (b. 1st Jan., 1915).—Educ. at Madras Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Sept., 1938,

and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte. and dep. collr., Dec., 1939; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1943; dist. judge, Dec., 1944.

VAISH, RADHA KRISHNA, M.A., Imperial Customs Service (b. 28th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service, Apl., 1924; asst. collr. of customs, Apl., 1926; confd. as asst. commr., July, 1943.

VALLURI KAMESWARA, RAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 15th July, 1914).—Educ. at the Board High Sch., Razole, Madras Univ., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 9th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte. and dep. collr., May, 1941; under-sec., fin. dept., Aug., 1941; dep. contr., procurement and purchase, May, 1944; dep. dir. of supply, dep. of civil supplies, Calcutta.

VAN DER BEEK, AUBREY GUILDFORD, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 22nd Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, 4th March, 1914, as asst. engr. (temp.); ditto (perm.), March, 1915; exec. engr., Burma rlys., Sept., 1932.

VANDERTAELEN, F. P., Indian State Rlys. (b. 2nd Jan., 1899).—Joined 13th Jan., 1928; ch. traff. mgr. G.I.P. Rly., July, 1945.

VAN SOMERAN, LEONARD WILLIAM, V.D., late Indian State Railways (E.B. Rly.) (b. 16th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

VANCE, ROBERT LANCELOT, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (legation surg.).—First commn., 30th May, 1916; in milty. employ to 29th commn., 1928; offg. C.M.O., W.I. States Agency and resdy., surg., Rajkot, May, 1928, and again, March, 1933; offg. civil surg., Quetta, Aug., 1928; confd., Apl., 1931; resdy. surg., Kashmir, May, 1935; legation surg., Nepal, March, 1937.

VARADACHARI, RAGHVA DIKSHIDAR, Rao Bahadur, Income Tax Dept. (commr. of inc. tax) (b. 23rd Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, Apl., 1923; inc. tax offcr., Apl., 1923, to Dec., 1936; asst. commr. of inc. tax, Dec., 1936; offcr. on spec. duty, Feb., 1939, to Apl., 1940; commr. of inc. tax, Punjab, 1941; confd. as commr., June, 1943.

VARADACHARIAR, SIR SRINIVASA, B.A., LL.B. (judge federal ct. of India) (b. 20th June, 1881).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, 3rd March, 1934; judge, federal ct. of India, Feb., 1939; kt., Apl., 1939.

VARGHESE, THOPPI ANTHONY, Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 17th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Madras Univ., Univer. Coll., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; sub-collr., Apl., 1932; sec., central road traffic bd., May, 1942; dep. sec. fin. dept., June, 1943; collr. and dist. mag., Apl., 1943.

VARMA, B. B., M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (b. 30th Nov., 1897).—Joined 18th Nov., 1920; direct. rly. bd., Nov., 1945.

VARMA, BADRI NATH, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 27th Aug., 1917).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in Central Prov. as asst. commr.

VARMA, BRIJ BIHARI, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 30th Nov., 1897).—Joined service, Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., O. & R. rly., Nov., 1921; exec. engr. provi. perm., E.I. rly., July, 1938; M.B.E., Jan., 1942.

VARMA, BRIJ BHUSHAN SARAN (see Brij, B. S. V.).

VARMA, RAGHUNANDAN PRASAD, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., N.W. rly.) (b. 5th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; dep. ch. eng. (provl.-perm.), Oct., 1939; divl. supt. (snr.), Jan., 1940; dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1941; ret'd., Dec., 1944.

VARMA, RUP LALL, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th Dec., 1904).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Assam, 2nd Jan., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1937; under sec., p.w.d., Oct., 1942.

VARMA, SARADA PRASHAD, Babu Indian Police (asst. supt., Orissa) (b. 15th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 2nd Jan., 1931; confd., Dec., 1931.

VARMA, SUKHEV PRASAD, Barr., late Judl. Dept., Bihar.—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, 23rd Jan., 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1946.

VAS, EDWIN THOMAS CHRISTIAN, B.Sc., M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 25th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service, 20th July, 1921; asst. consvr., Aug., 1921; dep. consvr., Feb., 1927.

VASADARJAN, Candadai, Maj. B.A., Ind. Posts and Tels. (b. 2nd Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Mar., 1922; confd. March, 1927; dep. p.m.g. (asst. dir.-genl.) Feb., 1946.

VASIST, SHYAM SUNDER, B.A. (Hons.), M.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (asst. transptn. supt., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 1st Aug., 1902).—Joined service, Feb., 1926; asst. transportation supt., Feb., 1929; asst. transptn. (traffic and commercial) supt., senior scale, provl., July, 1938; confd., Oct., 1939.

VASUDEV, KESHAVRAO BALKRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay C.S. (b. 14th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, Oct., 1907; dep. collr., Aug., 1909; actg. asst. judge and sess. judge, Apl., 1913; asst. judge and jt. judge and addl. sess. judge, Oct., 1921; judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1924; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1936; confd., Nov., 1937; ret'd., Jan., 1943.

VASUDEY, RAO (Sind) (b. 6th June, 1905).—Joined Jan., 1926; held non-gaz. appts. to Sept., 1944; asst. sec. fin. dept. and asst. dir. civil supplies (temp.) Sept., 1944; asst. sec. fin. dept., May, 1946.

VASUDEVA, BHANDARI, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 8th Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Sept., 1933.

VAZIFDAR, SOHRAB SHAPOORJI, L.M. & S. (Bom.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), Lieut.-Col. (ret'd.), late I.M.S. (b. 1st Aug., 1883).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

VELLODI, MULLATH VIKKARA, Rao Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. 23rd Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

VELLODI, MULLATH KADINGI, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Civil Service (dep. high commr. for India, in London) (b. 14th January, 1896).—Educ. at Presidency Coll, Madras; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1922; sub-collr. and mag. Jan., 1924; spl. asst. settlt. offcr., Jan., 1926; spl. settlt. offcr., Sept., 1927; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1936; offg. dir. of land records, Madras, Apl., 1933; jt. secty., bd. of rev., Madras, Nov., 1933; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from March, 1937; sec., govt. of Orissa, educ., etc., depts., Dec., 1937; secty. govt. of Orissa; revenue and developmts. depts., Nov., 1938; price adv. offcr., govt. of India, Feb., 1940; export trade contr., Bombay, Nov., 1942;

C.I.E., June, 1944; textiles commr. to Sept., 1945; dep. high commr. for India, Nov., 1945.

VENGANAD KOVILAGAM RAJAGOPALA AHTMBAN (see Rajagopala Thamban, V. K.).

VENKATA ACHARYA, ATUR RANGASWAMI, Rao Bahadur, B.E. Ind. Serv. of Engrs. (ch. engr.) (b. 21st June, 1895).—Joined the service as appr. engr., Madras, 30th June, 1917; asst. engr., June, 1918; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; dep. ch. exec. engr., July, 1927; suptg. engr., (confd.), Mar., 1944; ch. engr., Mar., 1946.

VENKATA RAO, GHANTA (sany. engr., Madras) (b. 18th Feb., 1886).—Apptd. dep. sany. engr., 3rd March, 1923; offg. sany. engr., Sept., 1931; confd., Feb., 1935.

VENKATA REDDI NAYUDU, RAI BAHADUR, SIR Kurma, K.C.I.E.—Member of govr.'s exec. council, Madras, 26th Apl., 1934 to March, 1937; actg. govr. of Madras, June, 1936; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

VENKATA, SUBRAMANYAN MINAKHISUNDARAM (see Subramanyan Minakhisundaram, V.).

VENKATACHAR, CADAMBI SHESHACHAR, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., Ind. C.S. (b. 11th Jul., 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ., University Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 15th Jan., 1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., 1925; offg. magte. and collr., March, 1927; under-sec. to the agent to the govr.-genl. in States of W.I., Oct., 1927; first asst. to resdt., Baroda, Nov., 1927; under-sec. to A.G.G. in States of W.I., Dec., 1928; census supt. C.I., Apl., 1930; offg. dep. commr., United Provs., Apl., 1933; agent to Govt. of India in British Malaya, Sept., 1937; O.B.E., June, 1941; C.I.E., June, 1946.

VENKATAKRISHNA AYYAR, LAKSHMINARAYANA AYYAR, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 7th May, 1888).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 18th July, 1912; asst. engr., July, 1913; exec. engr., July, 1922; suptg. engr., Dec., 1939; ch. engr., Madras, Aug., 1940; ret'd., May, 1943.

VENKATAPPIAH, BURRA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 4th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; confd. as dep. sec. (fin.), June, 1940; jt. sec. (fin.), temp., Aug., 1942; sec. fin. March, 1946.

VENKATARAMA AYYAR, THIRUVADAMARUDUR SUBRAMANYA., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Madras) (b. 10th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service as appr. engr., June, 1921; asst. exec. engr., June, 1922; offg. profro., coll. of engrg., Guindy, Aug., 1927; exec. engr., Nov., 1931; suptg. engr., Apl., 1943.

VENKATARAMA AYYAR, TIRUKKARUGAVUR NANA AYYAR, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 19th July, 1883).—Retd., July, 1938.*

VENKATARAMAN, B., Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Oct., 1901).—Joint dir. rly. bd., Oct., 1945.

VENKATARAMA RAMACHANDRA AYYAR (see Ramachandra Ayyar, V.).

VENKATARAMAN, KOMARATCHI SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 25th Jan., 1913).—Educ. at Pachayappa's Coll., Madras, Madras Univ., University Coll., London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935;

arrived in India, Oct., 1835, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1937; city, 1st cl., mag., Apl., 1940; judl. trng., June, 1942; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1944.

VENKATARAMAN, MADU SUBRAMANIAM IYER, B.A., (Traffic mgr. Madras Port Trust) (b. 18th June, 1902).—Apptd. traffic probr., Nov., 1925; spec. supt., Nov., 1930; asst. traffic mgr., Apl., 1931; actg. traffic mgr. var. from Mar., 1935.

VENKATARAMAN, SWAMINATHA ADUTHURAI, C.I.E., M.A., Ind. C.S. (dir.-genl. indus. and supplies) (b. 22nd June, 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922; and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Apl., 1931; dep. secty., fin. dept., Apl., 1932; regr. of co-operative societies, Madras, March, 1939; confd., collr., prov. subst., June, 1940; dep. dir.-genl. of supply, dept. of supply, Jan., 1942; jt. sec., branch secretariat, Calcutta, dept. of supply, July, 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; jt. sec. govt. of India, supply dept., Oct., 1944; dir.-genl., ind. and supplies, Dec., 1945.

VENKATARAMAN, ANAIKUPAM RAMASWAMI, Ind. Serv. of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 16th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service 1st May, 1933; confd., Sept., 1933; exec. engr., April, 1942.

VENKATARAMAN, SIR TIRUVADI SAMBASIVAYYER, C.I.E., B.A., Rao Bahadur, late Indian Agricultural Service (Madras) (b. 1884).—Retd., June, 1939.*

VENKATARAMANA RAO NAYUDU, PAKALA, Rao Bahadur.—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1935; retd., 1942.

VENKATARAMANA RAJU, PATTAYYA RAJU, Rao Bahadur, B.E., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Ind. Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 14th June, 1918; asst. engr., June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., Feb., 1922; offg. exec. engr., June, 1925; confd., June, 1928; suptg. engr., March, 1944.

VENKATARAMANI, P., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 28th March, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Nov., 1931; dep. consvr., Nov., 1936.

VENKATASUBBA RAO, SIR MUTTA, Kt., B.A., B.L., late Madras Judicial Dept., (b. 18th July, 1878).—Retd., July, 1938.*

VENKATASUBRAMANIAN, VAITHINATHIER (see Subramanian, V. V.).

VENKATESWARAN, AYYAR, Gopala, O.B.E., Ind. C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 19th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct. 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; sub-collr., May, 1932; ch. presdy. mag., Nov., 1941; sec., bd. of rev. Oct., 1944; and again, May, 1945; O.B.E., June, 1946.

VENKATESWARAN, SUNDARAM, C.I.E., M.B.E., B.A., Ind. C.S., (sub-collr. Madras) (b. 2nd Jan., 1901).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the serv., 30th Nov., 1925; sub-collr., Aug. 1927; sec. to bd. of rev., Madras, Nov., 1938; provl. textiles commr., Oct., 1942; M.B.E., June, 1943; C.I.E., June, 1946.

VENKATARAYA PAI, GURPUR, M.A., Madras Port Trust (b. 27th June, 1899).—Apptd. ch. acct., Madras Port Trust, 21st Apl., 1932.

VENUGOPAL, C. T., Indian State Ryvs. (b. 26th May, 1907).—Joined 15th Apl., 1930; dep. ch. accts. offr. G.I.P. Rly., Oct., 1944.

VERDON, PHILIP, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., (dist. medl. offcr., Madras) (b. 22nd Feb., 1886).—First commn., dated 8th Aug., 1917; in milty. employ until 30th March, 1925; dist. medl. offcr., Feb., 1925; offg. surg., 4th dist., Nov., 1928; confd., June, 1929; supt., govt. ophthalmic hospital, etc., Madras, Apl., 1937.

VERGHESE, GEORGE, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (Orissa) (b. 9th June, 1888).—First commn., 27th Aug., 1917; in milty. employ to March, 1933; supt., central jail, Orissa, July, 1933; dir. of health and inspr.-genl. of prisons, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1941; retd., June, 1943.

VERMA, CHHOTELAI, I.S.O., Rai Bahadur, late Central Provs. C.S. (b. 6th Jan., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

VERMA, KAMALAKANTA (ch. justice, high ct., Allahabad).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, Nov., 1937; Ch. justice, ditto, Sept., 1946.

VERMA, KRISHNA PRASAD, Rai Sahib, B.A., late Income Tax Dept., Central Provs. (b. 1st June, 1882).—Retd., June, 1938.*

VERNEDE, RAYMOND VEVEYSAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Bradford and Hertford Coll., Oxford, joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928; and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and coll.; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1935; confd., Nov., 1942.

VESUGAR, JAMSHED BURJORJI, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 26th May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 22nd Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1926; under-secty., p.w.d., b. and r. branch, Apl., 1937; consltg. engr. to govt. of India (roads), May, 1942; suptg. engr. (subst.), Aug., 1943; industl. advr. to govt. of India, Apl., 1944.

VIBART, FRANK, Indian State Ryvs. (supt., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 17th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1911; asst. traffic supt., Apl., 1915; supt., Sept., 1929; senr. sc., May, 1932.

VICK, HENRY JOHN HOWARD, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 28th Sept., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

VICKERS, CHARLES ALFRED, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 31st Oct., 1899).—Educ. at King Edward's High Sch., Birmingham, Quetta Cadet Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1924, and served in Burma as an asst. commr.; sub. divl. offcr., June, 1925; offg. under-sec., home and pol. dep., Nov., 1926; judge, sub-divl. courts, Aug., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1934; civ. aff. serv., Feb., 1945; dep. sec., home dept., June, 1945; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1945.

VICKERS, HAROLD JAMES, C.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl.) (b. 1st June, 1895).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1914; on milty. duty from 16th Sept., 1916 to 17th Feb., 1920; asst. supt., May, 1920; asst. dist. offcr., frontier constabulary, June, 1920; dist. offcr., ditto, Dec., 1922; supt., Oct., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1926; dep. dir., intelligence bureau, Nov., 1933; C.B.E., June, 1936; dep. inspr.-genl., Dec., 1940; Indian police medal, 1944; offg. inspr.-gen. police, N.W.F. Prov., Oct., 1945; prov. transpt. contr., etc., Oct., 1945.

VICKERY, PHILIP CRAWFORD, C.I.E.,

O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 23rd Feb., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1909; asst. supt., Apl., 1911; on mily. duty, 1st Nov., 1915 to 24th March, 1921; supt., Aug., 1921; O.B.E., June, 1923; on depn. outside India, from Oct., 1926; C.I.E., June, 1939.

VIDYARTHI, PUTTAN LAL, late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 1st July, 1886).—Retd. July, 1941.

VIJAYARAGHAVA ACHARYA, SIR TIRUVALLYANGUDI, K.B.E., M.A., Diwan Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. 27th Aug., 1875).—Retd., Oct., 1935.*

VIJAYARAGHAVAN, KHERUVARY KOTIETH, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 2nd Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Madras Christian Coll. and Law Coll.; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; tempy. dep. commr. of excise, May, 1930; offg. collr., Sept., 1934; offg. dep. secty., finance dept., Madras, Apl., 1935; confd. collr., Dec., 1940; sec. p.w.d., Apl., 1943; commr. of labour, June, 1944.

VIJAYASINHJI, KUMAR SHRI, Indian Police Bombay (b. 25th Jan., 1905).—Joined 17th May, 1930; prom. to Indian Police as supt., 15th Apl., 1944.

VILLAR, ARTHUR REGINALD, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 28th June, 1888).—Retd., Apl., 1942.

VINAYANAND PATHAK, PANDIT, late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 17th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service as dep. supt., 1st Nov., 1912; asst. supt., Feb., 1920; offg. supt., Nov., 1921; confd., Feb., 1927; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Aug., 1933; asst. I.G. rly. police, Aug., 1938; retd., 17th Feb., 1944.

VINDEN, H., C.I.E.—Apptd. dir. of seln. of personnel in employt. seln. bureau, govt. of India, Oct., 1945; C.I.E., June, 1945.

VINING, ARTHUR CYRIL, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. optg. supt., N.W. rly.) (b. 22nd Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 22nd Nov., 1909; on mily. duty, 1st Apl., 1916; dist. traffic supt., Aug., 1922; divl. transp. supt., Oct., 1924; dep. ch. opertg. supt. (provl.), Dec., 1937; confd., Dec., 1940; retd., Aug., 1944.

VINING, LEONARD EDWARD, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Oct., 1887).—Retd., Apl., 1941†

VIRA, DHARMA, O.B.E., Ind. C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 20th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. Lucknow Univ. and Lond. Sch. of Oriental Stud.; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arr. in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst mag. and collr.; mag. and collr. (confd.), Nov., 1945; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

VISHAN, BHAGWAN, Rai Bahadur, Lala (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 7th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 8th March, 1920; sub-divl. offr., Sept., 1933; on depn. to govt. of India, May, 1934; offr. in charge, excise intell. bur. Punjab., Aug., 1940; offr. on spec. duty, office of fin. commr., Nov., 1942; sub-divl. offr., March, 1943; dep. commr., Sept., 1943; Rai Bahadur, June, 1942.

VISHNU SAHAY (see Sahay, V.).

VISVANATHA AYYAR, PATHIVAD SESHACHALA, M.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 10th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Madras, Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London.

Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., May, 1933; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from Apl., 1936; collr. and dist. mag., May, 1943 to Jan., 1945; again sub-collr.; dep. sec. e. and p.h., Feb., 1946; sec., ditto, May, 1946.

VISVANATHA RAO, VELLIYUR NOTT, Diwan Bahadur, C.I.E., M.A., B.L., late Madras C.S. (b. 16th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service, 8th Feb., 1913; dep. collr., March, 1916; offg. collr., March, 1930; offg. secty. to govt., Madras law and educn. dept., Sept., 1931; sub-collr., June, 1931; actg. secty. to govt., Madras, law and educn. dept., Sept., 1931; secty. to govt. of Madras development dept., Feb., 1939; collr., Jan., 1940; retd., 16th Oct., 1944, and re-employed as collr.; C.I.E., June, 1946.

VISWANATHAN, VENKATA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 25th Jan., 1909).—Educ. at University Coll., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford. Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1936; asst. settlmt. offcr., Oct., 1936; settlmt. offcr., Oct., 1937; attaché, dept. of e.h. and l., govt. of India, Nov., 1940; under-sec., ditto, Apl., 1941; under-secy., dept. of Indians overseas, govt. of India, Oct., 1941; sec. to repr. of govt. of India in Ceylon, Aug., 1943; ditto, Burma and addtl. dep. sec., communcn. and reltns. dept., Sept., 1944.

VIVIAN, GRAHAM LINSSELL, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Ind. C.S., (United Provs.) (b. 1st Aug., 1887).—Educ. at Epsom Coll. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; ditto, and asst. settlt. offcr., Oct., 1916; jt. mag., June, 1917; on mily. duty, Sept., 1917, to Jan., 1919; special duty at Lucknow, Oct., 1922 to Feb., 1923; settlt. offcr., Oct., 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; confd., July, 1928; offg. presdt., ct. of wards, Feb., 1935, to Nov., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; advr. to govr., Apl., 1945; C.S.I., June, 1946; retd., Nov., 1946.

VOHRA, SAHDEV, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 5th July, 1917).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1940, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

VOMBATHKERE PANDRANG ROW (see Pandrang Row, V.).

VOORDUIN, WILLIAM LEONARD, (b. 26th July, 1897).—Apptd. hydro-elec. member, cent. tech. power bd., Dec., 1944.

VORLEY, JOHN STUART, C.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 19th Dec., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 23rd Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1924; C.B.E., Jan., 1942; on mil. duty Mar., 1943, to Nov., 1945; on l.p.r., Feb. 1947.

VYALL, FREDERICK STANLEY, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 8th Jan., 1887).—Retd., Jan., 1942.†

VYAS, BHANUSHANKAR SUNDARJI, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bombay) (b. 19th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; in Bhavnagar State as state engr., from May, 1924 to Jan., 1930; exec. engr., Oct., 1926; confd. as suptg. engr., Apl., 1944.

VYAS, DEVSHANKAR VAJESHANKAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 14th Aug., 1898).—Educ. at Bombay, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. i

1923; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1923, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; offg. judge and sess. judge, March, 1931; and again, Feb., 1933; confd., Nov., 1934; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1942; offg. judl. commr. W. India States Agency and Gujarat States, May 1945; dist. and sess. judge, Ahmedabad, Aug., 1945.

WACE, ERNEST WILLIAM CORNISH, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 11th May, 1894).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1914; on mil. duty from 5th March, 1915 to 17th Dec., 1917; asst. supt. Apl., 1918; supt., Sept., 1924; asst. to dep. inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Dec., 1926; dep. inspr.-genl. (confd.), Dec., 1939; addtl. ditto, C.I.D., March, 1940 to July, 1940; C.I.E., June, 1941; inspr.-genl. and jt. sec. to govt. May, 1945; dep. inspr.-genl. C.I.D., Oct., 1945; C.S.I., June, 1946; on l.p.r.

WACE, FERDINAND BLYTH, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Civil Service (Punjab) (b. 21st Feb., 1891).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 14th Dec., 1915, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from Sept., 1916, to Jan., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1923; colonization officer, Nili Bar, Nov., 1924; dep. commr. (provl.), Oct., 1926; confd., Oct., 1928; regr. co-op. societies, Punjab, Apl., 1933, and again, Jan., 1937; addl. secty. to govt., Punjab, July, 1935; offg. commr., Oct., 1936; commr., June, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; ret'd., 1946.

WADDELL, GILBERT, C.I.E., Ind. Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 24th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1914; offg. supt., Apl., 1920; confd., Feb., 1926; confd. in sel. grade, Oct., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1946.

WADDY, REV. RICHARD PATTESON STACY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 31st May, 1904).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., March, 1935; junr. chaplain, March, 1936.

WADE, HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. offcr., accts. dept.) (b. 7th Aug., 1893).—Educ. at Preston Gr. Sch.; served as second class clerk, India store depot, from 27th Jan., 1913; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 28th Feb., 1913, and att'd. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.; on mily. service from Aug., 1914 to April, 1919; services lent to high commr. for India from Apl., 1921; exec. offcr., Apl., 1922; higher exec. offcr., Apl., 1925.

WADHAMS, JOHN OSWALD, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 13th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Aug., 1929; on mil. duty, March, 1942 to Aug., 1942 and Nov., 1943 to Dec., 1945.

WADIA, BOMANJI JAMSHEDJI, late Judicial dept., Bombay.—Apptd. offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, 17th June, 1930; confd., June, 1931; ret'd., June, 1941.

WADIA, DARASHAW NOSHERWAN, M.A., B.Sc., F.G.S., F.R.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 23rd Oct., 1883).—Ret'd., Apl., 1939.*

WADIA, DARASHAW NOSHERWAN, (advr. on min. devel., planning and devel. dept.) (b. 23rd Oct., 1883).—Profr. of geology, P. of W. coll., Jammu (Kashmir) 1907-1921; asst. supt. geol. survey of India; geolog. surv. N.W. Punjab, N.W.F. Prov., and Kashmir Himalayas, 1921-1938; Palaeontologist, 1931-1934; Mineralogist, Ceylon govt., 1938-1944; advr. on min. devel., P. and D. dept., Jan., 1945.

WADIA, MANECK DHUNJISHAW, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col. (ret'd.), late I.M.S. (Punjab) (b. 5th July, 1883).—Ret'd., July., 1938.*

WADIA, SIR NAVROJI JEHANGIR, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Bombay) (b. 27th Aug., 1884).—Educ. at St. Vincent's High Sch., Poona; Bombay Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 19th Nov., 1909, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge; actg. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1923; dep. sec. govt. of Bombay, legal dept., and sec. to the leg. council (in addm.) Aug., 1925; reforms secy. (temp.), Dec., 1927; judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1928; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, Jan., and again June, 1933; confd., Dec., 1933; chairman, Bombay provl. delimitn. cttee., June, 1935; kt., Jan., 1944.

WADLEY, KEITH LLOYD HAMILTON, M.B.E., B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., cent. p.w.d.) (b. 5th Sept., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1930; transf. to Central P.W.D., Oct., 1935; exec. engr., Apl., 1940; M.B.E., June, 1943; suptg. engr., Feb., 1944.

WADLEY, M. F., Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th Oct., 1898).—Joined 13th Oct., 1919; contr. of stores, B.A. Rly., Dec., 1945.

WADSWORTH, SIR SIDNEY, Ind. C.S. (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 21st Dec., 1888).—Educ. at Loughborough Gr. Sch. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 8th Dec., 1913; asst. censor, Madras, Oct., 1914; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; temp. under sec. to govt., rev. (spl.) dept., June, 1918; sec., board of revenue, land revenue, Aug., 1922; actg. registrar, high ct., July, 1925; confd., Sept., 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1926; confd., July, 1927; judge, high ct., Madras, June, 1935; kt., Jan., 1946.

WADSWORTH, SYDNEY HERBERT, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 25th Dec., 1881).—Ret'd., Dec., 1941.*

WAGH, MAHARUDRA GANAPAT, Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 8th Nov., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Feb., 1940.

WAGLE, NITYANAND MANGESH, M.A., M.Sc. (Econ.), Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 16th Aug., 1914).—Educ. at Theosophical National Sch., Benares, Benares and Bombay Univ. and London Sch. of Economics; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 20th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

WAGSTAFF, HENRY WYNTER, C.S.I., M.C., Col., R.E., Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th July, 1890).—First Commn. dated 23rd Dec., 1910; asst. exec. engr. March, 1921; exec. engr. June., 1922; offg. supervisor of rly. lab., rly. bd., Apl., 1931; offr. on spec. duty under for. and pol. dept., Aug., 1936; conciliation officer (rlys.), Dec., 1937; dep. ch. engr. (provl.), Oct., 1939; dir., rly. bd., Dec., 1939; col., 1940; dep. ch. engr. (perm.), Jan., 1942; member rly. bd., May, 1942 to July, 1946; C.S.I., June, 1945; on l.p.r.

WAGSTAFFE, KEITH DESMOND, Indian Police (asst. supt., Punjab) (b. 15th June, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 20th Nov., 1927; confd., Nov., 1929; offg. supt., Jan., 1932; confd., Mar. 1942; King's police and fire service medal, June, 1942.

WAH, LAO HTIN (see Lao Htin Wah).
WAHI, PREM NATH, M.B., B.S., M.D., M.R.C.P. (Lond.), United Provs. Medl. Serv. (b. 10th Apl.,

1908).—Apptd. prof. of pathology, medl. coll., Agra (U.P.), 1st Aug., 1941.

WAIGHT, HARRY GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 16th March, 1892).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1928; sec., judl. dept., Bengal, Jan., 1944.

WAITE, HERBERT WILLIAM, C.I.E., late Indian Police (N.W.F.P.) (b. 30th June, 1887).—Retd., 1942.†

WAITE, REV. FRANCIS EDWARD WESTON, A.K.C., Indian Eccles. Estbt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 15th Apl., 1905).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Madras diocese, July, 1936; junr. chaplain, July, 1938.

WAKEFIELD, EDWARD BIRKBECK, C.I.E., Ind. C.S., Ind. Polit. Serv. (b. 24th July, 1903).—Educ. at Haileybury and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec., self-govt., dep., Punjab, Sept., 1930; asst. commr., Ajmer Merwara, Dec., 1930; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, March, 1933; on foreign service in Kalat state, Oct., 1933; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Feb., 1937; sec. to resdt. for Punjab States, Oct., 1937; presdt., council of regency, Nabha State, Nov., 1939; on deptn., 1941; on for. serv., Rewa states, Dec., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1945; dep. sec. (reforms) pol. dept., Jan., 1946; jt. sec. pol. dept. (temp.) July, 1946.

WAKELY, SIR LEONARD DAY, K.C.I.E., C.B., B.A., B.Sc., late India Office (b. 29th March, 1880).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. junior clerk, judl. and public dept., 15th Oct., 1902; resdt. clerk, Nov., 1902; private sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Apl., 1904; private sec. to perm. under-sec. of state, March, 1906 to Dec., 1909; senr. clerk, rev. and stat. dept., Oct., 1909; asst. sec., political dept., March, 1917, but temp. retained in rev. and stat. dept., until Api., 1919; acting sec., pol. dept., Feb., 1921; confd. as sec., pol. dept., Jan., 1924; C.B., June, 1924; actg. asst. under-sec. of state, Jan., 1929; confd., July, 1930; commr. of inc.-tax, Nov., 1930; actg. dep. under-sec. of state, Sept., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934; K.C.I.E., June, 1935; compiled decennial report on moral and material progress of India, 1913; ret'd., Oct., 1941; re-employed as asst. under-sec. of state, Oct., 1941 to Oct., 1943.

WAKELY, LEONARD JOHN DEAN, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep.-sec. to the govt. of India, defence dept.) (b. 18th June, 1909).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., Christ Church, Oxford, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., pol. dept., Nov., 1937; under-sec., defence dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1938; under-sec. to the Govr.-genl. (defence co-ordin.), Dec., 1938; under-sec. to the Govt. of India, defence co-ordin. dept. in addn., Feb., 1939; M.B.E., Jan., 1942; dep. sec., govt. of India (defence), Aug., 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1945; addl. dep. sec. govt. of India, legis. dept., Nov., 1945.

WALFORD, HENRY GORDON.—Apptd. addl. judge, Oudh ch. court, Lucknow, Jul., 1945.

WALI MUHAMMAD, M.A., Ph.D., late

Indian Educl. Service (United Provs.) (b. 18th Aug., 1886).—Retd., Aug., 1941.†

WALI-ULLAH, MOHAMMED (judge, high ct., Allahabad).—Apptd., Apl., 1944.

WALKER, FREDERIC PERCY, late Indian Police (b. 17th Nov., 1882).—Retd., July, 1937.*

WALKER, GEORGE ERIC DENHAM, M.B.E., Indian Police (b. 9th Sept., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn. 27th Nov., 1934; asst. to pol. offr., Sadiya, May, 1937; a.d.c. to the governor of Assam, Aug., 1940; asst. pol. offr. March, 1942; pol. offr. Aug., 1942; M.B.E., Jan., 1946.

WALKER, GAVIN, late Indian Police (b. 29th Nov., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Bengal, Nov., 1933; confd., June, 1936; ret'd., July, 1945.

WALKER, GEORGE DAVID, M.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 23rd Sept., 1884).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edin., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 4th Dec., 1908, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, Apl., 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1916 to Apl., 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; dep. commr., Nov., 1924; offg. commr., Nov., 1932; commr., Oct., 1935; Assam repres. to Indian legis. assembly (nom.), Jan., 1938; civ. liaison offr., Mar., 1942, to Sept., 1943; ret'd., Dec., 1943.

WALKER, SIR GILBERT THOMAS, Kt., C.S.I., M.A., D.Sc., Ph.D., F.R.S., late Meteorological Dept.—Dir.-genl. of observatories, Sept., 1906*;

WALKER, HAROLD MACKENZIE, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 26th July, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt. 17th Nov., 1915; divl. rolling stock offr., Nov., 1928; offg. divl. supt. (junr.), N.W. rly., Jan., 1936; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Sept., 1936 to Sept., 1937; dep. ch. mechl. engr., (provl.) May, 1944 to 1946; O.B.E., June, 1946.

WALKER, JAMES, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 4th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1925; dep. dir., dir.-genl. of supply, June, 1940.

WALKER, RONALD LESLIE, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian Civil Serv. (Bengal) (b. 9th Apl., 1896).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, fin. dept., March, 1924; under-sec., finl., commerce and marine depts., Nov., 1924; offg. dep. sec. to ditto, March, 1926; offg. secty., Indian Tariff Board, Apl., 1928; confd., Feb., 1929; offg. dir.-genl., comml. intell., Nov., 1928; offg. magte. and collr., July, 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; offg. dep. secty., fin., commerce and marine depts., Bengal, March, 1934; confd., Sept., 1934; offg. secty., ditto, Oct., 1934; secty., fin. dept. (confd.), Aug., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; advr. to govr., Aug., 1945; spec. offr. home and ch. min.'s dept., Mar., 1946; C.S.I., June, 1946.

WALKER, THOMAS JACK, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 4th July, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 15th Dec., 1923; offg. addl. supt., March, 1932; supt., July, 1936; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

WALKER, ULRIC WILLIAM FERRIER, C.I.E., M.C., M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (Punjab) (b. 3rd December, 1891).—Joined the service, Aug., 1920; prof. of surg., Punjab

veterinary colf., Oct., 1920; offg. princpl., ditto, March, 1929; Sept., 1930, Apl., 1932 and Nov., 1933; offg. dir. vetv. service, Punjab, Apl., 1935; dir., ditto, Jan., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1946; on l.p.r. May, 1946.

WALKER, WILLIAM FRANK, O.B.E., M.C., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., United Provs.) (b. 11th July, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; suptg. engr., Nov., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; ch. engr. (confd.), Nov., 1943.

WALL, WILLIAM GEORGE POYNER, M.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (princpl., trng. coll., Allahabad) (b. 7th March, 1889).—Joined the service as prof., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, 30th Nov., 1916; vice-princpl., training coll., Allahabad, Aug., 1923; offg. dir., technological laboratory, Bombay, Apl., 1926; princpl., govt. inter. coll., Allahabad, Dec., 1927; on spl. duty, office of dir. of public instrn., Apl., 1929; offg. inspr. of schools, Oct., 1930; confd., Nov., 1931; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., United Provs., Apl., 1932; princpl. trng. coll., Allahabad, and inspr. of schools, Oct., 1933; dir. of public instrn. May, 1943; (confd.). Sept., 1943.

WALL, ROY MANSFIELD, Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 30th Jan., 1920).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

WALL, WILLIAM RICHARD, O.B.E. (commr. of inc.-tax, Central and United Provs.) (b. 9th Nov., 1893).—Apptd. asst. commr. of inc.-tax, B. & O., 1st Dec., 1923; offg. commr. of inc.-tax, Apl., 1930, variously, to March, 1937; commr. of inc.-tax, Central and United Provs., March, 1937; commr., 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

WALLACE, CHARLES LINDSAY, M.C., B.A., late Indian C.S., United Provs. (b. 22nd Nov., 1887).—Retd., July, 1938.*

WALLACE, REV. JOHN MURDOCK, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (junr. chaplain) (b. 3rd Oct., 1907).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Madras Diocese, Oct., 1943; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1945.

WALLACE, WALTER IAN JAMES, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (offg. commr., Burma) (b. 18th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Bedford Modern and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1933; provl., Dec., 1937; confd. March, 1938; sec. def. dept., June, 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; civ. aff. serv., 1944; again dep. commr., 1945.

WALLER, SIR DAVID GRIERSON, Kt., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 4th July, 1872).—Retd., July, 1932.*

WALLER, RIGHT REV. EDWARD HARRY MANSFIELD, D.D., M.A. (Cantab.) late Madras Eccles. Dept. (b. 8th Dec., 1871).—Bishop of Madras from 1st Jan., 1923 to Nov., 1941.*

WALLER, FREDERICK JOHN, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Jan., 1884).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

WALLEY, ARTHUR FRANKS, Indian State Rlys. (supt. of tels., Burma Rlys.) (b. 14th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. tel. supt., 16th Nov., 1914; tel. supt., Nov., 1925.

WALLEY, DAVID O.B.E., Ind. C.S. (dep. sec. rev., United Provs.) (b. 15th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Gr. Sch., Swansea, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dep. secty., rev.

dept., United Provs., Apl., 1939; dep. ditto, Nov., 1939; O.B.E., June, 1946.

WALLIS, L. G. C., (see Coke-Wallis, L. G.).

WALLIS, WILLIAM CLIFFORD, India Office (b. 21st Apl., 1900).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 11th Aug., 1919, after previous service from 19th Oct., 1917; second class clerk, 29th Apl., 1920; exec. offcr., Apl., 1922; actg. higher ditto, Sept., 1936; confd., Jan., 1938; asst. admin. offcr., June, 1939; resdt. clerk, Apl., 1940 to Feb., 1942; pte. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state, Aug., 1941 to June, 1942; on loan to min. of prodn., June, 1942; ditto, govt. of India, July, 1944; addl. fincl. advr., mil. fin., July, 1944.

WALMSLEY, SIR HUGH, Kt., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal)—Retd., Nov., 1926.*

WALMSLEY, JAMES ANDREW, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bihar) (b. 4th Oct., 1912).—Educ. at Cambridge Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937; and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec., govt. of Bihar, fin. dept., May, 1940; sec. to govr., Oct., 1942; asst. fin. advr., supply, govt. of India, fin. and com. pool, Oct., 1943.

WALSH, SIR CECIL HENRY, Kt., K.C., M.A. (Oxon.), Barr., late Judl. dept., United Provs.—Retd., Nov., 1928.

WALSH, ERIC, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th June, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 20th Nov., 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. supt., May, 1927; confd., May, 1937.

WALSH, WILFRED CULLEN DE CLANCY, B.A., Indian Forest Service (ch. consvr., Bombay) (b. 5th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922; consvr. (confd.), May, 1942; offg. ch. consvr. of forests, Apl., 1945; ditto (confd.), Oct., 1945.

WALSH-ATKINS, LEONARD BRIAN, B.A., Burma Office (actg. princpl.) (b. 15th Mar., 1915).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. princpl., 11th Oct., 1937; resdt. clerk, May, 1939 to Apl., 1940; on mil. serv. from Nov., 1940, to June, 1945; actg., princpl. Nov., 1941; (in absentia); posted to Burma Office, July, 1945.

WALTERS, REV. THOMAS, Indian Eccles. Estab. (junr. chaplain) (b. 27th March, 1900).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Madras Diocese, Oct., 1938; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1940; on mily. duty from Sept., 1939, to May, 1946.

WALTON, SIR CUSACK, Kt., D.S.O., Col., R.E., retd., late Indian State Railways (N.W. rly.) (b. 26th Feb., 1878).—Retd., Feb., 1933.*

WALTON, SIR JOHN CHARLES, K.C.I.E., C.B., M.C., M.A., late Ind. Off. (b. 14th Mar., 1885).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; (Arnold Prize, 1909); sec. dept., Admiralty, Dec., 1908; junr. clerk, Indian Off. (mil. dept.), 29th Oct., 1909; resdt. clerk, Sept., 1910; transf. to judl. and public dept., Dec., 1911; reading clerk to council, Dec., 1911; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, July, 1913; on mily. serv. from May, 1916, to Dec., 1918; senr. clerk, March, 1917; asst. sec., March, 1921; sec. to Indian delgns. to Assembly of League of Nations, 1920 and 1923; actg. sec., industries and overseas dept., Jan. to March, 1924; a delg. for India, intl. opium conf., 1924-25; adv. intl. labour conf., 1927; advr. and sub. delg., ditto, 1928; actg. sec., pol. dept., Jan., 1929; ditto, Jan., 1930; sec., pol. (external) dept., Apl., 1930; asst. under-sec. of state, July, 1936;

commr. of inc.-tax, June, 1940; actg. under-sec. of state for Burma, May, 1941; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1942; on spec. duty, Oct., 1945; retd., Jan., 1946.

WANCHOO, KAILAS NATH, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 25th Feb., 1903).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Wadhamb Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, 1st Dec., 1926, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Oct., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge Oct., 1933; confd., Sept., 1936.

WANCHOO, NIRANJAN NATH, O.B.E., M.A., Ind. C.S. (dep. sec. govt. of Ind., food) (b. 1st May, 1910).—Educ. at Rangmahal Mission High Sch., Lahore, Univ. of the Punjab and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934 and served in Bihar, and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec., govt. of Orissa, h. and r. depts., Aug., 1938; on spec. duty, Madras, to Oct., 1940; under-sec., central govt., commerce dept., Nov., 1940; ditto, food, Dec., 1942; dep. sec., food dept., Feb., 1944; offcr. on spec. duty, Persian Gulf war trade bureau, March, 1942; dep. sec. govt. of India, (food), Oct., 1945; O.B.E., June, 1945.

WARD, WILLIAM JOSEPH, B.A., late Imperial Customs Service (b. 11th March, 1887).—Retd., Feb., 1941.

WARDLE, F. M., (b. 5th Aug., 1893).—Joined the serv., July, 1913; asst. engr., Jan., 1922; asst. exec. engr. (Jr. scale), July, 1931; exec. engr., Aug., 1944.

WARE, SIR FRANK C.I.E., F.R.C.V.S., Ind. Vety. Service (dir. of animal husbandry) (b. 22nd Feb., 1886).—Apptd. supt., civil vety. dept., Madras, Nov., 1912; ch. supt., civil vety. dept., Madras, Oct., 1925; offg. dir., Imperial Inst., vety. research, Muktesar, March, 1926, and again, June, 1929; prin., vety. coll., Madras, Nov., 1926; offg. vety. adviser to govt. of Madras, Nov., 1927; confd., May, 1928; dir. of vety. services, Madras, Sept., 1928; offg. dir., Imperial Inst. of vety. research, June, 1929; confd., Sept., 1930; offg. animal husbandry commr., Imperial council of agric. research, var., from Aug., 1935; confd., Jan., 1939; C.I.E., May, 1937; dir. of animal husbandry, Jan., 1944; kt., Jan., 1946.

WARMINGTON, REV. GUY WILSON, M.A., late Eccles. Dept. (b. 22nd May, 1886).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

WARNER, HERBERT WALTER, Indian Police (Burma) (b. 21st Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 19th May, 1920; offg. dist. supt., June, 1926; confd., Sept., 1928; asst. commr., Rangoon, Jan., 1936; admin. offr., Burma police, Dec., 1944; l.p.r. from Nov., 1945.

WARREN, AUBREY GRANVILLE, Lieut.-Col. (elec. inspr.) (b. 17th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. elec. inspr., United Provs., 1st Oct., 1914; offg. ditto, Sept., 1918; confd., Nov., 1934.

WARREN, DOUGLAS DAINTRY, C.I.E., M.C., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 17th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920; sub-collr. and jt. mag., June, 1922; offg. under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1926; pte. secty. to govt., June, 1929; dir. of land records, Madras, Apr., 1930; on for. serv. as commr., corp. of Madras, June, 1931; collr., July, 1940; sec., p.w.d., Madras, Dec., 1940; jt. sec. govt. of India (war transpt.),

Apr., 1943; offg. sec. ditto, Mar., 1945; jt. sec., ditto, Oct., 1945; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

WARREN, WILLIAM DOUGLAS MARDEN, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bihar) (b. 26th July, 1895).—Joined the service as dep. consvr., Bihar and Orissa, 19th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Bihar, 1924.

WARWICK, GEORGE DUNGATE, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 25th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 17th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Jan., 1927; dir., forest sch., Dec., 1936.

WASSOODREW, K. B. (see Vasudev, K. B.).

WATAL, ANAND PRAKASH (see Anand Prakash, W.).

WATERBURY, REV. FRANK GARFIELD, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 8th Nov., 1885).—Retd., Nov., 1940.

WATERFALL, SIR CHARLES FRANCIS, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 24th Feb., 1888).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 26th Nov. 1911, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr., dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1921; confd., Oct., 1924; dir. of land records, Central Provs., March, 1930; secty. in settlts. and land records, etc., depts., March, 1930; offg. finance sec. to govt., Central Provs., March, 1933; offg. commr., July, 1933; offg. ch. sec. to govt., Central Provs., June, 1934; C.I.E., June, 1935; ch. sec. to govt., Central Provs., Oct., 1936; C.S.I., May, 1937; chief commr., Andamans, Feb., 1938 to Dec., 1940; kt. Jan., 1946.

WATERFIELD, THOMAS EDWARD, Indian C.S., I.P. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 8th Oct., 1905).—Educ. at Clifton College, Magdalen College, Oxford; and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; regr., high ct., appellate side, Bombay, Dec., 1938; dist. and sess. judge, Ratnagiri, Aug., 1945.

WATNEY, NORMAN CHARLES, Indian State Rlys. (div. mech. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1901).—Asst. rolling stock offr., Jan., 1926; div. mech. engr., provl., June, 1939; confd., Aug., 1940.

WATSON, ANDREW HUGH, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 24th July, 1911).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 4th Dec., 1930; confd., Dec., 1932; civ. aff. serv. to Dec., 1945; dist. supt., 1946.

WATSON, CHARLES MARCUS GUY, Indian Police (b. 5th Nov., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1938.

WATSON, DENNIS GEORGE, C.I.E., Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 23rd April, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Feb., 1912; dist. supt., Apr., 1924; inspr.-genl., police, Indore, Oct., 1929, and again, May, 1937; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apr., 1936; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937; on for. service as I.G. of police, Indore State, March, 1937 to Feb., 1941; confd. as dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1941; confd. as inspr.-genl., Aug., 1944; C.I.E., June, 1945; l.p.r. from June, 1946.

WATSON, GEORGE LEO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Dec., 1909).—Educ. at the County Sch., Whitby, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.

WATSON, JOHN DOUGLAS, Indian State Rlys. (bridge engr.) (b. 17th March, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. exec. bridge engr., 26th March, 1926; exec. engr., bridges, N.W. rly., March, 1933.

WATSON, JOHN DYKES, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd July, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 4th Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933; services perm. transf. to M.S. and M. rly., Oct., 1937.

WATSON, ROBERT, O.B.E., N.D.A., Indian Agric. Service (b. 9th June, 1894).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, 28th Dec., 1921; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

WATT, WILLIAM HUTCHEON, O.B.E., R.N.R., (ret'd.), Lieut.-Commr., Civil Aviation Direct. (b. 8th March, 1892).—Joined the service as chief aerodrome offr. on 1st March, 1935; O.B.E., June, 1939; dep. dir.-genl. (airways and admin.), Dec., 1945.

WATTAL, PYARE KISHAN, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 10th May, 1890).—Joined the service, 9th May, 1912; asst. acctnt.-genl., Nov., 1912; dep. ditto, Apl., 1924; examr., outside audit, Bombay, Nov., 1924; services placed at disposal of Kashmir durbar, 11th June, 1926; dep. acctnt.-genl. (civil), Punjab, Nov., 1932; ditto (works), Oct., 1933; offg. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Apl., 1935; ditto, Madras, March, 1936; confid. as acct.-genl., Punjab, Sept., 1939; confid., acct.-genl. (cl. I), Nov., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1944.

WATTERS, WILLIAM JAMES, late Indian State Rlys., E.I. rly. (b. 26th Sept., 1882).—Ret'd., Nov., 1939.*

WATTS, HAROLD CHRISTOPHER, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Central Provs.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1895).—Ret'd., July, 1939.*

WAUGH, SIR ARTHUR ALLEN, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., United Provs.) (b. 25th July, 1891).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1914, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Apl., 1918; offg. excise commr., March, 1924; mag. and collr. (provl. sub.), July, 1930; confid., Apl., 1931; sec. to govt., U.P., rev. and p.w.d. (b. and r.), Apl., 1934, and again, Sept., 1935; C.I.E., May, 1937; settlement commr., United Provs., Apl., 1939; war prodn. commr., and sec., indus. dept., United Provs., Feb., 1942; C.S.I., Jan., 1943; sec., govt. of India, supply dept., May, 1943; commr. (confid.), Dec., 1944; member govt.-genl.'s exec. cl., Nov., 1945 to Sept., 1946; K.C.I.E., June, 1946.

WAVELL, FIELD MARSHAL THE RT. HON. ARCHIBALD PERCIVAL, of Cyrenaica and of Winchester, P.C., G.C.B., G.M.S.I., G.M.I.E., C.M.G. M.C. (b. May, 1883).—Apptd. member of govt.-genl.'s exec. cl. and C.-in-C., July, 1941; again, March, 1942; Viceroy and govt.-genl., Oct., 1943.

WAZIR ALI, Indian Fin. dept.—Apptd. on probn., Sept., 1938; asst. acct.-gen., July, 1940; asst. fincl. advr., supply dept., New Delhi, Jan., 1943; under sec. co-ord. cttee. of council, Oct., 1945; under sec. exec. council, Jan., 1946.

WAZIR CHAND, L. MEHRA, O.B.E., Indian Police (b. 11th Sept., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 6th Jan., 1930; confid., Feb., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1941.

WAZIR HASAN, SIR SAIYID, Kt., B.A., LL.B. (late chief judge, Oudh) (b. 14th May, 1874).—Ret'd., May, 1934.*

WEAKFORD, CHARLES FREDERICK, M.B.E., late Printing and Stationery Dept. (b. 29th Sept., 1885).—Ret'd., Sept., 1941.†

WEALE, CYRIL, O.B.E., Indian Police (asst. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 12th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 29th Nov., 1921; addl. supt., March, 1926; supt., March, 1932; prin., police training coll., Sardah, June, 1933; and again, Jan., 1935; asst. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1942.

WEBB, ARCHIBALD WILFRED TISDALL, I.A., ret'd., and Bombay Polit. Dept.—Apptd. census supt. of Rajputana and Ajmer-Merwara, 1939-1941; on spec. duty with govt. of India, home dept., 1st Dec., 1941; transf. to common-wealth relations dept. as princpl. refugee offr., Oct., 1943.

WEBB, SIR CHARLES MORGAN, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (ret'd.) (Burma).—Ret'd., Feb., 1926.*

WEBB, HORACE OSCAR, Engr.-comdr., R.I.M. Marine Dept., Bombay (b. 17th Nov., 1881).—Apptd. 2nd engr. and ship surveyor, March, 1927; princpl. engr. and ship surveyor, Bombay, Feb., 1929; engr. and ship surveyor, Karachi, Aug., 1931.

WEBB, WILFRED FRANCIS, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Feb., 1897).—Joined the dept. as asst. commr., Ajmer Merwara, 6th May, 1921; on duty under High Commr., Bagdad, from Dec. 1918; on for service in Tonk State from Oct., 1924; under sec. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1929; sec. to ditto, March, 1930; on for service in Rewa; diwan, Bundi State, Feb., 1932; pol. agent, Orissa States, Sambalpur, Oct., 1937; ditto, Chhaltesgarh, Raipur, May, 1938; pol. agent, Punjab Hill, States, Oct., 1939; diwan Cutch, State, Oct., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; pol. agt., Malwa, May, 1944; offg. resdt., cl. I and resdt. for Cent. India, Apl., 1945; pol. agt. in Bhopal, June, 1945; offg. resdt. cl. II and resdt. in Kashmir, Nov., 1945.

WEBB-JOHNSON, STANLEY C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Legislative Dept., (b. 1st March, 1888).—Apptd. asst. solcr. to govt. of India, Feb., 1920; 2nd solcr., Nov., 1927; O.B.E., Dec., 1930; offg. solcr., variously, from Apl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1941; ret'd., 1946.

WEBSTER, ANDREW McMILLAN, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 12th March, 1915).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confid., 18th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Aug., 1941; dist. supply offr., Coimbatore, Sept., 1945.

WEBSTER, GEOFFREY ALAN, Indian Police (b. 3rd March, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn., Nov., 1938; confid., Nov., 1940; addl. supt., Oct., 1943.

WEBSTER, GEORGE EVELYN EDEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 21st Apl., 1905).—Educ. at Calabar High Sch., Kingston, Jamaica, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1929; arrived Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt. fin. dept., Sept., 1932, July, 1934, and again, Sept., 1935; ditto home and pol. dept., Feb., 1935; offg. dep. commr., March, 1938; commr., provl., Aug., 1939; confid., Oct., 1940; on spl. duty in office of repr., govt. of Burma (Calcutta), May, 1942; dep. sec., fin.

dept., Oct., 1942; civ. aff. serv., Apl., 1944 to Nov., 1945; on l.p.r., Jan., 1947.

WEDDERSON, W. G. F., M.A., Burma Educ. Serv. (b. 13th Aug., 1893).—Apptd. as head master 24th July, 1930; dep. evac. off. civ. def., 1st June, 1941; on mil. duty Mar. 1942; asst. dir., public instrn., 24th July, 1946.

WEEKES, B. B., (b. 25th Mar., 1909).—Addl. dep. fincl. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1944; dep. fincl. advr., ditto, May, 1946.

WEIGHTMAN, HUGH, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab), Ind. C.S., Ind. Polit. Serv. (b. 29th Nov., 1898).—Educ. at Hymer's Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Jan., 1922; arrived, 17th Feb., 1922, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; presdt., Manipur State Durbar, June, 1925 to Feb., 1926; offg. under-sec. to govt., Assam, Oct., 1927; confd., March, 1928; under-sec. to A.G.G. in C.I., Apl., 1929; asst. pol. agt. and asst. commr. in Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1930; sec. to A.G.G. and ch. commr., Baluchistan, Apl., 1932; pol. agt., Kalat, June, 1935; ditto, Bahrain, Oct., 1937; again, May and Oct., 1938; dep. sec., ext. affairs dept., Oct., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; jt. sec., ext. affairs dept., Sept., 1941; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, May, 1944; offg. A.G.G., resdt. and ch. commr., Baluchistan, Aug., 1945; rev. and judl. commr., ditto, 1945; sec. ext. aff. dept., (confd.), Mar., 1946; C.S.I., June, 1946; on depn. to U.K., July, 1946.

WEIR, JAMES LESLIE ROSE, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Police Service (b. 29th Jan., 1883).—Retd., March, 1938.*

WEIR, RALPH SOMERVILLE, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service, U.P. (b. 10th Sept., 1884).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

WELLBORNE, CYRIL DE MONTFORT, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., late Indian Police, (Burma) (b. 2nd July, 1884).—Retd., June, 1939.*

WELLS, HARRY LESLIE, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 25th July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

WELSH, JOHN ALBERT VICTOR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 24th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as forest engr., Burma, 30th Nov., 1921; trans. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1931; asst. supply offr. Sept., 1942; dep. dir., Burma and supply mission, Oct., 1945.

WEMYSS, FREDERICK FLEMING, Indian police (dep. inspr.-gen., Burma) (b. 7th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 6th Sept., 1921; asst. commr. of police, Rangoon, Feb., 1927; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1930; confd., Dec., 1930; civ. aff. serv. to Oct., 1945; dep. inspr.-genl., 1945 to 1946.

WESSON, R. A., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., (civ. surg.) (b. 7th Feb., 1895).—Date of first commn., 2nd Feb., 1932 civil surg., United Provs., Dec., 1934.

WEST, ALGERNON SYDNEY, Indian State Rlys., Coal Dept. (mang., coke ovens) (b. 9th June, 1897).—Asst. mang., coke ovens, June, 1921; mang., coke ovens, Nov., 1936.

WEST, EDWARD JOHN WRIGHT, late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 4th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

WEST, STEWART ELLES LAWRENCE, C.I.E., O.B.E., V.D., Indian State Rlys. (mem. rly. bd.) (b. 2nd March, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 1st March, 1909; on mil. duty, 1st May, 1916; O.B.E., Feb., 1919; dist.

traffic supt., Jan., 1922; offg. dep. ch. comml. mang., Nov., 1925; transpn. supt., Nov., 1930; offg. dep. ch. comml. mang., Feb., 1932; and again, Jan., 1934; offg. dep. ch. oper. supt., N.W. rly., May, 1933; offg. divl. supt., junior, N.W. rly., March, 1934; confd., Sept., 1934; ditto (senior), May, 1937; dep. ch. oper. supt. (provl.), Dec., 1937; dir., rly. bd., July, 1940; ch. comml. mang., N.W. rly., Oct., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1943; memb. rly. bd., (confd.), Feb., 1945.

WEST, Lt.-Col. THOMAS TEMPLETON, Burma Front. Serv. (b. 14th Oct., 1915).—Apptd. 24th June, 1946.

WEST, WILLIAM DIXON, M.A., (b. 27th Jan., 1901).—Joined the service 12th Dec., 1923, as asst. supt. (geologist); dir. geol. survey of India, Dec., 1945.

WEST, WILLIAM FREDERICK, Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.) (b. 13th June, 1882).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, store dept., 22nd Nov., 1900; jun. clerk, Apl., 1909; senr. clerk, Nov., 1915; transfld. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; contr., tpl., 1922; actg. dir. of purchase, May, 1940; actg. dir.-genl., store dept., July, 1941; C.I.E., June, 1946.

WESTCOTT, THE MOST REV. FOSS, M.A., D.D., late Indian Ecclesi. Estabt. (b. 23rd Oct., 1863).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Peterhouse, Cambridge; Bishop in Chota Nagpore, 1905-1919; Bishop of Calcutta, and Met. of India and Ceylon, Aug., 1919; ret'd., 1945.

WESTERDALE, JOSEPH STANLEY, B.Sc., M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 10th Apl., 1886).—Retd., Apl., 1941.†

WESTLAKE, ALAN ROBERT CECIL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 18th July, 1894).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., Frogmal, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 7th Aug., 1921; arrived, 7th Oct., 1921; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; offg. collr., Feb., 1927; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1931; ditto (provl. subs.), Sept., 1935; sec., bd. of rev., Madras, Aug., 1933; collr., and dist. mag. (subst.), Sept., 1936; dir. of agric., June, to Dec., 1940; sec., educ. and pub. health dept., June, 1941; ditto, rev. dept., July, 1941; ditto, devel. dept., Jan., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1943; memb. bd. of rev., Apl., 1944; on depn. to U.K., Jan., to Mar., 1946.

WESTOBY, EDWARD HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offcr.) (b. 22nd Apl., 1900).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 6th Feb., 1920; actg. exec. offcr., June, 1927; confd., Nov., 1929.

WESTON, ERIC, B.A., Indian C.S.
