(judge, high ct., Bombay) (b. 8th Dec., 1892).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1916, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. judge, Sept., 1921; asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Jan., 1924; judge and sess. judge, Apl., 1929; judl. asst., and addl. sess. judge, Aden; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Oct., 1933; judl. commr., states of W.I., Sept., 1934; judl. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, June, 1937; judge, ct. of judl. commr., Sind, Nov., 1938; judge, chief ct. of Sind, Apl., 1940; ditto, Bombay high ct., Jan., 1943.

WESTON WAITE, FRANCIS EDWARD, A.K.C., Indian Ecclesi. Dept. (junior chaplain).—Apptd., 2nd July, 1936.

WEYMOUTH, JAMES CHARLES, late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 5th Sept., 1887).—Retd., March, 1942.†

WHEATLEY, WILLIAM GEOFFREY, Indian

Service of Engrs. (suprg. engr.) (b. 6th June, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 26th Sept., 1924; exec. engr., Apl., 1934; suprg. engr., under govt. of India, ordnance factory, Dec., 1942; liaison offr. Punjab irrig. and suprg. engr. on spl. duty, Dec., 1945.

WHEELER, REV. CHARLES ERNEST RUAPHEU, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 13th Oct., 1887).—Retd., Oct., 1942.

WHEELER, SIR EDWARD OLIVER, M.C., Brig., late R.E., Survey of India (b. 18th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd June, 1910; in mil. employ to 8th Dec., 1919; asst. suprt., 9th Dec., 1919; offg. dy. suprt., Oct., 1920; on depn. with expdn. for ascent of Mount Everest from 15th Apl. to 20th Oct., 1921; offg. suprt., March, 1926; confd., Oct., 1927; dir., March, 1939; surv.-genl., Apl., 1941; kt., Jan., 1943.

WHEELER, G. F., Lt.-COL., C.I.E., (dir., govt. of India Publications divn.) (b. 12th June, 1897).—Joined the service, 20th March, 1915; arrived in India, 3rd Sept., 1918; transferred from mil. to civil, 2nd Jan., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

WHEELER, SIR HENRY, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 2nd June, 1870).—Retd., Oct., 1927.*

WHEELER, ROBERT ERIC MORTIMER, M.C., D.Litt., F.B.A., F.S.A., (b. Sept., 1890).—Apptd. dir.-gen. of archaeology in India, 1944.

WHELAN, ERNEST CARNEGIE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (Burma) (b. 2nd Nov., 1889).—Retd., May, 1939.*

WHELAN, JOHN PRATT, Indian Police (dist. suprt. Central Provs.) (b. 18th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. suprt., Central Provs., 22nd Nov., 1923; offg. dist. suprt., Nov., 1927; confd., Nov., 1937; confd., March, 1940; asst. to D.I.G., Oct., 1940.

WHIPP, H., M.B.E., B.Sc., A.M.Inst.C.E. (Executive Engr., Public Health Circle, Bihar).—Joined service, 29th Dec., 1933; confd., Sept., 1936; special offcr. in charge of public health circle, March, 1939; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

WHITAKER, CECIL CHARLES, Indian Police (asst. suprt., Bihar) (b. 6th Nov., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. suprt., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

WHITBY, ROBERT BERTRAM HINDHAUGH, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. commr. mgr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 21st Aug., 1897).—Joined the service, Nov., 1919; asst. traffic suprt., Dec., 1920; suprt., senr. scale, Apl., 1936; dep. ch. comml. mgr., E.I. Rly., Nov., 1945.

WHITE, ARTHUR JOHN STANLEY, O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 28th Aug., 1896).—Retd., March, 1938.*

WHITE, AUGUSTUS RONALD, Burma C.S. (cl. I) (b. 11th Dec., 1922).—Educ. Charterhouse, apptd. on probn. after seln., Aug., 1946.

WHITE, CHARLES FITZWILLIAM, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 3rd Dec., 1885).—Retd., Dec., 1940.

WHITE, DESMOND ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 31st May, 1918).—Educ. at Agra Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.

WHITE, GERALD SEBASTIAN, O.B.E., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 12th Feb., 1878).—Retd., March, 1938.*

WHITE, REV. JACK, Indian Eccles. Estabt., Madras (senr. chaplain) (b. 12th March, 1903).—Apptd. 1st Apl., 1930; junr. chaplain, March,

1932; senr. ditto, March, 1942; actg. Archdeacon, Apl., 1944.

WHITE, JOHN, Capt., M.D., Ch.B. (Edin.) D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), I.M.S. (civil surg., Bengal) (b. 14th Feb., 1901).—Date of first commn., Apl., 1933; civil surg., Jan., 1939.

WHITE, SYDNEY WILFRED, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 22nd June, 1884).—Retd., July, 1939*; dep. dir., muntns. prodn., dept. of supply, Nov., 1940.

WHITEHEAD, GEORGE SYDNEY, B.Sc. (Econ.) Burma Office (b. 15th Nov., 1915).—Apptd. exec. offr., 12th Dec., 1934; on mil. serv., from June, 1940 to Sept., 1945; actg. higher exec. offr. (in absentia), Jan., 1941; pte. sec. to par. under-sec. of state, Dec., 1945 to Dec., 1946; actg. princpl., Dec., 1946.

WHITEHEAD, THOMAS ALEC, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 8th May, 1886).—Retd., July, 1940.†

WHITEHOUSE, GEORGE EDWARD, Indian Police (suprt., United Provs.) (b. 22nd July, 1911).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1930; asst. suprt., Nov., 1932; offg. A.D.C. to govr., U.P., Dec., 1933; offg. suprt., June, 1936; confd., Feb., 1944.

WHITEHOUSE, VICTOR, Indian Police (b. 31st May, 1914).—Apptd. asst. suprt., United Provs., on probn., 1st Nov., 1935.

WHITFIELD, WILLIAM HARVEY, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 18th March, 1917).—Educ. at Harris Acad., Dundee, St. Andrews' Univ. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

WHITNEY, ALBERT, late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. rly.) (b. 9th March, 1886).—Joined the service, 16th Jan., 1915; dist. signal engr., G.I.P. rly., March, 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1936; retd., Feb., 1943.

WHITNEY, WILLIAM WELCH, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th May, 1888).—Retd., March, 1940.†

WHITTAKER, ARNOLD, C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 27th July, 1900).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

WHITTLE, GEORGE WILLIAM MURDOCH, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr. United Provs.) (b. 7th Apl., 1907).—Educ. at Secondary Sch., Washington Village, Durham, Durham Univ., and King's College, Cambridge; joined the serv. 16th Oct., 1930; arr. in Ind., Dec., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; sec. steel contr. bd., July, 1941; dep. sec. supply, govt. of India, Jan., 1942; mag. and collr. (confd.), Nov., 1945; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

WHITTY, SIR JOHN TARLTON, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 1st Feb., 1876).—Retd., Feb., 1935.*

WHITWORTH, HUGH HOPE ASTON, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 21st May, 1914).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; M.B.E., Jan., 1945.

WHITWORTH, RUDGE, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Oct., 1886).—Retd., Oct., 1941.

WHYTE, SIR ALEXANDER FREDERICK, K.C.S.I., M.A.—Presdt., Indian legisl. assembly, 1920 to 1925.*

WICKENDEN, THOMAS DOUGLAS, C.I.E.,

B.A., Ind. C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Cent. Provs.) (b. 2nd March, 1903).—Educ. at St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 5th Dec., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1929; sub-judge, Oct., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1932, May, 1933, and again, May, 1934; confd., Jan., 1936; under legal trng. from May, 1941; legal sec. to govt., Dec., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1946.

WILBERFORCE-BELL, SIR HAROLD, K.C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Polit. Serv. (b. 17th Nov., 1885).—Ret'd., Nov., 1940.†

WILCOCK, JOHN STEWART, O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab), Indian C.S. (collr., Orissa) (b. 6th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, 30th Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., pol. and appts. dept., March, 1932; offg. sec. to A.G.G., Eastern States, March, 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1935; pte. sec. to govr., Orissa, Apl., 1936; sec., ditto, Apl., 1937, and 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1939; agent to the govr. and collr., Feb., 1940; sec., price control and ch. contr., prices and supplies, Apl., 1944; mag. and collr. (confd.) Mar., 1946; sec. to govr., Sept., 1945; addl. sec. to govr., pol. and appts. dept., Mar., 1946.

WILES, SIR GILBERT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th March, 1880).—Educ. at Perse Gr. Sch., and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of land records and regn., Nov., 1910; supt. of land records, Aug., 1915, to May, 1916; temp. dep. dir. of civil supplies, June, 1918; dep. sec. to govt., home dept., Nov., 1921; asst. collr. and sec. to govt., fin. dept., Dec., 1923; collr. and ditto, Oct., 1925; member, legisl. council, 1924, 1927, 1930, 1931, and 1932; also finl. advr. to govt., dev. and p.q. depts., April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; member, tariff bd., Sept., 1933; presdnt., ditto, Oct., 1934; chairman, Bombay Port Trust, June, 1935; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938; ch. sec. to govt. (pol. and serv. dept.), Bombay, Jan., 1938; advr. to govr. of Bombay, Nov., 1939; ret'd., March, 1941; advr. to sec. of state for India, May, 1941 to May, 1946.

WILKES, JOHN SEBASTIAN, late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 31st Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1908; asst. supt., May, 1910; supt., Nov., 1922; dep. commr., Apl., 1929; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1934; confd., June, 1936; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938; ret'd., Jan., 1943.

WILKIE, HUGH GRAHAM, C.M.G., B.A., Ind. C.S. (Burma) (b. 13th Mar., 1893).—Educ. at Uppingham and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to March, 1919; apptd., 7th Oct., 1919; arrived, 9th Dec., 1919, and served in Burma; colonization offer., Jan., 1922; headqrs. asst., May, 1923; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924; confd., May, 1931; administrator of govt. estates, Nov., 1926; land offer., Rangoon dev. trust, March, 1928; offg. secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma, Oct., 1930; secty., lands and reve. dept., May, 1937; commr., Apl., 1939; ch. secty. to govt. of Burma, 1944 and 1945 to 1946; C.M.G., Jan., 1946; l.p.r., from Feb., 1946.

WILKIE, RALPH SCUDAMORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. Burma) (b. 13th March, 1896).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 20th Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., June, 1924; asst. settl. offer., Nov., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1928; settl. offer., Nov., 1930; dep. commr., confd., Oct., 1934; commr. front. areas, Aug., 1942; civ. aff. serv., 1943; on spec. duty as commr. under dir., front. area admin., Jan., 1946; commr., (confd.), July, 1946.

WILKINS, PAUL BURGMAN, O.B.E., M.C., D.F.C., Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 11th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 11th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1929; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1933; supt., Jan., 1936; O.B.E., June, 1942; dep. commr., Bombay, March, 1943; offg. commr., June, 1946.

WILKINSON, HAROLD GLEADOW, B.Sc., Burma Engrg. Serv., (cl. I) (b. 4th June, 1891).—Apptd. exec. engr. 10th Oct., 1928.

WILKINSON, HECTOR RUSSELL, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 11th March, 1888).—Retd. Apl., 1939.*

WILKINSON, R. J., Col. dep. civil passage contr., defence dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1945; dep. civil passage contr., home dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1946; civil passage contr., home dept., Jan., 1946.

WILLAN, EDWARD GERVASE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 17th May, 1917).—Educ. at Radley and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 28th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Central Prov. as asst. commr.

WILLFORD, GEORGE MARK, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service as extra asst. consvr., July, 1915; dep. consvr., July, 1930.

WILLIAMS, ALFRED ARTHUR, O.B.E., B.A., Ind. C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th May, 1910).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's Sch., Crosby and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after the exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl.-offr., Oct., 1937; O.B.E., June, 1945.

WILLIAMS, ARTHUR DU COETLOGAN, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (advr. to govr., Bengal) (b. 27th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Marlborough and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; on mil. duty in England, Aug. to Dec., 1914; arrived, 7th May, 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty in Rawalpindi, Quetta and E. Persia, Dec., 1916, to March, 1919; on mil. duty in Quetta, May to Sept., 1919; jt. mag., 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, 1921; attache, legal. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1924; services lent as offg. dist. and sess. judge to govt. of Assam, Apl., 1925; offg. secty., legl. dept., govt. of Bengal and secty., legl. council, Feb., 1926; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, May, 1926; addl. dep. secty., legl. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1927; offg. dep. sec., ditto, Apl., 1929; on spl. duty, home dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1931; secty., legl. dept., govt. of Bengal, Feb., 1934; supt. and membr. of legal affairs, Bengal, July, 1934; offg. jt. sec. and draftsman, legl. dept., govt. of India, March, 1935; offg. sec., ditto, Oct., 1935; secty., council of state, Dec., 1935; dep. secty., legl. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1936; addl. jt. secty., ditto, Nov., 1936; offg. secty. to

govr.-genl.'s exec. cl. in addn., Nov., 1936; confd., March, 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938; addl. secty., def. dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1938; secty., govr.-genl. def. co-ordn., Dec., 1938; secty., def. co-ordn. dept., govt. of India, in addn., Feb., 1939; sec. to govr., Bengal, Sept., 1942; ch. sec., govt. of Bengal, Sept., 1943, to Oct., 1944; food and civil supplies commr., Oct., 1944; advr. to govr., Aug., 1945; C.S.I., Jan., 1946.

WILLIAMS, REV. BERNARD RHYS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (chaplain) (b. 11th July, 1908).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Nov., 1938; apptd. chaplain Oct., 1944.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES FREDERICK VICTOR, C.I.E., Ind. C.S. (jt. sec. govt. of India, home dept.) (b. 21st May, 1898).—Educ. at West Ham and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1923; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1923, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., Feb., 1928; under-secty. to govt., ch. secretariat, Madras, July, 1929; jt. secty., Indian round-table conf., London, Nov.-Dec., 1932; under-secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1933; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Apl., 1934; secty. to agent of the govt. of India in South Africa, Sept., 1934; collr. and dep. magte., Aug., 1937; collr., civil and mil. station, Bangalore, Aug., 1937; collr., I gr. (prov. subst.), March, 1939; confd. as collr., Nov., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1944; jt. sec. govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1945.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES ROBERT FARMER, M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1922; princpl., Madras forest coll., Coimbatore, July, 1924, to July, 1931; consvr., Nov., 1942.

WILLIAMS, DAVID, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 12th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as dist. loco supt., 25th March, 1920; asst. transpt. supt., 1st Nov., 1922; supt., May, 1926; offg. divl. transp. supt., variously from August, 1926; divl. transpt. supt., May, 1942; retd., Jan., 1945.

WILLIAMS, DONALD MORAY, Burma Serv. of Engrs. (cl. I) (b. 27th Sept., 1904).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr. Feb., 1929; confd., Feb., 1932; exec. engr., Feb., 1937; on mil. duty, June, 1942 to July, 1946.

WILLIAMS, EDWIN OWEN, Indian Forest Engrg. Service (Punjab) (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service, 11th Nov., 1921; lecturer, forest college, Dehra Dun, April, 1938; timber supply circle, March, 1940.

WILLIAMS, EMILY MATILDA, M.B.E., India Office (actg. hr. exec. offr., acct.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 21st Feb., 1894).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 5th Sept., 1921; higher clerical offcr., Sept., 1932; welfare offcr., 29th Dec., 1941; M.B.E., June, 1944; actg. hr. exec. offr., Apl., 1942.

WILLIAMS, GEOFFREY BAYNTON, C.I.E., M.C., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 19th Aug., 1896).—First commn. dated 13th Oct., 1914; in mil. employ prior to 27th July, 1925; asst. collr. and magte., Bareilly, July, 1925; under-secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in C.I., March, 1926; ditto, in States of W.I., Dec., 1926; guardian and tutor to heir-apparent of Nawab of Junagadh, Dec., 1928; secty. to A.G.G., in the states of W.I., Nov., 1932; under-secty., ditto, Oct., 1933; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha, Dec. 1933; secty. to A.G.G., Eastern states, Dec., 1935; pol. agent, Bhopal,

March, 1936; ditto, W. Kathiawar agency, Apl., 1940; ditto, E. Kathiawar agency, Sept., 1941; pol. agt., W. Rajputana States, Dec., 1943; pol. agt., Jaipur, in addn., Apl., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1945; pol. agt., Mewar and S. Rajputana States (addl.), July, 1945; sec. to resdt. for Rajputana (addl.), Nov., 1945; pol. agt. Jaipur, (addl.), Jan., 1946.

WILLIAMS, HERBERT BRIAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 8th July, 1918).—Educ. at Newton-in-Makerfield Gr. Sch., Lancs. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn. after seln., in May, 1940; and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; rationing contr., Sept., 1945.

WILLIAMS, JOHN HOWARD FRY, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 6th Oct., 1914).—Apptd. on probn., 10th Nov., 1935; asst. to pol. offcr., Sadiya, May, 1937; confd. as asst. supt. of police, July, 1937; asst. pol. offcr., Passighat, Jan., 1939; spec. duty, Naga Hills, Apl., to Aug., 1944; supt., Nov., 1945.

WILLIAMS, RICHARD AELWYN ELLIS, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. sec., govt. of Orissa) (b. 5th Dec., 1901).—Educ. at Univ. of Wales and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., pol. dept., March, 1930; offg. asst. pol. agent, Orissa feudatory states, March, 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933; sec. to govt., rev. and comm. depts., Bihar, July, 1940; ch. sec. govt. of Orissa, Nov., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

WILLIAMS, RUSHBROOK (see Rushbrook Williams).

WILLIAMS, SEVERN, O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 14th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

WILLIAMS, REV. WILLIAM PERCIVAL, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 10th March, 1929; junr. chaplain, March, 1931; offg. archdeacon, Nagpur, Feb., 1936; archdeacon, Nagpur, Oct., 1938; senr. chaplain, March, 1941.

WILLIAMSON, ALEC, C.M.G., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 7th Nov., 1886).—Educ. at Kirkcudbright Academy, Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settl. offcr.; on mil. service, Jan., 1918, to Apl., 1919; dep. chairman, bd. of trustees for development of Rangoon, March, 1925; dep. commr. (prov. sub.), Feb., 1926; offg. land offcr., Rangoon Development Trust, March, 1927; offcr. 1/c., Mandalay municipal cttee., Oct., 1929; offg. chairman, Rangoon dev. trust, July, 1933; excise commr., March, 1934; commr. of settl. and land records, Burma, March, 1936; offg. commr., Feb., 1938; confd., May, 1939; offg. fincl. commr. Nov., 1940; C.M.G., Jan., 1944; retd., 1945.

WILLIAMSON, GRAHAME, O.B.E., M.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M., Maj., —First commn., 12th Feb., 1913; in mil. employ to Sept., 1938; O.B.E., June, 1938; animal husby. commr., govt. of India, March, 1944.

WILLIAMSON, HAROLD, O.B.E., M.D., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., retd., late I.M.S. (b. 26th Feb., 1891).—First commn., 3rd Dec., 1921; offg. agency surg., Quetta, Apl., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933; resdy. surg., Hyderabad, Apl., 1935; civil surg., Sibi and Loralai, Oct., 1935; offg. resdy. surg., Mysore, March, 1937; surgeon to viceroy.

WILLIAMSON, SIR HORACE, Kt., C.I.E., M.B.E., late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 16th July, 1880).—Retd., Sept., 1936*; member, council of India, Dec., 1936; advisor to sec. of state for India, Apl., 1937, to March, 1942.

WILLMOTT, HENRY CLAUDE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 18th May, 1914).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; served with govt. of Assam from Mar., 1942; asst. dir. civ. def., Apl., 1942; dep., dir., ditto, July, 1942; adml. dep. commr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1943.

WILLMOTT, HERBERT, India Audit Office (higher exec. offcr.) (b. 9th June, 1887).—Apptd. clerical offr., 9th Nov., 1921; exec. offr., Apl., 1922; hr. exec. offr., March, 1936.

WILLOUGHBY, VERE WILLIAM DIGBY, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Aug., 1901).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; in mil. employ to 20th June, 1927; vice-consul, Ahwaz, Dec., 1928; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Nov., 1931; under-secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, March, 1932; secty. to ditto (temp.), July, 1934; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Aug., 1935; ditto, Zhob, Oct., 1935; vice-consul, Zabul, June, 1938; secty. to resdt. for Kolhapur and the Deccan States, Apl., 1939; asst. pol. agent, Orissa States, Dec., 1941; consul, Kerman, Nov., 1944; H.M. consul, Bandar Abbas (addl. charge), Sept., 1945; H.M. consul, Khorramshahr, May, 1946.

WILSON, ANDREW, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Serv. (b. 27th Aug., 1886).—Retd., Apl., 1940.†

WILSON, SIR ARNOLD TALBOT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.M.G., D.S.O., M.P., Bt. Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.).—Retd., Aug., 1921.*

WILSON, ARTHUR HENRY, O.B.E., B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 12th July, 1893).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd cl., July, 1920; D.M.A.G. (junr.), Dec., 1931; ditto (senr.), Nov. 1932; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., March, 1934; revtd. to mil. accts. dept., July, 1937; snr. dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Jan., 1940; cl. I offcr., July, 1940; command contr., Aug., 1940; O.B.E., June, 1942; mil. acct.-genl., Mar., 1945.

WILSON, ARTHUR NORMAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1935; vice-princl., govt. school of engrg., Rasul, Sept., 1936 to Nov., 1937; on depn. to cent. P.W.D., May, 1942; supt. engr., Apl., 1944.

WILSON, CAMPBELL AUBREY KENNETH, M.B.E., Maj., R.E., Surv. of India, (b. 20th June, 1905).—First commn., 3rd Sept., 1925; in mil. employ to Apl., 1929; proby. asst. supt., Apl., 1929; confd. Apl., 1931; supt., Mar., 1939; in mil. employ, July, 1941 to Jan., 1946; M.B.E., July, 1943.

WILSON, CECIL CLAUDE, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 11th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1908, as asst. consvr., dep. consvr., 1914; on mil. service, Oct., 1916, to March, 1919; actg. consvr., Sept., 1924; confd., March, 1926; forest economist, Dehra Dun, Nov., 1925; ret'd., July, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

WILSON, REV. GEORGE ALBERT, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 24th Apl., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probn.,

Madras, 29th March, 1926; junr. chaplain, March, 1928; senr. ditto, March, 1938.

WILSON, Rt. Hon. SIR GUY DOUGLAS ARTHUR FLEETWOOD, P.C., G.C.I.E., K.C.B., K.C.M.G.—Member of govr.-genl.'s council, fin. dept., 9th Nov., 1908, to June, 1913; discharged functions on behalf of the govr.-genl. after the attempt on the latter's life in Dec., 1912; K.C.M.G., 1909; G.C.I.E., Dec., 1911; P.C., 1914.

WILSON, IAN HENRY RICHARD, Maj., R.E., Survey of India (b. 11th Nov., 1902).—First commn. 31st Jan., 1923; in mil. employ to Dec., 1926; proby. asst. supt., Dec., 1926; confd., Oct., 1929; supt., (confd.), Nov., 1937; in mil. employ from Oct., 1940 to June, 1942; and again, July, 1942 to Mar., 1945.

WILSON, JAMES LEITCH, M.A., Edin., Punjab Educ. Service (b. 31st March, 1892).—Joined the service as offg. inspr. of schs., 27th March, 1925; confd., March, 1930; vice-princl. March, 1925; confd., March, 1930; vice-princl., Central Trng. Coll., Lahore, Nov., 1935; supt. of educn., Baluchistan, May, 1937; to Apl., 1943; princpl., govt. coll., Lyallpur, Apl., 1943; senr., sel. grade (cl. 1), July, 1943.

WILSON, JOHN CYRIL, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 24th May, 1902).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., Nov., 1930; asst. to inspr.-genl., dep. commr., police, Bombay, Apl., 1938; Indian police medal, July, 1940; dep. commr., Bombay, Aug., 1941 to Nov., 1942; dep. inspr.-genl., W. India and Gujarat states, Rajkot, Dec., 1942 to Nov., 1944; confd. as supt. (sel. grade), Mar., 1944; King's police medal, Jan., 1943; again dep. inspr.-genl. and advr. to resdt., Rajkot, Sept., 1944; again supt. (sel. grade), Aug., 1945.

WILSON, JOSEPH ARNOLD, B.A., Indian Forest Services (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 12th Dec., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

WILSON, SIR LEONARD, B.E., K.C.I.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th March, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 24th Apl., 1911; confd., June, 1915; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; offg. divl. trans. supt., Sept., 1925; confd., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. trans. supt., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. agent, Dec., 1929; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1930; confd., March, 1933; offg. agent, G.I.P. Rly., May, 1934; provl., confd., Feb., 1935; ch. commr. of rlys., Apl., 1941 to Sept., 1944; kt. June, 1941; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

WILSON, RT. HON. SIR LESLIE ORME, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., C.M.G., D.S.O., Lieut.-Col., R.M.L.I. (ret'd.).—Govr. of Bombay from 10th Dec., 1923, to 8th Dec., 1928.*

WILSON, LESLIE TALBOT, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Burma) (b. 11th Aug., 1899).—Joined the serv. as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 5th Apl., 1921; offg. dist. supt. and asst. commr. of police, Rangoon, March, 1926; confd., March, 1932; asst. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1936; civ. aff. serv., to Oct., 1945; dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1945.

WILSON, NORMAN METHUEN, C.I.E., O.B.E., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 23rd Feb., 1881).—Retd., Feb., 1941.†

WILSON, PERCY, A.R.I.B.A., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 4th Nov., 1891).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

WILSON, SIR ROGER COCHRANE, K.C.B.,

D.S.O., M.C., Lieut.-Gen., I.A. (b. 26th Dec., 1882).—Mil. sec., India Office, May, 1936.*

WILSON, ROBERT GRAHAM, Burma Front. Serv. (b. 7th Aug., 1917).—Apptd. 7th Jan., 1940.

WILTON DAVIES, PERCY WILLIAM, V.D., Indian State Railways (N.W. Rly.) (b. 14th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1912; asst. engr., Dec., 1913; exec. engr., March, 1931; dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1943; rettd., 1945.

WIMBERLEY, MAJ. EDMUND ARABIN, O.B.E., R.E. (dep. mint master) (b. 16th Sept., 1906).—Under mil. dept. up to 14th Apl., 1937; dep. mint master (on probn.), Apl., 1937; offg. mint master var. from Sept., 1938; confd. dep. ditto, Aug., 1940; offg. mint master, May, 1941; O.B.E., June, 1941; offer. i/c. new mint, Lahore, May, 1942.

WIMBUSH, ANTHONY, late Indian Forest Service (b. 19th May, 1886).—Retd., June, 1937.*

WIN, U ZAW, Burma Police (cl. I).—Apptd. asst. supt. of police, on probn., Dec., 1937; transf. to mil. dept., Nov., 1940; left in Burma, dist. supt., 1945.

WINDLE, OLIVER ETHELBERT, O.B.E., late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 20th July, 1884).—Retd., July, 1939; dep. presdt. civil selec. bd., govt. of India, Nov., 1945; presdt. ditto, May, 1946; addl. temp. member, Fed. public serv., commn., July to Aug., 1946.

WINGATE, RONALD EVELYN LESLIE, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. and Indian Political Service (b. 30th Sept., 1889).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

WINGFIELD, DESMOND ERNEST, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 17th Nov., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 26th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. supt., Nov., 1935; confd., Apl., 1944.

WINTERTON, EARL, RIGHT HON. EDWARD TURNOUR, P.C., M.P., J.P., D.L.—Parliamentary under-sec. of state for India, March, 1922, to Jan., 1924, and Nov., 1924, to June, 1929; delegate to Burma round-table conf., London, Dec., 1931; delegate to Indian round-table conf., London, Nov.-Dec., 1932.

WISE, SIR JOHN HUMPHREY, K.C.M.G., C.B.E., Indian C.S. (b. 11th March, 1890).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Oxford Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1913; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1914, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from Apl., 1915, to July, 1919; under-sec. to govt., Feb., 1921, to Aug., 1923; offg. sec. to finl. commr., Aug., 1923; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, health dept., from Nov., 1923, to Feb., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1924; secy., public service commn., Oct., 1926; dep. commr., Feb., 1930; secty., rev. dept., Burma, Nov., 1932, and again, Apl., 1934; secty., commerce and indus. dept., March, 1937; C.B.E., June, 1939; contr. of supplies, Burma, Oct., 1939; counsellor to the govr. of Burma, June, 1942; K.C.M.G., Jan., 1943; dep. govr., Sept., 1943; on depn. in U.K., March to May, 1944 and July to Sept., 1944; dep. govr., Jan. to May, 1945; on depn. to U.K., June to Aug., 1945; counsellor to govr., 1945-46; advr. to sec. of state for Burma, Nov., 1946.

WOLFENDEN, RALPH, M.B.E., M.Sc., M.I.M.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), Barr., late Indian Educ. Service (Bengal) (b. 18th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

WOOD, ANTHONY ST. JOHN, Indian Police, (asst. supt.) (b. 19th Jan., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

WOOD, ARTHUR EDWARD FRANCIS, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 18th June, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Dec., 1910; supt., Nov., 1925; rettd., 16th Nov., 1943.

WOOD, CHRISTOPHER FRANCIS, late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 26th Aug., 1884).—Educ. at Grocers' Company's Sch., London, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and asst. commr.; on mil. duty, I.A.R.O., Sept., 1916, to Feb., 1919; jt. mag., March, 1918; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1920; jt. sec. to board of rev., Feb., 1921; sec., ditto, Apl., 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1924; confd., July, 1925; offg. commr., Dec., 1925; confd., Apl., 1939; rettd., 2nd Dec., 1944.

WOOD, ERIC RAWLINSON, C.I.E., M.C., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Orissa) (b. 24th March, 1893).—Educ. at Denstone Coll., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1919; arrived, 1st Dec., 1919, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; ditto (provl. subs.), March, 1935; collr., March, 1936; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from Apl., 1936; secty. to govt. of Orissa, Educ. Dept., March, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; rettd., July, 1944.

WOOD, ERNEST CLEMENT, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 12th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Cheltenham and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, on mily. duty from Oct., 1916, to May, 1919; asst. to resdt., Travancore and Cochin, June, 1919; sub-collr., March, 1922; offg. commr. of Coorg, May, 1926; offg. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1926; confd., March, 1927; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1930; collr. (provl. subs.), May, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; secty. to govt., educn. and p.h. dept., July, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; member, bd. of rev., Apl., 1943; rettd.

WOOD, GEORGE HERBERT ANDERSON, Indian Fin. Dept. (b. 31st Aug., 1894).—Joined the service, 14th June, 1919; asst. acctnt. genl., Nov., 1920; eurrency offer., Madras, Oct., 1922; ditto, Lahore, May, 1925; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1926; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. Rly., June, 1932; services placed at disposal of finl. commr. of rlys., Feb., 1933; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts., June, 1935; dep. ch. accts. offer., E.R. rly., May, 1936; on for. service under Jodhpur State, Jan., 1937; dir., rly. clg. accts., Delhi, Oct., 1939; fin. advr. and ch. accts. offer. N.W. rly., May, 1941; ditto, O. & T. rly., May, 1944; ditto, N.W. rly., June, 1944; dir. of rly. audit, Apl., 1945.

WOOD, JOHN LUTHER, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 25th Sept., 1908).—Educ. at Burnley Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931; sub-collr., March, 1934; sec. to govt., I.A. Dept., Nov., 1945.

WOOD, LESLIE DERING, Indian Police (b. 5th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 24th Nov., 1928; offg. supt., Apl., 1935; confd., Dec., 1940; on I.P.R.

WOOD, PERCY, late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 17th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and acctnt., G.I.P. rly., 7th Nov., 1913; confd. as asst. auditor, G.I.P. rly., Dec., 1920; on depn. to Agency Dept. as supt. of statistics. G.I.P. rly.,

Feb., 1924, to March, 1929; served under finl. commr., rlys., Oct., 1929, and again, Dec., 1935; dep. ch. accts. offcr., G.I.P. rly., Oct., 1929; served under agent, G.I.P. rly., July, 1932; confd. as dep. agent, Jan., 1933; dep. ch. accts. offcr., Nov., 1936; ch. accts. offcr., G.I.P. rly., Feb., 1938 to 1946.

WOOD, WILLIAM GEORGE, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 27th July, 1897).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 1st Apl., 1927, after previous service from Apl., 1921; exec. offcr., 1st Nov., 1932.

WOOD, WILLIAM OUTERSON, O.B.E., Indian Police (b. 11th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., 20th Nov., 1923; awarded King's police medal, March, 1929; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; O.B.E., May, 1936; senr. asst. dir., intell., govt of India, Nov., 1940.

WOOD, W. W., (b. 24th Nov., 1896).—Apptd. princpl. Delhi Polytechnic, Dec., 1940.

WOODFORD, GEORGE PEARCE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 21st Dec., 1915).—Educ. at Kingswood Sch., Bath, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 23rd Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov. 1941; S.D.O., April, 1942.

WOODHEAD, SIR JOHN ACKROYD, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S., (b. 19th June, 1881).—Retd., Dec., 1939*; advr. to sec. of state for India, Dec., 1939; advr. to sec. of state for Burma, Sept., 1941, to Aug., 1942; ditto, for India, Aug., 1942, to Dec., 1944; C.G.I.E., Jan., 1946.

WOODHOUSE, ALBERT CYRIL, C.I.E., B.A., Madras C.S. (sub. collr.) (b. 5th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as dep. collr., 30th Nov., 1912; offg. collr. and magte., July, 1926; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; collr. (provl. perm.), Apl., 1939.

WOODHOUSE, HAROLD LISTER, M.C., Col., R.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 30th Jan., 1887).—Retd., Jan., 1942.†

WOOD ROBINSON, HAROLD, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. contr. of standardisation, rly. bd.) (b. 7th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. exec. bridge engr., 2nd Feb., 1923; bridge engr., N.W. rly., March, 1931; services loaned to M. and S.M. rly. from Feb., 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), N.W. rly., March, 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; dep. ch. contr. of standardisation, rly. bd., Apl., 1940.

WOODS-BALLARD, BASIL, C.I.E., M.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Sept., 1900).—First commn. dated 29th Jan., 1920; in milty. employ prior to 1st Jan., 1925; probnr. in pol. dept., May, 1925; asst. commr., D.I. Khan, Dec., 1926; city magte., Peshawar, Jan., 1928; supy. asst. commr., Hazara, June, 1929; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, July, 1919; under-secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Apl., 1931; asst. pol. agent, Quetta, Pishin, Oct., 1934; M.B.E., Nov., 1935; pol. agent, Loralai, Feb., 1936; sec. to the resdt. for the Punjab States, Nov., 1939; pol. agent and dep. commr. in Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1942; asst. pol. agt. and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin (addl. charge), Mar., 1945; pol. agt., Bhopal, Nov., 1945.

WOOLDRIDGE, LOVELL WILLIAM, O.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 9th Dec., 1901).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; in milty. employ to May, 1927; asst. pol. agent at Chilas, Nov., 1930; under-secty. to resdt.,

Hyderabad, Dec., 1934; asst. commr., Tank, Feb., 1937; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1937; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Oct., 1938; secty. and excise asst. to resdt., Central India, Nov., 1941; O.B.E., June, 1944; sec. to resdt. E. Rajputana, Oct., 1941; pol. agt. E. Rajputana States (addl. charge), Jan., 1946.

WOOLLER, ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 23rd May, 1912).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and sub divl. offcr., Apl., 1937; apptd. to Indian Pol. Service (subst.), and posted as asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1939; vice-consul, Zabul, Jan., 1941; ditto, Ahwar, Oct., 1941; on depn. to N.W.F.P., March, 1944; dep. dir. civil supplies and dep. sec. to govt., N.W.F.P., rev., dept., Sept. 1944; sec. govt. N.W.F.P., dev. dep. Aug., 1945; jt. sec. to ditto, Feb., 1946; dep. commr., Bannu, March, 1946.

WOOLTORTON, FRANCIS LESLEY DIGBY, Burma Engrg. Serv. (cl. I) (b. 1st Dec., 1902).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr. 21st Mar., 1929; confd. 18th Mar., 1932; exec. engr. 18th Mar. 1935; on mil. serv., June, 1945 to Jan., 1946.

WOOTTON, MALCOLM JAMES, Burma C.S. (cl. I) (b. 26th Feb., 1920).—Educ. Harvey Sch., Folkestone; apptd. on probn. after seln. Aug., 1946.

WORT, SIR ALFRED WILLIAM EWART, late Judl. Dept., Bihar (b. 17th March, 1883).—Retd., Sept., 1940.

WORTH, MEREDITH, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Repton, Grenoble, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; Brit. trade agent, Gyanthe, and asst. to pol. offcr. in Sikkim, Apl., 1933; offg. Brit. trade agent, Yatung, Apl., 1933; asst. pol. agent, Bahrain (tempy.), May, 1934; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, Oct., 1935; offg. pol. agent in addn., March, 1936; senior sub-judge, Hazara, Dec., 1936; asst. commr., Hazara, Jan., 1937; ditto, in addn., Manshera, Apl., 1937; confd., Apl., 1937; counsellor, Brit. Legation, Kabul, July, 1937; asst. commr., Hazara, Dec., 1937; secty. to resdt. for Baroda and Gujarat States, March, 1938, to March, 1939; on special duty as boundary settl. offr., Baroda, Oct., 1939; sec. to resdt. for the States of Western India, Nov., 1939; secty. to resdt. for Rajputana and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Sept., 1941; pol. agt., Orissa States, Sept., 1944; dev. offcr., Baluchistan, Apl., 1945; pol. agt., and dep. commr., Quetta-Pishin (addl. charge), May, 1945.

WORTHY, GEORGE TOYNBEE, late India Office (b. 9th Apl., 1882).—Retd. and re-empld. (temp.) Apl., 1942 to 31st Jan., 1946.

WRENCH, JOHN CECIL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 13th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 31st Jan., 1922; dep. consvr., Jan., 1925; offg. princpl., Madras forest coll., Jan., 1930, to Apl., 1934; dep. consvr., Feb., 1940; pers. asst. to ch. consvr., March, 1944.

WRENCH, JOHN MERVYN DALLAS, C.I.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 15th Sept., 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1942.†

WRIGHT, ALBERT ERNEST, B.A. (Oxon.), Imperial Customs Service (b. 30th June, 1902).—Joined the service, Nov., 1926; asst. collr. of customs, Nov., 1926; dep. commr., cent.

excises and salt dept., N. India, Feb., 1938; collr. of central excise, Calcutta, Dec., 1939.

WRIGHT, FRANCIS GERALD GRIBBLE, O.B.E., Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 2nd June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 4th Oct., 1921; offg. dist. supt., March, 1925; confd., Jan., 1929; O.B.E., June, 1943; asst. to inspr.-genl., May, 1942, to Feb., 1944.

WRIGHT, HERBERT LAWRENCE, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Punjab) (b. 24th Jan., 1887).—Retd., Dec., 1941.†

WRIGHT, HAROLD WESTLAND, O.B.E., Maj. R.E., Survey of India, (b. 18th June, 1901).—First commn. 13th July, 1921; in mil. employ to Jan., 1926; proby. asst. supt. Jan., 1926; confd., Nov., 1928; supt., Sept., 1937; in mil. employ, June, 1942 to June, 1943; O.B.E., June, 1943.

WRIGHT, PHILIP VICTOR, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd Sept., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 16th Dec., 1934; confd., Jan., 1937.

WRIGHT, ROBERT ERNEST, C.I.E., M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 2nd Oct., 1884).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

WRIGHT, ROBERT MARTIN, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Nov., 1908; supt., March, 1917; awarded King's police medal, 1918; princpl., police trng. coll., Bengal, Feb., 1928; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1929; confd., Feb., 1932; ret'd., May, 1944.

WRIGHT, ROBERT WALLACE, M.B.E., India Office (supt. of records) (b. 20th Sept., 1892).—Apptd. second-class clerk, India Office, 2nd July, 1912, and att'd. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.; transf'd. to registry and record dept., July, 1912, and to store dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from Nov., 1915; retransferred to registry and record dept., June, 1919; transf'd. to comm. and rev. dept., Feb., 1922; higher clerical offcr., Apl., 1922; resident clerk, March, 1923; parly. clerk, Apl., 1926; asst. sec., Indian delegation to 7th and 8th assemblies of League of Nations, 1926 and 1927; M.B.E., June, 1932; asst. supt. of records, Nov., 1937; supt. of records, Apl., 1939.

WRIGHT, SYDNEY WILLIAM WALTER, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 29th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 20th Nov., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Sept., 1931; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1933; confd. as dist. supt., July, 1937; awarded King's police and fire services medals, Jan., 1944.

WRIGHT, WALTER HENRY, late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 13th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service 22nd Nov., 1911; asst. supt., Nov., 1914; offg. supt., July, 1919, to May, 1921, and Oct., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1918; supt. (provl. perm.), Jan., 1927; confd., Dec., 1928; confd. dist. supt. (seln. grade), Oct., 1939; dep. inspr.-genl. (perm.), July, 1940; ret'd., Aug., 1946.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM ALAN, C.I.E., A.F.C., Ind. C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 27th Nov., 1895).—Educ. at Oundle and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1921; arrived, 30th Nov., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1924; confd., Mar., 1929; on mil. duty, 1941; addl. dep. sec. govt. of India, May, 1942; civ. aff. serv., June, 1945; offg. judge, high ct. Rangoon, Oct., 1945; C.I.E., June, 1945.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM MCKENDREE, M.B.E.,

Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 17th June, 1912).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., United Provs., Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; asst. dir., intell. bureau, govt. of India, Oct., 1940; M.B.E., June, 1944.

WRINCH, ROBERT CORNICK, India Posts and Tels. (p.m.g.) (b. 26th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. divl. engr., March, 1921; divl. engr., tels., Aug., 1928; dir., posts and tels., Sind and Baluchistan, March, 1934; offg. dir., tels., Apl., 1936; confd., Dec., 1936; confd. as p.m.g., Feb., 1943.

WUNNAM, ESWARA VENKATA SITAPATI RAO, LL.B. (London Univ.), Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical offcr., accts. dept.) (b. 20th June, 1893).—Apptd. to India Office, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., 19th Apl., 1920; transf'd. to Office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; clerical offcr., Oct., 1921; exec. offcr., 1st Apl., 1923; higher clerical offcr. (actg.), Apl., 1927; confd., June, 1927; actg. hr. exec. offcr., Oct., 1940, to March, 1941.

WYLIE, SIR FRANCIS VERNER, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Serv. (govr. of United Provs.) (b. 9th Aug., 1891).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 4th Dec., 1915, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on milty. duty from July, 1916, to March, 1919; temp. offg. as asst. commr., Charsadda, from May, 1919; pol. agent, Tochi, May, 1921; settlt. offcr., Peshawar, Sept., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1929; offg. dep. sec. for. and pol. dept., Dec., 1929, to June, 1930; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1930; dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1930; prime minister, Alwar, March, 1933; dep. agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1935; jt. secty. to the govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Apl., 1936; resdt., Jaipur, Aug., 1936; on spl. duty in for. and pol. dept., Sept., 1936; resdt., Jaipur, Feb. to Dec., 1937; govr., Central Provs., and Berar, May, 1938; K.C.S.I., May, 1938; pol. advr. to H.E. the Crown Representative, Dec., 1940; H.B.M.'s Minister at Kabul, Aug., 1941; pol. advr. to Crown Representative, Aug., 1943; govr. of the United Provs., Dec., 1945.

WYNDHAM, GEORGE COLVILLE, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag. Madras) (b. 19th June, 1916).—Educ. at Stowe, Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 9th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Dec., 1939; sub-collr. and jt. mag. Oct., 1941 to Oct., 1943; under-secty. to govr.-genl. (public), Nov., 1943, on l.p.t.

WYNN, KEITH GATCHFIELD, B.A., Burma C.S. (cl. I) (b. 19th Sept., 1918).—Educ. at Nether Edge Grammar Sch., and Sheffield Univ., apptd. on probn. after seln. in 1941; serv. commenced 10th Nov., 1942; S.D.O. Wakema, Oct., 1945.

WYNNE, MARTIN GOYMOUR, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 26th May, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 29th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; asst. commr., Port Blair, Nov., 1934, to Feb., 1938.

YADAVA MOHAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 10th Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as engr. student, 10th Oct., 1928; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1938.

YAHYA ALI SAHIB, KHAN BAHADUR, M.A., B.I., Judl. Dept., Madras (judge, high

ct.) (b. 23rd Aug., 1893).—Joined the service, June, 1936; apptd. dist. and sess. judge, on probn., June, 1936; confd. June, 1937; judge, high ct., Mar., 1945; addl. judge, high ct., Oct., 1945.

YAKUB SHAH, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 2nd Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Jan., 1929; asst. contr., rly. accts., March, 1930; offg. dep. ch. accts. officr., N.W. rly., Sept., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., Nov., 1933; exr., local fund accts., Punjab, May, 1935; offg. asst. secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1935; dep. dir., rly. bd. (temp.), May, 1936; offg. dep. dir., fin., rly. bd., Oct., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; dir., ditto, May, 1938; ch. auditor, N.W. rly., Dec., 1940; fin. advr. and ch. accts. officr., E.B. rly., May, 1941; confd., ditto, B. & A. rly., Oct., 1942; acct.-genl. cl. II (prov.), May, 1945; fincl. advr. and ch. accts. offr. N.W. rly., Mar., 1945; dir. accts. rly. bd., Apl., 1946.

YAR MAHOMED, ABDULLAH MEMON, B.A., LL.B., (Sind) (b. 10th March, 1896).—Joined 17th Aug., 1920; dep. collr. (1st gr.) (seconded), Nov., 1941; regr., co-op. socs. Apl., 1941.

YARDI, MADHUKAR RAMRAO, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 22nd Aug., 1916).—Educ. at Bombay Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1940 and served in Bombay and Sind as asst. collr.; offg. var. from Apl., 1945; dep. dir., civil supplies, and under sec. to govt. food, etc., deptg., Feb., 1946.

YARNOLD, FREDERICK HERBERT, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 18th Nov., 1911).—Educ. at the King's Sch., Worcester, and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., home dept., Nov., 1937; ditto, def. dept., Sept., 1938; headqts. asst., Mergin, March, 1941; asst. commr., March, 1941; M.B.E., Jan., 1943; civ. aff. serv., Nov., 1944 to Oct., 1945.

YATES, CLEMENT MERVYN SCOTT, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 7th June, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 7th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., June, 1932, and again, Feb., 1933; a.d.c. to govt., Bombay, Feb., 1936, to Sept., 1937; confd. as supt., Nov., 1939; Indian police medal, 1941; princpl. trng. sch., Nasik, Aug., 1943; asst. to inspr. genl. police, Poona, Sept., 1945.

YEATTS, WILLIAM WALTER MURRAY, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (collr. Madras) (b. 30th Sept., 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 28th Dec., 1920; sub.-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1922; under-secty. to govt., ch. sectt., Apl., 1926; census supt., Madras, Apl., 1930; dep. secty., reforms office, June, 1932; addl. dep. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Apl., 1935; offg. dep. secty., ditto, June, 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. jt. secty., ditto, variously from Sept., 1935; and again, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; census commr. for India, March, 1939; confd. as dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1939; it. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of supply, July, 1941 to Jan., 1945; and again May, 1945 to Oct., 1945; sec. (offg.) supply dept., Jan., 1945; and again, Nov., 1945; C.S.I., June, 1946.

YELD, PERCIVAL HARLEY, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Sept., 1884).—Retd., July, 1939.*

YENNEMADI, DATTATRAYA VENKATRAO, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay C.S., Judicial Branch (b. 23rd Apl., 1883).—Retd., May, 1938.*

YIN, KHIN MAUNG; B.Sc., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 19th Feb., 1897).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., educn. dept., May, 1927; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1931; confd., Jan., 1937.

YOGANANDAM, Rao Sahib, G., Madras Elec. Dept. (b. 5th Nov., 1901).—Joined the serv. as asst. engr., Jan., 1931; ditto confd., Apl., 1933; exec. engr., Apl., 1936; confd., Jan., 1939; suptg. engr., Apl., 1944.

YOKE, U BA, B.A., (Hons.) Burma Educ. Serv. (cl. I) (b. 10th Aug., 1895).—Apptd. 1st Feb., 1939; inspr. of schools, 8th Apl., 1939.

YORKE, ROBERT LANGDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Feb., 1887).—Educ. at Haileybury, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1917; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., Oct., 1917; revtd. to civil duty, Jan., 1919; actg. dist. and sess. judge from July, 1919; offg. dep. sec. to govt., judl. branch, March, 1926; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1926; judl. sec. to govt., May, 1927; apptd. judge, Oudh ch. ct., Jan., 1938; judge, Allahabad high ct., 6th Jan., 1943.

YOUNG, REV. ERNEST JOSEPH, B.A., late Indian Ecclesi. Estabt. (Bengal) (b. 26th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 6th Apl., 1918; junr. chaplain, Apl., 1918; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1928; offg. archdeacon of Calcutta, Mar., 1935; confd., Sept., 1936; ret'd., Oct., 1943.

YOUNG, FRANCIS WILLIAM WALLACE (asst. collr. of customs) (b. 26th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1906; held non-gaz. posts, June, 1906, to Jan., 1926; pay and accts. officr., Jan., 1926, to July, 1931; ch. accts. officr., Apl., 1931; asst. collr. of customs, March, 1938.

YOUNG, FREDERICK C.I.E., C.B.E., late Indian Police (United Provinces) (b. 16th May, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 16th Dec., 1909; supt., June, 1923; C.I.E., June, 1924; inspr.-genl. of police, Jaipur State, May, 1931; dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1939; C.B.E., Jan., 1944; ret'd., May, 1945.

YOUNG, GERARD MACKWORTH, C.I.E., M.A., F.R.G.S., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 7th Apl., 1884).—Ret'd., Aug., 1934*; re-empld. July, 1941.

YOUNG, CAPT. HAROLD MASON, Burma Front. Serv. (b. 25th Sept., 1901).—Apptd. 31st Aug., 1946.

YOUNG, HAROLD NORMAN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Sept., 1890).—Ret'd., Sept., 1940.*

YOUNG, JAMES, Maj., M.B.E., Indian Stores Dept. (dep. dir. of purchase) (b. 4th July, 1885).—In milty. employ from 20th May, 1915, to 15th Sept., 1924; M.B.E., Jan., 1920; asst. dir. of purchase, Sept., 1924; offg. dep. dir., ditto, May, 1925; confd., Feb., 1926.

YOUNG, JAMES WOLSTON, O.B.E., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 18th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1909; asst. acctnt.-genl., Sept., 1909; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; employed by Jodhpur Durbar from 10th March, 1923; delegate to Indian round-table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931.

YOUNG, SIR JOHN DOUGLAS, late Judl. Dept., United Provs. (ch. justice, high ct., Lahore) (b. 7th Apl., 1883).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, 15th Jan., 1929; ch. justice, high ct.,

Lahore, May, 1934; kt., Jan., 1935; retd., 19th Jan., 1943.

YOUNG, LAWRENCE HALFORD, Burma Front. Serv., (b. 16th July, 1918).—Apptd. on probn. after seln. in Aug., 1946.

YOUNG, REV. PHILIP NORTON FRUSHARD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Punjab, 2nd Nov., 1930; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1932.

YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, B.A., Ind. Police (asst. supt. Madras) (b. 20th May, 1911).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Madras, 23rd Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1935.

YUSAF, SALAHUDDIN AHMED (see Salahuddin, A. Y.).

YUSUF, SHEIKH MOHAMMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. sec. govt. of India, food dept.) (b. 13th Nov., 1913).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 19th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. sec. govt. of India, food dept., Apl., 1945.

YWET, U SEIN, Burma Educ. Serv. (cl. I) (b. 28th June, 1892).—Apptd., 1st Feb., 1939; inspr. of sch., 5th Mar., 1940; on spec. duty, dir. public instrn. office, 20th Mar., 1941.

ZACHARIAH, KURUVILA, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon), Indian Educ. Service (dir., public instrn., Bengal) (b. 4th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as prof., presdy. coll., 10th Jan., 1916; princpl., Hooghly Coll., July, 1930; dir., public instrn., Bengal, Aug., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1946.

ZAFAR, MUHAMMAD ABU, Indian Police.—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 11th Feb., 1935; confd., Feb., 1937.

ZAFAR HUSSAIN KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., LL.B., A.M.I.T., Indian State Ryls. (divl. transportation offcr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1894).—Joined service, Feb., 1917; asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1919; junr. scale, Aug., 1919; divl. transportation (traffic-commercial), offcr., senr. scale, Dec., 1937.

ZAFAR OMAR, B.A., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 27th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service as dep. supt., 7th Jan., 1908; supt., Sept., 1924; Indian police medal, Jan., 1938.

ZAFRULLAH KHAN, CHAUDHRI, SIR MUHAMMAD, K.C.S.I. (judge, Federal Court of India).—Apptd. temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Apl., 1935; confd., May, 1935; kt., June, 1935; chairman, central advisory council of ryls., July, 1935, to Nov., 1937; K.C.S.I., May, 1937; on spec. duty., Apl. to Aug., 1938; and again, Oct. to Dec., 1939; judge, federal ct. of India, Oct., 1941.

ZAHEER, SAHYID HASAN (see Hasan Zaheer Saiyid).

ZAHEER, SYED ALI, (b. 1897).—Legislative Posts and air member of Govr.-genl.'s Council, Sept., to Oct., 1946.

ZAHHADUR, RAHIM ZAHID SUHRAWARDY (see Suhrawardy, Sir Z. R. Z.).

ZAHID HASAN KHAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as appr. engr., Nov., 1926; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1927; exec. engr., confd., Feb., 1940.

ZAHID HUSAIN, M.A., C.I.E., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 6th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service, 14th March, 1918; dir. of fin., rly. bd., Apl., 1939; jt. finl. advr., mil. fin., March, 1940; addl. ditto (supply dept.), July, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; finl. commr., ryls., Oct., 1943; confd. acct.-genl. (cl. II), Dec., 1943; fin. member exec. council, Sept., 1945; reverted to Ind. Aud. dept., 1946.

ZAKIR HUSAIN, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 2nd Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 26th Sept., 1923; offg. supt., Sept., 1930; confd., Aug., 1936.

ZAMAN, AKHTARUZ, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 29th Jan., 1917).—Educ. at Hooghly Collegiate Sch., Chinsura, Kandi Raj Sch., Murshidabad, Calcutta Univ., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1941; settlement offr.

ZAMAN, S. R. (see Rashid-Uz-Zaman).

ZAN, U KYAW, A.T.M., Burma Agric. Dept. (b. 18th Nov., 1892).—Supt. farms, July, 1921; asst. dir. of agric., Dec., 1926; dep. dir. ditto, Feb., 1939; civ. aff. serv., Feb., 1945 to Oct., 1945; dep. dir. agric., Oct., 1945.

ZAN U THEIN, Burma Boiler Dept. (b. 9th May, 1897).—Asst. inspr. tech. inst., May, 1924; inspr. of steam boilers, Feb., 1928; inspr. boilers, Nov., 1932; chief ditto, Feb., 1946.

ZETLAND (MARQUESS OF), MOST HON. LAWRENCE JOHN LUMLEY DUNDAS, K.G., P.C., G.G.S.I., G.C.I.E., sec. of state for India, June, 1935, to May, 1940; sec. of state for Burma, Apl., 1937, to May, 1940.

ZIAUL HAQ, MIAN MUHAMMAD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 10th Sept., 1892).—Joined 20th July, 1915; extra asst. consvr., July, 1917; promoted to I.F.S., United Provs., Apl., 1931; dep. consvr., Oct., 1931.

ZIAULLAH KHAN, MUHAMMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 4th Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Lucknow Univ. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrive in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1937.

ZIN, (I) U, B.Sc., Burma Income Tax Dept. (b. 12th Aug., 1898).—Apptd. asst. commr. income tax, 1st Oct., 1938; left in Burma; resumed duty, 1st May, 1946.

ZINKIN, MAURICE, LL.B., Indian C.S. (b. 4th May, 1915).—Educ. at Haberdasher's Aske's Hampstead School, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. fin. advr., commns., Sept., 1945.

ZUBERI, MASARRAT HUSAIN, O.B.E., M.A., I.C.S. (b. 25th June, 1911).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Agra, and St. Cather. Coll., Cambridge; joined the serv., 24th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and serv. in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offr., Apl., 1939; pol. asst. and commdt., Border mil. police, March, 1940; sec., Punjab and N.W.F.P. jt. publ. serv. commn., Oct., 1940; under-sec., govt. of India, Sept., 1942; dep. sec. Apl., 1945; O.B.E., Jan., 1946.

    Pratap Singh of 38, 42

Baksh, Ahmed, K.B., 16
Baksh, M. K., 29

PUBLISHED BY HIS MAJESTY'S STATIONERY OFFICE

To be purchased directly from H.M. STATIONERY OFFICE at the following addresses:
York House, Kingsway, London, W.C.2 : 13a Castle Street, Edinburgh 2;
39-41 King Street, Manchester 2; 1 St. Andrew's Crescent, Cardiff;
80 Chichester Street, Belfast;
or through any bookseller

1947

Price £1 5s. 0d. net.
